entity_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
canonical_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
facts: Georgia Quick Start is ranked as the No. 1 workforce training program in the nation.
Georgia Quick Start has helped prepare more than 1.8 million Georgians for new jobs since its creation in 1967.
Quick Start provides free-of-charge job training for qualified companies.
Quick Start's training services have been utilized by a diverse group of manufacturers, including Kia Georgia in West Point.
Kia Georgia’s training facility opened in 2008.
Kia Georgia continues to work with Quick Start on updated training curricula with each expansion, including the addition of the EV9 line.
The success of the Kia Georgia training facility became a critical advantage for Georgia in the competition to land Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
Strategizing on how to train thousands of Georgians for Hyundai Motor Group’s first fully-dedicated electric vehicle (EV) facility in the U.S. began soon after HMGMA was announced in May 2022.
The plan is for an over 8,500-employee manufacturing project.
A multipronged approach is required to ensure future HMGMA and suppliers’ team members will be prepared for success.
The Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia is the first training center in Georgia dedicated exclusively to the EV market.
The Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia will be located on state property adjacent to HMGMA.
The Technical College System of Georgia, Quick Start, and HMGMA broke ground on the center in February 2024.
The facility is slated to open in late 2025.
While the main facility is under construction, Quick Start is providing training through its existing Quick Start Advanced Manufacturing Training Center in Pooler.
Quick Start has established temporary training sites at three locations to further support HMGMA and its affiliates.
source_title: Training Thousands of Georgians for the Hyundai Metaplant | Georgia

facts: Anovion Technologies is a supplier of premium synthetic graphite anode materials for lithium-ion batteries.
Anovion will build a new manufacturing facility in Bainbridge.
The project will create more than 400 jobs.
The project involves over $800 million in investment in Decatur County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the project on May 15, 2023.
Georgia is fast becoming the e-mobility capital of the nation.
Anovion is a climate tech-driven advanced materials company.
Anovion is one of the first qualified U.S. suppliers of synthetic graphite anode materials for use in e-mobility applications.
Graphite is the largest battery material used in electric vehicles by mass, more than copper, nickel, manganese, cobalt, and lithium.
Eric Stopka is the Chief Executive Officer of Anovion.
The Bainbridge facility will be Anovion's first large-scale expansion manufacturing facility in Georgia.
Georgia's strong technical workforce and top-tier schools will help advance research partnerships and contribute to a highly skilled talent pipeline for Anovion.
The region's growing availability of clean energy will result in increased sustainability for Anovion's manufacturing process.
The new facility will be 1,500,000 square feet.
The facility will utilize Anovion's proprietary furnace technologies.
The facility is located at Downrange Industrial Park in Bainbridge.
The facility will be the first of its size and scale in North America.
The facility will produce synthetic graphite for use in batteries that power EVs, electric storage systems, electronics, military applications, and other products.
Operations are expected to begin in late 2025.
The company will be hiring for technical, production, engineering, administrative, and management positions.
Bainbridge Mayor Edward Reynolds welcomed Anovion to the city.
Pete Stephens is Chairman of the Decatur County Board of Commissioners.
Decatur County leadership has focused on attracting quality companies and jobs to diversify its industry mix.
Taylor Walden served as Project Manager representing the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team.
The project was developed in partnership with the City of Bainbridge, Development Authority of Bainbridge, Decatur County, University System of Georgia, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
Pat Wilson is the GDEcD Commissioner.
Much of the critical supply chain for e-mobility and manufacturing is currently sourced outside of the U.S.
Anovion fills that need and delivers another critical component of the supply chain to Georgia.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry.
Since 2020, 40 EV-related projects have announced more than $21.9 billion in investment and 28,000 jobs in Georgia.
More than 10 of these projects are battery manufacturers, recyclers, and suppliers.
These battery projects represent approximately $8.5 billion in investment and 6,000 jobs.
Anovion Technologies is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.
Anovion is a U.S.-owned and U.S.-based advanced materials business.
Anovion is America’s first commercial-scale manufacturer of premium, cleaner anode-grade synthetic graphite across its existing footprint near Niagara Falls, New York, and Clarksburg, West Virginia.
Anovion began commercial production in early 2021.
Anovion is among a limited number of graphite anode producers in America to have successfully gained qualification for EV applications.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Anovion Technologies to Create Over 400 Jobs in Bainbridge, Invest $800M in Manufacturing Facility | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hanon Systems will create 160 new jobs and invest more than $40 million in a new manufacturing facility in Bulloch County on May 23, 2023.
The announcement was made while Governor Kemp was on an economic development trip to Israel.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia’s automotive and e-mobility industries continue to grow as the state welcomes Hanon Systems.
Governor Brian P. Kemp noted that as Georgia furthers its position as the EV capital of America, new jobs and opportunities are coming to hardworking Georgians across the state.
Min Sung, President and Chief Executive Officer of Hanon Systems, expressed pleasure in contributing to the local economy of Bulloch County through job creation.
Min Sung thanked the State of Georgia and the cooperation between its agencies for guiding the project.
Min Sung stated that the announcement aligns with Hanon Systems' strategy to support the industry shift toward electrification while growing its North America footprint.
Hanon Systems will build a new facility at Gateway Regional Industrial Park in Bulloch County to assemble automotive thermal solutions.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in May 2024.
Billy Allen, Chair of the Development Authority of Bulloch County, said Statesboro-Bulloch County is honored by Hanon Systems’ decision to locate its new facility there.
Billy Allen highlighted that the community is excited to welcome another automotive supplier working closely with the Hyundai Metaplant and the electric vehicle industry.
Billy Allen noted that the decision exemplifies the resources offered by the community and region to help companies succeed.
Griff Lynch, Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority (GPA), stated that the announcement further solidifies Georgia’s position as a growing hub for auto manufacturing.
Griff Lynch noted that GPA is proud to support the operations of Hyundai Motor Group and its suppliers as they deliver jobs and opportunity for Georgia residents.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at GDEcD’s Global Commerce team, represented the department on this competitive project in partnership with the Development Authority of Bulloch County, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that suppliers like Hanon Systems can successfully support larger industry ecosystems in Georgia through partnership-driven solutions ranging from workforce training to logistics systems efficiencies.

Pat Wilson noted that Bulloch County, as part of the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, has been part of a growing success story and that Georgia’s history of partnership continues to create opportunity for current and future generations.
Since 2020, over 40 EV-related projects have announced more than $22.7 billion in investment and 28,400 jobs in Georgia.
On October 25, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Hanon Systems was formed in 1986 and has grown into a leading supplier of automotive thermal and energy management solutions for electrified and conventional vehicles.
Hanon Systems' offerings include heating ventilation and air conditioning, powertrain cooling, compressor, fluid transport, and electronics and fluid pressure solutions.

links.context: Press Secretary contact for Governor's office
Deputy Press Secretary contact for Governor's office

source_title: Hanon Systems to Create 160 New Jobs in Bulloch County | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on September 25, 2024 that Shinsung Petrochemical (Shinsung) will invest $11.2 million in a new manufacturing facility in Toombs County.
The investment will create more than 30 new jobs.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “Eighty-three percent of expansions and new locations from the past fiscal year are on their way to rural communities, delivering more than 18,300 new jobs and $12.5 billion in investment to hardworking Georgians.”
Established in 1974, Shinsung specializes in products like automotive sealant.
The company will be a key supplier for Hyundai Motor Group and the Metaplant in Bryan County.
Chan Woo Park, COO of Shinsung, visited the location of the future facility in Lyons to celebrate the next step with new community partners.
Construction on Shinsung’s new manufacturing facility is expected to finish in 2025.
The facility will be located at 1407 Highway 1 North in Lyons within the Toombs Corporate Center at U.S. Highway 1.

Tim Truxel, Chair of the Toombs County Development Authority, highlighted the rewarding transformation of the Toombs Corporate Center over the last few years and expressed appreciation for corporate partners.
Lyons Mayor Willis NeSmith stated the community worked diligently to make the industrial park premier and that the location is ideal to service the growing automotive industry in Southeast Georgia.
David Sikes, Chair of the Board of Commissioners of Toombs County, welcomed Shinsung Petrochemical, Inc., to the county as a great addition to its diverse industrial base.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy for the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team, represented the department on this project in partnership with the Toombs County Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Power.
Commissioner Pat Wilson highlighted that generational investments in automotive and other key industries have created hope and opportunities across the state through partnerships at every level.
Since 2018, Georgia has attracted more than 36,000 new jobs and over $28 billion in investments in the e-mobility, clean energy, and battery-related supply chains.

links.context: Press contact email for Carter Chapman, Press Secretary to Governor Brian P. Kemp
Press contact email for Annalise Morning, Deputy Press Secretary to Governor Brian P. Kemp
Communications Manager contact email for Jessica Atwell at Georgia Department of Economic Development

source_title: Automotive Supplier Invests $11.2 Million in Toombs County | Georgia

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp joined President and Global COO of Hyundai Motor Company and President and CEO of Hyundai and Genesis Motor North America José Muñoz to celebrate Hyundai Motor Group’s historic impact on Georgia.
The Metaplant America facility will officially start vehicle production in the fourth quarter of this year.
Governor Brian Kemp stated Hyundai has been an incredible partner to Georgia for decades and highlighted the historic benefits brought by the project.
Governor Kemp noted Hyundai helps recruit businesses, gives back to communities, and partners with colleges and universities to connect Georgians with opportunities.

Hyundai and Genesis electric vehicles on display included the IONIQ 5, IONIQ 6, GV60, Electrified GV70, and the hydrogen-powered XCIENT Fuel Cell Truck for logistics between manufacturing facilities and suppliers.
Governor Kemp officially declared February 26 as Hyundai Day throughout the state.
The Georgia General Assembly passed a Resolution commending Hyundai Motor Group’s dedication to Georgia and recognizing February 26 as Hyundai Day at the State Capitol.

José Muñoz stated Hyundai views Georgia as a second home and expressed pride in investments creating tens of thousands of great-paying American jobs and bringing tens of billions of dollars in economic impact for decades.
Since the HMGMA project was announced in May 2022, LG Energy Solution was named as the joint venture partner for battery component manufacturing.
An additional expansion of the LG Energy Solution battery facility was announced.
Hyundai and SK On are building an EV battery manufacturing facility in Bartow County to supply Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. plants.
Since the initial announcement, Hyundai Motor Group’s planned investment in Georgia has reached more than $12.6 billion.
The project will create 12,000 direct, long-term jobs.

Attendees at the celebration included Lieutenant Governor Burt Jones, House Speaker Jon Burns, legislative leaders from both chambers, Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson, local elected and economic officials from the Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority, and company representatives.
Lt. Governor Burt Jones applauded Governor Kemp, state agencies, and the University System of Georgia (especially Georgia Tech) for their partnership with Hyundai.
Lt. Governor Jones stated stakeholder commitment enabled Hyundai's positive impact and creation of thousands of jobs.
Lt. Governor Jones noted these initiatives support Georgia's position as the number one state to do business.
Up to 300,000 Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia EVs are expected to be produced annually at the Metaplant.
17 HMGMA suppliers have announced projects across 10 Georgia counties beyond southeast Georgia.
Further supplier announcements are expected.
Supplier facilities are expected to create more than 6,700 new jobs and more than $2.5 billion in investment.
Supplier facilities will be operational prior to the opening of HMGMA.
Speaker Jon Burns praised Governor Kemp and the General Assembly for sound policy direction supporting Hyundai's Southeast Georgia investment.
Speaker Burns stated the Metaplant will have a generational impact as a force multiplier for investments in education, healthcare, housing, and transportation.
The nearly 3,000-acre Metaplant site has four building exteriors complete with internal outfitting underway.
The Metaplant’s architecture incorporates technologically advanced elements and design features representing the Savannah area, Hyundai’s brand identity, nature, and clean energy.
HMGMA is hiring full-time "Meta Pros" at a rapid pace for training and skills on machinery, office and clerical work, and numerous other employment roles.
HMGMA currently has 566 full-time employees.
Approximately 75 percent of the current workforce is from the Greater Savannah area.
An additional 37 employees are from Georgia but outside the local area.
Georgia Quick Start broke ground on its customized training center at the Metaplant site on February 8, 2024.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Georgia fulfilled a commitment with the groundbreaking and noted Georgians will be trained in high-skill careers as Meta Pros.
HMGMA partnered with local technical colleges to prepare students for entry-level employment at the Metaplant.

Electrical Vehicle Professional Technical Certificate of Credit (EVP TCC) training is available at Coastal Pines Technical College’s Baxley and Waycross campuses, Southeastern Technical College’s Vidalia campus, Savannah Technical College, and Ogeechee Technical College.
TCSG Commissioner Greg Dozier expressed pride in the partnership with Hyundai for innovation, excellence, and the future of the automotive industry.
In September 2023, Georgia Institute of Technology (Georgia Tech) and Hyundai Motor Company signed a memorandum of understanding to collaborate on research and applications for sustainable mobility.
The MOU covers developing a hydrogen economy, workforce development, and making cities smarter and more sustainable.
U.S. News & World Report ranked Georgia Tech as the top public university and No. 3 nationally in energy and fuel research.
Georgia Tech President Ángel Cabrera stated the partnership represents a significant corporate relationship to advance science, emerging technologies, and produce the next-generation workforce.
Since 1998, Hyundai has directed over $6 million towards local Georgia communities through philanthropic programs and partnerships.
The company is promoting STEM programs in local K-12 schools and hosting educational programs on alternative energy sources.
Hyundai supports local organizations dedicated to affordable homeownership and focuses on environmental stewardship championing the regional community.

Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority’s Trip Tollison stated Hyundai has given more than $1 million to regional nonprofits and formed key partnerships with local educational institutions.
Trip Tollison noted Hyundai committed to $7.59 billion in capital investment in HMGMA and will create 8,500 jobs.
More than 550 Meta Pros have already been hired, with 75 percent coming from the Savannah region.
Beginning with mass production start, electricity used to power the Metaplant will come from 100 percent clean power sources.
This aligns with Hyundai’s commitment to carbon neutrality by 2045.
A 41-acre park will be featured in front of the plant including trails, running tracks, environmental art displays, sports fields, and amenities for employee use.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated Electric Vehicle mass-production plant located in Bryan County, Georgia.
The facility will utilize a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system that organically connects all elements of the EV ecosystem to realize customer value.
The Georgia facility will become an intelligent manufacturing plant optimizing order collection, procurement, logistics, and production utilizing AI and data.
The innovative manufacturing system will create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise creating a value chain based on mobility, steel, construction, logistics, finance, IT, and service with about 250,000 employees worldwide.
source_title: Gov. Kemp, State Leaders Celebrate Hyundai Motor Group’s Historic Impact on Georgia | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: On November 01, 2023, Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hyundai Industrial Co., Ltd. will invest more than $24 million in a new manufacturing facility near Newnan in Coweta County.
The project will create 100 new jobs, with a primary focus on filling manufacturing production roles.
Hyundai Industrial purchased an existing building at 83 Amlajack Way in Coweta County to be retrofitted for production.
The facility will produce arm and head rests for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), including Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Hyundai Industrial is a global auto parts supplier established in 1978 and headquartered in Ulsan, Korea.
The company specializes in manufacturing car seats, arm rests, and head rests, with more than four decades of experience supplying some of the world’s biggest automotive brands.
Mr. Woosuck Kim, Team Leader at HDI America Inc., stated the company is excited to be part of Georgia's e-mobility transition and work with Coweta County for community and employee growth.
Coweta County Board of Commissioners Chairman John Reidelbach noted the facility will have close proximity to I-85, making transport to nearby automobile plants effortless.

Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project in partnership with the Coweta County Development Authority and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that with Kia in West Georgia and Hyundai on the eastern coast, suppliers like Hyundai Industrial can locate almost anywhere in the state while remaining central to at least two major manufacturers.

Since last year's Hyundai Metaplant announcement, partners like Kia and Hyundai have attracted a network of suppliers committing to bring nearly 6,000 jobs to Georgia, mostly to communities outside large urban areas.
Since 2020, Georgia has created more than $25.1 billion in investments and 29,000 jobs related to the electric vehicle market growth.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.

links.context: Job posting site for open manufacturing roles

source_title: Gov. Kemp: Hyundai, Kia Supplier to Invest in Manufacturing Facility in Coweta | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced on March 23, 2023 that Hanwha Advanced Materials Georgia, Inc. (HAGA) will create more than 160 new jobs in the City of Cartersville.
HAGA will invest an estimated $147 million in a new manufacturing facility to supply the Qcells facility in Cartersville.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia is leading the nation in attracting next generation jobs and has announced more than 4,000 related jobs since welcoming Qcells to the state in 2018.
HAGA is a subsidiary of Hanwha Group.
HAGA will supply Qcells with encapsulant film used in solar cells to ensure long-term panel durability.
HAGA will be the only company in the United States manufacturing solar encapsulants.

Inhwan Kim, CEO of Hanwha Advanced Materials, stated that their products are an important piece of the clean energy supply chain puzzle and building advanced materials in Georgia will create new careers in solar and help bring affordable, reliable clean energy to customers across the country.
HG Park, President of Qcells North America, stated that Qcells is doubling down on building a complete, domestic solar supply chain and this recent investment is critical to making that happen.
HG Park noted that working with Hanwha Advanced Materials will allow customers to confidently know that the solar they buy from them was made in America.
HAGA will construct its new manufacturing facility at Highland 75 Corporate/Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site in Cartersville.
The facility is predicted to come online in Summer 2024.
The company will be hiring for engineers and line operators.

Mayor of Cartersville Matt Santini stated that the community is thrilled to add another Hanwha affiliate, appreciating the investment in quality jobs and noting it diversifies the local industrial mix while illustrating growth in the clean energy market.
Bartow County Commissioner Steve Taylor stated that the news testifies to the community's patient planning efforts to jumpstart quality economic development years ago after difficult economic times.
Steve Taylor noted that HAGA's future jobs will be appreciated by job seekers and the local team that prepared the GRAD-Certified Highland 75 Corporate/Industrial Park.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.

The project was executed in partnership with the Bartow-Cartersville Joint Development Authority, Cartersville-Bartow County Department of Economic Development, Georgia Electric Membership Corporation (Georgia EMC), and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Hanwha Advanced Materials provides key components for the solar supply chain in Georgia, supporting the state's commitment to making energy solutions more affordable to businesses and consumers.
Pat Wilson noted that clean-tech jobs have been one of the fastest growing manufacturing sectors in Georgia over the past five years and HAGA adds another crucial part to Georgia's robust ecosystem.
Solar photovoltaic is the fastest-growing source of energy in Georgia, which ranks as a Top Ten state for solar energy production.
The Solar Energy Industries Association ranked Georgia seventh by cumulative solar capacity in 2022.

Georgia’s energy solutions providers are helping accelerate renewable energy product development by lowering risks, reducing costs, providing access to innovative industry research, and investing in superior infrastructure networks.
To reduce supply-chain barriers to the growth of the solar industry, companies like Qcells are dedicated to building a comprehensive solar value chain in the U.S.
In January, Qcells announced it would expand its solar panel production capacity in Georgia to 8.4 gigawatts, with 3.3 gigawatts manufactured at the Bartow County facility by 2024.

Hanwha Advanced Materials Company is a global leader in the irreplaceable, high-performance, lightweight composite materials market and provides products essential for lightweight composite materials and solar materials.

Qcells is one of the world’s leading clean energy companies, recognized as a manufacturer of high-performance solar cells and modules, intelligent storage systems, and large-scale renewable energy projects.
Qcells also provides renewable electricity retail services and packages to end customers worldwide.
Qcells is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea (Global Executive HQ) and Thalheim, Germany (Technology & Innovation HQ).
Qcells operates diverse international manufacturing facilities in the U.S., Malaysia, China, and South Korea.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Qcells Supplier to Invest $147 Million in Cartersville Manufacturing Facility | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Mercedes-Benz will establish Atlanta as its headquarters in North America by centralizing and uniting key corporate functions.
The company will move up to 500 jobs to the existing Mercedes-Benz facility, known as “1MB,” in Fulton County.
The company will make a multi-million dollar investment in a future state-of-the-art Research & Development (R&D) facility to also be located nearby.

Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia continues to lead the way in the future of mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz that are driving the automotive industry forward.
Governor Brian P. Kemp noted that Mercedes-Benz is a job creator with close ties to Georgia that is doubling down on its choice and growing its presence in the state.
Mercedes-Benz opened its “1MB” facility in 2018 in Sandy Springs.
The “1MB” facility currently supports approximately 800 jobs in Georgia.
Jason Hoff, CEO of Mercedes-Benz North America, thanked the State of Georgia for its support in deepening Mercedes-Benz’s roots in the Atlanta area.
Jason Hoff stated that bringing teams closer together will enable the company to be more agile, increase speed to market, and ensure the best customer experience.
The “1MB” facility located in Sandy Springs will house existing sales teams, financial services teams, and corporate functions.
The new state-of-the-art Research & Development hub will be located near Sandy Springs.
The company anticipates that the move to metro Atlanta will be completed by August 2026.
Mayor Rusty Paul of the City of Sandy Springs stated that Mercedes-Benz has been an outstanding corporate partner since establishing its headquarters there in 2018.
Mayor Rusty Paul noted that the expansion highlights the success of the city’s recent infrastructure and capital investments.
Chairman Robb Pitts of the Fulton County Board of Commissioners stated that Mercedes-Benz reaffirming its commitment proves Fulton County continues to be a destination for corporate solutions.
Chairman Robb Pitts noted that the county provides major companies an accessible, vibrant, and growing community for their business to thrive in.

Katie Kirkpatrick, President & CEO of the Metro Atlanta Chamber, stated that when the 1MB facility opened in 2018, it quickly became an integral part of the business landscape, driving economic growth and elevating the region’s global presence.
Assistant Director of Statewide Projects Elizabeth McLean represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The GDEcD partnered with the City of Sandy Springs, Select Fulton, Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power for this project.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Mercedes-Benz has become an integral part of the business community since relocating to Sandy Springs in 2018.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that the expansion and commitment to R&D highlights the talent and collaborative partnerships fostered by the University System of Georgia.
Mercedes-Benz AG is part of the Mercedes-Benz Group AG with a total of around 175,000 employees worldwide.
Ola Källenius is Chairman of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz AG.
The company focuses on the development, production, and sales of passenger cars, vans, and vehicle-related services.
The company aspires to be the leader in the fields of electric mobility and vehicle software.
The product portfolio comprises the Mercedes-Benz brand with Mercedes AMG, Mercedes Maybach, and G Class with their all-electric models as well as products of the smart brand.
Mercedes-Benz AG is one of the world’s largest manufacturers of high-end passenger cars.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Mercedes-Benz Establishing North American Headquarters, new Research & Development Hub in Metro Atlanta | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: October 25, 2022: Governor Brian P. Kemp, Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung, and company officials officially broke ground on Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America's new manufacturing plant at the Bryan County Megasite.
The $5.54 billion investment for this facility by the company and its affiliate suppliers was announced in May of this year.
The facility includes plans to produce a diverse range of innovative, fully electric vehicles (EV) and a new battery manufacturing facility.
HMGMA will create more than 8,100 jobs over the next few years.
The company is establishing a stable supply chain for EV battery and associated components in the U.S. market.
HMGMA’s new smart factory is expected to begin commercial production in the first half of 2025.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that record jobs and investment are coming to all parts of Georgia, citing award-winning workforce development programs and infrastructure as drivers for the state's economic success.

Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung said Hyundai and Georgia share qualities like respect for history, ingenuity, creativity, and determination to improve the world for the next generation.
Chung noted that Hyundai's EVs are recognized as best in class and the company aims to be the global leader in electrification, safety, quality, and sustainability.
Chung stated that through the Metaplant America, Hyundai will evolve beyond an automaker to become the world’s leading mobility solutions provider.

The groundbreaking celebration included a morning on-site event followed by an expo and program at Enmarket Arena in Savannah for hundreds of attendees highlighting "advanced technology and extraordinary human achievement."
HMGMA announced corporate social responsibility programs totaling $1.54 million to enrich local communities.
Programs through Hyundai Hope and Genesis Gives collectively committed $120,000 to roughly ten organizations supporting local communities in and around Bryan County.
Hyundai announced a $120,000 donation to Georgia Tech for STEM education scholarships.
Hyundai Hope On Wheels presented $500,000 in Scholar Hope and Young Investigator Grants to Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta.
Genesis Inspiration Foundation presented donations totaling $200,000 to two Georgia-based nonprofit organizations, Drawchange and Paint Love.

Kia’s "Accelerate the Good" program donated $600,000 to help local youth, with gifts to Family Promise of the Coastal Empire, America’s Second Harvest of Coastal Georgia, St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, and Inspiredu.

Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority Chairman Carter Infinger said the groundbreaking signifies HMGMA's commitment to the community and Savannah region, noting the $5.54 billion investment and 8,100 jobs alongside community contributions.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Hyundai placed a bet on Georgia sixteen years ago and has since become a Georgia company, changing lives in West Point and across the state.
Wilson said the Bryan County facility will directly create jobs of the future for the Savannah area and support opportunities in the EV revolution for Georgians across the state.
Central to the selection of the Bryan County Megasite was its close proximity to the Port of Savannah.
Under the leadership of Griff Lynch and the Georgia Ports Authority, the Ports of Savannah and Brunswick have expanded to meet recent challenges and moved record amounts of cargo.
With connections to the port by both rail and highway, Hyundai will be able to access global markets quickly and efficiently.
Non-affiliated HMGMA suppliers in connection with the project will invest approximately $1 billion in other locations around the state.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with about 250,000 employees worldwide.
The Group's mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
source_title: Gov. Kemp, Hyundai Motor Group Execs Break Ground at Georgia’s Bryan County Megasite | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

entity_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
canonical_name: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia
facts: When Hyundai Motor Group was deciding where to locate their visionary new Metaplant, they found much to like at the Bryan County site.
The State of Georgia committed resources to Quick Start to build and operate a training center dedicated to future mobility as an incentive that helped seal the deal for the Metaplant location.
A custom-designed training center was created as a complex of spaces and technologies derived from detailed studies of the industry’s needs.

Countless sessions with subject-matter experts, detailed research into the fabrication and assembly of electric vehicles, and a design vision that could create inspirational spaces were all needed to come up with the final building.
The Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia is currently under construction.
The Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia will open in 2025.
The training center will be operated by Georgia Quick Start.
Atlanta is listed as a location associated with the project.
The center will provide customized training for HMGMA Meta Pros as well as team members at affiliate suppliers located on the same site as the Hyundai Metaplant.
The initiative aims to ensure quality standards throughout the supply chain.
Savannah Technical College is listed as a partner or resource for the project.
Ogeechee Technical College is listed as a partner or resource for the project.
Oconee Fall Line Technical College is listed as a partner or resource for the project.
source_title: Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia - Georgia Quick Start

entity_name: Quick Start Advanced Manufacturing Training Center
canonical_name: Quick Start Advanced Manufacturing Training Center (QSAMTC)
facts: The Quick Start Advanced Manufacturing Training Center (QSAMTC) opened in 2018 on a 119-acre site in Pooler, Ga.
The QSAMTC facility spans 50,000 square feet.
Georgia has become a national leader in clean technology following years of market shifts and investment announcements.
Hyundai Motor Group announced plans to build a Metaplant in Bryan County.
Multiple suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant are also establishing operations in the region.
In 2022, Quick Start was tasked with building a training center specifically for the Hyundai Metaplant and its affiliates.
In 2022, Quick Start was also tasked with expanding capacity at the QSAMTC.
Quick Start provides comprehensive, customized, company-specific workforce training for qualified companies investing and creating jobs in Georgia.
Savannah Technical College is referenced on the page as a related entity.
source_title: Expanding capacity at Pooler training center - Georgia Quick Start

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) hosted its Grand Opening celebration on March 26, 2025.
The ceremony marks the completion of the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history.
Groundbreaking for the facility occurred just two and a half years prior to the grand opening.

Euisun Chung, Executive Chair of Hyundai Motor Group, stated that HMGMA represents advanced manufacturing capabilities, commitment to innovation, and investment in relationships with partners and communities in Georgia.
Chung noted the rich history of craftsmanship and manufacturing in the community, along with a talented workforce at HMGMA, is building the future of mobility with America, in America.

Event attendees included Hyundai Motor Group Executive Chair Euisun Chung, Governor Brian P. Kemp, U.S. Representative Buddy Carter, Hyundai Motor Group Vice Chair Jaehoon Chang, Hyundai Motor Company President and CEO José Muñoz, Kia Corporation President and CEO Ho Sung Song, company officials, state and local leaders, HMGMA Meta Pro employees, and other distinguished guests.
Governor Brian P. Kemp called HMGMA a once-in-a-generation opportunity made possible by commitment to working with job creators for decades.
Kemp highlighted collaborations between HMGMA and Georgia’s universities and technical colleges, including Georgia Quick Start’s on-site training facility, preparing Georgians for high-quality jobs.
Kemp noted award-winning infrastructure, such as ports connecting companies to markets nationally and globally.

Hyundai Motor Group (HMG) entered a landmark agreement with the State of Georgia in May 2022 to establish an electric and hybrid vehicle production and battery manufacturing facility in Ellabell, Georgia.
The site is located 20 miles outside of historic Savannah.
HMGMA was officially formed by June 2022.
Groundbreaking occurred within four months of the company's formation.
On October 3, 2024, less than two years after breaking ground, the first vehicle rolled off the assembly line.
The first vehicle produced was a Hyundai IONIQ 5.
Hyundai’s IONIQ 9 three-row electric SUV is now in production at HMGMA.
The first Kia model built at the facility will join production in 2026.
The plant manufactures electric vehicles for Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia brands.
The plant is also equipped to produce hybrid vehicles.
HMGMA initially has a capacity to produce 300,000 vehicles annually.
The Group's investment in Georgia related to HMGMA and joint battery ventures with LG Energy Solution and SK On is $12.6 billion.
This represents the largest single investment in the state's history.
The investment includes a total of 8,500 jobs at HMGMA by 2031.
The Group’s total investments in Georgia are expected to create nearly 40,000 direct and indirect jobs.
These investments are expected to generate $4.6 billion in individual earnings every year, according to the Center for Automotive Research.
As of July 2024, more than $2.5 billion in capital investment has been announced by HMGMA suppliers in 12 counties across Georgia.
Approximately 6,900 jobs have been announced by HMGMA suppliers as of July 2024.
This supplier investment more than doubles the originally anticipated $1 billion investment.
To date, Hyundai Motor Group has invested more than $20.5 billion in the U.S.
These investments have created or supported over 570,000 jobs nationwide.
Earlier this week relative to March 26, 2025, the Group announced a significant investment of $21 billion in the U.S. from 2025 to 2028.
$9 billion will be invested to establish an annual production capacity in the U.S. of 1.2 million vehicles.
The Group plans to expand HMGMA's annual production capacity to a total of 500,000 units.
This expansion represents an increase of 200,000 units from the original plan.
Existing U.S. production sites – Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama and Kia Autoland Georgia – will also see improvements in production facilities.
A total of $6 billion will be allocated to enhance the Group’s parts and logistics business.
This allocation includes establishing an Electric Arc Furnace-based integrated steel mill in the U.S. through its affiliate, Hyundai Steel.
The mill will have an annual steel production capacity of 2.7 million tons.
The mill will supply high-quality automotive steel plates to HMGMA when commercial steel production begins in 2029.
The Group will invest an additional $6 billion to drive innovation and expand strategic partnerships with U.S. companies.
Innovation areas include autonomous driving, robotics, artificial intelligence (AI), and advanced air mobility (AAM).
Through the $21 billion commitment, the Group anticipates creating 14,000 new direct full-time jobs in the U.S. by 2028.
The overall economic impact is expected to generate more than 100,000 direct and indirect job opportunities across related industries.
HMGMA’s architecture blends advanced technology with design elements reflecting Hyundai’s brand identity and the natural beauty of the Savannah area.
HMGMA integrated proof-of-concept manufacturing technologies and systems from Hyundai Motor Group Innovation Center Singapore (HMGICS), scaling them for larger production volume.
All HMGMA processes – order collection, procurement, logistics and production – are optimized using AI and real-time data.
Cutting-edge robotics and vision systems ensure high-quality vehicle production at HMGMA.
These manufacturing systems create a human-centered work environment with robots assisting human workers to improve efficiency.
HMGMA expands its mixed-model production capability and integrates various robot processes to enhance automation.
Automation includes automated guided vehicles for an optimized logistics system, Boston Dynamic’s Spot for exterior quality inspection in the weld shop, and parking robots in the assembly shop.
Automation in HMGMA's assembly shop is nearly double that of the Group’s existing facilities.
Oscar Kwon, CEO of HMGMA, stated it is a historic moment that their state-of-the-art smart plant is officially open.
Kwon emphasized creating a comfortable and welcoming work environment for Meta Pros alongside an energy-efficient and technologically advanced plant.
Kwon noted it is essential for Meta Pros to be happy, comfortable and safe for long-lasting careers at HMGMA.
All work zones, from office to production areas, are designed to encourage communication and build team collaboration.
Designs feature open layouts with ample daylight and indoor landscaping.
Production areas like the assembly building have team centers with skylights delivering natural light at the center of the building for employee breaks and meetings.
The 3,050-foot-long front of the HMGMA building’s exterior is lit in two parallel lines inspired by a stream.
This lighting outlines an area where interstate travelers can view vehicles as they travel by conveyor from paint to general assembly.
A solar parking lot located at the front of the site will generate 5.2 megawatts of energy.
This accounts for roughly five percent of the Metaplant’s total power consumption.
The solar panels are installed above 1,878 parking spaces, providing shade while producing electricity.
A 41-acre ecological park with trails, running tracks, sports fields, and picnic areas will be located in front of the Metaplant for Meta Pros to enjoy before or after work.
Construction of the park is ongoing.
HMGMA has deployed Hyundai Motor XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
A total of 21 XCIENT trucks are currently in operation.
These trucks represent more than a third of the Glovis America truck fleet at HMGMA.
The trucks transport vehicle parts from suppliers across the region to the 2,906-acre Megasite where HMGMA is located on a daily basis.
This initiative represents a significant step in HMGMA’s leadership efforts to reduce its carbon footprint and promote sustainable practices in daily logistics operations.
Hyundai Motor has supported several non-profit organizations impacting education, safety, and sustainability since 2022.
Since 2022, Hyundai and Genesis have donated nearly $4.5 million to local organizations in Georgia through social responsibility initiatives.
Kia's Autoland Georgia factory in West Point has provided over $22 million in support of Georgia communities since 2009.
This includes more than $6 million to support educational programs such as the THINC College & Career Academy and SAE’s “A World in Motion.”
HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group’s first dedicated electrified vehicle mass-production plant located in Bryan County, GA.
The plant boasts a highly connected, automated, and flexible manufacturing system connecting all elements of the EV and hybrid ecosystem.
All production processes are optimized utilizing AI and data.
Hyundai Motor Group is a global enterprise with about 250,000 employees worldwide.
The Group’s mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia and Genesis.

source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America Celebrates Grand Opening, Powering U.S. Economic Growth

facts: VinFast announced it will hold the groundbreaking ceremony of its electric vehicle factory in North Carolina on July 28.
This will be the first electric vehicle manufacturing facility in North Carolina.
The facility will help contribute to the supply of electric vehicles in North America and accelerate the global green mobility revolution.
VinFast's manufacturing facility will be built at the Triangle Innovation Point in Chatham County, North Carolina.
The site covers an area of approximately 1,800 acres.
The factory is designed to reach a capacity of 150,000 vehicles per year in phase 1.
The factory will consist of two main areas: electric vehicle production and assembly.
The complex will also house supplementary supplier businesses.
The project has received basic permits to begin Phase 1 construction.
When the manufacturing complex commences operations, it will create an ecosystem of suppliers and help generate thousands of new jobs.
Madam Le Thi Thu Thuy, CEO of VinFast Auto, stated that the North Carolina manufacturing facility is one of VinFast's key projects.
When it begins operations, the factory will be VinFast's primary supplier of electric vehicles to the North American market.
The facility will allow optimization of production and business activities.
VinFast hopes the construction in Chatham County will contribute to advancing the clean energy economy in the US and support North Carolina's green mobility strategy.
The factory is expected to start production in 2025.
Last year, VinFast was awarded a $1.2 billion incentive package from the State of North Carolina for this project.
The project also received critical financial support from the City of Sanford, Chatham County, and the Golden Leaf Foundation.
In addition to the factory, VinFast is accelerating its business and brand recognition in the U.S. by expanding its retail store and service center system.
VinFast is organizing local test drives and displaying products events throughout California to aid customer experience of its EVs.
VinFast is a member of Vingroup and was envisioned to drive the movement of global smart electric vehicle revolution.
VinFast was established in 2017.
VinFast owns a state-of-the-art automotive manufacturing complex with globally leading scalability that boasts up to 90% automation in Hai Phong, Vietnam.
source_title: VINFAST TO HOLD NORTH CAROLINA EV FACTORY GROUNDBREAKING CEREMONY JULY 28

facts: Senator Reverend Warnock broke ground at Hyundai Motor Group’s Metaplant and gave remarks at the Hyundai Community Celebration.
The event celebrates 8,100 new jobs coming to Coastal Georgia.
In May 2022, Hyundai announced plans to build a $5.5 billion EV plant in Bryan County.
The Senator participated in a groundbreaking event in the morning in Bryan County and gave remarks at a community celebration in Savannah in the afternoon.
Senator Reverend Warnock has been a vocal champion for creating green jobs and reducing America’s reliance on fossil fuels.

The Senator focused on investments for Georgia’s ports, including loosening up $8 million from the White House to ease congestion at the Port of Savannah when as many as 31 vessels were anchored outside the port.
Lack of domestic semiconductors previously led to work stoppages at Hyundai’s Kia plant in West Point, GA, an issue Senator Reverend Warnock successfully addressed in the jobs and competition bill.
Senator Reverend Warnock worked to strengthen Georgia’s skilled workforce by investing in increased research and development capacity.

At the Metaplant Groundbreaking Ceremony, Senator Warnock stated: “I’m glad we were able to push the jobs and competition bill across the finish line—signed into law, which invest in the kind of domestic manufacturing that we’re seeing right here… This is great news, great news for jobs, great news for young people who are looking for STEM research and development opportunities.”
“I’m from this region of the state, I know Bryan County…” said Senator Reverend Warnock at Hyundai’s metaplant groundbreaking ceremony.
The visit builds on Senator Warnock’s efforts in creating good-paying green jobs and reducing America’s reliance on imported fossil fuels.
Earlier this month, Senator Warnock announced he secured over $178 million for a tentative Augusta EV battery plant, which would bring new 600 jobs to Richmond County.
In February, Senator Warnock announced that he secured nearly $20 million in funds from the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law to build out Georgia’s EV infrastructure network across the state.

Senator Warnock has been in frequent contact with Hyundai about the auto manufacturer’s footprint and future in Georgia, and has encouraged efforts on the federal level to bolster the company’s presence—as well as EV and EV battery manufacturing, in general—in the state.

“I’ve been working with Hyundai officials over the past year and more as they’ve run into issues around supply chains. We’ve done what we can to strengthen our hand with the Savannah Port, at one point it was a major supply chain issue, I was able to get $8 million to address that. So I think all of those things move us closer to that goal and today we have this groundbreaking.”
source_title: Senator Reverend Warnock Joins Hyundai Motor Group for Bryan County Metaplant Groundbreaking, Celebrates 8,100 New Jobs Coming to Coastal Georgia » Reverend Raphael Warnock

facts: Rivian announced the first public customer deliveries of the all-new R2 mid-size SUV on June 9, 2026.
R2 vehicles have been rolling off the production line at Rivian's Normal, Illinois manufacturing facility as employees started taking delivery in April 2026.
Rivian is extending invitations to order for existing R2 reservation holders on a rolling basis starting June 9, 2026.
The R2 mid-size electric SUV was first unveiled on March 7, 2024, in Laguna Beach, California.
Deliveries begin with R2 Performance with Launch Package starting at $57,990.
R2 Premium configurations will follow in late 2026.
Multiple R2 Standard configurations will be available through 2027.
RJ Scaringe serves as the Founder and CEO of Rivian.
R2 entered an intensive multi-year development and validation program following its unveiling.
Every R2 in Rivian's development fleet accumulated tens of thousands of miles from coast to coast during testing.
Engineering teams validated R2 in temperatures ranging from -45°F (-43°C) to 122°F (50°C) across challenging terrains.
R2 is built on an all-new mid-size platform engineered for a more accessible price point without compromising capability.
R2 is nearly 2,000 lbs lighter than the R1 and sits on a shorter 115.6-inch wheelbase.
R2 features 9.6 inches of class-leading ground clearance, a 25° approach angle, and a 26° departure angle.
R2 interior features 40.9 inches of front headroom, 40.4 inches of rear legroom, and a 40/20/40 folding rear seat.
R2 offers 90.1 cubic feet of total storage with seats folded down.
R2 reservation holders will receive order invitations in rolling batches based on reservation date and delivery location.
Current Rivian owners will receive accelerated delivery timing balanced with non-owners to keep the production line moving.
Customers with expiring leases can enter lease information in their Rivian account for consideration.
By the end of June 2026, all reservation holders will receive an estimate of when to expect their order invitation.
The first vehicle available to order is R2 Performance with Launch Package starting at $57,990.
R2 Performance with Launch Package includes Autonomy+ featuring Universal Hands-Free assisted driving across 3.5 million miles of roads in the U.S. and Canada.
R2 Performance with Launch Package features dual-motor all-wheel drive producing 656 horsepower and 609 lb-ft of torque.
R2 Performance with Launch Package accelerates from 0–60 mph in as quick as 3.6 seconds.
R2 Performance with Launch Package has an EPA-estimated range of up to 330 miles.
R2 Performance trim includes semi-active suspension unique to that trim.
R2 offers an exclusive paid upgrade for a Launch Green exterior color and a special Rivian Green anodized key fob.
R2 includes a Tow Package with an integrated 2-inch hitch receiver and 4,400 pounds of towing capacity.
R2 Premium trim will arrive in late 2026 starting at $53,990.
R2 Standard trim begins in early 2027 with RWD Long Range starting at $48,490.
An additional R2 Standard version will arrive in Summer 2027 starting at $44,990.
Once an order is confirmed, customers can take delivery within 2–6 weeks.
R2 marks an evolution in software-defined vehicles, pairing an AI-ready architecture with 5G connectivity.
R2 features the most powerful infotainment compute in a consumer vehicle in North America and tactile haptic controls.
R2's multi-modal perception stack includes 11 HDR cameras totaling 65MP and a five-radar system.
Every R2 Performance with Launch Package includes Autonomy+ ($2,500 value) with future features added at no extra cost.
For all other trims, Autonomy+ hardware comes standard with an included 60-day trial, after which the service is optional at $49.99/month or a $2,500 one-time fee.
Autonomy+ brings L2+ hands-free assisted driving to 3.5 million miles of roads across the U.S. and Canada via Universal Hands-Free.
A limited rollout of Point-to-Point driving will come to R2 and R1 Gen 2 fleet later in 2026, followed by a widespread rollout in 2027.
R2 is outfitted with 200 sparse TOPS of edge AI compute dedicated to the in-cabin experience.
Rivian Assistant, an in-vehicle voice assistant, will launch on R2 later in summer 2026.
R2 features a redesigned steering wheel with haptic 'halo' dials designed in-house by Rivian.
R2 offers two digital displays: a driver display and a center display for navigation and infotainment.
R2 is built on an all-new, dedicated line at Rivian's Normal, Illinois manufacturing facility, which also houses the R1S and R1T.
The R2 program supports thousands of jobs in Central Illinois.
Rivian is constructing a second manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs, Georgia.
The Stanton Springs, Georgia facility is expected to begin assembling vehicles in late 2028.
In its initial phase, the Stanton Springs facility will provide up to 300,000 units annually in new capacity for R2, an upcoming robotaxi platform, and future models/variants.
R2 reservations remain open with a $100 refundable deposit.

source_title: Rivian Begins R2 Public Customer Deliveries, Opens Orders for Reservation Holders

entity_name: SK On-Hyundai Motor Group Georgia Battery Joint Venture
canonical_name: SK On-Hyundai Motor Group Georgia Battery Joint Venture
facts: SK On and Hyundai Motor Group approved plans to set up a joint venture in Georgia to make battery cells for electric vehicles.
The joint venture plans to invest $5 billion to build an EV battery cell plant in Bartow County, Georgia.
SK On and Hyundai Motor Group will each hold a 50 percent stake in the joint venture.
The new plant created by the two companies is expected to start manufacturing battery cells in the second half of 2025.
The plant will have an annual production capacity of 35 GWh.
The plant's capacity is enough to support the production of 300,000 EVs a year.
The future Bartow County plant in northwest Georgia is strategically located near Hyundai Motor Group’s existing and planned U.S. facilities.
Hyundai Motor Group broke ground last year on the new Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America in southeast Georgia to make future Hyundai, Genesis and Kia EVs.
In 2022, SK started production of EV batteries at its SK Battery America site in Commerce, Georgia.
SK Battery America is a $2.6 billion investment.
SK Battery America has an annual capacity of about 22 GWh.
SK Battery America's capacity is enough to support the production of more than 200,000 EVs a year.
EV sales in the U.S. increased by 70% in 2022 to reach nearly 800,000, according to the latest Global EV Outlook from the International Energy Agency.
Across its companies, SK has pledged to invest $85 billion in green businesses by 2030.
Across its companies, SK has pledged to cut carbon emissions by 200 million tons in 2030.
source_title: SK On and Hyundai Partner on Georgia EV Battery Plant | News | SK

facts: Rivian announced a new East Coast headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia on July 17, 2025.
The announcement was made jointly by Rivian, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, and Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens.
The new headquarters will feature a 500-person office space to complement the company's new manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The headquarters office is scheduled to open in late 2025.
Further expansion of the headquarters will occur in 2026 as construction accelerates at Rivian's new manufacturing site in Social Circle.
Rivian will occupy the top floor and lobby of Portman Holdings' Junction Krog District building located at 667 Auburn Ave NE.
The headquarters location is adjacent to the Eastside Trail of the Atlanta Beltline and is easily accessible by public transportation.
Rivian expects to employ approximately 100 people at the Atlanta site by the end of 2025.
The fully built headquarters will house approximately 500 employees.
Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated that Atlanta embodies top talent, exceptional creativity, and a desire to always be moving forward.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia is a prime location for company headquarters and expressed excitement about Rivian joining the list of brands based in the state capital.
Atlanta Mayor Andre Dickens highlighted that Atlanta continues to lead in EV innovation and technology integration, reinforcing the city's role in shaping its future economy.
Rivian plans to build a 7,500-person manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, near Social Circle, Georgia.
As it scales, Rivian plans to establish partnerships with universities, technical colleges, and local, regional, and state institutions in Georgia.
Rivian is an American automotive manufacturer that develops and builds electric vehicles and accessories.
The company's goal is to accelerate the global transition to zero-emission transportation and energy.
Rivian vehicles are manufactured in the United States and sold directly to consumer and commercial customers.
source_title: Rivian Announces New East Coast Headquarters in Atlanta - Newsroom - Rivian

entity_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
canonical_name: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture
facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution announced an EV battery cell manufacturing joint venture in the U.S. on May 26, 2023.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution signed a memorandum of understanding to produce EV batteries in the U.S. to accelerate electrification efforts in North America.
The signing ceremony took place at LG Energy Solution’s headquarters in Seoul on May 26, 2023.
Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, and Youngsoo Kwon, CEO of LG Energy Solution, attended the signing ceremony.
Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution will each hold a 50 percent stake in the joint venture.
The joint venture involves an investment of over USD 4.3 billion (KRW 5.7 trillion).

Jaehoon Chang, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, stated that the company is focusing on electrification efforts to secure a global auto industry leadership position and will create a strong foundation for the global EV transition through the new battery cell plant with LG Energy Solution.

Youngsoo Kwon, CEO of LG Energy Solution, stated that both companies are ready to drive the EV transition in America and will commit to offering ultimate sustainable energy solutions by advancing product competitiveness and global operational expertise.
The joint venture will have an annual production capacity of 30 GWh.
The facility's capacity will be able to support the production of 300,000 units of EVs annually.
The facility will be located in Bryan County, Savannah, Georgia.
The new facility will be adjacent to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, which is currently under construction.
Construction on the joint venture facility is scheduled to start in the second half of 2023.
The joint venture plans to start battery production at the end of 2025 at the earliest.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the new plant.
The assembled battery packs will be supplied to Hyundai Motor Group’s U.S. manufacturing facilities for the production of Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
The new facility aims to create a stable regional supply of batteries and allow the group to respond quickly to soaring EV demand in the U.S. market.
With this joint venture, LG Energy Solution now has seven battery plants currently operating or under construction in the U.S.
LG Energy Solution is concentrating most of its resources on expanding production capacity in the U.S.
LG Energy Solution aims to provide innovative products both in scale and with speed to expedite the clean energy transition in the U.S.

Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution have long been partners in electrification, having previously supplied EV batteries for vehicles including the Elantra Hybrid, Kona Electric, and IONIQ 6 dedicated EV.
The Hyundai Elantra Hybrid, an LPi hybrid vehicle introduced in 2009, was Hyundai Motor Group’s first electrified model.
In 2021, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution started construction of a battery cell joint venture in Indonesia, which is set to start production in the first half of 2024.
The companies aim to further strengthen their ties going forward with continued EV battery cooperation.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution to Establish Battery Cell Manufacturing Joint Venture in the U.S. | Hyundai Motor Group

facts: 9,460 jobs were created in the 4th quarter of 2022.
$10.5 billion in capital investment was announced in the 4th quarter of 2022.
The fourth quarter growth was driven largely by the electric transportation industry.
Hyundai suppliers announced locations in Georgia to support the previously announced Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan County.
A battery manufacturing facility located in Coweta County.
Almost 80% of job creation in the 4th quarter came from the manufacturing sector due to automotive industry locations.
Over 90% of capital investment in the 4th quarter came from the manufacturing sector due to automotive industry locations.
The year 2022 overall was record-setting for economic development in Georgia.
Only 10 locations accounted for 50% of job creation and 74% of capital investment in 2022.

A record 64% of job creation in 2022 was attributed to the manufacturing sector, an unusual trend considering service and non-manufacturing sectors have historically dominated job creation in Georgia.
Warehousing and distribution sector activity accounted for almost 6,000 jobs and $1.3 billion in capital investment in 2022.
Hyundai Motor Group and SK On announced 3,500 jobs in Cartersville.
Hyundai Mobis announced 1,500 jobs in Richmond Hill.
Freyr Battery announced 723 jobs in Newnan.
Ajin announced 630 jobs in Statesboro.
Solvay Specialty Polymers announced 100 jobs in Augusta.
Grupo Bimbo announced 295 jobs in Valdosta.
Orgill announced 355 jobs in Tifton.
NewCold announced 170 jobs in McDonough.
Exotec announced 150 jobs in Atlanta.
Norma Precision Ammunition announced 600 jobs in Ellabell.
Kiss USA announced 395 jobs in Ellabell.
Quikrete announced 150 jobs in Johns Creek.
Stuckey's announced 150 jobs in Wrens.
Vanderlande announced 150 jobs in White.

"With the establishment of a logistics center in the Savannah area, one of the United State’s leading container terminals; we are confident that a more smooth product supply to the eastern region, excellent manpower in the region, and active cooperation and help from local and state governments will lead to upgraded business operations."
