canonical_name: AVL Brands with U.S. Assembly & Manufacturing
facts: In response to evolving tariff regulations and growing demand for domestically sourced equipment, a curated list of AVL brands has been compiled whose products are either assembled in the U.S. or fully manufactured in the U.S.
The 'Manufactured in the U.S.' designation means products are designed, built, and produced in the U.S. with a majority of U.S.-sourced components.
The 'Assembled in the U.S.' designation means final product assembly, testing, and quality control are conducted in U.S.-based facilities, though some components may be globally sourced.
Generac designs and manufactures its battery systems and power electronics in Wisconsin.
Generac maintains full U.S. production oversight to ensure quality and performance for its durable energy solutions.
Tesla manufactures its Powerwall and other energy storage products at Gigafactory 1 in Nevada.
Tesla utilizes a vertically integrated process to produce cells, modules, and packs domestically.
Qcells operates one of the largest solar module manufacturing plants in the Western Hemisphere.
Qcells has end-to-end solar panel production based in Dalton, Georgia.
Sonnen manufactures its advanced home battery systems at its InnovationHub in Georgia.
The Sonnen facility handles full production, including system integration and testing.
Fortress Power assembles its lithium iron phosphate battery systems in Pennsylvania.
Fortress Power combines imported cells with in-house quality control and system integration.
HomeGrid’s energy storage systems are assembled in Nevada using a modular, stackable design.
U.S. assembly of HomeGrid products allows for shorter lead times and greater supply chain stability.
Mission Solar Energy assembles high-performance solar panels at its facility in South San Antonio’s Brooks campus.
The Mission Solar facility spans 246,000 sq ft.
source_title: AVL Brands with U.S. Assembly & Manufacturing

facts: ProColor Collision announced it has opened its first location in Georgia with ProColor Collision Dalton.
The independently owned facility serves drivers across North Georgia and South Tennessee from 927 S. Hamilton St.
The shop is owned and operated by Chris James, a lifelong Dalton resident with more than eight years of hands-on collision repair experience.
James has a career spanning insurance, mechanical, parts, paint, and fixed operations leadership.
James decided to join ProColor Collision after his two adult sons expressed interest in carrying on the shop's legacy.
James stated that ProColor Collision offers resources, structure, and guidance while allowing full ownership and control of the business.
The shop reports zero turnover, with the same employees who started with James years ago still serving customers today.
The facility recently underwent a major expansion, adding 5,000 square feet of workspace to support growing demand.
The facility is equipped with a Spanesi frame machine, OEM-certified GYS spot welders, and a full suite of Freon machines.
ProColor Collision Dalton serves a predominantly truck-heavy market with many customers driving full-size pickup trucks common throughout North Georgia's "truck country."
The team is equipped and experienced to repair vehicles of all makes, models, shapes, and sizes.
Formerly operating as Dalton Collision Center, the shop received the 2023 Best of Georgia Award determined entirely by customer votes.
ProColor Collision Dalton sponsors all four area high schools across all sports and activities, as well as Northstar School.
James prioritizes daily production meetings to ensure parts, repair steps, and paint timelines stay on track.
Nicky Woerner, vice president of sales and operations for ProColor Collision USA, stated that Chris embodies local roots, operational expertise, and a commitment to quality.
source_title: ProColor Collision Expands to Georgia

facts: Duckyang America leased a newly built, 270,144-square-foot distribution center at 89 Pegasus Parkway in LaGrange, Georgia.
Duckyang America is an affiliate of a Korean manufacturer and importer of automotive parts, including battery modules and related components.
The lease was one of the largest lease transactions in the LaGrange area.
The lease added critical momentum to the development of the surrounding Lafayette Logistics Park.
The deal sparked interest from other suppliers in the region.
The transaction earned a CoStar Impact Award, judged by local real estate professionals familiar with the market.
Duckyang America's distribution center is part of a new 134-acre industrial campus located near Interstate 85 known as the Lafayette Logistics Park.
The Lafayette Logistics Park was developed by Grandview Partners, a private real estate investment management firm headquartered in Greenwich, Connecticut.
Grandview Partners aims to serve manufacturing and supply chain companies with its developments.
Electric vehicle and component makers have invested some $27.3 billion in Georgia since 2018, according to the Georgia Department of Economic Development.

Missy Kendrick, chief executive of the economic development organization Choose Columbus, stated that the full building lease to an advanced automotive manufacturing operation will multiply economically and have a larger ripple effect in the community.
Missy Kendrick also noted that the deal further expands the regional market for advanced manufacturing.
Managing Director Wesley Budd, Senior Vice President Charlie Adams, and Vice President Jamie Leigh Boyd from NAI Brannen Goddard represented the landlord, Grandview Partners.
Associate Broker Kelly Choi of KW Commercial Atlanta Partners represented the tenant.
source_title: News | Electric vehicle supplier Duckyang America sparks interest in western Georgia

facts: Provides mobile EV charging units that come directly to customers anytime and anywhere in the Atlanta metro area.
Operates 24/7 for emergency calls with fast response times across the Atlanta metro area.
Covers a 45-mile radius from downtown Atlanta, serving the entire metro area and beyond.
EEERescue Atlanta operators aim to arrive within 30-60 minutes for emergency calls.
Charges virtually every EV on the road, with J1772 and Tesla adapters included.
Supports Level 1, Level 2, and DC fast charging capabilities.
Serves major EV brands including Tesla, Ford, GM, BMW, Audi, Rivian, Lucid, and all major EV brands.
Offers on-site mobile charging solutions for fleets without requiring the installation of new charging infrastructure.
Provides daily, weekly, or on-demand scheduling tailored to a fleet's operational hours.
Offers custom pricing for recurring contracts and volume discounts for large fleets.
Supports delivery vehicles, rideshare drivers, company EVs, and rental EVs by keeping them charged and ready.
Atlanta Mobile EV Charging is a proud member of the EEERescue National Network — America's #1 Mobile EV Rescue Platform.
EEERescue provides 24/7 mobile EV charging throughout Atlanta through the EEERescue National Network.
source_title: Atlanta Mobile EV Charging | 24/7 Emergency Service | Powered by EEERescue

facts: Auto parts manufacturer Sewon America is investing over $300 million toward a second facility in Georgia.
The investment was announced via a Feb. 21 press release.
Sewon’s new facility will be located in Rincon, Georgia.
The facility will produce EV body parts for original equipment manufacturers.
One of the OEM customers is the Hyundai Metaplant near Savannah.
Suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant have now committed over $1.8 billion in investment in Georgia.
This supplier investment commitment is at least $800 million higher than initial expectations.
Sewon’s investment is expected to create 740 jobs.
Sewon is based in South Korea.
Sewon already operates one facility in LaGrange, Georgia.
The second Rincon facility will be Rincon’s largest private investment.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp stated that on his first international trip as governor to Korea, he congratulated Chairman Kim on the company’s then-recent expansion and discussed continuing to work together.
Gov. Kemp added that those discussions led to the announcement as Sewon America continues to grow its Georgia operations.
In November, Joon Georgia became the first supplier to announce operations for the Hyundai Metaplant campus.
Joon Georgia plans to invest $317 million in a production facility in Bulloch County.
Later that same month, Hyundai Mobis became the second supplier to invest in the area.
Hyundai Mobis announced it would spend $926 million on an EV Power Electric system plant in Bryan County.
In January, Ecoplastic Corporation announced it is investing approximately $205 million in a new manufacturing facility in southeast Georgia.
Ecoplastic Corporation’s facility will supply components to the incoming electric vehicle campus.
Last month (February 2023), Seoyon E-Hwa announced it will invest almost $76 million toward its second manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The Hyundai Metaplant is estimated to create 8,100 jobs in Georgia’s coastal region.
Gov. Brian Kemp called the Hyundai Metaplant the “largest economic development project in state history.”
Hyundai began recruiting to fill the Metaplant jobs in November.
The recruitment information came from a Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority press release.
source_title: Another Hyundai supplier to expand near Georgia EV campus | Manufacturing Dive

facts: Sentury Tire is opening a $530 million manufacturing facility in LaGrange, Ga.
Rami Helminen serves as Sentury Tire executive vice president.
Rami Helminen stated the company selected LaGrange for its North American manufacturing location.
Rami Helminen cited education systems and a high quality workforce complementing leading edge technology in production and research and development.
Rami Helminen expressed confidence that Georgia has infrastructure to support a successful logistical operation.
The facility will supply growing aftermarket dealer sales and OEM sales to auto manufacturers in the North American market.
Sentury anticipates starting production at the LaGrange facility in 2018.
The facility will eventually provide 12 million passenger and light truck tires annually.
The new facility will create roughly 1,000 jobs.
Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal commented on Sentury Tire’s commitment to locate in LaGrange.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated the commitment speaks to Georgia’s business-friendly climate and the strong relationship maintained with economic partners in China.
Gov. Nathan Deal valued Sentury Tire’s investment in Georgia and the vision of the company’s leadership for continued growth in Troup County.
Gov. Nathan Deal expressed positivity that Georgia’s automotive manufacturing and technology capabilities will be assets to Sentury Tire as it serves a growing customer base.
Sentury Tire is headquartered in Qingdao, Shandong, China.
The tiremaker has facilities in both China and Thailand.
This LaGrange location will be Sentury’s first U.S. plant.
source_title: Sentury Opening Plant in Georgia - Tire Review Magazine

facts: The first production vehicle to be produced at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America will be the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5.
Production of the award-winning electric SUV is scheduled to start in the fourth quarter of this year.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America represents a $7.59 billion investment that will create 8,500 direct jobs in Georgia.
The company is currently training and preparing to begin production in the fourth quarter of this year.
The new plant will produce all trim lines of the IONIQ 5 except for the IONIQ 5 N.
The facility will eventually become the sole plant building IONIQ 5 models for the U.S. market.
Oscar Kwon, president and CEO of HMGMA, stated that the company is in the final phase of construction and getting ready for the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5 to roll off the line.
Hyundai markets its electric vehicle lineup as America’s Most Awarded EV Lineup.
The IONIQ 5 has been named Best Electric SUV, Best EV for the Money, and one of the Best Cars for Families by U.S. News & World Report this year.
Cars.com named the IONIQ 5 the Best Electric Vehicle for the second consecutive year.
In previous years, the IONIQ 5 was named the 2022 World Car of the Year, 2023 MotorTrend SUV of the Year, and 2022 Car and Driver EV of the Year.
The IONIQ 5 received the highest TOP SAFETY PICK+ award from the Insurance Institute For Highway Safety (IIHS).
The IONIQ 5 is Hyundai’s highest volume electric vehicle, with sales up 43% so far in 2024 and an increase of 82% in May alone.
Buyers who purchase the 2024 IONIQ 5 are eligible for a $7,500 cash rebate for the month of June.
Customers who lease the 2024 IONIQ 5 are currently eligible for a $7,500 federal tax credit.
Hyundai and SK On are building a $5 billion EV battery cell plant in Bartow County, Georgia that will employ 3,500 workers.
A Center for Automotive Research study projects that Hyundai’s investments in Georgia will create nearly 40,000 jobs and generate $4.6 billion in individual earnings annually.
Additional vehicles to be produced at the Metaplant will be announced in the future.
The Metaplant will produce vehicles for Hyundai, Genesis, and Kia brands.
source_title: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America Announces First Production Vehicle to Be 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5 - HMGMA

facts: Located in Savannah, Ga., the facility celebrated the start of production for the Kia Sportage Hybrid, marking its first Kia model and first hybrid electric vehicle (HEV).
Utilizes a hyper-flexible manufacturing architecture that enables seamless integration of completely different powertrains with minimal modifications.

Can pivot instantly between powertrain types, such as running a hybrid combustion system for a Sportage on the same line equipped for the heavy battery architecture of an all-electric Hyundai Ioniq 5.
Employs advanced automation including robotic arms welding frames, autonomous mobile robots (AMRs) delivering vehicles, and Boston Dynamics robot dogs patrolling for thermal anomalies.
Distinguishes between current "automation" as the execution layer and future "agentic AI" as the cognitive layer that will orchestrate supply chains and factory floors in real-time.
Plans to implement agentic AI for build-to-order manufacturing, allowing customers to custom-design vehicles online while AI sources parts and schedules robotic execution sequences.
Projects fixed conveyor belts will become obsolete over the next four years, replaced by AMRs routing vehicles dynamically through the plant based on specific build requirements.
Kia and Hyundai are already testing bipedal humanoid robots to take over high-strain, dexterous tasks that can injure human workers, with industry standardization expected by 2030.

Envisions a "Cognitive Factory" era from 2030 to 2035 where agentic AI controls logistics, AI-powered predictive maintenance eliminates unplanned downtime, and build-to-order becomes the dominant consumer purchasing model.

Projects large-scale additive manufacturing (industrial 3D printing) will mature by the late 2030s to print structural components, interior cabins, and solid-state battery housings using advanced polymers, carbon composites, and metal alloys.

Envisions plants like HMGMA becoming expansive enclosed 3D printers within 20 years, feeding raw materials like silicon, lithium, carbon fiber, and metal powders into one end to produce fully finished customized vehicles out the other.
Refers to its facility workers as "Meta Pros," emphasizing a permanent shift in labor from physical strength to cognitive ability for managing complex robotic systems.
States that state governments and corporate HR departments must provide substantial subsidies for worker upskilling in robotics oversight, AI diagnostics, and systems management.

Positions the facility's flexible architecture, advanced robotics, and cognitive AI integration as a competitive advantage over rigid competitors like Tesla (which uses Gigacasting) and BYD (which relies on vertical integration and brute-force scale).
source_title: Hyundai's Metaplant and the Future of Manufacturing

facts: Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) and Kia America celebrated the start of production for the 2027 Kia Sportage Hybrid, marking three major milestones: the first Kia model produced, the first hybrid electric vehicle (HEV), and the third vehicle assembled at the site.
The Kia Sportage Hybrid joins HMGMA’s lineup alongside award-winning Hyundai IONIQ 5 and IONIQ 9 electric vehicles, demonstrating Hyundai Motor Group’s comprehensive powertrain capabilities.
Production flexibility was built into HMGMA’s original design, enabling seamless integration of different powertrains with minimal modifications.
The plant has the ability to produce up to 10 different vehicles.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that mutual success is the direct result of a proven partnership approach creating long-lasting relationships between Kia America, HMG’s Metaplant, the local community, and hardworking Georgians.

Kia North America and Kia America President and CEO Sean Yoon called the event very important for Kia in Georgia and for customers across the US, noting Metaplant is Kia’s second major investment in the state and a testament to confidence in Georgia’s future as an automotive powerhouse.
Sean Yoon added that Metaplant will continue the strong momentum the Sportage Hybrid has achieved since being updated last year to quickly become one of Kia’s highest volume models.

HMGMA President and CEO Tony Heo stated that through the dedication of Meta Pros and a flexible manufacturing system, they successfully prepared to produce their first hybrid vehicle and first Kia model.
The event expands Kia America’s nearly two-decade commitment to production of award-winning vehicles in Georgia.
Kia’s West Point factory currently assembles the all-new 2nd generation Telluride, all-electric EV9 and EV6, and internal combustion powered versions of the Sportage and Sorento SUVs.
The celebration was attended by approximately 200 Meta Pros who shared in the milestone after extensive preparation for the new powertrain.
Many team members traveled to Kia facilities in West Point, Georgia and South Korea to learn best practices for Sportage Hybrid production.
Kia’s West Point facility currently employs over 3,200 team members.
HMGMA adds nearly 2,000 Meta Pros, with a very high percentage coming from the state of Georgia.
Distinguished guests at the ceremony included Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, Kia North America and Kia America President and CEO Seungkyu (Sean) Yoon, and Kia Georgia President and CEO Stuart Countess.
During the ceremony, the first Kia Sportage Hybrid assembled at HMGMA debuted by traveling to the stage on an Autonomous Mobile Robot (AMR), highlighting the plant’s advanced automation capabilities.
Governor Kemp rode in the vehicle as it was presented during the ceremony.
The addition of the Kia Sportage Hybrid marks a significant step in expanding HMGMA’s production portfolio across Hyundai Motor Group’s three brands: Kia, Hyundai, and Genesis.
Kia now has capacity for up to 550,000 vehicles across the West Point and HMGMA facilities on an annual basis.
Future model plans are not being released at this time.
Kia America is headquartered in Irvine, California, and was recognized as one of the TIME World's Most Sustainable Companies of 2025.

Kia serves as the 'Official Automotive Partner' of the NBA and WNBA and offers a range of gasoline, hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and electric vehicles sold through a network of nearly 800 dealers in the U.S.

The 2026 and 2027 all-electric EV6 and EV9, Sportage (excluding PHEV model), Sorento (excludes HEV and PHEV models), and Telluride are assembled in the United States from U.S. and globally sourced parts.
source_title: KIA AMERICA AND HYUNDAI MOTOR GROUP METAPLANT AMERICA CELEBRATE PRODUCTION OF THE 2027 KIA SPORTAGE HYBRID

facts: Hyundai announced plans to launch a scalable hydrogen production and dispensing facility in Georgia for Class-8 heavy-duty zero-emission vehicles.
The station will be located just 10 miles from the Port of Savannah within one of the nation's busiest freight hubs.
HTWO Energy Savannah development is a collaboration between HTWO Logistics, HydroFleet, and Capital Development Partners.
The facility is scheduled to begin operations later this year.
A second phase of the project will include commercial vehicle electric charging capability.

On the HTWO Energy Savannah site, hydrogen production and refueling stations generating 1,200 kilograms of hydrogen per day will support fast-fill zero-emissions heavy-duty trucking operations in the region.

According to Jim Park, senior vice president of Hyundai Motor North America's commercial vehicle and hydrogen fuel cell business, available infrastructure can be scaled to support up to 4,200 kilograms of hydrogen per day to meet future demand.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA), in cooperation with 3PL Glovis America, last year deployed 21 XCIENT heavy-duty hydrogen fuel-cell electric trucks for clean logistics operations.
Fourteen of those trucks were added to the Benore Logistic Systems fleet.
Located in Bryan County, Georgia, HMGMA is Hyundai Motor Group's first dedicated electric vehicle mass-production plant.
The XCIENT trucks transport vehicle parts from HMGMA suppliers across the region to the Megasite daily.
source_title: Hyundai plans commercial truck hydrogen and electric station in Georgia | Commercial Carrier Journal

facts: Hyundai's Georgia EV and hybrid plant has AI-powered robots, drones, and quality-control systems.
The technology helps to manage inventory, final inspections, and the parking of finished vehicles.
Drones buzz and scan stacked pallets to inventory parts.
Robotic arms swivel through their programmed arcs.
Cameras direct sanding tools to paint blemishes.
Uncrewed taxis move vehicles and parts to their next manufacturing destination.
A four-legged Boston Dynamics AI dog named Spot walks through the facility and scans components to ensure they're snapped into place.
The facility, called Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America, is roughly equivalent to 278 football fields and comes with a nearly $7.6 billion price tag, according to the company.
The HMGMA facility celebrated its grand opening in March.
While most US automakers are retrofitting decade-old plants with AI and robotics, Hyundai built HMGMA from the ground up with AI, Nvidia chips, and robotics at its core.
Each vehicle built in the plant runs through at least 23 AI-based or robotic systems before it's finalized, according to Hyundai.
Industry analysts told Business Insider that Hyundai's AI-first approach could be a boon for navigating policy changes and customer trends that impact the carmaker's bottom line.
The technology can help improve quality control measures and reduce manufacturing costs in ways that traditional manufacturing can't match.
Erin Keating, an executive analyst at Cox Automotive, told Business Insider that Hyundai's integration of humanoid robots sets a new benchmark for smart manufacturing.
Keating noted there are potentially cost savings and it likely offers the ability for Hyundai and others to respond faster to any industrial disruption.

At the center of Hyundai's Georgia operation is an integrated digital command hub that acts as a digital twin, or a computer simulation that mirrors the physical plant in real time, said Miles Johnson, Hyundai's communications representative for its electric-powered Ioniq-brand vehicles and tech.
Sensors collect data from across the production floor, then an AI algorithm creates a virtual replica of each step in the real-life manufacturing process.
Miles Johnson told Business Insider that AI can play a significant role in predicting optimized outcomes and identifying root causes of production issues.
Johnson stated that if a quality issue arises in production, the AI system can search past data it has been trained on to identify the likely cause and suggest a corrective action plan.
Keating said that Hyundai's AI can help detect defects in real time, analyze root causes, and suggest corrective actions that can significantly reduce rework, scrap, and warranty claims.
The brand's two retro-modern electric vehicles, the Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9 SUVs, are built with these highly technical processes.
The plant will eventually serve Hyundai's other brands as well; Kia production is set to begin in 2026, with Genesis vehicles planned for an unspecified future date.
In a press release, Hyundai said that the AI and robotics systems will assist humans, who will remain in charge of on-the-ground robotics maintenance, data analysis, and parts installation.
Hundreds of roles now involve programming the factory's 850 robots, troubleshooting AI, or interpreting production data rather than performing repetitive physical tasks.
The company aims to hire 8,500 employees at HMGMA by 2031 and has dubbed the project the largest single business investment in Georgia's history.
In March, Hyundai said it had already hired 1,000 full-time staff members at the plant.
The company also built a nearby Hyundai Mobility Training Center of Georgia, where it plans to train a pipeline of college-level talent for the plant.
The program is in coordination with three local technical colleges and is expected to open this year.
Both the Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9 were positioned to benefit from the then-President Joe Biden's Inflation Reduction Act.
The cars are both eligible for the $7,500 tax credits for EVs assembled in American plants.
Americans' uptake of EVs has underperformed analysts' predictions since Hyundai broke ground on HMGMA in October 2022.
EV adoption is facing more federal pushback as subsidies for EVs are set to end in September.
Hyundai is now betting that its EVs, including the three-row Ioniq 9 which launched in May 2025, will attract buyers without the need for tax incentives.
Some industry analysts say that Hyundai's AI-powered manufacturing can still work, even if it currently relies on EV sales.
AI's advantages like real-time quality control and faster problem solving could prove crucial as automakers face pressure from increasingly cheap Chinese vehicles and ongoing supply chain volatility.
Morningstar analyst David Whiston said that companies adopting AI for manufacturing and logistics are better positioned to manage costs and respond to disruptions.

Keating said that automakers leveraging AI for smart factories, autonomous logistics, and predictive analytics will be better positioned to scale production efficiently and meet regulatory and consumer demands faster.
Keating stated that AI and robotics are becoming non-negotiable for competitiveness.
source_title: Hyundai built its newest factory around AI-powered technology like robotics and digital twins | Business Insider Africa

facts: Hyundai Motor Group celebrated the grand opening of its new Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) production facility in Georgia.
The Trump administration announced a massive 25-percent tariff on all vehicles assembled outside the United States.
Initial plans called for 300,000 vehicles per year to be built at HMGMA.
The announcement noted the intention to expand production to 500,000 cars per year eventually.
HMGMA will produce hybrid and electric vehicles for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.
Hyundai has already announced that its Ioniq 5 crossover will be produced at HMGMA.
Hyundai has already announced that its upcoming Ioniq 9 three-row crossover will be produced at HMGMA for the 2026 model year.
Kia has not confirmed a vehicle for the facility yet, but Kia will start building at the HMGMA facility in 2026.
Hyundai Group announced the deal to build the plant in Georgia in May 2022.
The initial announcement came months before the Biden administration's Inflation Reduction Act passed that incentivized domestic production of EVs.
Kia's KMMG plant in Georgia already manufactures the brand's three best-selling SUVs (Telluride, Sorento, Sportage) as well as the K5 sedan and the EV9 electric three-row crossover.
Hyundai builds its best-selling crossovers, the Tucson and the Santa Fe, along with the Santa Cruz and Genesis Electrified GV70 at its HMMA plant in Alabama.
Hyundai Motor Company was founded in 1967 and is headquartered in Seoul, South Korea.
Hyundai may build a new midsize truck on the Chevrolet Colorado and GMC Canyon platform to take on the Toyota Tacoma.
The Metaplant may prove the difference between a vehicle like the World Car Award finalist Kia EV3 coming to America and not.
source_title: Hyundai's New Metaplant Could Save Its EVs In America

facts: Plans to build a massive electric vehicle factory in rural Georgia have divided Republicans ahead of their primary next week.
Donald Trump-backed David Perdue criticized rival Gov. Brian Kemp for offering taxpayer incentives to attract a “George Soros-owned woke corporation whose stated purpose is to combat climate change.”
Rivian, the well-capitalized electric pickup truck company that had one of the biggest IPOs in history six months ago, wants to spend $5 billion on a new assembly plant on farmland outside Atlanta.
The mammoth factory will create 7,500 jobs and produce up to 400,000 cars a year in what officials say is the largest economic development project in Georgia’s history.
The price tag for the deal was $1.5 billion in taxpayer incentives.
Perdue, a former U.S. senator, and other Republicans say Kemp cut a bad deal with a bad company.
Perdue said: “It’s a woke California company whose mission is to turn the world green... They aren’t interested in this part of the country. They just want to make money off of us.”

Vernon Jones, the Trump-backed candidate in a crowded Republican primary for the area’s open congressional seat, wrote on Facebook that Rivian is “a company whose corporate attitude is seemingly inconsistent with Georgia values.”
Jones pointed to the company’s vaccine mandate for employees and its “large focus on diversity & inclusion; including transgender benefits.”
Jones wrote: “Rivian needs to pull out, and Kemp needs to be voted out.”
The controversy exposes a growing rift inside the GOP between its traditional pro-business wing, embodied by Kemp, and an ascendent populist wing, embodied by Perdue.

J.C. Bradbury, a professor of economics at Kennesaw State University who has studied Georgia economic development plans, said: “There are legitimate reasons to criticize this, but I don’t know what George Soros has to do with it... You’re just connecting it to him like Six Degrees of Kevin Bacon.”
Soros, the liberal Jewish billionaire who is often cast as a boogeyman in conservative circles, owns a small minority stake in Rivian.
Larger investors in Rivian include Amazon, BlackRock and T. Rowe Price.

Perdue has visited the proposed site twice, hammered Kemp on the deal during their debate, spoken against it on national TV and ran a TV ad suggesting the plant was part of a corrupt deal between Kemp and Soros.
During a tele-rally for Perdue this month, Trump said: “I’m not surprised about George Soros getting all of this money from Kemp.

Anti-Rivian yard signs have sprouted up around the rolling green fields where the automaker hopes to soon break ground on a project so massive that it will straddle two counties and be bigger than the Pentagon.
Amazon wants to switch its delivery fleet to clean electric vehicles, starting with an order for 100,000 zero-pollution Rivian trucks that could be made in Georgia.
JoEllen Artz, the president of the grassroots No2Rivian group, said: “Sherman and his troops destroyed our community. Now this supposedly green company is coming to destroy it again.”
The No2Rivian group says it has raised over $250,000 and hired Atlanta lawyers to help wage their battle.
Artz added: “We want to keep it just like it is.”

In Rutledge, a bucolic one-intersection town that has served as a backdrop for films like “Selma,” locals are asking questions about what Rivian would mean for drinking water, traffic, schools, dark skies prized by astronomers at Georgia State University's nearby observatory, and their rural way of life.

Several residents worry the Rivian plant will usher in more development that will eventually swallow their hayfields and antebellum mansions, thanks to the area’s combination of cheap land and proximity to an interstate and a rail line.
Keith Wilson, who is running for the Morgan County Board of Commissioners to try to stop the Rivian plant, said he found no supporters for it after knocking on more than 800 doors in his campaign.
Wilson noted the county’s unemployment rate is just 2 percent, so the company should take their jobs where they’re needed.
Wilson said: “Perdue is going to get a lot of votes here... I think it might be enough to put him over the top in the primary. I really do.”

Local opponents like Artz and Wilson say their opposition has nothing to do with the fact that Rivian is from California and makes electric vehicles and say the opposition movement includes people of all political stripes.
They are happy to have a champion in Perdue — especially since he could potentially halt the project if elected governor.
source_title: Georgia electrical vehicle factory becomes Kemp, Perdue campaign battle

facts: Development for the Rivian factory near Social Circle, east of Atlanta, is underway again after a pause in construction last year.
Construction crews are scheduled to break ground on the Georgia factory in 2026.
The first vehicles from the new Georgia plant are expected to roll off the production line in 2028.
Georgia offered Rivian $1.5 billion in incentives to develop the 2,000-square-foot factory and promote economic development.
The deal with the state requires the company to deliver 80% of its promised jobs and investments by 2030.
The Georgia plant will produce the new R2 and R3 midsize SUV models.
Production of the R2 began at Rivian’s existing plant in Normal, Illinois, to make the cars available to consumers more quickly.
Rivian spokesperson Peebles Squire said the company is anticipating the new car will attract new buyers.

Peebles Squire stated: “We're opening up the Rivian brand to a whole new group of folks with a much more competitively priced vehicle that sits like right in that midsize SUV segment, and hopefully continues to invite people to make the switch to electric and really experience the brand firsthand.”
U.S.-based Rivian is waiting to see what impacts tariffs will have on the auto industry overall.
Spokesperson Peebles Squire said the stated goals of the Trump administration support the company's current strategy.
Peebles Squire stated: “The narrative here is all really about ensuring those jobs, bringing U.S. manufacturing back, and we're certainly aligned with the administration on that.”
The Georgia project aims to create 7,500 jobs by 2030.
Rivian recently opened a showroom in Alpharetta where consumers can see vehicles in person.
source_title: Update on new Georgia plant - site development resumed | Rivian Forums - R1 R2 R3 R4 News, Specs, Models, RIVN Stock -- Rivianforums.com

facts: Hyundai's Savannah, Georgia Metaplant produces the Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9 electric SUVs.
The facility was raided by law enforcement officers, including U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
Approximately 475 people were detained during the raid, with most being South Korean nationals who were not legally in the country.
Hyundai stated that the detainments will not impact vehicle production at the factory.
The Metaplant spans 16 million square feet and represents the largest-ever Homeland Security enforcement operation at a single location.
Detained workers appear to have been employed by LG Energy Solution, which co-owns the plant with Hyundai and operates a nearby battery facility, rather than directly by Hyundai Motor Company.
Hyundai officials confirmed that none of the detained individuals are directly employed by Hyundai Motor Company.

Hyundai emphasized its commitment to full compliance with all laws, including employment verification requirements and immigration regulations, and expects the same from partners, suppliers, contractors, and subcontractors.
A Hyundai spokesperson stated that vehicle assembly at the plant will not be affected by the arrests.

The company is reviewing its processes to ensure all parties working on its projects maintain high standards of legal compliance, including thorough vetting of employment practices by contractors and subcontractors.
The raid occurs as the Trump administration increases immigration-related detentions, arrests, and deportations nationwide.
A New York Times report indicated that attorneys for at least two detained suspects stated they were in the U.S. on a visa waiver program allowing stays of 90 days or less for business.

Reports indicate Korean companies frequently bring their own technicians to set up new operations due to specialized training, but U.S. visa laws lack proper mechanisms to sponsor trained workers to run factories.
ICE officials stated the raid was part of an active, ongoing criminal investigation and that arrested individuals were found working illegally in violation of visa terms or statuses.

ICE noted that people on short-term or recreational visas are not authorized to work in the U.S., and a Mexican green card holder was arrested after being deemed removable due to multiple criminal convictions.
The Metaplant is Hyundai's newest and most important factory, representing the largest economic development project in Georgia's history.
The project plans to create 14,000 manufacturing jobs and has been praised by both Democrats and Republicans.
Hyundai announced this year that the plant can also manufacture hybrid cars amid concerns over electric vehicle demand following the expiration of federal EV tax credits later this month.

Hyundai officials stated that the safety and well-being of all workers at its sites is a top priority and highlighted the importance of robust oversight throughout its supply chain and contractor network.
The Atlanta Journal-Constitution reported that subsidiaries of several Korean companies, including Kia and Hyundai, have faced lawsuits over immigration-related labor practices in recent years.

Labor advocates note that manufacturing companies are increasingly using third-party labor contractors to insulate themselves from liability regarding immigration or labor law violations within their facilities.
A Homeland Security Investigations official confirmed that various contractor and subcontractor companies are also under investigation.
In 2020, SK Battery had several dozen Korean workers arrested over alleged visa issues.
source_title: Hyundai Responds To Massive ICE Raid On Georgia EV Metaplant (Updated)

facts: Rivian held a formal groundbreaking ceremony for its new manufacturing plant in Stanton Springs, Georgia.
The facility is designed for cutting-edge advanced manufacturing, efficiency, and sustainability.
The nearly 2,000-acre site will employ modern construction techniques.
Advanced environmental management will be used at the site.
The site is intended to be elegantly integrated with the surrounding environment.
The facility is intended to feature recreational trails, including a Rivian experience trail.
The first phase of construction is expected to begin in 2026.
Customer vehicle production in Georgia is anticipated to start in 2028.
Upon completion of the second phase, the facility will have an annual production capacity of up to 400,000 vehicles.
The plant is expected to create 7,500 jobs by 2030.
The plant will build Rivian’s next generation vehicles.

Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated that the project cements Rivian's future at the Georgia plant and helps ensure America maintains its technology leadership and excellence in automobile manufacturing.
SOM is helping enable Rivian’s next generation of vehicles alongside Clayco and Affiliated Engineers.
source_title: Rivian Breaks Ground on New Plant in Georgia – SOM

facts: Kia Georgia presented a $100,000 donation to the Technical College System of Georgia (TCSG) Foundation at an event in Atlanta celebrating TCSG’s top students and instructors.
Kia Georgia President and CEO Stuart Countess presented the check at TCSG’s annual GOAL and Rick Perkins Awards banquet in Buckhead.

TCSG Commissioner Greg Dozier stated that Kia is a valued partner of TCSG and a remarkable corporate citizen in Georgia, and that the funding will help technical college students remain on track to graduate and join Georgia’s workforce.
Since 2016, Kia Georgia has provided funding to award the state GOAL winner with a brand-new Kia as the grand prize.
This year’s GOAL winner will receive a 2023 Kia K5, which is built at the Kia facility in West Point.
The remaining donation funding will support The Last Mile Fund to provide gap funding for more than 131,000 technical education students served within TCSG.
Stuart Countess, President & CEO of Kia Georgia, stated that TCSG undergirds workforce development by adapting to the evolving technical needs of state industries and businesses.
Stuart Countess noted that TCSG's impact is seen through economic growth in Georgia and the continued application of latest technologies in manufacturing, logistics, and health care.
In 2006, Kia made one of the largest single foreign investments in Georgia's history by establishing a state-of-the-art production facility in West Point.
Kia Georgia has an annual capacity of more than 340,000 vehicles.
Kia Georgia is home to the Telluride SUV, Sorento SUV, Sportage SUV, and K5 midsize sedan.
The company recently announced plans to produce the Kia EV9 at the West Point facility, launching its first battery-electric vehicle produced in the U.S.
The advanced manufacturing facility represents a $1.9 billion investment on a 2,200-acre site and operates 24 hours a day.

Kia Georgia’s partnership with TCSG produced a Maintenance Certification Program through collaboration with Quick Start to upskill production team members for maintenance tasks on highly automated production equipment.
The Maintenance Certification Program was expanded into West Georgia Technical College and integrated with the campus's standard offerings.
This program provides a steady stream of qualified candidates to fill the need for maintenance team members at Kia Georgia and other local manufacturers.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
Kia Georgia began mass production on Nov. 16, 2009.
Kia Georgia will soon produce the all-electric 2024 EV9 SUV.
source_title: Kia Georgia Donates $100,000 to Technical College System of Georgia Foundation – TCSG | Technical College System of Georgia

facts: A massive hailstorm damaged over 13,000 vehicles at the Kia Georgia plant in 2023.
The company implemented a cutting-edge solar canopy system from VPS to protect vehicles from severe weather and power the plant with clean energy.
The system will include 17,000 solar panels.
The system will generate over 10 megawatts of electricity.
The generated electricity is enough to supply up to 30% of the plant’s annual energy needs.
The project is part of Kia’s commitment to reducing carbon emissions by 97% by 2045.
The Kia Georgia plant is located in West Point.
The plant builds vehicle models including the Telluride, Sorento, Sportage, EV9, and EV6.
The solar project is about 40% complete.
The solar project is set to finish by the end of 2025.
The initiative aligns with Hyundai Motor Group’s multi-billion-dollar investment in EV and battery production across Georgia.
source_title: Kia Georgia Goes Green with Solar Panel Project | Jim Ellis Kia of Kennesaw

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Kia will invest more than $200 million at its West Point plant.
Kia will create nearly 200 new jobs at the facility.
The investment and hiring are to accommodate assembly of the company's all-electric 2024 EV9 three-row SUV.
The production line transformation will make the EV9 the fifth model assembled at Kia Georgia.
The EV9 will join the Telluride, Sorento, and Sportage SUVs, and the K5 mid-size sedan as models assembled at the plant.
EV9 assembly is expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
source_title: KIA to invest more than $200M, hire nearly 200 as it starts ...

facts: SK On is expanding its factory in Georgia to become a comprehensive production base in the U.S. with capabilities for both electric vehicle batteries and energy storage system (ESS) batteries.

In September of this year, SK On signed its first large-scale energy storage system battery order in the United States with Flatiron Energy Development, a renewable energy developer based in Colorado.
The order is valued at approximately 2 trillion won (about $1.4 billion).
The order involves 7.2 GWh of cost-effective lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries.

To fulfill this four-year contract that takes effect next year, SK On plans to convert part of the nickel-cobalt-manganese (NCM) electric vehicle battery production line at its SK Battery America plant in Georgia into an LFP battery production line.
Mass production of the LFP batteries is expected to begin in the second half of 2026.
After this collaboration, SK Battery America will upgrade to a dual-chemistry production base capable of simultaneously producing NCM and LFP batteries.
This allows SK On to respond more flexibly to changes in market demand in the fields of electric vehicles and energy storage systems in the United States.

By increasing its LFP battery production capacity, SK On will become one of only two companies in North America, along with Korean battery manufacturer LG Energy Solution, that can independently operate and produce both NCM and LFP batteries.
Samsung SDI is considering converting part of its joint venture production line with Stellantis into a production line for LFP batteries used in energy storage systems.
SK On's dual-track strategy is highly aligned with the demands of the U.S. market.
In the electric vehicle sector, there is still strong demand for NCM batteries with high energy density and long range.
In the energy storage system sector, LFP batteries are increasingly favored due to their cost advantages and safety.
According to data from the International Energy Agency (IEA), in 2023, high-nickel NCM batteries accounted for 90% of the installed capacity of electric vehicle batteries in the United States.
This proportion further increased to 92% this year.
Market research firm Mordor Intelligence pointed out that mainstream automakers prefer to choose NCM batteries to balance range and safety.

In April this year, Eric Kuyper, the Chief Technology Officer of American electric vehicle manufacturer Slate, explained that due to limited space under the vehicle chassis, to achieve long range, currently only NCM batteries are feasible.
Slate signed a supply agreement with SK On.
LFP batteries are rapidly capturing the U.S. energy storage system battery market, which was once dominated by China.
The increasing tariffs in the U.S. are currently at 40.9% and expected to rise to 58.4% next year.
These tariffs are eroding the cost advantage of Chinese competitors.
The price of Chinese LFP batteries is expected to rise from $73 per kilowatt-hour in 2023 to about $87 this year.
This price approaches the price range of Korean companies' U.S.-made batteries, which is between $85 and $90 per kilowatt-hour.
A knowledgeable source stated that by enriching its product matrix, SK On is building a more solid foundation for growth in the U.S. market.
Given the U.S. government's tightening restrictions on Chinese batteries, the ability to simultaneously produce NCM and LFP batteries domestically has significant strategic importance.
source_title: SK On Expands EV and ESS Battery Plant in Georgia, United States | Plastmatch News

facts: The factory slated to provide batteries to Hyundai’s electric vehicle plant near Savannah will open later this month following a prolonged delay caused by an immigration raid last year that rippled across Georgia and the globe.
Hyundai CEO José Muñoz announced the battery plant’s imminent opening Tuesday at the Semafor World Economy conference in Washington.
Semafor is a digital news outlet and the annual event features Fortune 500 CEOs, government officials and business leaders.
The battery factory is a joint operation between the Korean carmaker and Korean battery giant LG Energy Solution.
The facility is located on the Hyundai Metaplant campus along I-16, about 30 minutes west of downtown Savannah.
The plant will produce batteries for Hyundai's electric vehicle plant near Savannah.
source_title: Hyundai battery plant in Georgia reportedly set to open months after ICE raid

facts: SK Battery America laid off nearly 1,000 workers at its Georgia plant.
source_title: SK Battery America lays off nearly 1,000 workers at Georgia plant - NewsBreak

facts: Construction at the HL-GA Battery Company facility on the Hyundai megasite is paused until 2026 following an immigration raid.
Homeland Security officials said 475 workers were detained at the construction site during the execution of a search warrant on September 4.
More than 300 of those detained workers were South Korean nationals.
Officials said the raid led to construction at the site being halted.
In information released Friday, construction is not planned to resume for the next several months.
According to a spokesperson, construction at the plant will remain on hold until the first half of 2026.
LG Energy Solution stated that the U.S. is an extremely important market for them.
LG Energy Solution has invested more than $25 billion in North American manufacturing.
LG Energy Solution expects to create about 14,000 jobs in North America by 2027.
In the mid-term, LG Energy Solution plans to locate half of its total global battery cell-making capacity in the U.S.
LG Energy Solution remains committed to its projects in the U.S. and will continue to navigate the circumstances with the aim to continue necessary investments and business.
Moving forward, LG Energy Solution will continue to cooperate with the Korean and U.S. governments on the visa process.
source_title: Georgia: Hyundai facility construction paused until 2026 - WJCL

facts: The US immigration raid targeted a $4.3bn battery plant being developed by South Korean companies.
On September 4, 317 South Koreans were detained at the Hyundai and LG Energy Solution site in Georgia.
The detained individuals were engineers and technicians setting up the sprawling plant, which will power up 300,000 electric vehicles each year.
The joint venture is called HL-GA Battery Company.
President Donald Trump called on foreign investors to transfer knowledge to support more US jobs following the raid.
FDI experts argue it would be impractical for locals to perform the temporary work at the Bryan County site due to a lack of institutional knowledge of how the specialist machinery works together.

Hyung-Gul Kim, associate professor of international economic law at TLBU Graduate School of Law in South Korea, states that more than 90 per cent of the equipment is custom-made, designed and manufactured in Korea before being brought into the US for installation, testing, and refinement.
Kim notes it is very difficult for US engineering companies to perform this work.
Within large FDI projects, brick-and-mortar construction and manufacturing operations can often be performed by local labour with the right training.
Bringing sophisticated plants online requires knowledge, skills, and coordination honed over years by investors and sometimes their partner companies.

South Korea has fostered a 'mother factory' strategy where firms fly in businesses and workers from their supplier network to replicate the ecosystem at new plants abroad rather than relying on local engineers and technicians.
Of the 317 detained Koreans, 47 were reportedly employees of LGES while the rest were hired by the JV’s partner companies and subcontractors.
US authorities claim the detainees breached their stay permit terms, which are understood to have been B1 visas, ESTAs (allowing certain types of business), and B2 visas (predominantly for tourism).
Kim compares plant setup to a conveyor system, noting Korean firms prefer doing the whole process with partnering companies they have worked with for more than 30 years.

Kim's observations are based on research he co-led in 2023 involving site visits to incentives-chasing mega projects in the US by Samsung, LG, and Hyundai, plus Hyundai’s biggest production plant and R&D centre in Ulsan, South Korea.

Didi Caldwell, president and CEO of site selector Global Location Strategies, says it is almost unheard of for foreign investors in advanced technologies to not fly in workers who have established the same operations elsewhere.
In 2023, Taiwanese chipmaker TSMC announced delaying the commissioning of a fab in Arizona due to a local shortage of specialised expertise to handle and install its advanced equipment.
Experts warn that training local workers in these processes is a formidable task.
Kim notes equipment installation is a one-off job for a single company’s new plant, making little sense to have a team of local specialists dedicated to such sporadic tasks.
An LGES spokesperson states that support from engineers with specialised knowledge and experience is essential, but the firm will hire more local personnel to operate production lines.
Jae Kim, president of the Southeast US Korea Chamber of Commerce, says hiring Korean specialists is also a business decision aimed at earning the best financial returns.
To build a US battery plant on the fastest timescale, working with Korean suppliers and workers with know-how from other battery plants will work better than finding and training new local companies.
Both Jae Kim and Hyung-Gul Kim note that language and cultural barriers are additional factors in hiring decisions.
Hyung-Gul Kim notes Korean workers are very hard working and having them work as a team around the world makes a big difference in timing and scheduling.
He adds that while some processes can be conducted by US local companies, it may cause problems in teamwork.
The immigration raid shocked other investors in the US who rely on foreign specialists.
Caldwell says the raid has sent shivers through the FDI community.
Data from fDi Markets suggests South Korean, Chinese, Japanese and Taiwanese investors have pledged to invest some $276bn into US high-technology mega projects since the start of 2020.
source_title: fDi Intelligence – Your source for foreign direct investment information - fDiIntelligence.com

facts: A Homeland Security official said 475 individuals were detained Thursday during an immigration raid at the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America megasite in Ellabell.
Steven Schrank is the special agent in charge for Homeland Security Investigations, a branch of Immigration and Customs Enforcement, or ICE.
Schrank described the operation as “the largest single-site enforcement operation in the history of Homeland Security Investigations.”
The operation was a multi-month criminal investigation dubbed “Operation Low Voltage” according to a press release from the U.S. Attorney’s Office, Southern District of Georgia.
The investigation concerned collecting evidence related to the unlawful employment of illegal aliens working at both Hyundai and its affiliated contractors.
Authorities received leads from community members and former workers over the past year in support of the investigation.
Schrank stated, “This operation underscores our commitment to protecting jobs for Georgians and Americans, ensuring a level playing field for businesses that comply with the law.”
Hundreds of ICE and Homeland Security agents executed the federal search warrant at HL-GA Battery Company, LLC in Ellabell.
HL-GA Battery Company sits next to Hyundai’s electric-vehicle producing Metaplant.
LG Energy Solutions is the parent company of HL-GA Battery.
LG Energy Solutions is currently partnering with Hyundai in building the adjacent battery plant, which is scheduled to open in 2026.
Mary Beth Kennedy is a senior public relations specialist for HL-GA Battery Company.

HL-GA Battery Company released a statement saying it is cooperating fully with the appropriate authorities regarding activity at its construction site and has paused construction to assist their work.
The majority of the 475 people detained are South Korean nationals.
Most of the detained individuals were taken to the D. Ray James Correctional Facility in Folkston in South Georgia for further processing.

Schrank noted that while some of the detained workers illegally crossed the U.S. border, others entered the country legally but had expired visas or had entered on a visa waiver that prohibited them from working.
Some of those detained worked for the battery manufacturer, while others were employed by contractors and subcontractors at the construction site.
No “substantial” uses of force were used in the process of detainment.
Several people attempted to flee the location, running into a sewage pond located on the premises.
One agent suffered a minor laceration welt during the operation.
The Georgia State Patrol and the Georgia Department of Public Safety each previously confirmed they assisted federal agencies in serving the warrant.
The investigation is ongoing, and no criminal charges have been announced as of Friday.
Operations at Hyundai’s EV manufacturing plant weren’t interrupted by the raid.

Hyundai Motor Company stated that none of those detained is directly employed by Hyundai Motor Company and emphasized its commitment to prioritizing safety and complying with all laws and regulations.
source_title: Officials: 475 people detained during Thursday immigration raid at megasite battery plant construction site - Statesboro Herald

facts: Hyundai Mobis announced plans to add an EV battery plant to its Montgomery campus.
The announcement creates 400 new jobs for Montgomery’s workforce.
The MOBIS module division was founded in 2002.
The MOBIS module division sits on 83 acres.
Hyundai Mobis is a Tier One supplier to Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama and KIA Motor Manufacturing Georgia.
The company holds ISO14001 Certification for Environmental Management.
The company holds IATF16949 Certification for Quality Management.
The new EV battery plant is scheduled to open in 2024.
Construction for the new plant is slated to start in December 2022.
Alabama Governor Kay Ivey stated that Alabama’s auto manufacturing sector is evolving rapidly to capitalize on the EV revolution sweeping the industry.
Governor Kay Ivey noted that the new Hyundai Mobis battery plant represents another milestone in that transition.
The 400 jobs will not be available to fill until 2024.
Other opportunities at Hyundai Mobis are currently listed on the company's job board.
source_title: Hyundai Mobis creates 400+ new jobs in Montgomery through EV battery plant | The Bama Buzz

facts: Governor Kay Ivey joined executives of Hyundai Mobis to announce the company’s plans to invest $205 million to open an EV battery module plant in Montgomery.
The new plant will eventually employ at least 400 people.

Once it reaches full production, the 450,000-square-foot facility will be able to supply over 200,000 EV batteries annually to the Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA) factory in Montgomery and the Kia Georgia plant.

Governor Kay Ivey stated that "Alabama’s auto manufacturing sector is evolving rapidly to capitalize on the EV revolution that is sweeping the industry, and this new Hyundai Mobis battery plant represents another milestone in that transition."
The announcement ceremony took place on Thursday morning in the Old House Chamber at the Alabama State Capitol.

H.S. Oh, vice president of the electric powertrain business for Hyundai Mobis, said the new Alabama EV battery assembly facility will play a critical role in expanding the company’s global production network.
H.S. Oh noted that "as the EV market continues to grow, having a strong production capacity will be key in allowing Hyundai Mobis to see continued growth in the market."
Hyundai Mobis has operated an existing manufacturing facility on 83 acres in Montgomery since 2002.
The existing plant is the largest Tier 1 supplier to HMMA and Kia Georgia, supplying complete cockpit modules, front and rear chassis modules, bumper assemblies, and instrument panels.
Alabama Department of Commerce Secretary Greg Canfield said Hyundai Mobis’ investment project "reflects an acceleration in the development of the EV supply chain in Alabama’s auto industry."
Construction on the new facility is expected to begin as early as December.
The new facility will be located on the HMAA campus just off Interstate 65 in Montgomery.
Production of EV battery assemblies is projected to begin in 2024.
Earlier this year, Hyundai announced plans to begin EV production at its Montgomery manufacturing plant through a $300 million investment that will create 200 jobs.
Hyundai’s Alabama workforce will produce the all-electric Genesis GV70 luxury SUV and a hybrid electric version of the Santa Fe SUV.

Montgomery Mayor Steven Reed stated that "Montgomery and Hyundai Mobis have a powerful partnership" and that this investment is a continuation of the biggest year for economic development in the city's history.

Montgomery County Commission Chairman Doug Singleton said the commitment to the partnership with Hyundai Mobis continues to deepen and they are charting the course for success and opportunity in the River Region.
Montgomery Chamber of Commerce Chairman Cedric Campbell stated that "partnership and teamwork are key to this kind of economic growth" and expressed gratitude for the impact on the entire region.
Seoul, South Korean-based Hyundai Mobis is a longtime supply partner for Hyundai Motor Co. and its Kia and Genesis affiliates.
Besides providing major component systems for the automakers, Hyundai Mobis also offers after-service parts.
source_title: Governor Ivey Announces Hyundai Mobis Plans EV Battery Module Plant in Montgomery, Creating 400 Jobs - EIN Presswire

facts: Hyundai Metaplant is a $7.56 billion electric vehicle factory rising from amid the pine forests of rural Bryan County, Georgia.
More than 1,471 workers have already been hired as the manufacturer pushes to get the first car off the assembly line by December.
Georgia and southeast coastal counties are subsidizing Hyundai and its suppliers with $2.1 billion in tax breaks, construction costs, discount land, and other perks.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced the deal would produce good-paying jobs for hard-working Georgians.
Hyundai Metaplant, the LG joint-venture battery facility, and five affiliated Hyundai suppliers must together invest $5.545 billion and hire at least 8,500 workers by 2031.
The hired jobs must have an average annual yearly salary of $58,105 and be retained until 2048.
Only two of the eight publicly available agreements—Hyundai Metaplant and Hyundai Mobis—promise salaries higher than $58,000.
Six other companies beholden to state development benefits have average annual salaries less than the suggested retail price of the electric SUVs their workers are building.
Two workers practicing car seat assembly make $20 per hour on the factory floor applying fabric and foam to steel frames.
These workers are training to meet production targets for 300,000 electric vehicles a year.
Their annualized pay equals $41,600, which is roughly two-thirds the price of the 2025 Hyundai IONIQ 5 electric SUV with a suggested retail price of $66,100.
The headline salary of $58,105 represents an average across all on-site workers plus benefits.
This pay scale is above average worker salaries in Coastal Georgia counties, which ranged from $30,686 in Bulloch to $46,305 in Effingham according to 2022 U.S. Census Bureau data.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) pays production workers a base salary of $22.40 an hour, or around $46,592 a year.
Maintenance positions at HMGMA start at $30.70 an hour.
The other six companies in the Metaplant economic development agreement set their pay independently of HMGMA despite committing together to the $58,105 average salary.
HMGMA will not gather salary data from on-site affiliates until March 2025 for their first annual report to the state.
HMGMA spokesperson Bianca Johnson stated the company does not coordinate with affiliates on pay nor plans to.
Of the 1,471 new workers reported as of June 30, 859 are employed by HMGMA directly and the remaining 616 work for affiliated companies.
If the seven companies do not produce 8,500 jobs with an average salary of $58,105 by 2031, HMGMA risks losing billions in public aid and could be forced to pay back part of its incentive package.
Bianca Johnson stated confidence that the average pay of all on-site entities will reach or exceed $58,105 by Dec. 31, 2031, as required by the EDA.
More than half a dozen other suppliers have promised to create almost 4,500 additional jobs in separate agreements with the state.
Hyundai Mobis has promised the highest average pay of $61,642 plus benefits a year, or nearly $30 an hour.
Seohan Auto Georgia will build brake systems and drive shafts at its Liberty County plant and will pay workers an average salary of $31,304 a year plus benefits, equivalent to around $15 an hour.
The seat-building team saw a surprise raise from $20 to $22 an hour last month before HR announced it was an error that would be deducted from future paychecks.
The 35-person seat-building team had to arrange repayment plans via a table set up by Hyundai HR in the break room.
Workers use a makeshift parking lot on the 3,000-acre Metaplant construction site where one worker's windshield was damaged and another suffered a punctured tire from industrial nails.
The company does not offer a safe parking zone, pay for damages, or provide a shuttle for workers.
HMGMA spokesperson Johnson stated the Metaplant is an active construction site and they strive to create a safe work environment but cannot prevent all issues.
Many seat-building workers finished paying back the mistaken raise and their paychecks returned to $20 an hour.
One worker said HR verbally promised the team will make more when the manufacturing plant officially opens, but is unsure if he will stay that long.
Seohan Auto Georgia employs 188 workers averaging $31,304 a year plus benefits at [$15/hr].
PHA employs 402 workers averaging $37,440 a year plus benefits at [$18/hr].
Seoyon E-Hwa Savannah employs 500 workers averaging $37,440 a year plus benefits at [$18/hr].
Sewon America employs 740 workers averaging $37,440 a year plus benefits at [$18/hr].
Joon Georgia employs 630 workers averaging $45,000 a year plus benefits at [$21.65/hr].
Ecoplastic Corporation employs 456 workers averaging at least $45,000 a year plus benefits at [$21.65/hr].
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (including affiliate suppliers and the joint-venture battery facility) employs 8,500 workers averaging $58,105 a year plus benefits at [$28/hr].
Hyundai Mobis employs 1,578 workers averaging $61,642 a year plus benefits at [$30/hr].
source_title: As workers train, Hyundai Metaplant salary picture takes shape | Georgia Public Broadcasting

The new plant will eventually employ at least 400 people.

Once it reaches full production, the 450,000-square-foot facility will be able to supply over 200,000 EV batteries annually to Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Alabama (HMMA) in Montgomery and the Kia Georgia plant.

Governor Kay Ivey stated that Alabama’s auto manufacturing sector is evolving rapidly to capitalize on the EV revolution sweeping the industry, and this new battery plant represents another milestone in that transition.
Governor Ivey expressed excitement about the company’s new investment and what it represents for the next chapter of automaking in Alabama.
The formal announcement ceremony took place Thursday morning in the Old House Chamber at the Alabama State Capitol.

H.S. Oh, vice president of the electric powertrain business for Hyundai Mobis, said the new Alabama EV battery assembly facility will play a critical role in expanding the company’s global production network.
H.S. Oh noted that having strong production capacity will be key to allowing Hyundai Mobis to see continued growth as the EV market continues to grow.
H.S. Oh stated that the new Montgomery facility will be an important step in that process and expressed excitement for the project to be under way.
The EV battery module plant expands Hyundai Mobis’ already large presence in Montgomery.
Hyundai Mobis has operated a manufacturing facility on 83 acres in Montgomery since 2002.
This existing facility is the largest Tier 1 supplier to HMMA and Kia Georgia.
The existing plant supplies auto plants with complete cockpit modules, front and rear chassis modules, bumper assemblies, and instrument panels.
Alabama Department of Commerce Secretary Greg Canfield stated that Hyundai Mobis’ investment reflects an acceleration in the development of the EV supply chain in Alabama’s auto industry.

Construction on the new facility is expected to begin as early as December.
The new facility will be located on the HMMA campus just off Interstate 65 in Montgomery.

Earlier this year, Hyundai announced plans to begin EV production at its Montgomery manufacturing plant through a $300 million investment that will create 200 jobs.

Montgomery Mayor Steven Reed stated that Montgomery and Hyundai Mobis have a powerful partnership and expressed hope to continue strengthening it while supporting the investment.
