Mayor Santini stated this helps accomplish the goal of recruiting world-class companies and notes it is another clean energy company coming to the community, illustrating the growth and importance of that market.

This announcement comes only two months after Qcells revealed its plans to invest an historic $2.5 billion into building ingot, wafers, and cells as well as expand solar panel manufacturing in Georgia.
Qcells also recently announced plans to buy polysilicon from REC Silicon, which is using clean energy to power its facility in Moses Lake, Washington.
Soon, Qcells products, from polysilicon to panel, will be sustainably made in America.
This investment comes after years of partnership with leaders in Georgia and following the passage of the Solar Energy Manufacturing for America Act (SEMA) within the Inflation Reduction Act.
The demand for American-made solar panels is increasing rapidly driven by efforts to ensure energy independence, lower energy costs, and create new careers in energy.
Qcells’ domestic manufacturing expansion will fulfill the growing need for these clean energy solutions and strengthen Georgia’s position as a global leader in solar.
source_title: North American Clean Energy - Qcells’ Supplier Expands Supply Chain Investment in Georgia

facts: Qcells announced it has resumed solar panel assembly at its facility in Cartersville, Georgia, following customs clearance delays.
The Cartersville factory plans to advance from solar panel assembly and integrate ingot, wafer, and cell production this year at an annual capacity of 3.3 GW.
Marta Stoepker, head of communications at Qcells, stated the company is proud to be back to work manufacturing American-made energy.
Qcells aims to build a complete American solar supply chain and adapt to operational hurdles as they occur.
The company plans to welcome hundreds of new employees to finalize its factory in Cartersville, Georgia.
By the end of 2026, Qcells expects to have nearly 4,000 people manufacturing panels and components at the Cartersville facility.
In November, Qcells furloughed 1,000 employees from its two Georgia plants, representing about one-third of the company’s workforce.
Qcells laid off 300 people because U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) was increasingly detaining its imported solar cells and other module components.
Qcells confirmed it has brought back every furloughed employee.
Products were stopped at the border under the Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act, which prevents Chinese goods manufactured using forced labor from entering the United States.
Qcells is headquartered in South Korea and claims it uses no Chinese components in its solar modules.
CBP started detaining Qcells products last summer.
The Cartersville plant started operating in April 2025.
The Cartersville facility joins Qcells’ 5.1-GW factory in Dalton, which opened in 2019.
State Rep. Kasey Carpenter (R-Dalton) is hosting a press conference highlighting Qcells investments in Georgia on Tuesday at 2:30 p.m. (EDT) at the State Capitol.
source_title: Qcells resumes solar panel production in Georgia after customs hurdles

facts: Manufacturer Aspen Aerogels Inc. canceled plans to construct a $325-million plant in Statesboro, Ga., as told to investors on Feb. 12.
Donald Young is the CEO of the Northborough, Mass.-based company Aspen Aerogels Inc.
Aspen Aerogels will instead focus on maximizing capacity at its existing manufacturing plant in Rhode Island.
Aspen had planned to produce thermal barriers for electric vehicle batteries at the canceled facility.
The canceled plant was designed to be 500,000 square feet on a 90-acre site in southeast Georgia.
New York-based Turner Construction Co. was selected as the contractor for the project.
CEO Donald Young stated during an investor call that the company is taking decisive actions to navigate an evolving environment.
Aspen will relocate some equipment from the canceled project to upgrade and expand its Rhode Island plant.
The announcement follows automakers reporting slower-than-expected EV demand.
The cancellation coincides with the administration of President Donald Trump pulling back Biden-era policies that supported EVs.

President Donald Trump issued an executive order on the first day of his new term revoking a 2021 Biden order that set a non-binding goal of having EVs account for half of new cars sold in the U.S. by 2030.
A Feb. 6 Federal Highway Administration memo informed state transportation officials that it was suspending a $5-billion program providing funding for EV chargers.
Aspen had previously planned to open the Statesboro plant in 2024.
Executives previously told investors that contracting out for some materials allowed for a later start of plant operations.
Aspen now states it can meet long-term demand by expanding capacity at its East Providence, R.I., plant and relying on external manufacturing as needed.

CFO and treasurer Ricardo Rodriguez stated that in early 2023, the company decided to right-time construction of its planned second aerogel manufacturing facility in Statesboro, Georgia, due to pre-empting a reset in EV demand expectations.
Aspen subsequently ramped up its external manufacturing capacity for its energy industrial business following the early 2023 decision.
Solar and battery storage manufacturer Freyr Battery also canceled its $2.6-billion plant project in Coweta County, Ga., in late January.
source_title: EV Battery Component Maker Cancels $325M Georgia Plant | Engineering News-Record

facts: FREYR Battery, Norway canceled its plan to build a gigafactory in Georgia.
source_title: FREYR Battery, Norway cancels plan to build Georgia gigafactory - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Coweta County is located about 40 miles southwest of Atlanta and is home to approximately 153,000 people.
The county's economy is driven by 23 companies from 15 different countries, 10 of which are headquartered there.
One-third of Coweta’s large manufacturing workforce is employed by these companies.
Manufacturers are drawn to the county for convenient interstate access, proximity to Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport, a trained workforce, and affordable real estate.
Hitachi Construction Machinery Americas expanded its current location into its regional headquarters, adding 92 jobs and $33 million in capital investment.
The Hitachi expansion space spans more than 88,000 square feet.
County Administrator Michael Fouts states the average annual wage for Hitachi workers is $111,000.
Korean-based auto-parts supplier Hyundai Industrial announced in November a $24 million investment to retrofit an existing building in Coweta County.
The Hyundai Industrial investment will create 100 jobs.
Hyundai Industrial specializes in manufacturing car seats, arm rests and head rests for OEMs, including the Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan County.
Norwegian battery developer FREYR Battery is a recent investor to the area.
Coweta County Economic Development Authority President Sarah Jacobs says FREYR is helping put Georgia on the map as part of the “Battery Belt.”
Jacobs notes Coweta’s proximity to Atlanta, labor market, and local education partnerships have positioned the community well for attracting advanced manufacturing investments like FREYR.
FREYR will create more than 720 new jobs over the next six years.
FREYR plans to spend $1.7 billion on the first phase of its “Giga America” factory.
A later FREYR expansion could include solar parks with storage modules and materials processing, bringing the total investment to nearly $2.6 billion.
Jacobs notes FREYR jobs are “lifetime career” jobs that do not require four years of education.
FREYR jobs will pay an average salary of $60,000, which is 127% of the average Coweta County wage.
Cargill Newnan protein processing plant is adding 120 employees to its facility.
Winpak Films is investing $890,000 in 44 acres of property adjacent to its existing facility in Senoia.
University of West Georgia (UWG) President Brendan Kelly says various companies rely on UWG graduates to supply needed talent.
UWG built computing, cybersecurity and applied business analytics programs with input from 20 tech companies throughout the region.
Students who graduate from UWG's industry-built academic programs often earn six-figure salaries.
West Georgia Technical College (WGTC) covers seven counties and has five full-service campuses and several instructional sites.
WGTC President Julie Post says the school offers over 100 programs at $100 a credit hour, with most careers earning far more than $50,000 a year in two years or less.
WGTC is looking to expand its precision manufacturing and maintenance program because Coweta is largely a manufacturing area needing that employee base.
WGTC puts 98% of its graduates to work in businesses that need them, with about 87% placed in the field they studied.
Coweta’s Central Educational Center (CEC) is a publicly funded charter college and career academy combining business engagement with technical college system and local school district expertise.
The CEC program was certified by the General Assembly in 2011, which provided an additional investment of more than $160 million.
CEC CEO Mark Whitlock says students graduate from high school with technical college certificates of credit, showing high-skill, higher-wage career paths.
Some CEC graduates in their early 20s are making close to six figures with no college debt while working in local jobs.
source_title: Newnan | Coweta County: Town and Country - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: T1 Energy announced it has selected Yates Construction as contractor for preconstruction services and site preparation for its $850 million solar cell manufacturing facility in Austin, Texas.
The G2 Austin factory is expected to begin producing solar cells in the second half of 2026.
The G2 Austin factory is planned to reach an annual output of 5 GW of cells per year.
The facility is expected to create up to 1,800 full-time jobs.
T1 Energy said the site will produce advanced TOPCon solar cells.
Commissioners of Milam County, Texas unanimously voted to provide T1 Energy a long-term tax abatement package subject to the company meeting employment and investment thresholds at the facility.

Milam County Judge Bill Whitmire stated: “We’re thrilled to welcome T1 Energy to Milam County—this partnership brings not just innovation, but the kind of high-quality, good-paying jobs that empower our local families and strengthen our community.”
Yates Construction joins SSOE Group, which has been providing project engineering for the project since December 2024.
Solar cell production is a critically underserved leg of the solar manufacturing supply chain in the United States.
Facilities nationwide are actively producing over 56 GW of solar modules per year.
Only 2 GW of solar cell production capacity is operational and 19 GW under construction, according to the Solar Energy Industries Association.
Solar cell production is a more advanced manufacturing process and typically requires more capital investment to launch than module assembly.
T1 Energy launched as a rebrand of Freyr Battery, acquiring solar manufacturing assets from major global supplier Trina Solar for $340 million in late 2024.
In early 2025, Freyr Battery scrapped plans for a $2.6 billion battery energy storage system factory in the state of Georgia, pivoted to solar manufacturing, and rebranded as T1 Energy.
The acquisition from Trina Solar included a 5 GW, 1.35 million square foot automated solar module manufacturing facility in Wilmer, Texas.
In Q1, 2025, T1 Energy reported production of 443 MW of solar modules at the site, called G1 Dallas.
In its Q1, 2025 earnings report, the company reduced its production forecast for solar modules to a range of 2.6 GW to 3.0 GW, down from a previous expectation of 3.4 GW.
The company cited “near term trade policy uncertainties that are obscuring Bill of Materials cost visibility and creating a temporary lull in bidding activity.”
The lowered forecast is also due to its decision to convert production lines from PERC to TOPCon technology and a “potential 800 MW inventory build.”

Daniel Barcelo, chief executive officer of T1 Energy, stated: “We are well positioned to manage this sales environment with 1.7 GW of 2025 contracted module offtake coverage, a robust cash and liquidity position, and the continued production and sales ramp up at G1 Dallas.”
source_title: T1 Energy begins site preparation for 5 GW solar cell factory in Texas - pv magazine USA

facts: Archer Aviation is designing and building the Midnight, an electric takeoff and landing aircraft (eVTOL) with 12 propellers that takes off and lands like a helicopter and flies like a plane.
The company envisions using the Midnight as a rideshare or air taxi.
Tosha Perkins, Archer’s chief people and partnerships officer, stated that one of the design goals was to create something exciting and fun to get into.
Georgia offered an aggressive incentives package that heavily influenced Archer’s decision to build the Midnight east of Atlanta.
The incentive package is worth about one-third of the company’s pledged $118 capital investment.
The capital investment is intended to create 1,000 incentivized jobs.
Georgia’s location was selected over a final contender site in Dallas-Fort Worth.
Georgia already hosts approximately 800 aerospace companies and has a growing electric vehicle sector.

Tosha Perkins cited the talent pool as the number one reason for choosing Georgia, specifically noting the availability of general talent within aerospace, automotive, electric vehicle, and general manufacturing.
The selected site is adjacent to a municipal airport in Covington.
Georgia’s generally benign weather was cited as advantageous for testing the aircraft.
Perkins highlighted that relationships in Georgia and their willingness to assist with training and workforce development were critical factors beyond the financial incentives.
Technical colleges and other organizations in Georgia came together to provide Archer with necessary support outside of the incentive package.
The Archer facility will consist of three buildings spread across approximately 100 acres.
The company’s target move-in date for the facility is the end of the year.
Commercialization of the Midnight aircraft is targeted for 2025.
Archer has partnered with a local chapter of the Future Farmers of America to repurpose trees cleared from the site into furniture for the new facility.
Perkins noted that Georgia went beyond the incentive package by offering connections and support aligned with Archer’s identity as a sustainable company.
Archer Aviation has raised more than $1 billion but has not yet turned a profit.
Tosha Perkins spoke with Site Selection from Santa Clara, which is Archer’s current headquarters.
source_title: Innovative Archer Aviation Has Landed in Georgia – Site Selection Magazine

facts: Kempower and charge point operator PowerUp America have opened a new public DC fast charging station in Manchester, Kentucky.
The opened station is the first of 12 charging projects the companies are building together in the Southeast.
source_title: Georgia | Electrek

facts: Businesses are choosing to invest in Georgia over other states due to the great economic benefits that the Right to Work state provides.
The most recent Right to Work Georgia investments will soon be coming from Archer Aviation, Komar, and FREYR Battery.
Altogether, they will create a total of 2,017 new Georgia jobs.
Archer Aviation is investing $118 million in order to add a new facility in Right to Work Newton County, Georgia.
Archer Aviation's investment will mean 1,000 new jobs.
Archer Aviation utilizes an innovative, state-of-the art eVTOL aircraft design to sustainably tackle mobility issues in urban centers.
Komar Brands is investing $87 million in order to add a new facility in Right to Work Bryan County, Georgia.
Komar Brands will create 294 new jobs.
Komar Brands is partnering with Georgia to establish a new manufacturing and distribution center as part of its strategic vision.
Komar Brands plans to break ground on its facility in the very near future.
Georgia's consistent economic policy and dedication to speed-to-market solutions have made it a dynamic place to grow.
Strong partnerships between local communities and the Georgia Ports Authority make doing business in Georgia easier for companies like Komar Brands.
FREYR Battery is investing $2.57 billion in order to add a new location and create 723 new jobs in Right to Work Coweta County, Georgia.
FREYR Battery is deeply committed to decarbonizing the transportation and energy sectors through its U.S. expansion plan.
FREYR Battery expects to spur job creation and develop localized, decarbonized supply chains in the U.S. to enhance energy security and economic activity.
Metro Atlanta has quickly become a hub for battery cell research and production.
FREYR Battery will benefit from metro Atlanta's vibrant research ecosystem and diverse talent pool to support its next-generation efforts.
source_title: New Right to Work Georgia Companies Create Over 2k Jobs - NRTWC

facts: Unified Legacy plans to invest $125 million in a new manufacturing facility in Macon, Ga.
The facility will expand domestic production capacity for the aerospace, defense and industrial sectors.
The project will create 500 jobs over the next several years.
GE Appliances has added a new production line at its Roper Corporation facility in LaFayette, Georgia.
The addition is part of a multimillion-dollar investment to expand and modernize manufacturing operations at the company’s flagship cooking products plant.
A German drug packaging and medical device manufacturer is investing $88 million to build a new assembly plant in Peachtree City, Ga.
The manufacturer will hire more than 200 new employees.
Investments from Hwashin, Hyundai Industrial Co. and Daechang Seat Corp. USA bring the Peach State an estimated $2.5 billion.
Hyundai Motor Group’s Georgia supplier list has grown longer this month.
Three additional auto parts suppliers have announced plans to establish assembly plants in the state.
Seoyon E-HWA, a global automotive supplier, will invest $76 million to build a new assembly plant in Savannah, Ga.
The plant will make interior and exterior auto parts.
Roper Corp., a Haier company and subsidiary of GE Appliances, will invest $118 million to expand its assembly plant in LaFayette, Ga.
The project is expected to create 600 jobs in Walker County.
source_title: Georgia manufacturing - Page 1 | ASSEMBLY

facts: Archer Aviation Inc., a $3.85 billion market cap company, announced the opening of its new eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
The ARC facility is situated next to the Covington Municipal Airport and spans approximately 400,000 square feet.
The company received the certificate of occupancy for the ARC facility earlier this week and celebrated with a ribbon-cutting ceremony.
Tooling load-in has already begun at the ARC facility.
Initial production of the Midnight aircraft is expected to kick off in early 2025.
Archer aims to achieve a production rate of two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
The company maintains a strong financial position with a current ratio of 6.03, indicating ample liquidity to fund its expansion plans.
Stellantis N.V. has been closely collaborating with Archer to expedite the mass production of the Midnight eVTOL aircraft.
As part of the partnership, Stellantis is set to contribute capital, advanced manufacturing technology, expertise, and experienced personnel.
The collaboration between Archer and Stellantis is focused on scaling production to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp has expressed support for the new facility, highlighting its economic benefits and job creation potential for the state of Georgia.
Congressman Mike Collins has also expressed support for the new facility, highlighting its economic benefits and job creation potential for the state of Georgia.
The completion of the ARC facility represents a shift in the industry from research and development to commercialization.
Archer's Midnight aircraft is designed to provide a sustainable, low-noise, and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested urban areas.
The completion of the ARC facility is a testament to the rapid development of the eVTOL industry and its move towards a more environmentally friendly approach to mobility.
Archer Aviation's stock has surged nearly 200% over the past six months according to InvestingPro.
Canaccord Genuity has raised its price target for Archer Aviation following an announcement of an exclusive partnership with defense contractor Anduril Industries.

The collaboration with Anduril Industries aims to develop an unmanned hybrid vertical takeoff and landing (VTOL) aircraft for defense applications, managed under the newly formed Archer Defense segment.
Archer Aviation has secured a multi-party collaboration agreement with entities in the United Arab Emirates and Abu Dhabi to establish electric air taxi operations in the region.
This partnership makes Archer the first eVTOL aircraft manufacturer in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.
The company reported a strong liquidity position with over $500 million in cash reserves during its third quarter 2024 earnings call.
Needham initiated coverage on Archer Aviation with a Buy rating, citing the industry's momentum and Archer's capacity to fulfill its substantial aircraft order book.
source_title: Archer opens new eVTOL aircraft facility in Georgia By Investing.com

facts: Korean auto parts maker Duckyang is investing $10 million to open its first U.S. manufacturing facility in Jackson County.
The 230,000-square-foot facility will create 285 jobs in Braselton.
In 2018, Facebook announced it was opening a massive data center in Newton County with an initial investment of $750 million, requesting to be powered 100% by renewable energy.
Georgia continues to be recognized for its business-friendly environment, with announcements about new manufacturing operations locating or expanding there happening daily.
The state recently moved to address whether it can provide the advanced workforce manufacturing now requires.
In a year full of twists, Georgia’s Northeast counties held steady with the automotive, distribution and manufacturing sectors keeping employment up.
More than 2,575 jobs were created through $2.03 billion in investments in the region.
source_title: Jackson County Archives - Page 2 of 2 - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Cabot Properties has acquired Braselton 85, a 223,000-square-foot, Class A industrial property in Braselton, Ga., from Rockefeller Group.
The newly built facility came online this year and is fully leased to a South Korean automotive manufacturer, Duckyang America Inc.
The tenant will be using the industrial space as a warehouse and for light manufacturing.
According to Jackson County data, the developer purchased the parcel located at 984 Broadway Ave. in 2019 for $1.9 million.
The speculative distribution center sits on a 21-acre site and began to take shape last fall, with Pattillo Construction as general contractor, and was completed in March.
The building offers 47 dock doors and two drive-in ones, as well as 32 feet clear heights.
The facility is outfitted with an ESFR sprinkler system, it has 26 trailer drops and 134 auto parking spots.
The property is roughly 50 miles northeast of downtown Atlanta, with Interstate 85 less than 1.5 miles away.
A Cushman & Wakefield Capital Markets Group team lead by Vice Chairman Stewart Calhoun and Senior Director Casey Masters represented the seller.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce division, represented by Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim, the Jackson County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Quick Start and Georgia EMC worked together to bring the South Korean automotive manufacturer to Braselton.
Duckyang moved in in March.
Duckyang plans to invest $10 million in its first U.S. manufacturing facility, Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced in May.
The facility is expected to create 285 jobs in production, maintenance, logistics and warehouse management.
The manufacturer will supply SK Battery America with automotive battery modules and energy storage systems.
Founded in 1977, Duckyang is based in Ulsan, South Korea.
The manufacturer supplies cockpit modules to clients such as Hyundai, Kia and Renault Samsung Motors.

The opportunity to work with the former two companies, that already have a U.S. presence, was an incentive to bring Duckyang to Georgia, according to a prepared statement from Dong-in Son, CEO of Duckyang.
source_title: Rockefeller Group Sells Atlanta-Area Asset - Commercial Property Executive

facts: Rockefeller Group sold a 222,618-square-foot speculative distribution center in Braselton, Ga. to Cabot Properties.
The building is fully leased to Duckyang America Inc., a Korean automotive equipment supplier who is using the building for warehousing and light manufacturing.
The facility is located on Broadway Avenue along the I-85 North corridor in the North Atlanta submarket.
Braselton 85 broke ground in fall 2020 and was completed in March.
Duckyang America Inc. took occupancy of the facility in March.

The newly built modern state-of-the-art facility includes an ESFR sprinkler system, 26 on-site trailer drops, 134 auto parking spaces, 47 dock doors with two drive-in doors, 32′ clear height, 185′ truck court with 60′ concrete apron and LED lighting.
Duckyang America Inc. manufactures and distributes cockpit modules, instrument panels and door trims for the automotive industry.
Michael E. McCarthy of Cabot Properties described Duckyang America Inc. as one of the top suppliers to the electric vehicle industry.

Steven McGee, Director, Southeast Development for Rockefeller Group, stated that demand for new Class-A industrial space in Atlanta remains strong heading into the second half of 2021 and that Atlanta and its suburbs are a key distribution hub for both national and international companies.
Steven McGee noted that Braselton 85’s location along I-85, where a number of major manufacturers have recently begun operations, made it ideal for Duckyang and investors like Cabot.
Steven McGee said interest in the building was significant since Rockefeller Group purchased the site in 2019.
Stewart Calhoun and Casey Masters of Cushman & Wakefield represented the seller, Rockefeller Group, in the transaction.
Atlanta-based Pattillo Construction served as the general contractor on the project.
source_title: Rockefeller Group Sells 222,618-SF Atlanta Distribution Center to Cabot Properties - Rockefeller Group

facts: Rockefeller Group recently completed two distribution buildings totaling 427,655 square feet on 39 acres in Braselton, Ga.
Braselton 85- Phase II is located on Jesse Cronic Road and Highway 124 along the I-85 North corridor in the North Atlanta submarket.
Braselton 85- Phase II broke ground in 2023.
Phase II includes two rear-load distribution buildings totaling 207,009 and 220,646 square feet respectively.
Both Phase II buildings have 32’ clear ceiling heights and 52’ x 50’ column spacing.
The Phase II buildings include ample auto and trailer parking.
Braselton’s location along I-85 provides potential tenants with a distribution channel from Atlanta to Charlotte, bypassing metro-Atlanta traffic.
Rockefeller Group recently sold the smaller of the two Phase II buildings.
The 220,646 square foot distribution center is available for sale or lease.
The business park is Rockefeller Group’s second development in Braselton.
Rockefeller Group completed and sold Braselton 85 in 2021, a 222,618-square-foot distribution center located one mile from the new project.
Braselton 85 was leased to Duckyang America Inc., a Korean automotive equipment supplier.
Braselton 85 was sold to Cabot Properties.
source_title: The REview

facts: Duckshin Housing, a construction materials manufacturer headquartered in Seoul, Korea, plans its first U.S. facility in Athens-Clarke County.
The company will invest more than $15 million in the new facility.
The project will create 100 new jobs.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the investment and job creation on August 3, 2023.
Duckshin Housing produces architectural deck plates and frames made of galvanized steel and rebars.
The company's deck plates are designed for efficient production, cost-effectiveness, and enhanced safety.
The new facility will be located on Olympic Drive at the Athena Industrial Park in Athens.
Construction is expected to begin later in 2023.
Upon opening, Duckshin will hire for welder, machine operator, forklift driver, and other operations positions.
Job postings will be published on the company's website when available.
Duckshin plans to work with local employment services for hiring.
Chairman Myung Hwan Kim stated that locating in Athens allows the company to be closer to growing construction projects in Georgia and across the U.S.
Mayor Kelly Girtz of the Athens-Clarke County Unified Government welcomed Duckshin Housing, noting its products enhance construction safety and efficiency for Southeast communities.
Yoonie Kim, Director of Korean Investment, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s Global Commerce team on the project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Athens-Clarke County Unified Government and Georgia EMC.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that locating construction material companies in the state can reduce construction costs for future facilities.
Advanced manufacturing is one of Georgia’s leading industries, having announced more than 7,700 new jobs through economic development projects in the last six months of 2022.
Since 2019, Athens-Clarke County has announced more than 3,400 new jobs and $903 million in investments.

links.context: Press Secretary for Governor Brian P. Kemp
Deputy Press Secretary for Governor Brian P. Kemp
Communications Manager for Georgia Department of Economic Development

source_title: Duckshin Housing Plans First US Facility in Athens-Clarke County | Georgia

facts: EnergyTek Corporation is planning to invest at least $275 million in establishing new manufacturing operations in Moultrie, Colquitt County.
Following a multi-year ramp up, the company expects to create a total of 1,300 jobs at full operations.
EnergyTek plans to manufacture utility scale and commercial solar panels, inverters, EV charging systems, micro grids, and other renewable energy components in Moultrie.
The new manufacturing operations will be located in Citizen Business Park, a “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) site, in Moultrie.
Construction on the first phase is expected to begin in the fourth quarter of 2025.
The company will be hiring for management, engineering, production, and administrative positions over the next few years.

EnergyTek Corporation is focused on bringing low cost of electricity infrastructure to commercial and utility scale customers by vertically integrating the value chain between power generation, management, and distribution.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia’s commitment to innovation continues to attract companies like EnergyTek that are investing and creating good jobs in every corner of the state.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson congratulated partners in Colquitt County on this important milestone for the Citizen Business Park.

Dr. Cristian Toma, CEO of EnergyTek, said the multi-phase investment will allow the company to manufacture multiple aspects of the energy and electrification supply chain, simplifying vertical integration.

Dr. Cristian Toma also noted that Georgia’s industrial ecosystem and world-class logistics will support efficient distribution and supply chain integration as EnergyTek scales up production in Moultrie over the next few years.
Chairman Ross Dekle of the Moultrie-Colquitt County Development Authority stated that EnergyTek has chosen Moultrie and Colquitt County as the site of its new manufacturing facility.

Chairman Ross Dekle added that the significant investment will create high-quality, well-compensated manufacturing jobs for residents and represents a meaningful contribution to the continued growth and prosperity of the community.

Assistant Director of Existing Industry and Regional Recruitment Tina Herring represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project in partnership with the Moultrie-Colquitt County Development Authority, City of Moultrie, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
source_title: EnergyTek Corporation Selects Colquitt County | Georgia

facts: Deputy Secretary Wally Adeyemo issued a memorandum dated August 16, 2023, to interested parties regarding the Inflation Reduction Act’s first year.
Congress passed and the President signed the Inflation Reduction Act one year prior to the memo's date.
The law is described as the single most significant legislation to combat climate change in American history and one of the largest investments into the American economy in a generation.
Over the past year, companies have announced nearly 200 new projects totaling over $110 billion of investment in building America’s clean energy economy.

Treasury analysis indicates that announced investments in clean energy, electric vehicles, and batteries are concentrated in communities with lower wages, lower college graduation rates, and lower employment rates.
China has historically spent over $400 billion per year on industrial policy, largely through direct subsidies to firms and below-market credit to state-owned enterprises.
The Inflation Reduction Act uses modest incentives to promote private investment and will unlock $3 trillion of investment in America for renewable energy technology.
Progress is measured across three key metrics: creating good-paying jobs (especially in disadvantaged communities), strengthening energy security/resilience, and tackling climate change.
A central tenet of the law is spurring job creation across the United States, aligning with Secretary Yellen’s modern supply-side economics framework.

Over the past twelve months, investments have started translating to American jobs, detailed in a 70-page list covering tens of thousands of jobs from Colorado Springs to Minneapolis and Albuquerque to Toledo.
The annual economic opportunity for workers in the wind and solar industries will grow by $220 billion per year in wages by 2035.
Clean energy investments could support over 1.5 million clean energy jobs in the United States by 2030.
Events of the past year, including climate emergencies, supply chain snarls, and Russia’s weaponization of exports, demonstrated the acute need for energy security investment.
The Inflation Reduction Act accelerates development and deployment of clean energy, including key supply chains, to build a resilient energy sector.
Estimates suggest the law can bring down costs of certain emerging technologies by as much as 25 percent and position America as the global leader in these areas.
Analysts expect twice as much wind, solar, and battery storage deployment over the next seven years compared to the base case without the law.
A typical American household could save around $200 per year on electric bills due to the law's impact.
Households taking advantage of electric vehicle incentives can save thousands per year on energy and fuel costs, with EV drivers seeing fuel costs drop by about 60 percent.
The President’s 2030 emissions reduction goal is within reach, with independent studies suggesting the law will drive significant emissions reductions.
Lower costs of emerging climate technologies will lead to global deployment, eventually reducing hundreds of millions of tons of emissions around the world each year.
Increased clean energy deployment and reduced particle pollution will avoid thousands of premature deaths and asthma attacks.
The incentives are part of a broader whole-of-government approach that includes the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law and electric grid infrastructure upgrades.
Federal policies run in tandem with state, local, and private-sector action and market forces.
In its first year, the Treasury Department focused on achieving three goals: creating good-paying jobs, strengthening energy security, and tackling the climate crisis.
Treasury efforts spanned the Office of Tax Policy, Climate Hub, Inflation Reduction Act Program Office, and the Office of the Deputy Assistant Secretary for Management and Budget.
Treasury is working closely with the IRS to modernize customer service and technology to ensure clean energy tax incentives are delivered accurately and seamlessly.

Treasury will complete the first phase of implementation in the coming weeks, having provided information or proposed guidance for Investment and Production Tax Credits’ cross-cutting bonus provisions and new credit monetization provisions.
Plans for phase two of implementation will be announced in September.
On the day of the law’s enactment, Treasury issued guidance on the electric vehicle tax credit and worked to ensure consumers could easily find a list of eligible vehicles online.
Since enactment, Treasury and the IRS have issued 38 pieces of guidance on clean energy provisions while engaging with hundreds of stakeholders and reviewing nearly 5,000 comments.
On August 10, 2023, Maxeon Solar Technologies Ltd. announced plans to spend $1.2 billion to build the biggest US factory for polysilicon solar panels in Albuquerque, New Mexico.
Construction on the Maxeon plant is set to start in the first quarter of 2024 with production expected to begin in 2025.
The Maxeon facility will produce up to 3 gigawatts of solar cells and panels a year, doubling the company’s manufacturing capacity.
One gigawatt of solar capacity is enough to power about 173,000 American homes.

On August 7, 2023, solar parts manufacturer Unimacts announced it would triple operations in Las Vegas, Nevada, having hired 80 people and opened a facility making metal torque tubes at the start of 2023 thanks to the IRA.
CEO Matt Arnold stated that the metal tubes allow solar panels to rotate during the day, boosting efficiency by about 10 percent.

Unimats can make enough torque tubes in a year at its Las Vegas facility to support two gigawatts worth of solar panels and is looking to expand capacity to support six gigawatts while employing over 200 people.

On August 2, 2023, Governor Bill Lee and state economic development leaders announced that Hanon Systems will invest nearly $170 million to establish a new manufacturing presence in Huntingdon Park, Loudon County, Tennessee, creating 600 new jobs.
Hanon Systems is a leading supplier of thermal and energy management solutions for the automotive industry, employs more than 22,000 people across 21 countries, and is headquartered in South Korea.

On August 2, 2023, XCharge North America announced a 3,500 square foot manufacturing facility in Kyle, Texas, aimed at R&D, distribution, and services for central EV charging solutions, with plans to expand to approximately 20,000 square feet.

The XCharge facility aims to enhance local EV accessibility, as only 20 public-access Level-2 and DC Fast charging stations currently serve a population exceeding 68,000 within San Marcos city limits and surrounding counties.

On July 24, 2023, Switzerland-based Meyer Burger announced plans to open a high-performance solar cell manufacturing facility in Colorado Springs in 2024, creating more than 350 jobs with an average annual wage of nearly $78,000.

Production at the Meyer Burger plant is planned to start during the second half of 2024 and will be its first Western Hemisphere site to manufacture chips and wafers for cutting-edge solar technology.

On July 24, 2023, Canadian solar panel maker Heliene announced plans to spend about $145 million on a new facility in the Minneapolis-St. Paul area with an annual capacity of about one gigawatt of modules and 1.5 gigawatts of cells.

On July 24, 2023, Stellantis and Samsung SDI announced a signed memorandum of understanding to build a second EV battery plant in the United States under their existing StarPlus Energy joint venture, expected to begin production in 2027 with an initial annual capacity of 34 gigawatt hours.

On June 26, 2023, Tennessee Governor Bill Lee and state officials announced that Enchem America Inc. will invest $152.5 million to establish electrolyte manufacturing operations in Brownsville, Tennessee, creating 190 new jobs in Haywood County.

The Enchem facility will be located at the I-40 Advantage Industrial Park, operate similarly to its U.S. headquarters in Commerce, Georgia, and manufacture electrolytes for EV batteries and other EV battery-related services.

On June 23, 2023, automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea announced plans to establish a new facility in Locust Grove, Georgia, creating over 160 new jobs and bringing $72 million in investment to Henry County.
The new NVH Korea facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles in the United States for manufacturers such as Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.

On June 22, 2023, the Colorado Office of Economic Development and International Trade announced that VSK Energy Inc. plans to invest $250 million to develop a solar photovoltaic module manufacturing facility in Brighton, Colorado, bringing as many as 900 new jobs.
VSK Energy Inc. is a U.S.-based joint venture between several international solar manufacturers and plans to move into the 76 Commerce Center along Interstate 76 in 2024.
