canonical_name: Alternative Fuels Data Center Hydrogen Fueling Stations
facts: The increased number of retail hydrogen fueling locations in select markets is supporting the initial rollout of fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEVs).
Manufacturers including Hyundai and Toyota are currently offering production FCEVs for sale or lease to customers in markets where hydrogen fuel is available, primarily in California.

With careful planning, the focus has been to add hydrogen fuel at existing gasoline stations covering regions in northern California near San Francisco and southern California near Los Angeles and San Diego, with additional connector and destination stations.
Mobile hydrogen fuelers, where liquefied or compressed hydrogen and dispensing equipment is stored onboard a trailer, are being developed to support the expansion of hydrogen infrastructure.
Efforts are underway to expand hydrogen fueling locations in Hawaii and across the East Coast, with other markets expected to develop as consumer demand increases.
In 2024, there were 54 retail stations available nationwide, mostly concentrated in California.
Public retail stations are available for all light-duty FCEVs, accept payment at the point of sale, and do not require special training to access.
Private stations support fleets and some are primarily used for demonstration and/or research.
As the demand for FCEVs grows, the business case for building more stations with higher capacities improves and the volume in production of station components will bring station costs down.
The rollout of heavy-duty hydrogen trucks, such as line-haul trucks, will necessitate very large stations compared to light-duty needs.

The increase in production and distribution of hydrogen for heavy-duty stations could improve efficiency and utilization of expensive capital equipment, leading to lower fuel costs per kilogram, benefiting both heavy- and light-duty customers.
Hydrogen infrastructure is also developing for buses, medium-duty fleets, and material handling equipment.

Unlike public consumer stations that need multiple locations to cover wherever the consumer may travel, private fleet fueling stations require fewer locations or just a central location to meet specific fleet needs.
The availability of stations providing reasonably priced hydrogen in places where vehicles will be deployed remains a key challenge to the adoption of fuel cell vehicle technology.
To address this challenge, the U.S. Department of Transportation Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) established a National Alternative Fuels Corridors grant program in 2022.

In consultation with the U.S. Department of Energy, FHWA awards designation status to selected corridors to meet the national goal of achieving strategic deployment of hydrogen fueling infrastructure in the designated hydrogen corridors.
The Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act includes $7 billion in funding for seven Regional Hydrogen Hubs (H2Hubs) to accelerate the commercial-scale deployment of low-cost hydrogen.
Additional funds from recipients will leverage nearly $50 billion to grow a hydrogen production, storage, distribution, and end-use network across the country.
These H2Hubs will include projects focused on deployment of infrastructure to enable hydrogen vehicle adoption for fleets and the public.

Building hydrogen fueling infrastructure can be expensive, which is why the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 incentivizes energy-efficient projects by providing a tax credit, 45V, of up to $3 per kilogram of hydrogen.
This comprehensive set of rules for taxpayers to claim the 45V credit provides certainty to current and future hydrogen producers to stabilize and expand the market for their products.
As of 2024, there are 54 open retail hydrogen stations in the United States.
Additionally, there are over 20 stations in various stages of planning or construction.
Most of the existing and planned stations are in California and one in Hawaii.
As the market expands, hydrogen fueling stations will be matched with vehicle rollout as both grow together.
Customers are expected to have similar experiences at hydrogen fueling stations as at gasoline stations, with most hydrogen dispensers being added at existing gasoline stations.
The Alternative Fueling Station Locator allows users to search for public and private hydrogen fueling stations.
Many of the hydrogen safety codes and standards today are based on practices from the chemical and aerospace industries.
DOE is coordinating the efforts of codes and standards organizations to develop more robust codes and standards that ensure the safe use of hydrogen for transportation and stationary applications.
One of the outputs of this effort is NFPA 2, a harmonized national standard for hydrogen vehicle infrastructure.

DOE is developing and testing complete system solutions that validate integrated hydrogen and fuel cell technologies for transportation, infrastructure, and electric generation in a systems context under real-world operating conditions.
The National Laboratory of the Rockies also has information about hydrogen and fuel cell technology validation.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Hydrogen Fueling Stations

entity_name: High-Performance Computing for Energy Innovation (HPC4EI) Program
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation (CMEI) announced a new solicitation for projects that improve America’s manufacturing competitiveness through the use of powerful supercomputers.
DOE will award up to $400,000 per project.
In total, DOE will make over $10 million available for potential selections.
Eligible applicants include U.S.-based small and medium-sized manufacturers (SMMs), universities, institutes, and non-profit organizations.
Selected teams will work with staff from one or more DOE National Laboratories to advance material designs and solve manufacturing challenges.
The program is called the High-Performance Computing for Manufacturing (HPC4Mfg) program, which is part of the High-Performance Computing for Energy Innovation (HPC4EI) initiative.
HPC4EI is funded by both DOE’s Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO) and Industrial Technologies Office (ITO).
The HPC4EI initiative is managed by Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory.

Projects funded through this solicitation will accelerate the development of new materials and improve manufacturing and industrial processes through artificial intelligence, machine learning, and digital simulation.

Topic Area 1 proposals should demonstrate credible pathways to advancing American manufacturing competitiveness, improving supply chain security, enhancing energy system reliability, and creating high-quality domestic jobs.
Areas of interest for Topic Area 1 include the development of advanced materials to improve the performance, durability, and cost-effectiveness of energy systems and technologies.
Areas of interest for Topic Area 1 also include manufacturing processes to strengthen U.S. industrial competitiveness and energy security.

Topic Area 2 proposals should demonstrate potential to strengthen global competitiveness of the U.S. industrial sector, reduce the energy intensity of industrial processes, improve the reliability of energy infrastructure, and onshore industrial excellence.

Areas of interest for Topic Area 2 include the development, optimization, scale-up, and integration of technologies that significantly improve productivity optimization in energy-intensive industries to bolster manufacturing competitiveness.
Areas of interest for Topic Area 2 also include equipment and processes that improve industrial process heating, including reducing cost, improving efficiency, or enhancing product quality.
The solicitation encourages applicants to partner with a range of universities, community colleges, and non-profit organizations.
The funding opportunity was released on April 28, 2026.
Concept papers are due by May 27, 2026, at 5:00 p.m. PT.
Full applications will open in July 2026 and are due in August 2026.
Selection notifications are expected in October 2026.

links.context: Link to apply for the funding opportunity

source_title: Funding Notice: Over $10 Million Available for High-Performance Computing Innovation Program | Department of Energy

facts: Kia Georgia's West Point assembly plant is undergoing a transformation that will allow for the assembly of the all-electric 2024 EV9 three-row SUV in the second quarter of 2024.
The expansion costs over $200 million.
The project will create nearly 200 additional jobs in Troup County.
The EV9 will join the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs and the K5 mid-size sedan as the fifth model assembled at Kia Georgia.
Sean Yoon, president & CEO of Kia North America and Kia America, stated that the EV9 has the potential to be another 'game changer' for Kia.
The EV9 will be the most sophisticated vehicle Kia has ever built and will be a standout in the EV market.
The EV9 will be assembled in West Point, Georgia.
The EV9 will be the first EV for Kia to be assembled in North America.
The EV9 combines all the best SUV elements of the award-winning Telluride with the best EV elements of the award-winning EV6.
Kia America is headquartered in Irvine, California.
Kia serves as the 'Official Automotive Partner' of the NBA.
Kia sells a range of gasoline, hybrid, plug-in hybrid and electrified vehicles through a network of over 750 dealers in the U.S.
The K5, Sportage, Sorento, and Telluride (excludes HEV and PHEV models) are assembled in the United States from U.S. and globally sourced parts.
The EV9 is expected to be assembled in the United States from U.S. and globally sourced parts.
Kia Georgia, Inc. is the first manufacturing site in North America for Kia Corporation based in Seoul, Korea.
Kia Georgia has an annual production capacity of 340,000 units.
Kia Georgia is located on 2,200 acres in West Point, Ga.
Kia Georgia began mass production on Nov. 16, 2009.
source_title: Kia Georgia Plans Expansion to Accommodate EV9 Production

facts: Superior Essex Inc. maintains its principal executive offices at 150 Interstate North Parkway, Atlanta, Georgia 30339.
The company manufactures and supplies wire and cable products for the automotive end-market, alongside communications, energy, industrial, and commercial/residential sectors.
Product lines include magnet wire, fabricated insulation products, and copper and fiber optic communications wire and cable products.
Superior Essex distributes magnet wire, insulation, and related products to smaller original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) and motor repair facilities.
The company converts copper cathode to copper rod for internal consumption and for sale to other wire and cable manufacturers and OEMs.
Manufacturing operations are located in the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Portugal, Italy, Mexico, and China.

On January 23, 2008, the company announced a consolidation and restructuring of its North American magnet wire and distribution and copper rod segments’ manufacturing facilities to align production with industry demand and customer requirements.

The restructuring plan involves a phased closure of the magnet wire manufacturing and copper rod continuous casting facilities in Vincennes, Indiana, and the relocation of existing production to other North American facilities.
The Board of Directors authorized the restructuring on January 16, 2008, communicated it to employees on January 23, 2008, and expects closures to be completed by the first quarter of 2009.

Total estimated restructuring charges are $22 million, consisting of approximately $15 million in non-cash accelerated depreciation, $3 million in cash for employee severance and retention, and $4 million for equipment relocation and facility closure costs.

For the three months ended March 31, 2008, restructuring charges totaled $9.4 million, with $8.0 million attributed to the North American magnet wire and distribution segment and $1.4 million to the copper rod segment.
Charges for the period include $7.429 million in accelerated depreciation, $1.335 million in employee severance, $237,000 in employee retention costs, and $430,000 in equipment relocation costs.
Employee retention incentives are structured to reward Vincennes employees who remain employed until specified termination dates while meeting defined safety and attendance goals.

In March 2008, Essex Europe began discussions with French employee representative bodies regarding the potential closure of its magnet wire manufacturing facility in Chauny, France, which is leased from Nexans through October 2009 and currently employs approximately 130 people.

entity_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office
canonical_name: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office (AMMTO)
facts: AMMTO supports a globally dominant U.S. manufacturing and industrial base for a resilient energy system and secure supply chain.
AMMTO's mission is to drive and inspire innovation that transforms materials, manufacturing, and workforce and advances America’s energy economy.

AMMTO provides analysis, planning, management, and direction necessary for a balanced program of research, development, demonstration, technical assistance, and advanced manufacturing workforce development.
AMMTO operates as a suboffice within the U.S. Department of Energy's Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation.
On December 17, 2025, DOE announced strategic investments to advance material supply chains and product lifecycles in support of a robust manufacturing sector and secure supply chain.
On November 19, 2025, DOE pursued novel manufacturing processes and digital systems that enhance materials and energy efficiency to deliver benefits throughout the economy.
On April 9, 2025, AMMTO advanced workforce education and entrepreneurial ecosystems for key energy system-enabling technologies, such as semiconductors and batteries.
The office is located at Mail Stop EE-1, Department of Energy, Washington, DC 20585, under the Office of the Assistant Secretary for Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy.
source_title: Advanced Materials and Manufacturing Technologies Office | Department of Energy

facts: TI Fluid Systems officially opened its all-new e-Mobility Innovation Center (eMIC) in Auburn Hills, Michigan on June 26, 2024.
The Auburn Hills eMIC is designed to nurture customer collaboration for developing thermal management systems for hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and battery electric vehicles.
The new facility spans 48,000 square feet.
The facility was constructed around TI Fluid Systems' Core Competency model, combining virtual engineering, designing, processing, prototyping, product testing, and vehicle testing under one roof.
This integrated approach reduces development time for prototype thermal management components and systems from six months to as little as two weeks.
The new methods can achieve system-weight reductions of up to 60 percent.
The new methods can achieve overall cost savings of up to 30 percent.
The facility features equipment enabling rapid prototyping of fully functioning components and systems using 3D-printing and injection molding.
The facility includes two vehicle lifts and 42 electric vehicle charging stations.
Hans Dieltjens serves as the president and CEO of TI Fluid Systems.

Hans Dieltjens stated that the Auburn Hills eMIC represents a significant step forward in automotive thermal management development in North America, enabling faster development, collaboration, and innovation crucial for hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and battery electric vehicles.
The Auburn Hills facility is one of five eMICs opened worldwide since 2022.
The other four eMICs are located in key regions for automotive and e-Mobility development: China, Germany, Japan, South Korea, and the USA.

Hans Dieltjens noted that constructing five eMICs in key e-Mobility development regions underscores TI Fluid Systems' commitment to supporting customer competitiveness in a fast-changing automotive landscape.
The construction of the eMICs highlights how an innovative approach to thermal management impacts the efficiency, performance, and cost of electric vehicles.
TI Fluid Systems is listed on the London Stock Exchange under the ticker TIFS.
TI Fluid Systems has its corporate headquarters in Auburn Hills, Michigan.
TI Fluid Systems employs 8,600 people globally.
TI Fluid Systems operates 21 manufacturing locations in the Americas, including 12 locations in the United States.

Alongside thermal management solutions for hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and battery electric vehicles, TI Fluid Systems is a leading global provider of fuel systems, lines and connectors, and thermal management solutions for internal combustion engine vehicles.
TI Fluid Systems serves all major automotive manufacturers with more than 100 years of automotive supply experience.
TI Fluid Systems operates across 27 countries worldwide.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems' New Michigan e-Mobility Innovation Center to Fast-track North American EV Development

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation (CMEI)
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation (CMEI) is advancing America's critical minerals supply chains and accelerating next-generation energy technologies to strengthen national energy security and power the future.
New funding opportunities for the EV and battery supply chain are announced regularly by CMEI.

DE-FOA-0003514 is a Notice of Funding Opportunity titled "Fiscal Year 2025 Transportation Technologies Office (TTO) Program Wide Funding Opportunity Announcement" with a concept paper deadline of 4/1/2025 and a full application deadline of 6/18/2025.

DE-FOA-0003383 is a Funding Opportunity Announcement titled "Fiscal Year 2024 Vehicle Technologies Office Batteries Funding Opportunity Announcement" with a concept paper deadline of 9/9/2024 and a full application deadline of 10/30/2024.

DOE-FOA-0003434 is a Notice of Intent to Issue Funding Opportunity Announcement DE-FOA-3434 titled "Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act Electric Drive Vehicle Battery Improving Economics, Recovery and Reuse, and State of Health" with no concept paper or full application deadlines listed.
Official notices of solicitations can be found at the CMEI Program Information Center or Grants.gov.
The published list of funding opportunities is for information only, may not be inclusive of all solicitations, and has funding amounts and schedule dates subject to change.

The Small Business Innovations Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs are federal research and development initiatives specifically targeting small businesses, allowing awardees to retain technology rights and encouraging commercialization.

TTO supports SBIR/STTR through broader DOE efforts to increase commercialization of DOE-supported R&D, stimulate private sector innovation, and improve the return on investment from federally funded research.
State, local, and federal incentives, including tax credits for alternative fuel vehicles and infrastructure, are listed on the Alternative Fuels Data Center.

The DOE Transportation Technologies Office (TTO) provides a recorded Information Session Series explaining the NOFO process and application components to help potential applicants understand requirements and submission procedures.

As of October 2024, the funding opportunity process changed from Funding Opportunity Announcement (FOA) to Notice of Funding Opportunity (NOFO) format, making some older references in the session series out of date but still relevant for understanding VTO funding requirements.

Session 1 of the TTO Information Session Series covers basics of a VTO NOFO, including concept paper and full application phases, key content to review, specific language to pay attention to, and required components.

Session 2 covers preparing a NOFO budget justification and other application components, including SF 424, allowable project costs, budget justification, cost share, project team information, letters of commitment, and Statement of Project Objectives (SOPO).

Session 3 provides tips for strong NOFO applications from a reviewer’s perspective, identifying strengths and weaknesses based on TTO’s Technology Integration Program’s technical review criteria, and includes examples for developing an application budget justification.
source_title: Funding Opportunities | Department of Energy

entity_name: Tennessee State Government EV Policies and Incentives
canonical_name: Tennessee Electricity Laws and Incentives Program
facts: The Tennessee Department of General Services (DGS) must ensure at least 25% of newly purchased passenger motor vehicles procured for use in ozone nonattainment areas are all-electric, hybrid electric, natural gas, or propane powered vehicles if available.
If EVs, HEVs, NGVs, or propane vehicles are unavailable in ozone nonattainment areas, conventional gasoline vehicles achieving at least 25 mpg may satisfy the 25% requirement.

In areas not designated as ozone nonattainment areas, at least 25% of newly purchased passenger motor vehicles must be EVs, HEVs, NGVs, propane powered vehicles, or conventional gasoline vehicles achieving at least 25 mpg if available at competitive prices.
For non-passenger vehicles, Tennessee state fleets must make a reasonable effort to purchase at least 5% as natural gas or propane vehicles.
State fleets must make every effort to ensure 100% of newly purchased motor vehicles are energy-efficient vehicles.

Energy-efficient vehicles are defined as passenger vehicles using alternative fuels per the Energy Policy Act of 1992, HEVs, conventional gasoline vehicles achieving at least 25 mpg, or ultra-low sulfur diesel vehicles achieving at least 30 mpg.

DGS must inventory the state’s passenger vehicle fleet and prepare annual progress reports detailing energy-efficient state fleet vehicle purchases by category and total counts by efficiency category.
EV owners in Tennessee must pay an annual fee in addition to standard registration fees, increasing on a set schedule.
The annual EV fee is $200 from 2024 to 2026.
The annual EV fee increases to $274 in 2027.
The annual EV fee remains at $274 for 2028 and later, adjusted for annual inflation.
HEV and PHEV owners are subject to an annual fee of $100 from 2024 to 2028.
The annual HEV and PHEV fee remains at $100 for 2028 and later, adjusted for annual inflation.
Low-speed and medium-speed vehicles are exempt from the EV/HEV/PHEV annual fees.
EV chargers providing electricity exclusively for EVs are not considered public utilities within the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) service area.
EV chargers located outside of TVA’s territory may be subject to different regulations.
NGVs and EVs may exceed Tennessee’s gross and axle vehicle weight limits by up to 2,000 pounds.
The maximum gross vehicle weight for NGVs and EVs cannot exceed 82,000 lbs under the weight exemption.
The vehicle weight exemption applies on all interstate highways.

The U.S. Department of Transportation’s NEVI Formula Program required the Tennessee Department of Transportation (TDOT) to submit an annually updated EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan for fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
TDOT's NEVI plans must describe how the state intends to distribute NEVI funds and outline physical and cybersecurity strategies.
TDOT's NEVI plans must include a Community Engagement Outcomes Report and address updated NEVI Guidance.
The Tennessee Department of Environment and Conservation (TDEC) provides funding for repowering or replacing Class 4-8 school, shuttle, and transit buses with alternative fuel or all-electric models.
TDEC provides funding for replacing Class 4-7 local freight trucks and Class 8 local freight trucks and port drayage trucks with alternative fuel or all-electric models.
Alternative fuels eligible for TDEC funding include compressed natural gas, propane, and hybrid electric technologies.
TDEC also provides funding for light-duty EV charging infrastructure.
Private, public, and non-profit organizations, including state, local, and tribal governments, are eligible for TDEC vehicle and charging funding.
In partnership with the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA), TDEC provides funding for the Fast Charge TN Network to support DC fast charging station deployment.
Eligible applicants for the Fast Charge TN Network include TVA-served Local Power Companies whose service territory is located along corridors.
The TDEC grant programs are funded in part by Tennessee’s portion of the Volkswagen Environmental Mitigation Trust.

links.url: 2023 DGS Annual Report on Energy-Efficient Purchasing
TDEC Project Solicitations, Fast Charge TN Network, and Fast Charge TN Map websites

source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Electricity Laws and Incentives in Tennessee

entity_name: Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc.
canonical_name: Toyota Industries Electric Systems North America, Inc.
facts: TIESNA will create more than 250 new jobs while expanding the Toyota Industries Corporation campus in Pendergrass.
The expansion includes a new manufacturing facility valued at $69 million in investment.
TIESNA broke ground on the new facility on November 14, 2023.
Japanese Consul General Mio Maeda and local representatives joined the groundbreaking ceremony.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia has distinguished itself as a leader in the automotive industry.
Governor Brian P. Kemp pledged to continue working with state and local partners to bring opportunity to communities across Georgia.
TIESNA is a subsidiary of Toyota Industries Corporation, which is headquartered in Japan.
Toyota Industries develops and manufactures automobile-related products, including car air-conditioning compressors and car electronics components for worldwide carmakers.
Toyota Industries has operated in Pendergrass since 2004.
The group's presence in Pendergrass began with TD Automotive Compressor Georgia, LLC (TACG).
Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America, Co. (TICA) was established in Pendergrass in 2012.
TACG and TICA currently support more than 400 jobs in Jackson County.
TIESNA's new facility will be located at the 1700 Valentine Industrial Parkway campus in Pendergrass.
The new facility will produce DC-to-DC converters that convert high voltage to low voltage to supply power to auxiliary equipment.
Operations at the new TIESNA facility are expected to begin in 2025.
Jackson County Commission Chair Tom Crow welcomed TIESNA as the third Toyota Industries-related company in Jackson County.
Tom Crow stated that TACG and TICA have been outstanding corporate citizens in the community.
Tom Crow pledged Jackson County's support for Toyota Industries' success.
Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, welcomed TIESNA's expansion as part of Georgia's growing automaking sector.
Griff Lynch noted that significant investments from Toyota Industries and other automotive suppliers are delivering career opportunities for Georgians.
Senior Regional Project Manager Kristen Miller represented the GDEcD Global Commerce team on the project.
The project was supported in partnership with the Jackson County Area Chamber of Commerce, Georgia Power, the Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Toyota Industries has become part of the fabric of the Jackson County community since 2004.
Pat Wilson emphasized that building long-term relationships gives Georgia a competitive advantage for company expansions.
GDEcD has maintained 50 years of representation in Japan to keep direct communication lines open with corporate headquarters like Toyota Industries.
Pat Wilson expressed excitement about welcoming TIESNA to Jackson County and congratulated the involved partners.
Georgia has positioned itself as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry for over a century.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in Georgia's automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to fiscal year 2021.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia's original equipment manufacturers resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Toyota Industries Corporation was established in 1926 in Japan for the manufacturing and sales of automatic looms.
Toyota Industries currently holds more than 280 consolidated subsidiaries worldwide.
Toyota Industries holds the largest world market share in air-jet looms, car air conditioning compressors, and lift trucks.
TIESNA was established in 2013 in Novi, Michigan.
TIESNA operates as a sales company handling automotive electronics components such as AC inverters, voltage stabilizers, and motor controllers.
TIESNA's customers include major original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in North America.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Toyota Industries Corporation Expands Georgia Presence, Creating 250 New Jobs | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

entity_name: U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
canonical_name: U.S. Department of Energy Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation
facts: The U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation announced $134 million for two projects to strengthen domestic rare earth element supply chains.
The announcement was made on June 2, 2026.
The funding aims to reduce reliance on foreign sources and improve U.S. energy security.

The selected projects will demonstrate the commercial viability of recovering and refining rare earth elements from unconventional feedstocks, including mine tailings, electronic waste, and other waste materials.

Assistant Secretary of Energy (EERE) Audrey Robertson stated that achieving energy independence requires finding value in overlooked resources and expanding capabilities to recover rare earth elements from waste products.

Rare earth elements such as praseodymium, neodymium, terbium, and dysprosium are vital components in advanced manufacturing, defense systems, and high-performance magnets used in power generation and electric motors.

Colorado School of Mines was selected for award negotiations to design, construct, commission, and operate a rare earth element demonstration facility near the Gramercy alumina refinery in St. John the Baptist Parish, Louisiana.
The Colorado School of Mines facility will process "red mud," a critical mineral-rich bauxite waste product.

By separating rare earth oxides and refining them into rare earth metals, the Colorado School of Mines facility will demonstrate the commercial feasibility of an integrated domestic rare earth extraction, separation, and refining process.
The Colorado School of Mines project partners include ElementUSA, Pacific Northwest National Laboratory, Principal Mineral, and Rare Earth Technologies Inc.

Phoenix Tailings was selected for award negotiations to design, construct, commission, and operate a demonstration-scale facility to produce high-purity rare earth metals from domestic industrial waste-derived feedstocks.
The Phoenix Tailings project will establish a new commercial pathway for domestic production of heavy rare earth metals.
Phoenix Tailings will partner with the Massachusetts Institute of Technology and the University of Minnesota on its project.
DOE’s Manufacturing Deployment Office manages this funding opportunity through its Rare Earth Elements Demonstration Facility Program.
The Rare Earth Elements Demonstration Facility Program is designed to demonstrate full-scale integrated rare earth extraction and separation facilities in the United States.
Selection for award negotiations is not a commitment by DOE to issue an award or provide funding.
Before funding is issued, DOE and the applicant will undergo a negotiation process, and DOE may cancel negotiations and rescind the selection for any reason during that time.
DOE award amounts are subject to change pending negotiations.
source_title: DOE’s Office of Critical Minerals and Energy Innovation Announces $134 Million To Bolster Rare Earth Element Supply Chains | Department of Energy

entity_name: National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Formula Program
canonical_name: National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Formula Program
facts: The U.S. Department of Transportation’s Federal Highway Administration NEVI Formula Program provides funding to states to strategically deploy electric vehicle charging infrastructure.
Funding is available for up to 80% of eligible project costs.
Eligible costs include the acquisition, installation, and network connection of EV chargers to facilitate data collection, access, and reliability.
Eligible costs include proper operation and maintenance of EV chargers.
Eligible costs include long-term EV charger data sharing.
EV chargers must be non-proprietary and allow for open-access payment methods.
EV chargers must be publicly available or available to authorized commercial motor vehicle operators from more than one company.
EV chargers must be located along designated FHWA Alternative Fuel Corridors.

If a state and their FHWA Division Office determine that all Alternative Fuel Corridors in the state have been fully built out, the state can propose alternative public locations and roads for EV charger installation.
FHWA must distribute NEVI Formula Program funds made available each fiscal year through FY 2026.
Each state receives an amount equal to the state FHWA funding formula determined by 23 U.S. Code 104.
To receive funding, states must submit updated plans to their FHWA Division Office for review and publicly post them annually.
State plans must describe how the state intends to distribute NEVI funds and related physical and cybersecurity strategies.
State plans must include a Community Engagement Outcomes Report.

Federal law requires that 10% of NEVI Formula funding be set aside each fiscal year for DOT to provide grants for states and localities requiring additional assistance to strategically deploy EV charging infrastructure.

The FY 2022 10% NEVI set aside funding was used for the EV Charger Reliability and Accessibility Accelerator Program, which provided grants focused on repairing or replacing non-operational EV chargers.
The remainder of set-aside funds were allocated to the Charging and Fueling Infrastructure Round 1B awards.
The program is classified as an Incentive under Federal jurisdiction.
The program was enacted on Nov 15, 2021.
The program supports EVs and PHEVs technologies.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: National Electric Vehicle Infrastructure (NEVI) Formula Program

entity_name: Section 247 Maintaining and Enhancing Hydroelectricity Incentive Program
canonical_name: Section 247 Maintaining and Enhancing Hydroelectricity Incentive Program
facts: On September 5, 2024, the Grid Deployment Office announced the selection of 293 capital improvement projects for negotiations across 33 states to receive over $430 million in Maintaining and Enhancing Hydroelectricity Incentive payments.
DOE resumed negotiations with the selectees on April 27, 2026.

Per statutory requirements, DOE will fund all qualified capital improvement projects at limits set forth in Section 247, covering 30% of total project costs not to exceed $5,000,000 within a qualified hydroelectric facility.
The total incentive per facility cannot exceed $5,000,000 even if multiple project applications are submitted.
A total of 47 projects are located throughout Alabama, Arkansas, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, and West Virginia.
The Southeast Region requested a total of $83 million in incentive payments for its 47 projects.
Capital improvement categories for the Southeast Region projects include Grid Resiliency (18 projects), Dam Safety (21 projects), and Environmental Improvements (8 projects).
Projects in the Southeast Region will provide economic benefits by committing to hiring local workers or contractors.

Projects in the Southeast Region will provide economic benefits by providing apprenticeship, pre-apprenticeship, and youth education programs to showcase opportunities available in the clean energy workforce.

Nearly 70 contracts are already in place with, or commitments have been made to a contractor preference for, businesses majority owned or controlled by underrepresented persons or groups of underrepresented persons in the Southeast Region.

The Buzzards Roost Hydroelectric Facility in Greenwood County, Chappells, South Carolina requested $5 million to redesign and construct an emergency spillway channel and structure to prevent spilling of water over the top of the dam and maintain continued water supply to two counties during major flooding.
The Buzzards Roost Hydroelectric Facility is located at the outfall of Lake Greenwood and began operations in 1939.

Duke Energy Carolinas, LLC requested $5 million for the Dearborn Dam project in Great Falls, South Carolina to conduct a major habitat and recreational water flow project on the Long Bypass Reach and Short Bypass Reach along the Great Falls Reservoir.
The Dearborn Dam project resulted in the creation of whitewater river channels, enhancement of natural habitats, construction of a large access area, and restoration of a historic building.
The Dearborn Dam facility is located on the Catawba River and began operations in 1923.
source_title: Section 247: Maintaining and Enhancing Hydroelectricity Incentives Selections | Department of Energy

facts: Volvo Group North America participates in the Better Plants Challenge under the Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative.
Committed to a 25% reduction in portfolio energy intensity from a 2014 baseline by 2024, based on a commitment of 14 plants.
Currently has 13 plants committed to this goal.
Has achieved 26% cumulative progress toward the 2014 baseline goal.
Committed to an additional goal of a 25% reduction in portfolio energy intensity from a 2009 baseline by 2024, which was met in 2014.
Has achieved 26.8% cumulative progress toward the 2009 baseline goal.
Committed to an additional goal of a 15% reduction in energy intensity by 2033, covering 15 facilities.
Participates in the DOE Waste Reduction Network program.
Participates in the Superior Energy Performance 50001 program.
source_title: Volvo Group North America | Better Buildings & Better Plants Initiative

The business type is Domestic Profit Corporation.
The business status is Active/Compliance.

facts: Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A. announced its participation in the Drawdown Georgia Business Compact.
The Drawdown Georgia Business Compact is a statewide initiative facilitated by the Ray C. Anderson Center for Sustainable Business at the Georgia Institute of Technology.
The Compact was launched in October 2021 as a business-focused collaborative initiative focused on galvanizing climate action in Georgia.

Martin Peters, Director of External Affairs for Yamaha U.S. Marine Business Unit, stated that Yamaha's support underscores its mission to achieve carbon neutrality within its facilities worldwide by 2035.
This carbon neutrality goal includes Yamaha's facilities located in Newnan, Georgia, specifically Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America.
Through the initiative, Yamaha will access research and collaborate with conservation-minded Georgia businesses to reduce carbon emissions.
Drawdown Georgia was launched in 2020 as a research-based initiative born from a multi-university collaboration led by Georgia Tech.
Research partners for Drawdown Georgia included Emory University, Georgia State University, and The University of Georgia.
The research team released initial findings in October 2020 identifying 20 high-impact climate solutions to advance drawdown in Georgia.

Michael Oxman, Managing Director of the Ray C. Anderson Center for Sustainable Business, stated that Drawdown Georgia uses these climate solutions as a roadmap to bring together businesses, universities, NGOs, and policymakers.
Drawdown Georgia takes inspiration from Project Drawdown, localizing climate solutions that are cost-competitive and market-ready for Georgia.
The initiative has grown into a movement including many partners and funders working to advance climate solutions in Georgia.
Yamaha U.S. Marine Business Unit is based in Kennesaw, Georgia.
The unit markets and sells marine outboard motors ranging from 2.5 to 450 horsepower.
The unit also markets and sells fiberglass, jet-drive sport boats ranging from 19 to 27 feet, and personal watercraft.

The unit includes manufacturing divisions of Yamaha Marine Systems Co., Inc., including Kracor of Milwaukee (rotational molding), Bennett Marine of Deerfield Beach, Florida (trim tabs), and Yamaha Marine Precision Propellers of Indianapolis (stainless steel propellers).
Yamaha Marine Group is a division of Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A., based in Cypress, California.
source_title: Yamaha Lends Support to Drawdown Georgia

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC)
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) and Yamaha Motor Corp., USA (YMUS) are celebrating 30 years of U.S. manufacturing at the Newnan, Georgia facility as of June 11, 2018.
Over three decades, YMMC has produced more than 3.5 million vehicles including ATVs, SxS vehicles, golf cars, and watercraft for worldwide distribution.
Yamaha’s "Assembled in USA" brand represents the highest standards in powersports manufacturing and supports thousands of local U.S. jobs.
YMMC President Mike Chrzanowski stated that U.S. production ensures legendary quality and reliability while providing high responsiveness to U.S. market demands and streamlined distribution.
Over the past 15 years leading up to 2018, Yamaha invested more than $354 million to create and maintain a state-of-the-art facility in Newnan.
The Newnan manufacturing facility spans 1,355,000 square feet.
Of Yamaha’s approximately 3,400 total U.S. employees, more than 1,500 people are directly employed at the YMMC factory.
The YMMC factory is certified under the ISO9001 Quality Management System and ISO14001 Environmental Management System.

Founded in 1986, the YMMC facility produces Golf Cars, Personal Watercraft, Side-by-Side Vehicles, and All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs) to high standards for quality, safety, and environmentally-responsible manufacturing.
Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS), headquartered in California since 1960, also maintains facilities in Wisconsin and Georgia, along with factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.
YMUS products are sold through a nationwide network of dealers in the United States.

links.context: Full lineup of vehicles assembled in USA

source_title: Yamaha Celebrates 30 Years of U.S. Manufacturing

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) re-issued a $900 million solicitation to support the deployment of small modular reactors.
The solicitation aims to better align with President Trump's agenda to unleash American energy and AI dominance.
U.S. Secretary of Energy Chris Wright stated that America’s nuclear energy renaissance starts now.
Secretary Wright emphasized that abundant and affordable energy is key to the nation’s economic prosperity and security.
Secretary Wright described the solicitation as a call to action for early movers seeking to put more energy on the grid through advanced light-water small modular reactors.
U.S. electricity demand is forecast to soar in the coming years driven by consumer needs, data center growth, increased AI use, and the industrial sector’s need for constant power.
Small modular reactors could provide reliable power for energy-intensive sectors, with flexible deployment thanks to their compact size and modular design.

Light-water small modular reactors could leverage the existing service and supply chain supporting the country’s current fleet of light-water reactors, helping speed up near-term deployment of new nuclear reactors.
DOE is offering funding to de-risk the deployment of Generation III+ light-water small modular reactors (Gen III+ SMR) through two tiers.

Tier 1: First Mover Team Support will provide up to $800M to support up to two first mover teams of utility, reactor vendor, constructor, and end-users/off-takers committed to deploying a first plant.

Tier 1 facilitates a multi-reactor, Gen III+ SMR orderbook and the opportunity to work with the National Nuclear Security Administration to incorporate safeguards and security by design into the projects.
Tier 2: Fast Follower Deployment Support will provide approximately $100M to spur additional Gen III+ SMR deployments by addressing key gaps in design, licensing, supply chain, and site preparation.
The selection of awardees will be solely based on technical merit.
Applications are due on April 23, 2025, at 5:00 p.m. ET.
Previous applicants who applied to the 2024 solicitation must resubmit their proposals following the new guidance to receive consideration.
New applications are also welcome.
source_title: $900 Million Available to Unlock Commercial Deployment of American-Made Small Modular Reactors | Department of Energy

facts: YKK USA manufactures parts for cars, bridging zipper manufacturing with automotive supply chains.
YKK’s Macon plant does business with almost every automobile maker in North and Central America.
The Macon plant manufactures a range of applications for more than 100 customers.
The company works directly with OEMs, Tier 1, and Tier 2 companies from the initial development phase to vehicle launch.
A typical car in Georgia might include at least 15 YKK-made parts.
YKK won the 2021 Automotive Supplier of the Year (200 or more employees) award in the GEAR Awards.

The GEAR Awards are presented by the Georgia Department of Economic Development and voted on by leaders across Georgia’s automotive industry, representing advanced manufacturing companies, universities, and innovation centers.
YKK products ensure cars are assembled properly and safely, with quality parts lasting the vehicle's lifetime.
YKK products contribute to sustainability by allowing manufacturers to eliminate heavy plastic parts and thereby increase fuel efficiency.
Automotive parts made at the Macon facility include zippers, metal, and plastic components for seat trim closure applications.
The Macon facility produces hardware for strap hold-down applications.
The Macon facility produces buttons for child safety car seat attachments.
The Macon facility produces clips, rails, and extruded hook fasteners for seat trim attachments.
The Macon facility produces truck tonneau cover hold-downs.
YKK is AITF 16949 certified, a mandatory industry requirement that aligns automotive quality management systems throughout the world.

Jim Reed, president of YKK Corporation of America, states that clients use YKK products to make trim, foam buns, headrests, and armrests supplied to seat assembly and OEM plants to build complete vehicles.

YKK supplies customers with closures and fasteners to manufacture tops for Jeeps and SUVs, truck tonneau covers, commercial truck compartment curtains, battery hold-downs, car interior components, and off-road vehicle cabin covers.
YKK designs, manufactures, leases, and services machinery for the automotive industry.
Once a vehicle is in production, YKK provides continuous support to automotive factories, design studios, and engineering departments, monitoring product performance throughout the vehicle's life.
YKK has been located in Georgia for 52 years.
YKK employs around 820 Georgians in Marietta and Macon.
YKK’s North and South America regional headquarters is located in Marietta, as is the company’s American headquarters.
YKK’s architectural products division, YKK AP, is headquartered in Atlanta and employs approximately 800 people in Atlanta, Marietta, Dublin, and Macon.
YKK USA’s Macon manufacturing facility is one of the company’s largest plants outside Japan.
Former Governor Jimmy Carter convinced YKK founder Tadao Yoshida that Macon was the ideal location for YKK’s first vertically integrated production plant in the United States.
Georgia governors have continued to support the Macon plant.

Company leaders welcomed Governor Brian Kemp to the Macon plant to help launch Georgia Made™, a program through GDEcD that provides support in logistics, manufacturing, and marketing of Georgia products.
YKK founder Tadao Yoshida identified Georgia’s sound water source as an important resource for manufacturing.
The interstate systems and deep-water port of Savannah provide a perfect system for importing materials and exporting finished products for YKK.
By air, YKK can reach any location in the continental U.S. in four hours, and by truck the farthest location is five days, with half the country accessible in two days.
Jim Reed praises Georgia’s reliable workforce and cutting-edge high school and university systems.
YKK has an excellent working relationship with many Georgia agencies, appreciating their responsiveness and genuine concern.
The factors that first drew YKK to Georgia are still the factors that support its success, according to Jim Reed.
Jim Reed believes the GEAR award will highlight awareness of YKK’s role as a supplier of vital products to the automotive industry.

The company promoted the GEAR award externally and internally, honoring automotive-related employees at an awards ceremony and launching a social media campaign to showcase the faces behind their products.
Jim Reed states that Georgia resources have positioned YKK to grow from a small manufacturing company to become the global leader in the fastening industry.
Georgia is now regarded as the center of Japanese industry in the U.S. Southeast.
YKK remains grateful to early visionaries who pursued foreign investment in the state and proud of its role in building the robust relationship between Japan and Georgia.
Jim Reed appreciates Governor Kemp and previous governors for ensuring Georgia is business-friendly, stating Georgia is the ideal place for any company to call home.

The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) is the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment, encouraging expansion of existing industry and small businesses, locating new markets, attracting tourists, and promoting the state for arts, film, music, and digital entertainment.
source_title: YKK named Automotive Supplier of the Year in 2021 Georgia Gear Awards | Georgia

facts: YKK AP America Inc. is a technology-oriented manufacturer of commercial façade systems and residential windows and doors.
The company will invest up to $125 million in an expansion that includes a new manufacturing facility and additional machinery and equipment in Bibb County.
The new facility will create 100 new jobs in addition to the 250 jobs YKK AP currently supports in Macon, Georgia.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the investment on August 16, 2022.
YKK AP specializes in architectural products for commercial and residential buildings.
YKK AP entered the U.S. market in 1991 with a vertically integrated, commercial products factory in Dublin, Georgia.
YKK AP maintains its headquarters in Atlanta, Georgia.
YKK AP and its Ontario-based subsidiary Erie Architectural Products employ over 1,200 employees in North America.
Nearly 900 of the company's employees are located within the State of Georgia.
Oliver Stepe serves as President of YKK AP America Inc.
The new facility will expand workforce capacity and introduce further vertical integration of vinyl extrusion, glass, and window production.
The expansion aims to create enhanced efficiencies to manufacture high-quality windows and doors for homebuilders and homeowners.
The investment supports YKK AP's long-term vision to lead the residential window and door market and expand business in and beyond the Southeast region.
The new state-of-the-art facility will be located at the Macon-Bibb I-75 Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified site.
After completion, the company will relocate its operations and employees from its current site on Ocmulgee Boulevard to the new facility.
The Macon facility will become home to the company’s residential division, which manufactures vinyl windows and doors for builders and homeowners.

The Macon facility will serve as a model plant for future North American manufacturing facilities, featuring new technologies, advanced sustainable manufacturing processes, and enhanced work environments for employees.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in early 2024.
The company is currently hiring for numerous positions.
Macon-Bibb County Mayor Lester M. Miller stated that the investment brings an additional 100 jobs to the area and uses the new facility as a blueprint for future facilities.
Senior Project Manager Candice Scott represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on the project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority and Georgia Power.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that YKK AP opened its first branch in Atlanta in 1986 and established its U.S. manufacturing presence in middle Georgia five years later.
Companies in advanced manufacturing, like YKK AP, played a key role in creating the second most new jobs in Georgia last year.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific certification requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Through the GRAD program, Georgia has more than 60 industrial certified sites that are ready for fast-track industrial projects.
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.
All building products are manufactured in the company’s world-class production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.
Together with its subsidiary brand Erie Architectural Products (Erie AP), the company serves architects, glazing contractors, homebuilders, and resellers across the country.
source_title: Governor Kemp: Building Materials Manufacturer YKK AP America Expands Residential Window Production Capabilities with New Facility in Bibb County | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: The U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Loan Programs Office (LPO) is preparing an Environmental Assessment (EA) to consider the environmental impacts associated with providing potential financial assistance (a federal loan guarantee) for the construction of 80 megawatt (MW) and 120 MW solar photovoltaic Installations in Guayama and Salinas, Puerto Rico.

The Jobos solar project in Guayama consists of an 80 megawatt (MW) PV facility, a 110 MW battery energy storage system (BESS), on-site step-up substation, on-site offices and control building, and a 1,000-meter 115 kilovolt (kV) transmission line.
The Salinas solar project consists of a 120 MW PV facility, a 175 MW BESS, an on-site electrical substation, onsite offices and control room, and a 4,717-meter 115 kV transmission line.
No public comment opportunities are currently available for these projects.
source_title: DOE/EA-2256: Clean Flexible Energy LLC, Jobos and Salinas Projects; Guayama and Salinas, Puerto Rico | Department of Energy

entity_name: GEORGIA MATCH Direct College Admissions Initiative
facts: Announced by Governor Brian P. Kemp during the inaugural Governor's Workforce Summit on October 5, 2023.
Described as one of the largest state-run direct admissions initiatives in the nation.
Represents the culmination of multiple state agencies working together to ease the process of connecting students with higher education opportunities.
Designed to engage all learners and households, including those that do not typically consider postsecondary education pathways.
No other state in the country currently has a direct admissions initiative of this scope and scale.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: "GEORGIA MATCH is a great example of the historic success we can achieve when stakeholders across the education spectrum work together. This program will ensure that every high school student in our state knows they have options to learn and succeed here in the No. 1 state for business."

Developed in collaboration with the Governor's Office, Governor’s Office of Planning & Budget, Georgia Student Finance Commission, Technical College System of Georgia, Governor’s Office of Student Achievement, Georgia Department of Education, and University System of Georgia.
The number of schools listed on a GEORGIA MATCH letter is determined by the individual student’s 11th grade HOPE GPA and freshman index score.
All students will receive a letter of academic eligibility for admission to the twenty-two Technical College System of Georgia institutions.
Students with higher GPAs will also see up to twenty-three University System of Georgia institutions.
The three USG institutions not participating in the GEORGIA MATCH program utilize a different holistic admissions process.

Beginning next week, over 120,000 high school seniors in Georgia will receive a personalized letter from Governor Kemp listing the public technical institutions, colleges and universities that are holding a spot for them in the Fall 2024 semester, based on academic eligibility.
The letters will provide information on next steps to claim a spot at a college on the student's individual list.
All GEORGIA MATCH participating institutions will waive application fees for students during November 2023 for students who apply through GEORGIA MATCH.

links.context: View personalized MATCH list, request more information, and claim spot

source_title: Gov. Kemp Announces GEORGIA MATCH Direct College Admissions Initiative | Governor Brian P. Kemp Office of the Governor

facts: Corporations across diverse sectors, including FORTUNE 500s like Aflac and UPS and industry leaders like Aurubis and Porsche, are recognizing the benefits of establishing their presence in Georgia.
Georgia is consistently ranked the Top State for Business.
The state offers attractive incentives such as a low corporate tax rate.
Georgia’s workforce consists of 5.3 million people, providing companies with a diverse talent pool.
The talent pool is strengthened by investments in technical college training, apprenticeship programs, and K-12 education.
Georgia Quick Start is ranked the nation’s No. 1 workforce training program and offers customized training to create a workforce ready to start production on Day One.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development operates the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) Certification Program to provide development-ready sites.
GRAD Certified sites have been critical in winning key projects, and the state continues to increase its supply of development-ready properties.
Georgia continues to invest in a seamless system of ocean, rail, truck, and air transportation to support burgeoning industries.
Companies have access to Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport, the Port of Savannah, and the Port of Brunswick.
The state offers a high quality of life as an incentive for companies and their people.

facts: Rivian announced it is increasing its initial production capacity to 300,000 vehicles annually for its future manufacturing facility in Stanton Springs North, Georgia.
The updated capacity represents a 50 percent increase over the initially planned capacity of 200,000 units.
The capacity increase aims to facilitate a lower cost per unit while providing significant room for future expansion of capacity in later phases.
Rivian has worked with the U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Energy Dominance Financing (EDF) to update its DOE loan.
Vertical construction at the Georgia facility is expected to commence in 2026.
Rivian now expects to first draw on the loan by early 2027.
The company remains on track for vehicle production to begin in late 2028.
The total DOE loan is aligned with the updated facility design and roadmap at up to $4.5 billion ($4,006 million principal and $494 million capitalized interest) for the initial phase.
Rivian is transitioning to vertical construction, which is expected to begin this spring.
Preparations are underway for the development of the stamping press area at the facility.
The stamping press area is one of the most capital-intensive and technically demanding projects within the plant from a construction perspective.
Progress on the facility’s primary buildings will accelerate this summer as they begin to take shape.
Rivian recently announced a partnership with Uber for its Georgia operations.
Rivian is expected to build up to 50,000 robotaxis at its Georgia plant beginning in late 2028.
Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated the company is very excited to partner with the U.S. Department of Energy to grow its manufacturing footprint in Georgia.
The R2 vehicle model dramatically expands Rivian's market opportunity according to leadership.

Thousands of dedicated people will soon work in the Georgia plant, instrumental to Rivian’s growth as it scales American manufacturing and works to ensure the U.S. retains its leadership in innovation and technology.
Rivian is an American automotive technology company that develops and manufactures category-defining electric vehicles as well as vertically integrated technologies and services.
Rivian vehicles are manufactured in the United States and are sold directly to consumer and commercial customers.

facts: The Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (Public Law 117-169) amended the Qualified Plug-in Electric Drive Motor Vehicle Credit (IRC 30D), now known as the Clean Vehicle Credit.
A new requirement for final assembly in North America took effect on August 17, 2022.
The final assembly requirement applies to new electric, fuel cell electric, and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles acquired, delivered, and placed in service after August 16, 2022.

For vehicles placed in service on or after January 1, 2023, the Clean Vehicle Credit provisions are subject to updated guidance from the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and the U.S. Department of the Treasury.
Beginning January 1, 2024, buyers can reduce the vehicle's upfront purchase price by the amount of their Clean Vehicle Credit by choosing to transfer their credit to the dealer.
Before 2024, eligible vehicle buyers could only receive the amount of their credit after filing their tax return.
Starting January 1, 2024, dealers must submit information to the IRS through IRS Energy Credits Online to determine vehicle eligibility and amount of a Clean Vehicle Credit at the point of sale.
Without this submission, buyers cannot claim a tax credit on their return nor can they transfer it to a dealer.
A dealer must provide the buyer a copy of the IRS's approval of the dealer's submission.
The list of EVs with final assembly in North America applies only to vehicles purchased and delivered between August 17 and December 31, 2022.

Model Year 2022 and Model Year 2023 vehicles on the list are based on Fuel Economy labeling information and Part 583 data submitted by manufacturers to the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) respectively as of December 31, 2022.
Verification of final assembly for vehicles purchased beginning January 1, 2023, should use the VIN decoder.
Manufacturer sales caps apply to vehicles placed in service before January 1, 2023.

Manufacturers can use the website contact form to request changes to this vehicle list.
Final assembly in North America is not required for tax credit eligibility for vehicles purchased before August 17, 2022.
The build location of a particular vehicle should be confirmed by the VIN or an information label affixed to the vehicle.
The U.S. Department of Transportation's NHTSA provides a VIN decoder that can be used to identify a vehicle's build plant and country of manufacture along with other details about the vehicle.

Verification of North American final assembly is an initial step toward identifying vehicles that may be eligible for the Clean Vehicle Credit, but further conditions for eligibility may apply depending on the purchase date.
For some manufacturers, the build location may vary based on the specific vehicle, trim, or the date in the Model Year when it was produced because some models are produced in multiple locations.
To be eligible for the Clean Vehicle Credit, a vehicle must have undergone final assembly in North America.
In general, North America includes the United States and Puerto Rico, Canada, and Mexico for purposes of determining the location of final assembly.

facts: In December 2024, the Department of Energy announced the closing of a $9.63 billion direct loan to BlueOval SK LLC (BOSK).
The loan funds the construction of three manufacturing plants.
The plants will produce batteries for Ford Motor Company’s future Ford and Lincoln electric vehicles (EVs).
The three facilities created more than 5,000 construction jobs as the plants were being built.
The facilities will create up to 7,500 BOSK operations jobs.
Together, the plants will manufacture batteries to enable more than 120 gigawatt hours of U.S. battery production annually.
The batteries will displace over 455 million gallons of gasoline per year for the lifetime of the vehicles.
Through Ford’s workforce development team, BOSK is partnering with the Tennessee and Kentucky state governments to develop curricula that supports new technical schools in Kentucky and Tennessee.
The training programs will prepare community members for quality jobs at those facilities.
To ensure the availability of skilled union labor for construction, BOSK is managing the projects under addendums to the National Maintenance Agreement.

BOSK is partnering with the Tennessee and Kentucky state governments to create new community college campuses that will serve technical schools and train community members for operations jobs at the facilities.
BOSK is committed to building a diverse contractor and supplier network through outreach events, partnerships with relevant government offices, and connections with certification organizations.
BlueOval SK, LLC (BOSK) is a joint venture between Ford Motor Company (Ford) and SK On.
The manufacturing facilities are located in Glendale, KY and Stanton, TN.
The loan program is ATVM.
The loan type is a Direct Loan.
The guaranteed loan amount is $9.63 billion, last updated in December 2024.
Permanent U.S. jobs supported by the project are estimated at 7,500 at the time of closing.
source_title: BlueOval SK | Department of Energy

facts: Club Car is a world-leading manufacturer of small-wheel, zero-emissions electric vehicles.
Club Car unveiled the CRU, a street-legal lifestyle vehicle designed to disrupt the low-speed vehicle (LSV) market for consumers.
The CRU expands Club Car's leading electric vehicle portfolio into a new category of Neighborhood Electric Vehicles (NEVs).
NEVs are designed to meet the needs of consumers seeking a more fun and sustainable option for short trips.
The CRU was designed in partnership with BMW Designworks.
The CRU features a sleek, ergonomic shape and superior handling.
The CRU has a capacity for six occupants and a top speed of 25 miles per hour.
The vehicle includes a table for riders to gather around and front bucket seats that can be flipped around to face rear passengers.
Club Car is launching the CRU through a campaign encouraging consumers to "Be a Detourist."
The campaign illustrates the spontaneous, social, and easygoing lifestyle the vehicle enables.
Ross Atherton, Vice President at Club Car, stated that the CRU is a game-changer for the electric vehicle market and a missing link in the micro-mobility ecosystem.
Atherton noted that with more than 60 years of innovation and a reputation for safety and high quality, Club Car is reimagining an entirely new category of Neighborhood Electric Vehicles.
The CRU serves as a sustainable and fun alternative to using a full-sized car to run errands or take quick trips around town.
The vehicle directly meets the needs of consumers who currently use golf cars or other vehicles to run errands or take quick trips down the road in their neighborhoods.
Club Car CRU is launching to a limited dealer network across Atlanta, Raleigh, Dallas, Phoenix, Miami, Tampa, Orlando, and Charleston.
Additional markets will open this year.
Club Car was founded in 1958.
Club Car's product portfolio includes gas and electric golf, utility, and personal transportation vehicles for commercial and consumer markets.
As an industry leader in electrification and sustainability, Club Car is on the forefront of environmentally responsible Zero Emission Vehicle (ZEV) technologies.
