facts: Benefits of tax credits earned by S-corporations or partnerships are usually passed through to the shareholders or partners.
The law limits the amount of job tax credit that may be claimed against withholding by excluding Port Traffic Credit and Joint Development Authority credit amounts from being applied to withholding.
If a company claims Port Traffic or Joint Development Authority credits in addition to the Job Tax withholding credit, those specific amounts may only be applied against income tax liability.
For flow-through entities making an irrevocable election to claim the withholding credit, amounts that cannot be applied to withholding are allowed to flow through.

A taxpayer may apply the 50% limitation of one credit and the 50% limitation of another credit to offset up to 100% of income tax liability, though some credits like research have requirements that may prevent a full 100% offset.
Investment tax credits require an application process that must be approved before the credit can be claimed on a tax return.

The regulation requires the investment tax credit application to be filed within 45 days of project completion, which for most taxpayers means within 45 days of the close of the tax year in which the project was completed.
Applicants should allow a minimum of 60 days for the investment tax credit application to be processed.
Applications filed after the 45-day period must petition the Commissioner for approval to file late.

An investment tax credit application must be signed and dated by a corporate officer, include a descriptive project narrative, and contain a spreadsheet detailing assets to be claimed with descriptions, manufacturing process explanations, dates placed into service, and purchase prices.

If a consultant files an investment tax credit application on behalf of a taxpayer, a properly executed Power of Attorney is required; otherwise, any questions or correspondence will go directly to the taxpayer at the address or phone number provided on the application.
Only real and personal property qualify for the investment tax credit.
The Port Traffic Credit is claimed on the form for the tax credit it accompanies or replaces, requiring all requirements of the original credit to be met first.

Claiming the Port Traffic Credit requires attaching a statement to the Georgia return reflecting base year and prior year traffic to substantiate the increase; failure to provide this disallows the credit.
For job tax credit purposes, the port traffic increase must be established in the year of eligibility with sustained increases shown for subsequent years.
Qualifying for the Georgia Low Income Housing Credit requires the qualified Georgia project to be eligible for the federal housing credit under Section 42 of the IRC of 1986.

Claiming the Georgia Low Income Housing Credit on a tax return requires submitting a partnership K-1 (if claiming as a flow-through), the associated Federal Form 8609 tax credit certificate, and a schedule with building-by-building allocation for each claimed property.
source_title: General Business Credits - FAQ | Department of Revenue

facts: The expanding electric vehicle industry in Georgia represented almost a third of both job creation and capital investment in 2023.
A total of 26,474 jobs were created in Georgia in 2023.
A total of $18.2 billion in capital investment was recorded in Georgia in 2023.
Seven automotive industry projects were announced over the last three months of 2023, stretching from the coastal region to northwest Georgia.
A total of 4,990 jobs were created in Georgia during the fourth quarter of 2023.
A total of $3.4 billion in capital investment was recorded in Georgia during the fourth quarter of 2023.
A wave of automotive parts manufacturers located across the state in support of the electric vehicle industry in 2023.
Hyundai Industrial established a new facility in Coweta County.
Hwashin established automotive parts manufacturing operations in Dublin.
New automotive manufacturing facilities are located surrounding the Hyundai megasite in coastal Georgia.
Daechang Seat Corp. is creating 500 jobs in Savannah for automotive seat frames.
Hwashin is creating 460 jobs in Dublin for automotive chassis and body parts manufacturing.
Toyota Industries is creating 255 jobs in Pendergrass for automotive parts manufacturing.
Club Car is creating 150 jobs in Appling for compact, street-legal electric vehicle manufacturing.
Total jobs and capital investment estimates are derived from project announcements made in partnership with the State of Georgia Department of Economic Development.
These estimates may not include all projects and are subject to change as yearly totals continue to be reported by businesses.
source_title: Georgia's 2023 Locations & Growth | Select Georgia

facts: In model year (MY) 2023, the highest top range for an EV was 516 miles on a single charge (Lucid Air).
The median range for all EV models rose to a new high of 270 miles.
The number of models and their ranges have greatly increased since modern EVs were introduced in 2011, when four models were available with ranges spanning 63 to 94 miles per charge.
Range is based on Environmental Protection Agency estimates.
Data does not include plug-in hybrid vehicles.
Only vehicles that were certified by the EPA are included in the data.
Source is the U.S. Department of Energy and U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Fueleconomy.gov website, accessed November 7, 2023.
source_title: FOTW #1323, January 1, 2024: Top Range for Model Year 2023 EVs was 516 Miles on a Single Charge | Department of Energy

facts: In October 2024, the U.S. Department of Energy’s Loan Programs Office announced a $2.26 billion loan to Lithium Americas Corp’s subsidiary, Lithium Nevada Corp., to finance construction of lithium carbonate manufacturing facilities at Thacker Pass in Humboldt County, Nevada.
The loan includes $1.97 billion of principal and $289.7 million of capitalized interest.
The project is located next to a mine site containing the largest confirmed lithium resource in North America.
Once fully operational, the facility will produce approximately 40,000 tonnes per year of battery-grade lithium carbonate for lithium-ion batteries.

The announcement reinforces the Biden-Harris Administration’s whole-of-government approach to strengthening America's critical materials supply chain for clean transportation and national energy security.
The project is expected to create approximately 1,800 jobs during construction.
The project will support 360 permanent jobs in operations.
The project supports efforts to expand and secure reliable, sustainable domestic supply chains for critical materials to reach clean energy and climate goals.
The project aims to reduce reliance on economic competitors like China.
Lithium carbonate from Thacker Pass could support battery production for up to 800,000 electric vehicles annually.
The produced lithium carbonate could avoid the consumption of 317 million gallons of gasoline per year.
The project will benefit in part from union labor under President Biden’s Investing in America agenda.

The engineering, procurement, and construction management contractor entered into a National Construction Agreement (Project Labor Agreement) with North America’s Building Trades Unions for construction.

The Project Labor Agreement aims to minimize construction risk, ensure skilled labor availability, address remote location challenges, and prioritize employment of local and regional skilled craft workers.
The agreement prioritizes the employment of Native Americans and residents of under-represented communities.
The loan was issued through the Advanced Technology Vehicle Manufacturing (ATVM) Loan Program as a direct loan.
The project statistics were last updated in October 2024.
The loan amount represents the approximate amount approved at closing, including principal and any capitalized interest.
source_title: THACKER PASS | Department of Energy

facts: Educational webinars and handouts provide information about the benefits of electric school buses and examples of their use.
The resource series is titled "Flipping the Switch on Electric School Buses".
A step-by-step guide to the school bus electrification process is available in the Electric School Bus Planning Guide.
A technical assistance video series targets K-12 school districts interested in implementing electric school buses.
The series begins with an introduction to Clean Cities and Communities coalitions and discusses how they can provide education and technical assistance throughout implementation.
The introductory module covers key decision factors, charging infrastructure, and vehicle availability for electric school buses.

A webinar module introduces the Clean Cities and Communities partnership and explains how coalitions assist school districts in learning about electric school buses and connecting them with technical assistance.
Another module covers electric bus basics, key benefits and challenges, charging infrastructure basics, and vehicle availability.
Part 2 of the series focuses on working with electric utilities, providing guidance on starting conversations with utilities and assessing power needs for future charging infrastructure.
Associated handouts for Part 2 cover understanding various utility rate structures.
A webinar module discusses how to work with utility partners and incorporate power need considerations into electrification plans.
Another webinar module covers utility rate structures and how EV charging impacts monthly bills.
Part 3 focuses on vehicle requirements, covering currently available electric school bus models and cost factors.
Guidance is provided on analyzing vehicle routes to determine if electrification is a good option for a fleet.
The series covers vehicle range and efficiency considerations.
Information is provided on incentives, battery life, and other considerations like battery recycling.
A webinar module discusses currently available electric school bus vehicle models and vehicle cost factors.
Another webinar module covers route analysis, range, and efficiency considerations for fleet electrification.
A webinar module discusses incentives to help offset up-front costs, battery life, and battery recycling considerations.
Part 4 focuses on charging infrastructure, covering determining charging needs and selecting a charger.
The series provides information on important charging infrastructure installation considerations.
A webinar module covers how to determine charging needs and select a charger.
Another webinar module discusses charging infrastructure installation considerations.
Part 5 focuses on charging infrastructure planning and solutions, covering interconnection challenges and solutions.
The series provides an overview of vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology, including addressing its barriers and opportunities.

A webinar module covers electric school bus charging infrastructure interconnection challenges and solutions, including utility structure, grid and facility considerations, and possible interconnection solutions.
Another webinar module discusses V2G barriers and opportunities for electric school buses.
Part 6 focuses on vehicle in-use performance, covering how to track electric school bus performance and why it is important.
The series covers electric school bus energy use and fuel economy.
Information is provided on electric school bus range and reliability.
A webinar module discusses tracking performance for electric school buses.
Another webinar module covers electric school bus energy use and fuel economy.
A third webinar module covers electric school bus range and reliability.
Part 7 focuses on driver and technician training, providing an overview of how to provide training on electric school buses.
The training module discusses why training is important and outlines four basic levels of training.

Part 8 focuses on electric school bus cost factors, covering bus capital and infrastructure costs, operational and maintenance costs, incentives and financing options, and how to conduct financing discussions.
A webinar module covers bus capital costs and infrastructure costs.
Another webinar module discusses electric school bus operational costs.
A third webinar module covers electric school bus maintenance costs.
A fourth webinar module discusses electric school bus incentives and financing options.
A fifth webinar module covers how to have financing discussions for electric school buses.
The Clean School Bus Program under the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act (IIJA) provides $5 billion over 5 years for electric and alternative fuel buses.
Each year, $500 million will be provided exclusively for EV buses and $500 million for EV and other alternative fuel buses.
The IIJA also provides up to $300 million for a new Joint Office of Energy and Transportation to provide and coordinate technical assistance to states and communities.
In partnership with the National Laboratory of the Rockies and the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, the Joint Office is offering technical assistance on electric school bus deployment topics.
Technical assistance covers electric bus basics, charging equipment, utility connections, bus performance, and operational considerations like routing and maintenance.

entity_name: Georgia Alternative Fuel Laws and Incentives
canonical_name: State of Georgia Alternative Fuel Laws and Incentives
facts: Distributors who sell or use motor fuel, including special fuels, are subject to an excise tax of $0.26 per gallon, which may be adjusted based on vehicle efficiencies and the consumer price index.
Motor fuels that are not commonly sold or measured by the gallon and are used in motor vehicles on public highways are taxed according to their gasoline gallon equivalent (GGE).
Effective January 1, 2027, a GGE of electricity may not exceed 11 kilowatt-hours.
Effective January 1, 2027, a GGE of hydrogen must be at least 2.2 pounds.
Effective January 1, 2027, a GGE of compressed natural gas (CNG) must be at least 110,000 British thermal units.
Effective January 1, 2027, a GGE of liquefied natural gas (LNG) must be at least 6.06 pounds.
CNG is defined as a mixture of hydrocarbon gases and vapors, consisting principally of methane in gaseous form that has been compressed for use as a motor fuel.
LNG is defined as methane or natural gas in the form of a cryogenic or refrigerated liquid for use as a motor fuel.
Propane and special fuels sold in bulk to a licensed consumer distributor are exempt from this tax.
The excise tax for electricity begins January 1, 2027, and only applies to electricity sold at a public electric vehicle (EV) charging stations.
The Georgia Department of Revenue may assess, levy, and collect tax for any other motor fuels used on public highways using a GGE rate.
An income tax credit is available for businesses for 10% of the cost to convert a vehicle to natural gas, electricity, propane, and hydrogen, up to $2,500 per vehicle.
Converted vehicles must be fueled solely by an alternative fuel and must meet the emissions standards for low-emission or zero emission vehicles defined by the Board of Natural Resources.
If not used during any taxable year, this tax credit may be carried forward for up to three years.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Hydrogen Laws and Incentives in Georgia

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp. (DAS), an automotive parts company, will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter.
The new facility will create 300 new jobs.
The project will generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
The initiative will involve collaborative indirect investments.
DAS was established in Korea in 1987 and specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.

Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS, stated that the strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in the decision-making process.
Metter was deemed an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub due to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
The facility will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations at the new facility are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will recruit for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.

Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated that the City of Metter is proud to welcome DAS and noted the impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving socioeconomic wellbeing.
Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing Candler County as its new home to spur more economic development.

Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated that the authority is proud to support the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state and work together to drive economic activity and jobs.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was executed in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network as a result of years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.

Over the past century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned itself as a leader in developing emerging technologies for the automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
The announcement was made on Sept. 6, 2023.
source_title: DAS Corp. to Bring 300 Automotive Jobs to Metter – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

facts: The Paducah Gaseous Diffusion Plant (PGDP) was constructed in 1952 to produce enriched uranium, initially for the nation’s nuclear weapons program and later for nuclear fuel for commercial power plants.

The plant is owned by the Department of Energy (DOE), which oversees environmental cleanup activities at the site, including environmental remediation, waste management, depleted uranium conversion, and decontamination and decommissioning.
Commercial enrichment was conducted under lease from 1993 until 2013 when operations ceased and the gaseous diffusion facilities were returned to the DOE Environmental Management (EM) program.

EM has conducted extensive cleanup activities at the site since the late 1980s and is currently deactivating the returned plant facilities while continuing the aggressive remediation program being managed by its Portsmouth/Paducah Project Office.

On June 23, 2026, members of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Office of Environmental Management workforce discussed job opportunities with area teachers and administrators during a tour of the Paducah Site.
On June 2, 2026, incoming next-generation workforce members at the Paducah Site will undergo new training by longtime employees on the site’s past uranium enrichment operations.

On May 26, 2026, two universities in Ohio and Kentucky began collaborating to build STEM interest in local high school students and inspire them to pursue careers at the DOE’s Portsmouth and Paducah sites.

On May 19, 2026, two educators in communities near the DOE’s Portsmouth, Ohio, and Paducah, Kentucky, locations started maximizing resources to cultivate next-generation talent to advance the nation’s energy sector and future redevelopment at the DOE sites.
source_title: Paducah Site | Department of Energy

canonical_name: Georgia Point Source Air Emissions Inventory (EI)
facts: Facilities whose potential to emit (PTE) emissions equal or exceed specific thresholds for pollutants including SO2, VOC, NOx, CO, Pb, PM10, PM2.5, and NH3 are required to submit data for the 2025 calendar year.
The 2025 EI is an annual reporting year with higher thresholds than a triennial year.
Facilities identified by the Air Protection Branch (APB) as potentially needing to participate will receive process information by email by the beginning of January.
Facilities must opt out or submit their 2025 EI data by the deadline of May 31, 2026.
The Emission Inventory is required by federal regulation 40 CFR Part 51 Subpart A and Georgia Rule 391-3-1-.02(6)(b)1.

The Point Source Air Emissions Inventory consists of two parts: the Facility Inventory (FI), which contains relatively static emission units and release points, and the Emission Inventory (EI), which contains changing process details, operating parameters, and emissions.

Georgia collects air emission inventory data using the Combined Air Emissions Reporting System (CAERS), an EPA-developed application, replacing the former "Georgia Environmental Connections Online" (GECO) system since 2019.

The submission process requires facilities to update contact information via GECO, determine participation status in CAERS via the EPA Central Data Exchange (CDX), and submit/certify an opt-out form or the 2025 EI in CAERS.
Facilities that did not operate from January 1 to December 31, 2025, do not need to submit an opt-out form but must still update contact information and answer prompts in CAERS.
The "preparer" role creates and prepares reports on the EPA CDX platform but cannot certify the submission, while the "certifier" is the authorized facility representative who submits the report.
Potential to emit (PTE) is based on the latest applicable permit, with sources including permits, permit narratives, and Title V applications.
Important dates include: GECO application posting on Dec. 1, 2025; GA Kickoff Training on Dec. 10, 2025; CAERS opening on Jan. 20, 2026; and the May 31, 2026 deadline for report submission.
Virtual help sessions are provided on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 10:30 AM starting in March 2026.

As of June 19, 2023, Georgia Rule 391-3-1-.02(6)(a)4. was removed, eliminating the requirement for facilities with NOx and/or VOC actual emissions greater than 25 tons per year in specific counties to submit annual Emissions Statement data.
source_title: Point Source Emissions Inventory | Environmental Protection Division

facts: Hyundai Motor Manufacturing Middle East (HMMME) is a joint venture between PIF, which owns a 70% stake, and Hyundai Motor Company, which holds the remaining 30%.
The new facility celebrated its groundbreaking ceremony in the King Salman Automotive Cluster within King Abdullah Economic City (KAEC).
The plant will manufacture internal combustion engine (ICE) and electric vehicles (EV).
The manufacturing plant targets an annual production of 50,000 vehicles to supply Saudi Arabia.
This facility represents Hyundai's first manufacturing plant in the Middle East.
The plant is scheduled to roll out its first vehicle by the fourth quarter of 2026.

Yazeed A. Alhumied, Deputy Governor and Head of MENA Investments at PIF, stated that the groundbreaking strengthens Saudi Arabia's automotive industry and underscores PIF's commitment to building local capabilities, attracting cutting-edge technology, and creating highly skilled jobs in the automotive and mobility sector.

Jaehoon Chang, Hyundai Motor Group Vice Chair, said the groundbreaking marks a new chapter for future mobility and technological innovation, aiming to contribute to regional talent development under Saudi Vision 2030.
Wongyun Park, Vice President and CEO of HMMME, stated that the facility will become a platform for growth and industrial excellence in the Kingdom.
The manufacturing plant will create thousands of jobs and facilitate knowledge transfer and skills development by harnessing local workforce skills.
The localization of Hyundai's vehicles will accelerate the growth of Saudi Arabia's automotive and mobility ecosystem.

This partnership is part of a series of PIF initiatives establishing Saudi Arabia as a global automotive player, driving transformation in the sector and boosting domestic manufacturing capabilities, infrastructure, and supply chains.

facts: Hyundai Motor Group concluded CES 2026 with major announcements and industry recognition underscoring its leadership in human-centered, AI-driven robotics and Physical AI.
The Group’s CES 2026 activities focused on strategy, real-world deployment, product validation, and global partnerships with AI leaders under the theme ‘Partnering Human Progress’.
At Hyundai Media Day, Hyundai Motor Group unveiled its AI Robotics Strategy to lead the emerging Physical AI industry.
The Group unveiled the product version of Boston Dynamics' new Atlas robot during a presentation that featured a live demonstration of the Atlas prototype version.

Atlas is planned for phased deployment beginning with sequencing tasks at Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) in Savannah, Georgia, with expansion to additional processes following validation.
CNET Group named Atlas a Best of CES 2026 Winner.
Hyundai Motor Group Robotics LAB’s MobED (Mobile Eccentric Droid) earned the CES 2026 ‘Best of Innovation’ Award in Robotics.
Mass production of both MobED Pro and MobED Basic models begins in the first quarter of 2026.
MobED addresses research, logistics, delivery, and mobility use cases.
At CES Foundry 2026, the Group’s Robotics LAB announced significant advancements in Physical AI following its partnership with AI semiconductor specialist DEEPX.
The “edge brain” chip will be applied across future robots and AI security and delivery platforms.
Hyundai Motor Group is establishing capabilities to scale human-centered AI robotics and Physical AI applications through its Group-wide value chain and diversified product portfolio.

By capturing and applying real-world data across manufacturing, logistics, and sales, Hyundai Motor Group is creating a continuous feedback loop that strengthens AI learning, improves product performance, and accelerates deployment.
This data-driven approach positions the Group to extend its expertise beyond mobility into robotics and other Physical AI-enabled industries.
Hyundai Motor Group has about 250,000 employees worldwide.
The Group’s mobility brands include Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis.

facts: A Korean auto supplier will invest $10 million and hire 285 people northeast of Atlanta to make parts that will accompany electric vehicle batteries that SK Innovation will produce nearby.
Duckyang Industrial Co. said its plant in Braselton will make battery modules and energy storage systems for SK Innovation's $2.6 billion, 2,600-worker plant in Commerce.
Duckyang did not disclose when it would start production at the new facility.
The company will occupy a 230,000-square foot (21,000-square meter) building in an industrial park near an Amazon warehouse.
The company already supplies cockpit modules, a dashboard assembly of more than 130 parts that includes the steering wheel, instruments and electronics, to automakers including Kia and Hyundai.
Kia has a factory in West Point and Hyundai has a factory in Montgomery, Alabama.

Duckyang CEO Dong-in Son stated that entering the U.S. electric vehicle battery market with SKBA will provide an opportunity to cooperate with many other automotive companies, including Hyundai and Kia Motors, that already have a manufacturing base in the U.S.
The company hopes to supply interior parts and electrical vehicle parts from Braselton.
Duckyang could claim various tax breaks, including an income tax credit allowing it to annually deduct $1,250 per job from state income taxes, up to 50% of its tax liability.
These potential savings could save the company up to $1.8 million over five years, as long as workers make at least $28,000 per year.
Georgia will provide job training for the project but will not provide any discretionary incentives, according to Gordon.
Braselton and Jackson County could give property tax breaks to the company.
Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson said the plant shows how a flagship company like SK Innovation can bring other suppliers in its wake.
Wilson stated that Georgia is focused on growing the entire electric mobility supply chain.
source_title: Korean firm to invest $10M, hire 285, to make battery parts - Yahoo News UK

facts: Rockefeller Group recently completed two distribution buildings totaling 427,658 square feet on 39 acres in Braselton, Ga.
Braselton 85 Phase II is located at Jesse Cronic Road and Highway 124 along Atlanta’s I-85 Northeast industrial corridor.
The development includes two rear-load distribution buildings totaling 207,009 square feet and 220,649 square feet respectively.
Both buildings feature 32′ clear ceiling heights and 52′ x 50′ column spacing.
The site provides ample auto and trailer parking.
Braselton’s location along I-85 offers a distribution channel to Atlanta’s population of more than 6.2 million people and all major Southeastern population centers.
Rockefeller Group sold the 207,009 square foot building to Great Lakes Technologies (“GLT”), a WEN Company.
GLT will utilize the new state-of-art facility for warehousing and distribution purposes.
The acquisition marks an expansion of GLT in the Southeast region.
This business park represents Rockefeller Group’s second development in Braselton.
Rockefeller Group previously completed and sold Braselton 85 Phase I in 2021, a 222,618-square-foot distribution center located one mile from the new project.
Braselton 85 Phase I was leased to Duckyang America Inc., a Korean automotive equipment supplier, before being sold to Cabot Properties.
source_title: About - Braselton 85 Phase II

facts: The newly-built Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America has an investment cost of $7.6 billion.
The plant is located in Georgia and boasts the largest automobile manufacturing space in the United States.
Two EVs, the Ioniq 5 and Ioniq 9 SUVs, are currently produced at the Metaplant.
Future production plans include adding other brands like Kia and Genesis to the facility.
Each vehicle goes through 23 AI-based or robotic processes using a blend of human labor and high-tech systems.
Approximately 850 robots plus AI are used for quality control inspection and vehicle movement at the plant.
Robots such as “Spot” are used to inspect vehicles at the facility.
Drones are used to scan and track inventory at the plant.
AI is used to troubleshoot and identify root causes for early corrective action, which lessens defective units, scrap, and warranty costs.
Vehicle assembly is handled by robotics and AI, resulting in more consistent quality and fewer errors per vehicle.
Humans write the programs for the robotics and AI to help identify defective vehicles long before they leave the manufacturing plant.
By 2031, Hyundai plans to employ 8,500 workers to handle robotics maintenance, oversee data analysis, and install parts on vehicles.
So far, about 1,000 full-time workers have been hired at the plant.
Human workers are freed from mundane, repetitive tasks to focus on higher-order activities like programming robots or interpreting production data.
Humans perform tasks that robots cannot efficiently do, such as handling thin and flexible materials.
A CNBC Television video dated March 26, 2025, announced the plant's plan to hire 8,500 people.
A 11Alive video dated March 27, 2025, covered Hyundai opening the new metaplant in Georgia.
A WJCL News video dated March 26, 2025, reported on Hyundai cutting the ribbon on the new Bryan County plant and announcing an expansion.
A WJCL News video dated May 13, 2025, covered humanoid robots coming to the Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan County.
The content is based on a Business Insider article titled "Hyundai built its newest factory around AI-powered technology like robotics and digital twins" by Ben Shimkus, published August 20, 2025.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant’s AI and Human Processes – OM in the News

facts: Georgia is on an aggressive trajectory to advance a full electric vehicle (EV) supply chain, with significant activity occurring in the past year alone.
Ascend Elements recently launched an EV battery recycling plant in Georgia.
Several EV and vehicle battery manufacturers announced plans to locate in Georgia around the same time as Ascend Elements' launch.
Korean-based SungEel HiTech is preparing to establish operations in Georgia.
SungEel HiTech will invest $37 million in a new battery recycling plant under its subsidiary, SungEel Recycling Park Georgia.
Georgia state officials and SungEel HiTech declined to provide interviews regarding the venture.
Georgia reports that more than 30 “EV-related” projects have brought in about $13.3 billion in investments since 2020.
The state is home to operations from about 23 e-mobility companies.
Georgia has more than 1,200 miles of federally designated EV-ready alternative fuel corridors along its interstates and highways.
Georgia has plans for expansion leveraging $135 million in expected funding from the Biden Administration’s Bipartisan Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act.
SungEel's new plant will recycle end-of-life batteries and battery manufacturing scrap, including nickel, cobalt, and lithium.
SungEel utilizes a recycling process with a reported greater-than 95 percent metal recovery rate.
SungEel's plant will be located at the Hayestone Brady Business Park in Toccoa, GA.
The SungEel plant is projected to generate 104 jobs.
The facility is certified under the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) program, indicating it has been vetted and approved for fast-track development.
SungEel's operation has a slated launch date of 2024.
Suk Jae Yim, representative of SungEel Recycling Park Georgia, described SungEel's entry as “The last piece of the puzzle to build a sustainable ecosystem of Georgia’s electric vehicle supply chain.”
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the proximity of SungEel's new plant to other members of Georgia’s electric mobility ecosystem will be key to further success.
School bus manufacturer Blue Bird rolled out an all-electric bus in 2018.
EV parts manufacturer Solvay produces thermoplastic composites in the region.
YKKUSA manufactures fasteners for EV interiors in the region.
Hyundai Motor Group reportedly plans to break ground on an EV and battery manufacturing facility in Georgia in January 2023.
The Hyundai facility is expected to operate at full capacity of about 300,000 units no later than mid-2025.
EV manufacturer Rivian has sited a plant outside of Atlanta.
The Rivian plant is reported to begin production in 2024.
The Rivian plant will have an annual capacity of 400,000 vehicles.
Incentives and policies are driving much of the activity, including an electric vehicle supply equipment (EVSE) tax credit for businesses to buy and install public infrastructure.
Georgia offers rebates for installing Level 2 chargers.
A “plug-in electric vehicle charging” rate incentive is available for residents to encourage nighttime and very early-morning EV charging.
Much of the growth is attributed to Korean companies setting up operations in the region, including Enchem, Duckyang, and EcoPro BM.
Enchem, Duckyang, and EcoPro BM either manufacture technology around batteries and energy storage systems or supply materials like nickel cathode.
Much of the activity is spurred by SK Battery’s expansion into Georgia.
SK Battery America's $2.6 billion facility is now in operation while construction continues.
The SK Battery America facility will eventually produce enough gigawatt hours annually to power 430,000 vehicles.

Roger Lin, Ascend's vice president of Marketing and Government Relations, stated that Georgia was selected primarily because of its growing EV and battery ecosystem and the essential proximity to partners like SK Battery America.
Ascend Elements' new Georgia plant is the largest of its kind in North America.
The Ascend Elements plant can recycle 30,000 tons of lithium-ion battery materials and scrap per year.
State officials anticipate an ongoing accelerated growth pattern for the state's EV supply chain.
Governor Brian Kemp launched the Electric Mobility and Innovation Alliance (EMIA) in 2021.
EMIA is a statewide initiative between the public and private sectors and nonprofits to further scale electrification in Georgia.
source_title: Georgia Ramps up Electrification Supply Chain

The mark was published for opposition on December 24, 2024.
The company custom manufactures components and materials for lithium-ion batteries, namely electrolytes, carbon nanotubes (CNTs), conductive additives for electrolytes, and lithium salts.
The company recycles solvents used in the production of lithium-ion batteries, specifically N-Methyl-2-Pyrrolidone (NMP).
The trademark covers international class 001 for chemicals used in industry and class 040 for treatment of materials.
Josephine A. Brosas at Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP in Los Angeles, CA serves as the legal correspondent for the mark.
source_title: Trademarks On Call : ENCHEM MATERIALS, INC.

facts: South Korean battery materials manufacturer Enchem Ltd. has confirmed plans to make an investment of more than USD 60 million in Jackson County, Georgia.
The project will include two new manufacturing plants.
The project will create more than 300 jobs.
Governor Kemp welcomed Enchem to Georgia, citing the project as exactly the type of quality project the state hoped to attract during its first international trip to Korea last June.
Governor Kemp noted that the industry is building a hub in Georgia as the market for electric vehicles and batteries continues its rapid growth.
Governor Kemp stated that strong international partnerships between South Korea and Georgia will continue to benefit Georgians and create opportunities throughout the state.
Enchem was founded in 2012.
Enchem specializes in the development and manufacturing of electrolytes for rechargeable batteries and electrostatic double-layer capacitors (ELDCs).
Enchem is the first announced supplier for SK Innovation.
SK Innovation broke ground on its USD 1.67 billion battery manufacturing plant in Jackson County in March 2019.
Enchem America CEO Oh, Jung Kang stated that the company is excited to build its factory in Georgia and will benefit from its infrastructure, transportation, and excellent weather.

GDEcD Director of Korean Investment Yoonie Kim represented the Global Commerce Division on this project in partnership with the Jackson County Development Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson congratulated Enchem on its decision to invest in Georgia.
GDEcD has maintained strategic representation in Korea since 1985 to demonstrate ongoing commitment to the Korean market and community.
GDEcD relies on partnerships and programs like Georgia Quick Start to ensure workforce and company success.
In fiscal year 2019, South Korea was the top player in both foreign direct investment and jobs in Georgia, investing USD 1.87 billion and creating 2,507 jobs.
source_title: Korea’s Enchem planning two sites in Georgia

Enchem America Inc was founded in 2019.
Enchem America Inc headquarters are located in Commerce, Georgia.
Enchem America Inc reported revenues of $380 MM in 2024.

facts: Enchem is a global electrolyte specialist.
On June 30, Enchem announced a clear trend of sales growth in the second quarter of this year.
Q2 sales growth was driven by expanded electrolyte shipments to major customers in North America.
Expectations for improved performance are rising in the global battery industry due to gradual recovery in demand for electric vehicle batteries.
Demand is also increasing for batteries used in energy storage systems (ESS) and artificial intelligence (AI) data centers.
Enchem is increasing its electrolyte supply volume in line with production expansion of major customers in North America.
Shipments to North American customers in the second quarter fueled sales growth, resulting in a year-on-year increase in overall revenue.
Enchem completed production of the electrolyte volumes scheduled for supply in June and fulfilled most shipments according to customer delivery schedules.
The company expects its second-quarter sales to increase significantly compared to the same period last year.
Specific figures for sales and earnings will be confirmed after final shipments, customer inspections, accounting settlements, and external auditor reviews are completed.
Sales expansion is driven by both market price increases from higher lithium salt prices and actual increases in electrolyte supply volume.
Production capacity utilization at Enchem’s North American facilities is rising in tandem with production expansion by North American customers.
Growing electrolyte shipments are leading to a gradual easing of fixed cost burdens.
Profitability is expected to improve due to fixed cost dilution from increased sales.
The company anticipates efficiency gains in production, procurement, and logistics costs will contribute to a reduction in operating losses compared to the previous quarter.
Strengthened inventory and working capital management is expected to help reduce operating losses.
Tariff refunds related to its North American business are expected to contribute to reduced operating losses.
Enchem continues to monitor cost structure and management resource operations, focusing on plant utilization rates and customer-specific profitability.
In the second half of the year, normalization of production at major customers’ plants in North America is expected to serve as an additional growth driver.
Expanded supply to leading Chinese battery companies is also expected to be a growth driver in the second half.

If plant operations at certain customers—whose production was temporarily halted or adjusted in the first half—return to normal, electrolyte shipments to those customers are expected to resume gradually.

In China, Enchem is working to transition new customers who have undergone sample supply, technology verification, quality assessment, supplier registration, and plant audits to mass production supply.
Enchem plans to sustain sales growth and earnings improvement in the second half by recovering shipments to existing North American customers and expanding supply to new customers in China.
If Q2 revenue growth and reduced operating loss trends continue into the second half, the company’s foundation for reaching a quarterly break-even point (BEP) will be further strengthened.
The actual timing for achieving BEP may vary depending on customers’ production plans, order volumes, delivery and inspection schedules, raw material prices, exchange rates, and tariff refund timing.
An Enchem representative stated that in Q2, actual supply volume increased to major customers in North America and China, resulting in substantial year-on-year sales expansion.
The representative added that with higher capacity utilization and cost efficiency gains, they expect notable improvement in their earnings structure compared to the previous quarter.
A spokesperson stated that in H2, normalization of major customers’ production and expanded supply to new customers will be reflected sequentially.
The company will focus on managing utilization rates and customer-specific profitability to ensure sales growth leads to tangible profitability improvements.
source_title: Enchem Expands North American Shipments Amid Battery Market Recovery... Q2 Growth Trend - The Asia Business Daily

facts: Enchem is a leading electrolyte manufacturer in Korea strengthening its global market strategy in North America and Europe despite ongoing losses.
In the United States, Enchem is increasing supplies of energy storage system (ESS) products.
In Europe and China, Enchem aims to boost supplies for electric vehicle batteries, expecting a rebound in performance.
Electrolyte is considered one of the four essential materials for batteries, along with cathode materials, anode materials, and separators.
The electrolyte acts as a substance that allows lithium ions within the battery to move freely between the cathode and anode, stabilizing the surfaces and enhancing battery life and performance.
Enchem is expanding its production of lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery electrolytes in North America.
Demand for LFP batteries used in electric vehicles and ESS is increasing, driving rising supplies to joint ventures like LG Energy Solution and General Motors (GM).
This year, Enchem is expected to supply approximately 13,000 tons (t) of electrolyte for ESS.
Next year, this figure is estimated to increase by more than 360% to 60,000t.
Established in 2012 as a latecomer in the electrolyte market, Enchem now has the largest production capacity in Korea.
In the global market, Enchem fluctuates between the 3rd and 4th places, with all competitors being Chinese companies.
Major clients for Enchem include LG Energy Solution, SK On, Tesla, and Panasonic, with speculation about potential collaboration with China's CATL.
Enchem holds the top market share in North America and has focused on local production since its entry in 2019, primarily centered around its Georgia plant.
The annual production capacity of the Georgia plant is about 105,000t, with plans to expand it to 150,000t by the end of this year and to 200,000t by next year.
Additional production facilities will be operated in Tennessee (250,000t), Indiana (250,000t), and Ontario, Canada (250,000t).

Among domestic battery material corporations, Enchem is the only company receiving benefits from the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) in the form of advanced manufacturing production credits (AMPC).
Enchem is expected to receive over 17 billion won this year following 16.2 billion won last year.

AMPC is a subsidy paid to corporations producing batteries and solar power in the U.S., and Enchem's electrolyte has been recognized as an item subject to AMPC specifically classified as 'active materials for electrodes.'
Enchem is expanding its presence in the European electric vehicle battery market, currently operating production facilities in Poland and Hungary.
Enchem plans to begin supplying to a large-scale battery plant in France starting at the end of this month.
In Dunkirk, France, there are plans to build a plant with an annual production capacity of approximately 135,000t, aiming for completion next year.
Despite ongoing losses due to sluggish electric vehicle market conditions, profitability is expected to recover once supply expansion in North America and Europe gains full momentum.
At the end of last year, Enchem reported an operating loss of 65.3 billion won, shifting from a profit of 3 billion won in 2023.
Enchem reported a first-quarter loss this year amounting to 19.2 billion won.
The net loss for last year was 571.2 billion won, more than ten times larger than the previous year's net loss of 50.1 billion won.
The estimated usage of electrolytes is about 1,000t per 1 gigawatt hour (GWh) of battery capacity.

According to market research firm SNE Research, the demand for electrolytes was about 1.42 million tons last year and is projected to rise to 4.46 million tons by 2034 with an expected annual growth rate (CAGR) of approximately 11%.
source_title: Enchem expands electrolyte production in North America and Europe despite losses - CHOSUNBIZ

facts: Enchem America, Inc. planned to build a $152 million electrolyte manufacturing facility in Haywood County.
The planned facility would have brought 190 jobs to Haywood County.
Enchem America announced its plan to build on the “shovel ready” I-40 Advantage Industrial Park in Brownsville in 2023.

The site is roughly 16 miles from BlueOval City, a massive electric vehicle manufacturing campus slated to produce Ford Motor Company’s new all-electric pickup truck and electric vehicle batteries in partnership with SK On.

The BlueOval City campus is expected to serve as manufacturing space for BlueOval SK, Ford and electric vehicle component suppliers, injecting jobs and residents into the surrounding rural West Tennessee communities.

The Enchem facility would have produced electrolytes used for electric vehicle batteries, and was expected to magnify the company’s ability to supply and support its electric vehicle partners in the United States.
Enchem decided to withdraw from the project in mid-September 2025 after careful consideration and an extensive review of their North American business strategy.
The withdrawal decision was primarily driven by a recent shift in demand from their key North American customers.

Ford Motor Company announced it will delay mass production of the next-generation pickup truck at BlueOval City until 2028, though Ford still plans to start producing prototypes of the truck there in 2027.
Brownsville-Haywood Economic Development Corporation Executive Director Aaron Stewart is working to terminate the state incentives Enchem was set to receive for the project.
The company received a $2 million recruitment grant in 2023, according to a state database.
Stewart will work with the state and the Tennessee Valley Authority, which has contributed grant funds to prepare the industrial park for future development, to put the site back on the market.
Enchem completed preliminary site grading but had not yet begun construction.
The site preparation was part of the state’s Select Tennessee Certified Sites program.
More details about the site’s future will be discussed at the Brownsville-Haywood Economic Development Corporation board meeting on Oct. 8.

Stewart highlighted the $5.6 billion investment in the BlueOval City campus, noting nearly 11 million square feet under a roof with machinery and equipment still being installed and calibrated at a slower pace.
source_title: EV battery component company backs out of plans for $152M Haywood County facility • Tennessee Lookout

facts: A South Korean battery component maker has backed out of plans to build a $152.5 million electrolyte manufacturing facility.
The facility would have brought 190 jobs to Haywood County in West Tennessee.
Enchem America announced its plan to build on the "shovel ready" I-40 Advantage Industrial Park in Brownsville in 2023.
source_title: EV battery component company backs out of plans for $152M West Tennessee facility | Chattanooga Times Free Press

facts: On May 28, 2026, GrowHub Limited signed a legally binding summary term sheet with EnChem America for a reverse triangular merger.
Under the deal structure, EnChem will merge into a newly formed GrowHub subsidiary and become the surviving entity.

EnChem’s shareholders are slated to receive 85% of the combined company’s equity on a fully diluted basis, leaving existing GrowHub holders with 15%, subject to customary regulatory and shareholder approvals, including Nasdaq rules on new share issuance.
The deal values EnChem at a minimum of $400 million and treats it as the accounting acquirer.
The transaction represents a transformative reverse takeover that would effectively pivot GrowHub into the battery electrolyte materials sector.
The structure includes a valuation floor that allows EnChem to walk away if GrowHub’s trading price falls below $4 before closing.
The deal is designed to support substantial post-merger capital raising via a large shelf registration and follow-on financings once the combined company is in place.
GrowHub Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated holding company listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol TGHL and reporting as a foreign private issuer.

GrowHub serves as a public vehicle for corporate combinations and capital-raising transactions and is now positioning itself as the listed platform for EnChem America, a U.S. subsidiary of Korea’s EnChem Co., focused on battery electrolyte materials.
source_title: GrowHub Signs Binding Reverse Merger Deal With EnChem America - The Globe and Mail

facts: Enchem announced on January 25 that it has consecutively secured product supply contracts with various global top-tier customers in the North American market, including K-battery companies LG Energy Solution and SK On.

Since establishing its local subsidiary, Enchem America, in 2019, Enchem has continued a bold “proactive investment” strategy and succeeded in establishing itself as a key material company in the North American secondary battery market.
In 2020, Enchem purchased the Toyota plant site located in Georgia and completed its first North American factory within two years.
Through this acquisition, Enchem became the only domestic company at the time to establish a large-scale “local production-local supply” system.

Enchem rapidly grew based on its existing large-scale factory operation know-how and large-scale production capacity (CAPA) capable of immediate response, currently leading electrolyte supply within the North American market.
Enchem’s Georgia plant has an annual electrolyte production capacity of 40,000 tons, making it the largest electrolyte factory in North America.
The Georgia plant site covers approximately 350,000 pyeong (1,141,000㎡), which is one-quarter the size of Yeouido.
A 20,000-ton annual capacity NMP plant is also located at the Georgia site.
Enchem plans to initially expand the Georgia plant to an annual capacity of 100,000 tons by the end of this year.

Subsequently, Enchem aims to increase production capacity to a total of 200,000 tons by the end of next year, covering the entire North American region until the completion of a second plant currently planned for construction in the western region.
An Enchem official explained that the Georgia plant was the greatest competitive advantage that allowed Enchem to rapidly expand its market share in North America at an early stage.
The official added that the Georgia plant remains the only large-scale electrolyte factory in the North American market, serving as a strong entry barrier.

The official noted that the Georgia plant has been receiving more attention following the disclosure of detailed guidelines for the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) Foreign Excluded Overseas Concern (FEOC).
The official emphasized that the already secured large-scale expansion site, automated processes, and unparalleled large-scale factory operation capabilities are invaluable assets unique to Enchem.
Enchem’s Georgia plant began initial electrolyte supply to SK On’s Georgia plant in March last year.
The Georgia plant started supplying products to the Ohio plant of Ultium Cells, a joint venture between LG Energy Solution and GM, in August of the same year.

This year, Enchem is preparing to supply products to a Japanese battery company located in Nevada, BlueOval (Kentucky), a joint venture between SK On and Ford, and Ultium Cells’ second plant in Tennessee.
Enchem is pursuing a “pinpoint strategy” focused on the U.S. East and West coasts to establish a more efficient local production and supply system.
This reflects Enchem’s strategic intention to further segment the North American market and implement construction and expansion plans accompanied by customer-tailored sales strategies.

The company explained that since it is currently securing early references through a first-mover advantage in the North American market, operational strategies will also become a crucial point going forward.

A company representative added that Enchem aims to rapidly expand its North American market reach under a site strategy centered on the main Georgia plant, covering the eastern, western, and northern regions.
The representative added that in addition to the Tennessee plant currently underway, Enchem is also considering building an additional electrolyte plant in Ontario, Canada.
source_title: Enchem's Aggressive Expansion in North American Secondary Battery Market... Smooth Progress in Market Share Growth - The Asia Business Daily

facts: Tennessee Gov. Bill Lee, Department of Economic and Community Development Commissioner Stuart McWhorter, and Enchem America Inc. officials announced that Enchem America Inc. will locate US manufacturing operations in Brownsville.
The announcement was made on October 1, 2018.
source_title: Governor Lee, Commissioner McWhorter Announce Enchem America Inc. to Locate US Manufacturing Operations in Brownsville | The Mckenzie Banner

facts: On June 15, Enchem announced that its U.S. subsidiary is currently undergoing the tariff refund process under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA).
The total IEEPA tariff refund amount filed by the U.S. subsidiary is $5.45 million (approximately KRW 800 million).
Approximately $3.44 million (about KRW 500 million), or around 63% of the filed amount, has already been refunded.
The remaining tariff refund amount is expected to be refunded in sequence according to U.S. government administrative procedures.
This refund measure follows a ruling by the U.S. Court of International Trade (CIT).
The alleviated tariff burden from local distribution and import processes is expected to contribute to improved financial soundness and cash flow for the U.S. subsidiary.
Enchem's U.S. subsidiary secured accumulated Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (AMPC) benefits from 2023 to 2025 amounting to approximately $29.78 million (about KRW 4.5 billion).
An additional $15 million (about KRW 2.3 billion) in AMPC benefits is expected to be received this year.
The cumulative four-year AMPC benefit is projected to reach a total of $45 million (about KRW 6.8 billion).
The North American battery market is expanding beyond electric vehicles (EVs) to include the energy storage system (ESS) sector.
Explosive expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) data centers and increased investment in power infrastructure are driving a surge in ESS demand across North America.
The surge in ESS demand is fueling the need for long-life and high-safety electrolytes, which are key factors determining battery lifespan and safety.
Enchem plans to accelerate its entry into the ESS market by leveraging the competitiveness it has already established in the electrolyte market for EVs.

An Enchem representative stated that the tariff refunds and AMPC benefits resulted from a global supply chain strategy involving calculated risks and proactively establishing a local electrolyte production system in Georgia, United States.
The U.S. battery industry increasingly values transparency in raw material sourcing and supply chain stability centered on allied nations.
According to the Enchem representative, the value of its local production base in Georgia will continue to rise as these industry priorities grow.
source_title: Enchem to Receive $8 Million in U.S. Tariff Refunds... Cumulative Tax Benefits Expected to Reach $68 Million - The Asia Business Daily

facts: F&P Georgia MFG is located at 88 Enterprise Dr SE, Rome, GA 30161-3525, US.
The company specializes in manufacturing subframes, modular assemblies, lower arms, trailing arms, and various stampings.
F&P Georgia MFG is currently listed as closed.
The company leverages advanced manufacturing techniques and thorough training for its associates.
F&P Georgia focuses on continuous improvement and technological advancement to set industry standards.
The company emphasizes a culture of diversity and respect to support customers from product development through mass production.
source_title: F&P Georgia MFG, 88 Enterprise Dr SE, Rome, GA 30161-3525, US - MapQuest

facts: F & P Georgia Manufacturing Inc. is a Tier 1 automotive parts supplier based in Rome, Georgia.
The company disclosed a data breach affecting 12,132 individuals across the U.S., including 22 residents of Indiana.
The company detected suspicious activity on its network and confirmed that an unauthorized actor accessed and exfiltrated files between June 24 and July 2, 2025.
Exposed data included Social Security numbers and driver’s license information, raising concerns about potential identity theft.
F & P Georgia reported the breach to the Indiana Attorney General on April 20, 2026.
F & P Georgia reported the breach to the Massachusetts Office of Consumer Affairs and Business Regulation on April 20, 2026.
The company began notifying affected individuals on April 20, 2026.
As part of its response, the company is offering 12 months of free credit monitoring and identity restoration services through Cyberscout.
The offered services include single-bureau credit monitoring, credit reports, and real-time alerts for credit file changes.
Affected individuals must enroll in the services within 90 days of receiving their notification letter using a unique activation code.
For further assistance, the company established a dedicated helpline and a mailing address at 88 Enterprise Drive, Acworth, GA 30101.
The breach underscores the ongoing risks of cyber threats in the manufacturing sector and the importance of timely breach notifications.
The investigation into the breach has been completed.
source_title: F & P Georgia Manufacturing Inc.: F & P Georgia Data Breach Affects 12,132 Individuals

A Facility Maintenance position is open in the Operations department.
A Maintenance Equipment Technician position is open in the Operations department.

There are six total job openings listed on the page.
source_title: Job Openings - F&P Georgia

facts: F&P Georgia announced a $22 million expansion to its Rome facility.
The expansion is driven by contracts with automakers Honda and Tesla.
F&P's facility is located on 51 acres of the Floyd County Industrial Park.
F&P operates as a tier one automotive supplier.
This expansion is only the most recent in a series of expansions at the company’s Rome plant.
The expansion will add an additional 25 jobs to the company's current workforce.
Nat Massey, senior manager at F&P Manufacturing, confirmed the addition of 25 jobs.
The Rome-Floyd County Development Authority signed off on backing the expansion and a 10-year tax abatement on Monday.
F&P will manufacture suspension components for Tesla's Cyber Truck, which will be made in Austin, Texas.
Nat Massey stated, “We are very excited about this new venture with Tesla and our continued work with Honda.”
Plant Manager Bruce Critchlow has been with F&P since its beginnings in Floyd County.
Bruce Critchlow thanked Rome, Floyd County, and the Rome-Floyd County Development Authority for their continued support of F&P Georgia.
Critchlow stated, “From our initial construction to the most recent of six major expansions, the Rome and Floyd County communities have always supported F&P and our plans for growth.”
Critchlow noted that when he started at F&P, there was just an empty field and a handful of associates.
The facility now has 400,000 square feet under its roof and employs 520 top notch associates.
F&P opened in Rome in 2001 with 70 employees.
The company expanded several times before 2012, when it went into a second building in the industrial park.
In 2014, F&P added another 50,000 square feet to that building.
The most recent expansions prior to this announcement occurred in 2020 and again in 2022.
RFCDA Chair Jimmy Byars stated that F&P is already one of the top 10 employers in Floyd County.
Jimmy Byars added, “Our existing industries are extremely valuable to our community, and we will support them in every way possible.”
source_title: Tesla and Honda Contracts Lead to $22 Million Expansion at Rome's F&P Facility - NewsBreak

facts: Rivian is increasing planned production capacity at its Georgia plant to 300,000 vehicles annually.
This represents a 50% increase from the facility's original 200,000-unit target.
The capacity update aims to lower cost per unit.
The update supports future expansion as Rivian scales its next-generation R2 platform.
The project is backed by an updated U.S. Department of Energy loan of up to $4.5 billion.
Rivian expects to begin drawing funds from the DOE loan in early 2027.
Construction at the Georgia site is set to begin in 2026.
Vehicle production at the facility is targeted for late 2028.
CEO RJ Scaringe stated, “We’re very excited to partner with the U.S. Department of Energy to grow our manufacturing footprint in Georgia… R2 dramatically expands our market opportunity.”
Rivian’s updated DOE loan totals up to $4.5 billion, including $4,006 million in principal and $494 million in capitalized interest.
The company is preparing to transition into vertical construction.
Work is currently underway on key areas such as the stamping press section.
Activity at the Georgia site is expected to accelerate through 2026 as primary buildings begin to take shape.
The Rivian R2 will start production at the company's Normal, Illinois plant.
The Georgia facility will support new programs beyond core vehicle production.
Rivian plans to build up to 50,000 vehicles at the Georgia plant for its robotaxi partnership with Uber.
Robotaxi production at the Georgia plant is scheduled to start in late 2028.
The R2 program anchors Rivian’s capacity expansion strategy.
Rivian’s updated capacity plan ties directly to its R2 production strategy.
At its 2024 Investor Day, Rivian outlined a current manufacturing capacity of up to 215,000 units annually at its Normal, Illinois plant.
Roughly 155,000 of the Normal plant's annual capacity is allocated to the R2.
The Georgia plant is expected to significantly expand Rivian's manufacturing footprint.
Rivian estimates the Georgia site’s two production lines could support up to 400,000 vehicles annually.
The Georgia production lines will manufacture the R2, R3, and R3X models.
The recent update may allow the Georgia plant's output to exceed the originally planned 400,000 vehicles annually.
One production line at the Georgia site will be upgraded to a capacity of 300,000 units per year.
Rivian has begun ramping R2 production, including the addition of a night shift.

links.context: Article about Rivian and Uber's robotaxi deal

source_title: Rivian boosts Georgia plant capacity to 300,000 vehicles annually

facts: Hyundai Motor Group has officially opened its $7.59 billion electric and hybrid vehicle manufacturing facility in Ellabell, Bryan County, Georgia.
The HMG Metaplant America (HMGMA) spans 2,900 acres.
The facility represents what the company calls North America’s largest and most advanced smart factory.
The facility is designed to produce up to 500,000 vehicles annually by 2031.
Current production capacity is set at 300,000 vehicles per year.
The plant began construction in October 2022 following a $12.6 billion investment agreement with the State of Georgia signed in May of that year.
The first vehicle, a Hyundai IONIQ 5, rolled off the production line in late 2024.
The facility currently employs 1,200 workers and is expected to create 8,500 full-time jobs by 2031.
Georgia-based Future Technologies served as the lead system integrator for the facility’s private 5G network.
The private 5G network provides critical connectivity infrastructure for industrial automation and autonomous mobile robots.
Nokia supplied the private cellular core and radio access network equipment for the deployment.

Peter Cappiello, chief executive at Future Technologies, stated: “Future Technologies has been headquartered out of Georgia for over 25 years. We appreciate the value that this investment by Hyundai Motor Group brings to the local market. We are very proud [of] our contribution to this project as the lead system integrator for the facility’s private 5G solution. Our skilled engineers and technicians designed and delivered the network, providing critical connectivity infrastructure to support industrial automation and autonomous mobile robots.”
The facility incorporates extensive automation throughout its operations.
Autonomous mobile robots and automated guided vehicles handle material transport.
Unmanned aerial vehicles manage inventory planning and storage.
AI vision systems are deployed across multiple production stages, including verifying component assemblies through a “One-Kit” system.
AI vision systems detect holes and cracks in panels.
AI vision systems align metal sheets.
AI vision systems guide precise positioning of automatic loading systems.
A digital command center integrates and digitizes production data across the facility.
The body shop features automation rates nearly double those of Hyundai’s other facilities globally.
The press shop utilizes smart systems that control power and speed for optimal metal forming.
A Robot Main Buck system integrates robots with vision technology for precise positioning during assembly.
Quality control processes are heavily automated with AI vision systems performing final inspections of paint jobs.
Specialized systems handle everything from electrocoating to waterproofing and soundproofing applications.

The facility claims several world-first implementations, including an autonomous adjustment system for vehicle door gaps, a metal finish line flow-type AGV designed as a collaborative workspace, and an automated door installation system.
The facility will produce electric and hybrid vehicles for the US market.
Current production includes the Hyundai IONIQ 5 and the recently launched IONIQ 9 electric SUV.
Kia models are scheduled to begin production in 2026, followed by Genesis models in subsequent years.
The plant is based on Hyundai’s Innovation Center in Singapore (HMGICS), though scaled for significantly higher production volumes.
HMGMA is part of a broader $20.5 billion investment Hyundai has made in US manufacturing operations, which the company states has created 570,000 jobs.
An additional $21 billion investment has been committed through 2031.

The additional investment includes expanding HMGMA’s capacity from 300,000 to 500,000 vehicles annually and constructing a $5.8 billion electric arc furnace steel mill in Louisiana that will supply automotive steel plates to the Georgia facility when commercial production begins in 2029.
The Georgia investment also encompasses a joint battery venture at HMGMA with LG Energy Solution and SK On, representing the largest single investment in the state’s history.

Hyundai plans to increase its total US annual production capacity to 1.2 million vehicles by 2028, with additional smart manufacturing upgrades planned for its existing Alabama and Georgia facilities.
source_title: Hyundai Opens America’s Largest Smart Factory with AI and Private 5G - PrivateLTEand5G.com

facts: F&P Georgia Mfg Inc was targeted in a ransomware attack.
The incident involved the actor known as Nitrogen.
Nitrogen is identified as a Tier-1 supplier of suspension components to the automotive industry.
The incident was discovered on 2025-08-06 at 15:41:41.781221.
The incident details were published on 2025-08-06 at 15:41:06.422852.
The victim is located in the US.
The information is based on public claims made by the ransomware group "nitrogen" and cannot be confirmed for accuracy.
source_title: Ransom! F&P Georgia Mfg Inc
