facts: Georgia Gov. Brian P. Kemp and the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) announced that through Q3 of FY 2022 (July 1, 2021 – March 31, 2022), job creation and investments from economic development projects surpassed FY 2021 year-end totals.
GDEcD’s Global Commerce team supported 251 project locations that will result in the creation of more than 35,400 new jobs and $12.9 billion in investment for the state.
There was a recorded 48% increase in jobs compared to three-quarters through the previous fiscal year and a 6% increase in jobs compared to the prior year’s overall total.
There was a recorded 54% increase in total investment compared to the same time period last year.
Nearly 78% of both projects and investments were outside the 10-county metro Atlanta area, and over half of the jobs created were outside that area.
Key industry sectors supported include automotive, advanced manufacturing, logistics and distribution, software and technology, and food processing.
Georgia’s electric vehicle (EV) and sustainability ecosystems have grown tremendously over the past year.
Georgia is continuing to recruit the entire EV supply chain to ensure an effective, efficient, and affordable environment for EV production.
Rivian plans to develop a $5 billion campus at Stanton Springs North and an electric vehicle service center in Atlanta that will provide training on EVs.

SK Battery America partnered with American lithium-ion battery recycling and engineered materials startup Ascend Elements to recycle both cell and module lithium-ion battery manufacturing scrap from SK’s Jackson County facility.
Ascend Elements announced its first commercial-scale battery recycling plant in Covington in January.
Aurubis will invest $340 million in a ‘first-of-its-kind in the U.S.’ state-of-the-art recycling and secondary smelting facility for multi-metal recycling in Augusta.
Aspen Aerogels announced plans to locate a new 500,000-square-foot advanced manufacturing facility in Statesboro.
Aspen Aerogels will produce PyroThin materials that provide thermal management, mechanical stability, and fire protection properties to EV battery systems at its new Statesboro facility.

Georgia’s No. 1-ranking workforce training program, Georgia Quick Start, helped secure numerous projects including Pepsi Co.’s expansion in DeKalb County, Remington Firearms’ R&D center in LaGrange, and Titan Steel Doors’ expansion in Hart County.
The Pepsi Co., Remington Firearms, and Titan Steel Doors projects alone represent 1,082 new jobs and $373 million in investment.
Georgia Quick Start has provided specialized training tailored to business needs for more than 40 years and is administered through the Technical College System of Georgia.
The Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) deepwater and regional ports projects played a key role in securing diverse projects including Serena & Lily Inc.’s new facility in Effingham County.
The GPA has been reporting record-breaking numbers for 19 consecutive months as it expedites plans for long-term growth.

The Savannah Harbor Expansion Project (SHEP) was recently completed, deepening a 40-mile stretch between the port and the Atlantic Ocean to accommodate super-sized freighters from the expanded Panama Canal.
The Appalachian Regional Port drove Roper Corporation’s decision to invest $118 million in expanding its facilities in Walker County.
Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport and Georgia’s extensive rail network are assets driving the state’s growth.

The Georgia Department of Transportation maintains more than 1,200 miles of highway as part of the state’s highway and interstate system, connecting companies to 80% of the country in two days or less of driving time.
This transportation system was integral to landing Ryder System Inc.’s new facility in Henry County that will create more than 250 new jobs.
source_title: GA: Georgia Economic Development Surpasses 2021 FY Totals, with $13B in Investment, 35K Jobs | Trade and Industry Development

facts: Daesol Ausys, a South Korean automotive supplier, announced it will invest $72 million to establish a new manufacturing facility in Harris County.
The company specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
Daesol Ausys is a “key supplier” for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
The new facility will be located in West Point, Georgia.
The facility will specifically produce luggage boards and covers.
The project is expected to create more than 140 new jobs in Harris County.
Daesol Ausys’ new operations in West Point are expected to begin in December 2024.
Min Ho Kwon, CEO of Daesol Ausys, stated: “We’re committed to delivering world-class services that exceed our clients’ requirements and expectations.”
Min Ho Kwon added: “We are very excited about this project in the Northwest Harris Business Park and look forward to continuing the partnership with the State of Georgia and Harris County.”
Governor Brian Kemp stated: “West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right, and we are excited that Daesol Ausys will join other innovators in Harris County.”

Governor Brian Kemp noted: “Our state’s automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for the jobs of the future.”
South Korea was one of the top three international sources of job creation in Georgia during Fiscal Year 2023.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: “Economic development is a team sport that is driven by the local level. Congratulations to Harris County and our community partners for helping bring Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.”

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson added: “The automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state. We’re excited to see the continuation of this growth and look forward to the opportunities that Daesol Ausys will provide to the people of Harris County.”

links.context: Official Georgia GDEcD tweet announcing the facility

source_title: Automotive Supplier Announces $72 Million Investment in Georgia | The Star News Network

facts: 2019 Georgia exports reached an all-time high of $41.2 billion, with total trade at $143.3 billion.
Demand for Georgia exports grew even after the first phase of U.S.-China tariffs went into effect.
Georgia's agriculture exporters make up 10% of its exports, or about $4.2 billion.
Automotive-related exports generate another $3.2 billion, mostly to Canada, Mexico, the United Arab Emirates and Germany.
Aerospace companies generated $10.8 billion in exports last year, making it the state's top export industry and Georgia the nation's fifth-largest exporter in the space.
Canton-based J&S Chemical is an in-process specialty chemical manufacturer for metal car parts that exports to nearly 30 countries.
J&S Chemical faced a raw material shortage three years ago, followed by the trade war with China and the pandemic.
Kia Motors began making face shields in its West Point facility while auto production resumed.

Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport experienced 4% growth in cargo operations since 2015 until volume dropped from nearly 700,000 metric tons in 2018 to 640,000 metric tons in 2019 due to the trade war with China.
Air cargo volume at Hartsfield-Jackson fell 9.6% in January 2020 but grew by almost 1% over 2019 in February 2020 due to an influx of air cargo charter flights for medical and PPE supplies.
February 2020 saw an 11.23% increase in air cargo volume from January 2020 at Hartsfield-Jackson.
In March 2017, Hartsfield-Jackson introduced TruckPass, a staging system to alleviate roadway congestion in the South Cargo area and digitize freight loading and unloading processes.
Hartsfield-Jackson expanded warehouse capacity with Cargo Building C, a 120,000-square-foot warehouse with 2,500 square feet of temperature-controlled space for perishable goods and pharmaceuticals.
Worldwide Flight Services was the first lessee of Cargo Building C at Hartsfield-Jackson and began operations in late 2019.

The Georgia Ports Authority is committed to investing in strategic projects, including the first phase of the Mason Mega Rail project which will double the Port of Savannah’s rail capacity and is scheduled to come online at the end of summer 2020.
At the Ocean Terminal, the first phase of renovations to partially convert the terminal to handle containers is expected to be completed at the end of 2020.
A berth renovation project at the main Garden City Terminal continues at full speed to allow more high volume ships.
Hartsfield-Jackson plans to launch partnership agreements with airports throughout the world to increase cargo volume and passenger numbers, with Amsterdam’s Schiphol Airport partnering in June 2019.
Hartsfield-Jackson is on track to start development of a public-private partnership for an air cargo terminal that will support the growth of pharmaceutical, aerospace and automotive industries.
GMA organized weekly roundtable calls to help Georgia businesses connect supplies with demand during the pandemic.
J&S Chemical sold more than 700 gallons of sanitizing solution to the Bobby Dodd Institute as of press time after joining a GDEcD representative's recommended call.

The GDEcD’s International Trade Division launched a free online tool called the Export Georgia, USA Directory in February to serve as an online platform for international buyers to search for Georgia suppliers and services.
Georgia Ports Authority Executive Director Griff Lynch stated that global trade was projected to have another record year before the pandemic hit.

Mary Waters, deputy commissioner of international trade for the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), noted that underlying diverse markets and business environment fundamentals will allow Georgia to turn a corner after the pandemic.

Georgia Manufacturing Alliance CEO and founder Jason Moss emphasized that supply chain redundancy is critical because missing components like nuts or bolts can stop production lines, especially in aerospace and automotive markets relying on imported parts.

J&S Chemical director of global sales and marketing Thomas Smith explained that the company had to tighten communication with suppliers across multiple stops along the supply chain as the pandemic spread from China to European suppliers.

Hartsfield-Jackson director of air service development Elliott Paige highlighted the need to build resiliency and efficiency in operations and capacity to handle future industry changes, cyclical market shifts, or pandemics.
source_title: Georgia Manufacturing Alliance Featured in Georgia Trend Magazine -  Georgia Manufacturing Alliance

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that DAS Corp. (DAS) will establish a new manufacturing facility in Metter, Georgia.
The facility will create 300 new jobs and generate more than $35 million in investment in Candler County.
The initiative will involve collaborative indirect investments.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.

Governor Brian Kemp noted that Georgia's logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners in all corners of the state and to markets across the world.
DAS was established in Korea in 1987 and specializes in designing and manufacturing seating systems, safety seating components, and seating structures.
DAS is a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (KMMG) and Hyundai Motor Group.

Sean Kim, Chief Operating Officer at DAS, stated that the strategic location near prominent cities like Savannah and Macon facilitated convenient access and played a pivotal role in the decision-making process.
Sean Kim noted that Metter was deemed an ideal location in anticipation of its role as a global electric vehicle production hub thanks to its close proximity to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The new DAS manufacturing facility will be located in the Candler County Industrial Park in Metter.
The facility will primarily produce automotive seat structures.
Operations are projected to begin in the latter half of 2024.
Over the course of the next five years, DAS will be recruiting for assembly technicians.
DAS intends to establish collaborations with local employment services as part of its hiring process.

Metter Mayor Ed Boyd stated that the impact on the community will be transformative, stimulating investment in housing, creating other business opportunities, and improving the social economic wellbeing of citizens.
Glyn Thrift, Chairman of the Candler County Board of Commissioners, thanked DAS for choosing Candler County as its new home and stated they will provide a boost to spur more economic development.

Griff Lynch, President and CEO of the Georgia Ports Authority, stated the authority is proud to support the growing automotive manufacturing presence in the state by driving economic activity and jobs.

Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy at the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team, represented the department on this competitive project in partnership with the Candler County Industrial Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Electric Cities of Georgia, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia is well-positioned for the arrival of Hyundai’s supplier network as a result of years of consistent investment in site preparation, workforce solutions, and connectivity.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that DAS is another exciting addition to Georgia’s automotive community.

Over the past century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
DAS Corp. maintains a strong focus on innovation and quality to consistently provide premier automotive seating solutions tailored to evolving customer needs.
Strategic collaborations, including partnerships with Hyundai Motor Group and Kia Motors, continue to drive progress within the constantly evolving automotive industry for DAS Corp.
DAS Corp. garnered a total revenue of approximately $900 million USD during the fiscal year of 2022.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: DAS Corp. to Bring 300 Automotive Jobs to Metter - EIN Presswire

facts: In January 2026, Boston Dynamics unveiled the production-ready Atlas humanoid robot at CES and deployed them at Hyundai's new manufacturing facility near Savannah, Georgia.
This deployment marks the first time humanoid robots have moved from research laboratories into real-world manufacturing environments working alongside human employees on actual production tasks.
The production Atlas is an electric humanoid robot standing 1.9 meters (6.2 feet) tall and weighing 90 kilograms (198 pounds), with 56 degrees of freedom enabling human-like dexterity.
It can lift up to 50 kilograms (110 pounds) instantaneously or 30 kilograms (66 pounds) sustained, has a 2.3-meter reach, and operates autonomously in temperatures ranging from -20°C to 40°C.
The robot features autonomous battery swapping and can navigate to charging stations independently, enabling continuous operation without human intervention.
Hyundai plans to begin deploying Atlas robots at its U.S. manufacturing facility in 2028, starting with parts sequencing tasks and expanding to component assembly by 2030.
Hyundai aims to build manufacturing capacity for 30,000 Atlas units annually by 2028, representing one of the largest humanoid robot production commitments in history.
Boston Dynamics first unveiled Atlas in 2013 as part of a DARPA robotics challenge.
The production Atlas unveiled in January 2026 replaces the previous hydraulic system with an electric design to improve efficiency and reduce maintenance requirements.
The robot's AI capabilities have been enhanced to enable learned skills to be deployed across entire Atlas fleets.
According to technical specifications, the production Atlas features tactile sensors in its three-fingered grippers for sensing pressure and texture.
The robot has a 360° camera view providing comprehensive situational awareness for navigating factory environments.
The robot can operate in three modes: fully autonomous, via teleoperator control, or with tablet steering interface.
Hyundai Motor Group acquired an 80% stake in Boston Dynamics in 2021, making the robotics company a subsidiary.
The deployment at Hyundai's Georgia facility is officially called the Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA).
The facility is Hyundai's newest and most advanced manufacturing plant, designed from the ground up to incorporate advanced automation and robotics.
According to Reuters reporting, Hyundai plans to expand Atlas deployment across its entire global manufacturing network as the technology proves successful.
Field testing at the Georgia facility began in January 2026, with robots performing actual manufacturing tasks.
According to CBS 60 Minutes coverage, the robots were demonstrated sorting roof racks for the assembly line autonomously.
Initial tasks include parts sequencing and sorting, which are repetitive and well-defined.
Hyundai's commitment to manufacturing 30,000 Atlas units annually by 2028 represents an unprecedented scale for humanoid robot production.
Tesla's Optimus robot has deployed over 1,000 units in Tesla's own factories as a direct competitor.
Industry analysis indicates Atlas demonstrates superior adaptability for complex tasks with Large Behavior Models (LBM), while Optimus leads in cost optimization targeting a price below $20,000.
The production Atlas incorporates a 4-hour battery life with autonomous swapping to enable continuous operation.
Hyundai's deployment strategy follows a phased approach beginning in 2028 focusing on parts sequencing tasks.
By 2030, Hyundai expects Atlas to move into component assembly tasks requiring coordination between multiple parts.
Hyundai positions the deployment as "human-centered automation," emphasizing robots as tools that reduce physical strain rather than replace human workers.
The company acknowledges personnel will still be needed to maintain, train, and work alongside the robots.
Boston Dynamics is partnering with Google DeepMind to enhance Atlas's AI capabilities using machine learning and robotics expertise.
Hyundai's phased deployment strategy suggests a careful economic validation process to validate ROI at each phase before committing to broader deployment.
The company plans to expand Atlas deployment across its entire global manufacturing network following a similar phased approach.
source_title: Boston Dynamics Atlas Enters Production: Hyundai's Georgia Factory Marks the First Real-World Deployment of Humanoid Robots in Manufacturing | Programming Helper Tech

facts: In January 2023, GSC Steel Stamping, LLC purchased the assets of Dixien, LLC.
GSC Steel Stamping, LLC is a minority- and family-owned business that specializes in the production of automotive parts.
The acquisition allowed GSC Steel Stamping to continue its 43-year history of stamping automobile parts.
The company's factory is located in Lake City, Georgia.
The factory spans 200,000 square feet.
The facility is the largest automobile stamping plant owned by a minority group globally.

facts: Plaintiff in the case is Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.
Defendant in the case is GSC Steel Stamping, LLC.
The lawsuit is classified as a breach of contract under diversity jurisdiction.
The complaint was filed on May 15, 2025, and the case was terminated on June 3, 2025.
Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. filed a verified complaint seeking injunctive relief and damages against GSC Steel Stamping, LLC.
VW filed a motion for preliminary injunction and temporary restraining order on May 15, 2025.

Exhibits to the motion include nomination agreements, VW terms and conditions, a purchase order dated November 5, 2024, GSC payment proposals, letters from VW to GSC dated January 15, 2025 and March 25, 2025, GSC's proposed exit agreement, email chains from April 13, 2025 and May 14, 2025, and a VW asset list with photos.

The complaint references additional exhibits including nomination agreements, VW terms and conditions, a purchase order dated November 5, 2024, a March 25, 2025 letter regarding breach of contract, a proposed exit agreement by GSC dated April 13, 2025, an email chain from April 13, 2025, a VW asset and supply list, an email regarding refusal to pick up tools dated May 14, 2025, and a civil cover sheet.
A motion hearing was held on May 20, 2025, before District Judge Travis R McDonough at Courtroom 3, 900 Georgia Avenue, Chattanooga, Tennessee.
The court ordered the parties to appear for a telephonic status conference on May 27, 2025.
On May 28, 2025, the court ordered the parties to file a status report or take other appropriate action by June 3, 2025.
GSC Steel Stamping, LLC was represented by William A. Harris, III as of May 23, 2025.
Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. filed a notice of voluntary dismissal on June 2, 2025.
The summons was returned unexecuted on June 2, 2025.
The case was terminated on June 3, 2025.
source_title: Volkswagen Group of America, Inc. v. GSC Steel Stamping, LLC (1:25-cv-00160), Tennessee Eastern District Court

facts: Astemo, Ltd. continues its NTT INDYCAR SERIES partnership with Team Penske for a 15th consecutive season in 2026 as a sponsor of the No. 2 Dallara/Chevrolet driven by Josef Newgarden.
The Astemo name will appear on the front, sides and rear wing of the bright red No. 2 Team Penske Chevrolet at nine races this season.
Astemo supplies fuel-system components for all of the 2.2-liter twin-turbocharged Chevrolet race engines in the series.

Senior Vice President of Sales and Marketing for Astemo Americas Rob Sharpe noted that Team Penske and Astemo announced plans to extend their INDYCAR partnership for the 2025 season and beyond in 2024.
The 2025 season ended on a high note with a Newgarden Team Penske win at the Borchetta Bourbon Music City Grand Prix in Nashville.

Sharpe stated that partnerships with Team Penske and Chevrolet have proven highly successful both on and off the track, and business relationships developed from motorsports programs are without equal.
Astemo is the primary sponsor of the No. 2 car for the recently announced Freedom 250 Grand Prix of Washington, D.C., celebrating the nation's 250th anniversary.
Team Penske is celebrating its 60th anniversary in 2026 and will be focused on adding to its record 20 Indianapolis 500 victories and 17 INDYCAR series titles in the 2026 season.
Following back-to-back wins at the Indianapolis 500 in both 2023 and 2024, Astemo expects Newgarden and his No. 2 Team Penske Chevrolet team to continue their winning ways in 2026.

Josef Newgarden is a two-time series champion and the first driver in more than 20 years to achieve back-to-back Indianapolis 500 wins, having completed 14 seasons of competition in North America's top open-wheel series and last year being prominently featured in marketing campaigns by Fox, an INDYCAR broadcast partner.

Astemo's motorsports program schedule includes races in St. Petersburg FL, Avondale AZ, Arlington TX, Birmingham AL, Long Beach CA, Indianapolis IN (twice), Detroit MI, Madison IL, Elkhart Lake WI, Lexington OH, Lebanon TN, Portland OR, Markham Ontario, Washington DC, West Allis WI (twice), and Monterey CA throughout 2026.
Astemo is headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, and is a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd., Honda Motor Co., and JIC Capital, Ltd.
Astemo has 80,000 employees worldwide with operations in the Americas, Asia, China, Europe, and Japan.

The company is a global mega-supplier in the automotive industry with Electrification Business Division, Vehicle Business Division, and Motorcycle Business Division engaged in the development, manufacture, sales, and service of automotive parts as well as transportation and industrial machinery and systems.

Astemo Americas, Inc. manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide, adhering to ISO 9001/IATF 16949 industry standards.
source_title: Astemo's INDYCAR Program with Team Penske and Chevrolet to Continue in 2026

facts: Astemo Americas is introducing new mobility technology to its North American customers, expanding its footprint in the Americas and making name and branding changes.
The company has 80,000 employees worldwide, including more than 40 offices and manufacturing sites with more than 14,000 employees in the Americas.

Astemo Americas CEO and President Tim Clark states the company aims to strengthen its position as a leader in electrification and mobility intelligence as the industry moves into the software-defined vehicle age.
The company is accelerating investment in advanced mobility, including electrification, advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), autonomous driving, and advanced chassis technology.
Astemo is showcasing next-generation autonomous driving, EV, chassis, suspension, steering, and brake technologies to major OEM customers in the Americas this summer.
Technologies on display include 3D sensing-and-control, semi-active suspension, steer-by-wire, and brake-by-wire for use on future new-model vehicles sold in the Americas.

Astemo Americas Vice President for Advanced Engineering Harsha Badarinarayan notes that advanced AI-based multi-camera systems are gaining wide acceptance among automakers for future AD/ADAS applications.

Astemo is developing automated driving solutions using proprietary AI-based stereo vision technology that generates a 360° denser point cloud than conventional LiDAR along with color and brightness information.

The company is showcasing a full suspension system with AI-powered control logic using deep-learning algorithms that delivers ride-and-handling improvements at a lower cost compared to similar systems today.
The showcased suspension system includes a "preview" camera to prepare for bumps and potholes in the road ahead.
Additional suspension technologies include a performance series damper system focused on Off-Road vehicles and a Virtual Development Lab to support customers in eliminating physical prototypes.
Astemo's latest generation of steer-by-wire technology features a hand-held device designed to replace a vehicle's steering wheel as part of the Smart Steer-by-Wire System.
The company is showing its electro-mechanical brake-by-wire system, Smart Brake, for the first time in North America, which reduces lag-time between driver input and brake response.

Smart Brake's e-pedal eliminates the traditional hydraulic connection between the brake pedal and vehicle brakes, simplifying vehicle assembly, extending battery range, and reducing maintenance by eliminating brake fluid use.
Astemo's Smart Brake project is financially supported by the French Government as part of the France 2030 investment plan.

The company was formerly known as Hitachi Astemo Americas but has officially changed its name to Astemo Americas following a corporate headquarters name change from Hitachi Astemo, Ltd. to Astemo Ltd.
The new branding message is "Astemo, Mobility Beyond – From the Core of Mobility, Freedom," featured on the global website and marketing materials.
The name Astemo is derived from "Advanced Sustainable Technologies for Mobility," underscoring a focus on cutting-edge, sustainable mobility solutions.
Astemo Americas CEO Tim Clark notes the company has outgrown its technical center and headquarters in Farmington Hills, Michigan, and plans to expand to a new location in the near future.
Astemo is a joint venture between Hitachi, Ltd., Honda Motor Co., and JIC Capital Ltd., headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

The company operates Electric Business Division, Vehicle Business Division, and Motorcycle Business Divisions engaged in development, manufacture, sales, and service of automotive parts and transportation/industrial machinery systems.

Astemo Americas manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide, adhering to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.
source_title: Changes Are Underway At Astemo Americas - A Leading Auto Supplier

facts: Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. has been in business for around 116 years.
The company generates approximately USD 500,000 - 999,999 in revenue annually.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. employs around 96 people at its Berea, Kentucky location.
The facility is located at 301 Mayde Rd, Berea, KY 40403.
Business hours are Monday through Friday from 8 AM to 5:30 PM, with Saturdays and Sundays closed.
Lauren Reno serves as the Branch Manager for Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc.
The company has nine additional contacts associated with it: Jim Barba, Angie Tillery, Jason McGinnis, Ray Harvey, Benny Slater, Naoki Makita, Pam Turner, Sam Gibson, and Dottie Billups.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. reports annual sales of approximately USD 500,000 - 999,999.
source_title: Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. | (859) 986-2359 | Berea, Kentucky

facts: Hitachi Astemo Americas, Inc. is a newly created company with 90,000 employees.
The company manufactures and markets engine management, electric powertrain, and integrated vehicle controls for major automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Hitachi Astemo is a leader in adhering to ISO/TS:16949:2009 industry standards.

Challenges from the global pandemic, including work-from-home shifts and supply-chain problems, are spurring Hitachi Astemo to accelerate its move to more data-driven production processes in the U.S. and globally.
The company announced investments in robotics, artificial intelligence, and other data-driven technologies to implement sweeping changes at manufacturing plants worldwide.
Sudhanshu Gaur serves as Hitachi America vice president and chief architect, Smart Manufacturing at Hitachi Astemo.

Gaur stated that the global pandemic, changes in work habits, and recent supply chain problems are accelerating adoption of data-driven production processes supported by machine learning, artificial intelligence, time-sensitive networking, and collaborative robotics.
Gaur said, “We are standing at the cusp of a fourth industrial revolution driven by exponential growth in IT technologies.”
Gaur noted there is a great opportunity for manufacturers to embrace these changes to unlock greater business value while bringing more flexibility and resiliency into their operations.
Gaur is responsible for the digital transformation of Hitachi Astemo’s global manufacturing operations to improve overall productivity and ensure continued leadership in an Industry 4.0 vision.
Gaur was recently named one of the top 25 leaders in the transformation of manufacturing by the Society of Manufacturing Engineers.
Gaur served as keynote speaker at The Assembly Show held at the Donald E. Stephens Convention Center in Rosemont, Illinois.
Gaur predicted that Hitachi Astemo’s nearly 140 manufacturing plants around the world will benefit from access to Astemo’s Global IoT platform and services within the next four years.

During his keynote address titled “Smart Manufacturing – Today and Tomorrow,” Gaur outlined a vision of digital platforms connecting all aspects of manufacturing operations, including production, engineering, quality, inventory, and supply-chain management.

He noted that digital platforms introduce additional flexibility into production processes and allow companies to more quickly address disruptions caused by VUCA (volatility, uncertainty, complexity, and ambiguity).

Gaur explained that while fully automated plants are more efficient, they pose risks because tightly linked systems are generally not designed to handle unpredictability, complex situations, and rare events.
He advocated for a gradual adoption of advanced digital technologies, bringing necessary automation while “upskilling” the workforce to manage and leverage complex systems.

The cost for implementing a data-driven system at a medium-size U.S. manufacturing plant with 1,000 employees and $200 million in annual sales is estimated at $20,000 in one-time implementation charges plus $60,000 or more in annual running costs.

For a large global enterprise with about 100 plants, 60,000 employees, and $10 billion in annual sales, implementing a fully digital platform could cost $1 million or more in one-time charges plus more than $3 million in annual running costs.

Successful implementation of smart manufacturing strategies involves multiple layers of the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), including fog computing and edge intelligence, as well as 5G and cloud computing.
Hitachi Astemo was formed by the recent merger of Hitachi Automotive Systems with Honda’s Keihin, Showa, and Nissin groups.
Hitachi holds a 66.6 percent share of voting rights in Hitachi Astemo, while Honda holds the remaining 33.4 percent.
The company holds leadership positions in AD/ADAS, software, powertrain systems, chassis systems, and motorcycle systems.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo Americas Sees Industrial Automation and IoT Innovation as Key to the Future of Manufacturing - Industrial Innovation

facts: Operates worldwide as a global mega-supplier of automotive parts with approximately 80,000 employees across the United States, Asia, China, Europe, and Japan.

Develops, manufactures, sells, and services automotive parts, transportation and industrial machinery, equipment, and systems through three business segments: Electrification Business, Vehicle Business, and Motorcycle Business.
Fiscal 2024 revenues were approximately 2,200 billion yen.
Fiscal 2024 operating income was approximately 67.0 billion yen.
As of March 2025, has 98 group companies.
As of March 2025, operates 134 bases in 26 countries and regions (45 in Japan, 89 overseas).
Consolidated number of employees is approximately 80,000 as of March 2025.
Has approximately 70 customers who are automobile and motorcycle manufacturing companies worldwide.
Holds the No. 1 company share globally in the motorcycle product market for injectors, throttle bodies, suspensions, and brakes according to 2024 Astemo research.
Ranked 19th (5th in Japan) among the top 30 automotive parts suppliers by revenue in FY2024 according to MarkLines.
Holds approximately 16,800 patents as of March 2025.
Research and development expenses are approximately 162.0 billion yen as of March 2025.
Provides advanced mobility solutions in three business areas: electrification, vehicle, and motorcycle businesses.
Mission is to contribute to a sustainable society and improved quality of life by providing world-leading advanced mobility solutions.
Develops advanced technologies focused on Environment, Safety, and Digitalization.
Environment technology includes rare-earth-free motors that combine rare-earth-free magnets and synchronous reluctance motors to support outputs from main drives to auxiliary drives.
Environment technology includes in-wheel motors as a next-generation mobility solution that contributes to reduced environmental impact and improved ride comfort.
Environment technology includes e-Axles for small motorcycles that integrate the motor, inverter, gearbox, and cooling fan for a compact, lightweight, and cost-efficient design.
Safety technology includes 360-degree 3D sensing technology that detects fallen objects and road surface abnormalities around the vehicle.
Safety technology includes NOA (Navigation on Autopilot) combined with 360-degree 3D sensing technology to reduce driver burden and realize comfortable autonomous travel.
Safety technology includes electric brakes, a next-generation brake system that enhances safety through high-precision brake control.
Safety technology includes the Steer-by-wire system (SBWS®), a linkless steering system providing safety and new value with an extensive product lineup.
Safety technology includes ADAS for motorcycles that uses road surface detection through a stereo camera to enable improved stability control.
Digitalization technology includes an IoV platform that links on-board and back-end aspects to enable quick circulation from data collection to software updates and service development.
Digitalization technology includes value creation DX, contributing to the continuous evolution of vehicles through generative AI and digital environments.
Digitalization technology includes dynamics integrated control platform technology to optimize vehicle motion according to driver preferences for a more comfortable driving feel.
source_title: Get to Know Astemo | Company Information | Astemo, Ltd.

facts: Automotive supplier Astemo Ltd. is making a $95 million investment to create a regional headquarters in Wixom.
The investment will create 200 jobs and retain 175 jobs.
Governor Gretchen Whitmer celebrated the investment on Tuesday, September 2, 2025.
Astemo will build a facility in Wixom to accommodate physical space needed to showcase cutting-edge capabilities to attract talent and increase collaboration with business units and customers.
Astemo chose Michigan for its expansion over competing sites in Ohio.

Kohei Takeuchi, President & CEO of Astemo Ltd., stated that as a global mega-supplier of automotive components and systems specializing in advanced mobility solutions like electric powertrains, chassis systems, and autonomous driving technologies, the investment cements presence in Michigan and enhances the state's standing as best-in-nation for the automotive industry.

Oakland County Executive David Coulter stated that choosing Oakland County for the new headquarters and technical center in Wixom will support 350 people who will work at the supplier and boost the county's status as a prime location for advanced mobility technology.
Astemo anticipates hiring over 150 engineer-type positions as part of the 200 jobs coming with the new regional site in Wixom.
The company currently employs over 15,000 employees in North America and 80,000 employees globally.
Astemo offers opportunities for training and/or upskilling through a tuition reimbursement program.

Quentin L. Messer, Jr., CEO of the Michigan Economic Development Corporation (MEDC), stated that the investment expands Michigan's supply chain and talent capacity for mobility and advanced manufacturing, noting Team Michigan is deepening relationships in a globally competitive environment to execute the state's 'Make it in Michigan' economic development strategy.
As part of the selection process, the City of Wixom offered a letter of support for the project.
City Manager Steven Brown stated that Wixom has a long history as home to world-leading automotive suppliers and looks forward to building the state-of-the-art facility with Astemo Americas.

This is the second time Governor Whitmer has led an investment mission in Japan, following a previous mission in September 2023 which was the first Michigan governor business visit to Japan since 2017.
source_title: Gov. Whitmer Welcomes New Regional Headquarters for Astemo Americas | Michigan Business

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company (H&V) is a global manufacturer of nonwoven materials and engineered papers used in filtration, energy, and industrial applications.
The company is divided into three business divisions: air and liquid filtration media; battery separators and energy products; and industrial products.
In 2014 the company operates 12 manufacturing sites, 15 sales offices, and four research centers globally.
After 1950 the company later launched production of microfiberglass battery separators.
The company has a manufacturing site located in Hawkinsville, GA.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose - Wikipedia

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose (H&V) is a global manufacturer of advanced materials including nonwovens used in filtration, battery and industrial applications.
The company will invest $40.2 million to expand its operation in Floyd County, VA.
The expansion will add more than 28,000 square feet to an existing facility to accommodate new production equipment.
The project is intended to meet increased demand for the company's battery and industrial applications.
Virginia successfully competed with Georgia for the project.
The expansion will create 25 new jobs in Floyd County.
Governor Glenn Youngkin announced the investment and job creation.

Josh Ayer, CEO of H&V, stated the facility is essential to serving global and domestic customers and that Virginia was chosen for its positive business environment and strong state and county support.
The company has been part of the Floyd, VA community since 1976.
Joe Turman, Chairman of the Floyd County Board of Supervisors, noted the new investment is over three times bigger than the largest private investment in the county's 191-year history.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership worked with Floyd County to secure the project for Virginia.
Governor Youngkin approved a $558,700 grant from the Commonwealth’s Opportunity Fund to assist Floyd County with the project.

Caren Merrick, Secretary of Commerce and Trade for Virginia, highlighted the company's role in Virginia's advanced materials industry over the past 46 years and thanked H&V for creating high-quality jobs.
source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose to Expand Virginia Site - Nonwovens Industry

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company holds patent application 20260081303 for battery separators comprising embossments that may take the form of indentations formed in a surface via an embossing process, filed July 22, 2025, and published March 19, 2026.
Hollingsworth & Vose Company holds patent 12401090 for battery separators comprising embossments formed by an embossing process, filed February 10, 2020, and granted August 26, 2025.
The company has patent applications describing thermal barrier materials with desirable thermal properties such as low thermal conductivity and high thermal stability.

The company's thermal barrier materials can have structural properties including high porosity, high pore tortuosity, and low air permeability to enhance mechanical resiliency, compressibility, elasticity, and thermal insulation performance.

Hollingsworth & Vose Company holds patent applications for filter media designed to filter gas streams like air, featuring stable filtration efficiency, increased normalized gamma, relatively low pressure drop, and low basis weight.
The company's filter media can be a composite of two or more fiber layers designed to enhance function without substantially negatively impacting the performance of another layer.
Hollingsworth & Vose Company holds patents for filter media with stable filtration efficiency, high oil repellency, low instantaneous resistance, and stable service life.
The company's filter media can optionally include a support layer with relatively high air permeability, such as greater than or equal to 1100 CFM.

Hollingsworth & Vose Company has patent applications for filter media comprising non-woven fiber webs with enhanced physical properties from combinations of fibrillated fibers, glass fibers, and/or binder fibers.
The company holds patents for filter media including nonwoven fiber webs comprising nanofibers.

Hollingsworth & Vose Company has patents for filter media bonded to filter housings that can withstand sterilization processes like autoclaving without compromising adhesion strength or structural integrity.
The company holds applications for filter media comprising water-repellent additives, including fluorinated and non-fluorinated types, and/or fibrillated fibers such as lyocell.
Hollingsworth & Vose Company has patents covering multilayered media with irregular structures that are reversibly stretchable and/or comprise a polymeric film.

The company holds patents for fiber webs used in filter media that include fibrillated fibers and optionally non-fibrillated fibers, with limited or no glass fiber to impart mechanical properties and filtration efficiency like dust holding capacity.

Hollingsworth & Vose Company has patents for filter media comprising a pre-filter layer designed to improve upstream removal of fine particulate matter, increase service life, and increase gamma without adversely affecting downstream efficiency layers.

The company holds patents for filter media comprising nanofibers where first and second fiber webs have similar water contact angles or surface energy to enhance structural stability under high temperature, pressure, or steam sterilization.
Hollingsworth & Vose Company has patents for filter media comprising a waved filtration layer with a gradient in fiber diameter across its thickness to produce low pressure drop and long lifetime.
source_title: Patents Assigned to Hollingsworth & Vose Company - Justia Patents Search

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company has been awarded the 2026 BCI Innovation Award by Battery Council International for its development of a unique glass fibrous matrix electrode.

The innovation, known as Glass Electrode Material (GEM), helps generate and support a unique microporous structure within the lead active mass of a battery to enhance chargeability performance and cycle life reliability without impacting water loss.
GEM technology improves recharge efficiency through enhanced charge acceptance, reducing fuel-related emissions while improving battery performance, durability, and cycle life reliability.
With approximately three times higher Dynamic Charge Acceptance (DCA), GEM enables next-generation start-stop systems that supports fuel economy gains of up to 5%.
Its fast recharge response also makes it well suited for advanced 12-V architectures in hybrid (HEV, PHEV) and electric vehicle auxiliary systems.
The technology platform is applicable across multiple energy storage applications, including automotive batteries, motive power for forklifts, and stationary backup power solutions.
Hollingsworth & Vose developed GEM in partnership with ArcActive.
Roger Miksad, President and Executive Director of BCI, stated that battery innovation is thriving and Hollingsworth & Vose exemplifies technological leadership advancing lead battery capabilities.

Nicolas Clement, R&D Director, and Dr. Amogh Karwa, Director of Product management, stated that GEM technology has true potential for creating a more sustainable future and will be a great tool for battery manufacturers to enhance performance and compete with alternative chemistries.
East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
Battery Council International annually presents this award to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, the former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.
Hollingsworth & Vose is a family-owned global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications, headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, U.S.A.
source_title: BCI Names Hollingsworth & Vose its 2026 Innovation Award Winner

Roger Miksad, President and Executive Director of BCI, stated that battery innovation is thriving and Hollingsworth & Vose is a great example of technological leadership and product evolution that advances the capabilities of the storied lead battery.

East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
Battery Council International annually presents this award to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, the former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.
H&V is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications.
H&V’s advanced materials contribute to a cleaner world through their use in products that provide clean air, clean liquids, and energy storage.
The company has been family-owned for seven generations and is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, U.S.A.
Battery Council International (BCI) is the leading trade association representing the global battery industry.
Founded in 1924, BCI advocates and educates on behalf of battery manufacturers and recyclers, marketers and retailers, suppliers of raw materials and equipment, and battery distributors.
BCI conveys an industry-wide commitment to sustainability, safety, and science.

facts: Hyundai has begun producing electric SUVs in Georgia less than two years after breaking ground on its sprawling, $7.6 billion manufacturing plant west of Savannah.
Hyundai's Georgia factory held an employee-focused celebration when its first EV for commercial sale rolled off the assembly line.
The event occurred on a Thursday.
Bianca Johnson is the spokesperson for Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
The statement regarding the production start was provided to The Associated Press on Monday.
Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America validated its production processes to ensure vehicles meet Hyundai Motor Group's high quality standards.
Initial production of customer vehicles has started ahead of schedule.
A grand opening celebration at the Georgia plant is expected in the first quarter of 2025.
Hyundai and battery partner LG Energy Solution plan to employ a total of 8,500 workers at the Bryan County site in Ellabell once the plant is fully operational.
Hyundai plans to produce up to 300,000 EVs per year in Georgia, as well as the batteries that power them.
The plant's vehicle production areas have been completed and are being staffed by more than 1,000 workers.
The battery-making facilities at the plant remain under construction.
The first vehicles being produced at the Georgia site are 2025 models of Hyundai's Ioniq 5 electric SUVs.
The American-made EVs are expected to arrive at U.S. dealerships before the end of 2024.
During the first half of 2024, the Ioniq 5 was America's second-best-selling electric vehicle not made by Tesla.
Hyundai broke ground on its Georgia plant in late October 2022.
The project is the largest economic development project the state of Georgia has ever seen.
The project received $2.1 billion in tax breaks and other incentives from state and local governments.
Hyundai accelerated the plant's timeline to start making EVs within two years of groundbreaking due to federal electric vehicle incentives rewarding domestic production.
The Inflation Reduction Act was passed in 2022 with provisions intended to combat climate change.
The law includes a tax credit that saves EV buyers up to $7,500.
The $7,500 tax credit applies only to cars made in North America with domestic batteries.
Hyundai executives initially complained that the Inflation Reduction Act was unfair.
Hyundai President and Global Chief Operating Officer Jose Munoz stated that the law caused the automaker to push to open the Georgia plant sooner.
source_title: Hyundai begins producing EVs at Bryan Co. Metaplant - Statesboro Herald

Nicolas Clement, R&D Director at H&V, and Dr. Amogh Karwa, Director of Product management, stated that GEM has true potential for creating a more sustainable future and will be a great tool for battery manufacturers to enhance performance and help lead batteries compete better with alternative chemistries.
East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
Battery Council International (BCI) annually presents the Innovation Award to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, the former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.
BCI is the leading trade association representing the global battery industry, founded in 1924.
BCI advocates and educates on behalf of battery manufacturers and recyclers, marketers and retailers, suppliers of raw materials and equipment, and battery distributors.
Hollingsworth & Vose is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications.
H&V's advanced materials contribute to clean air, clean liquids, and energy storage.
H&V is family-owned for seven generations and is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, U.S.A.

source_title: Hollingsworth & Vose wins 2026 BCI Innovation Award

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose Company has been awarded the 2026 BCI Innovation Award by Battery Council International for its development of a unique glass fibrous matrix electrode.

The innovation, known as Glass Electrode Material (GEM), helps generate and support a unique microporous structure within the lead active mass of a battery to enhance chargeability performance and cycle life reliability without impacting water loss.
GEM technology improves recharge efficiency through enhanced charge acceptance, reducing fuel-related emissions while improving battery performance, durability, and cycle life reliability.
With approximately three times higher Dynamic Charge Acceptance (DCA), GEM enables next-generation start-stop systems that supports fuel economy gains of up to 5%.
Its fast recharge response makes it well suited for advanced 12-V architectures in hybrid (HEV, PHEV) and electric vehicle auxiliary systems.
The technology platform is applicable across multiple energy storage applications, including automotive batteries, motive power for forklifts, and stationary backup power solutions.
Hollingsworth & Vose developed GEM in partnership with ArcActive.
East Penn Manufacturing and Silicon Joule received Honorable Mention recognition for their joint work on the first commercial bi-pole battery based on lead chemistry and treated silicon wafers.
Battery Council International annually presents this award to celebrate innovation in equipment, processes, services, and products that advance the lead battery industry.
The award was established to honor industry thought leader Sally Breidegam Miksiewicz, the former CEO of East Penn Manufacturing Co.
Roger Miksad serves as President and Executive Director of Battery Council International (BCI).
Nicolas Clement is the R&D Director at Hollingsworth & Vose.
Dr. Amogh Karwa is the Director of Product management at Hollingsworth & Vose.
H&V is a global manufacturer of advanced materials used in filtration, battery, and industrial applications.
H&V is family-owned for seven generations and is headquartered in East Walpole, Massachusetts, U.S.A.
For roughly two centuries, H&V has supported the economy of the future.

facts: Hollingsworth & Vose is a manufacturing company specializing in advanced filtration and energy solutions.
The company provides a wide range of products, including filtration media and battery separators.
Battery separators are supplied to customers in industries such as automotive, healthcare, and food and beverage.
The company operates globally.
Hollingsworth & Vose leverages its extensive R&D capabilities to innovate and meet diverse customer needs.
Hollingsworth & Vose was founded in 1843.
The company has between 1001 and 5000 employees.
Company specialties include water and air filtration, nanofibers, and nanowires.
