facts: Republican Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced that Kia will invest over $200 million at its West Point facility.
The investment will create almost 200 new jobs at the West Point facility.
The investment is intended to accommodate assembly of Kia's new all-electric 2024 EV9 three-row SUV.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia's longstanding partnership with Kia has led to generational job creation and growth for the West Point area.
Governor Brian Kemp noted that the project will help propel Georgia toward its goal of becoming the "e-mobility capital."
The EV9 will become the fifth model assembled at Kia Georgia through a production line transformation.
The EV9 joins the Telluride, Sorento and Sportage SUVs, and the K5 mid-size sedan in Kia Georgia's assembly lineup.
EV9 assembly at the facility is expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Currently, more than 40 percent of Kia vehicles sold in the U.S. are manufactured at the West Point facility.
Adding the EV9 to the lineup will further increase the West Point facility's output.
The EV9 is Kia's first three-row EV SUV and the first Kia EV to be assembled in North America.
The EV9 serves as the flagship vehicle for Kia's global Plan S strategy focused on sustainable mobility leadership.
Sean Yoon, President & CEO of Kia Motors North America and Kia Motors America, stated that the EV9 has the potential to be another change catalyst for Kia, similar to the Telluride.
Sean Yoon described the EV9 as the most innovative vehicle Kia has ever built and a standout in the EV market.
The EV9 will be assembled in West Point, Georgia.
So far, Kia has invested more than $1.9 billion in the state of Georgia.
Kia Georgia is responsible for more than 14,000 plant and supplier jobs in the region.
Governor Kemp and state lawmakers recognized January 31 as "Kia Day" earlier this year to commend Kia's vehicle manufacturing and impact.
Kia's 2006 announcement to establish a state-of-the-art vehicle assembly facility in West Point was one of the biggest single foreign investments in Georgia's history.
source_title: Kia Investing More Than $200 Million In Georgia Facility, Creating 200 New Jobs | Doc and the Block

facts: Kia has shifted its U.S. electric lineup focus from imported EVs to North American production.
Only select battery electric vehicles (BEVs) qualify for the full $7,500 federal tax credit under current guidance.
The qualifying status of each Kia model depends on final assembly location, MSRP limits, and compliance with critical mineral and battery component requirements.
Only vehicles assembled at the West Point, Georgia plant shared with Hyundai are eligible for the full $7,500 credit.
The 2025 Kia EV6 is available in Wind, GT-Line, and GT trims, but only those built in Georgia qualify for the credit.
The 2025 Kia EV6 delivers up to 310 miles of EPA-estimated range and supports ultra-fast DC charging via 800V architecture.
The 800V fast-charging architecture enables adding 200 miles of range in about 18 minutes under ideal conditions.
The EV6 and EV9 support 10–80% charges in under 20 minutes at compatible stations.
SUVs must have a final MSRP under $80,000 to qualify for the full credit, while sedans and crossovers must be under $55,000.
The 2026 Kia EV9 is Kia’s first three-row electric SUV launching in late 2025, with only lower- and mid-tier trims built in Georgia qualifying for the credit.
Higher trims of the 2026 Kia EV9 are priced above the $80,000 limit for SUVs and are ineligible for the full credit.
The 2025 Kia Niro EV is a compact crossover with current production being mixed, meaning only future North American builds will qualify for the credit.
The Kia Sorento PHEV offers a modest electric range of approximately 32 miles and is eligible only for a partial $3,750 credit.
The federal tax credit expires on September 30, 2025, unless extended by Congress, with no grace period or retroactive eligibility.
Purchasers must have finalized ownership or lease agreements before September 30, 2025, to claim the credit.
Income limits do not apply to the federal tax credit itself, but the taxpayer's annual tax liability must equal or exceed $7,500 to claim the full amount.
Leased vehicles may qualify for the credit regardless of assembly location because the fleet owner claims it and typically passes the benefit to lessees through lower payments.
The 2025 Kia EV6 starts around $45,000 for the Wind RWD trim.
The 2026 Kia EV9 starts near $59,000, with top trims exceeding $75,000.
The 2025 Kia Niro EV starts under $43,000.
Dealer markups may apply on high-demand trims, especially Georgia-built versions.
Manufacturer rebates or state-level credits may stack with the federal benefit, though local rules vary by region.
The 2025 EV6 remains Kia’s top-selling electric model in the U.S., competing directly with the Tesla Model Y, Ford Mustang Mach-E, and Hyundai Ioniq 5.
Kia offers advanced warranty coverage including an 8-year/100k mile battery warranty.
The EV9 earned a Top Safety Pick+ rating from IIHS in early 2025.
Recommended service intervals for Kia EVs are every 12 months or 7,500 miles, focusing on brakes, tires, and battery health checks.
All qualifying Kia EV models include advanced safety suites featuring forward collision warning, lane keep assist, automatic emergency braking, and adaptive cruise control.
The EV6 supports bidirectional charging, allowing owners to power tools during outages.
Availability of Georgia-built trims is limited, creating tight trim selection due to MSRP caps.
Buyers are advised to ask dealers specifically for 'Georgia-built' or 'North America assembled' VINs and use dealer ordering systems instead of relying on lot stock.
source_title: Which 2025 Kia Electric Cars Qualify for the $7,500 Tax Credit? A Practical Guide

facts: In 2009, Hyundai Motor Group opened its first Kia car manufacturing plant in the U.S. near a small town on the Georgia-Alabama border.
The project promised thousands of jobs and regional transformation for an area historically predominantly textiles.

Kia spokesperson Randy Jackson stated that the economic impact will change the face of the region and make it better for everyone, with more hotels, restaurants, and housing developments coming in five to ten years from now.
Hyundai Motor Group built a plant 250 miles west of Savannah in West Point, Georgia.
The deal became the largest in Georgia’s history.

In 2005, Jane Fryer was then president of the LaGrange Chamber of Commerce and Economic Development Authority when she took the first call from the state about an undisclosed company interested in a 2,200-acre site in West Point, Georgia.
The LaGrange Development Authority owned land in the area, but the company wanted land farther down where no industrial land was available at the time.
The project turned into reality three years later in 2009 when Kia began production at its first U.S. car manufacturing plant.
Fourteen years ago, the site was all farmland owned by 30 property owners who agreed to sell their generation-owned land for the plant.
The facility has grown to a $1.8 billion investment off Interstate 85.
The plant employs about 3,000 people.
The plant produces about 340,000 cars each year.
Rick Douglas, Senior Manager Public Relations, stated that the area unemployment rate dropped from 12 percent at the time to three percent now.
The plant started manufacturing the Kia Sorento in November 2009.
It now produces three more models including the flagship Telluride.
Ninety percent of the cars made here go to dealerships in the U.S.
Kia suppliers are spread along I-85 from Valley, Alabama all the way east to Greenville, Georgia.
These suppliers have created another 14,000 jobs to support this particular operation.
The plant has led to strong partnerships with area schools, technical colleges and universities to develop an automotive workforce.
There has been a dramatic turn toward technology and smart manufacturing as a result of companies coming into the area.
The project led to a melding of American and Korean culture in the region while producing the best quality vehicles built in North America.
source_title: Kia car manufacturing plant in Georgia transforms the region

facts: A group of migrant workers and auto part suppliers for Kia and Hyundai are seeking court approval of a $1.2 million settlement to resolve class action claims of labor trafficking and racketeering.

Mexican engineers and technicians alleged that auto parts manufacturer SL Alabama LLC, staffing agency Allswell, and recruiting firm SPJ Connect Inc. operated a “bait and switch” scheme to recruit high-skilled workers on NAFTA professional visas and place them in low-paid, manual labor jobs.
TN visa holders recruited by Allswell or SPJ and employed at SL production facilities between Oct. 25, 2019 to the present will be eligible for the settlement fund.
The class members in exchange will release the defendants from any claims related to their recruitment or employment.

The lawsuit is one of several in the Southeast in which auto manufacturers have been accused of abusing the TN visa program, which allows professional workers from Mexico and Canada to fill jobs in the US.
Logistics companies Hyundai Glovis and GFA Alabama were accused in a June complaint in the Northern District of Georgia of exploiting the TN visas for cheap labor.
The TN visa program was established as part of the North American Free Trade Agreement and continued in the same form after being replaced by the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement in 2020.
Plaintiffs alleged they were paid less and required to work longer hours than US workers and non-Hispanic employees.
Plaintiffs claimed they were threatened with deportation in response to complaints about their employment and pay.
The case is Peregrina v. SL Alabama, LLC, filed in the US District Court for the Northern District of Georgia as No. 3:23-cv-00206, with a settlement proposed on July 19, 2024.

facts: One of the final and largest pieces of Kia Motors Manufacturing Inc.’s second 5,400-ton transfer stamping press arrived at the West Point, Georgia facility on April 18, 2011.
The new press will be used to stamp and shape millions of vehicle panels at the $1 billion facility and is a key element of the plant’s ongoing production expansion.
KMMG will be hiring new team members to support the increased production driven by the new equipment.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development served as the lead agency on the project from the beginning and was critical in all aspects of the site’s development.
The Georgia Ports Authority facilitated the arrival and discharge of the massive cargo shipment.
The Georgia Department of Transportation escorted numerous shipments of cargo from Savannah to West Point.
Georgia Quick Start served as Kia’s workforce development and training partner for the project.

Governor Nathan Deal stated that the equipment arrival is a fine example of Georgia’s commitment to supporting existing industry for growth, citing resources like Quick Start workforce training and strengthened ports and highways.
In 2010, Kia Motors recorded its best-ever full-year sales with 356,268 units sold in the U.S., increasing market share for the 16th consecutive year to an all-time high of 3.1 percent.
Kia set its company’s all-time single month sales record in March (prior to April 2011), topping the previous record by more than 40%.

The crown assembly and 3,500 tons of additional stamping equipment traveled more than 15,000 miles from Masan, Korea to the Port of Savannah, then across Georgia’s highways and interstates to West Point.
KMMG is the first manufacturing site in North America for Seoul-based Kia Motors Corporation.
The plant began mass production of the 2011 Kia Sorento on November 16, 2009.
At full capacity, the plant will have the ability to build 300,000 vehicles annually using U.S. and globally-sourced parts.
KMMG Director of Human Resources Randy Jackson thanked local communities in and around Troup County for their cooperation and welcoming environment.

facts: Superior Essex is a fiber optic cable manufacturer based out of Atlanta, Georgia.
The company manufactures products in Brownwood, Texas and maintains locations in Hoisington, Kansas.
Kate Fegley-Lummus serves as the marketing project and partner manager at Superior Essex.
Fegley-Lummus transitioned from a 13-year career in NASCAR to telecommunications approximately two years ago.
The company aligns its strategy with BEAD and prior federal funding programs including Capital Projects Fund, RDOF, and Middle Mile.

To address a projected shortage of 200,000 technicians for broadband deployment, the company supplies fiber optic cable to trade and technical schools for splicing training.
Superior Essex has completed the self-certification process for BABA compliance in coordination with the NTIA.
The company updated its part numbers to explicitly identify BABA-compliant cables for BEAD and other grant-funded projects.
A dedicated webpage listing BABA-compliant products is maintained on the company's website.
Superior Essex emphasizes keeping manufacturing jobs in the United States as a core operational goal.
source_title: Behind the Scenes: Superior Essex's Mission to Expand U.S. Broadband Connectivity! | Fierce Network

facts: Superior Essex is a global leader in the magnet wire and communication cables industry.
The company announced the next phase in the rebranding of its magnet wire business.
As part of its recent buyout of Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, the company will change its brand name to Essex Solutions.
Under the new name, Essex Solutions will encompass all current Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire locations worldwide.
Essex Energy in Italy will now be included in Essex Solutions, further reinforcing the company's global reach and capabilities.
Related businesses Lacroix + Kress, IVA Insulations, and Hi-Wire will join the Essex Solutions organization while continuing to operate under their existing brand identities.
This consolidation will enable better coordination and collaboration among the brands, resulting in improved efficiency and effectiveness.
Daniel Choi, President and CEO of Superior Essex, stated that choosing Essex Solutions represents the comprehensive nature of their business operations.
Choi noted that aligning brands under one grouping allows them to offer a complete suite of solutions for customers and take further advantage of vertical integration and global reach.
The company is seeing many markets experiencing rapid transformations and aims to proactively shape the xEV automotive industry, as well as power, electrification, and renewable energy.
During the transition period, customers and stakeholders may see materials bearing either the existing Essex Furukawa or the new Essex Solutions name.
All changes are expected to be completed by April 30, 2025.
Essex Solutions will unveil a new logo in the near future to reflect its evolution and commitment to innovation and excellence.
Superior Essex is the parent brand of Essex Solutions, Superior Essex Communications, IVA, Lacroix + Kress, Hi-Wire, and Essex Brownell.
The company has over 3,000 employees in 11 countries across three continents.
Superior Essex is the leading global provider of magnet wire and is at the forefront of communications fiber application as well as Power over Ethernet (PoE) technology.
The company is instrumental in creating disruptive technological advancements in the electrification of the automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Superior Essex is committed to pioneering smart building technology while also setting the pace on sustainability.
source_title: Superior Essex Announces Essex Solutions, Relaunching Magnet Wire Brand

facts: For over 90 years, Superior Essex has met and exceeded wire and cable requirements for the automotive, industrial, commercial, residential, energy and communications markets.
The company traces its history back to 1930, when the Essex Wire Corporation was founded in Detroit, Michigan.
Superior Cable Corporation was founded in 1954 in Hickory, North Carolina.
In 1999, Superior TeleCom (formerly Superior Cable) acquired Essex International (formerly Essex Wire).
The acquisition formed the company now known globally as Superior Essex.
Superior Essex is currently based in Atlanta, Georgia.
The company produces communication cable designed to be more adaptable, efficient, and sustainable.
Annie Bevan serves as the Global Head of Sustainability at Superior Essex.
Annie Bevan has over 10 years of experience developing processes, procedures, and standards for sustainability claims in products, supply chains, buildings, and manufacturing operations.
She is widely regarded in the industry as an expert in sustainability program development, corporate social responsibility, and third-party verification.
In her role, she oversees Superior Essex's market-leading sustainability program and works to accelerate it through engagement with customers and suppliers.

Annie Bevan supports or has supported various leading entities as a technical advisor and collaborator, including the US Green Building Council’s Materials and Resources TAG for the LEED rating system.

She also collaborates with the International Living Future Institute (ILFI), my green lab, the Department of Defense, the General Services Administration, Clean Production Action, the GreenScreen Program, the Sustainable Purchasing Leadership Council, and the Collaborative for High Performing Schools.
source_title: Annie Bevan, Superior Essex | materialsCAN BTF | Built Environment Plus

facts: South Korea's Mirae Asset Global Investments invested $200 million in Essex Solutions, a US magnet wire manufacturing affiliate under cable and industrial electric machinery conglomerate LS Group.
Essex Solutions succeeded pre-IPO under a deal with a consortium consisting of Mirae Asset Global Management and KCGI.
The consortium acquired a 20% stake in the company through a paid-in capital increase.
Essex Solutions is the worldwide No. 1 company in the magnet wind market.
Its predecessor, Essex, was an American wire company founded in 1930 and renamed Superior Essex (SPSX) in 1954 after acquiring the telecommunications wire business.
LS Group acquired SPSX in 2008 with an investment of about 1 trillion won.
In 2020, LS Group established Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire (EFMW), a joint venture specializing in windings with Japan's Furukawa Electric.
In April last year, the company acquired all of EFMW's shares from Furukawa Electric and rebranded to Essex Solutions.
Essex Solutions' main products are magnet windings for electronic devices such as transformers and motors.
A market expected to grow rapidly in the future is special windings for electric vehicles, which are wound in coils around motors to convert electrical energy into kinetic energy.
LS will soon select a listing organizer for Essex Solutions and plans to go public this year.
Initially, the company had planned to list on the Kosdaq and is also considering listing on the Nasdaq due to growing interest from US investors.
source_title: Mirae Asset invests $200 mn in LS Group affiliate in US  - KED Global

facts: Headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
Leading manufacturer of cable and wire with a 75-year heritage.
Among the largest industry players in North America with a significant global presence.
Operates 23 production facilities in the United States, Mexico, and Europe.
Has an established global distribution network.
Customer base includes cable television and telephone companies, distributors, and manufacturers in various industries.
Offers more than 500 magnet wire and winding wire products.
Products marketed through Essex Group Inc. in North America and China.
Serves the European market through EssexNexans joint venture.
Magnet wire products are critical components in millions of items, including automobiles, home appliances, generators, motors, transformers, and commercial machinery.
Has business units devoted to specific end markets.
World's largest producer of outside plant copper communications cables via Superior Essex Communications LP.
Serves original equipment manufacturers and the motor repair market via Essex Brownell, Essex Express, and Active Industries units offering electrical insulation products.
Formed in 1998 by the combination of Essex Group Inc. and Superior TeleCom.
Roots trace to Detroit, Michigan, where Addison E. Holton established Essex Wire Corp. in 1930.
Early workforce produced battery cables, wiring harnesses, and other wire items for the Ford Model A from leased space in Ford Motor Company's Highland Park plant.
Acquired Logansport, Indiana-based RBM Manufacturing Company, a producer of electrical switches and automotive components.
Bought a rubber insulation plant in Jonesboro, Indiana in 1932 known for Paranite building wire.
Acquired a vacant industrial facility in Fort Wayne, Indiana in 1936 formerly housing Dudlo Manufacturing Company.
Produced enough magnet wire to build millions of transformers used by America and its allies during World War II.
Plants produced thousands of miles of field telephone wire for the Army Signal Corps and wiring harnesses for B-24 bombers.
Awarded the 'E' Award by the Secretary of War.
Relocated headquarters to Fort Wayne in 1954.
Walter F. Probst succeeded Addison Holton as president in 1958; Holton retired in 1962 and Probst became board chairman.
By 1965, sales totaled $355 million with seven divisions, 41 plants throughout North America, and 26 warehouses.
First listed on the New York Stock Exchange in 1965 with 5 million shares.

Unveiled an $8 million expansion initiative in 1965 including $3.2 million for Essex Wire and Cable division, $2.5 million for Essex Automotive division, and $2.3 million for acquisition/expansion of other facilities.
Paul W. O'Malley named company president in early 1966; Probst continued as chairman and CEO.
By 1968, building a new 30,000-square-foot facility in Fort Wayne for Metal Products division; had 67 plants in 30 states and Canada.
Acquired Transport Motor Express Inc. in a $12 million stock deal in 1968.
Acquired 14 other companies in 1969.
Essex Wire Corporation operated under name Essex International Inc. by 1970 with an employee base of 25,700.
Operated a facility in Northern Ireland at the end of the 1970s.

Divisions included Magnet Wire; IWI; Wire and Cable; Controls; Metal Products and Plastics; Power Conductors; Communications and CATV; Industrial Laboratory Products; Wire Assembly; and Electro-Mechanical.
Sales reached approximately $845 million in 1973 with about 100 plants.

Merged with East Hartford, Connecticut-based United Aircraft Corporation (United Technologies Corporation) in 1974 creating the nation's 30th largest company with combined 1973 sales of $3.13 billion.
Walter Probst retired as chairman and CEO in 1974, succeeded by Paul O'Malley.
James A. O'Connor succeeded O'Malley in 1981 after 31 years at Essex.
Faced criticism from labor leaders in the late 1970s for being anti-labor, encouraging multiple unions, scattering production, paying low wages, and providing poor benefits.
U.S. Justice Department charged Essex and five other wire manufacturers with price fixing in September 1978.
Founder Addison Holton died at age 83 in 1980.
Announced plans to invest $3 million in its Fort Wayne facility (world headquarters) in 1982, scheduled for completion in 1984.
Employed 8,982 workers worldwide with an annual payroll of $26.8 million by 1983.
Became a privately owned firm in 1988 when MS/Essex Holdings bought it from United Technologies; John M. Bruce was president and CEO.
Purchased by Bessemer Holdings in 1992, remaining privately owned.
Became publicly traded again in 1997.
Acquired magnet wire and electrical motor distributor Brownell in 1995, merged into distribution arm as Essex Brownell.
Acquired Active Industries (converter/fabricator of electrical insulation products) in 1998.

Announced acquisition by Superior Telecom Inc. in September 1998 in a deal valued at $1.4 billion in cash, stock, and debt; finalized in 1999 making Essex a subsidiary and creating North America's largest cable and wire manufacturer.
Steven Abbott became Superior's president and chief operating officer following the deal.
Superior Telecom started in 1954 in Hickory, North Carolina as Superior Cable Corporation producing plastic insulated twisted pair cable and wire.
Production took place in a new 32,000-square-foot plant authorized by Hickory Development Corp. at a cost of $160,000.
First reel of cable produced in August, delivered to Hickory Telephone Company.
Walker Geitner was first president; explained cables transfer intelligence for telephone, fire alarms, traffic control, and pipeline systems.
Geitner named chairman in 1959; James L. Robb succeeded him as president.
Established second manufacturing plant in Rocky Mount, North Carolina in 1960.
Expanded headquarters plant three times by 1962 to 150,000 square feet; production expanded to coaxial cables and wire for missile, aircraft, and industrial applications.
Rolled out industry's first all-plastic drop wire in 1963.
Erected new cable plant in Brownwood, Texas in 1964.
By 1965, home plant had 125,000 square feet of manufacturing space, 35,000 square feet of office space, and 35,000-square-foot warehouse; annual sales reached nearly $20 million.
Acquired by Continental Telephone in 1967, renamed Superior Continental Corp.
Annual sales reached $90 million in 1969 and approximately $125 million in 1971.
Announced plans to build an eight-story corporate office facility in Hickory for $3 million in 1971.
Divested by Continental Telephone in 1976; purchased by Robert C. Pittman and A. L. Viles for $17.1 million in cash and stock.
Deal included Hickory Brand Telephone Cords, Keller, Texas-based Communication Apparatus Company, and a non-operating plant in Angier, North Carolina.
Acquired by Siecor Corporation in 1980; Siecor relocated from Horseheads, New York to Hickory in 1981.
Divested by Siecor five years later (1986); went independent and marketed fiber optic cable.
Alpine Group Inc. acquired Superior in 1993; added premises data cables to lineup.
Went public in 1996 on NYSE; annual sales reached $500 million in 1997.
Changed name to Superior Essex following acquisition of Essex International in 1999.
Held about 30 percent of market for copper building wire by August 1999.
Announced closure of three wire manufacturing plants and layoff of 469 employees in 1999 to optimize financial returns amid oversupply.

Closed plants in Glendale, Arizona; Pauline, Kansas; and Tiffin, Ohio; continued production at sites in Sikeston, Missouri; Florence, Alabama; Anaheim, California; Lafayette, Indiana; and Columbia City, Indiana.
Awarded three-year contract to supply cable and wire to Alltel Communications Products Inc. in 2000 serving 8.5 million customers in 25 states.
Launched e-commerce Web site in late 2000 for order entry, shipment tracking, and industry news.
Occupied a 228,000-square-foot warehousing and distribution facility in northeast Indiana by December 2001 previously occupied by Raytheon.
Maintained workforce of 5,000 in 2003 while giving employees better benefits without increasing out-of-pocket contributions.
Stephen M. Carter was CEO as of early 2004.
Announced plans to acquire North American outside plant communications cable and wire operations of Belden Inc. in April 2004 for approximately $95 million.
Carter stated acquisition aligns capacity with demand which declined 50 percent in North America since 2000, requiring consolidation beyond internal solutions.
Confident in transitioning Belden's customer base which generated approximately $200 million in revenues last year.
Announced revenues reached $1.8 billion in 2005, up from $1.4 billion in 2004.
Carter attributed increased revenues to consolidating acquisitions and noted increased earnings despite skyrocketing energy costs.
Principal Subsidiaries: Superior Essex Communications LP; Essex Group, Inc.
Principal Competitors: Alcatel; APA Cables and Networks Inc.; General Cable Corporation.
source_title: Superior Essex Inc. - Company Profile, Information, Business Description, History, Background Information on Superior Essex Inc.

facts: Superior Essex Communications President Brad Johnson will participate in a leadership panel on optical cables at the CRU Wire & Cable Conference.

The conference agenda includes a special focus on the use of wires and cables in the automotive industry, with insights into trends specific to power, communication, construction, and automotive sectors impacting business operations.

Natalie Noor-Drugan, Senior Analyst and Editor-in-Chief at CRU, noted that wire and cable companies must provide increasingly high-tech products and services to meet government goals for electric vehicles (EVs) and connected vehicles, as well as the clean energy transition, smart grids, smart cities, 5G, IoT, and factory automation.
Companies in the sector must increasingly demonstrate sustainable supply chains, low environmental impact products, and compliance with recent regulations to succeed.
Superior Essex is a leading manufacturer and supplier of communications cables and accessory products serving the communications industry and related distribution markets for over 80 years.
Superior Essex Communications operates as a trading name for Superior Essex International LP and other companies within Superior Essex Inc.
The company's corporate address is located at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.

Brad Johnson stated that joining the leadership panel offers a significant opportunity to highlight how Superior Essex Communications leads in next-generation wire/wireless, digital infrastructure, sustainability, and technology innovation to help shape the future wiring landscape.

The CRU Wire & Cable Conference will be held on June 11-12, 2019, at Steigenberger Wiltcher's in Brussels, Belgium, exploring EU construction, power, and communication projects and wire/cable quality standards.

source_title: Superior Essex Communications on Leadership Panel at CRU - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex Communications announced the release of the Hybrid I/O Riser 2x2 cable for indoor/outdoor applications on June 25, 2019.

The Hybrid I/O Riser 2x2 is the first and only hybrid communication cable listed at Wet Locations UL; UL Direct Burial listed for interlock armor version, and UL listed for use in Class 3 and Class 2 circuits.
The cable meets ICEA 696 for indoor and outdoor use, with dry water-blocking technology in the core and in simplex 2 mm.
Both stranded conductors are protected with special PVC designed to meet UL 'wet location' test requirements and available in AWG 12, 14, and 16 conductor sizes.
The I/O Riser 2x2 Hybrid can be used without armor for standard indoor applications or with interlock armor making it the only fiber cable also rated by UL for Direct Burial without conduit.

Both products, F3C4 (unarmored) and F1C4 (armored), feature a UL sunlight-rated PVC jacket, annealed stranded copper conductors, wet location PVC insulation, buffer optical fiber, dielectric aramid strength member, dry water-blocking cord, and glass yarn.
Product F3C4 has a diameter of 0.33 while product F1C4 adds interlock aluminum armor expanding it to a diameter of 0.64.

Brian Ensign, Vice President of Manufacturing Demand and Sales for Superior Essex Communications, stated that this pioneering technology is an important step to expand PoE technology beyond the 100-meter limit.

Brian Ensign noted there is a market gap to expand the use of fiber and hybrid cable technology by filling it with this product, opening many possibilities for what and where fiber products can be done and used.
For Superior Essex Communications, this represents an exciting technology extension in the market, and they are happy to take a leadership position.
The I/O Riser 2x2 Hybrid is another product in the PowerWise Fiber product line released by Superior Essex Communications.

The PowerWise Fiber platform is cabling technology that enables delivering power and data through one cable, offering capabilities to optimize, customize, and connect any environment to meet unique business demands.
Superior Essex is a leading manufacturer and supplier of communication cables and accessory products serving the communications industry and related distribution markets for over 80 years.
Superior Essex is a trademark of Superior Essex International LP and other companies from Superior Essex Inc.
Superior Essex corporate address is located at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.
source_title: Superior Essex Communications Releases First-of-Its-Kind Hybrid Indoor/Outdoor Cable - Superior Essex – Corporate

facts: Superior Essex Communications is a leading global manufacturer and supplier of communications cable and accessory products.
The company announced the release of its latest MissionNext report on June 12, 2024.

The MissionNext report highlights sustainability initiatives, including progress in reducing environmental footprint, minimizing waste, conserving energy, setting carbon targets, and advancing product transparency.
The report emphasizes customer value through continuous innovation, quality focus, and new products designed to improve performance, reliability, and cost-efficiency for customers.
The report details industry progress driven by thought leadership, collaboration, advocacy, involvement in key industry initiatives, partnerships, and standards development.

Annie Bevan, Global Head of Sustainability at Superior Essex Communications, stated that the company's commitment to sustainability, innovation, and customer satisfaction is at the core of its operations and reflects dedication to creating a more sustainable future.
source_title: Superior Essex Communications Unveils Latest MissionNext Report

facts: Superior Essex Communications is a leading manufacturer and provider of communication cables in North America.
The company received recognition in more than a dozen industry awards and finalist nominations throughout 2020.
Awards recognized the company's product and solution innovation, sustainability efforts, marketing campaigns, and assets.
Brad R. Johnson serves as President of Superior Essex Communications.
Melanie Singleton is the Director of Marketing Communications at Superior Essex International LP - Communication.

The company leads in innovation through Power-over-Ethernet, smart buildings and cities, 5G fiber infrastructure including hybrid and composite solutions, and technological advancements in its product development center.
Superior Essex Communications was the first company to receive Zero Waste to Landfill certification at its Hoisington (KS) facility.
It is the first wire and cable manufacturer to contribute points toward LEED certification.
It is the first to offer verified sustainable cables across more than 55 product families.

facts: Essex Brownell, a division of Superior Essex and one of the leading North American distributors/manufacturers of magnet wire, motors and drives, electrical insulation, tapes, repair parts, and shop and test equipment, renewed its lease for its Northeast distribution center in Edison, NJ.
The lease renewal is a five-year extension on 60,000 square feet of space dedicated to supporting regional inventory.

The distribution center in the New York metropolitan area will continue to host an expanding product line covering all Essex Brownell product families, including but not limited to flexible insulations, varnishes and resins, motors, parts, equipment, and lead and magnet wire.
Michael McIlwain, President of Essex Brownell and Hi-Wire, stated that the lease renewal signals a long-term investment in this location and in Essex Brownell overall.

McIlwain noted that during the global pandemic, stability and reliability are very important aspects of doing business, and the renewal sends a clear signal that Essex Brownell is committed to the Northeast and Mid-Atlantic regions while sourcing globally and delivering locally.
Superior Essex is the parent brand for Essex Furukawa Magnet Wire, Superior Essex Communications, and Essex Brownell.

Superior Essex's technologies are crucial for creating disruptive advances in the electrification of automotive, energy, and industrial sectors.
Essex Brownell operates as an international distributor of magnet wire and auxiliary products used to build and repair motors, generators, transformers, and electrical components.
Essex Brownell provides discrete materials along with custom manufacturing and rewinding services through its Essex Active transformation facilities.
Superior Essex corporate headquarters is located at 5770 Powers Ferry Rd. NW Suite 400, Atlanta, GA 30327.

facts: Suzuki’s Georgia-based ATV manufacturing facility celebrated the production of its 250,000th ATV on March 31st, 2008.
The facility will celebrate 2008 as the 25th anniversary of the industry’s first 4-wheeled ATV, the 1983 Suzuki LT-125, with anniversary events scheduled throughout the summer.
Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corp. (SMAC) opened in Rome, Ga., in May 2002 as Suzuki’s only U.S.-based manufacturing facility.
SMAC initially began producing the Eiger series of ATVs upon opening.
The facility currently produces KingQuad 400s, 450s, and 750s.
SMAC employs 300 workers who build ATV frames, mold plastic, and assemble units.
Production capacity exceeds 200 units per eight-hour shift.
In the previous year (2007), almost 60,000 ATVs were produced at the facility.

A commemorative ceremony was held at the Rome factory on March 31, 2008, attended by employees, local dignitaries, media, invited guests, and the Team Rockstar / MSR / Suzuki / Yoshimura / Pro Taper race team.
SMAC’s ATV Sales Manager Alan Horne highlighted the successful cooperation between Suzuki and its host community of Rome during the ceremony.
Rome Mayor Wright Bagby attended the ceremony and thanked Suzuki for helping expand the city's manufacturing base over the last 10 years.
Mayor Bagby stated that Suzuki helped save manufacturing jobs and maintain a higher percentage of manufacturing jobs in Rome than most places in the South or country.
Kory Ellis, Manager for Team Rockstar / MSR / Suzuki / Yoshimura / Pro Taper, introduced the factory's ATV race team at the event.
AMA ATV Motocross Series current points leader Dustin Wimmer rode the historic 250,000th ATV off the production line.
The red KingQuad 750 AXi 4×4 was signed by all factory personnel and local government attendees.
Mel Harris, Vice President of American Suzuki’s Motorcycle/ATV division, praised Rome, Ga., and SMAC employees for their commitment to the project and Suzuki's dedication to the ATV market.
source_title: U.S. Factory Produces 250,000th Suzuki ATV - Roadracing World Magazine | Motorcycle Riding, Racing & Tech News

facts: Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) celebrated its 10-Year Anniversary on February 25, 2011.
The plant was built in the United States in 2001 to demonstrate Suzuki’s commitment to the all terrain vehicle (ATV) business.
Initially the plant built the all-new 400cc Eiger and 500cc Vinson ATVs.
Currently, SMAC assembles the award winning KINGQUAD line of ATVs for the North American and global markets.
Mr. Nobuyuki Yamane serves as President of SMAC.

Mr. Yamane stated: “As we celebrate 10 years as a company, we reflect back on the last couple of years which have been very difficult due to the overall economic situation and its effect on the entire ATV industry. We look forward to the future and a rebound in the ATV industry as we focus our Management Plan for FY2011 as we look ’10 Years and Beyond’ as a company.”
SMAC has recently increased production capacity.
SMAC recalled some team members affected by previous workforce reductions.
SMAC looks forward to increasing production in FY2011.
SMAC is located in Rome, Ga.
The plant's skilled and dedicated team members are helping to secure Suzuki’s reputation as “First on Four Wheels.”
source_title: Suzuki Manufacturing Of America Celebrates 10th Anniversary - Roadracing World Magazine | Motorcycle Riding, Racing & Tech News

facts: When opened in 2001, the Suzuki Manufacturing of America Corporation (SMAC) created hundreds of jobs in Rome, Ga., to assemble Suzuki’s 400cc and 500cc ATVs.
Now celebrating 14 years of ATV assembly, the facility today has expanded to cover more than 130,000 square feet on a 35-acre site in the suburb northwest of Atlanta.
Workers there now handle metal fabrication, injection molding, and engine assembly in the process of assembling Suzuki’s complete line of award-winning KingQuad ATVs.

Rod Lopusnak, Director, Operations Head, Motorcycle/ATV Sales Operations, stated that the SMAC facility has produced over 400,000 ATVs that have been distributed not only here in the U.S., but also to Suzuki distribution partners around the world.

Ricky Carmichael and Carey Hart, leaders of the RCH Soaring Eagle/Jimmy Johns/Suzuki Factory Racing Supercross team, toured the facility with the Fox Sports 1 TV crew leading up to this weekend’s AMA Supercross race in nearby Atlanta.
Multi-time Supercross champ and Suzuki rider Carmichael joined Hart in cutting the 14th Anniversary cake at the facility.
Carmichael and Hart spent time with SMAC employees in celebration of the event, and the two signed autographs at the facility.
The SMAC facility’s recent addition of engine assembly makes Suzuki even more responsive to the needs of its dealers and customers in 52 countries around the world.
The expansion also adds manufacturing jobs in this northwest Georgia town, and reaffirms Suzuki’s commitment to assembling KingQuad ATVs in the United States.
Suzuki Motor of America, Inc. (SMAI) distributes Motorcycles, ATVs, Scooters, Outboard Motors and Automotive Parts and Accessories via an extensive dealer network throughout 49 states.
Suzuki Motor Corporation (SMC), based in Hamamatsu, Japan, is a diversified worldwide manufacturer of Motorcycles, ATVs, Scooters, Automobiles and Outboard Motors.
SMC was founded in 1909 and incorporated in 1920.
SMC has 147 distributors in 201 countries.

facts: Suzuki Motor is the latest major Japanese automaker to bet on solid-state batteries for its upcoming electric vehicles.
On March 4, Suzuki announced an agreement with Kanadevia Corp to acquire its all-solid-state EV battery business.
Kanadevia is a leading Japanese industrial and engineering firm that began developing all-solid-state lithium-on batteries (AS-LiB) in 2006.
Kanadevia's all-solid-state batteries have been deployed in real-world situations, including space applications.
In February 2022, Kanadevia and the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) launched an all-solid-state battery to the International Space Station, marking the “world’s first” mission of its kind.

Kanadevia stated its all-solid-state batteries offer superior safety and performance across a wide temperature range from -40℃ to +120℃, making them suitable for aerospace and other high-temp, vacuum conditions.
Suzuki will acquire Kanadevia’s all-solid-state battery technology, including its proprietary dry manufacturing process.
The acquisition deal is expected to close on July 1, 2026.
The batteries have passed nail-piercing tests without igniting, smoking, or exploding, whereas traditional lithium-ion batteries would ignite under the same conditions.
Suzuki launched its first mass-produced electric vehicle in late 2025, the e-Vitara, which is a twin to the Toyota Urban Cruiser EV.
Suzuki joins Toyota, Honda, and Nissan with plans to develop all-solid-state batteries to boost range and safety for upcoming EVs.
In January, Japanese oil giant Idemitsu broke ground on its large-scale solid electrolyte pilot plant in collaboration with Toyota.
The electrolytes from Idemitsu's pilot plant will be used in all-solid-state batteries for future Toyota electric vehicles.
Toyota, Nissan, and Honda aim to launch EVs equipped with all-solid-state batteries around 2027 or 2028, with mass production planned closer toward the end of the decade.
While Japanese brands are racing to introduce promising new battery tech, China and German automakers have made recent “breakthroughs” as solid-state batteries move from the lab to the real world.

Suzuki did not reveal details such as estimated driving range or energy density for its acquired technology, while Toyota revealed a solid-state battery pack last year claiming it could achieve a 1,200 km (745 miles) range and fast charging in under 10 minutes.

Recent progress in Japan, China, Europe, and the US is proving that electric vehicles will become more efficient, faster charging, and more affordable as batteries, powertrains, and other EV tech continue advancing.
At its Disruptive Technology event this week, BYD introduced its new LFP Blade Battery 2.0 and Flash Charging tech, which enable over 1,000 km of CLTC range and 10-minute fast charging.
source_title: Japanese brand buys solid-state EV battery company, not Toyota

facts: Suzuki Motor of America, Inc. (SMAI) announced the creation of a new manufacturing plant in Hamamatsu, Japan, home to its parent company, Suzuki Motor Corp.
The new facility combines engineering, development, engine production, and vehicle assembly into one location to increase efficiency in motorcycle production and delivery.
The five-year consolidation plan for the new facility was originally announced in 2014 and is near completion as of June 2018.

Previously, Suzuki motorcycles were developed and manufactured across three separate locations in Japan: product engineering and development at the Ryuyo facility, engine production at the Takatsuka plant, and motorcycle assembly at the Toyokawa plant.
The consolidated operations are located in the new Hamamatsu Plant in the Miyakoda district.
The new facility occupies a nearly 700,000 square foot building on a site covering more than 40 acres.
The facility will produce the majority of Suzuki two-wheeled products for U.S. distribution.
The site location was chosen in part due to its geographically desirable location and proximity to Suzuki headquarters.

Kerry Graeber, Vice President MC/ATV Sales and Marketing for SMAI, stated that the consolidation underlines Suzuki’s commitment to engineering and manufacturing motorcycles at the forefront of performance and innovation.
Suzuki operates an All-Terrain Vehicle assembly plant in Rome, Georgia.
The Rome, Georgia facility has been active since 2001.
Suzuki Manufacturing of America (SMAC) exclusively builds utility-focused ATVs for worldwide distribution at the Rome, Georgia site.
Suzuki launched all-new versions of its KingQuad 750 and KingQuad 500 in May.

facts: Thermal ceramics are a class of advanced ceramic materials and components specifically designed to withstand, generate, manage, or retain heat in high-temperature environments.
Product categories include ceramic heaters, thermal radiation ceramics, thermal insulation ceramics, and ceramics for high-temperature load-bearing applications.

GORGEOUS CERAMICS utilizes advanced processes such as isostatic pressing, dry pressing, injection molding, tape casting, precision machining, and high-temperature sintering to customize parts according to client drawings.
Customizable products include thermocouple protection tubes, crucibles, firing plates, furnace structural components, heating assemblies, and various customized high-temperature parts.
These products can meet the high-temperature application needs of industries such as semiconductors, metallurgy, heat treatment, chemicals, and new energy.
Commonly used material systems for thermal ceramics include alumina (Al₂O₃), zirconium oxide (ZrO₂), silicon carbide (SiC), silicon nitride (Si₃N₄), and aluminum nitride (AlN).
Ceramic heating elements are mainly used in industrial, semiconductor, energy, and medical industries to provide fast, stable, and precise heating.
Ceramic crucibles are mainly used in metallurgy, semiconductors, chemicals, and laboratories for melting and sintering materials at high temperatures.
Thermal radiation ceramics are used to emit and distribute infrared heat for high-temperature heating, drying, and heat treatment systems.
Thermal insulation ceramics effectively reduce heat loss and protect equipment by maintaining a stable temperature in high-temperature, high-energy-consumption systems.
Ceramic igniters provide rapid ignition for combustion systems in industrial equipment and household appliances.
Forming and manufacturing technologies include dry pressing, isostatic pressing, injection molding, extrusion molding, and casting.

Post-processing and finishing capabilities include high-temperature sintering, atmosphere-controlled sintering, CNC precision machining, precision grinding and polishing, and high-precision dimensional control and tolerance management.
The company accepts customization for all types of ceramic designs based on provided drawings.
