facts: L.K. Technology Holdings Limited (Stock code: 558) announced annual results for the twelve months ended 31 March, 2026.

The group operates in the intelligent equipment manufacturing industry and faced cyclical adjustments with demand-side pressures from downstream vehicle enterprises' slowing retail sales growth and tightened capital expenditures.
Revenue for the year amounted to HK$5,609 million; gross profit was HK$1,375 million with a 24.5% gross margin; adjusted net profit was HK$49.5 million with a 0.9% net profit margin.
Total assets reached HK$13.3 billion (up 11.2% year-on-year), with net cash of HK$1.32 billion.
The group has three core business segments: die-casting (metal blank forming), injection molding (plastic part forming), and CNC machining centre (high-precision finishing).
Die-casting machinery is the core business, generating HK$3,663 million in revenue (65.3% of total), primarily serving automotive vehicle industries.

Injection molding segment generated HK$1,798 million in revenue (32.1% of total), with toy industry revenue growing over 55% for two consecutive years to 16.7% of contribution, achieving a 3-year CAGR of 38.7%.
CNC machining centre business generated HK$148 million in revenue (2.6% of total).
R&D expenses reached HK$318 million during the year, representing a 24.6% year-on-year increase.
The group commercialized the world’s first 16,000T ultra-large intelligent die-casting unit, setting a new industry benchmark.
The group continues to secure and deliver multiple 9000T-class ultra-large die casting machines.

LK TECH's 6,000T–16,000T ultra-large intelligent die-casting cells integrate digital twin technology with the LK-NET cloud die-casting management system and were selected as one of the “Outstanding Cases of New Quality Productive Forces in the Automotive Industry 2025”.
These solutions are fully compatible with mass production requirements for full vehicle body structural components.
As at the end of the year, LK TECH ranked first globally in cumulative number of ultra-large die-casting machines delivered.

The group's TPI semi-solid magnesium alloy forming technology covers a full-scale product matrix from small precision parts to ultra-large structural parts and supports modular transformation of traditional die-casting equipment.
This technology enables a 50% reduction in energy consumption and a 20% increase in product toughness, supporting commercialization of magnesium alloys.
The group's self-developed 5,000-tonne TPI semi-solid forming module secured an order from a leading OEM for inner tailgate panels for new energy vehicles.
Integrating functions previously requiring nine separate sheet metal parts into one component achieves a 45% weight reduction and increases material utilization to 70%.
Orders for TPI magnesium alloy equipment grew strongly, with shipment value exceeding RMB100 million.
Revenue from the related magnesium alloy business increased by 291.1% in the second half of the financial year compared to the first half.

The group developed zirconium-based amorphous alloy die casting equipment integrating vacuum melting, automatic solid material feeding, and full-process automation, reaching internationally advanced standards for high-end products like foldable screen hinges and medical implants.

Low-pressure die-casting machines feature innovative inert gas pressurisation and waste heat recovery technologies, reducing overall energy consumption by 30%, used for automotive wheels and motor end covers.
Horizontal extrusion casting machines meet production requirements for high-performance components such as automotive steering knuckles, subframes, and battery end plates.
The group developed an integrated intelligent machining system for post-processing large die-cast components, with local data processing exceeding 90% and abnormality prediction accuracy over 95%.
Technology completed engineering validation on five-axis gantry machining centres and post-processing equipment for large die-cast components.
The group overcame technical challenges in high-precision forming for hydraulic valves, enhancing in-house capabilities for core component supply.
Leveraging digital twin technology, the group developed intelligent die-casting cells capable of intelligent process parameter prediction, remote diagnostics, and digitalized management.
To respond to cyclical fluctuations, the group diversified into new productivity tracks including energy storage, AI computing power, humanoid robots, and specialised casting.
Revenue from Europe surged by 64.4% year-on-year due to robust demand for new energy equipment and high-end manufacturing.

The group secured a high-end equipment order from a well-known European vehicle enterprise, breaking market barriers for Chinese companies entering mainstream European and American vehicle supply chains.
Revenue from other emerging markets and the Asia-Pacific region increased by 76.9% year-on-year due to industrial relocation and rapid order growth.
The localized sales and service network covers Thailand, Vietnam, and Malaysia.
Revenue from the North American market declined year-on-year, but diversified global presence mitigated regional fluctuations.

CEO Liu Zhuo Ming stated the group will leverage technological strengths against cyclical headwinds, push into energy storage, photovoltaics, robotics, and computing power cooling, and build a smart service system centred on ‘AI + Equipment’.

LK TECH is one of the world’s largest die-casting machine manufacturers, engaging in design, manufacture, and sales of die-casting machines, plastic injection moulding machines, and CNC machining centres.
Products are widely used in automotive bodies and accessories, household appliances, accessories, and electronic products.
The group has 15 manufacturing bases and 60 sales and service centers worldwide.
LK TECH owns Idra, a world-class precision machinery brand that developed the world’s first Giga Press ultra-large die-casting equipment adopted by leading global new energy vehicle manufacturers.
Sales and service companies were established in the United States and India to capture overseas markets.
The group operates a casting factory in Fuxin, China, for producing cast iron/steel components to strengthen upstream and downstream synergies.
source_title: L.K. Technology Announces 2025/2026 Annual Results

entity_name: TI Fluid Systems e-Mobility Innovation Center (eMIC)
canonical_name: TI Fluid Systems e-Mobility Innovation Center
facts: TI Fluid Systems, a manufacturer of thermal management and fluid handling systems, has officially opened its new e-Mobility Innovation Center (eMIC) in Auburn Hills, Michigan.

The 4,460m2 facility is designed to foster collaboration with customers and simplify the development of thermal management systems for hybrid, plug-in hybrid and battery-electric vehicles in North America.
The Auburn Hills eMIC incorporates TI’s Core Competency model, integrating virtual engineering, designing, processing, prototyping, product testing and vehicle testing into a single location.
The company states this integration will reduce the development time for prototype thermal management components and systems from six months to just two weeks.
The facility can reportedly achieve system-weight reductions of up to 60%.
The facility can reportedly achieve overall cost savings of up to 30%.
The facility is equipped with technology for rapid prototyping, including 3D printing and injection molding.
The facility includes two vehicle lifts and 42 electric vehicle charging stations.
This setup aims to help customers navigate the complex transition to zero-emission vehicles.
Hans Dieltjens serves as president and CEO of TI Fluid Systems.

Hans Dieltjens stated that the all-new Auburn Hills eMIC represents a significant step forward in automotive thermal management development in North America, enabling faster development, collaboration, and innovation crucial for hybrid, plug-in hybrid, and battery-electric vehicles.
Since 2022, TI Fluid Systems has opened five eMICs worldwide across key regions for automotive and e-mobility development: China, Germany, Japan, South Korea, and the USA.
Dieltjens noted that constructing five eMICs in key e-mobility development regions underscores the company's commitment to supporting customer competitiveness in a fast-changing automotive landscape.
He added that an innovative approach to thermal management impacts the efficiency, performance, and cost of electric vehicles.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems opens new Michigan facility for development of thermal management systems | Automotive Powertrain Technology International

facts: Ganpati Goel is the senior global supply chain manager, power electronics, at Lucid Motors, and the author of peer-reviewed research on AI-driven EV procurement and dual-sourcing risk.

The Liberation Day tariff announcement of April 2025 added an estimated $3,600 per vehicle in import duty costs for the average US assembled vehicle manufacturer and roughly $6,000 to an imported vehicle, according to Cox Automotive.

By early 2026, US EV sales had fallen 22.6% year-to-date while total vehicle sales contracted 5.3%, according to Omdia data presented at Automotive Logistics' Finished Vehicle Logistics North America 2026 conference.

Iranian missile strikes on Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG hub drove helium spot prices above $450 per thousand cubic feet, causing a 40% spike that is now rationing semiconductor production at TSMC and Samsung.
The author has managed cross-border EV component procurement across eight countries: the US, Mexico, the Philippines, Canada, India, Germany, Japan, and South Korea.

The 2020–22 semiconductor crisis, the 2024 tariff escalations on Chinese-origin printed circuit board assemblies (PCBAs), and the Iran-Hormuz semiconductor shock have each forced real-time decisions with documented financial consequences.

The AMS/ABB Automotive Manufacturing Outlook Survey 2025, drawing on 473 industry respondents, found that supply chain disruption has ranked among the top two challenges every year since 2022 and now tops cost concerns at 45%.
Supply chain disruption ranks ahead of raw materials at 42% and labour at 39% in the AMS/ABB survey.
Full domestic sourcing of EV components is a 2035 aspiration.

Rare earth processing, advanced printed circuit board assembly, and precision powertrain machining are geographically concentrated due to decades of capital investment and process know-how that cannot be replicated overnight by executive order.
Most supplier contracts default to the seller's preferred Incoterm, typically EXW or FOB, because it is the path of least resistance at negotiation.

When duty liability was reclassified on Chinese-origin components in 2024 and 2025, procurement teams operating under DDP terms were insulated while those under FOB or CFR absorbed retroactive duty exposure that had never been priced into their cost models.

Free Carrier (FCA) has emerged as a more defensible structure for cross-border EV transactions because it transfers risk at the named point of origin, is compatible with letter-of-credit financing, and allocates export clearance to the supplier.
Teams should audit their Incoterm mix annually against the current tariff environment rather than only at contract inception.

In live procurement operations across an eight-country network, FCA renegotiation following the February 2026 Supreme Court tariff rulings delivered immediate cost savings on impacted purchase orders and removed recurring refund-recovery overhead.
Qatar supplies approximately one-third of global helium, which is used in semiconductor wafer fabrication at 3nm and 2nm nodes.
South Korea sourced 64.7% of its helium imports from Qatar in 2025 and was operating on chipmaker stockpiles estimated at around six months as of early 2026.
Taiwan faces comparable exposure to the helium supply loss.
EV power electronics rely on advanced integrated circuits and semiconductors produced at fabs now under supply pressure.

Procurement teams buying battery management unit PCBAs, variable frequency drive components, and onboard charger assemblies face extended lead time notifications from tier one suppliers due to the helium crisis.
The AMS/ABB survey confirms that 28% of respondents identify supply chain visibility as a critical gap, and 64% expect increased adoption of supply chain management software over the next year.

The traditional tariff management approach became obsolete during 2024 due to material HTSUS classification changes, Section 301 rate increases doubling to 50% on Chinese semiconductors effective January 2025, and IEEPA tariffs layered on top.
High-performing procurement teams have moved to a continuous tariff signal process involving a designated commodity owner monitoring HTSUS updates and Federal Register notices weekly.
Tariffs now represent the single largest cost pressure, cited by 45% of AMS/ABB survey respondents ahead of raw materials and labour.

The Red Sea diversion of 2024, the Strait of Hormuz disruption of 2026, and port congestion events across Los Angeles-Long Beach exposed structural vulnerabilities in single-lane logistics strategies.

The correct architecture for critical EV components like BMU PCBAs, power conversion modules, and contactor assemblies is a standing secondary routing with a named alternate forwarder, alternate port pair, and trigger protocol typically activated at a 72-hour delay notification.

Research supports a transit buffer inventory calibration of three to six weeks of consumption for single-source critical components, with an estimated 50–60% reduction in supply disruption risk under adverse routing conditions.
The AMS/ABB survey found that 27% of respondents are actively shifting from single to dual sourcing.
Production downtime at a luxury EV OEM can exceed $500,000 a day.

Standard contract templates create enforcement gaps across civil and common law systems in Asia, North America, and Europe, particularly regarding force majeure definitions, IP ownership for custom tooling/dies, and termination-for-convenience notice periods.
Jurisdiction-specific review is particularly consequential in South Korea, India, and China where local statute may conflict with boilerplate buyer-owns-tooling language.
The AMS/ABB survey's medium-term optimism shows 60% of respondents expect production growth in the three-year outlook.
source_title: Five decisions that can create resilient EV supply chains

facts: ABC Technologies announced a recommended all-cash offer to acquire the entire issued and to be issued ordinary share capital of TI Fluid Systems plc through ABC Technologies Acquisitions Limited.
TI Fluid Systems is a London-listed, market-leading global manufacturer of thermal and fluid system solutions for the full range of current and developing vehicle architectures.
TI Fluid Systems operates across 27 countries and serves all major automotive manufacturers.
The combined group would create an industry leader with an extensive global product portfolio and financial strength to support long-term growth objectives.
The acquisition will provide access to a broader and more diversified range of customers, including some of the largest and most recognizable automotive OEMs and Tier One suppliers worldwide.

Terry Campbell, President and CEO of ABC Technologies, stated the transaction is a transformative strategic opportunity which unlocks value for all stakeholders and provides a platform for further growth.

The combined business will enable better customer service and continued building of a winning future, with persistence in seeking alignment with organizations that have proven capabilities to further ABC’s success story.

Combining the rich heritages of ABC Technologies and TI Fluid Systems will create a business that benefits from an enhanced go-to-market proposition and greater financial strength to support long-term growth objectives.
ABC Technologies is majority-owned by funds managed by affiliates of Apollo Global Management, Inc.
Shareholders of TI Fluid Systems will be entitled to receive 200 pence a share, valuing TI Fluid Systems at an enterprise value of approximately £1,831 million.
The acquisition is currently expected to complete in H1 2025, subject to shareholder and other relevant legal and regulatory approvals.
Lazard acted as lead financial advisor to ABC Technologies for the transaction.
Citi, TD Securities, and Scotiabank also acted as financial advisers for the acquisition.
Kirkland & Ellis International acted as legal advisor to ABC Technologies.
Paul, Weiss, Rifkind, Wharton & Garrison acted as legal advisor in respect of regulatory and financing matters.
source_title: ABC Technologies Announces Recommended Offer To Acquire TI Fluid Systems - ABC Technologies

facts: ESi was officially announced a 2023 GLOBE Award recipient by the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
The award program is managed by the GDEcD International Trade team and highlights Georgia companies that contribute to the state’s economic growth through global trade and export development.
This is ESi’s first time receiving the GLOBE Award.
The recognition honors international outreach efforts driven by ESi’s Georgia office.

ESi President and Chief Operating Officer Will Pinkston stated that many employees from across the company have been instrumental in introducing services into new international markets, creating a wealth of opportunities for ESi, its partners, and its clients.
Jay Sohn served as Consultant and lead for Asia Pacific engagement.
Matt Kenner served as Principal and Sr. Director of Mechanics for work in the Republic of Korea.
Hernán Mercado-Corujo served as Managing Consultant and Director of Automotive Technology.
Diana Estrada served as Sr. Consultant for efforts in Mexico and Central America.
Bob Winn served as Principal Emeritus.
Alfred Pettinger served as Principal and Vice President.
Bill Broz served as Principal.
Chuck Fox served as Sr. Director of Technical Services.
Tim Jung served as Sr. Managing Consultant and Director of Aviation.
Kara Greene served as Sr. Staff Consultant.
Heather Slatton served as Business Development Director.
Kate Champion served as Claims Relationship Manager.
Tony Dill (COL Ret) leads ESi’s Security and Defense Services Group, expanding services to allies and defense companies abroad.
ESi provides on-demand engineering consulting and technical support services spanning nearly every engineering specialty.

ESi serves clients across diverse industries including construction, legal, industrial and manufacturing, insurance, oil and gas, pharmaceutical and medical devices, power and energy, security and defense, transportation, and more.
ESi utilizes a go-to-market approach that taps into industry, cross-industry, and technical expertise from across its 20 offices to tailor teams to client needs.
In 2022, ESi began exporting services from its Georgia office to new markets in the Republic of Korea and expanded business development efforts in Mexico and the United Kingdom.
The GLOBE Awards program began in 2014 to recognize local businesses contributing to Georgia’s economic growth through global trade.
Since 2014, GDEcD has presented 323 GLOBE Awards to companies across 46 Georgia counties.

links.context: Official list of this year's winners
Announcement from the Georgia Department of Economic Development

source_title: ESi Named 2023 GLOBE Award Recipient | Engineering Systems Inc.

entity_name: Ti Cold & Karis Cold Cold Storage Facility
facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Ti Cold and Karis Cold officially broke ground on a new $60 million cold storage facility for PermaCold Logistics in Darien, McIntosh County.
The industrial cold storage construction project will support 50 jobs upon completion of phase one.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the announcement is the largest known economic development win for McIntosh County and marks another success for rural Georgia.
Ti Cold provides a full suite of cold storage construction and development services, including complete design and engineering, master site planning, operations guidance, and consulting services.
Sam Tippmann serves as the Founder and CEO of Ti Cold.
The new facility is located at Tidewater Industrial Park, a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) site, in Darien.
The advanced cold storage complex will have a clear height of 50 feet and feature a flexible, convertible design that maintains temperatures ranging from 40 degrees to minus 10 degrees Fahrenheit.
The facility will incorporate an environmentally friendly refrigeration system.
Structural steel pallet racks will accommodate over 30,000 pallet positions.
The facility will post more information regarding open positions throughout the construction process.
Kate Pontello Karwacki is the Chairwoman of the McIntosh County Board of Commissioners.
The project highlights the site's strategic location along the I-95 corridor and proximity to ports, alongside a welcoming business climate.
Dorie Bacon, Senior Regional Project Manager, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project.
GDEcD partnered with the McIntosh County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Power for this development.
Pat Wilson is the Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
Ti Cold is a specialty, award-winning industrial cold storage builder and developer with decades of experience in designing and building state-of-the-art facilities.

Karis Cold is a private investment and development company with expertise in the cold storage industry, investing in and developing properties across the U.S. with build-to-suit solutions, sale-leaseback financing, and expansions/redevelopment.
Jessica Atwell serves as the Communication Manager for the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Ti Cold and Karis Cold Building $60 Million Facility in McIntosh County

facts: TI Fluid Systems plc is a global industry leader in highly engineered automotive fluid storage, carrying and delivery systems and thermal management products and systems.
The Group announced its financial results for the year ended 31 December 2024 on 11 March 2025.
Adjusted Revenue for 2024 was €3,360.3 million, a decrease of 4.4% (or 3.5% in constant currency) from €3,516.2 million in 2023.
Adjusted EBIT for 2024 was €260.8 million, an increase of 0.5% (or 1.9% in constant currency) from €259.6 million in 2023.
Adjusted EBIT Margin % for 2024 was 7.8%, an increase of 40 basis points from 7.4% in 2023.
Adjusted Net Income for 2024 was €136.7 million, compared to €132.8 million in 2023.
source_title: TI Fluid Systems: Full Year Results 2024

facts: Tie Down Engineering is located at 605 Wheaton Drive, Atlanta, GA 30336, United States.
The company is undertaking a "new" 160k facility expansion.
The expansion features the only 2MM pound plus automated sheet/plate steel handling system in Georgia.
The facility includes the latest high powered fiber laser metal processing systems from both Japan and Europe.
Tie Down Engineering implemented the very first welding robot in Georgia back in 1981, which is not operable but held in storage for sentimental purposes.
The new facility is about 50% complete and will be 100% full by Q2 2020.
The company's whole campus in Fulton industrial park approaches 700k sqft.
Tie Down Engineering holds over 90 patents and 7,000 products.
Chuck Mackarvich serves as Chairman and Founder and continues to lead one of America’s leading contract and custom manufacturers.
source_title: Tie Down Engineering -  Georgia Manufacturing Alliance

facts: Precision metal fabricator and manufacturer Unified Legacy has announced it will invest $125 million in a new manufacturing facility in Macon, Ga.
The expansion is creating 500 new jobs over the next several years.
The company manufactures complex products and components for the defense, aerospace, data center, and industrial markets.

With advanced machining, welding, and fabrication capabilities, the company delivers custom ground support equipment, welded assemblies, generator enclosures, fuel storage tanks, and precision-machined components.
The company’s existing manufacturing facility, Unified Defense, has been operating in Byron, Ga., since 2022.
Construction of the new Macon facility is expected to begin this year.
source_title: Unified Legacy to create 500 jobs with Georgia expansion

facts: Rivian is increasing planned production capacity at its Georgia facility by 50%.
The expansion aims to lower unit costs, strengthen U.S. manufacturing capabilities, and support large-scale EV and autonomous vehicle production.
The new manufacturing facility is located in Stanton Springs North.
Planned output has been raised to 300,000 vehicles annually in the first phase.
The 50% increase over the original 200,000-unit plan aims to improve cost efficiency through higher production volumes.
The expansion creates additional headroom for future growth as demand increases.
Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated: “We’re very excited to partner with the U.S. Department of Energy to grow our manufacturing footprint in Georgia.”

Scaringe added: “R2 dramatically expands our market opportunity. The thousands of dedicated people who will soon work in our Georgia plant will be instrumental to Rivian’s growth as we scale American manufacturing and work to ensure that the U.S. retains its leadership in innovation and technology.”
Scaling production early in facility design is highlighted as a core industry trend to drive down cost per unit.
Development of the stamping press area, described as one of the most technically demanding parts of the facility, is already underway.
Rivian is working with the U.S. Department of Energy to secure a loan of up to $4.5 billion for the project.
The DOE loan is aligned with the plant's updated design and production roadmap.
The facility is expected to support thousands of manufacturing and construction jobs.
The Georgia plant will also play a role in next-generation mobility beyond traditional EV manufacturing.
Rivian plans to produce up to 50,000 robotaxis at the site starting in 2028 through a partnership with Uber.
source_title: Rivian Expands Georgia Plant Capacity to 300,000 Units to Drive Manufacturing Efficiency and Scale - North America Outlook

facts: Tie Down Engineering is a manufacturer and supplier of products for the agriculture, safety, marine, manufactured housing, and roofing industries.
The company has been located in the Fulton Industrial Boulevard area for more than four decades.
Tie Down Engineering was founded in Miami in 1968 by Chuck MacKarvich.
The company initially produced tie-down anchors for manufactured homes.
Founder Chuck MacKarvich remains chairman today.
The company relocated from South Florida to Atlanta in 1981 due to challenges of operating a rapidly expanding manufacturing business from Miami.
Chuck MacKarvich selected Atlanta because drawing a circle around the metropolitan area provides access to approximately 70% of the U.S. population within one day’s truck drive.
Atlanta serves as a crossroads between the East, West, North, and South.
Quick access to four major interstates intersecting Atlanta influenced the choice of the Fulton Industrial area.
In the early 1980s, Tie Down Engineering built a 40,000 square-foot facility on Stonehill Drive in the Fulton Industrial area.
The company purchased adjacent property to allow for future growth.
Tie Down Engineering currently operates 12 buildings, eight of which are contiguous.
The company employs over 300 people.
Tie Down Engineering manufactures more than 8,000 products across six different divisions.
The company is known for innovative product design, custom manufacturing, and streamlining the assembly process.
Operations run over approximately 800,000 square feet of ISO-9001 certified facilities in Atlanta.
All Tie Down Engineering products are manufactured in America at its Atlanta headquarters.
Chuck MacKarvich was a proponent of creating the Fulton Industrial Community Improvement District (CID).
He served as a board member and treasurer for the CID for nearly five years.
Many of the CID's early meetings were held at the Tie Down Engineering office.
Chuck MacKarvich stated that the CID cleaned up the area, improved public safety, and increased property values compared to the previous lack of government support.
The company's two sons, son-in-law, and nephew all work in the business.
Family members agree the Atlanta location is perfect for Tie Down Engineering.
source_title: Business Spotlight | Tie Down Engineering | Fulton Industrial CID

facts: Executives with United States Steel Corporation expect to maintain capital expenditures of $2.5 billion for 2023 but project a lower spend for 2024.
Chief Financial Officer Jessica Graziano discussed the company's spending plans on the second-quarter earnings conference call held on July 27, 2023.
The company forecasts about $800 million of additional strategic capex in 2024.
Early read on sustaining capex is probably in the neighborhood of $600 million to $700 million.
Total projected capex for 2024 is somewhere in the neighborhood of $1.4 billion to $1.5 billion.
Capex from existing strategic projects will be substantially done in 2024.
The company aims to maintain a strong balance sheet while it invests and executes its strategy, with potential for a new sizable repurchase authorization and a fresh review of its dividend policy.
Industrial Info Resources is tracking 61 active projects from U.S. Steel worth nearly $7 billion.
The $3 billion Big River 2 minimill in Osceola, Arkansas, remains on track for a startup later in 2024.
CEO David Burritt noted that 87% of the spend for the Big River 2 minimill has already been committed and 59% of the project is complete.
Once complete, the Big River 2 minimill combined with existing Big River Steel will form a 6 million-ton mega mill supplying advanced and sustainable steels in North America.
A non-grain-oriented (NGO) electrical steel line at Big River Steel operations is on track to launch in the third quarter of 2023.
U.S. Steel has received its first customer orders for NGO steels in both industrial and electric vehicle markets.
The NGO steels are strategically located to support manufacturing concentration in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
Thinner NGO steels will allow electric vehicle motors to go further between charges.
U.S. Steel announced a supply agreement with General Motors Company to supply the EV automaker with a sustainable-steel product from Big River.
Construction is underway at the Big River Steel site for a 325,000-ton-per-year galvanizing line.
Completion of the new galvanizing line is planned for the second quarter of 2024.
source_title: U.S. Steel Expects Dip in 2024 Capex

facts: Baltimore is the largest auto port in the U.S. and a vital part of the global automotive supply chain.
The port handles more than 800,000 vehicles a year.
A significant portion of vehicles are imported from Europe and Mexico, with many exported by Detroit automakers.
BMW and Volkswagen distribution centers are located on the side of the harbor beyond the bridge, making them less affected by the collapse.
President Biden touted the Port of Baltimore as a model port in November 2021 during a deepening supply chain crisis.

The ongoing transition of global supply chains involves more nearshoring and friend-shoring with neighbors like Mexico and friends like European countries and Japan, positioning the Port of Baltimore perfectly for this shift.
The bridge collapse created a major disruption to the logistics networks of companies like Amazon, which relies on Baltimore as a major East Coast logistics hub.
Rerouting decisions for cargo ships are already underway and will continue for months.
Alternative ports like Brunswick, Georgia, and Norfolk, Virginia, won't be able to fully absorb the volume going through Baltimore.
Consumers may experience delivery delays depending on how quickly vehicle inventories are depleted, and perhaps price increases in certain cases.
Supply chain resilience built since the early 2020s means disruptions should be less significant, with supply chains showing robustness in 2024 not as pronounced in previous years.
The ecosystem surrounding the port includes a trucking industry and intermodal transportation networks that will experience ripple effects from the collapse.
source_title: Supply chains could feel ripple effects from Baltimore's Key Bridge collapse | Hub

facts: China’s energy and auto giant BYD announced an ultra fast EV charging system that it says is nearly as quick as a fill up at the pumps.
BYD, China’s largest EV maker, said its flash-chargers can provide a full charge for its latest EVs within five to eight minutes.
BYD plans to build more than 4,000 of the new charging stations across China.
Charging times and limited ranges have been a major factor constraining the switch from gas and diesel vehicles to EVs.
Chinese drivers have embraced that change, with sales of battery powered and hybrid vehicles jumping 40% last year.
BYD’s news appeared to give Tesla a jolt on Monday, as the U.S. EV maker’s share price sank 4.8%.
BYD began pre-sales of its Han L and Tang L models, which are upgraded versions of earlier models.
The Chinese company started out making batteries and has been refining its battery and energy storage technology while building an auto empire that is expanding outside China.
BYD says its 1 megawatt flash chargers can provide power for 400 kilometers (nearly 250 miles) in five minutes.
Ultra-high voltage and a large current are required to maximize charging speeds, according to BYD’s founder Wang Chuanfu.
Wang Chuanfu stated: “To completely solve users’ anxiety over charging, our pursuit is to make the charging time for EVs as short as the refueling time for fuel vehicles.”
BYD’s flash-charging system relies on silicon carbide power chips with voltage levels of up to 1,500V that it developed on its own.
Its Blade lithium-iron phosphate battery is perhaps the world’s safest and most efficient EV battery, according to industry analyst Michael Dunne.
Tesla opted to use BYD's Blade battery in some of its EVs.
BYD reported it made just over 4.3 million “new energy vehicles” last year, up 41% from a year earlier.
Last year's production included 1.8 million battery electric vehicles and 2.5 million plug in hybrids.
The price of BYD's shares traded on China’s smaller market in Shenzhen has surged nearly 50% in the past six months.
While BYD’s fanciest, latest premium models are expected to sell for up to about $40,000, it also makes much less expensive EVs including the Seagull, which sells for around $12,000 in China.
BYD barely nudged ahead of Tesla in production of battery-powered EVs in 2024, making 1,777,965 compared with Tesla’s 1,773,443.
In early January, Tesla said its sales dropped in 2024, a first in more than a dozen years, as rivals such as BMW, Volkswagen and BYD gained market shares with competitive EVs.

Industry analyst Michael Dunne noted BYD's weaknesses, pointing out that JD Power’s 2024 China New Energy Vehicle Initial Quality Study ranked the BYD Seal and BYD Song Plus battery electrics at the bottom of its rankings.
source_title: China's BYD launches EV charging system it says works nearly as fast as a fill up | AP News

facts: Ford CEO Jim Farley was recently seen discussing the company's plans for its newest EV plant outside Memphis, Tennessee.
The new Tennessee EV plant is projected to create 6,000 new jobs once operational.
The new Tennessee EV plant is projected to generate $5.6 billion.
Ford announced potential losses due to significant investments in boosting EV production.
By the end of 2023, Ford aims to produce 600,000 electric vehicles annually.
Ford aims to produce two million electric vehicles annually by 2026.
Jim Farley advocates for a robust, U.S.-based supply chain from mining to battery production to reduce dependency on overseas sources.

Jim Farley stated: "We have to have mines and processing to build a digital economy here in the U.S. We cannot continue to import batteries and rare earth from overseas. We have to move it to America."
Ford's electric vehicle division is currently in a "startup" phase, requiring heavy investment in finances and resources to build foundations for a sustainable electric future.
Ford's all-new electric truck will be equipped with updatable software akin to smartphones.
source_title: Jim Farley's Vision: A Thriving U.S.-Based EV Supply Chain, Not an EV Ban

facts: Commissioner Pat Wilson was named the 2023 “Georgian of the Year” by Georgia Trend.
The award recognizes Wilson as the top salesperson for the state and commends his work promoting Georgia to attract investments and create jobs.
Wilson credits the state’s success to a team approach in conducting business and emphasizes keeping relationships strong with partners and businesses across the state and globe.
Wilson was appointed economic development Commissioner by former Governor Nathan Deal six years prior to the article's publication.
Georgia maintained its place as the No. 1 state in the nation to do business for the ninth consecutive year in 2022.
Former Georgia Governor Sonny Perdue credited Wilson with helping make Georgia one of the best places to grow a business by communicating the actions taken by the governor and legislature.
In August 2022, GDEcD announced that investments in expansions and new locations for fiscal year 2022 totaled more than $21.2 billion.
The FY22 investments were projected to create 51,132 direct new jobs in the state, setting new records for Georgia.
GDEcD’s work providing solutions for companies and maintaining open communication helped Georgia secure two of its largest-ever investments in FY22.
Wilson expressed emotional gratitude for his position and the impact jobs have had on people's lives.
GDEcD teammates Lee Thomas, Director of the Georgia Film Office, and David Nuckolls, Executive Director of the Center of Innovation, were also recognized in Georgia Trend’s lists.

facts: Toyota-Denso Automotive Compressor Georgia (TACG) and Toyota Industries Compressor Parts America (TICA) manufacture and supply vehicle air conditioning compressors for more than half of the North American auto industry.
Toyota Industries Group is situated on a 150-acre campus off Valentine Industrial Parkway in Pendergrass, Ga., and distributes its energy-efficient products throughout North America.
The company's compressors have become the industry standard and are used in over half of all cars, trucks, and vans manufactured in North America.
Well-known automotive brands such as Ford, Honda, BMW, Lexus, Subaru, Fiat-Chrysler, and Toyota Motor vehicles rely on TACG and TICA compressors for their superior performance and reliability.
The company assembles inverters that supply electronics to power the plug-in devices used in vehicles.
TACG was established in 2004, specializing in the manufacturing of automobile air conditioning compressors.
To meet increasing demand, TICA was built adjacent to TACG in 2012, expanding production capacity by manufacturing the component parts used in TACG’s AC compressors.
In November 2020, TICA expanded its production capabilities to include DC-AC inverters.
These DC-AC inverters enable the use of electronic devices such as cellphones, laptops, job-site tools, and small appliances in vehicles.
Toyota Industries Electronics North America (TIESNA) was founded in Michigan in 2013 as a sales company for Toyota Industries’ electronics products.
TIESNA is expanding its footprint into northeast Georgia while maintaining its position as a leader in the automotive industry.
Breaking ground occurred in Pendergrass, Georgia, representing a significant milestone for the expansion.
Production at the new TIESNA facility will commence starting July 2025.
The new TIESNA facility will produce the DC-DC converter and other electronics products.
Kotaro Nishimoto serves as President of TACG & TICA in Georgia.
Sadanori Suzuki serves as President of TIESNA for Georgia & Michigan.
Associates at the facilities receive highly competitive wages, outstanding benefits, and opportunities for growth in a strong and stable industry.
The company is committed to environmental responsibility through research and development of eco-friendly automotive solutions for the automotive manufacturing industry.

facts: Trenton Pressing is located in Trenton, Ga., and operates as a longtime Tier 1 and Tier 2 supplier of stamped parts and complex assemblies for automotive companies and golf cart manufacturers.
During the pandemic, new owners purchased the business to diversify its customer base by adding metal fabricating capabilities to its existing stamping, robotic welding, and assembly services.
The company's ultimate goal is to build full turnkey fabrications for customers rather than just supplying individual parts.
Brad Coffman works at Trenton Pressing and brings 30 years of experience in the metal fabricating industry, focusing on laser cutting and press brake forming.
The company built a new 100,000-sq.-ft. building on its campus specifically for warehousing and assembly activities.
The new building freed up space in Plant 1 to accommodate a laser cutting machine and a press brake.
Plant 2 houses the company’s 15 robotic welding cells, as well as manual welding and assembly operations.
In late 2023, Trenton Pressing purchased a BesCutter 20-kW fiber laser with a 5- by 10-ft. bed and automatic loading/unloading capability from Automated Solutions.
The new laser cutting machine became operational in early 2024 and is designed to process up to 2-in. carbon steel plate.
The laser can cut using compressed high-pressure air for 3/8- to ½-in. material, which the company finds more economical than using oxygen or nitrogen.
An automated load and unload gantry system was installed alongside the laser, using vacuum suction cups to load raw sheet metal and fork-style lifters to remove cut parts.
The automated system features two tables—one for sheet metal and one for finished parts—and can run unattended for eight hours without reloading material.
To prevent machine downtime, the company purchased two cutting heads that can be switched out when needed, while operators can change the protective lens in seconds.

In early 2025, Trenton Pressing installed a Haco EuroMaster 275-ton CNC press brake equipped with 6-axis backgauging, CNC crowning, automatic angle measurement during bending, hydraulic upper tool clamping, and offline programming capability.
The press brake features a 10-ft. bed and a 21.25-in. daylight opening to accommodate parts coming off the laser cutting machine.
Brad Coffman noted that finding press brake operators is difficult and that it takes longer to train a press brake operator than a laser operator due to a steeper learning curve.
The company plans to train one of its engineers to run the press brake, creating two forming experts who can help train other staff members.
Fabricated parts are moved via kanban trucks that travel back and forth among the buildings, with the box truck making at least three stops per day at each facility.
After leaving fabricating, materials go to the new building where they are distributed as needed to welding, coaters, or directly to customers.
Management used the opportunity of moving assembly to the new building to refinish Plant 1 floors using large gas-powered floor grinders and polyurethane paint.
Approximately 60% of all factory floors now feature these refinished concrete surfaces, which management considers a selling tool due to the owners' high standards.
Randall Hamilton serves as Trenton Pressing’s plant manager and operates the Haco EuroMaster 275-ton press brake.
source_title: Metal forming company jumps into the thick of metal fabricating | Nanning Metal Stamping Group Co.,Ltd

facts: Georgia’s fiscal year 2026, which began in July, has been one of record-breaking achievement.

Domestic and international corporations are launching new operations throughout the state’s 159 counties while existing companies expand to support the manufacturing and artificial intelligence sectors.

Misti Martin, deputy commissioner of global commerce for the Georgia Department of Economic Development, states that Georgia’s success comes from deep partnerships and a focus on diversified economic development across agriculture, manufacturing, logistics, technology, and tourism.
Gov. Brian Kemp announced that the state surpassed $60.2 billion in exports in calendar year 2025.
The export increase was 12.7% year-over-year, compared to a national average of 5.6%.
GDEcD’s figures from January 1 to November 25, 2025 cover the state's economic metrics for that period.
There were more than $30.3 billion in investments during that period.
There were an estimated 420 new locations or expansions across the state.
These investments are expected to create 26,170 new jobs over the next few years.
The investment figures span all industries, including automotive and agribusiness.
Much of the economic growth lies in less populated areas throughout the state rather than just Metro Atlanta.
In the last fiscal year (July 1, 2024-June 30, 2025), 77% of expansions and new locations were outside the 10-county Metro Atlanta region.
Expansions at existing Georgia companies accounted for around three-quarters of the projects during that fiscal year.
These existing company expansions built on an estimated 50,000 jobs already held by Georgians.
Georgia is ranked the No. 1 state for doing business for a record 12 consecutive years.

facts: The Georgia Manufacturing Extension Partnership is a program of the Enterprise Innovation Institute at Georgia Tech.
Gov. Brian P. Kemp recognized GaMEP for 65 years of service to the manufacturing industry at the Georgia State Capitol on Monday, July 14th, 2025.
The commendation acknowledges GaMEP's commitment to supporting manufacturers across the state through educational opportunities and technical assistance.
GaMEP leverages world-renowned expertise and resources to advance manufacturing and economic prosperity across Georgia.
According to the National Associations of Manufacturers, the manufacturing industry adds $82 billion to the Georgia economy and employs 425,000 residents.
By 1960, more than 4,500 manufacturers had established operations in Georgia due to strategic efforts by state leaders and economic development assistance from Georgia Tech.

The Georgia General Assembly voted to establish the Georgia Tech Industrial Extension Service (now known as GaMEP) to provide technical support to manufacturers facing new challenges from industry growth.
GaMEP created statewide field offices that deliver on-site technical expertise to help manufacturers thrive and maintain a direct link between industry and innovation.
Tim Israel, GaMEP director and EI2 associate vice president — corporate engagement/firm-based programs, stated that the program's role is to support manufacturers to grow the state's economy.
In 2024, GaMEP generated a return on investment of $294 for every state dollar allocated to manufacturing projects.
Over the past decade, GaMEP provided assistance and education to more than 3,900 manufacturers across 144 counties.
During that same decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers create or retain 14,500 jobs.
Over the past decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers invest $1 billion in capital improvements.
Over the past decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers realize $3.5 billion in sales.
Over the past decade, GaMEP helped manufacturers save nearly $450 million in costs.
GaMEP primarily serves small- to medium-sized manufacturers, with 75% of its clients employing less than 250 workers.
GaMEP's top-served industries include fabricated metal product, food, machinery, chemical, and transportation equipment manufacturing.

David Bridges, EI2 vice president, noted that when Georgia manufacturers become more productive and profitable, they hire more people, pay better wages, and stabilize local economies, especially in rural and underserved areas.
David Bridges also stated that GaMEP's work creates pathways for career advancement for frontline workers who might not have had previous opportunities.
GaMEP's reach spans 10 regions across the state, each led by a dedicated region manager who lives and works locally to connect manufacturers with expert teams and resources.
Lloyd Avram, Georgia Association of Manufacturers CEO and president, called GaMEP a trusted collaborator and valued partner in strengthening manufacturing across Georgia.
Lloyd Avram stated that together they have supported thousands of manufacturers statewide to embrace innovation, improve operations, and remain competitive.

Coastal Region: GaMEP served 269 manufacturers across Bryan, Bulloch, Camden, Chatham, Effingham, Glynn, Liberty, Long, McIntosh, and Screven counties, creating/retaining 283 jobs, $23,171,292 in capital improvements, saving $1,645,061 in costs, and realizing $13,965,000 in sales, with Roger Wood Foods as a success story.

Northeast Region: GaMEP served 280 manufacturers across Banks, Dawson, Forsyth, Franklin, Habersham, Hall, Hart, Lumpkin, Rabun, Stephens, Towns, Union, and White counties, creating/retaining 1,029 jobs, $88,443,395 in capital improvements, saving $63,999,228 in costs, and realizing $259,453,900 in sales, with Freudenberg Sealing Technologies as a success story.

Northwest Region: GaMEP served 387 manufacturers across Bartow, Catoosa, Chattooga, Dade, Fannin, Floyd, Gilmer, Gordon, Haralson, Murray, Paulding, Pickens, Polk, Walker, and Whitfield counties, creating/retaining 1,090 jobs, $92,948,931 in capital improvements, saving $141,460,651 in costs, and realizing $326,366,408 in sales, with Trenton Pressing as a success story.

South Region: GaMEP served 176 manufacturers across Appling, Bleckley, Candler, Dodge, Emanuel, Evans, Jeff Davis, Johnson, Laurens, Montgomery, Tattnall, Telfair, Toombs, Treutlen, Wayne, Wheeler, and Wilcox counties, creating/retaining 969 jobs, $219,300,221 in capital improvements, saving $6,596,254 in costs, and realizing $39,632,275 in sales, with Healthy Pet as a success story.

Southeast Region: GaMEP served 166 manufacturers across Atkinson, Bacon, Ben Hill, Berrien, Brantley, Brooks, Charlton, Clinch, Coffee, Cook, Echols, Irwin, Lanier, Lowndes, Pierce, Tift, Turner, and Ware counties, creating/retaining 281 jobs, $20,684,800 in capital improvements, saving $3,098,700 in costs, and realizing $77,724,500 in sales, with CJB Industries and South Georgia Pecan as success stories.

facts: Georgia Power filed its 2025 Integrated Resource Plan (IRP) on January 31 with the Georgia Public Service Commission (PSC).
The IRP proposes power uprates at four units at the Hatch and Vogtle nuclear power plants to meet long-term energy needs.
The plan details necessary investments in the generation fleet and transmission system to support Georgia's expected extraordinary growth.
Georgia Power projects approximately 8200 MW of electrical load growth over the next six years.
Peak demand is projected to increase by more than 2200 MWe by the end of 2030 compared to the 2023 IRP Update approved in April 2024.
The company identified opportunities to upgrade existing nuclear units to provide additional baseload capacity without requiring incremental transmission system investment.
Georgia Power is proposing extended power uprates (EPU) at Vogtle units 1 and 2, and Hatch units 1 and 2.
Between 2028 and 2034, the company plans to add a total of 112 MWe of capacity across the four units: 27 MWe each at Vogtle 1 and 2; 30 MWe at Hatch 1 and 28 MWe at Hatch.
Extended power uprates involve significant modifications to major plant equipment to increase reactor thermal output.

For the Hatch units, Georgia Power plans a boiler water reactor (BWR) upgrade called the 'Maximum Extended Load Line Limit (MELLA+)' enhancement to allow higher thermal power without increasing core flow.
The company is considering transitioning Vogtle units 1 and 2 outage windows to a 24-month cycle to extend unit runtimes and decrease refueling outages.
Georgia Power is working with the US Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) for the required review and licensing process, which includes reviewing license amendment requests for each facility.
The company believes additional nuclear power will be needed long-term to reliably and economically serve customer energy needs.
Nuclear power provides a pathway to reduce carbon emissions and mitigate cost pressures from potential future environmental regulations on fossil-fired fleets.

Expansion planning uses multiple views of future technology costs, electricity consumption, and fuel prices, with B2025 scenarios selecting nuclear generation in six of nine scenarios over a 20-year horizon as early as 2037.
The company acknowledges that new nuclear construction carries substantial risks and believes risk mitigation solutions must be developed before proposing additional new nuclear generation.
Georgia Power is prioritizing viable new nuclear options by performing site assessments, evaluating emerging technologies, and engaging stakeholders to improve deployment methods.
Kim Greene, chairman, president and CEO of Georgia Power, stated that the state is well-positioned for the future thanks to proactive planning and policies like the IRP.
Kim Greene added that the 2025 IRP provides a comprehensive plan to support Georgia's continued economic growth and serve Georgians with clean, safe, reliable, and affordable energy into the future.
source_title: Georgia Power plans additional nuclear capacity - World Nuclear News

facts: The 2030 CO2 reduction targets are unreachable without hybridization.

The Valeo 48 Volts powertrain system architecture is a hybridization solution suited to all types of vehicle, allowing significant gains compared to a 12 Volts hybridization system and a full electrification solution for light vehicles.
Reaching the voltage level to 48 Volts maximises the hybrid system benefits in terms of CO2 reduction and fuel economy.

This powertrain system, which can be used in either 100% electric is on average 20% more affordable than high-voltage motors (greater than 60 Volts), thanks to a simplified architecture that reduces safety equipment requirements.
Valeo offers two levels of 48 Volts hybridization: Level 1 provides 5% fuel savings with increased comfort and power compared to a 12 Volts system.
Level 1 includes regenerative braking, which stores energy in the 48 Volts lithium-ion battery.
Level 1 includes torque assist, where the 48 Volts starter generator uses recovered energy to assist the internal combustion engine when accelerating.
Level 1 includes electrical supply, where energy stored in the 48-volt lithium-ion battery is fed through the 48 Volt converter to power vehicle accessories like the car radio and windscreen wipers.
Level 2 provides hybrid with 100% electric propulsion, offering around 15% fuel savings with greater comfort and power.
Level 2 builds on Level 1 by adding a 48 Volts electric propulsion engine.
Combined with a high-capacity battery in Level 2, the vehicle can be driven in 100% electric mode.
Level 2 delivers similar performance to a High Voltage hybrid system.
Valeo’s 48 Volts starter-alternator ranges from 4 to 6 kW and has already been sold in more than 1 million units by Valeo.
The 48 Volts starter-alternator was developed using know-how acquired with the 12 Volts starter-alternator.
The 48 Volts 15 kW electric motor is oil-cooled and can be fitted to a hybrid dual-clutch gearbox, with series production scheduled for 2021.
The 48 Volts 25kW water-cooled electric motor can be positioned on the rear axle of the vehicle to provide an electric 4WD function and enhance the driving experience.
Valeo is producing the 48 Volts power converter (DC/DC converter) that transfers energy stored in the battery to the vehicle’s 12 Volts on-board power supply.
Series production of the air-cooled version of the 48 Volts power converter began in 2017, meeting cost-performance requirements.
A new generation of the 48 Volts power converter launched in 2021 is capable of meeting all energy needs thanks to its adaptable air or water cooling.

Valeo solutions include an additional 48 Volts lithium-ion battery, a 48 Volts converter, a new 48 Volts starter generator, and a range of electric motors incorporating regenerative braking, torque assist, and electrical supply functions.

Valeo offers the very first 48 Volts low-voltage rechargeable vehicle featuring a Four-Wheel drive electric architecture with a 48 Volts starter-alternator and a 48 Volts electric motor on the rear axle.
This four-wheel drive electric architecture significantly improves handling in hard conditions such as snowy environments.
By combining 48 Volts hybridization with a larger 48 Volts battery, this plug-in hybrid solution offers an electric range of up to 80 km.
The plug-in hybrid solution guarantees a drastic overall reduction in CO2 emissions.
This solution enables all-electric travel for around forty kilometers, with the thermal engine taking over for longer journeys.
Using geolocation, the 100% electric mode is automatically activated when the vehicle enters a low emission zone.
When a driver enters a route into the GPS, the vehicle can adapt the use of the electric drive system accordingly.
Compared to conventional high-voltage plug-in hybrid vehicles, the Valeo 48 Volts plug-in hybrid is more economical, as the average cost of the 48 Volts technology is around 20% lower.
Valeo proposes to reuse the 48 Volts technology initially developed for hybrid vehicles for new mobility applications such as light 2, 3, or 4 wheels.
Valeo is supplying the engine for the Citroën AMI.
The 100% electric prototype operating at low voltage in 48 Volts is the very first 48 Volts democar powered entirely by Valeo, excluding batteries.
This two-seater electric vehicle can reach a maximum speed of 100km/h and has a range of 150km.
Its small size and weight make it suitable for short journeys and city trips, and it can be recharged at any electric charging point.

Valeo’s 48 Volts 100% electric vehicle is more economical (20% less expensive) than a current all-electric solution because its low-voltage system does not require specific protection, whereas high-voltage electric vehicles are subject to restrictive regulations and accreditations.
The cost of a small 48 Volts 100% electric vehicle could be around €7,500 after deduction of purchase subsidies.

This type of vehicle equipped with Valeo’s solution gave manufacturers the impulse for the new era of low-voltage urban mobility, which arrived on the streets of French cities in 2020 with the very first full electric 48 Volts Citroën AMI.
source_title: 48 VOLTS AFFORDABLE ELECTRIFICATION | Valeo Service

facts: Renault Group, Valeo and Valeo Siemens eAutomotive signed a memorandum of understanding to form a strategic partnership for the design, co-development and manufacture in France of a new-generation automotive electric motor.
The partnership aims to eliminate the use of rare earths in the electric motor.
The three partners will combine their know-how to design a unique electric powertrain system that offers more power on less energy without using rare earths.
Each of the three partners will contribute to the development and production of two key parts of the electric motor: the rotor and the stator.
Renault will develop and produce the EESM (electrically excited synchronous motor) rotor technology, designed without the use of rare earths, offering enhanced energy performance.
The overall architecture of the all-in-one motor for Renault Group will be designed by Renault.
Valeo and Valeo Siemens eAutomotive will develop and produce the stator based on Valeo’s technological expertise in copper wire assembly.
Valeo's expertise allows it to use a higher density of copper in the stator to generate more power without having to use more electrical energy.
Renault Group, Valeo and Valeo Siemens eAutomotive will be the first to mass produce a 200kW electric motor without using rare earths, starting in 2027.
Production of the motor for Renault Group’s own needs will be based at Renault Group’s Cléon plant in Normandy, France.

Luca de Meo, Renault Group’s Chief Executive Officer, commented that they are delighted to partner with Valeo and will design and develop a new generation of high-tech electric motors produced at the Cléon plant.

Christophe Périllat, Valeo’s Chief Executive Officer, stated that the strategic partnership will result in a major step forward for electric mobility and create a new-generation automotive motor that eliminates rare earths.
The new motor will meet stringent environmental requirements and the highest performance standards.
source_title: Renault Group, Valeo and Valeo Siemens eAutomotive partner to produce next-gen electric motor

facts: Industrial SalesLeads released its 2024 Georgia industrial capital projects report.
The analysis covers new construction, facility expansions, relocations, and significant equipment upgrades across manufacturing and related sectors.
Research confirms 217 newly identified projects statewide in 2024.
Manufacturing Facilities account for 49 projects.
Processing Facilities account for 30 projects.
Distribution and Industrial Warehouse account for 116 projects.
Power Energy Oil and Gas account for 3 projects.
Laboratory Facilities account for 2 projects.
Mine accounts for 1 project.
Data Center accounts for 5 projects.
New Construction accounts for 99 projects.
Expansion accounts for 44 projects.
Renovations and Equipment Upgrades account for 80 projects.
Relocations account for 32 projects.
Plant Closings account for 10 projects.
A project is defined as having active planning or approvals indicating new build, expansion, relocation, or major renovation with material capex and a defined site or short list of sites.
Air Emissions Control Equipment needs are identified in 66 projects.
Compressed Air Systems needs are identified in 196 projects.
Control Systems and Instrumentation needs are identified in 66 projects.
Conveyors needs are identified in 113 projects.
Floor Coatings needs are identified in 123 projects.
Material Handling and Storage needs are identified in 133 projects.
Mechanical Construction needs are identified in 187 projects.
Packaging Equipment needs are identified in 48 projects.
Process Equipment needs are identified in 25 projects.
Tanks and Vessels needs are identified in 18 projects.
A Capex Qualified Opportunity is defined as a planned project with verified capital budget and timeline sufficient to drive near to mid-term purchasing decisions across equipment and services.
The team identified 18 Georgia projects valued at $100 million or more in 2024.
Oglethorpe Power is planning $2 billion for a 1,200 MW natural gas plant in Forsyth GA.
Oglethorpe Power is planning $360 million for a 240 MW plant in Box Springs GA.
A plastic recycling service provider in Thomaston GA is investing $950 million for a 2.5 million sf processing and warehouse campus, starting in 2025 with completion in 2027.
A paper towel and tissue manufacturer in Macon GA is investing $600 million for expansion and equipment upgrades.
A hygiene products manufacturer in Macon GA is investing $418 million for manufacturing warehouse and office expansion and upgrades.
A solar panel glass manufacturer and recycling company in Cedartown GA is investing $344 million for a new manufacturing facility, with completion in 2026.
A sauce and condiment manufacturer in LaGrange GA is investing $288 million for a processing facility.
A bathroom fixture manufacturer in Morrow GA is investing $238 million for manufacturing and warehouse expansion and upgrades.
The municipality of Rome GA is investing $200 million for a water treatment plant on Riverside Pkwy.
An automotive component manufacturer in Augusta GA is investing $184 million for a new manufacturing facility, with completion in 2027.
A specialty bus manufacturer in Fort Valley GA is investing $160 million for manufacturing facility renovations and upgrades.
A solar component manufacturer in Adairsville GA is investing $154 million for a two-phase manufacturing expansion at 500 Soho Drive.
Distribution and warehouse lead volume with 116 projects, followed by manufacturing and processing, while energy and data centers show targeted growth.
An Identified Project is defined as a SalesLeads verified opportunity that includes company site details, scope, likely timeline, and key decision makers for outreach.
Industrial SalesLeads has provided industrial capital project intelligence and outsourced lead generation services since 1959.
Their Industrial Market Intelligence platform surfaces companies planning new construction, expansions, relocations, equipment modernization, and plant closures in industrial facilities.
The company provides hundreds of industrial reports within a variety of industries each month.

facts: Engineers have long recognized the problem of truck underride, where passenger vehicles go under trucks.
A patent for a side underride protective device for street cars was issued on July 14, 1896 and cited by numerous more recent underride patents.
A patent was issued in 1913 for a “Safety Device for Motor Vehicles” to provide underride protection for the sides of large trucks.
H.H. Robinson was granted a patent for a Guard System for Tractor Trailer Combinations.
US4688824 was filed on Mar 4, 1986 and published on Aug 25, 1987 to applicant Herring John D for a Safety device for vehicles.
CN100572144C was filed on May 27, 2005 and published on Dec 23, 2009 to Volvo Lastvagnar Ab for a Device for a vehicle side anticollision box, and a vehicle comprising such a side anticollision box.
US7407204 was filed on Nov 27, 2006 and published on Aug 5, 2008 to Volvo Lastvagnar Ab for an Arrangement for a vehicle side fairing, and a vehicle comprising such a side fairing.
US20070085355 was filed on Nov 27, 2006 and published on Apr 19, 2007 to Volvo Lastvagnar Ab for an Arrangement for a vehicle side fairing, and a vehicle comprising such a side fairing.
US7780224 was filed on Jun 9, 2008 and published on Aug 24, 2010 to Vanguard National Trailer Corporation for a Crash attenuating underride guard.
US20080303311 was filed on Jun 9, 2008 and published on Dec 11, 2008 to Vanguard National Trailer Corporation for a Crash attenuating underride guard.
US7887120 was filed on Jan 26, 2009 and published on Feb 15, 2011 to Transtex Composite Inc. for Aerodynamic trailer skirts.

US7942467 through US7942471 were filed on Dec 24, 2009 and published on May 17, 2011 to Transtex Composite Inc. covering aerodynamic skirt support member, securing mechanism, panel, opening, and shape.
US20090189414 was filed on Jan 26, 2009 and published on Jul 30, 2009 to Mathieu Boivin for Aerodynamic trailer skirts.
US20100096880 through US20100096883 were filed on Dec 24, 2009 and published on Apr 22, 2010 to Mathieu Boivin covering aerodynamic skirt panel, opening, and shape.
US20100187856 was filed on Dec 24, 2009 and published on Jul 29, 2010 to Mathieu Boivin for an Aerodynamic skirt support member.
US20100264691 was filed on Apr 15, 2010 and published on Oct 21, 2010 to Giromini Richard J for a Side underride cable system for a trailer.
US8292351 was filed on Apr 3, 2011 and published on Oct 23, 2012 to Transtex Composite Inc. for a Resilient strut for aerodynamic skirt.
US9004575 was filed on Aug 3, 2011 and published on Apr 14, 2015 to Gary Alan Grandominico for an Aerodynamic skirt assembly.
US20110175396 was filed on Apr 3, 2011 and published on Jul 21, 2011 to Mathieu Boivin for an Aerodynamic skirt.
US8398150 was filed on Apr 17, 2012 and published on Mar 19, 2013 to Wabash National, L.P. for a Side skirt system for a trailer.
US8449017 was filed on Sep 11, 2012 and published on May 28, 2013 to Transtex Composites Inc. for an Aerodynamic skirt resilient member.
US20120169086 was filed on Mar 7, 2012 and published on Jul 5, 2012 to Giromini Richard J for a Side underride cable system for a trailer.
US8579359 was filed on Jan 15, 2013 and published on Nov 12, 2013 to Wabash National, L.P. for a Side skirt system for a trailer.
US8678474 was filed on May 17, 2013 and published on Mar 25, 2014 to Transtex Composite Inc. for a Self-repositioning aerodynamic skirt.
US8783758 was filed on Mar 19, 2013 and published on Jul 22, 2014 to Wabash National, L.P. for a Folding side skirt system for a trailer.
US8801078 was filed on Oct 9, 2013 and published on Aug 12, 2014 to Wabash National, L.P. for a Side skirt system for a trailer.
US9199676 was filed on Jul 2, 2014 and published on Dec 1, 2015 to Wabash National, L. P. for a Side skirt system for a trailer.
US9409610 was filed on Mar 11, 2015 and published on Aug 9, 2016 to Wabash National, L.P. for a Side skirt system for a trailer.
US9573636 was filed on Mar 12, 2015 and published on Feb 21, 2017 to Ridge Corporation for an Aerodynamic skirt assembly.
US9688320 was filed on Oct 27, 2015 and published on Jun 27, 2017 to Wabash National, L.P. for a Side skirt system for a trailer.
In 1977, William Page was granted US4060268 for a guard rail for side protection on large wheel vehicles.
In 1996, Volvo began manufacturing trucks with Front Underrun Protection in Europe.
A patent was filed in 2007 for an “Underrun protector and method of providing underrun protection.”

A patent was filed in 2009 for a “Side impact guard device for industrial vehicles, particularly trailers or semi-trailers” (US7967349), filed Apr 7, 2009 and published Jun 28, 2011 to C.R.F. Societa Consortile Per Azioni.

In 2012, Sapa Extrusions inventor/engineer Malcolm Deighton filed for a patent for a “Semi trailer under-run protection device” which was later developed into a rear underride guard successfully crash tested on a trailer in April 2017.
In 2015, Aaron Kiefer, crash reconstructionist and forensic engineer, was issued a patent (US9463759) for an innovative combination side & rear trailer underride protection system.
A 2015 patent for a Trailer rear impact guard cites numerous other underride protection patents (USD790407).
In 2016, Stephen Batzer and Patrick Rogers were granted a patent for an Adjustable side under-ride guard for sliding axle trailer.
On October 11, 2016, Aaron Kiefer was granted a TrailerGuard system underride protection patent (pat9463759).
In 2016, Stephen Batzer and Patrick Rogers were granted a patent for a Telescoping side under-ride guard for sliding axle trailer.

In 2017, Seven Hills Engineering inventor Perry Ponder's AngelWing side guard Underride Protection was successfully crash tested at 35 MPH by IIHS on 3/30/2017 and 40 MPH on 8/29/2017, with a patent pending.
On March 1, 2017, the IIHS TOUGHGuard Award was announced for improved rear underride guard design by trailer manufacturers.
In 2017, SAPA (Hydro Extrusions) published results of a 40 mph crash test of a Rear Impact (Underride) Guard in the Sapa 40-30 RIG Test Engineering Report Version 1.3.
On September 29, 2017, Wabash Trailers announced a prototype side guard.
In 2018, Aaron Kiefer was granted a patent for an underride collision device for large trucks (pat9908493).
On June 20, 2019, Vanguard Trailers was granted an application for a strap side guard patent.
In 2019, Fortier was granted Patent No.: US 10,343 for a Side protection device for a vehicle, related kit and vehicle provided with the same.
In 2020, Wabash received a Side Underride Guard Patent (Wabash 2020 SUG patent).
In 2021, a patent was issued for an Underride Guard Assembly for Trailers.
In 2022, a SIDE UNDERRIDE GUARD ASSEMBLY FOR A TRAILER patent was issued.
In 2023, Utility Trailer Manufacturing received a side guard patent.
In 2023, Great Dane Trailers received a side guard patent.

entity_name: $1.5 Billion Transportation Infrastructure Investment in Georgia
facts: Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced a new $1.5 billion transportation infrastructure investment for the state on Thursday.

The funding will advance transportation projects reviewed by the State Transportation Board during its monthly meeting and serves as a down payment for future expansion as Georgia experiences record job and population growth.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that unprecedented job creation has been welcomed to all regions of the Peach State over the last few years, and this funding will ensure the infrastructure network meets the needs of that growth.
The investment was first announced during the 2024 Legislative Session.
The investment will more than double the state’s funding to cities and counties for local transportation infrastructure.
$250 million is allocated for Local Roads Funding, which more than doubles State funding for every city and county for local transportation investments.
$593 million is allocated for Capital Construction, increasing GDOT’s capital construction program by 58 percent to help manage cost increases due to inflation.
The Capital Construction funding will expedite projects in GDOT’s existing pipeline of projects.
The Capital Construction funding will help accelerate 24 projects by a total of 43 years.
$50 million is allocated for Capital Maintenance, increasing GDOT’s capital maintenance budget by 32 percent to resurface state highways around the state.
$98 million is allocated for Airport Aid, advancing improvements at various general aviation airports.
$500 million is allocated for a new Freight Program that includes 18 projects devoted to freight-carrying infrastructure.
The Freight Program aims to improve efficiency, safety, and reliability for the transportation of goods across Georgia.

Lt. Governor Burt Jones stated that Georgia is the No. 1 state to build and grow a business and emphasized that investing surplus budget funds in existing and future transportation infrastructure is critical to ensure businesses can expand and goods and services can be transported.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns stated that Georgia has witnessed unprecedented growth and development in every corner of the state over the last several years.

Speaker Jon Burns noted that this historic growth presents a significant opportunity to strengthen and secure roads, bridges, and economic development centers facing increased demand for the foreseeable future.
source_title: Gov. Kemp announces details for $1.5 billion investment in transportation infrastructure in Ga.

facts: Vernay Labs has its headquarters in Atlanta, GA.
The company designs and custom manufactures critical flow-control valves for leading OEMs and medical device manufacturers worldwide.
During the COVID-19 pandemic, Vernay supplied the majority of ventilator manufacturers across the world.
Vernay is a key supplier to Ventec Life Systems, which partnered with General Motors to urgently increase output of critical-care ventilators.
Vernay components are used in visible medical devices and in the industrial mixing of chemicals for sanitizers and disinfectants.
The Griffin, GA plant successfully managed double-digit volume increases during manufacturing facility shutdowns in China and Italy through collaboration between global teams.
Vernay has engineering, testing, and manufacturing capabilities in the U.S., Italy, the Netherlands, and China, with sales and customer service offices in Germany, Japan, and Singapore.
Vernay is investing in the United States and Europe to add more than 15,000 square feet of clean room space.
The company earned ISO Class 8 clean-room certification for the assembly area at its Griffin, GA plant.
Quality Systems Manager Christina Ward oversees the ISO Class 8 certification effort and is a 21-year veteran in medical manufacturing.
Ward explained that certifying an adjacent manufacturing space as an ISO class 8 clean room is challenging because the area houses more than 18 molding presses and 30 finishing/assembly stations.
Ward is leading efforts to educate and train employees working in the medical area on necessary protocols.
Ward noted that gaining additional clean-room certification will open the door to new customers and allow expansion of offerings to existing customers.
Vernay is building a new clean room at its Asti, Italy facility, due to be completed by year’s end, as part of an expansion of the entire 43,000-square-foot site.
The Asti site currently has a soft-wall, ISO 8 clean room used for washing and packaging products, which is being expanded to address increased customer demands.

Managing Director Vanna Villata explained that the company performs custom elastomeric molding, overmolding, and assemblies of rubber, plastic, and metal parts, designing and manufacturing fully functioning assemblies and connectors for complex flow control needs.

The 125-person Asti plant produces more than 300 million parts annually for the global fluid-control marketplace and is poised to grow exponentially to serve increased medical demands in Europe and Asia.

Vernay Italia shut down for more than a month in spring 2020 due to the region’s COVID-19 outbreak, with Technical and Quality Director Mauro Gertosio noting that authorities initially did not recognize the importance of their products to the medical supply chain.
Gertosio assembled a team focused on the medical sector, and the site gained ISO-13485 certification in just a few weeks during the pandemic after already adhering to the guidelines.
Gertosio explained that the company worked diligently with the Italian government to be designated as an essential business and reopened the plant in May 2020.

Reopening allowed the resumption of supplying parts for ventilators, blood pumps, oxygenators, and disposable surgical supplies, while also accelerating plans to expand the facility and gain ISO Class 7 clean room certification.
Over 85% of all components produced by Vernay are custom parts designed specifically for a customer’s unique need, following a streamlined methodology from concept to launch.

Devices relying on Vernay components include those used in cancer tissue diagnosis, bio-reactors, microfluidics, minimally invasive surgeries, pediatric respiratory inhalers, anesthesia delivery, one-way valves and septums for bag systems, dialysis, and negative wound pressure therapies.

Vernay produces engineered solutions for critical medical applications using transfer, compression, injection, flashless, and liquid injection molding techniques, along with overmolding, bonding, welding, inline leak and flow testing, and camera systems.

The company has developed more than 33,000 elastomeric materials and compound formulations tailored to each end use, utilizing proprietary linear and non-linear molding software simulation to understand elastomer flow behavior.
Vernay is positioned to take on additional medical business with ISO 8 clean room certification in the U.S. and expansion plus ISO 7 clean room certification in Europe.
Vernay Laboratories has been a technology leader in customized flow-control components since its founding in 1946 and formulates its own compounds for each specific application.

The privately held firm produces more than 1 billion components per year using a library of more than 33,000 elastomeric materials and compound formulations, operating six plants globally in the U.S., the Netherlands, Italy, and China.
source_title: Critical Business: Controlling the Flow - Vernay
