facts: Georgia’s economy is projected to grow by 2.4% in 2025, a slowdown from the 3.1% growth seen in 2024.
Georgia’s GDP is projected to grow faster than the nation’s, which forecasters project to grow by 1.6% in 2025.
The economic slowdown is attributed to a deliberate policy shift rather than an external economic shock or factor outside state control.
Inflation successfully decreased from 8% in 2022 to 3% in 2024 and is projected to remain at 3% in 2025.
The Federal Reserve began reducing monetary policy restraint in September by cutting interest rates, with additional rate cuts expected in 2025.
Quantitative tightening is expected to end, leading to moderate expansion of credit and money supply.
The probability of Georgia slipping into a recession is 25%.
The number of jobs in Georgia is projected to rise by 1% in 2025, slower than the 1.5% gain estimated for 2024 but higher than the national job growth rate of 0.6%.
Georgia’s unemployment rate is expected to increase from 3.7% to 4%, remaining lower than the projected U.S. level of 4.3%.
The rise in unemployment will be driven by slowed hiring rather than layoffs.
Health care and housing are projected to lead Georgia’s economic growth in 2025.
A growing population and increasing number of senior residents will continue to boost health care hiring across the state.
Homebuilders are expected to ramp up construction of single-family homes due to slightly lower mortgage rates, favorable demographics, and a post-pandemic housing shortage.
Georgia homebuilders will increase the number of single-family homes built in 2025 by 9%.
Home prices, which have increased 65% since before the COVID-19 pandemic, are expected to hold steady.
Georgia is likely to experience job losses in retail and information sectors due to technological advances and competition from online retailers.
Georgia has broken records in attracting new jobs and industries, though this pace will slow due to the national and global economic slowdown.
The build-out of existing projects is expected to create thousands of new jobs over the next few years.

Advantages for economic development include a favorable mix of industries, supportive demographics, excellent transportation and logistics infrastructure, a competitive tax climate, and low costs of living and doing business.

links.context: Detailed economic forecast and outlook information

source_title: Georgia’s economy will grow in 2025, but more slowly

facts: Vista Metals has recently completed the construction of its seventh aluminium casthouse at Adairsville, Georgia.
This achievement marks a pivotal point in the company's extensive $100 million long-term capital strategy and constitutes the second phase of the initiative.
The newly established casthouse in Georgia encompasses integrating melting furnaces, homogenising ovens, and accompanying support apparatus.
VMC Specialty Alloys LLC, also known as Vista Metals Georgia, functions under a licensing arrangement with Vista Metals Corp, headquartered in Fontana, CA.
With Vista Progressive Metals LLC, a key player in aluminium lithium products, this consortium stands at the forefront of manufacturing speciality aluminium products.
Its products cater to diverse sectors, including aerospace, commercial aircraft, defence, automotive, and general industrial applications.

Kelly Thomas, the President of Vista Metals, said, "Vista's investment in this expansion will further support the growth in the core markets we serve, including outer space, commercial aviation, defence, and industrial applications."
"In addition, we are completing final equipment installation in our new world-class finishing facility, which is a critical part of this expansion phase."
Vista Metals has unveiled plans for a $60 million investment in a fresh site catering to the aerospace sector with specialized aluminium offerings as part of its expansion strategy.
This addition, situated in Bowling Green, Kentucky, will augment Vista Metals' current operations in California and Georgia.
Andy Primack, the CEO of Vista Metals, said, "We are excited to announce our new location in Kentucky, with a plan to bring over 50 new jobs to the area."

"Bowling Green is a growing city with a strong industrial business base. Critical to our success is its proven track record of attracting qualified manufacturing talent, the close interaction between business and education, and its long-term community development plan."
The new Kentucky site will serve the aerospace extrusion, forging, and rolling markets with downstream processing and value-added products.
It will also cater to commercial aviation and extend its support to defence, automotive, and various industrial sectors.
With initial site groundwork underway, Vista Metals is gearing up to gradually expand to meet the rising demands in its primary markets.
source_title: Vista Metals adds seventh aluminium casthouse to Georgia facility

canonical_name: VMC Specialty Alloys LLC (dba Vista Metals Georgia)
facts: Vista Metals Georgia announced the completion of its 7th cast-house in Adairsville, Georgia on April 30, 2024.
The cast-house completion is a significant milestone in the company’s ongoing $100 million long-range capital plan.
This expansion represents the second phase of the capital plan and includes the installation of melting furnaces, homogenizing ovens, and support equipment at the Adairsville facility.
Kelly Thomas serves as President of Vista Metals Georgia.
Kelly Thomas stated that Vista’s investment in the expansion will further support growth in core markets including outer space, commercial aviation, defense, and industrial applications.
Kelly Thomas added that final equipment installation is underway in a new world-class finishing facility, which is a critical part of this expansion phase.
On March 19, 2024, Vista Metals announced an investment in a new location to serve the aerospace industry with specialty aluminum products.
The new facility will be located in Bowling Green, Kentucky.
The Kentucky facility is part of Vista Metals’ long-range investment plan and will expand upon existing capabilities in California and Georgia.
The initial investment for the new Kentucky site is nearly $60 million.
The new site will primarily support downstream processing and value-added products serving aerospace extrusion, forging, and rolling markets.
In addition to commercial aviation, the Kentucky location will support defense, automotive, and general industrial applications.
Initial site preparation for the Kentucky facility is underway as of the March 19, 2024 announcement.
Vista Metals plans to commence a phased build-out at the Kentucky site to support growth in its core markets.
Kentucky Governor Andy Beshear commented on the continued growth of Kentucky’s metals sector and thanked Vista Metals leaders for their commitment to the state.
Andy Primack serves as CEO of Vista Metals.
Andy Primack stated that the company plans to bring over 50 new jobs to the Bowling Green area.
Andy Primack noted that Bowling Green has a strong industrial business base, a proven track record of attracting qualified manufacturing talent, and close interaction between business and education.
Kelly Thomas, President of Vista Metals, stated that the Kentucky opening underscores the company’s commitment to the aerospace industry and robust supply chains.

Kelly Thomas noted that OEMs look to more than double commercial aircraft build rates over the next two decades, prompting investments to enable customers to test and qualify the facility ahead of projected growth.
VMC Specialty Alloys LLC operates under a licensing agreement with Fontana, California-based Vista Metals LLC.
Vista Progressive Metals LLC produces aluminum lithium products.
The group of companies are market leaders in manufacturing specialty aluminum products supporting aerospace, commercial aircraft, defense, automotive, and general industrial applications.

facts: Vista Metals Georgia has broken ground on a new $30 million expansion project at its Adairsville manufacturing facility.
The expansion follows the completion and launch of its sixth casthouse in the spring of 2021.
This expansion represents the second phase of the company’s nearly $100 million long-range capital plan.
The long-range capital plan includes the installation of a seventh aluminum cast-house, melting furnaces, homogenizing ovens, and support equipment.
The new casthouse expansion project will occur simultaneously with the construction of a new finishing facility.
The new finishing facility will support downstream processing and value-added products for the aerospace extrusion, forging, and rolling markets.
The expansion projects are expected to be fully operational sometime in 2024.
Andrew Primack, president of Vista Metals Georgia, stated that the continued investments demonstrate commitment to customers and the market space.
The long-range plan calls for the eventual installation of cast-houses 8 and 9 to ensure sufficient casting and finishing capacity for a recovering market.
Vista Metals Georgia operates under a licensing agreement with Fontana, Calif.-based Vista Metals Corp.

Along with Vista Progressive Metals, a producer of aluminum lithium products, the three companies are leaders in manufacturing specialty aluminum products supporting aerospace, commercial aircraft, defense, automotive, and general industrial applications.
source_title: Vista Metals Launches $30 Million Expansion - Metal Center News - Solutions for Profitable Distribution

KDC GA’s mission is to ensure timely and reliable product delivery to affiliates through operational excellence and automation.
KDC GA Corp. is built to support high-volume logistics with speed and precision.
The company operates a state-of-the-art Georgia facility to ensure that partners receive consistent, dependable service.

facts: Lithium Ionic Corp. announced the appointment of Clovis Torres to its Board of Directors.
Mr. Torres has more than three decades of experience across mining, energy, infrastructure, project finance, M&A, regulatory affairs, compliance, crisis management, and public company governance.
His appointment strengthens Lithium Ionic's Board as the Company advances its 100%-owned Bandeira Lithium Project in Minas Gerais, Brazil.
Mr. Torres previously served as an Executive and General Counsel of Vale S.A. for 17 years ending in 2018.
At Vale, his responsibilities included legal, governance, compliance, institutional relations, M&A, tax, energy, and human resources.
During his tenure at Vale, he served as Secretary to the Board of Directors and led the company's Crisis and Ethics Committees.
Mr. Torres served as a member of the Board of Directors of Petrobras S.A.
He previously served as Chairman of the Board of BR Distribuidora S.A.
He previously served as Chairman of the Board of Directors of IBRAM, the Brazilian Mining Institute.
He previously served as President and Chief Executive Officer of Furnas Centrais Elétricas S.A., one of Brazil's largest power generation and transmission companies.
At Furnas, he led initiatives related to privatization preparation, renewable power development, hydrogen energy, and infrastructure modernization.

Earlier in his career, Mr. Torres held senior roles in project finance, international investment, and legal advisory with the International Finance Corporation, Cargill, Clyde & Co., and leading Brazilian law firms.
He is currently Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer of Belo Sun Mining Corp.
He is a founding partner of Mello, Torres Advogados, a Brazilian law firm focused on project finance, M&A, restructuring, mining, power, environmental law, and crisis management.

Under his leadership as Chairman and CEO, Belo Sun recently secured the reinstatement and subsequent reissuance of the Installation License for its 100%-owned Volta Grande Gold Project in Pará State, Brazil after the license had been suspended since 2017.
Blake Hylands, P.Geo., Chief Executive Officer of Lithium Ionic, commented that they are very pleased to welcome Clovis to the board.

Hylands stated Torres's depth of experience in Brazilian mining, energy, infrastructure, project finance, governance, compliance, and stakeholder engagement is directly relevant as they advance Bandeira toward construction readiness.
Hylands noted Torres's proven ability to navigate Brazil's permitting and regulatory environment will be invaluable for de-risking and advancing Bandeira toward construction.
The appointment reflects a continued commitment to strong independent oversight, responsible project development, and deepening Brazilian leadership and governance capabilities.

Mr. Torres commented that he is pleased to join the Board at an important stage in the Company's development as Bandeira is a high-quality lithium project located in one of Brazil's emerging mining districts.

He looks forward to working with the Board and management team to support the responsible advancement of the Project and contribute his experience in Brazilian mining, infrastructure, governance, and project development.
Mr. Torres holds an LL.M. with a specialization in International Law, Trade and Finance from Tulane Law School and a law degree from Universidade Católica de Salvador.
He is fluent in Portuguese, English, and Spanish, with a working knowledge of French.

He has been recognized with the Legislative Merit Medal from the Brazilian Congress, the Medal of Honor from the State of Minas Gerais, and Latin American and Global General Counsel of the Year awards from the Association of Corporate Counsel and International Law Office.
Lithium Ionic is a Canadian lithium development company focused on responsibly advancing its 100%-owned Bandeira Lithium Project in Minas Gerais, Brazil.
The project region is coined the "Lithium Valley" that is emerging as a premier hard-rock lithium district.
The Company is executing on a focused development strategy centered on engineering de-risking, permitting advancement, commercial planning, and construction readiness.
The goal of the Bandeira Lithium Project is to become a near-term producer of high-quality spodumene concentrate for global battery supply chains.
source_title: Lithium Ionic Strengthens Board with Appointment of Brazilian Mining and Governance Executive Clovis Torres | INN

facts: Grid Metals announced a maiden National Instrument 43-101 compliant Mineral Resource Estimate on its 75% owned Donner Lake Lithium Project in Manitoba, Canada.
The resource consists of 6.81 million tonnes (Inferred) grading 1.39% Li2O.
Grid Metals signed a binding lease agreement with 1911 Gold Corporation to use its nearby True North mill for the production of lithium spodumene concentrate.
The True North mill is an existing processing facility that will be reconfigured to enable lithium spodumene production.
The True North mill is a permitted gold mill utilizing a flotation circuit with a nominal annual capacity of 475,000 tonnes.
The mill lease term is up to five years plus a two-year cancellation period.
The True North tailings facility is also permitted, though an additional permit is required to reconfigure the mill and tailings for lithium processing.
Grid Metals signed a contract with Primero Group to complete a scoping-level mill reconfiguration study within 90 days.
Grid Metals has ongoing discussions with Tantalum Mining Company of Canada Limited for toll milling available resources at the currently operating Tanco Mine, located 35 km south of Donner Lake.
The Donner Lake resource remains open for expansion, with ongoing property exploration focused on generating new exploration targets.
Robin Dunbar is the CEO of Grid Metals.

The global maiden resource estimate consists of 2.10 MMt grading 1.42% Li2O from Open Pit resources (cut-off grade of 0.3% Li2O) and 4.71 MMt grading 1.37% Li2O from below pit underground resources (cut-off grade of 0.5% Li2O).
The mineral resources are hosted by steeply dipping pegmatite dykes that are exposed at surface and display consistent widths, spodumene content, and lithium grade.
Lithium mineralization at the Main and NW dykes remains open to depth with strong Li2O values in many of the deepest holes.
The Company has identified a number of other exploration drill targets at Donner Lake and has an extensive land position it is actively exploring.
The True North mill is part of an integrated, fully permitted gold mine, mill, and tailings complex last operational in November 2022.
The True North mill is located 85 km by road from the Donner Lake Property.
An application to permit the processing of lithium material at the True North mill will be required.

Primero Group Ltd. has been engaged to complete a plant reconfiguration scoping-level study to assess the viability and cost of reconfiguring the True North mill to process spodumene-bearing material.
The scoping study is expected to be substantially complete before the end of the 90-day due diligence period stipulated in the Agreement with 1911 Gold.
Grid Metals has a pre-production MOU with Tanco to process lithium material at its operational lithium circuit.
The maiden lithium resource estimate was prepared by SGS Geological Services and reported in accordance with National Instrument 43-101 standards.
The NI 43-101 technical report for the resource estimate will be accessible on SEDAR within 45 days of the news release.

The current mineral resource estimate comprises spodumene-enriched pegmatite from three northwest striking pegmatite dykes: the Main Dyke, a smaller sub-parallel dyke located ~50 metres north of the Main Dyke (the Hangingwall Dyke), and the NW Dyke.
The Main Dyke interpretation used 57 drillholes totalling 13,478 metres of core.
The NW Dyke interpretation used 34 drillholes totalling 7,815 metres of core.
The resources cover an approximate strike length of 1,100 metres for the Main Dyke and 700 metres for the NW Dyke.
Vertical extents are approximately 300 metres for the Main Dyke and ~250 metres for the NW Dyke.
Open pit resources cover the uppermost ~100 metres in both the Main and NW dykes and are constrained by optimized pit models.
Initial metallurgical testwork confirms that spodumene accounts for over 90% of the lithium-bearing minerals in the Main and NW dykes.
The open-pit resources total 2.10 MMt grading 1.42% Li2O, including 1.14 MMt grading 1.48% Li2O at the Main and Hangingwall dykes, and 0.96 MMt grading 1.36% Li2O at the NW Dyke.
The underground resources total 4.71 MMt grading 1.37% Li2O, including 3.67 MMt grading 1.39% Li2O at the Main Dyke and 1.04 MMt grading 1.11% Li2O at the NW Dyke.
The mineral resources remain open at depth, particularly at the Main Dyke.
The Company anticipates a new phase of exploration drilling later in the year.
The mineral resource estimate was created using SGS's proprietary modelling software, Genesis, and open pit shells were created in Whittle software.
The mineralised wireframe was restricted to drill core logged as lithium-bearing pegmatite.
Block models were created using a 5m x 3m x 5m block size in the x-, y-, and z-planes.
Grade was estimated using inverse distance squared (ID2) methodology and three passes of estimation.
All resources are classified as Inferred Resources, consistent with the 2014 CIM Definitions for Mineral Resources and Mineral Reserves.
The Mineral Resource Estimate has an effective date of June 27, 2023.
The Qualified Person for the MRE is Mr. Rohan Millar, P.Geo., an employee of SGS.
All resources are presented undiluted and in situ, constrained by continuous 3D wireframe models, and are considered to have reasonable prospects for eventual economic extraction.
Mineral resources which are not mineral reserves do not have demonstrated economic viability.
It is envisioned that parts of the Donner Lake deposit may be mined using open pit mining methods.
In-pit mineral resources are reported at a cut-off grade of 0.3% Li2O within a conceptual pit shell.
Underground Mineral Resources are estimated from the bottom of the pit and reported at a base case cut-off grade of 0.5% Li2O.
Bulk density values (specific gravity 2.7 grams per cubic centimetre) were determined based on physical test work from each deposit.

The in-pit base case cut-off grade of 0.3% Li2O considers a lithium concentrate 6% (LC6) Li2O price of US$1800/tonne, a mining cost of US$3.50/t rock and processing, treatment and refining, transportation and G&A cost of US$45.00/t mineralised material, and an overall pit slope of 55 degrees.

The below-pit base case cut-off grade of 0.5% Li2O considers a lithium concentrate 6% (LC6) Li2O price of US$1800/tonne, a mining cost of US$60.00/t rock and processing, treatment and refining, transportation, and G&A cost of US$45.00/t mineralised material.
The True North mill is located in Bissett, Manitoba, approximately 85 km by existing all-weather road from the Donner Lake Lithium Project.
The original processing facility at the True North site was constructed in 1931 but was destroyed by a fire in 1980.
The facility was reconstructed in 1996 with an operating capacity of 900 tonnes per day, and mill capacity was increased to 2,300 tpd in 2011.
Since 2011, the mill capacity has been scaled back to 1,300 tpd.
The mill was last in operation in November 2022 and was reprocessing gold tailings.
The current processing facility consists of crushing, milling, centrifugal gravity concentration, and flotation circuits.
Initial metallurgical test results concluded that standard flotation was the best method for recovering lithium from Donner Lake samples.
Heavy Liquid Separation was conducted as an initial evaluation of the Dense Media Separation process but was determined to not be of material value for enhancing lithium recovery.
Grid Metals engaged Primero to complete a plant reconfiguration study including preliminary capital costing analysis.
Primero has worked with lithium companies including Sigma Lithium, Core Lithium, and Sayona Mining.
The initial lease term for the True North mill is five years, followed by a two-year notice period for cancellation, or it may be extended by mutual agreement for an additional five years.

The lease agreement conveys rights to Grid Metals including use of the mill to process lithium material and the right to reconfigure the existing mill circuit provided it is returned to original working condition at lease end.
Grid Metals has the right to use the tailings facility and apply for required permits and government approvals for processing lithium-bearing material.
Grid Metals has the right to add infrastructure to the mill assets, subject to an obligation to remove any such infrastructure upon termination of the Definitive Agreement.
Under certain conditions, Grid would provide cost plus toll milling of gold material for 1911 Gold during the lease term.
Payments by Grid to 1911 Gold include an upfront non-refundable payment of C$300,000.
Grid must make an equity participation payment in a 1911 Gold private placement of C$450,000 within 90 days of signing the Agreement.
Within 90 days of signing, Grid must make a total payment of C$1,000,000 consisting of a C$250,000 lease payment and C$750,000 of financial assistance.
Commencing January 1, 2024, monthly payments will cover ongoing site expenses for site security, environmental monitoring, and maintenance.
Financial assistance payments include up to C$1,000,000 due on December 31, 2023; C$1,000,000 on December 31, 2024; and C$900,000 on December 31, 2025.
A payment of C$1,000,000 is due upon commencement of commercial production by Grid at the mill, defined as processing at least 200,000 tonnes of lithium material.
A payment of C$1,000,000 is due on the fifth anniversary of the lease.
A payment of C$2,000,000 is due if the parties agree to extend the lease for an additional five-year period at the end of the first five-year term.
Grid must pay a 1% net smelter returns royalty in favour of 1911 Gold, subject to Grid receiving a right of first refusal on any disposition by 1911 Gold.
A fee payment of $7.50 per tonne of lithium material processed through the mill facility is required during the lease term.
Grid must assume C$10,000,000 of reclamation obligations prorated equally over years 6-10 of the lease if extended.
Grid may terminate the agreement up to 90 days from signing after making initial cash and equity payments, or thereafter once cumulative total payments reach C$2,500,000.
1911 Gold shall permit Grid to register a floating charge security interest on the mill assets equal to Grid's total financial assistance payments and capital expenditures after 90 days from signing.
Grid is pursuing a two-stage permitting process at Donner Lake, starting with an Advanced Exploration Permit application to enable a bulk sample of up to 50,000 tonnes.
The second permitting step is an application for an Environmental Act Licence (mining permit).
The True North Mill will require an amendment to its existing permits to enable processing of lithium material.
Dr. Dave Peck, P.Geo., has reviewed and approved the technical content of the release.
Grid Metals is focused on both lithium and Ni-Cu-PGM projects in the Bird River greenstone belt in southeastern Manitoba, approximately 150 km northeast of Winnipeg.
The company controls two major lithium properties with known high-grade lithium pegmatites: Donner Lake and Falcon West.
The 75% owned Donner Lake Property is situated 35km north of the producing Tanco mine, one of only two lithium-producing mines in Canada.
In addition to lithium assets, the Company controls a PEA stage Ni-Cu-PGM-Co project (Makwa-Mayville) undergoing exploration and development activity.
The Donner Lake and Falcon West properties are located in the traditional territory of the Sagkeeng First Nation.
source_title:
Grid Metals Announces Maiden Mineral Resource at Donner Lake Lithium Property; Lease Agreement Signed for True North Mill Provides Additional Flexibility for Future Lithium Production |
                                    Newswire

facts: Vista Metals announced it will invest approximately $60 million in a new facility in Bowling Green, Warren County, Kentucky.
The California-based company plans to focus on commercial aviation products that serve the aerospace extrusion, forging and rolling markets.
The new 75,000-square-foot plant will also be able to support customers in the defense, automotive and general industries.
Sitework is already underway at the project location.
The project location will likely encompass 100 acres.
Construction will follow a phased timeline.
Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear's office confirmed the sitework and construction timeline details.
The Kentucky Economic Development Finance Authority (KEDFA) preliminarily approved a 15-year incentive agreement with Vista Metals as part of the Kentucky Business Investment program.
The performance-based agreement means Vista Metals will not receive any money until it hits capital investment and job targets.
The agreement will pay the company up to $2.5 million in tax incentives.
KEDFA also approved Vista Metals for up to $500,000 in tax incentives through the Kentucky Enterprise Initiative Act (KEIA).
KEIA allows qualified businesses to recover Kentucky sales and use taxes on construction costs and building fixtures, as well as on equipment used for research, development and electronic processing.
Vista Metals will be able to take advantage of no-cost recruitment services via Kentucky's workforce service providers.
Vista Metals will receive job placement services through Kentucky's workforce service providers.
Vista Metals will access reduced-cost customized training via Kentucky's workforce service providers.
Vista Metals will receive job-training incentives via Kentucky's workforce service providers.
"We are excited to announce our new location in Kentucky, with a plan to bring over 50 new jobs to the area," said Andy Primack, CEO of Vista Metals.

"Bowling Green is a growing city with a strong industrial business base," Primack said. "Critical to our success is their proven track record of attracting qualified manufacturing talent, the close interaction between business and education and their long-term community development plan."
Envision AESC, a Japanese company, has a $2 billion electric vehicle battery plant located in Bowling Green.
The Envision AESC plant is still under construction.
Envision AESC broke ground on its project in April 2022.
Envision AESC topped out the structure of its project in September of last year.
The Envision AESC project spans three million square feet.
The Envision AESC plant is expected to supply enough battery power for 300,000 vehicles a year by 2027.
source_title: Vista Metals building $60M Kentucky facility

facts: Austrian automotive supplier voestalpine Automotive Body Parts Inc. announced plans to open an $11.1 million Birmingham facility expected to create 42 jobs.
The company will produce a variety of automotive components, including stamped and hot formed parts, at an existing facility located at 260-A Lyon Lane in Birmingham’s Oxmoor Valley.
Governor Robert Bentley stated that automotive manufacturing is a significant industry in Alabama and praised the state's workforce as a strong partner for voestalpine's project.
The expansion into Alabama stems from the overall growth of the automotive segment and increased demand from voestalpine's customers.
The Birmingham facility spans 52,000 square feet and provides capacity for future growth.
Local managing director Philipp Schulz stated the investment is an important step in the company's U.S. growth strategy and expressed excitement about joining Alabama's automotive supplier network.
The company thanked state and local officials along with the Birmingham Industrial Development Board for their assistance in the project.
Voestalpine is a key supplier to European automakers and is expanding into Alabama as the state's auto supply chain undergoes significant growth.
Last year, more than $3 billion in new investment was announced in Alabama's auto sector, with most of that coming from suppliers.
Employment by automotive suppliers in Alabama has more than doubled since 2010.

Greg Canfield, secretary of the Alabama Department of Commerce, described voestalpine as a leading partner to the European auto industry and a strong addition to Alabama's expanding automotive supply chain.
Greg Canfield stated that the state's economic development team will continue working hard to build out the supply chain with new jobs and new investment.
Voestalpine worked closely with the Alabama Department of Commerce, City of Birmingham, Alabama Power Co., and Birmingham Business Alliance on the expansion.
As part of "Project Alpine," the City of Birmingham agreed to abate the non-educational allocated portion of sales and property taxes related to the project.
The tax abatement incentive is valued at an estimated $960,237 over 10 years.
Birmingham Mayor William A. Bell Sr. noted that this project marks the second company within the voestalpine group to locate within the city, the first being voestalpine Nortrack.
Jefferson County Commissioner David Carrington welcomed voestalpine to the area and expressed anticipation for its positive economic impact and future expansion opportunities.
The Linz, Austria-based parent company voestalpine Group AG comprises 500 companies with locations in more than 50 countries across five continents.
Beyond automotive and railway systems, the parent company operates in segments including energy, construction, mechanical engineering, and aerospace.
BBA senior project manager Jeff Traywick stated the Birmingham location will provide voestalpine ready access to existing and potential new automotive customers while offering overall efficiencies.
source_title: Auto supplier voestalpine to invest $11 million in Alabama facility - Made in Alabama

facts: voestalpine in Cartersville, Georgia in the US chose AP&T’s extensive service agreement to secure production capacity and minimize unplanned downtime of press-hardening lines.
The facility makes structural parts for some of the world’s chief OEMs whose production relies on having a constant flow of parts and components.
Unplanned downtime in any part of the delivery chain can have dire consequences for voestalpine and its customers.
Fredrik Stålberg is the Managing Director and CTO of voestalpine in Cartersville.
About a year ago, voestalpine was experiencing operational pressure with production running around the clock manufacturing enormous volumes.
To minimize risk from potential operational issues, voestalpine decided to enter a service agreement with AP&T.
The agreement covers two press-hardening lines from AP&T that have been in operation since 2017 and are critical to the facility’s production capacity.
voestalpine originally entered a basic service agreement for the two lines but soon discovered the value of a more comprehensive setup.
Magnus Svenningsson is the Director of Aftermarket Sales at AP&T.
The agreement signed in the spring of 2022 includes regular preventative maintenance, 24/7 online support and on-site assistance with priority response time at a fixed monthly cost.
voestalpine now has access to quick, qualified support in the evenings and on weekends, benefiting greatly from remote resolutions and short-notice technician visits.
Tobias Kronstedt is the Aftermarket Service Manager for AP&T in the US.
The new agreement allows AP&T to provide voestalpine with exactly the support needed to secure production.
voestalpine is considering taking advantage of more aspects of AP&T's service offering in the future.
voestalpine is currently in discussions with AP&T about designing an agreement to guarantee uptime, potentially by handing over stocking spare machine parts to AP&T for guaranteed delivery times.
This potential future arrangement could free up warehouse space and reduce tied-up capital for voestalpine.
Andrea Clark is the President of AP&T North America.

source_title: Voestalpine in Cartersville, Georgia, USA secures production with AP&T service commitment

facts: Sweden's Volvo Cars is expanding production at its US plant to localize production, deflect tariffs, and build more cars that appeal to buyers in one of its biggest markets.
Volvo, wholly owned by China's Geely, announced it would bring a fourth vehicle to its production facility located in Ridgeville, S.C.
The new vehicle is a next-generation hybrid model designed to meet the 'specific demands' of the US market.
Volvo offered no details of the new vehicle, but others have surmised it will be a large crossover SUV of some sort.
Volvo stock trading in Sweden jumped over 3% following the announcement.
This summer, Volvo announced that it would bring its bestselling XC60 midsize SUV to the South Carolina production line starting in late 2026.
The company currently builds the XC60 at its plant in Gothenburg, Sweden.
The XC60 is an important car for Volvo stateside; the company has sold over 27,000 XC60s in the US in the first eight months of 2025, an increase of almost 20% year over year.
The South Carolina plant currently produces the fully electric Volvo EX90 SUV and its sister car, the Polestar 3.
The South Carolina plant has installed production capacity of 150,000 cars per year.
Altogether, Volvo's investment at its US plant stands at $1.3 billion.
Volvo Cars CEO Håkan Samuelsson stated: 'Our investment plans once again reinforce our long-term commitment to the US market and our manufacturing operations in South Carolina.'
Samuelsson led Volvo from 2012 to 2022 and replaced prior CEO Jim Rowan in March of this year.
Volvo has auto production plants in Gothenburg, Ridgeville, Chengdu, Daqing, Taizhou in China, as well as in Ghent, Belgium.
Volvo said its regionalization efforts allow the company to 'better meet' the needs of its customers.
The imposition of President Trump's tariffs on global imports is driving the expansion, making building in the US paramount.
Samuelsson stated regarding expanding its US manufacturing footprint: 'The tariffs have accelerated this process, it would be fair to admit that.'
Cars imported from Sweden and the EU will eventually face 15% tariffs, an improvement from the current 27.5% rate, but the deal is currently tentative.
Importing vehicles from China plants, where the entry-level EX30 EV is produced, faces 100% tariffs, making it a non-starter.
Volvo moved production of its EX30 from China to Ghent in response to tariff pressures.
Polestar stopped selling its China-made Polestar 2 sedan in the US earlier this year.
source_title: Volvo stock jumps on expanded US production, new hybrid model to counter tariffs

facts: O’Steen Automotive Group acquired two dealerships in Athens, Georgia from Smith Automotive Group on February 23.
The acquisition marks O’Steen’s first venture into the Acura brand alongside Volvo.
The dealerships have been rebranded as O’Steen Acura Athens and O’Steen Volvo Cars Athens, linked by a shared service drive in the vibrant college town known for the University of Georgia.
This transaction broadens O’Steen’s presence in the Southeastern U.S. market and adds Acura to its portfolio.
AB Volvo appointed Stuart Potter as managing director of Volvo Group Southern Africa.

Investors view Potter's appointment as a sign of renewed emphasis on strengthening customer relationships amid the automotive industry's transition toward electric vehicles and sustainable transport solutions.
AB Volvo’s stock has experienced short-term volatility but maintains robust long-term performance supported by strong shareholder returns over one- and three-year periods.
AB Volvo is expanding its lineup of electric trucks and buses to meet growing demand for zero-emission transport in Europe and North America.
The company utilizes a solid order book and disciplined pricing approach to manage inflationary pressures effectively.
Analysts suggest AB Volvo’s current share price does not fully reflect its growth potential, with fair value estimates considerably higher than current trading levels.
Challenges facing the company include slower development of EV charging infrastructure and rising battery costs.

Volvo Construction Equipment (Volvo CE) is undertaking a significant renewal focused on enhancing mining operations to improve technology adoption, workforce productivity, and supply chain efficiency.

The company is positioning itself for growth through strategic acquisitions, leadership upgrades, and investment in sustainable technologies while addressing operational challenges across various regions and sectors.
source_title: Volvo Strengthens US Presence with Georgia Dealership Acquisition - Msymi.com

facts: Volvo Group North America represents about 29% of total 2025 sales for the Volvo Group.
The company employs more than 19,100 people in North America.
The company operates 16 manufacturing and remanufacturing facilities in the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
The company is driving the transition to sustainable and safe transport, mobility, and infrastructure solutions in North America.
The company is making the journey to become a net-zero society.
Volvo Group has made significant strides in North America.
The company operates the largest R&D site for Volvo Group Trucks.
Its products and services help put food on tables, carry people to work or school, build roads, keep cities clean, and meet growing global demands.
Volvo Cars is no longer a part of Volvo Group.
Volvo Group North America Headquarters is located at 7900 National Service Road, Greensboro, NC 27409.

Nova Bus Headquarters is located at 1000, Industriel Blvd., St-Eustache, QC J7R 5A5, Canada.
Prevost Bus Headquarters is located at 35 Gagnon Boulevard, Sainte-Claire, Quebec G0R 2V0.
The United States Office is located at 8003 Piedmont Triad Parkway, Greensboro, NC 27409.
