facts: Wabash National plans to relocate its entire refrigerated trailer operation to its plant in Little Falls.
The expansion plan includes the addition of 216 jobs in Little Falls.
The plan includes a $23 million investment in equipment for the facility.
The plan includes another $12 million in salaries by the end of 2023.
In April, the city of Little Falls and Morrison County approved a five-year extension to the 10-year tax rebate Wabash received when it first moved to the area in 2017.
The existing tax rebate amounts are $50,000 per year from the city and $40,000 per year from the county.
The previous rebate was contingent upon the addition of 140 new jobs and a $14 million investment to the facility.
Wabash is one of 14 plants owned by the Indiana-based company throughout the U.S.
Wabash initially considered Georgia for Phase III of its expansion but chose Little Falls due to strong community relationships.
Wabash requested an additional five years of tax rebates, extending them until 2037 for a total of 20 years.
By the end of next year (2023), Wabash hopes to be manufacturing 3,000 refrigerated trailers in Little Falls.
Wabash also plans to manufacture 2,000 smaller trailers in Little Falls by the end of next year.

The Little Falls Economic Development Authority (EDA) and Morrison County Rural Development Financing Authority (RDFA) voted unanimously to accept Wabash’s proposal and extend the tax rebates through 2037.
The rebate amounts from each group were based on taxes paid on the facility by Larsen Boats during its last year in the facility.
Little Falls City Administrator Jon Radermacher stated that after 20 years, Wabash will ultimately receive a $1 million subsidy from the city.
The EDA levy has the capacity to handle the extension, and it is not a new tax or property tax increase.
Tax increment financing was not possible because there wouldn't be enough new taxable increment developed by the expansion.
Under the agreement structure, Wabash will pay its taxes each year and then get reimbursed $50,000 from the city and $40,000 from the county.
The reimbursement amounts are capped; if taxable property value climbs higher, the entities retain the remainder prior to the agreement's sunset.
Wabash will receive $450,000 from a Minnesota Investment Fund (MIF) grant tied to job creation numbers.
The Little Falls City Administrator stated the city is closing in on $2.5 million in its EDA fund between local, state, and federal dollars.
Morrison County Board of Commissioners heard a presentation from Community Development of Morrison County Executive Director Carol Anderson on the proposal.
Wabash acquired the facility in 2017 after Larsen closed it.
Wabash replaced much of the equipment in the facility from when it was owned by Larsen that it thought it would be able to use.
The workforce and existing equipment were cited as key reasons for Wabash's expansion plans.
Wabash is discontinuing refrigerated trailer production in Lafayette, Indiana, and moving all refrigerated trailer manufacturing to Little Falls.
No jobs will be lost at the Lafayette, Indiana plant; they will switch to making dry trailers instead of refrigerated ones.
The chassis for the trucks manufactured in Little Falls are still being made in Indiana, with only about four shipped to Little Falls at a time.
Wabash's new vacuum system technology helps achieve a 25% energy savings in its state-of-the-art refrigerated trailers, up from an initial goal of 3%.
The new Little Falls trailers are completely sealed and lighter than traditional trailers, avoiding the 500-700 extra pounds of water weight gained over six years by regular semi-trailers.
Wabash plans to meet with local school superintendents and Central Lakes College representatives to customize training and promote programs for specialized jobs.
The goal of the training initiatives is to keep local students in the area and prepare them for work at Wabash.
The expansion could create demand for other manufacturers to relocate to the area, particularly suppliers like chassis makers.
Morrison County Commissioner Mike Wilson expressed enthusiasm about potential equipment support from Wabash for schools.
Morrison County Board Chair Greg Blaine emphasized the importance of organizational culture and workforce sustainability for the company's future success.
Local government entities view maintaining the workforce as a shared responsibility to ensure Wabash's success in Little Falls.
Community Development of Morrison County Executive Director Carol Anderson noted that Wabash's arrival prevented the facility from sitting empty after Larsen's layoff.
source_title: Wabash to move entire refrigerated division to Little Falls | News | hometownsource.com

facts: Developed Molded Structure Composite (MSC) technology as a revolutionary new material with lighter weight and improved thermal properties for the refrigerated van market.
In 2020, worked with a major grocery company that piloted an MSC truck body designed specifically for home delivery of groceries.
Positions composite expertise as a competitive differentiator within the electric chassis space.
Lighter weight composite materials for truck bodies and trailers directly impact vehicle range and payload in the EV space.
Completed an agreement to manufacture Gruau refrigerated inserts for the Ford Transit within the United States.
Gruau inserts provide 30% to 50% thermal efficiency compared to standard refrigerated body construction, reducing spoilage and improving food safety.
Developing Tech Net technology to reduce carbon impact from product use and create opportunities to reduce natural resource consumption in manufacturing processes.
Overall backlog ended Q4 2020 at approximately $1.5 billion, up sequentially by approximately $500 million from the end of Q3.
Successfully hired approximately 600 new employees across the business during Q4 2020.
Q4 2020 hiring activity added about 15% to the total workforce.
Expects to add another 900 employees during the first half of 2021.
Total hiring from September 30 to March 31 expected to be roughly 1,500 hourly employees.
Initiated full year 2021 revenue outlook at just under $2 billion ($1.9 billion to $2 billion).
Expects 2021 earnings per share of approximately $0.75 at the midpoint.
Q4 2020 consolidated revenue was $404 million with consolidated new trailer shipments of approximately 10,600 units.
Q4 2020 operating income was $10 million with an operating margin of 2.5%.
Commercial Trailer Products (CTP) segment generated $283 million in revenue and $23.3 million non-GAAP adjusted operating income in Q4 2020.
Average selling price for new trailers within CTP was about $27,000 in Q4 2020.
Diversified Products Group (DPG) generated $75 million of revenue and $3.3 million non-GAAP adjusted operating income in Q4 2020.
Sold a niche Tank Trailer business at the end of Q4 responsible for approximately $20 million during 2020, which manufactured aluminum tank trailers geared toward the Pacific Northwest.
Final Mile Products (FMP) generated $52 million of revenue with an operating loss of $4.5 million in Q4 2020.
FMP EBITDA was a loss of only $600,000 in Q4 2020.
Expects FMP to begin showing positive EBITDA in the first half of 2021 and be solidly EBITDA profitable full year 2021.
Generated $104 million of free cash flow during 2020.
Closed the sale of its Columbus, Ohio branch location during 2020.
Closed the sale of the Beall brand of Tank Trailers during 2020.
Utilized $11.2 million to pay down debt, $8.7 million to repurchase shares, and invested $6.4 million in capital projects during Q4 2020.
Expects 2021 capital spending to rebound between $35 million and $40 million.
Long-term target is achieving a consolidated operating margin of 8% over the next two to three years.
CTP expected to exit 2021 at near optimal levels of margin performance going into 2022, while FMP will continue stepping up margins in 2022 and 2023.
Key executives on the call include Brent L. Yeagy (President and CEO), Mike Pettit (CFO), and Ryan Reed (Director of Investor Relations).

facts: Industry veteran Mike Reaves returns to Wheelabrator to head up the North American EMP (“Equipment Modernization Program”) team as a technical specialist.
Mike devoted nearly 20 years of his career to Wheelabrator, joining in 1997 and initially designing new equipment.
He subsequently worked as engineering manager and across engineering services, quality assurance, and new product development.

In his ultimate role as director of aftermarket engineering, he was part of the team behind the multi-patented EZEFIT® blast wheel (living on in today’s EZX® technology) and built out the equipment upgrade offering.
He completed an MBA from Columbus State University, which prompted his move in 2017 into a chief operating officer role outside the world of shot blasting.
Almost ten years later, he rejoins to shape the EMP offering for a new era – faster, smarter, more digital.
Marcus Montie, Senior Vice President of Norican North America, comments that if machines last decades, innovation can’t wait until the next equipment purchase.

Marcus Montie notes Mike saw this early on and built products and services around it, pushing the limits of what you can get out of existing machines and being part of some of the most successful aftermarket developments of the past decades.

Marcus Montie adds that Mike rejoins at a time when digital tools and innovative wear parts enable continuous injection of new technology on existing shot blast equipment, and will ensure customers get maximum performance and value from installed machines.
source_title: Mike Reaves returns to Wheelabrator | Wheelabrator

facts: WIKA Instrument Co. has a new 110,000 square foot manufacturing facility located just outside of Atlanta in Lawrenceville, Georgia.
The Lawrenceville facility features state-of-the-art equipment and manufacturing techniques.
The company uses the latest technology to test and monitor each production process every step of the way to ensure products meet the highest quality standards.
WIKA's parent company is WIKA Alexander Wiegand GmbH & Co., located near Frankfurt, Germany.
The name WIKA is taken from the first two letters of the founders of the company, Alexander Wiegand and Philipp Kachel.
In 1996, WIKA will celebrate 50 years in Europe and 30 years in the United States.
WIKA employs over 2500 people worldwide.
WIKA has additional manufacturing plants in Germany, Brazil, South Africa, and Switzerland.
WIKA is currently producing over 24 million instruments per year.
WIKA manufactures pressure gauges, chemical seals, transmitters, and thermometers for customers in more than 100 countries worldwide.

facts: WIKA is expanding its partnership with AWC to bring WIKA precision products and services to 12 states in the southeastern US with local AWC support.
WIKA's comprehensive evaluation affirms that AWC’s expanded geographic footprint will deliver unparalleled advantages to both existing and prospective clientele.
The collaboration will not lead to any alterations in the manufacturing or design of the WIKA product portfolio, assuring steadfast maintenance of product quality and uninterrupted service.
WIKA and AWC have maintained an enduring partnership spanning nearly a decade.
Todd Gardner serves as the WIKA USA President.
Todd Gardner stated that the expanded geographic partnership with AWC marks a significant milestone reinforcing the strength of their partner relationship.
Todd Gardner noted the strategic expansion bolsters commitment to serving partners effectively and exemplifies synergy fostering innovation and excellence.
The expanded partnership guarantees a smooth and streamlined transition for customers, ensuring uninterrupted access to products and services.
WIKA Group employs 11,200 people globally.
WIKA is a global market leader in pressure and temperature measurement, setting standards in level, force, flow measurement, and calibration technology.
WIKA offers high-precision products, solutions, and services under the “Smart in sensing” initiative.
AWC is an Automation and Controls Technology company with 600+ employee-owners.
AWC focuses on two main areas: Process Technology & Instrumentation and Industrial Automation.
AWC operates as an authorized local sales, support, and service partner to leading manufacturing partners.
AWC provides a full suite of products, services, and integrated systems to optimize US Manufacturing and Utility operations.
AWC was founded in 1965.
AWC operates 32 full-service locations across the U.S., spanning from Denver, CO to Tampa, FL.
AWC maintains a heavy concentration of locations along the Gulf Coast of TX and LA.
Trey Castleberry serves as the AWC Marketing Director.

facts: WIKA USA partnered with Georgia Power to install six Level 2 electric vehicle chargers at its Lawrenceville location.
The initiative supports energy diversity and environmental sustainability efforts.
Georgia Power launched the Electric Transportation Make Ready Program to facilitate electric charging infrastructure installation at public and private locations across the state.
WIKA USA senior managers, including Bobby Herring, Director of Facilities for WIKA Americas, applied to participate in the Make Ready program.
Under the program agreement, Georgia Power installs and maintains all necessary e-charging infrastructure—including panels, switchgear, wiring, conduits, and disconnects—at no cost to WIKA USA.
WIKA USA was responsible for purchasing the charging stations, allocating parking lot space, and installing protective wheel stops.
Bobby Herring described the project as an easy decision and a smart investment that promotes sustainable transportation through low/zero emissions, energy efficiency, and affordable transport modes.
The charger installation was completed in May 2022.
The WIKA USA headquarters facility currently operates six Level 2 charging stations with the capacity to expand to a total of 10 stations.
Charging service is provided completely free of charge to employees and visitors.
Within the first four months of operation, users consumed almost 3,000 kW of electricity, which is equivalent to approximately 90 gallons of gasoline.
Employee feedback regarding the on-site charging convenience has been positive.
Bobby Herring expressed hope that the facility's chargers will encourage others to purchase electric vehicles or plug-in hybrids.
source_title: EV Charging Stations Pave Way for Clean Transport - WIKA blog

facts: WIKA USA, a leading manufacturer of pressure and temperature measurement instrumentation, is located in Lawrenceville, GA.
WIKA USA launched the MH-4 CAN, a cost-effective and technically capable OEM CAN pressure sensor for mobile working machines.
The MH-4 CAN features advanced electronics, robust sensor design, and comprehensive validation testing to offer performance stability and operational reliability throughout its life cycle.

Chad Reinhard, Senior Product Manager at WIKA USA, stated that the MH-4 CAN is a game-changer for the mobile working machine industry and allows customers to customize and adapt the sensor to specific needs.
The MH-4 and MH-4 CAN are ideal OEM pressure sensors designed for high-volume production of any kind of mobile working machinery.
WIKA has over 75 years of experience advancing instrumentation for pressure, temperature, level, and flow measurement while embracing digitalization and decarbonization.
WIKA operates manufacturing locations around the globe to support flexibility and meet high delivery demands.

facts: Electric vehicle manufacturers are building large facilities in Georgia.
Reshoring production has helped make supply chains more secure.
Reshoring is especially important to industries favored by recent shifts in U.S. industrial policies.
More emphasis on near sourcing and friend sourcing will help reduce supply chain stress and improve the prospects for the growth of Georgia’s large transportation and logistics industry.
A substantial increase in U.S. vehicle sales is expected, which bodes well for Georgia’s vehicle and vehicle parts manufacturing industries.
Georgia’s economic developers broke records for the 3rd year in a row, landing 426 projects in fiscal year 2023 compared to the record-setting 358 projects announced in 2022.
Site selection professionals ranked Georgia as the No. 1 state for business for the 10th consecutive year in 2023.

facts: Hyundai's new factory in Georgia will roll out the Ioniq 5 as its first vehicle this autumn.
Starting in October, Hyundai will begin producing the US-made Ioniq 5 at its new plant in Georgia.
According to Hyundai CEO Jose Muñoz, the US-made Ioniq 5 is expected to qualify for the full $7,500 federal EV tax credit.
The South Korean-made Ioniq 5 does not currently qualify for this credit except through a leasing loophole.
Hyundai will not start making batteries at the Georgia plant until about a year after vehicle production commences in October.
In the interim, batteries for the Ioniq 5 will be sourced from a Hungarian factory operated by SK On, which is Hyundai's partner for its Georgia battery production.
Hyundai announced in October of last year that its cars would gain access to Tesla Superchargers by the fourth quarter of 2024.
All new or refreshed Hyundai EVs will come equipped with NACS charging ports starting in the fourth quarter of 2024.
The fourth quarter spans from October to December, leaving room for some of the first US-made Ioniq 5s to potentially be equipped with Combined Charging System (CCS) ports instead.
Hyundai has not explicitly confirmed whether the initial models off the line will feature NACS ports, adding a layer of uncertainty for early buyers.
Hyundai representative Christopher Paukert reiterated the company's previous announcement but did not provide a definite answer on the charging port issue.
Manufacturing the Ioniq 5 in the US aligns with broader industry trends and regulatory incentives aimed at encouraging domestic EV production.
The ability to offer the full $7,500 federal tax credit makes the Ioniq 5 more attractive to US consumers, potentially driving higher sales volumes.
Establishing a manufacturing presence in the US allows Hyundai to better navigate supply chain challenges and reduce dependence on international logistics.
Local battery production will strengthen Hyundai's position by enhancing its supply chain resilience and reducing costs as the plant ramps up.
source_title: Hyundai to roll out US-made Ioniq 5 from Georgia plant, securing full EV tax credit - Autonews News

facts: Woodbridge Foam Corp. is an automotive seating and interior design company located in Woodbridge, Ont.
Members of Unifor Local 112 ratified a new three-year contract with Woodbridge Foam Corp.
The contract eliminates a two-tier wage system that had been in place for 13 years.
There are 255 members covered by the contract, consisting of production workers and Skilled Trades personnel.
The agreement provides significant wage increases for the membership.
The agreement includes increased vacation allocation for members.
The agreement establishes a new Defined Benefit Pension Plan.
The contract is retroactive from June 1, 2023.
The contract expires on May 31, 2026.
Local 112 President John Turner stated that negotiating the agreement was a long and challenging process but felt supported by member solidarity and union backing.
Turner noted the contract ends a 13-year struggle against the two-tier system and eliminates huge discrepancies in wages and benefits for members doing the same work.
Turner emphasized that the new contract provides wage equality and a clear path to meaningful retirement for all members.
source_title: Woodbridge Foam ratifies new deal, eliminating two-tier system | Unifor

facts: Woodbridge provides seating system solutions that support structure, optimize comfort, and achieve a refined aesthetic.
Woodbridge foam can handle impact and be used as a lightweight alternative for under-vehicle shields and bumper assemblies.

Woodbridge foam solutions are applicable to many interior parts including overhead, door bolsters, door armrests, console armrests, cargo systems, load floor systems, parcel shelves, energy-absorbing foams, and technical foams.

Woodbridge works with a number of bedding and furniture science partners, including universities and government agencies, to co-develop a wide range of breathable and hypo-allergenic polyurethane foam.
Woodbridge has established strategic partnerships that allow it to deliver fully-integrated systems and solutions anytime, anywhere.
Woodbridge's manufacturing and engineering expertise enables it to address challenges, make changes, and run production lines more efficiently than other suppliers.

facts: UAW-represented Woodbridge Corp – Atlanta Foam workers of Local 472 walked out on strike early Wednesday morning on October 30 in Lithonia, Ga.
Management has yet to make a significant offer on three critical issues for workers: wages, benefits, and seniority.
Workers at Woodbridge produce seating and dashboard components for Yamaha and Nissan.
Workers have been working under an expired contract since September 30.
On August 22, workers voted 98% in favor of authorizing a strike.
UAW Local 472 President Rachel Johnson stated she is on strike for better wages, healthcare, and respect for the work she does.

UAW Region 8 Director Tim Smith stated that despite numerous meetings and bargaining sessions, management has yet to make a significant offer on three critical issues for workers: wages, benefits, and seniority, and that the company has no intention of reaching a fair and equitable agreement.
Woodbridge workers are the latest UAW members to Stand Up during contract negotiations following the historic Stand Up strike that led autoworkers at the Big Three to win record raises and benefits.
source_title: UAW Members at Woodbridge Corp Walk Out on Strike - UAW | United Automobile, Aerospace and Agricultural Implement Workers of America

facts: Woodbridge has corporate headquarters in Mississauga, Ontario, Canada, and automotive headquarters in Troy, Michigan, USA.
The company employs more than 7,000 teammates across over 40 locations in eight countries.
Woodbridge offers material technologies for automotive, commercial, recreational, packaging, healthcare, and building product applications.
The company provides prototyping, consulting, technical support, chemical R&D, and accredited laboratory testing services.

Woodbridge supports OEMs with performance data, equipment monitoring, virtual capabilities, and computer modeling to analyze and optimize production for Manufacturing 4.0 and digitization integration.
For driverless vehicles, Woodbridge provides mass reduction solutions including thinner seating profiles, condensed packaging, and enhanced configurability for adjustable or stowable parts.
The company helps OEMs create quieter cabins through acoustic solutions that reduce noise, vibration, and harshness.
To address the growing electrified vehicle sector, Woodbridge offers lightweighting solutions with structural integrity to offset electric battery weight.
Woodbridge's seating solutions for electrified vehicles insulate and dissipate heat to minimize power usage and extend battery charge.
For ride-hailing and public transit applications, Woodbridge delivers seating solutions designed for increased passenger volume and frequency, offering short-term comfort and long-term durability.
The company utilizes hypoallergenic and antimicrobial materials in its seating solutions to improve cabin air quality by lowering volatile organic compound (VOC) emissions.
Woodbridge has developed high-performance polyurethane spray foam thermal-insulating solutions that deliver industry-leading temperature transfer resistance for construction insulation.
The company provides complete seating systems and assemblies engineered for commercial, industrial, powersports, and personal-use transportation.
PolyLink Solutions, a subsidiary of Woodbridge, thermoforms and manages molded particle foam projects primarily supplying the automotive, pharmaceutical, and construction markets.
PolyLink's engineered products include one-way protective packaging, insulated containers, returnable dunnage and trays, handheld totes, and plastic pallets.
Woodbridge leverages extensive research and development in physical property testing, lab testing, and technical services.
The company offers healthcare products designed to promote proper alignment, healing, circulation, and rehabilitation.
Woodbridge partners with bedding and furniture science organizations, including universities and government agencies, to co-develop breathable and hypoallergenic polyurethane foam.
Woodbridge delivers custom machinery, tooling, and chemical formulations to optimize assembly and product performance.
The company utilizes lean processes to develop, iterate, and deliver solutions with speed and flexibility.
Woodbridge maintains renowned R&D labs and extensive chemistry knowledge demonstrated through industry expertise in comfort.

facts: The global automotive foam market is projected to grow from USD 52.3 billion in 2026 to USD 72.1 billion by 2036 at a CAGR of 3.3%.
Polyurethane foam will dominate the market with a 42.0% share in 2026.
The interior seating application segment leads with a 38.0% market share in 2026.
Passenger cars represent the dominant vehicle type, capturing approximately 63.0% of total market share in 2026.
The OEM segment is the dominant end-use category, capturing approximately 71.0% of total market share in 2026.
Electric vehicle platforms are increasing the need for lightweight and advanced foam solutions to enhance interior comfort and reduce cabin noise.
The US automotive foam market expands at a 3.5% CAGR through 2036, driven by strong electric vehicle programs and lightweighting priorities.
Production centers in Michigan, Texas, Tennessee, and Kentucky are adopting advanced foam formulations to improve efficiency and comfort, particularly in EV platforms.
Established tier-one supplier networks in the US enable rapid material deployment aligned with OEM specifications.
Collaboration between automotive engineers and material developers in the US supports innovation in performance and sustainability, including recycled and low-emission foam systems.

Key companies profiled in the market include BASF SE, Dow Inc., Huntsman Corporation, Recticel NV, Woodbridge Foam Corporation, Armacell, JSP Corporation, Rogers Corporation, Saint-Gobain, and Covestro AG.

Market leaders such as BASF SE, Dow Inc., and Huntsman Corporation benefit from broad automotive foam portfolios and global manufacturing footprints, supporting expansion into electric vehicle applications.
Challenger companies including Recticel NV, Woodbridge Foam Corporation, and Covestro AG compete through specialized product offerings in major automotive manufacturing hubs.
Specialty suppliers such as Armacell, JSP Corporation, and Rogers Corporation focus on niche applications like acoustic performance and advanced polymer formulations.
Saint-Gobain maintains competitiveness through diversified materials expertise and integrated automotive solutions.
Rising global vehicle production and expanding manufacturing capacity drive demand for interior comfort solutions in the automotive foam market.
Growing electric vehicle adoption and interior design innovation are increasing foam usage for seating, acoustic insulation, and vibration control.
Market restraints include volatile petrochemical raw material prices for isocyanates and polyols, which challenge stable pricing and production planning.
Environmental regulations related to VOC emissions and end-of-life recycling add compliance costs and may require product reformulation.
Competition from alternative materials such as advanced textiles and lightweight composites pressures foam adoption.
Key trends include growing adoption of bio-based foam formulations in developed markets driven by sustainability mandates.
Manufacturers are integrating automated dispensing, real-time density control, and quality monitoring systems to reduce waste and improve consistency.
source_title: Automotive Foam Market | Global Market Analysis Report - 2036

facts: In 2008, Proseat (joint venture between Recticel S.A. (51%) and the Company (49%)) has signed an agreement for the takeover of all the shares of Indepol, a Spanish moulded foam manufacturer located near Barcelona.
In January 2005, Proseat acquired a 25% interest in Indepol.
By purchasing the remaining 75% of the shares, Proseat has now increased its stake in Indepol from 25% to 100%.
Indepol manufactures moulded foam seat components for the automotive industry (Seat, GM and Ford) and has an annual turnover of EUR 35 million and a workforce of 400.

In Oct. 2008, the Company announced the introduction of BioFoam seat cushioning in several Fiat vehicles assembled in Brazil, including the Palio, Strada, Uno, Siena, Idea and Punto, and also in Iveco trucks.
BioFoam is plant-based polyurethane foam solution for automotive applications.

In Nov. 2007, the Company and Intier Automotive Seating partnered to introduce BioFoam seat cushions in the Ford Escape launching in 2008, with the urethane foam cushions being supplied by the Company and the final seat assembly being performed by Intier.
In Nov. 2007, the Company and Inoac Corp., a polyurethane foam supplier, announced to establish four joint venture companies in North America.
Inoac will invest in four companies of Woodbridge in North America as early as Dec. 31, 2007 to establish fifty-fifty joint ventures.

While Inoac will make 50% investments in the Company's three companies in Texas and one in Mexico, the Group company will make a 50% investment in Kyushu Inoac Co., Ltd., Yukuhashi City, Fukuoka Prefecture.

In May 2007, Johnson Controls joined with Proseat (a joint venture between Recticel and the Company) to set up a new manufacturing joint venture for the production of molded foam seating cushions in Slovakia and Poland.
The two plants are located in Lucenec, Slovakia and Zory, Poland.
Johnson Controls will hold a majority 65% stake in the joint venture with Proseat holding the remaining 35%.
In Slovakia, the joint venture will use the existing manufacturing capacity at the Johnson Controls plant in Lucenec.
The new plant in Poland will be operational in November 2007.
In Sep. 2006, the Company and Recticel (Belgium) decided to extend their cooperation further by integrating Woodbrige's United Kingdom molded foam activities in Proseat.
The present joint venture between RecTicel (70%) and the Company (30%) - Proseat is responsible for the production and marketing of seat cushions in molded foam for the European automotive industry.
Proseat is a market leader in Europe with 6 production plants distributed over 4 countries ( Belgium, Germany, France and the Czech Republic).
In May 2006, a new joint venture in India was formed.
The venture is called Sheela Woodbridge Urethanes Pvt.Ltd. and will pursue business opportunities for molded polyurethane foam and foam-in-place products in the Indian market.
In Sep. 2005, the Company announced the formation of a new joint venture in China with GSK Corp.
Wuhan Woodbridge GSK Form Co., Ltd. will start production of formed seat for Honda, Peugeot, Nissan and Jaing Ling.
In May 2006, the Company established a new commercial office in Tokyo, Japan.
The establishment of this office supports its growing activities with Japanese based OEM's.
In Jan. 2009, the Company has announced a new environmental-friendly, low mass automotive foam GreenLite.
GreenLite applications include seat frames, seat cushions, and headliners.
In Jan. 2008, the the Company announced the most extensive interior product line offering the industry's highest levels of Green polyurethane foams, BioFoam.

This earth-friendly solution for a full line-up of automotive interior applications includes; seat cushions, seat fabric composites, head restraints, arm rests, occupant protection components, trim cover laminates and overhead systems.
Over the next 3 years Woodbridge targets BioFoam's Green Polyol inclusion to reach; 30% for overhead components, occupant protection foams, seat cushions, and the 50% mark for head restraints.
Several vehicles launching in 2008 will feature BioFoam products.
In cooperation with Magna, the recently announced 2009 Ford Escape is one of the latest examples.
source_title: Woodbridge Foam Corporation Business Report up until FY ended Dec. 31, 2009 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: Woodbridge Group manufactures Strata-Plush™ coverstock headliner foams designed for wet and dry headliner forming and various soft-trim applications.
Strata-Plush products are available in flat and round blocks and polyether and polyester foam grades.
The foaming processes are finely tuned for density, firmness, cell size, cell structure, and airflow to provide outstanding performance.
Applications for the headliner foams include headliners, sun visors, door trim, and interior systems.
Products offer a 50” (1.27M) diameter round block option.
Products provide 300+ linear yard rolls without foam join seams.
Formulations deliver superior product consistency for cell structure and visual appearance.
Foams are formulated for crisp definition, superior craftsmanship, and designed to recover post forming.
High firmness and crushable options are available.
Thin-gauge thickness capability is 1.65mm.
The foams have excellent acoustical properties and a best-in-class footprint for customer value.
Typical density ranges from 1.6-2.2 lb./ft3 or 25.5-35.25 kg/m3.
Firmness range is 40-100 (25% IFD lb./50 in2).
Thicknesses range from 065-0.275 inches or 1.65-7mm.
Chemistry consists of polyether and polyester open-cell polyurethane.
Pour types include round block and flat block.
Roll fabrication methods include looping and peeling.
Lamination options include PE film, PU film, adhesive films and webs, textile scrims, and non-woven scrims.
Other key options include a variety of colors, FR/non-FR, acoustics, airflow, and low emission (VOCs and odor).
Strata-Plush headliner foams are designed to meet most OEM specifications and requirements.
Foams meet Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standard FMVSS302.
Foams meet General Motors specifications GMW15058, GM6291M, GM6083, and GM2101M.
Foams meet Ford specifications WSS-99P48, WSS-M2D243, WSS-M2D491, and ESB-M4D113.
Foams meet FCA specifications MSAY309 and CS-13398.
Foams meet Toyota Motor Company specification TSM6712G.
Foams meet Nissan specification NESM8047.
Foams meet Tesla specification TM-4001.
Foams meet Honda specification HES C209.
Foams meet Hyundai Kia specification MS-300-34.
Foams meet Daimler specifications DBL5450, DBL5454, and DBL5438.
Foams meet Volkswagen specification VW50180.
Product variants include RB, FB, HD, LV, LF, and SF, available in Ether or Ester chemistry with standard, low, or high definition variations.
source_title: Coverstock Headliner Foam | Woodbridge

facts: Located in Lithonia, Ga., UAW-represented Woodbridge Corp – Atlanta Foam workers of Local 472 walked out on strike early Wednesday morning on October 30.

UAW Region 8 Director Tim Smith stated that despite numerous meetings and bargaining sessions, management has yet to make a significant offer on three critical issues for workers: wages, benefits, and seniority.
Tim Smith added that the company has no intention of reaching a fair and equitable agreement, leaving workers no choice but to strike.
Workers at Woodbridge produce seating and dashboard components for Yamaha and Nissan.
The workforce has been working under an expired contract since September 30.
On August 22, workers voted 98% in favor of authorizing a strike.
UAW Local 472 President Rachel Johnson stated, “We’re standing together to demand what we’re owed,” and noted she is on strike for better wages, healthcare, and respect for the work she does.
Woodbridge workers are the latest UAW members to Stand Up during contract negotiations following the historic Stand Up strike that led autoworkers at the Big Three to win record raises and benefits.
source_title: Woodbridge Corp. Archives - UAW | United Automobile, Aerospace and Agricultural Implement Workers of America

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Woory Industrial Co., Ltd. will establish a new manufacturing facility in Dublin, Georgia.
The facility will create more than 130 new jobs and an estimated $18 million in investment in Dublin-Laurens County.
Governor Brian Kemp stated the company is helping Georgia become the 'e-mobility capital of the nation' and highlighted historic levels of new jobs and investment brought by the emerging EV industry.
Woory Industrial Company was established in 1989 and specializes in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning components and systems for the global automotive industry.
The company is concentrating on the development of new products for electric vehicles (EV) and hydrogen-fueled cars.

Jungwoo Kim, CEO of Woory Industrial Company, stated the U.S. EV market is the key market among all automotive markets and that Georgia will be a good opportunity for Woory to take another step forward as part of the vanguard of EV development.
Woory's new facility is located at 404 Kellam Road in Dublin and will be the company’s first U.S. manufacturing location.
The facility will produce electrical heaters, control units, and actuators for EV and internal combustion carmakers in North America.
Operations are expected to begin in November 2023.
Unlike internal combustion engine vehicles, electric vehicles do not have a heating source, so heaters are required for cabin heating and maintaining battery temperature.
Woory's pioneering development of electric heaters over the last 10 years has made it a leader in this growing market segment.
Woory will be hiring personnel from all fields necessary for an automotive parts manufacturing plant.
Dublin Mayor Joshua Kight stated that Woory’s commitment to place an existing manufacturing building back into service helps promote infill development and creates new career paths for the community.

Dr. Fred Williams, Chair of the Board of Directors of the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, noted that Dublin and Laurens County have a rapidly growing automotive sector due to their geographic location on Interstate 16 and access to a skilled workforce.
The partnership with area education systems and training providers will ensure training programs are in place to fill Woory's talent pipeline for years to come.

Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this project in partnership with the Dublin-Laurens County Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Georgia has invested for decades to match competitive projects with communities and sites prepared for development, and congratulated Dublin-Laurens County on bringing e-mobility jobs to middle Georgia.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia's pursuit of job creation opportunities along the EV supply chain has resulted in over $22.7 billion in investment and the creation of more than 28,400 jobs.

Woory began operating a small factory producing fuel senders in Suwon, South Korea almost 35 years ago and has grown into an international enterprise with production plants around the world employing over 1,300 people.

Woory's business is focused on the development and production of HVAC auto components for all types of vehicles, including electric, internal combustion engine, eco-friendly, and hydrogen-fueled cars.
Woory has been nominated as a supplier for Tesla, Rivian, Lucid, and Canoo.
Woory is currently supplying the HV heater system used to heat the cabins of EVs.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Woory to Open EV Parts Manufacturing, R&D Facility in Laurens County - EIN Presswire

facts: Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal visited Carrollton Friday for the grand opening at the Yachiyo auto parts manufacturing plant.
The company made a $30 million investment to construct the new facility.
The project will create more than 200 new jobs.
Yachiyo is a supplier for Honda automotive vehicles.
The new manufacturing facility is located in the Carrollton Industrial Park, off Columbia Drive.
The plant will manufacture plastic gas tanks and sun roofs for Honda automobiles.
The company hired its first 60 employees for the facility.
Production is expected to begin on Aug. 25.
Hiroshi Sasamoto serves as president of Yachiyo Industry Japan.
Tetsuya Kitamura serves as president of Yachiyo of America Inc.
Tomohisa Kobori serves as president of Yachiyo Manufacturing of America.
Daniel Jackson is the president of the Carroll County Chamber of Commerce.
Mike Oatridge is the president of Honda Manufacturing of Alabama.
Japan is the largest international investor in Georgia in terms of dollar value.
There are approximately 400 Japanese organizations and companies located in the state of Georgia.
These Japanese companies employ 25,000 individuals in Georgia.
The Georgia General Assembly removed sales tax on energy used in manufacturing to attract foreign industry investment.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that these policies resulted in an explosion of manufacturing jobs coming to Georgia.
Georgia is now designated as the number one state in the country to do business.
Chris Carr is the commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
The Quick Start program and Georgia Department of Labor were involved in facilitating the project.
Rob Davyduck is the manager of Yachiyo's Carrollton plant.
Yachiyo was founded in 1947 in Japan as a paint company.
The company became an auto parts supplier in 1953.
Robyn Miller is the senior manager for the Carrollton plant.
The initial 60 employees were hired from 908 applicants who attended a job fair earlier this year.
Recruitment efforts for Yachiyo to Carrollton began in October 2012 when the company's first team visited.
Brian Gill, a Carroll County Chamber of Commerce vice president, handled overseas recruiting efforts.
Donna Lackey, a Carroll County Chamber of Commerce vice president, provided assistance and offices to help the company get started.
Carrollton Mayor Wayne Garner stated that local officials get along with each other and that city and county government work together well, which is beneficial for the community.
Marty Smith is the chairman of the county Board of Commissioners.
A special ceramic tray created by Carrollton artist Don McWhorter was presented to Yachiyo plant officials during the ceremony.
Local government and company officials dedicated the planting of five trees near the building after the indoor ceremony.
source_title: Governor speaks at Carrollton Yachiyo opening | News | times-georgian.com

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Plant construction for Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America started in 1986 in Newnan, Georgia.
The company began operations in 1988 with a small crew assembling golf cars on a 280-acre campus comprising six plants.
The first unit to roll off the manufacturing line was a golf car in June of 1988.
WaveRunner production was added by January of the following year (1989).
Over the years, production gradually expanded to encompass ATVs and Side-by-Side Recreational Offroad Vehicles.
The facility currently employs over 2000 team members.
Yamaha has produced over four million recreational vehicles across its product lines.
Every WaveRunner, Golf Car, and side-by-side ROV that Yamaha markets is manufactured in Newnan, Georgia.
Most of the world’s Yamaha ATVs are also assembled in Newnan, Georgia.
Original team member Ken Ellerbee started at the plant in January 1988 as an electronics technician working on robotics systems for welding and has since advanced to SMART Construction Manager.
The company maintains a commitment to environmental health and safety, holding ISO 9001 and 14001 certifications.
Yamaha received the Georgia State Natural Leadership Award for Continual Environmental Improvement.
The facility was named Coweta Water Authority Plant of the Year.

Every dollar collected from Yamaha’s extensive recycling program is reinvested into corporate social responsibility initiatives, including scholarships, science education for local students, and disaster response.

Yamaha regularly sponsors entire classes from Title 1 schools to experience a science classroom on the lake through the Chattahoochee Riverkeeper’s floating classroom program for water quality and conservation experiments.
The company is investing in a new SMART Warehouse facility spanning 200,000 square feet to improve manufacturing efficiencies.
The SMART Warehouse utilizes high-speed automation technology to immediately send parts to team members on the production floor.
Yamaha has adopted a Carbon Neutral program aimed at reducing the carbon footprint for its entire campus by 2035.
Progress toward the carbon neutral goal is being driven through the installation of solar panels across the rooftop of Plant 2.
Bob Brown serves as Vice President of finance and operational support at Yamaha and is a Chamber Board member.
source_title: Yamaha Celebrates 35 Years | Yamaha

facts: Yamaha Golf-Car is a major golf car brand committed to workforce development.
Yamaha Golf-Car President Tom McDonald met with Secretary of the Interior David Bernhardt at the Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC) facility in Newnan, Ga.
Mike Chrzanowski, President of Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMCC), and Ben Speciale, President of Yamaha U.S. Marine Business Unit, signed the President’s Pledge to America’s Workers.
The signing reinforces Yamaha’s commitment to workforce development within its U.S. manufacturing facilities.
Tom McDonald stated that jobs created through Yamaha facilities in Georgia make a positive impact on the state’s economy and contribute to the growth of the outdoor industry overall.
The golf industry supported $191.9 billion in total annual activity in 2016.
Golf industry support included 1.9 million jobs and 58.7 billion in wages and benefits.
The U.S. experienced record golf participation with rounds played up 14% in June.
Golf participation increased by 19.7% in July.
Industry experts expect August results to show similar increases when posted next week.
Yamaha will continue to invest in training and best practices necessary to create successful manufacturing career paths within each of its divisions.
Yamaha operates a technician training facility in Kennesaw, Ga. that ensures success for Dealers and Distributors across the country.
The Pledge to America’s Workers is an initiative that stemmed from the National Council for the American Worker, which was established on July 19, 2019.
The council leads a national strategy for training and re-training workers in high demand industries, such as recreational manufacturing.
More than 430 companies have signed the pledge to date.
Signatories are contributing to more than 16 million new training opportunities for American students and workers over the next five years.
source_title: Doing the Work – Golf Car News

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. (YMMC) employs more than 2,000 metro Atlanta residents to design and build recreational vehicles at its Newnan manufacturing facilities.
All of the world’s Yamaha golf cars and Side-by-Sides are manufactured at the facility in Coweta County, Georgia.
Most of the world’s Yamaha WaveRunners and ATVs are manufactured at the facility in Coweta County, Georgia.

In 2025, YMMC transitioned from a traditional open-request format to a structured grant cycle for its Outdoor Adventure Grant Program, aligning resources with Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) goals.

Six inaugural Outdoor Adventure Grants were awarded to the Central Lions Fishing Team, East Coweta High School Bass Fishing Team, Heard County High School Bass Fishing Team, SouthWest Atlanta Youth (SWAY) Mountain Bike Team, Swing Chic Foundation Girls Golf Team, and Whitewater High School Bass Fishing Team.

Bob Brown, President at Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp., stated that the shift to a grant-based cycle reflects the company’s long-term commitment to purposeful giving and that outdoor adventure builds confidence, resilience, and connection.
Spring Outdoor Adventure Grant applications will open on January 15 for local high schools and qualified 501(c)(3) nonprofit organizations.

links.context: Eligibility, focus areas, and application information for the grant program

source_title: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. Announces New Outdoor Adventure Grant Program, Awarding Six Grants to Local Teams | The Citizen

facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation (YMMC), based in Newnan, Georgia, broke ground on a new 'SMART' Warehouse.
The SMART Warehouse is the first-of-its-kind automated facility for Yamaha Motor Company worldwide.
The new warehouse will be 200,000 square feet and utilize state-of-the-art automated storage and retrieval systems.
The facility will reduce response time for fulfilling dealer orders for Yamaha WaveRunners, Golf Cars, ATVs, and Side-by-Sides by more than 50 percent.
Mike Chrzanowski serves as President and CEO of Yamaha Motor Corporation.

The warehouse will improve operational efficiency and material flow velocity to factory assembly lines to meet demand that currently exceeds supply for outdoor recreational products produced at the factory.
SMART is an acronym for Sequenced Material and Reduced Transportation.
The medium parts automated storage and retrieval system will retrieve and put away 240 containers per hour, compared to the current rate of 170 containers per hour.
The small part automated system will handle 2400 containers per hour, compared to the current capability of 550 containers per day.
New systems will enable Yamaha to reduce forklift usage by 50 percent using automatic conveyors and tuggers.
The investment underscores Yamaha's long-term commitment to YMMC employees, the City of Newnan, and Coweta County.
The warehouse will bring higher paid and more skilled jobs into Coweta County.
New job roles will include robotics experts, software developers, and systems engineers.
The SMART Warehouse was designed by SSI SCHAEFER.
The facility will include an observation platform for local school students to learn about manufacturing and automated processes.
Yamaha expects to complete the SMART Warehouse in 2024.
Yamaha Motor Corporation, USA (YMUS) operates corporate offices in California and two in Georgia, with facilities in Wisconsin and Alabama, and factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.

YMUS sells products including motorcycles, scooters, ATVs, side-by-sides, snowmobiles, WaveRunners, boats, outboard motors, outdoor power equipment, power assist bicycles, golf cars, and robotic machines through a nationwide network of distributors and dealers.

Additional U.S.-based subsidiaries include Yamaha Marine Systems Company (YMSC) with divisions Bennett Marine, Kracor Systems, Siren Marine, Skeeter Boats, G3 Boats, and Yamaha Precision Propeller across multiple states.

links.context: Corporate Communications Manager Bob Starr contact

source_title: YAMAHA MOTOR MANUFACTURING CORPORATION BREAKS GROUND ON INNOVATIVE SMART WAREHOUSE IN NEWNAN, GEORGIA - The Golf Wire

facts: After 50 years in California, Yamaha Motor Co. Ltd. is moving its U.S. base to Kennesaw.
By 2028, it plans to complete a slow migration of its headquarters.
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp hopes the move will be repeated by other companies — perhaps more quickly in the future.
Yamaha already employs 2,300 people in the Peach State through various entities.
Some 2,000 employees work at the Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. of America factory in Newnan.
The Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp. of America factory in Newnan opened 40 years ago.
Other Yamaha affiliates have followed the company to Georgia over time.
Gov. Kemp headlined the opening of a new marine innovation center in 2023.
The governor's office pointed to Georgia’s No. 1 state for business ranking to hint that other firms should follow suit.

Gov. Kemp stated, “This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world. To any other California-based companies looking for a better home, we’ll give you plenty of reasons to keep Georgia on your mind.”
Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp’s first trip to Japan last fall included a full slate of company visits and prospect meetings, with the Yamaha headquarters move proving particularly fruitful.
The governor met with Yamaha executives during his first trip to Japan last fall for the SEUS-Japan alliance annual meeting.
It’s unclear whether the meeting accelerated the company’s plans to move its headquarters.
Yamaha said the move was an effort to streamline operations amid tariffs and market changes.
Kennesaw for many years has been “where all the action is” for Yamaha Motor USA, according to Chief Executive Mike Chrzanowski in early 2025.
source_title: Gov. Kemp’s Office Trolls California, Linking Japanese HQ Move to October Trip  - Global Atlanta

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp of America
facts: The side-by-side vehicles were manufactured in Newnan, Georgia by Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp of America.
Yamaha Motor Corp. USA is recalling about 4,130 units of Wolverine RMAX Off-Road Side-By-Side vehicles citing fire and explosion hazards.

The recall involves model numbers YXF10WPZMG (Wolverine RMAX 4 SE), YXF10WPLML (Wolverine RMAX 4 LE), YXF10WPAMS (Wolverine RMAX 4), YXE10WPZMG (Wolverine RMAX 2 SE), YXE10WPAMS/YXE10WPAMW (Wolverine RMAX 2), and YXE10WPLML (Wolverine RMAX 2 LE).
The recalled vehicles were sold in silver, white, black, yellow, gray and blue colors.
The vehicles were sold at Yamaha side by side dealers nationwide from February 2021 through July 2021 for between $22,700 and $26,700.
According to the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission, the recalled vehicles can have a damaged fuel tank causing fuel to leak, posing fire and explosion hazards.

The recall was initiated after the Cypress, California-based company received two reports of the fuel tanks being assembled incorrectly, though no incidents of fuel leakage or injuries have been reported.
Consumers are urged to immediately stop using the recalled vehicles and contact an authorized Yamaha dealer to schedule a free inspection and repair.
source_title: Yamaha Recalls Wolverine RMAX Off-Road Side-By-Side Vehicles

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) / Yamaha's Georgia Facility
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: The Yamaha manufacturing facility in Newnan, Ga., was featured in a new episode of the RFD-TV network program "Small Town, Big Deal."
The episode featuring Newnan and Yamaha first aired on Thursday, June 11, 2015, at 8:30 p.m. Central Standard Time.
YMMC serves as Yamaha’s worldwide hub of off-highway vehicle (OHV) manufacturing.
All of the company’s SxS and the majority of their ATV vehicles are produced at the Newnan facility for national and international distribution.
The Newnan facility covers 280 acres with more than 1.3 million square feet of manufacturing facilities.
The facility employs approximately 1,473 people.
Yamaha has invested more than $250 million into U.S. manufacturing over the past 15 years to create the Newnan facility.
The facility produces ATV or Side-by-Side vehicles, Golf Cars, and Personal Watercraft.

Mike Chrzanowski, executive vice president of YMMC, stated that Yamaha strives to maintain the highest production quality and environmental standards while giving back to the community through corporate social responsibility programs.
Rodney Miller, co-host of "Small Town, Big Deal," noted that Yamaha has had a tremendously positive impact on Newnan from both an economic and philanthropic standpoint.
Yamaha has facilities in Wisconsin and Georgia, as well as factory operations in Tennessee and Georgia.
YMUS products are sold through a nationwide network of dealers in the United States.
source_title: Yamaha’s Georgia Manufacturing Facility Featured in “Small Town, Big Deal” Television Program  - ATV On Demand

facts: Yamaha Motor Company is relocating its U.S. headquarters to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The company produces motorcycles, personal watercraft, various marine products, ATVs and other motorized products.
Yamaha currently employs 2,000 Georgians at various plants in Newnan and Kennesaw.

The company stated it is “undertaking structural reforms aimed at improving the profitability of its U.S. operations in response to cost increases resulting from U.S. tariffs and changes in the market environment.”
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp commented on the relocation move on Tuesday, March 10.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “After many years of great partnership, we are honored and proud to welcome Yamaha’s American headquarters to the No. 1 state for business. This is another loud and clear testament to what we offer job creators from around the world.”
The relocation process will start in 2026 and continue into 2028.
Yamaha first began manufacturing in Georgia in 1986.
In 1999, the company relocated its marine division to Kennesaw.
The Kennesaw plant was expanded in 2023.
In 2019, Yamaha moved its motorsports division to Georgia.
Yamaha’s U.S. headquarters had previously been based in Cypress, California for 50 years.
As part of the relocation, the company will sell its fixed assets in California, including land, offices and warehouses.
source_title: Yamaha Motor Co. relocates U.S. headquarters to Georgia after 50 years in California

facts: The Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation plant is located in Newnan, Ga., southwest of Atlanta.
The facility produces four vehicle segments: ATVs, PWCs (WaveRunners), side-by-sides, and golf cars.
In May, Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S.A. announced that the Georgia factory would take over production from Japan of all Yamaha ATVs.
The Grizzly 700 and 550 FI (standard and EPS) utility ATVs made the move to the Georgia plant in May.
The transfer brings all Yamaha 4×4 ATV and side-by-side production to Georgia.
Additional ATV models will be transferred to the facility in 2012 and 2013.
A press conference brought together dozens of local and state dignitaries, though Georgia Gov. Nathan Deal was absent due to a scheduling conflict.
Newnan Mayor Keith Brady proclaimed “Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation Day” in the city.
Yamaha has invested $250 million into the plant over the last 10 years.
Two Grizzly lines will now be produced at the Georgia facility, replacing Japanese production.
A new “Assembled in the U.S.A.” logo was unveiled in front of hundreds of plant workers.
Henio Arcangeli Jr., president of Yamaha Motorsports Group, stated the initiative is important for Yamaha, its 1,300 dealer partners across the United States, and the people of Georgia.
Arcangeli noted that transferring production to Newnan will better support dealer partners and customers with high-quality ATVs engineered in the state-of-the-art facility.
The production transfer of the Grizzly models will take place through 2012.
The plant should be able to add about 200 new jobs to the facility due to the expansion.
The expansion will also add jobs to the 125 North American suppliers located nearby.
The Newnan factory first began making golf cars in 1988.
WaveRunner PWCs found a home in Newnan in 1989.
Utility ATVs began production at the facility in 1998.
Rhino side-by-sides came on board at the plant in 2003.
YMMC senior vice president Mike Chrzanowski stated that in 2011, the facility started production of two of the biggest, best-selling ATVs in Yamaha’s lineup.
Chrzanowski claimed nowhere in the world has another factory with the range of capabilities YMMC has to produce four different product lines using an ingenious flexible assembly line.
Harry Ito, president of Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corp., presented Grizzly ATVs to both the Coweta County Sheriff’s Department and the Coweta County Fire Department.
Ito expressed gratitude to management and employees for their dedicated service ensuring the success of the production transfer.
Chrzanowski noted that since April 2009, more than 600 man-hours had gone into preparing for the shift in production from five to seven ATV base models.
Chrzanowski emphasized that the hard-working and dedicated workforce is the most important factor in designing, developing, testing, and producing products.
As the U.S. economy continues to improve, Yamaha looks forward to increased volume from the new model and the resulting increase in new jobs.
Yamaha officials pointed to optimized manufacturing capacity, consolidated production management and manufacturing technologies, streamlined supply chain, and speed to market as benefits for dealers.
Congressman Lynn Westmoreland (R-Ga.) welcomed the expansion during a “Chamber of Commerce” day presentation and provided ideas for other uses of the state’s workforce.
Westmoreland told Yamaha Motor Corp. U.S.A. president Mr. Toshi Kato and Mr. Ito that he believed they could build pianos if allowed.
source_title: Yamaha brings all of its ATV production to Georgia | Powersports Business

facts: Yamaha Motor Co., Ltd. has announced plans to relocate its U.S. subsidiary, Yamaha Motor Corporation, U.S.A. (YMUS), from Cypress, California, to Kennesaw, Georgia.
The multi-year transition will run from late 2026 through the end of 2028.
In conjunction with the move, Yamaha will sell all fixed assets at its 25.1-acre Cypress campus, including land, offices, and warehouse facilities.
The company plans to utilize a sale-and-leaseback arrangement for a period of time to maintain business continuity during the transition.
Details regarding the sale price, buyer, and timing are still under review.
The Cypress site has served as Yamaha’s U.S. headquarters since 1979, after the company acquired the land in 1978.
Yamaha’s Marine Business Unit relocated to Kennesaw in 1999.
Yamaha’s Motorsports business unit relocated to Kennesaw in 2019.
Today, the Cypress facility primarily houses corporate administrative functions and Yamaha’s Financial Services operations.
The consolidation places Yamaha’s core U.S. operations — including marine and motorsports — under a unified Georgia base.
The move potentially streamlines decision-making, logistics coordination, and cross-division strategy for dealers and the company.
Yamaha positioned the relocation as part of broader structural reforms aimed at improving asset efficiency and enhancing profitability in the United States.
Rising costs associated with U.S. tariffs and shifts in the market environment are cited as factors driving the restructuring.
Yamaha is working to build a profit structure “not solely dependent on top-line growth,” to create a more resilient and adaptable organization over the medium to long term.
The announcement reflects a wider trend among OEMs reassessing real estate footprints, overhead costs, and regional operating efficiencies as the post-pandemic market normalizes.

Yamaha first entered the American market in 1960, establishing Yamaha International Corporation in Los Angeles after determining that reliance on third-party trading companies limited growth and profitability.
Under then-president Genichi Kawakami, the company shifted to direct wholesale distribution and aggressively developed a nationwide dealer network.
Nearly 50 years after establishing its Southern California headquarters, Yamaha is consolidating around Georgia, where its marine and motorsports divisions have already operated for years.
source_title: Yamaha to relocate U.S. headquarters to Georgia

entity_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
canonical_name: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America
facts: Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America (YMMC) celebrated the completion of its one millionth personal watercraft after 29 years of manufacturing WaveRunners.
The one millionth model was a 2018 Yamaha VX Deluxe WaveRunner in Black and Azure Blue Metallic.
The special one millionth unit will have a permanent place in Yamaha’s Newnan, Georgia manufacturing facility.
The milestone marks the culmination of more than 29 years of manufacturing personal watercraft at the Georgia-based facility.
The first WaveRunner was produced on Monday, November 21, 1988, at 7:00 a.m.
Mike Chrzanowski serves as president of Yamaha Motor Manufacturing Corporation of America.
Mike Chrzanowski stated that the event marks the culmination of hard work from Georgia-based employees and noted that several team members present were there when the first WaveRunner was produced.
Dean Burnett serves as president of Yamaha WaterCraft Group, a division of Yamaha Motor Corporation USA.
Dean Burnett stated that producing watercraft in Georgia impacts the lives of local community employees and called the milestone a testament to their daily hard work.
The celebration involved pausing the assembly line for a group photo around the one millionth WaveRunner.
Yamaha WaterCraft Group is a division of Yamaha Motor Corporation USA.
WaveRunner manufacturing traces its production process back to single sheets of fiberglass.
source_title: Video: Yamaha Watercraft Celebrates One Millionth WaveRunner - The Watercraft Journal | the best resource for JetSki, WaveRunner, and SeaDoo enthusiasts and most popular Personal WaterCraft site in the world!

facts: Yazaki North America is a manufacturer of automotive systems and components.

facts: Yazaki will expand its operations in Guatemala with a second plant in San Marcos to produce wiring harnesses for Ford F-150 vehicles.
The new facility is expected to generate up to 3,500 jobs and strengthen Guatemala’s role in the regional automotive supply chain.
Yazaki North America (YNA) will construct its second plant in the municipality of Pajapita, San Marcos.
The new site is located about seven kilometers east of YNA's existing facility in Ayutla.
Initial earthworks for the new facility have already begun.
The new facility will cover 35,000 square meters.
The new plant is expected to start operations in September 2027.
At full capacity, the new plant is expected to generate around 3,500 formal jobs.
This expansion follows the success of Yazaki’s first plant in Ayutla, which was inaugurated in 2023.
The first plant already produces wiring harnesses and supplies directly to Ford.
Kenichi Fujisawa serves as president and CEO of YNA.
Kenichi Fujisawa stated that "Ford is satisfied and ready for operations to begin at the second plant."
Formal hiring is expected to start in mid-January next year.
Government officials highlighted that Yazaki is the first global automotive company manufacturing in Guatemala within the electrical and electronic segment.
An official stated, "This marks an important step in the evolution of our industrial base and represents progress in the sophistication of our economy."
The automotive value chain operates through levels of specialization, with Yazaki positioned at Tier 2.
Japan’s ambassador to Guatemala is Ryosuke Kuwana.
Ryosuke Kuwana emphasized that the project strengthens Guatemala's integration into North America’s automotive value chain.
A company executive noted that "The quality of the Guatemalan workforce was key in our decision."
Authorities reported that in 2025, 80 companies were evaluating investment opportunities in Guatemala.
Nine companies are already established in Guatemala, including Nextil.
Most of these established companies are focused on light manufacturing.
Foreign direct investment reached US$1.6945 billion in 2024.
Projections for foreign direct investment are US$1.8 billion in 2025.
Expectations are to exceed US$2 billion by 2026.
Initiatives such as recent visits from international companies and upcoming Japanese business missions could further boost investment.
source_title: Yazaki is Ready to Open Second Plant in San Marcos to Expand Production for Ford

facts: Yazaki North and Central America named Osram Opto Semiconductors Green Supplier of the Year at its annual awards ceremony.
Yazaki recognized Osram Opto Semiconductors as the Best in Class Green supplier for 2012-2013, honoring them with both awards of excellence.
Osram was selected from Yazaki’s top 103 suppliers in North and Central America.
Olga Alavanou serves as executive vice president, Supply Chain Management for Yazaki.
Yazaki’s Green Supplier of the Year program reinforces its commitment to helping create a society capable of sustainable development.
Frank Tillner is Chief Operating Officer at Osram Opto Semiconductors.
Osram follows a responsible, sustainable, and careful environmental policy.
Osram's programs save energy, protect water, and avoid chemical waste in both production and products.
Osram was chosen for significant contributions to environmental preservation through manufacturing, production, and delivery processes.
The award ceremony was held on June 5, 2013, to honor World Environment Day.
The Yazaki Green Supplier of the Year program was established in 2007 as a benchmark for all Yazaki companies and affiliates throughout North and Central America.
OSRAM is headquartered in Munich and is one of two leading lighting manufacturers globally.
Osram Opto Semiconductors GmbH is headquartered in Regensburg, Germany.
Osram provides semiconductor technology solutions for lighting, sensor, and visualization applications.
Osram operates production sites in Regensburg (Germany) and Penang (Malaysia).
Osram's North America headquarters is located in Sunnyvale (USA).
Osram's Asia region headquarters is located in Hong Kong.
Yazaki Corporation is a global leader in the research, development, and delivery of vehicle power and data solutions for vehicle applications.
Yazaki produces electrical distribution systems, vehicle information products, solid-state power centers, connection systems, and electronics.
Yazaki employs more than 220,000 people worldwide across 41 countries.
Yazaki has been committed to environmental preservation for more than 70 years.
Yazaki develops advanced electric components for hybrid electric vehicles.
Yazaki promotes recycling and the efficient use of resources.
World Environment Day is an annual United Nations event that climaxes on June 5 each year, having begun in 1972.
source_title: pr-20-06-2013 | ams OSRAM

facts: Yazaki Corporation, headquartered in Minato-ku, Tokyo with President Riku Yazaki, has won the Automotive News PACE Award 2024.
The award was sponsored by Automotive News (America) and given for the flexible printed circuit (FPC) type busbar module developed by the company.

The Automotive News PACE Award is given to suppliers who develop innovative technologies and manufacturing processes in the automotive industry and contribute to its development in a game-changing way.
Yazaki Corporation received a double award last fiscal year and has now won an award two years running.
Yazaki stated it will continue striving for the development of products that put customers first and work on improving the safety and comfort of mobility society.
The spread of hybrid EVs and battery EVs is advancing rapidly in the midst of the transformation surrounding the automotive industry.
As the need for extended range increases with these vehicles, it is expected that battery capacity will become increasingly large.
In association with greater battery capacity, components need to be smaller to improve mounting efficiency and have a lower profile to secure cabin space.
Ensuring the safety of high-voltage batteries is also necessary alongside size reduction.
Yazaki developed the FPC type busbar module to contribute to the reduction of battery size and weight and securing safety.
The product replaces the normal electric wires of a voltage detection circuit with an FPC on busbar modules.
The busbar modules have functions that detect cell voltage and temperature while simultaneously connecting multiple cells in series in the high voltage battery of an electric vehicle.
The adoption of FPC eliminates the excess space required by wire-type modules.
The busbar module on its own is about 50% lighter than a wire-type.
Mounting chip fuses on the FPC near to a cell protects the circuit as a
A unique tolerance and a vibration-absorbing structure improves assembly characteristics and
FPC enables complete automation of production, which is difficult with electric wire-type.
source_title: Yazaki: Automotive News PACE Award 2024 for the “FPC type busbar module” developed by the company | Automotive World

facts: YKK AP America Inc. installed a new goods-to-person (GTP) automation system at its residential window plant in Macon, Georgia.
The GTP system was installed at the 400,000-square-foot purpose-built facility’s shipping department to further reduce the manual movement of heavy windows.
Dennis Howard serves as senior manager of transportation and logistics at YKK AP.
Daymon Loyd serves as director of residential manufacturing at YKK AP.
The GTP system improves operations and quality and reduces time to load by 39%.
The GTP system uses autonomous robots to move finished products from the end of the line to material storage units and into a fenced GTP field that houses final products.
YKK AP is making strategic investments in automation to reduce risk and improve safety.
YKK AP's goal for residential manufacturing was to reduce material handling to improve quality, starting with the shipping department where most material handling occurs.
The Macon facility opened in early 2024.
Upon opening, YKK AP aimed for zero injuries, zero backorders, and 100% on-time delivery.
The facility currently achieves a 97% on-time delivery rate.
YKK AP invested in the GTP system to close the gap from 97% to 100% on-time delivery.
The facility handles 80% of its products with the GTP system.
The remaining 20% of products are too large for the material storage units.
Integrating the new autonomous system with YKK AP’s IT infrastructure was the most significant deployment challenge.
Training for the system interface (not end users) served as a challenge, requiring an aggressive training schedule.
The bulk of the training involved troubleshooting technical issues as they arose.
YKK AP did not have to retrain employees but still had to train them to use the specific system.
The system's user-friendly interface made the training process seamless.
The GTP system is vital for companies seeking to improve safety via automation, requiring openness to change.
This was YKK AP’s residential manufacturing team’s first deployment of GTP technology.
The team had to remain open and flexible to changing original plans to accommodate the technology correctly.
Much of the capital equipment was on the manufacturing floor before it was in action, requiring time and trial and error to deploy.
The team has witnessed the project from start to finish and now sees it operating at its full potential.
source_title: YKK AP America Introduces Autonomous GTP System at Georgia Window Facility

facts: The 400,000-square-foot new residential window and door manufacturing facility serves as an advanced, model plant for YKK’s AP’s North American operations.
YKK AP was one of the first Japanese companies to establish operations in the state of Georgia.
The new plant is located on over 50 acres.

The facility expanded YKK AP’s residential manufacturing business with the addition of new machinery and technologies, advanced sustainable manufacturing processes, and additional capacity to meet market demand for single and multi-family residential window products.
JE Dunn worked closely with SSOE on this highly collaborative project to deliver the new plant.
The project includes 12,000 square feet of office space.
The project includes all required campus infrastructure, roads, parking, truck docks, and other site requirements.
YKK AP’s new plant added nearly 100 new jobs in Macon.
YKK AP’s residential products business has been headquartered in Macon since it was established.

facts: YKK AP America Inc. announced the creation of its Manufacturing Excellence organization along with leadership appointments across commercial and residential manufacturing operations.

Daymon Loyd will oversee key YKK AP manufacturing priorities including customer satisfaction, on-time delivery, quality, equipment reliability, process optimization, and strategic oversight of the Dublin plant’s investment and modernization programs.

Mike Turner, president of Commercial Business at YKK AP America Inc., stated that Daymon’s appointment reflects a continued commitment to building a strong manufacturing organization positioned to support customers and long-term growth.

Josh Jensen, president of Residential Business at YKK AP America Inc., emphasized that focused plant leadership is critical to delivering quality, consistency, and service in the residential business.
Derek will step into the residential plant leadership position as a 10-year employee with experience in manufacturing engineering, process improvement, and quality control.
YKK AP America Inc. is a leading manufacturer of award-winning commercial facade systems and residential windows and doors.
The company is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
All building products are manufactured in production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.
Together with its subsidiary brand Erie Architectural Products (Erie AP), the company serves architects, glazing contractors, homebuilders, and resellers across the country.
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.
