facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. is celebrating the 50th anniversary of its Macon Factory on May 4, 2024.

The anniversary event features speeches from Mayor of Macon Lester Miller, Consul General of Japan Mio Maeda, YKK Corporation Chairman Masayuki Sarumaru, President of YKK Corporation of America Jim Reed, and President of YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. John Smith.
The company will honor employees marking their 45th through 50th anniversaries with the firm during the event.
The ceremony includes the rededication of the Heart of Friendship sculpture, relocated to Carolyn Crayton Park, originally donated by YKK in 1974.
President John Smith highlighted the Macon Factory's pivotal role in embodying the company’s philosophy and its commitment to the community over fifty years.
Japan-based YKK first entered the U.S. market in 1960 as a zipper distributor in New York City near the garment district.
Founder Tadao Yoshida established a vertical integration model known as the “Cycle of Goodness” philosophy, controlling the entire production process to ensure quality and cost efficiency.
A site selection team explored Tennessee, North Carolina, South Carolina, Alabama, and Georgia as the U.S. textile industry shifted southward.
Georgia was selected due to its robust infrastructure, including four interstate highways, the deep-water port of Savannah, and a major international airport.

A meeting arranged by Cloyd Hall, a Macon native and Special Assistant to then-Governor Jimmy Carter, facilitated a connection between Yoshida and Carter that clinched the decision to settle in Georgia.
YKK established its manufacturing facility just south of Macon in the newly developed Ocmulgee East Industrial Park.
The facility's development began with a visit by prominent Macon leaders to YKK’s manufacturing center in Kurobe, Japan.
Under leadership of Yoshinori Kitano, construction and operation strategies were crafted for the new plant.
Newly hired engineers, including future YKK Corporation of America CEO Alex Gregory, were trained in Japan to transfer specialized manufacturing techniques to Macon.
The Macon plant officially opened in 1974.
Initially, the facility produced zippers tailored for the American market, including varieties for clothing, furniture, and outdoor equipment.
Over the decades, the factory has expanded dramatically and now employs over 600 local residents.
The Macon facility has diversified its product line into advanced solutions for the automotive, government, and medical sectors.
In 1977, YKK played a pivotal role in establishing a sister city relationship between Kurobe, Japan, and Macon, Georgia.
Over four decades, the sister city partnership facilitated cultural exchange with more than 1,100 citizens from both cities visiting each other's countries.
YKK is an annual sponsor of the Macon Cherry Blossom Festival.
In 1990, YKK donated $300,000 toward the construction of a dedicated building for festival organizers.
The city of Macon donated $60,000 worth of land to complement the 1990 festival building donation.
Chairman Tadahiro Yoshida personally attended the 1990 groundbreaking ceremony for the festival building.
YKK remains committed to enhancing the prosperity of Macon and enriching Georgia while advancing the global textile industry.
source_title: YKK USA Celebrates 50 Years of Innovation and Excellence at Macon Factory - Middle Georgia CEO

facts: YKK Corporation and Panasonic Holdings Corporation of Japan announced the completion of the share transfer of Panasonic Housing Solutions Co., Ltd. (PHS).
The share purchase agreement was executed on November 17, 2025.
Through the transaction completed on March 31, 2026, the YKK Group acquired 80 percent of PHS shares previously held by Panasonic Holdings Corporation.
Panasonic Holdings Corporation will continue to hold the remaining 20 percent of PHS shares.
PHS will continue to operate under the Panasonic brand and PHS company name.

The partnership combines YKK AP's advanced technological capabilities and extensive product lineup with PHS's strengths in manufacturing, sales, and engineering of interior and exterior housing equipment and construction materials.
Oliver Stepe serves as president and CEO of YKK AP America Inc.

Oliver Stepe stated the partnership marks an important step in advancing innovation and expanding the diversity of YKK AP's architectural products business on a global scale, including potential opportunities in North America.
The YKK Group operates in approximately 70 countries and regions around the world.
YKK Corporation was originally founded in 1934 as San-S Shokai.
YKK Corporation changed its name from Yoshida Kogyo K.K. to YKK Corporation in 1994.
The YKK Group employs 46,305 people, including 18,468 in Japan and 27,837 overseas.
Consolidated net sales for the fiscal year ended March 2025 totaled 998.2 billion yen.
YKK AP America Inc. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
All building products are manufactured in world-class production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.
YKK AP America Inc. serves architects, glazing contractors, homebuilders, and resellers across the country together with its subsidiary brand Erie Architectural Products (Erie AP).
YKK AP America Inc. is a subsidiary of YKK AP Inc., a global architectural products company based in Tokyo, Japan.
source_title: YKK Corporation Completes Share Transfer Agreement for Panasonic Housing Solutions

facts: YKK AP America Inc. held a ribbon-cutting ceremony to mark the official opening of its first greenfield residential manufacturing facility in Macon-Bibb County, Georgia.

The event was held in Macon with Commissioner Pat Wilson of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, Mayor Lester Miller of Macon-Bibb County, Chairman Robby Fountain of the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority, and other local leaders in attendance.
The facility completed construction in December 2023 and began operations in January 2024.
It currently employs more than 250 employees in the Macon community.

Mayor Lester Miller stated that YKK AP’s expansion into a new, leading-edge facility is a testament to its strong connection with the community and marks the beginning of a new chapter offering opportunities to enrich the local economy, improve families' lives, and strengthen Macon's position as a hub of innovation and industry.

Oliver Stepe, president and CEO of YKK AP America Inc., stated that the facility symbolizes the company's growth and confidence in its business and employees moving forward, and expressed gratitude to the state of Georgia, the Macon-Bibb County Industrial Authority, and partners for helping celebrate the milestone.
source_title: YKK AP Holds Ribbon-Cutting Ceremony for New Macon Facility | Window + Door

facts: YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. announced the 50th anniversary of its Macon Factory on November 9 with a Family Day dedicated to employees.
The Macon Factory is located just south of Macon in the newly developed Ocmulgee East Industrial Park in Georgia.
Japan-based YKK first entered the U.S. market in 1960 as a zipper distributor in New York City, strategically located near the garment district.
Founder Tadao Yoshida’s vision extended beyond distribution to a model of vertical integration that epitomized the company’s “Cycle of Goodness” philosophy.

Site selection teams explored locations in Tennessee, North and South Carolina, Alabama, and Georgia before Georgia emerged as a frontrunner due to its robust infrastructure, including four interstate highways, the deep-water port of Savannah, and a major international airport.
The decision to settle in Georgia was clinched by a fortuitous meeting arranged by Cloyd Hall, a Macon native and Special Assistant to then-Governor Jimmy Carter.
Under the leadership of Yoshinori Kitano, construction and operation strategies were crafted for the new plant.
Newly hired engineers, including future YKK Corporation of America CEO Alex Gregory, were trained in Japan to ensure the transfer of specialized manufacturing techniques to the Macon facility.
The new plant opened in 1974.
The facility initially focused on producing zippers tailored to the specific needs of the American market, including varieties for clothing, furniture, and outdoor equipment.

Over the decades, the factory has expanded dramatically, employing over 600 local residents and diversifying into advanced solutions for industries including automotive, government, and medical sectors.
In 1977, YKK played a pivotal role in establishing a sister city relationship between Kurobe, Japan, and Macon, Georgia.
Over the past four decades, this partnership has fostered cultural exchange with more than 1,100 citizens from both cities visiting each other’s countries.
YKK has been a stalwart supporter of the Macon Cherry Blossom Festival, providing substantial sponsorships and active participation in festival activities.
In 1990, YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. donated $300,000 towards the construction of a dedicated building for the festival organizers.
The city of Macon complemented the donation by providing $60,000 worth of land for the festival building.
Chairman Tadahiro Yoshida personally attended the groundbreaking ceremony for the festival building in 1990.
YKK Corporation was founded in Tokyo in 1934 and continuously sets industry standards for quality, innovation, and sustainability in fastening products.
The company produces zippers, plastic hardware, hook and loop fasteners, webbing tapes, and snap and buttons.
YKK leverages its integrated production, service, and supply chain solutions across 70+ countries and regions.

At the anniversary event, speeches were delivered by Mayor of Macon-Bibb County Lester Miller, Consul General of Japan Mio Maeda, YKK Corporation Chairman Masayuki Sarumaru, President of YKK Corporation of America Jim Reed, and President of YKK (U.S.A.) Inc. John Smith.
John Smith highlighted the pivotal role of the Macon Factory in embodying the company’s philosophy and its enduring commitment to the community.
Two foreign-owned suppliers were selected to receive 2021 GEAR Awards, a recognition bestowed by the Georgia Department of Economic Development for contributions to the state’s automotive industry.
YKK (U.S.A.) Inc., a Japanese-owned company with a longstanding factory in Macon, was named supplier of the year in the large companies category (those with more than 200 employees).
YKK produces zippers for seats, hook-and-loop fasteners, plastic hardware, and snaps and buttons for seats and truck tonneau cover hold-downs.
YKK counts 100 customers in the auto sector.
YKK executives have long been advocates for smooth, predictable rules in the North American trade in cars.
YKK set up shop in Macon in the 1970s, becoming Georgia’s first Japanese investor.
The GEAR award for YKK was announced in December and presented at a Jan. 19 ceremony in Macon.

Andrew Capezzuto, chief administrative officer and general counsel of GDEcD, stated that “The 2021 GEAR award for supplier of the year recognizes companies that are good corporate stewards,” while presenting the award in Macon.
Solvay, a Belgian chemical company with an Alpharetta-based specialty polymers division, received recognition for its growing impact on Georgia.
Solvay’s division provides plastics that can withstand extreme temperatures, high pressures and corrosive chemicals for automotive applications.

Solvay received the GEAR Innovation and Emerging Technology Award for introducing Amodel Supreme polymer, designed for the high-voltage environments of electric vehicles, a segment where Georgia is making a strong global investment push.
Solvay also produces the similar Amodel BIOS thermoplastic, which is made partially from biological materials and produced using renewable energy.
Solvay was slated to be a key stop on Belgian Princess Astrid’s tour of Georgia during a trade mission last October.
The Solvay global CEO was scheduled to fly over for the event.
The trade mission has been postponed until June due to COVID-19, with a new itinerary yet to be released.
Comer-based SMI Composites won an award in the 199 employees or fewer category.
Douglas-based Rock Solid Cargo was named transportation equipment manufacturer of the year.
Nivel Manufacturing in Cairo received the corporate citizenship award.
The GEAR awards began in 2016, with foreign companies perennially posting positive showings and sometimes dominating most of the field.
source_title: Two Foreign Firms Take Home Georgia Automotive Awards - Global Atlanta

facts: Released its annual report, “YKK AP Integrated Report 2024”, on Nov. 14, 2024, to communicate progress to stakeholders.
Progress supports the organization’s global sustainability goals, “Evolution 2030.”
Committed $330 million globally through 2030 for renewable energy, efficient equipment, and automation.
Investments include YKK AP America’s new Residential facility and planned facilities upgrades to achieve an 80% reduction in global Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 2030.
80% of YKK AP America’s aluminum is now sourced from low-carbon North American suppliers, lowering Scope 3 emissions in line with a goal to cut these by 30% by 2030.
Recycles 100% of in-house aluminum scrap, with 30% recycled content in its aluminum billets to support a sustainable circular model.
Develops high-insulation, energy-saving products designed to reduce energy use and carbon emissions in buildings.
Prioritizes responsible procurement and environmentally conscious material sourcing throughout the supply chain.
President and CEO Oliver Stepe and Chief HR Officer Patrys Wiid highlighted the CARE Conversations program as essential to fostering an engaged workplace.

Manufactures building products at world-class production facilities located in Dublin, Ga., Macon, Ga., and Lakeshore, Ontario.

Serves architects, glazing contractors, homebuilders, and resellers across the country.

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal, along with representatives of the Georgia Ports Authority, the Greater Hall Chamber and Norfolk Southern Railroad announced plans to make Hall County the site of the GPA's next inland port.
The Northeast Georgia Inland Port will be situated in the heart of the manufacturing and logistics corridor along Interstate 85.
Hall County and the surrounding region are an important area for the production of heavy equipment, food and forest products.
The inland port will act as an economic development tool, drawing new investment from business and industry to Hall and its surrounding counties.
Norfolk Southern will provide service on a direct rail route to and from the Port of Savannah's Garden City Terminal for handling both import and export containers at the Gainesville terminal.
Jeff Heller serves as Vice President Intermodal & Automotive at Norfolk Southern.
Hall County and the surrounding region in Northeast Georgia are key areas of expansion in the state for manufacturers and distributors.
The GPA's inland port at Gainesville combined with Norfolk Southern's rail service will provide crucial links in the supply chains of local industries, consumers, and the rest of the world.
The project will serve as a catalyst for new opportunities for industrial development.
Improved access to rail at the new terminal will increase logistics options and overall efficiency while reducing congestion on Georgia highways.
Currently, containers moving by truck travel a 600-mile roundtrip to and from the Port of Savannah.
When the new rail yard opens, drivers will be able to make shorter trips from area manufacturers and distribution facilities to the Northeast Georgia site.
Griff Lynch serves as Executive Director of the Georgia Ports Authority.
The new Gainesville location is part of the Network Georgia initiative, providing new and existing port customers with additional logistics options.
The new rail hub will allow importers and exporters to move loaded containers to the coast with greater efficiency.
The facility will provide a ready source of empty containers for Georgia exports.
Jimmy Allgood serves as GPA Board Chairman.
The new cargo handling option will transform the local rail market and help port customers optimize their supply chains.
The inland terminals are bringing ports and producers closer together, providing new economic opportunities.
Georgians will benefit from increased employment options as more companies expand or locate in the region.
Georgia Ports currently support more than 440,000 jobs across every corner of the state.
The new terminal is located in the Gateway Industrial Centre on GA 365 and covers 104 acres.
The facility's services will draw from a 1.5-million population in Hall, Gwinnett and surrounding counties.
Tim Evans serves as vice president of economic development for the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce.
The new inland port terminal will shorten the supply chain for many manufacturers, processors and distributors in the region.
Rail deliveries to and from the Port of Savannah can shorten truck delivery times from approximately seven hours to less than 30 minutes.
The facility provides direct access to the Port of Savannah, which is the fastest growing container port in the U.S.
Auto Metal Direct, a worldwide distributor of auto body panels and trim for classic cars and trucks, announced its intent to open a new 318,000 square-foot distribution and fulfillment center.
Construction on Auto Metal Direct's new facility will soon begin in the Gateway Industrial Centre.
The $15 million Auto Metal Direct development will bring 40 jobs to Hall County upon completion.
Mark Headrick serves as President of Auto Metal Direct.
Auto Metal Direct receives hundreds of containers each year, making Georgia Ports Authority services essential for its business.
The proximity of the new inland port was an influencing factor in Auto Metal Direct's decision to locate its new building there.
Auto Metal Direct expects quicker service, lower costs, and ease of movement at its new location.
Pat Wilson serves as Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner.
The Georgia Ports Authority acts as a critical economic development partner and catalyst for growth across the state.
The ports have positioned Georgia as a global leader in logistics infrastructure and helped locate new projects and industry expansions across the state.
The expansion of Auto Metal Direct will further bolster the strength of the workforce in the community.
The Northeast Georgia Inland Port will create exciting economic opportunities for the community and region.
The Northeast Georgia Inland Port is the second Network Georgia-related announcement of the year.
The GPA held a grand opening for the Appalachian Regional Port on Aug. 22 on a 42-acre site just north of Chatsworth, Ga., in Murray County.
The Hall County inland terminal is scheduled to be complete in 2021.
At full build-out, the terminal will have the capacity to handle up to 150,000 containers per year.
Phil Sutton serves as Vice President Administration at Kubota Manufacturing of America Corporation.
The Georgia Ports Authority has supported Kubota for many years by helping reduce lead times for in- and outbound shipments.
Using the Port of Savannah provides Kubota a cost-effective alternative to West Coast ports.
Kubota anticipates potential cost savings from the new inland port, including reduced chassis and container fees and reduced FTZ administrative fees.

Kubota sees huge potential to significantly reduce reliance on OTR motor carriers and reduce lead times for inbound parts and outbound finished goods.

The proximity of Georgia poultry producers to the port gives the industry a competitive advantage for efficient and cost-effective overseas delivery.
The Georgia Ports Authority's investment in infrastructure improves poultry shipment service, with the new inland rail yard continuing to enhance connectivity for the Georgia poultry industry.

facts: Georgia continues to strengthen its business environment through a combination of tax reform, litigation reform, and targeted industry incentives.
Two major pieces of tort reform legislation passed in 2025.

SB 68 introduced comprehensive lawsuit reform, including limits on anchoring for non-economic damages, bifurcation of trials into liability and damages phases, restrictions on negligent security liability, discovery stays during motions to dismiss, and limits on voluntary dismissals.
SB 69 regulates third-party litigation funding by requiring registration, restricting foreign adversary participation, and capping funders’ influence over legal decisions.

HB 1470 (2026) created a new cause of action for abusive litigation to address bad faith lawsuits alleging violations of federal ADA laws relating to website access, helping create a more predictable legal environment and strengthening protections for small businesses.
Established by a 2018 constitutional amendment and 2019 enabling legislation, the Georgia Statewide Business Court began hearing cases in 2020.
The Georgia Statewide Business Court offers a specialized venue for complex commercial disputes, improving predictability and efficiency in high-value cases.

Further changes in 2026 expanded the jurisdiction of the court making it the exclusive forum for certain types of corporate disputes, while allowing for businesses to include forum selection clause in their governing documents to have such disputes be settled in the business court.
HB 1185 (2026) enhances protections for corporate leadership, tightens shareholder derivative suit requirements, and promotes a more predictable, efficient, and fair legal environment.
These changes allow for Georgia businesses to focus on the growth and innovation without being exposed to frivolous litigation and other disruption.

In 2026, the business community and rural Georgia partners teamed up to add transferability to the existing Georgia Jobs and Investment Tax Credits for forestry manufacturers (including biofuel manufacturers).
Transferability makes for an aggressively competitive tax incentive and supports rural investment at a higher rate (HB 134).
Approved by Georgia voters in November 2024, the Georgia Tax Tribunal transformed into the Georgia Tax Court, shifting from the executive branch to the judicial branch.

Starting in July 2026, appeals from tribunal decisions will go directly to the Georgia Court of Appeals rather than the Fulton County Superior Court, streamlining the tax litigation appeals process and reducing delays. (HB 1267/HR 598, 2024)
In 2026, Georgia reduced the corporate and flat individual income tax rate from 5.19% to 4.99% with further reductions to 3.99% over eight years if the state meets certain financial benchmarks.
In 2026, the state also increased the standard deduction by 25%…
HB 1023 (2024) extended the state corporate filing deadline by one month.
Georgia is taking steps to address and improve our state’s overall housing supply shortage by improving residential permitting efficiency to reduce development costs and timeframes.
SB 447 (2026) strengthens the 45-day permit application review timeline for land-disturbance permits and requires municipalities and counties to establish and display clear application criteria.
HB 1058 (2022) allows affiliated corporate groups to elect consolidated Georgia corporate income tax filings without prior Department of Revenue approval.
The election is binding for five years, streamlining compliance for multi-entity corporations.
SB 168 (2021) codified a COVID-19 era executive order allowing corporations to conduct their shareholder meetings virtually or offer a virtual option.
HB 579 (2025) allows division directors of each licensing board to issue licenses and update regulations for licensed professions to enhance efficiency.

Further, in 2026, the General Assembly passed SB 207 which clarifies the standards for professional license eligibility, creates a predetermination process with an option to appeal for certain occupational licenses, and provides more due process for licensing applicants.
Georgia maintains a competitive R&D tax credit program, encouraging business investment in innovation and product development.
This remains a key component of the state’s pro-business tax structure.

HB 136 (2025) expands the tax credit for child and dependent care expenses to a 50% match of the federal credit, creates a new Georgia Child Tax Credit, and creates a new incentive for employers to cover childcare expenses.
Support for child care helps attract and retain a skilled workforce by easing child care burdens for employees.
HB 192 (2025) aligns the State Board of Education’s curriculum with the High-demand Career List to align the education pipeline with the skills demanded by job creators.
This is part of a broader, ongoing effort in Georgia to strengthen the workforce, expand opportunities for students and address the challenges of talent development.
HB 266 (2025) increases the exemption on U.S. military retirement pay from Georgia income tax from $35,000 to $65,000, which can attract and retain skilled veterans in the workforce.
source_title: Business-Friendly Legislation in Georgia - Metro Atlanta Chamber

facts: Adient plc became an independent company on October 31, 2016, as a result of the separation of Johnson Controls International plc's automotive seating and interiors businesses.
Adient is a global leader in the automotive seating supply industry with leading market positions in the Americas, Europe, and China.
The company operates more than 200 wholly- and majority-owned manufacturing or assembly facilities across 31 countries.
Adient employs approximately 75,000 people worldwide.
The company produces and delivers automotive seating for all vehicle classes and major OEMs, delivering into more than 20 million vehicles every year.
Adient's proprietary technologies extend into complete seating systems, frames, mechanisms, foam, head restraints, armrests, and trim covers.
The company utilizes a Global Core Product Portfolio (CPP) strategy for part and design reuse to reduce complexity, inventory, floor space, development time, product cost, and investment.

Adient's worldwide engineering network includes ten core development centers located in Plymouth (USA), Trencin (Slovakia), Burscheid (Germany), Yokohama (Japan), Solingen (Germany), Chongqing (China), Kaiserslautern (Germany), Ceska Lipa (Czech Republic), Ansan (South Korea), and Pune (India).
In China, Adient operates through 8 joint ventures with 35 manufacturing locations in 22 cities.

Adient supplies seating to major global OEMs including BMW, Mercedes-Benz Group, Ford Motor Company, General Motors Company, Honda Motor Company, Hyundai Motor Company, Jaguar Land Rover, Kia Corporation, Mazda Motor Corporation, Mitsubishi Motor Corporation, Nissan Motor Corporation, Renault Group, Subaru, Stellantis N.V., Suzuki Motor Corporation, Toyota Motor Corporation, Volkswagen Group, and Volvo Car Group.

The company also supplies regional OEMs such as BAIC Motor Co., Ltd., Changan Automobile (Group) Co., Ltd., Chery Automobile Co. Ltd., FAW Group Corporation, Proton Holdings Berhad, Ashok Leyland, Tata Motors Limited, and Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd.
Adient supplies newer automotive manufacturers including Tesla Motors, Inc., NIO, and Xpeng Motors.
The company has more than 8 joint venture partnerships with key OEMs, including Guangzhou Automobile Group Co., Ltd., Beijing Automobile Works Co., Ltd., and FAW Group Corporation.
Adient faces competition from traditional seating suppliers, component specialists, and competitors partnered with OEMs through ownership or interlocking business relationships.
Key independent competitors include Lear Corporation, Toyota Boshoku Corporation, Faurecia SA, and Magna International Inc.
Raw materials used by Adient include steel, aluminum, polyurethane chemicals, fabrics, leather, vinyl, and polypropylene.
The adoption of electric vehicles (EVs) is accelerating globally due to product offerings from legacy manufacturers and new entrants, government incentives, and consumer acceptance.
While seating systems are not largely impacted by the shift to EVs, key attributes of seat design are evolving as the market pivots toward EVs.

This EV market shift provides Adient with opportunities to provide value-added solutions through its Evolution of Seating Systems Sustainability (ES3) initiative and capture market share through new entrants.
Adient has developed an interiors concept for autonomous driving that addresses major seating and interior trends for the future automotive industry.
The company aims to reduce scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions by 75% by 2030, using 2019 as the base year.
Adient demonstrates material content compliance through the International Material Data System (IMDS) to meet national and international standards, laws, and regulations.
source_title: Adient plc - Annual Report: 2022 (Form 10-K) | Annual Statements

facts: Adient and Autoliv have co-developed innovative safety solutions that significantly enhance occupant protection in deeply reclined seating positions, commonly referred to as zero-gravity.
The co-developed solutions are now ready for mass production.
The new seating concept combines Adient's Z-Guard with Autoliv's advanced safety technologies.
Zero-gravity seating is gaining popularity in mid- to high-end vehicle models due to its ergonomic benefits.
Deeply reclined seating postures can compromise the effectiveness of conventional safety systems typically designed for standard upright seating.
Adient's Z-Guard is built around the principle of Multi-Dimensional Collaborative Protection, integrating advanced constraint and energy-absorbing technologies into a dynamic safety system.

A key innovation in Z-Guard is the Active Cushion Collapse Mechanism, which rapidly collapses the seat frame in a crash to absorb energy, adjust posture, reduce spinal impact, and protect vital body regions.

Another key innovation is the Adjustable Seat Belt Outlet, which adapts to various passenger postures and body types to enhance comfort while maximizing precise restraint and minimizing shoulder slippage risks.

Autoliv's complementary technologies include an Integrated Seatbelt System for improved stability, a Dynamic Lumbar Retractor for controlled restraint force, a Pelvic Cushion Airbag to reduce pelvic impact, and a Head Side Airbag for enhanced side collision protection in reclined positions.
Together, the combined systems safeguard critical areas including the head, neck, chest, and pelvis.
The Z-Guard concept enables deep integration with vehicle driver assistance systems tailored to OEM requirements and real-world scenarios.
Leveraging predictive signals, the system utilizes high-speed, motor-driven seat repositioning to adjust occupant posture before a collision to reduce injury risk.

If a vehicle cannot detect a crash in time or lacks active repositioning capabilities, Z-Guard provides robust protection through dual pretensioners, cushion buffering, and a collapse mechanism to prevent forward movement and submarining.
The new seating concept is designed to meet the evolving demands of cockpit electronic architecture in the era of software-defined vehicles.
Adient is leading the commercialization of Z-Guard.
Z-Guard is scheduled for production in a high-volume model from a major global OEM.
Adient (NYSE: ADNT) is a global leader in automotive seating with around 70,000 employees in 29 countries.
Adient operates more than 200 manufacturing and assembly plants worldwide and produces/delivers automotive seating for all major OEMs.
Adient's expertise spans from complete seating systems to individual components, covering the entire automotive seat-making process from research and design to engineering and manufacturing.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. and Palantir Technologies Inc. announced a partnership to build the AI foundation for the future of next-gen aviation technologies.
Archer will initially leverage Palantir Foundry and AIP to accelerate the scaling of its aircraft manufacturing capabilities.
Archer and Palantir plan to work in parallel to develop software for the next-gen of critical aviation systems, including air traffic control, movement control and route planning.
The partnership will be formalized later today during a signing ceremony between Palantir co-founder and CEO Alex Karp and Archer Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein at Palantir’s AIPCon.

Archer and Palantir plan to leverage Palantir Foundry and AIP to accelerate the scaling of Archer’s aircraft manufacturing capabilities at its facilities in Georgia and Silicon Valley, with the intent to advance the development of software solutions to drive innovation across the entire value chain.

The partnership will include the development of next-gen software utilizing AI to improve a range of aviation systems, including air traffic control, movement control and route planning, with the goal of improving efficiency, safety and affordability across the industry.

Palantir co-founder and CEO Alex Karp stated that the partnership is redefining the future of flight, making it not only more efficient but also more accessible by integrating advanced AI capabilities with Archer’s innovative approach to aircraft manufacturing and operations.

Archer Founder and CEO Adam Goldstein noted that while the aviation industry has an unmatched level of safety, much legacy technology has only incrementally advanced, and AI and software present an inflection point that will shape the future of aviation.
Archer is designing and developing key enabling technologies and aircraft necessary to power the future of aviation.
source_title: ARCHER AND PALANTIR TO BUILD THE AI FOUNDATION FOR THE FUTURE OF NEXT-GEN AVIATION TECHNOLOGIES - World Airnews

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. closed a local incentive transaction for its Covington, GA manufacturing facility project on February 28, which combined with a Georgia REBA grant totals approximately 33% of the planned capital investment.
On March 2, Archer began construction on Phase 1 of the project, involving the build-out of an approximately 350,000-square-foot facility capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The Covington facility is strategically located adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport and is believed to be the first industry-high-volume manufacturing facility for eVTOL aircraft.
The facility can be expanded by an additional 800,000 square feet, which is estimated to support long-term production targets of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
Archer and Stellantis will join forces at the facility to begin manufacturing Archer's Midnight eVTOL aircraft in 2024.
In January, Archer announced an expanded partnership with Stellantis under which the two companies will manufacture the Midnight production aircraft.
Stellantis will contribute advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, experienced personnel, and capital to the partnership.
The partnership aims to enable rapid scaling of aircraft production and help Archer avoid hundreds of millions of dollars in spending during the manufacturing ramp-up phase.
The goal of the partnership is for Stellantis to mass-produce Archer's eVTOL aircraft as its exclusive contract manufacturer.

Adam Goldstein, Archer's Founder and CEO, stated: "The key to unlocking the promise of what the eVTOL aircraft industry can bring is scale, and we are thrilled by the support the State of Georgia and Newton County are providing to this first of its kind facility in such an exciting new industry."
Archer plans to hold a ceremonial event in April in Covington, GA, to mark the construction milestone.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer's team is based in Santa Clara, CA.
source_title: Archer Closes Incentive Transaction; Begins Construction on First of its Kind High-Volume eVTOL Manufacturing Facility in Covington, GA | Nasdaq

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. received the certificate of occupancy for its high-volume manufacturing facility, which is connected to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Archer is now working to complete the tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line at the facility, referred to as ARC.
Production is planned to begin early this year, with the goal of ramping to a rate of two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
Archer built ARC in close partnership with Stellantis, and the two companies are continuing to progress their plans to mass-produce Archer’s Midnight aircraft.
Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology and expertise and experienced personnel with the goal of scaling this facility to 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Archer founder and CEO Adam Goldstein stated that construction on ARC was completed fast and on budget, shifting the industry from R&D into commercialization.

Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer Arnaud Deboeuf highlighted supporting Archer’s electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise as a great opportunity for both companies.

ARC is where Archer plans to produce its all-electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft, Midnight, designed to offer passengers a sustainable, low-noise and safe alternative to ground transportation in congested cities around the world.
Aerospace and defense products are Georgia’s No. 1 export ($11.1 billion in 2023) and the state’s second largest manufacturing industry, generating a $57.5 billion economic impact.
More than 800 aerospace companies call Georgia home, including Pratt & Whitney, Delta, and Gulfstream Aerospace.
Other recent investments in Georgia include StandardAero’s $33 million expansion, and Gulfstream Aerospace’s $150 million expansion.

Governor Brian Kemp commented that Archer’s choice to build the new manufacturing facility in Newton County marks a next step in an incredible relationship to create quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.

Congressman Mike Collins, who sits on the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, stated the facility completion highlights Georgia’s leadership in innovation, advanced manufacturing and job creation.

Archer's indicative backlog swelled to $6 billion by the end of 2025, indicating potential market demand despite production and certification challenges.
Analysts project Archer's revenue to rise from $9.5 million in 2026 to $428.4 million by 2028.
With a market cap of $3.6 billion, Archer's valuation at 7 times its 2028 sales appears relatively reasonable if production ramps up.
Archer Aviation reported a net loss of $618.2 million in FY 2025.
Archer has a conditional $1 billion aircraft purchase agreement with United Airlines.
Joby Aviation's revenue surged to $53.4 million in FY 2025 from $136,000 in 2024, though it faces a net loss of approximately $930 million.
Archer focuses on developing eVTOL aircraft for the U.S. and UAE markets, while Joby Aviation leverages partnerships with Toyota and Delta Airlines for market penetration.
Archer faces challenges with FAA certification timelines and ongoing litigation with Joby Aviation.
Joby Aviation relies on future funding from Toyota and must manage operational challenges related to integrating its Blade acquisition and ramping up production.

facts: Archer is starting production of its first Midnight aircraft at its ARC facility in Covington, GA.
Archer plans to build up to 10 Midnight aircraft this year to support ongoing certification testing programs and deployments with key partners.
Archer unveiled a "Launch Edition" commercialization program for the Midnight aircraft to deploy commercially to dozens of early adopter markets ahead of FAA type certification.
Abu Dhabi Aviation (ADA) is Archer's first Launch Edition customer, with plans to deploy an initial fleet of Midnight aircraft beginning later this year.
Archer will provide ADA with a team of pilots, technicians, and engineers to support the initial operational ramp.
In December, Archer announced an exclusive partnership with Anduril Industries Inc. to jointly develop a hybrid vertical take off and landing (VTOL) aircraft for critical defense applications.
Archer believes potential demand for the hybrid defense aircraft is stronger than expected and expects sizable programs of record without needing FAA certification.
Archer's total liquidity is over $1 billion, including a Q4 2024 ending cash balance of $835 million and $302 million raised through an equity offering in February.
Archer's spending in Q4 2024 remained within its guided range and stayed nearly flat for the second straight quarter.
Archer reported a net loss of $536.8 million for fiscal year 2024.
Archer's total operating expenses were $509.7 million for fiscal year 2024.
Adam Goldstein serves as Archer's founder and CEO.
Jeff Greenwood serves as Archer's Chief Pilot.
The company is designing and developing key enabling technologies and aircraft necessary to power the future of aviation.

facts: Archer’s first-quarter revenue reached $1.6 million with an adjusted EBITDA loss of $172.5 million, surpassing Wall Street’s $175 million projection.
The company’s net loss expanded to $217.7 million amid increased expenditures for FAA certification activities, testing operations, and military contracts.
Archer achieved a historic first by becoming the initial eVTOL manufacturer to finalize Phase 3 in the FAA’s four-stage Type Certification framework during April.

The organization has now advanced to Phase 4 of the FAA certification process, which requires proving its Midnight aircraft complies with FAA safety regulations through rigorous examination and validation.
The firm concluded the first quarter holding approximately $1.8 billion in liquidity and maintains its 2026 target for commencing U.S. commercial flights.
ACHR shares gained roughly 6% during extended trading hours to around $6.96, despite trading 26% lower year-over-year.

First-quarter revenue improved to $1.6 million versus $0.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2025, with proceeds originating from Archer’s broadened activities at Hawthorne Airport in Los Angeles rather than commercial passenger transport.

Chief Executive Adam Goldstein characterized the period as “another banner quarter,” noting tremendous progress toward launching U.S. operations this year and rejecting a narrow air taxi designation for the company.
Archer is building a hybrid military aircraft through a collaboration with Anduril, establishing a parallel development stream beyond its civilian transportation goals.
The company reaffirmed its role as the designated air mobility provider for the LA28 Olympic Games.
Cash reserves decreased by $188.8 million compared to the fourth quarter, with $149.1 million consumed in operational activities and $32.6 million allocated to capital investments.

Analyst projections suggest Archer will consume approximately $600 million throughout 2026 and $740 million in 2027, with positive free cash flow not materializing until 2029 when yearly revenue is forecast to hit $1.6 billion.
For the second quarter, Archer provided guidance calling for an adjusted EBITDA loss between $170 million and $200 million, with the Street’s current estimate at $177 million.

Notably absent from Archer’s Q1 shareholder communication was any reference to the United Arab Emirates, where the company had previously outlined plans to launch commercial operations during 2026 amid ongoing geopolitical complications.
Coverage from five Wall Street analysts yields a Strong Buy consensus rating with an average target price of $14.25, suggesting potential upside of approximately 117% from present trading levels.
source_title: Archer Aviation (ACHR) Stock Jumps 6% on Strong Q1 Earnings and FAA Breakthrough - Parameter

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. is a publicly traded company headquartered in San Jose, California.
The company is developing electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
Its eVTOL aircraft are designed to allow airline operators to transport people in and around cities via an air taxi service.
The aircraft have a claimed range of up to 100 miles (160km).
The aircraft have a claimed speed of up to 150 miles per hour (240km/h).
United Airlines is Archer's first major corporate partner.
United Airlines ordered two hundred Archer electric aircraft.
Maker, Archer's full-size demonstrator aircraft, was unveiled on June 10, 2021, at an event in Los Angeles, California.
Maker is a fully electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft with 12 electric propellers.
Maker features six tilt-props (each with five blades) for forward and VTOL flight.
Maker features six aft propellers that are stationary (each with two blades) for VTOL-only flight.
The aircraft is powered by six independent battery packs.
In November 2021, Archer moved Maker from its headquarters to its flight test facility to start initial test flights.
Maker received its airworthiness certificate to start flight test operations from the FAA in December 2021.
Archer completed its first flight in December 2021.
Archer's aerial ridesharing service, also referred to as Urban Air Mobility (UAM), has been pushed back one year to 2025.
The UAM service is planned to begin operations in Miami, Florida and Los Angeles, California.
Archer is working with Urban Movement Labs and the Los Angeles Department of Transportation to help build necessary infrastructure and service routes.
Archer is working with the City of Miami on similar plans.
Midnight is Archer's planned production aircraft.
The Midnight aircraft was unveiled on November 17, 2022, at an event in Palo Alto, California.
Midnight is a piloted, four-passenger aircraft designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time between flights.
Midnight has 12 propellers: six tilt props in the front of the wing for forward and VTOL flight and six aft propellers that are stationary for VTOL flight only.
Midnight is designed to travel at up to 150mph (240km/h) with a maximum range of 100 miles (161km).
The aircraft is powered by six independent battery packs.
On June 12, 2024, Midnight completed its first transition flight.
Archer flew over 400 test flights in 2024.
The Midnight aircraft's first test flight outside the United States took place in July 2025 at Al Bateen Executive Airport in Abu Dhabi.
The flight evaluated performance under local environmental conditions as part of preparations for regional certification.
In February 2026, Archer Aviation announced plans to integrate Starlink satellite connectivity into its Midnight electric air taxi aircraft.
The system is intended to provide reliable communication between the aircraft and ground operations while supporting future urban air mobility services.
Archer was founded on October 16, 2018, by Adam Goldstein and Brett Adcock to develop electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft.
The company was originally started by Goldstein and Adcock and privately funded.
Marc Lore, a Walmart executive, supported its launch.
In 2022, Brett Adcock departed from both the leadership team and the board.
Adam Goldstein is currently the sole CEO and Chairman of the Board of Directors of the company.
Initially, Archer worked on developing aircraft with the Herbert Wertheim College of Engineering at the University of Florida.
Goldstein and Adcock are alumni of the University of Florida.
Archer operates a research lab on the University of Florida's campus in Gainesville, Florida, which was funded by Goldstein and Adcock.
In August 2022, United Airlines paid Archer a $10 million deposit for 100 electric flying taxis.
On November 10, 2022, Archer and United Airlines announced plans for the first electric air taxi route in the US.
The initial route is between Newark Liberty International Airport and the Downtown Manhattan Heliport.
Archer became an IPO (ACHR) on September 20, 2021.
On November 17, 2022, Archer unveiled details of its production vehicle dubbed "Midnight".
The aircraft is designed to carry passengers on short trips of around 20 miles (32km) between airports and downtown city centers.
In November 2022, Archer unveiled its Midnight production aircraft, an electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft that can carry four passengers and a pilot.
It is designed to be optimized for back-to-back 20-mile trips with a payload of over 1,000 pounds.
Midnight is the evolution of Archer's previous aircraft, Maker.
Midnight is expected to be certified by the FAA in late 2024.
Archer announced its Maker prototype achieved full transition from vertical to horizontal flight on November 29, 2022.
This milestone validated flight control systems and aircraft architecture applicable to the Midnight production aircraft.
In January 2023, Archer announced a partnership with Stellantis, a multinational automobile manufacturer.
Stellantis would provide up to $150 million in equity capital to support Archer's growth.
The partnership includes collaboration on the development and production of Archer's eVTOL aircraft for urban air mobility.
The partnership solidifies Stellantis as Archer's exclusive contract manufacturer for mass production of its eVTOL aircraft.
In March 2023, Archer and United Airlines announced plans for an electric air taxi route between O'Hare International Airport and Vertiport Chicago on the city's near west side.
On June 5, 2024, Archer received its Part 135 Air Carrier and Operator Certificate from the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
In July 2024, Archer and Southwest Airlines established an agreement to develop operational concepts for air taxi networks.
In February 2025, Archer Aviation received its Part 141 certification from the FAA, enabling the company to establish its pilot training academy.
In June 2025, at the Paris Air Show, Archer Aviation and Dubai-based Jetex signed an agreement to collaborate on developing eVTOL facilities.
The collaboration starts in the United Arab Emirates at the Jetex Abu Dhabi private terminal.
Jetex will provide access to its network of private terminals to support the launch of Archer’s Midnight air taxi services in the region.
In July 2025, Archer Aviation's Midnight eVTOL aircraft conducted its first test flight outside the United States at Al Bateen Executive Airport in Abu Dhabi.
The flight tested performance under UAE-specific conditions including high heat, humidity, and dust.
The flight marked a major milestone in advancing Archer's planned urban air mobility operations in the region.
It was supported by the Smart and Autonomous Systems Council (SASC) and observed by senior officials from the UAE General Civil Aviation Authority and related aviation authorities.
In November 2025, Archer acquired Hawthorne Municipal Airport in Los Angeles for US$126 million for use by its EVTOL craft.
Archer's revenue was $1.6 million in Q1 2026 and -$317.3 million in 2023.
Total assets were $578.3 million in 2023 and total equity was $493.3 million in 2023.
The company had 900 employees in 2025.
Key people include Adam Goldstein (CEO), Tom Muniz (COO), and Geoff Bower (CFO).
The company's ISIN is US03945R1023.
The company operates in the Aviation industry.
Archer was founded on October 16, 2018.
Headquarters location is San Jose, California, U.S.
Area served is worldwide.
Products include Electric aircraft, Electric Air Taxi, and Electric Vertical Takeoff And Landing Aircraft.
source_title: Archer Aviation - Wikipedia

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) announced it received the certificate of occupancy for its high-volume manufacturing facility, ARC.
The ARC facility is connected to the Covington Municipal Airport in Georgia.
Archer is currently working to complete the tooling load-in for the initial manufacturing line at the ARC facility.
Production at the ARC facility is planned to begin in early 2025.
Archer aims to ramp production to a rate of two aircraft per month by the end of 2025.
Archer built the ARC facility in close partnership with Stellantis.
Archer and Stellantis are continuing to progress plans to mass-produce Archer's Midnight aircraft.
Stellantis is contributing capital, advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, and experienced personnel to the ARC facility.
The goal is to scale the ARC facility to produce 650 aircraft annually by 2030.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp commented on Archer's choice to build the new manufacturing facility in Newton County.
Governor Kemp reaffirmed Georgia's commitment to working with innovative companies like Archer to create quality jobs and opportunities for years to come.
Congressman Mike Collins of Georgia sits on the House Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
Congressman Mike Collins stated that the completion of Archer's manufacturing facility in Covington highlights Georgia’s leadership in innovation, advanced manufacturing, and job creation.

Congressman Mike Collins expressed excitement to support this transformative chapter in aviation, which will drive progress, deliver economic benefits for Georgia, and create opportunity for everyone involved with and around the facility.
Adam Goldstein, Archer's founder and CEO, stated that the completion of the facility is a testament to the industry shifting from R&D into commercialization.
With construction on ARC complete, Archer's team is focused on the start of production planned for early next year.
Adam Goldstein expressed pride in the Archer team and partners for completing the project fast and on budget.

Stellantis Chief Manufacturing and Supply Chain Officer Arnaud Deboeuf stated that supporting Archer's electric aircraft growth with deep manufacturing expertise is a great opportunity for both companies.
Arnaud Deboeuf congratulated the Archer team on this milestone, calling it a significant step in taking clean mobility to the sky.
source_title: Archer Completes Construction of High-Volume Manufacturing Facility | Aviation Pros

facts: Final assembly is now complete on Archer’s first Midnight aircraft.
Archer is preparing for its planned first flight this summer.
The Midnight aircraft has recently garnered significant attention from the U.S. Department of Defense given its payload capabilities.
This Midnight aircraft will enable Archer to perform critical “company testing” to accelerate and reduce risk on its certification program with the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).
The company plans to begin “for credit” certification testing early next year with piloted Midnight aircraft.
Archer’s strategy allows it to fly many of the same test points needed during piloted “for credit” flight testing to validate the aircraft before FAA witnesses and participates in the testing.
Component manufacturing is already underway for Archer’s conforming Midnight aircraft.
Archer is targeting completion of final assembly of its initial conforming Midnight aircraft in Q4 2023.
Archer is targeting beginning piloted flight test operations in early 2024.
Adam Goldstein, Founder and CEO of Archer, announced the completion of final assembly and stated the aircraft will accelerate and reduce risk on the certification program.
The goal is to enter into service in 2025.
Key strategic partners advancing aircraft development and commercial operations include Stellantis and United Airlines.
The company has established a significant lead over industry peers on manufacturing and commercial operations fronts with the build out of its high-volume manufacturing facility.
Archer’s Midnight aircraft has a range of up to 100 miles.
The aircraft is designed to perform rapid back-to-back flights with minimal charge time in between.
Archer aims to transform inter-city travel by replacing 60-90 minute commutes by car that can take over an hour in traffic with ~10-20 minute electric air taxi flights.
The electric air taxi flights are described as safe, sustainable, low noise and cost competitive with ground transportation.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) has updated on the progress of its high-volume manufacturing facility in Covington, GA.
Archer Aviation confirms its Covington, GA manufacturing facility is on track for completion in 2024.
Last week, Archer displayed its Midnight aircraft at Atlantic Aviation’s new terminal at DeKalb–Peachtree Municipal Airport.
The event welcomed officials from Georgia’s Office of the Governor, Congressional Offices, Atlanta City, State Legislature, Federal Aviation Administration, and local governments.
Construction of the Covington facility is advancing smoothly.
Archer has finished the first phase of construction, which included grading and foundation work.
The second phase of construction, involving the erection of exterior walls and roof, is nearly complete.
The remaining phases of construction, including interior fit-out and equipment installation, are set to finish in the coming months.
Upon completion, the Covington facility will span nearly 400,000 square feet.
The facility aims to produce up to 650 Midnight aircraft yearly, potentially making it one of the aviation industry’s largest manufacturing facilities by volume.
Archer is collaborating closely with Stellantis on this project to develop plans for mass production of the Midnight aircraft.
Adam Goldstein, Archer’s CEO and founder, stated that their teams are working hard to complete the facility by year-end.
Once finished, Archer will focus on scaling production to 650 aircraft annually.
Archer aims to be the first in the eVTOL industry to achieve large-scale manufacturing.
The Midnight is an electric vertical take-off and landing (eVTOL) aircraft and a strong entrant in the burgeoning urban air mobility market.
The Midnight is built around Archer’s proprietary electric powertrain.
The Midnight offers speeds up to 150 mph, turning long ground commutes into quick flights.
The Midnight has capacity for back-to-back 20-50 mile flights with minimal charging time.
The Midnight operates 100 times quieter than helicopters at cruising altitudes.
The Midnight features redundant systems for airline-level safety.
The Midnight has capacity for a pilot, four passengers, and carry-on luggage.
The Midnight aims to provide a sustainable, quiet, and safe alternative to ground transportation.
Urban Air Mobility (UAM) involves using small, highly automated aircraft for passenger or cargo transport within urban and suburban areas.
eVTOL aircraft offer the potential to significantly reduce traffic congestion, increase accessibility, and cut down on travel times.
The UAM sector is currently experiencing rapid development.
Advancements in battery technology, electric propulsion, and autonomous flight systems are paving the way for a new era of urban transportation.
Major aerospace companies and startups are actively developing various eVTOL prototypes, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible.
High cost associated with developing and operating these new technologies is a key challenge before widespread commercialization.
Ensuring the safety of passengers and those on the ground is paramount.
Redundancy systems and robust regulations are crucial to building public trust in UAM.
Integrating UAM into existing urban environments and infrastructure presents a challenge.
Cities will need to consider noise pollution and potential environmental impacts when planning for vertiports, the designated landing and take-off zones for eVTOLs.
The potential benefits of UAM include creating new business models, generating job opportunities, and positively impacting urban planning and development.
source_title: Archer Aviation's Manufacturing Facility On Track for 2024 Completion

facts: Archer Aviation Inc. announced it began construction March 2 on a site adjacent to Covington Municipal Airport for the facility to manufacture electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft.
The company's mission is to unlock the skies.
A ceremonial event will mark the achievement in April.
Santa Clara, California-based Archer is planning a $118 million complex on 96 acres of city-owned land on the western edge of the airport between Williams and Airport roads.
Hiring was expected to begin late this year (2023).
The facility was slated to open in the first three months of 2024, according to a November announcement.
Employment at the facility could reach 1,000 jobs.
The planned 350,000-square-foot facility will be capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The facility is designed for possible expansion by 800,000 square feet, which is estimated to support long-term production targets of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
This type of high-volume manufacturing facility is believed to be a first for the eVTOL industry.

Archer’s manufacturing facility was a joint project between the Newton County Industrial Development Authority, Covington city government, Georgia Department of Economic Development, Metro Atlanta Chamber of Commerce and Georgia Electric Membership Corp. Economic Development Division.
Covington City Council in January approved an agreement for the company to lease the land as part of a tax abatement plan in exchange for producing the jobs and investment it is promising.

The company announced it had approved a local incentives deal that, along with a state Regional Economic Business Assistance (REBA) grant, comprised about one-third of the planned capital investment in the plant.
Georgia was chosen over Texas and others competing for the company’s first manufacturing facility.
Archer announced in January a partnership with Stellantis to make the automaker the exclusive contract manufacturer for mass production of Archer’s eVTOL aircraft.

Company founder and CEO Adam Goldstein said the key to unlocking the promise of the eVTOL aircraft industry is scale, and expressed thrill over the support from the state of Georgia and Newton County.
Goldstein stated they are taking an important step working closely with Stellantis to ramp up high-volume manufacturing capabilities at the new Covington facility.
The partnership with Stellantis will leverage each company’s respective strengths and competencies to bring the Midnight aircraft to market.
Stellantis produces vehicle brands such as Jeep, Ram and Maserati.

Archer brings its team of eVTOL, electric powertrain and certification experts while Stellantis will contribute advanced manufacturing technology and expertise, experienced personnel and capital to the partnership.
The combination is intended to enable the rapid scaling of aircraft production to meet Archer’s commercialization plans.
The partnership allows Archer to strengthen its path to commercialization by helping it avoid hundreds of millions of dollars of spending during the manufacturing ramp-up phase.
Archer is designing and developing electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft for use in urban air mobility networks.
Archer’s mission includes freeing everyone to reimagine how they move and spend time.
source_title: Archer begins construction on Covington vertical takeoff aircraft facility - The Covington News

facts: Morgan Stanley identified the low altitude landscape as an interesting and largely overlooked opportunity in a recent investor note.
Two of the largest categories propping up the low altitude market are electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircrafts and drones.
The low altitude market could be worth $9 trillion by 2050 according to Morgan Stanley's estimates.
Companies such as Archer Aviation or Joby Aviation are some of Wall Street's most popular names in the eVTOL market.
Tesla could be seen as an outside winner from scaling low altitude aircraft given the company's advanced battery technologies.
Other Elon Musk companies such as SpaceX and Starlink could potentially become increasingly involved with defense contracting as it pertains to building satellites or drones.
Tesla, Archer, and Joby have all earned a spot in Cathie Wood's Ark Invest portfolio.
Palantir Technologies is another large position in Cathie Wood's portfolio.
Many investors associate Palantir with the defense sector due to the company's close ties to the U.S. military.
Outside of the Department of Defense (DOD), Palantir has forged a number of unique strategic relationships in commercial defense contracting.
Palantir works with leading drone companies Red Cat Holdings and Anduril.
Archer recently partnered with Palantir in an effort that focuses on designing next-generation aviation systems.
Palantir currently trades near a record-high price-to-sales (P/S) multiple of 111.
The low altitude opportunity bridges the aviation and defense industries, two industries not traditionally considered core AI markets.
Palantir is uniquely positioned to scale both its commercial and government operations as low altitude services become more digitized.
Palantir's potential applications include areas such as commercial aviation, autonomous systems development, and logistics coordination in defense environments.
Palantir's software tools are used by the U.S. military, with applications generally revolving around data aggregation, efficient decision-making, and safety protocols.

The author believes shares may be overbought at the moment but remains optimistic about Palantir's future and its potential to use technical expertise to continue forming use cases beyond traditional data analytics for enterprises.
The low altitude opportunity represents a chance to transform legacy industries through technology, particularly in overlooked areas touching AI.

facts: Archer Aviation Inc., a leader in electric vertical takeoff and landing (eVTOL) aircraft, announced plans to locate its manufacturing facility in Covington, Georgia.
The selected site is adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport.
Archer plans to initially build out a 350,000-square-foot facility.
The facility will be located on a 96-acre site.
The initial facility is capable of producing up to 650 aircraft per year.
The planned eVTOL aircraft manufacturing facility is expected to create over 1,000 jobs over the long term.
Construction is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024.
Initial production is expected to begin in the second half of 2024.
The facility will be capable of being expanded by an additional 550,000 square feet.
The expansion is estimated to support production of up to 2,300 aircraft per year.
In selecting the location for its first manufacturing facility, Archer completed a comprehensive survey of potential sites across the United States.

Key site selection considerations included the availability of talent in the local labor market, utility availability, ability to conduct seamless flight test operations, construction costs, and logistics.
Archer will receive an incentive package that includes land conveyance, tax incentives, and a Georgia REBA grant.
Archer anticipates that financing for the project will be provided by Synovus, the largest bank headquartered in Georgia.
Archer CEO Adam Goldstein stated that after many months of discussion and evaluation, they are excited to select Georgia as the location for their manufacturing facility.
Goldstein noted that the U.S. has long led the world in aerospace technology and Georgia has played a vital role in that industry.

Goldstein stated that Archer's eVTOL technology can transform how urban and rural communities live and commute, and the factory can create pathways to highly skilled manufacturing jobs and other ladders of social and economic opportunity.
Goldstein expressed gratitude to Governor Kemp along with Bob Kosek, Sara Gershon, Serra Hall, Frank Turner, and their teams for working together to pioneer innovative aviation technologies.
Governor Brian P. Kemp commented that Georgia has made nurturing innovation a priority as they work with businesses to define the future.
Kemp stated that Georgia's established leadership in the aerospace industry was essential to landing Archer Aviation in Newton County.
Kemp expressed thrill at welcoming Archer to Georgia, where they can take advantage of the signature workforce training program Quick Start to help fill over 1,000 jobs in Covington long-term.
Georgia is home to more than 800 aerospace companies.

Georgia maintains a robust aerospace education pipeline featuring a dozen high schools offering training in the field, five technical colleges with aviation programs, and several universities offering degrees in aerospace engineering.

The Georgia Center of Innovation for Aerospace, a strategic arm of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, has been cultivating an ecosystem of opportunities for innovative companies like Archer for more than 10 years.
source_title: Archer Aviation Selects Site in Georgia Adjacent to the Covington Municipal Airport for its Manufacturing Facility | Aviation Pros

facts: May 2026 US auto sales are projected to reach approximately 1.44 million units, according to S&P Global Mobility.

This translates to an estimated sales pace of 15.8 million units (seasonally adjusted annual rate: SAAR), down slightly from the March (16.2M) and April (15.9M) readings but above the year-ago result (May 2025 – 15.6M) for the first time in seven months.
S&P Global Mobility projects US auto sales volumes to reach 15.8 million units in 2026, representing an estimated decline of more than 3% from the 2025 level of 16.38 million units.
The 15.8-million-unit setting is slightly downgraded from previous expectations.

Chris Hopson, manager of North American light vehicle sales forecasting for S&P Global Mobility, stated that auto sales remain steady and solid in wake of swirling geopolitical and new vehicle affordability conditions.

Sales pace over the past three months has remained relatively constant, with underlying movement towards more fuel-efficient vehicles, specifically hybrid electric vehicles (HEV) as gasoline prices remain high, while consumer spending retrenchment signals are still being watched.
A notable downshift for BEV sales and market share was expected through the first half of 2026 as automakers and consumers adjust to post-incentive conditions.
Rising fuel prices since March have renewed interest in fuel efficient vehicles.

BEV share of new sales in March and April has not reflected a meaningful advance, though BEV sales are up from immediate post-incentive expiration results realized from September 25-February 26 but remain down from year-ago levels.

OEM adjustments regarding inventory and strategy assessments could be playing a role in new vehicle BEV share, while reports of a rise in BEV activity in the used vehicle sector may limit stronger new BEV sales advances despite surging fuel prices.
The roll out of new BEV products over the course of 2026 should provide additional momentum for BEV sales alongside uncertainty on how high and long fuel prices will remain.

The 2026 US auto sales market will continue to contend with affordability pressures and the impact of shifting EV policies, with S&P Global Mobility monitoring the evolving environment as manufacturers and consumers adapt to changing incentive landscapes.

facts: Aurubis AG broke ground on June 17, 2022, for the United States' first large secondary smelter for multimetal recycling.
Total investment in Augusta, Richmond County in Georgia is estimated at €300 million (approx. $320 million).
The plant is expected to be fully operational by mid-2024.
Aurubis Richmond will create over 120 new jobs directly.
Once fully on-line, the facility will have the capacity to process about 90,000 tons of complex recycling materials annually.
The materials processed include printed circuit boards, copper cables, and other recycling materials containing metals.
Aurubis is a leading provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.

Roland Harings, CEO of Aurubis AG, stated the company has doubled down on commitments to use its technology and experience to become a forerunner for recycling valuable materials containing copper, nickel, tin, and other industrial and precious metals locally in the US.
The US currently produces about 6 million tons of recycling materials that contain valuable metals.
Aurubis aims to take advantage of this potential and ensure these materials get reused as critical input materials for many industries in the US.
Roland Harings noted that current supply cannot meet demand for metals such as copper, and the US is lacking recycling capacities.
The Augusta facility will reduce waste and direct key metals to industry including renewables such as solar and wind, along with electric vehicle and battery manufacturers.
Copper is 100-percent recyclable without any loss in performance.
Secondary smelter recycling at an industrial scale requires significant long-term investment.
Aurubis' state-of-the-art environmental technology meets or exceeds the highest State of Georgia and federal government standards.

Aurubis Richmond will have a very low impact on air quality, estimated at less than 0.2% share of dust emissions in Richmond county based on the actual emissions at the Aurubis recycling site in Germany.
Governor Brian Kemp stated this is the largest single investment made by a German company in Georgia history.
The facility is expected to become a major player in the North American supply chain and a significant employer in greater Augusta, GA.
Aurubis' metals are crucial enablers to promote global market trends such as renewable energy, digitalization and electric vehicles.

The company is making a targeted investment to expand its own recycling business by building this plant.

facts: Georgia’s largest-ever single German investment is getting even larger.

Aurubis SA, the Hamburg-based company set to put a recycling facility in Augusta by 2024, announced a second phase that will significantly increase its planned expenditure, provide 80 more jobs and double its output of blister copper used as a raw material across many pivotal industries.
Aurubis initially announced in November that it would invest 300 million euros (about $340 million at the time) and hire 125 people for the first phase of the greenfield site.
Much of the infrastructure work at the Augusta site has been completed since a groundbreaking in June.

The company announced on Wednesday that it would add another 250 million euros (about $264.5 million) to the project as demand outstrips projections from when the project was first envisioned 18 months ago.
Aurubis’s board approved an additional 90 million euros to adjust for higher-than-expected construction costs.
Upon completion, Aurubis will have invested a total of 640 million euros (about $679 million) and will employ 200 people at the site in Richmond County.
CEO Roland Harings stated that because Aurubis produces raw metals rather than finished goods, the expansion was not directly aimed at qualifying for incentives under the Inflation Reduction Act.

Domestically produced copper and other metals are essential to shoring up supply chains and helping EV battery companies qualify for federal tax credits, which bolsters Aurubis's rationale for expanding its U.S. presence.

CEO Roland Harings stated that the world needs recyclable metals like copper for cars, mobile phones, batteries, cables, and computers, and today's high recycling demand cannot be met by existing facilities.
Harings noted that Aurubis's increased commitment to the U.S. will be a game-changer for the environment, manufacturers, and the transition to a renewable economy.
In Augusta, Japanese copper foil supplier Denkai announced a $400 million investment following Aurubis's announcement.
Recycling demand continues to grow at more than 5 percent per year in the U.S.
Hyundai Motor announced a $5.5 billion Meta Plant for batteries and EV assembly near Savannah.
Hyundai later agreed to a $4 billion-plus joint-venture battery facility with SK On in Bartow County.
Norway's FREYR battery is building a $2.6 billion plant in Newnan.
An electric vehicle uses more than twice as much copper as a gas-burning vehicle, partly due to more wiring and cathode material inside the battery.
Harings stated that massive investments are building up positive neighborhoods, environments, and industrial value chains in the country, and Aurubis is convinced it can play an important role.
Aurubis will use massive rotating smelters called top blown rotary converters (two planned for each phase) in a pyrometallurgical process to separate materials.
The facility's first phase is set to start production in the second half of 2024 with an output of 90,000 tons of blister copper.
By 2026, when the second phase comes online, capacity will double to 180,000 tons.
The goal is to establish a modular system that allows Aurubis to add capacity when opportunities arise.
Construction has faced challenges due to labor shortages and rising costs, though foundation work and pilings are nearly finished and drainage preparation is almost complete.
CEO Roland Harings noted that the project is on track despite real challenges in the construction supply chain because many other companies are also building.
Aurubis's gross profit before taxes reached 532 million euros during 2021-22, representing a 40 percent increase largely driven by rising metal prices.
The company is financing its expansion through its own cash flow and existing credit facilities, absorbing no debt for the Georgia project.
Aurubis faces no exchange rate worries because it has ample access to U.S. dollars through a "natural hedge" from selling metals on global markets denominated in dollars.
Harings stated that the company likes a strong U.S. dollar versus the euro.
Aurubis eventually plans to build a solar farm on or near its Georgia site, similar to practices at some of its European plants.
Getting the factory up and running and delivering metals to market currently takes priority over the solar farm objective.
A recycling infrastructure ecosystem, including business partners that sort printed circuit boards, wiring, and other materials, will set up near the Aurubis plant to extend its economic impact.

facts: German copper producer Aurubis is expanding its Augusta facility even further.

facts: Aurubis AG's Supervisory Board resolved further capital expenditure of about €530 million to double processing capacity at its Richmond recycling site in Georgia, US.
About €90 million in additional capital expenditure adjustments for infrastructure requirements and inflation have been approved specifically for the Richmond plant.
The Richmond plant will receive a second recycling module with a capital expenditure volume of about €250 million.
The expansion stage at the Richmond site is scheduled to start production in 2026.
The previous throughput volume of 90,000 tons of complex recycling materials will be raised to 180,000 tons due to the doubling of the expansion stage.
The expansion follows the highest environmental standards and enables environmentally friendly processing of the input materials.
Aurubis is creating about 80 new jobs in Richmond as part of the expansion.
After the completion of both recycling modules, the Richmond site will employ about 200 people.
