facts: Supply contract for a second plant module at the new Aurubis Richmond recycling plant in Georgia was signed as part of a strategic partnership.
The plant utilizes a modular system that allows for flexible operations and quick growth in the dynamic US recycling market.
Aurubis's investment aims to close essential raw material cycles.
Multimetal producer and copper recycler Aurubis AG and Düsseldorf-based plant and machine manufacturer SMS group GmbH are expediting the development of the US recycling market for non-ferrous metals.
The two companies signed a supply contract for a second plant module for the new Aurubis Richmond recycling plant in Georgia.
Aurubis is considerably accelerating its growth course in the future recycling and circular economy market.
Additional equipment will increase the capacity of the metal recycling plant in Richmond, currently under construction, from an annual 90,000 tons to 180,000 tons of complex metal scrap materials.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings stated that the next expansion stage of their new site in Georgia delivers on their “Driving Sustainable Growth” strategy.
The project aims to keep strategically important and highly demanded mineral raw materials in the resource cycle.
SMS group recycling equipment is designated for the second module of the new Aurubis plant in the US.
The core technology is a top-blown rotary converter (TBRC), which processes complex recycling materials to recover raw materials like copper, nickel, tin, precious metals, and platinum group metals.
Recovered metals serve as crucial raw materials for future technologies, including energy transition and sustainable mobility.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings noted that bundling SMS group's plant know-how with Aurubis' expertise creates an environment for highly efficient metal recovery.
The partnership leverages the enormous potential of the US recycling market, with SMS group's backing ensuring professional and timely project realization.
SMS Executive Board member Michael Rzepczyk stated that the plant helps Aurubis assume a leading role in US multimetal recycling.
Planned production volume doubling at the new US site takes recycling to the next level in the US, according to Michael Rzepczyk.
The partners aim to build a more sustainable future by closing value chains and maximizing valuable metal recovery from recycling materials.
The recycling technology features modularity, allowing additional components to be added to expand the plant to meet future requirements.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings explained that the modular system enables a prudent market strategy to flexibly respond to US needs and benefit from constant market growth.
For approximately €640 million across both phases, Aurubis is building the first secondary smelter specializing in multimetal recycling in the US.
Groundbreaking for the Aurubis Richmond plant occurred in mid-2022, with construction running at full steam.
Stage one of the plant is scheduled to go online in the first half of 2024.
The TBRC equipment is designated for the second stage, which is anticipated to start production in 2026.
The Georgia investment supports Aurubis' sustainability strategy, complementing its existing annual processing of roughly 1 million tons of recycling material.
The Aurubis Richmond recycling plant will add an additional 180,000 tons of processing capacity annually.
The Georgia investment will help meet group growth targets, comply with the highest US environmental standards, and create around 200 new jobs.
Aurubis CEO Roland Harings emphasized that economic growth and comprehensive corporate responsibility go hand in hand.
Aurubis AG is a leading global provider of non-ferrous metals and one of the largest copper recyclers worldwide.
The company processes complex metal concentrates, scrap metals, organic/inorganic metal-bearing recycling materials, and industrial residues into high-quality metals.
Aurubis produces over 1 million tons of copper cathodes annually, yielding products like wire rod, continuous cast shapes, profiles, and flat rolled copper/copper alloy products.
Aurubis also produces precious metals, selenium, lead, nickel, tin, and zinc.
The product portfolio includes additional items such as sulfuric acid and iron silicate.
Sustainability is fundamental to Aurubis' strategy, guided by the maxim "Aurubis responsibly transforms raw materials into value," integrating sustainable conduct into its corporate culture.
The company's sustainability approach involves careful resource management, responsible social/ecological conduct, and sensible growth.
Aurubis has around 6,900 employees, production sites in Europe and the USA, and a global distribution network.
Aurubis shares are listed on the German Stock Exchange's Prime Standard Segment, MDAX, Global Challenges Index (GCX), and STOXX Europe 600.
SMS group is renowned worldwide for future-oriented technologies and outstanding service for the metals industry.
SMS group leverages 150 years of experience and digital know-how to provide innovative products/processes, generating worldwide sales of around €2.6 billion.
SMS group supports customers throughout plant lifecycles to enable profitable and resource-efficient value creation chains.
Paving the way for a carbon-neutral and sustainable metals industry is SMS group's stated goal.
SMS group employs 14,500 people globally.

facts: Aurubis AG broke ground on its first large secondary smelter for multimetal recycling in the United States in Augusta, Georgia.

facts: Aurubis has opened the first secondary smelter specializing in multimetal recycling in the US.

Operations are conducted with high regard for air, water, and wildlife protection, aligning with Aurubis’ mission of being a good environmental steward.
Aurubis has a strategic vision to be carbon-neutral well before 2050.

The ribbon-cutting ceremony emphasized Aurubis Richmond’s role in contributing to a circular economy by supplying essential metals to key industries such as renewable energy, electric vehicles, and battery manufacturing.
The facility is expected to play a critical role in the North American supply chain, cementing Aurubis’ position as a global leader in multimetal recycling.
Aurubis Richmond is committed to being a major employer in the greater Augusta area, providing over 230 high-quality jobs and contributing to the local economy.
The company pledged a community benefit agreement with Augusta Technical College, Aiken Technical College, and the Richmond County School System.
As part of the community benefit agreement, Aurubis Richmond will invest in scholarships and job training programs for young workers.

source_title: Press release: Aurubis celebrates ribbon-cutting at the first multimetal recycling ... | Presseportal

facts: Businesses investing in Right to Work Georgia include Aurubis (a metal recycling company), American Peanut Growers, and Remington.
Aurubis is adding a new location soon in Augusta.
Remington is locating its headquarters in LaGrange.
American Peanut Growers is expanding in Donalsonville.
Altogether, these three investments will create 1,071 new jobs for the state.
Aurubis is investing $340 million to add a new location in Right to Work Augusta, Georgia.
The Aurubis investment will create 125 new jobs.

Aurubis states that with this investment, they are setting a clear example of sustainable growth and will become a forerunner for multi-metal recycling in the U.S., a role they already hold in Europe.
The Georgia Ports Authority is playing a role in bringing jobs and investment to Georgia.
Exports produced by the Aurubis recycling operation will strengthen the state’s global position via Savannah, which is already one of the nation’s most balanced ports in terms of import-export trade.
Aurubis sought site size, full utility services, and workforce; the corporate park and workforce were positives in Augusta’s favor.
American Peanut Growers Group is investing $85 million to expand in Right to Work Donalsonville, Georgia.
The American Peanut Growers expansion will create 90 new jobs.
Agriculture is Georgia’s oldest and largest industry and continues to be a major driver of economic prosperity in every corner of the state.
Georgia is perennially the number one state in the nation in the production of peanuts.
Partners at the Development Authority of Seminole County and Donalsonville supported American Peanut Growers Group's investment.
Remington is adding headquarters and manufacturing in Right to Work LaGrange, Georgia.
The Remington investment totals $100 million.
The Remington investment will create 856 new jobs.
Georgia’s firearms industry is responsible for thousands of jobs and millions of dollars of investment in communities.
A state official welcomes Remington to the Peach State, noting they are a proud owner of some of Remington’s first-class products.
The official looks forward to seeing the oldest firearms manufacturer in America thrive in Georgia’s pro-business environment.
For more than two centuries, Remington Arms has been part of the fabric of the country.
A trusted brand like Remington has been interwoven into the lives of many who grew up with a connection to the outdoors and outdoor sports.
Remington Firearms will locate their global headquarters and advanced manufacturing operations in Georgia, joining a strong community of great companies in the state.
source_title: Recap: Peanuts, Guns, and Metal for Right to Work Georgia! | NILRR

facts: Aurubis Richmond initiated a multimetal recycling plant project in Augusta, Georgia, in 2022 as a $340-million investment.
By the end of 2022, Aurubis expanded its plans and doubled the investment for the Augusta facility.
The officially open multimetal recycling plant represents an $800-million project and is the first of its kind in the United States.
The facility grinds up electronic material and metals to create new metals for other uses.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp attended the ribbon-cutting ceremony at the Augusta plant.
The project will bring approximately 235 jobs to the area.
Governor Kemp emphasized that the company focuses on bringing jobs paying over the average county wage, confirming these positions meet that standard.
Aurubis Health and Safety Coordinator Tessie Wilkerson stated the facility employs people from everywhere, including experts from Germany.
Tessie Wilkerson noted there is significant room for growth and expansion in the near future at the plant.
Augusta Mayor Garnett Johnson attended the ribbon-cutting alongside current employees and local leaders.
Mayor Johnson recalled a ground-breaking ceremony held on June 17, 2022, in an open field over two years prior to the article's publication.
Mayor Johnson described Aurubis as a community partner that utilizes world-class technology, protects the environment, and creates jobs.
Aurubis CEO Toralf Haag identified Georgia as an ideal location for growth due to government support and local colleges supplying the workforce.
Toralf Haag cited additional advantages including good construction support, a strong labor market, a favorable environment, and proximity to ports, interstate highways, and the Augusta airport.
The plant plans to process more than 180,000 tons of materials annually.
Governor Kemp stated the facility will supply rare earth minerals, copper, aluminum, and other materials needed to build vehicle batteries.
The German-based company aims to continue its sustainability projects and expand throughout the United States.
source_title: Georgia unveils Aurubis' first U.S. Multimetal Recycling Plant | News | wfxg.com

facts: Aurubis and other recent CSRA business expansions have generated an $800 million economic impact in the region.

Governor Brian P. Kemp, in conjunction with the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), has announced that the state has been recognized as the No. 1 state for business by Area Development magazine for the 12th year in a row.
The recognition marks an unmatched record of success and consistency in economic development for the state.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that site selectors who decide where companies invest have voted Georgia as the ‘Top State for Business’ in this annual poll from the leading publication for these decisionmakers for 12 straight years.
Governor Kemp noted that while the ranking has been earned for over a decade, it is not guaranteed and reflects a sustained commitment to excellence and a team approach to economic development.
Governor Kemp thanked the GDEcD team, legislative partners, and local leaders and economic developers across the state for making the achievement possible on behalf of hardworking Georgians.
The 2025 Top States for Doing Business rankings from Area Development are determined by scores in 15 categories based on input from approximately 50 leading site consultants nationwide.

Lt. Governor Burt Jones stated that the announcement reflects the partnership between Governor Kemp’s Office, the legislature, state agencies, and local leaders and economic developers across the state.
Lt. Governor Jones expressed gratitude for all of the hardworking Georgians who continue to show up every day and invest in their future and the betterment of all Georgians.
Lt. Governor Jones highlighted that Georgia's workforce continues to excel and serves as an example of why Georgia is the best state to live, work, and raise a family.
Georgia ranked in the top five in 10 categories and claimed the overall No. 1 ranking.
The Top States for Doing Business Survey is the 16th in the annual series.

Building on this recognition, Georgia continues investing in future opportunities through the Rural Site Development Initiative, which helps local communities prepare development-ready industrial sites.
source_title: Make it 12 in a row for business success - Augusta Business Daily

facts: GM autonomous vehicle (AV) and robotaxi subsidiary Cruise has deployed its Cruise AVs to Atlanta, Georgia.
The white and salmon Chevy Bolt EV-based self-driving vehicles have arrived in Atlanta for testing.
Atlanta is the eighth city where Cruise is now operating its AV robotaxis.
The Cruise AVs will only be involved in initial testing there for the time being.
Cruise says “we can’t wait to see what each neighborhood has to offer” with regards to Atlanta.
The announcement comes less than a week after Cruise began testing its driverless taxis in Nashville, Tennessee.
Company CEO Kyle Vogt pointed out at the time that Nashville is the seventh metropolis where Cruise has established a presence in the course of a year.
Cruise AV operations began in San Francisco, California before expanding to Phoenix, Arizona and Austin, Texas shortly prior to the end of calendar 2022.
Robotaxis arrived in two more Texas cities, Houston and Dallas, in spring 2023.

These deployments all took place on the west coast or in the southwest until Cruise brought its autonomous vehicles to the eastern seaboard with its expansion to a sixth city, Miami, in late July 2023.
Cruise continues its advance through the warmer cities of the eastern U.S.
Cruise has achieved several important milestones along the way, such as surpassing three million driverless miles traveled by its AVs without significant accidents resulting.
The robotaxi service also recently claimed to have 390 AVs operational.
CEO Kyle Vogt claimed this fleet size is the “largest and fastest-growing AV fleet in the world.”
While Cruise is expanding its robotaxi testing and service area steadily using its current Cruise AV, it continues to await NHTSA approval for its purpose-built Cruise Origin autonomous vehicle.
The Cruise Origin is a roomy vehicle with a pair of facing bench seats to carry passengers and sliding side doors for easy access.
Deployment of the Cruise Origin will be key to Cruise’s goal of generating $1 billion in annual revenue by 2025.
Without NHTSA approval, Cruise has only been able to test a human-operated variant of the Origin in Austin.
Meanwhile, Cruise’s operating cost per mile is improving, approaching the under-$1 per mile mark.
The subsidiary’s CEO claims this under-$1 per mile mark is “the magic threshold at which robots actually become cheaper for most people than owning a car.”
source_title: GM's Cruise AVs Now Testing In Atlanta, Georgia

facts: Amazon Web Services has unveiled plans to invest $11 billion in Georgia to enhance its AI and cloud computing infrastructure.
The investment will fund the construction of cutting-edge data centers in Butts and Douglas counties.
The project is intended to create 550 direct high-tech jobs.
The project will also create many indirect construction and supply chain jobs.

Roger Wehner, vice president of economic development at AWS, stated that AWS's ongoing infrastructure investments across the United States demonstrate a commitment to powering customers' digital innovation through cloud and AI technologies.
AWS expects the planned multibillion-dollar investment to create more than 550 well-paying jobs and drive significant economic growth for Georgia.
The initiative showcases Georgia's increasing relevance as a cutting-edge, digital hub.
The infrastructure will support workloads for various AI applications, including machine learning models and generative AI applications.
Dr. Romona Jackson Jones, Douglas County Commission Chairwoman, welcomed AWS bringing secure, resilient, and advanced cloud computing technology to the county.
Dr. Jackson Jones noted the investment will bring hundreds of high-paying, high-tech jobs that will benefit the community and citizens long-term.

Dr. Jackson Jones described the initial investment as a game-changer that will place the community on the forefront of innovation and establish Georgia as a leader in technology, innovation, and artificial intelligence.
AWS has contributed investments of over $18.5 billion to Georgia since 2010.
Beyond job creation, AWS has promised disaster relief initiatives.
AWS has pledged renewable energy projects, including the Bulldog Solar farm.
AWS has committed to STEM education programs such as Girls’ Tech Day and Think Big Spaces.
Russ Crumbley, chairman of the Butts County Board of Commissioners, expressed pleasure that Amazon Web Services chose to locate new data centers in Butts County.
Crumbley stated the investment will be the most significant in Butts County history and represents a collaborative effort involving local government entities.
The expansion aligns with AWS’s wider strategy of innovating and sustaining operations across its platform.
AWS plans to utilize robust infrastructure, skilled workforce development, and educational partnerships to solidify Georgia's position as a leading technology state.
source_title: AWS Commits  Billion to AI and Cloud Expansion in Georgia

facts: The Hyundai Metaplant has officially opened its doors in Bryan County, Ga.
The grand opening festivities mark the culmination of a project nearly three years in the making.
The facility was originally announced in 2022.
The Metaplant is expected to create 14,000 direct jobs in the area.
Georgia Governor Brian Kemp and Representative Buddy Carter (GA-01) attended the opening of the over $7 billion facility alongside Hyundai executives.
As of March 24, there are 1,232 employees at the plant, not counting Korean expats.
Seventy-two percent of the current workforce is from the Greater Savannah area, within a 60-mile radius of the plant.
The total number of Georgia employees is 1,077, which makes up 87% of the current workforce.
One hundred fifty-five employees are from outside of the state.
Eight hundred ninety-five employees are from the greater Savannah area.
Four hundred thirteen employees (33%) are from Chatham County.
Ninety-one employees (7%) are from Bryan County.
One hundred eighty-five employees (15%) are from Bulloch County.
One hundred three employees (8%) are from Effingham County.
Joze Munoz, President and CEO of Hyundai Motor Company, announced plans to increase production capacity from 300,000 units per year to 500,000 units per year.
The increased production target requires the workforce to grow closer to 14,000 employees instead of the originally planned 8,500.
The plant will not require a new building construction but will need a significant investment in equipment to support the expansion.
Current production stands at 250 cars per day.
The Hyundai Metaplant is considered one of the biggest economic projects in Georgia's history.
source_title: Hyundai Metaplant holds grand opening

canonical_name: Georgia Autonomous Vehicle Laws and Regulations
facts: Georgia has taken steps to enable the testing and deployment of autonomous vehicles (AVs) while balancing safety, liability, and public policy.
In Georgia, an autonomous vehicle refers to a motor vehicle equipped with automated driving systems capable of performing some or all driving tasks with limited or no human intervention.
The state distinguishes between vehicles that require a licensed driver in the operator’s seat and those that can operate with a remote or onboard autonomous system.
For regulatory purposes, AVs are classed based on their level of autonomy, the presence of a safety operator, and their ability to handle driving tasks under varied conditions.
Georgia has established channels to regulate the testing and deployment of AVs to ensure safety and accountability.
Key regulatory elements typically include a formal testing program, mandatory reporting, and adherence to performance standards.
Operators may need to obtain permission from relevant state agencies, such as the Department of Public Safety (DPS) or the Department of Transportation, before testing on public roads.
The approval process often involves submitting safety plans, a description of testing routes, vehicle specifications, and a description of the remote or onboard control system.
For deployment beyond testing, a broader authorization or waiver may be required to operate on public roadways without a traditional driver present in the vehicle.
Georgia law generally requires AV operators to demonstrate financial responsibility to cover potential damages or injuries arising from autonomous driving operations.
Operators typically must maintain appropriate automobile liability insurance coverage that extends to AV use and ensure that the vehicle and its operator meet registration requirements.
Operators should verify the exact coverage limits, naming entities responsible for the autonomous system, and any self-insurance provisions that may apply for fleets or research programs.
Clear documentation helps allocate liability between the vehicle owner, the operator, and the manufacturer in the event of an incident.
Liability for AV operations in Georgia often hinges on causation and fault in the event of a crash or safety incident.
The state emphasizes safety standards for automated systems, including robust testing data, safety case documentation, and post-incident reporting.
Operators are expected to implement cybersecurity measures to protect vehicle software, sensors, and data streams from tampering.
Safety evaluations typically cover perception, decision-making, and control modules, and may require ongoing monitoring for performance degradation.
AV operations generate substantial data, including sensor feeds, mapping, localization, and vehicle health information.
Georgia’s framework encourages responsible data handling, including privacy protections for individuals and transparent data-sharing practices with regulators and partners.
Cybersecurity requirements are emphasized to safeguard systems against intrusion, spoofing, or manipulation of autonomous controls.
Operators should implement encryption, secure software update channels, and routine security audits to maintain integrity and protect consumer trust.
In addition to state-level rules, Georgia cities and counties may impose local ordinances governing AV operations, curbside usage, or rights-of-way access.
Operators should consult municipal codes to determine permitted testing times, required signage, and any restrictions near schools, hospitals, or dense pedestrian zones.
Coordinated efforts between state agencies and local governments promote smoother deployment and clearer expectations for operators.
Georgia typically expects AV programs to meet higher technical standards, including redundancy in critical sensors, fail-safe mechanisms, and reliable remote operation capabilities when applicable.
Vehicle manufacturers and operators should provide documentation on system architecture, sensor fusion algorithms, decision logic, and testing results.
Regular maintenance and software updates are essential to maintain safety performance over the vehicle’s lifecycle.
A clear change-management process helps ensure that system updates do not introduce new risks.
Best practices for AV testing in Georgia include establishing a transparent testing plan, maintaining an incident log, and sharing anonymized performance data with regulators.
Operators should develop a robust human-machine interface to manage any required human oversight and ensure that safety drivers are properly trained if they are present.

Data disclosure policies, including what data is collected, how long it is stored, and who can access it, should align with state privacy expectations while enabling regulators to review safety performance.
Georgia is actively evaluating updates to its autonomous vehicle framework as technology matures.

Potential future developments include expanded operating domains, broader exemptions for fully autonomous operations, standardized certifications for AV components, and harmonization with national standards.

Stakeholders anticipate ongoing collaboration among the Georgia Department of Public Safety, Georgia Department of Transportation, insurers, and industry groups to refine testing pathways, liability rules, and consumer protections.
Operators should monitor regulatory alerts and maintain flexible compliance strategies to adapt to policy changes.
Operators should verify whether their vehicle configuration requires a licensed driver or qualifies for remote operation.
Operators must obtain the appropriate testing or deployment approvals from state authorities.
Operators need to secure adequate liability insurance and confirm vehicle registration status.
Operators must implement robust safety and cybersecurity measures.
Operators should establish data governance and privacy practices.
Operators should check local ordinances in any testing area.
Operators must document safety cases, incident reports, and performance data.
Operators should stay informed about policy developments that could expand or restrict AV operations in Georgia.

Current regulations and guidance can be consulted through official sources such as the Georgia Department of Public Safety, the Georgia Department of Transportation, and relevant state legislative portals.
Industry associations and research institutions may publish summaries of Georgia AV laws and ongoing regulatory efforts.
source_title: Autonomous Vehicle Laws in Georgia – Bridge Legal

facts: During Fiscal Year 2023, total investments in facility expansions and new locations across Georgia totaled more than $24 billion.
Fiscal Year 2023 resulted in 38,400 new jobs through 426 projects supported by the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s GlobalCommerce team.
This marks the third consecutive year that Georgia broke records for economic development during Fiscal Year 2023.

When excluding the two largest projects in state history, Rivian and Hyundai Motor Group, from previous year's job creation figures, total jobs as a result of FY23 projects exceeded the previous record by more than 2,800 new positions.
Annual investment totals have increased by 131 percent over the last three years.
Eighty-two percent of new jobs created and more than $20 billion in investments are directed to communities outside the 10-county Atlanta region.
Metro Atlanta attracted $3.6 billion in investment during the last fiscal year.
Expansions account for 71 percent of total projects, while new locations account for 65 percent of new jobs announced across the state.
Between July 2018 and July 2023, at least 40 e-mobility projects have been announced in Georgia.
These e-mobility projects commit more than 30,000 jobs and over $25 billion in investment to the state.
Job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent in FY23 compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Mobility and cleantech projects delivered three investments of $1 billion or more during FY23.
Mobility and cleantech projects accounted for six of the largest projects by job creation in FY23.
The Georgia Center of Innovation connects public and private partners to foster innovation in strategic ecosystems.

Partnerships on workforce solutions like the “Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development” (GRAD) certification and Georgia Quick Start programs spurred a 112 percent growth in advanced manufacturing investments since FY21.
source_title: Georgia Record Economic Development – MEAG Power

facts: Randy Lemasters of Statistical Surveys was presented the NATM Outstanding Associate Member Award.
The NATM Outstanding Associate Member Award is presented annually to an NATM trailer manufacturing associate member recognizing his or her contributions to NATM and trailer manufacturing industry.
Load Trail, LLC, of Sumner, Texas, was awarded the NATM Green Manufacturing Award.
The award recognizes innovative solutions for trailer manufacturing with reduced impact on the environment.
PPG Industries Inc is a founding sponsor of the Green Manufacturing Award and annually donates $5000 to the charity selected by the award recipient.
Ron Yarnell, OEM sales manager at PPG Industries Inc, assisted NATM president Mike Lloyd with the presentation of the award and check to Load Trail.
Load Trail selected Roots and Wings International Ministries of Honey Grove, Texas, to receive the $5000 contribution from the Green Manufacturing Award.
Load Trail received the Green Manufacturing Award for changing its pretreatment chemistry (iron phosphate) to a technically advanced, non-regulated zirconium pretreatment.
The new pretreatment was specifically formulated for Load Trail and named “Load Trail Green Z.”
source_title: NATM Convention in New Orleans draws 869 attendees and 278 exhibitors, and features 230 booths | Trailer Body Builders

facts: Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing has one WARN filing on record in Georgia, affecting 100 workers.
The facility closure is located at Cordele 502 S Midway Rd, Cordele, Georgia.
The mass layoff affects exactly 100 workers.
The effective date of the plant closing is January 16, 2026.
The WARN notice was filed on February 5, 2026.
Employers with 100 or more employees must provide 60 days written notice before mass layoffs or plant closings.
Workers who received less than 60 days advance notice may be entitled to back pay and benefits for each day of the shortfall.
Affected workers are advised to file for unemployment insurance with their state workforce agency as soon as possible.
Affected workers can review COBRA options to continue health coverage for up to 18 months.
America's Job Centers provide free career counseling, resume help, interview prep, job search assistance, and retraining vouchers for workers affected by WARN Act layoffs.
CareerOneStop offers free career counseling, resume help, job search assistance, and retraining programs.
TopResume provides career experts who evaluate resumes and provide actionable feedback within 48 hours.
Workers can sign up to receive a daily digest when Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing files a new WARN notice.
source_title: Big Tex Trailer Manufacturing Layoffs 2026 - WARN Act Filings | WARNact

facts: The federal Occupational Safety and Health Administration fined Blue Ridge Manufacturing LLC $64,400 for 20 serious safety and health violations at its plant in Blue Ridge, Ga.
OSHA inspected the plant in March following a complaint against Blue Ridge Manufacturing LLC.
Safety inspectors found unguarded equipment, missing safety equipment, and unsafe noise levels exposing manufacturing workers to risk of common injuries or fatal workplace accidents.
Violations included failure to provide workers with lockout/tagout training to protect them during maintenance and service work on dangerous equipment.
Violations included failure to train workers to run powered industrial trucks.
Violations included failure to inspect a crane used for picking up steel parts.
Violations included failure to ensure exits were unlocked and clearly marked.
Inspectors found workers could be injured by being struck or caught by unguarded equipment, electrical shock and burn hazards.
Dust levels at the plant were six times higher than allowable limits.
The company failed to keep required injury and illness logs showing where injuries occurred.
Georgia has more than 250 automotive and parts manufacturing facilities that employ nearly 18,000 workers, according to Georgia.org.
Kia opened its first U.S. manufacturing operation in West Point, Georgia.
Porsche, Lotus, and BBS located their North American headquarters in Georgia.
General Motors launched a GM Innovation Center in conjunction with the Georgia Institute of Technology.
The developments are encouraging for the state’s economy, ensuring the state will be able to offer high-tech and industrial jobs for many years.
The automotive industry is incumbent on focusing on worker welfare to ensure work atmospheres are free of risks that could cause injury or death.
source_title: Osha Says Blue Ridge Manufacturing Violated Safety Rules, Put Georgia Workers At Risk Of Serious Workplace Injury | Georgia Personal Injury Lawyer Blog | January 23, 2014

facts: Georgia Crown Distributing Company is hiring for warehouse positions (as of Sep 26, 2022).
Warehouse duties include operation of production equipment, inventory of products, cleaning and sanitizing of equipment and warehouse areas, and maintaining organization of the warehouse.
An immediate opening was posted on Jul 29, 2020 for receiving, parts, stocking bins, and plant organization.
The receiving/parts role requires a self-motivated employee willing to lean, with general knowledge of parts receiving, counting, and stocking shelves as well as general organizational skills.
An immediate opening was posted on Jul 29, 2020 for a powder coating applicator.
The powder coating role requires a general understanding of industrial painting, with prior powder coating experience being a plus.
An immediate opening was posted on Jul 29, 2020 in the finish department, noting that all areas have openings and a new shift is being added.
An immediate opening was posted on Jul 29, 2020 in the welding department for fabricating steel truck beds and accessories.
Welding responsibilities include welding, fitting, grinding, hand plasma torch use, and other metal working processes.
Welding requirements include prior MIG welding experience and the ability to read blueprints.
source_title: Manufacturing Archives - Fannin County Development Authority

facts: The Georgia Ports Authority (GPA) is opening its new inland port in Gainesville, Ga., on May 4, 2026.
The infrastructure was formerly known as the Blue Ridge Connector.
The project entailed a $134 million investment.
The facility will have an annual capacity of 200,000 containers.
The port features a direct connection to Savannah’s global ocean carrier network, which operates 40 ships per week.
The facility provides a direct rail with five-day-a-week service between Northeast Georgia and Savannah.
The rail service serves as an alternative to a 600-mile round-trip truck route.
The project is expected to benefit the entire Atlanta metropolitan area.
GPA President and CEO Griff Lynch stated the inland rail facility will significantly offset truck traffic congestion in Atlanta and improve air quality.
The new facility is estimated to replace 26,000 truck round-trips in its first year alone.
The Georgia Ports Authority is executing a nearly $5 billion infrastructure investment plan over the next decade.
The $5 billion initiative aims to expand berths, yards, gates, inland ports, and rail capacity.
GPA Board Chairman Alec Poitevint emphasized that new infrastructure assets require planning and time, advocating for steady investment to deliver port capacity ahead of customer needs.
The renovation of Ocean Terminal in Savannah is progressing as part of the broader expansion efforts.
Phase one of the $1.6 billion Ocean Terminal project will open in July 2027.
Phase two of the Ocean Terminal project will open in December 2028.
The renovated dock at Ocean Terminal will stretch nearly 2,700 feet, allowing two large ships to dock simultaneously.
A new gate complex at Ocean Terminal featuring 12 inbound and six outbound lanes will open by November 2026.
A GPA-funded $29 million overpass is already open, enabling trucks to enter the I-16 corridor directly and keeping traffic off neighborhood streets.
The Port of Brunswick is undergoing renovations, with dredging operations expected to wrap up by the end of March 2026.
Additional work at the Port of Brunswick aimed at returning the inner and outer harbor to authorized depth should be completed in summer 2026.
source_title: Georgia Ports Authority’s Gainesville inland port set for May inauguration - FreshFruitPortal.com

facts: The City of Blue Ridge issues all land use and building permits to businesses locating within its city limits in accordance with city land use and zoning ordinances.
There is no zoning in the unincorporated areas of Fannin County, as well as the Cities of McCaysville and Morganton.
Businesses locating in the unincorporated areas of Fannin County, McCaysville, or Morganton must comply with Fannin County land use and building ordinances.
All Fannin County ordinances and inspection requirements are available for downloading.
The Fannin County Health Department handles inquiries regarding environmental health inspections, food service establishments, sewage, and tourist accommodations.

facts: Bosch posted consolidated sales of $17.3 billion USD (16 billion euros) and achieved total net sales of $18.6 billion USD including sales of non-consolidated companies and internal deliveries to affiliated companies in 2024.

The North American region achieved the largest sales increase globally for the Bosch Group in 2024, emphasizing the company’s focus on the region and particularly the U.S. market as part of achieving regional balance globally.
Stefan Hartung, chairman of Bosch, stated that by 2030 the company aims for the North American region to represent 20 percent of global sales, with the U.S. representing the majority.

Over the past five years, Bosch has invested more than $2 billion in capital expenditures in the United States, representing more than 75 percent of its capital expenditures in the North American region over that time.
Since 2023, Bosch has announced plans to invest around $6 billion in U.S.-based acquisitions.

Paul Thomas, president of Bosch in North America and president of Bosch Mobility Americas, stated that Bosch is investing to grow in the U.S. market by developing regionally relevant products and solutions.
Bosch has been in the United States since 1906 and will celebrate its 120-year anniversary in the U.S. in 2026.
The company currently employs more than 20,000 associates and operates 15+ manufacturing sites across the United States.
Both the number of associates and U.S. manufacturing sites are expected to grow in 2025 following the closure of a major planned acquisition.
In 2024, Bosch's Mobility business in North America increased sales to third parties to $10.7 billion USD despite market challenges.

Bosch continues to invest in and book customer wins for technologies supporting U.S. mobility trends, including software-defined vehicles (SDV), advanced-driver assistance systems (ADAS), hybrid technology, and vehicle motion control.

Bosch has continued to innovate on internal combustion engine (ICE) technology, including technology to support hybrids, maintaining revenue as ICE vehicle production decreases and BEV vehicles increase.
The company supports customers with battery electric and hydrogen solutions, providing both fuel cell and hydrogen combustion technology.
Paul Thomas stated that Bosch's goal is to provide manufacturers and U.S. consumers with affordable options across its Mobility portfolio.
Bosch's technology leadership in the U.S. mobility sector is realized through a workforce at nine locations in California, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Minnesota, and South Carolina.
Bosch plans to manufacture second-generation silicon carbide (SiC) chips on 200mm wafers in Roseville, California beginning in 2026.
The transformation of the acquired Roseville facility represents an investment of up to $1.9 billion.
During the Roseville facility transformation, Bosch has retained nearly all 250 associates and provided advanced training through its global manufacturing network.

A major driver of growth for Bosch Mobility in North America in 2024 was the Cross-Domain Computing Solutions division, which delivers ADAS and vehicle computers to support U.S. automakers' software-defined focus.
Bosch has expanded its software and hardware for assisted and automated driving to facilitate rapid development in ADAS, particularly Level 2 and Level 2++.
For its new radar sensor, Bosch developed and manufactured all core elements in-house, including a computing chip (SoC) using RF CMOS technology with transistor structure sizes of only 22 nanometers.
Bosch is the first Tier 1 supplier to introduce this 22-nanometer radar sensor technology in serial production.

In the U.S., Bosch signed a major award with the Southeastern Pennsylvania Transportation Authority (SEPTA) to install tram forward collision warning systems across its entire fleet via retrofit, following a pilot program that began in 2022 on four vehicles.
SEPTA serves a region of nearly four million people in Philadelphia and its surrounding counties.

In 2024, Bosch announced plans to acquire the HVAC solutions business for residential and light commercial buildings from Johnson Controls, including brands like York and Coleman, with closure expected during 2025.
The Bosch Group generated 90.3 billion euros in sales revenue in 2024, a decrease of 1.4 percent year-over-year (or 0.5 percent after adjusting for exchange-rate effects).
Stefan Hartung stated that with normal inflation between 2 and 3 percent, Bosch aims to achieve annual growth of between 6 and 8 percent on average until 2030.
Bosch increased its sales revenue by 4 percent in the first quarter of the year compared to the previous year.
The company is aiming for a target margin of 7 percent in 2026, viewing it as an extremely challenging task given current market conditions.
As one of Europe’s biggest corporate venture-capital investors, Bosch announced a new fund through its subsidiary Bosch Ventures providing around $270 million USD.
Bosch expects developments in electromobility, hydrogen, and software-defined vehicles to be major stimuli for growth in its core Mobility business sector.
Factory automation at Bosch is set to focus on growth areas such as battery, semiconductor, and consumer goods production.
Bosch is underlining its commitment to climate action with new scope 3 targets aiming to bring down carbon emissions outside its direct sphere of influence further by 2030.
The company wants to double its corresponding CO2 reduction target from 15 percent to 30 percent by 2030 compared to 2018.
source_title: Bosch focuses on growth in United States - Bosch Media Service US

facts: Bosch is creating a single unit to oversee software and systems development for vehicles.
The consolidation of the new division will start in early 2021.
The new division will combine parts of Bosch's automotive electronics, chassis, and powertrain divisions with its car multimedia division.
The consolidated division will have 17,000 employees in more than 20 countries.
Bosch is described as the world's largest auto parts supplier.
Bosch is shifting its emphasis from hardware to software as vehicles transform into mobile devices combining transportation with digital services like e-commerce and infotainment.
The shift toward vehicle software is a trend driven heavily by electric car maker Tesla Inc.
Bosch board member Harald Kroeger, a former Daimler AG executive and onetime Tesla board member, is spearheading the new division.
Harald Kroeger stated: "Supplying software from a single source is our response to the enormous challenge of making cars ever more digitalized."
Bosch is joining automakers such as Tesla, Volkswagen AG, and General Motors Co., along with large suppliers like Aptiv PLC, in consolidating vehicle functions into a single digital "architecture."
Similar to what Tesla has been doing for more than five years, the consolidated system will oversee computers, control units, and sensors.
The centralized architecture can be revised more easily and quickly through wireless over-the-air updates.
Centralized computers and software in vehicles will link functions such as automated driving, advanced driver assistance, digital dashboards, and Internet connectivity.
Bosch supplies key components to many of the world's automakers, from Tesla to Daimler.
source_title: Robert Bosch GmbH: Bosch creates single unit to oversee software, systems development, ETAuto

facts: Bosch Automotive Workshop Services LLC (Bosch Auto Service) has announced its expansion into the Southeastern states of Georgia, Florida, North Carolina, South Carolina and Tennessee.
The company plans to expand into additional states in the future.
Bosch Auto Service Franchisees have access to scalable growth opportunities through the Bosch service model that allows business owners to expand their portfolio in the automotive sector.
Franchise workshops will receive custom business solutions including dedicated consultants who specialize in fixed operations and workshop financial planning.
Workshops will get technical training through Bosch-certified experts focused on the latest in vehicle service.
Franchise resources include customer acquisition and retention programs, analytics, state-of-the-art diagnostics, OEM-approved parts and more.
The program provides strategic partnerships to increase visibility, value, efficiency and business growth.
Franchisees gain brand recognition with over 100 years of Bosch experience and innovation in the automotive sector.
Uli Jaschek serves as the CEO of the Bosch Auto Service franchise.
Uli Jaschek stated that the program is supported by extensive Bosch expertise and innovation, elevating automotive service standards through top-tier maintenance and repair.
Uli Jaschek added that they are pleased to bring the program to the Southeast to meet the growing demand for quality automotive service with care centered around precision and reliability.
source_title: Bosch Auto Service expands franchising opportunities to Southeast region - Aftermarket Matters

facts: Bosch announced plans to cut a "five-digit number" of jobs, with reports specifying up to 13,000 positions.
The job cuts are primarily within Bosch's automotive division.
The restructuring stems from a "major cost-cutting exercise" aimed at addressing a €2.5 billion annual shortfall in its mobility unit.
The planned layoffs affect about 10% of Bosch's German workforce.
This follows the elimination of 4,500 jobs last year.
Stefan Grosch serves as Bosch's HR director and head of industrial relations.
Bosch's mobility division produces essential components like fuel injectors and driver-assistance software.
Stefan Grosch stated: "Demand for our products is shifting significantly to regions outside Europe."
Stefan Grosch added: "We need to orient ourselves to where our markets and customers are."
Bosch stated it would be "cutting costs across the board – from materials and logistics to capital spending and jobs."
The escalation of the Ukraine conflict in 2022 led Germany and other EU nations to pivot away from inexpensive Russian energy imports to costlier alternatives.
This pivot dramatically increased production costs for energy-intensive manufacturing.
A fierce price war in China is severely cutting into profit margins for both car manufacturers and their suppliers.
Marco Zehe serves as head of electrified motion at Bosch.
Marco Zehe acknowledged industry pressure, stating: "There is great price and competitive pressure on the entire automotive industry on both carmakers as well as their suppliers."
A central challenge has been the slower-than-anticipated adoption of electric vehicles in Europe.
German automakers and their suppliers invested billions in anticipation of a rapid EV shift, but consumer demand has not kept pace, leaving significant overcapacity.
Marco Zehe said: "Electromobility has not taken off as quickly as forecast."
Marco Zehe added: "That means we have lots of overcapacity, particularly in Europe and particularly in Germany."
Carmakers are increasingly sourcing components locally rather than importing them from Germany as they expand production in key markets like North America and Asia.
Markus Heyn serves as head of Bosch Mobility.

Markus Heyn summarized the localization trend to the Stuttgarter Zeitung: "The trend towards localisation is unstoppable. The days when Germany could produce a great deal for the rest of the world are over."
Volkswagen, Europe's largest automaker, saw its after-tax earnings slump by 36% in the second quarter.
Volkswagen is planning its own extensive restructuring, potentially involving tens of thousands of job cuts.
BMW reported a 29% year-on-year drop in first-half profits, citing U.S. import duties and intense "competitive pressure."
According to the German Press Agency (dpa), Germany's industrial sector has lost more than 100,000 jobs over the past year.
German Chancellor Friedrich Merz acknowledged that the country was “not just in a period of economic weakness, we are in a structural crisis of our economy,” caused by a loss of competitiveness.
Frank Sell serves as head of the Bosch Mobility works council.
Frank Sell vowed to fight the job cut plans, labeling them "historically unprecedented."
Frank Sell criticized the company for offering no guarantees against site closures in Germany.
Frank Sell warned: "Bosch is not only breaching the trust of those who have made this company big and successful but leaving behind social devastation in many regions."
Stefan Grosch told reporters that Germany remained "central" to Bosch's future.
Stefan Grosch stated: "We stand by it as a location and stand by Europe and are doing all we can to continually improve our competitiveness by our own efforts."
source_title: An industry under pressure: Bosch announces massive restructuring

facts: The Bandag plant will be built in Rustavi, representing a $2 million investment.
A landmark agreement between Tegeta Motors and Bridgestone will see a Bandag plant built in the Caucasus for the first time.
Tegeta Motors will become the exclusive regional operator of the Bandag plant.
The plant will use Bandag’s cutting-edge tire retreading technology to extend the life of truck and bus tires.
Bandag is a Bridgestone subsidiary that produces millions of high-quality retreaded tires annually for global markets.
The agreement was signed in Tbilisi at Tegeta Motors’ multi-functional service center.
Jacques Fourier, President of Bridgestone Core Tire Business Africa & Middle East, flew to Georgia to attend the signing event.
Ekaterine Kavtaradze serves as Chief Executive Officer of Tegeta Holding.
The partnership between Tegeta Motors and Bridgestone spans 27 years and is entering a new phase with this agreement.
Tegeta Motors currently serves as the regional distributor for the Bridgestone brand in Georgia.
At the initial stage, the plant aims to produce up to 5,000 retreaded tires per year in the region.
Production capacity is planned to double over the next five years.
$2 million has been invested in the project thus far.
Initially, Tegeta Motors will retread tires for all types of trucks and buses in full compliance with international standards.
The holding plans to export retreaded tires to Armenia and Azerbaijan in the next stage.
Each retreaded tire costs 30%-40% less than purchasing a new tire.
Refurbished tires have a service life of up to 5 years.
Construction of the Bandag plant in Rustavi is scheduled to begin this year, with completion targeted for late 2026.
Representatives from Bridgestone and Bandag will oversee construction to ensure compliance with global standards.
The facility will be outfitted with state-of-the-art Bandag machinery sourced from Europe and the US.
Specialists from Bridgestone will provide targeted technical training for the Georgian team before production begins.
Six months of on-site supervision by Bridgestone and Bandag specialists will follow once the plant becomes operational.
The retreading process uses a shearograph to detect internal flaws in tire casings with exceptional precision.
Tegeta Motors possesses the only shearograph of its kind available in the region, which will be exclusive to the Rustavi plant.
Once structural integrity is confirmed, the remaining tread is replaced with new, high-quality material.
The cost per kilometer of a refurbished tire is significantly lower than that of a new tire while delivering comparable performance.
Bandag’s retreading technology requires less raw material and energy, reducing waste generation and carbon emissions.
Tegeta Motors emphasizes environmental sustainability and eco-friendly solutions as core priorities for the facility.
Future plans include building a tire recycling facility capable of managing the entire tire life cycle and minimizing waste.
Tegeta Motors has operated since 1995 as part of Tegeta Holding and is a leading company in Georgia’s automotive sector.
The company provides comprehensive services for cars, minibuses, trucks, buses, and special-purpose equipment across 28 locations nationwide.
Tegeta Motors serves approximately 500,000 retail customers and 35,000 corporate clients.
The company represents over 300 international brands in Georgia, including Bridgestone, Michelin, Varta, Shell, Motul, and Hengst.
source_title: Tegeta Motors Signs Agreement With Bridgestone to Build a State-of-the-art Bandag Plant • Forbes Georgia

facts: Bandag, a global leader in tyre retreading solutions, has partnered with Tegeta Motors and Bridgestone MEA to establish the first Bandag retreading factory in the Caucasus region.
The facility is located in Rustavi, Georgia.
The project is backed by an initial investment of US$2 million.
The facility is expected to produce up to 5,000 retreaded tyres annually.
Plans are in place to double capacity within five years.
Construction will commence this year (2025).
Completion is targeted for late 2026.
The Tegeta Bandag Factory represents a major step towards expanding sustainable mobility practices across the region.

facts: Bandag has signed an agreement with Tegeta Motors to build a state-of-the-art Tegeta Bandag Factory in Rustavi, Georgia.
The new facility marks an important step in expanding sustainable retreading solutions across the Caucasus region.
The project is supported by an initial investment of USD 2 million.
The plant aims to produce up to 5,000 retreaded tyres annually.
Production capacity is expected to double within five years.
The facility is developed in partnership with Tegeta Motors and supported by Bridgestone MEA.
This will be the first Bandag retreading factory in the Caucasus.
Construction is scheduled to begin this year, with completion targeted for late 2026.
The project marks a significant milestone in Bandag’s commitment to advancing circular mobility and promoting environmental responsibility.
The initiative strengthens Tegeta Motors’ position as a leading provider of advanced automotive services in the region.
The facility will initially retread tyres for buses and trucks, ensuring every product meets international safety and performance standards.
This approach is expected to significantly reduce environmental impact and lower operational costs for corporate and commercial customers.
Representatives from Bridgestone and Bandag will provide construction oversight to ensure the facility adheres to global quality standards.
The plant will be equipped with state-of-the-art Bandag machinery sourced from Europe and the United States.
The retreading process reinforces the casing of a used tyre with the same premium materials used in new tyre manufacturing.
Retreaded tyres cost 30-40% less than new tyres.
Retreaded tyres deliver comparable performance to new tyres.
Retreaded tyres provide a service life of up to five years.

Bandag’s retreading technology requires less raw material and energy, extends a tyre’s life through multiple cycles, reduces waste generation, minimizes carbon emissions, and supports more efficient use of natural resources.
Ekaterine Kavtaradze serves as the CEO of Tegeta Motors.
Tegeta Motors will integrate the retreading operations into its existing ecosystem, which includes tyre services, oil and battery operations, and recycling activities.
Bridgestone MEA continues to provide technical expertise and innovation support throughout the factory’s development.
source_title: Bandag to Build State-of-the-Art Retreading Factory in Georgia in Collaboration with Tegeta Motors and Bridgestone - Middle East Business News and Information - mid-east.info

facts: Bridgestone Americas is on pace to achieve one of its highest year-over-year results in its Bandag business with a 9% increase over 2021.
The company forecasts year-end sales of Bandag retreads will reach more than 7 million units in North America.
Bridgestone anticipates continued growth in 2023 with a $60 million investment in its Abilene, Texas retread plant to increase production capacity.
Retreading extends the life of premium tire casings, reduces environmental impact of end-of-life tires, maximizes fleet uptime, and mitigates overall operating costs.
Retreading is a critical component for fleets to unlock ESG and sustainability goals contributing to a circular economy.
In 2022, Bandag retreads are forecast to deliver environmental benefits of approximately 105 million gallons of oil saved.
In 2022, Bandag retreads are forecast to deliver environmental benefits of approximately 290 million pounds of landfill waste avoided.
In 2022, Bandag retreads are forecast to deliver environmental benefits of approximately 207 million pounds of tire waste avoided.
In 2022, Bandag retreads are forecast to deliver environmental benefits of approximately 5.6 billion KwH of energy saved, which is the equivalent of powering 500,000 U.S. homes for a full year.
Jason Roanhouse serves as executive director of Bandag Operations at Bridgestone Americas.
Bridgestone announced a new $60 million investment in its Bandag tread manufacturing plant in Abilene, Texas.
The Abilene plant expansion will add 50,000 square-feet and additional production days to meet accelerating demand for retread products.
The expansion will increase the plant's retread output by 16%.
Bridgestone introduced the all-new Bridgestone Last Mile Tire System (LMTS) in the second half of 2022.
The LMTS program will begin utilizing retread for small and medium-sized fleets in Q1 2023.
The goal of the LMTS program is to extend the benefits of retreading to a broader population of fleet customers.
The LMTS allows smaller fleets operating out of the same terminal to realize greater economies of scale in their tire operations.
The LMTS provides access to Bandag retread solutions and Bridgestone Fleet Care products.
The LMTS program was launched in summer 2022 at the Route Consultant Expo, where more than 500 fleet contractors expressed interest as potential early adopters.
Bandag operates over 200 retread manufacturing plants in North America.
Bandag has a network of more than 1,100 authorized dealer commercial locations.
Bandag represents nearly one in every two retreaded truck tires on North American roads today.
Bandag’s proprietary 10-step manufacturing process has kept an estimated 300 million tires out of the waste stream and saved over 4 billion gallons of oil over the years.
Bandag’s vertical integration with Bridgestone helped to reduce supply chain challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic and beyond.

The continued expansion of Bridgestone Bandag retreading aligns with the Bridgestone E8 Commitment, which consists of 8 values starting with “E” (Energy, Ecology, Efficiency, Extension, Economy, Emotion, Ease, and Empowerment).
source_title: Press Release Details

facts: Bridgestone Bandag Tire Solutions, a division of Bridgestone Americas Tire Operations, announced that two of its pickup and delivery tires were approved for use on Environmental Protection Agency SmartWay-certified equipment.
The R260F and R250F all-position radials are designed to promote long tread life and low cost per mile in pickup and delivery, urban and regional hauling environments.

Chris Hoffman, manager of TBR and Retread Product Marketing, stated that the approval is a tremendous benefit for customers participating in SmartWay, as most previously approved tires were long-distance radials not built for high-scrub environments like beverage hauling, package and parcel delivery, grocery, food service, and other P&D operations.
Adding the R260F and R250F to the verified technology list allows SmartWay fleets to specify the right tire for the job.

The R260F has 22/32-inch tread depth, uses Bridgestone's patented Equalizer Rib structures to fight irregular wear and groove-bottom platforms to enhance retreadability, and features thick protector ribs on both sidewalls to fight curbing, with ribbed sidewalls allowing reversal on the wheel for continued protection.

The R250F has straight-line grooves that promote traction and durable protector ribs on both sidewalls to fight damage from curbing, cuts and abrasions, with a casing incorporating four steel belts with an all-steel casing ply for long life and retreadability.
Chris Hoffman noted that the R260F and R250F are California Air Resources Board (CARB) compliant, which benefits regional and P&D fleets operating in California.
source_title: Bridgestone Bandag P&D Tires Are SmartWay-Approved | Heavy Duty Trucking

The company selected Georgia due to great international transport connections and logistical proximity between BMW in South Carolina and Mercedes in Alabama.
Carcoustics Group utilizes a business model that combines a central research center with a manufacturing structure to maintain proximity to major markets.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced an international mission to Europe on January 21, 2025, to strengthen economic and cultural ties in Germany and Poland while developing new relationships for Georgians.
Over the last six years, international companies have invested over $38 billion in Georgia, according to Governor Kemp.
These investments created more than 58,000 new opportunities for Georgians in every corner of the state, according to Governor Kemp.
Communities outside of metro Atlanta attracted over 80% of those jobs, according to Governor Kemp.

Georgia’s partnership with Germany is instrumental to its economic success, as Germany is one of the state's largest trade partners and a major contributor to job creation and investment, stated Governor Kemp.
The mission aims to reinforce collaboration with Germany while opening dialogues with Poland, an emerging market, to ensure a brighter future for Georgians, stated Governor Kemp.

As we prepare to return money to Georgia taxpayers and invest in key areas like public safety and education, this mission is an opportunity to reinforce that collaboration while also opening dialogues with an emerging market, ensuring a brighter future for all Georgians, stated Governor Kemp.
Governor Kemp and representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) will conduct meetings with firms already operating or under construction in Georgia.
Notable German companies on the itinerary include BASF, Gerresheimer, Carcoustics, KIRCHHOFF Automotive, and GEDIA Automotive Group.

German companies excel in industries like life sciences and automotive manufacturing, and Georgia serves as a strategic gateway for them and other European businesses entering the U.S. market, stated GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson.
Over the past two decades, German automotive firms alone have contributed approximately 2,200 jobs and $830 million in investments to Georgia communities, according to Commissioner Wilson.
The growth of life sciences companies like Gerresheimer demonstrates how international businesses become integral to the state's economy, according to Commissioner Wilson.

Exploring emerging markets such as Poland allows Georgia to stay ahead of global trends, build new partnerships, and support local companies seeking to expand internationally, according to Commissioner Wilson.
The State of Georgia has maintained continuous representation in Europe since 1973.
Germany is the fourth largest export and import market for the State of Georgia.
More than $12.8 billion in total trade moved between Georgia’s global gateways and Germany in 2023.
German companies have invested more than $3.9 billion in Georgia since 2010 through projects with state involvement.
An estimated 650 German-owned facilities support approximately 42,000 jobs for Georgians across the state.

Georgia-based companies such as Alimera Sciences, Bridgestone Golf, Delta Air Lines, Eversheds Sutherland, Graphic Packaging Holding, Novelis, Porex Corporation, WestRock, and others maintain operations in Germany.
BASF’s manufacturing presence in Georgia includes operations in Attapulgus, Edgar, and Savannah.
Gerresheimer first established a production facility in Peachtree City in 1993.
Gerresheimer expanded its Peachtree City location in 2009 with the establishment of a Technical Competence Center (TCC).
Gerresheimer announced additional expansions in Georgia in December 2023 and May 2024.
In 2017, Carcoustics established Carcoustics USA South Inc. in Buford, Georgia, including a central research center and a manufacturing presence.
KIRCHHOFF Automotive opened an assembly facility in Lawrenceville in 2023.
In 2020, GEDIA Automotive Group announced an advanced manufacturing facility that will begin production with a focus on parts for electric vehicles in the greater Dalton area.
source_title: Governor Kemp to Reinforce Economic Partnerships in Germany and Poland - Augusta CEO

facts: Club Car is an Augusta-area golf cart maker completing a 105,000-square-foot facility in Appling.
Club Car’s Columbia County footprint is about to get 20% bigger.
The local golf cart manufacturer announced it would expand into a 105,000-square-foot facility at White Oak Business Park.
The new facility is located near the company’s existing 550,000-square-foot distribution facility that broke ground in 2018.
The additional space will be used for “warehousing and manufacturing functions.”
The new facility is expected to be completed by the end of the year.
Club Car President Mark Wagner stated the company is dedicated to providing the best vehicles and service for customers.
Mark Wagner noted that the new facility gives the company the opportunity to extend operations in the United States.
Club Car began making golf carts in 1958.
The company’s product line has grown to include utility vehicles for commercial use and nongolf transportation for personal use.
Rick Evans, chairman of the Board of Directors for the Development Authority of Columbia County, stated that Club Car has a longstanding relationship with Columbia County.
Rick Evans expressed value for the investments Club Car continues to make to expand and manufacture world-class golf carts and utility vehicles in Columbia County.

facts: Famed American golf-cart manufacturer Club Car has opened a new factory in Augusta, Georgia.
The facility is located in the unincorporated community of Appling.
The factory represents an $8 million capital investment.
The facility will manufacture the brand’s Urban line of small electric commercial vehicles.
The factory was officially kicked into gear on Thursday, April 18, with a ribbon-cutting ceremony featuring Georgia Governor Brian Kemp.
Club Car’s Urban XR and Urban LSV models were engineered for versatile duty beyond groundskeeping.
The company pitches the Urban line as “eco-friendly, crash-tested vehicles that help you get the job done.”
The most affordable Urban model, the off-road-only XR, has a top speed of 19 mph and an MSRP of just under $27,000.
The more expensive LSV is road-legal in some states and capable of 25 mph.
Both versions can carry a payload of up to 2,204 pounds and were developed with industrial spaces and last-mile delivery in mind.
Either vehicle version can be ordered with a pickup bed or a van box.
The Appling facility employs 40 people.
The facility is able to build up to 20 Urban vehicles per day.
The new factory is a response to increasing US demand for Club Car products.
source_title: Club Car Launches Georgia UTV Factory | UTV Driver

facts: Club Car announced it is expanding in Columbia County and will be building a 105,000 square foot facility in Appling.

The additional space at the new facility will be used to support warehousing and manufacturing functions.

Rick Evans, Chair of the Board of Directors for the Development Authority of Columbia County, stated that Club Car has a long-standing relationship with Columbia County and values the investments made to expand and manufacture world-class golf cars and utility vehicles in the county.
Club Car has an over 60-year history in the design and production of small-wheel vehicles.
Club Car is the leading manufacturer of gas and electric golf, utility, and personal transportation vehicles.

Club Car is the official golf car of the PGA of America, PGA Tour Tournament Players Club (TPC) Network, and European Tour.

facts: UAW Local 174 members at Constellium Automotive in Van Buren Charter Township, MI, ratified a new contract after standing on the picket line for nearly a month.
The ratified agreement provides improved contractual language, including a stronger grievance procedure and additional holidays.
The agreement secures strong pay increases for workers.
UAW Local 174 members returned to work on Monday following the contract ratification.
Region 1A Director Laura Dickerson stated that contract negotiations were very difficult because the company was unwilling to work with them and repeatedly cancelled negotiating sessions.
Laura Dickerson noted that members stood together and forced Constellium to offer a contract that reflects their immense value to the company.
Constellium Van Buren supplies parts for the Ford F-150, F-150 Lightning, Explorer, and Super Duty at six UAW-represented Ford Assembly plants.
source_title: Constellium Workers Ratify New Contract, Win Strong Pay Increases, More - UAW | United Automobile, Aerospace and Agricultural Implement Workers of America

facts: Constellium SE reported financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2023.
Fourth quarter 2023 shipments were 336 thousand metric tons, down 9% compared to Q4 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 revenue was €1.6 billion, down 13% compared to Q4 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 Value-Added Revenue (VAR) was €681 million, down 2% compared to Q4 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 net income was €11 million compared to net income of €30 million in Q4 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 adjusted EBITDA was €171 million, up 15% compared to Q4 2022.
Fourth quarter 2023 cash from operations was €185 million and free cash flow was €58 million.

facts: Continental will discontinue operations at its Aldora Mills textile manufacturing plant in Barnesville, Georgia by the end of 2026.
The Aldora Mills facility employs approximately 230 people.
The facility produces tire cord fabric, hose yarn, and knitted fabric used exclusively within Continental’s Tires and ContiTech group sectors.
Continental acquired the Aldora Mills facility in 1988.
The decision to discontinue operations follows a comprehensive business review to safeguard the company’s profitability, competitiveness, and business performance in the Americas region.
The plant has not been cost competitive for many years despite continuous turnaround efforts.
Ongoing changes in global market dynamics and supply chains have made it necessary to take this decision.
Continental will support affected employees during the transition by offering career counseling and access to employment resources in collaboration with local partners.

facts: Continental presents the electric motor rotor temperature sensor (eRTS).
The eRTS delivers significantly more precise measurement results than current software-based temperature simulation.
The tolerance range for temperature measurement is reduced from 15 to just three degrees Celsius.
The eRTS and its data can help vehicle manufacturers reduce the amount of rare earths used in electric motors.
Rare earths are currently used to make magnets inside PSM rotors more heat-resistant.
Precisely measured temperatures allow rare earths to be dosed more accurately during development.
The cooling system can be controlled more precisely and purposefully during operation using the sensor data.
Utilizing the limits of the previously generous tolerance range can increase performance.
Rotors can reach temperatures of up to 150 degrees Celsius under full load and require cooling to prevent permanent magnets from demagnetizing.
Current solutions calculate rotor temperature based on stator temperature sensor data, phase current measurements, and environmental variables rather than measuring directly.
The two-part eRTS consists of a small sensor called 'Mote' located near the magnets in the rotor.
The second part is a receiver unit called 'Transducer' located outside the motor.
The transducer is connected to the inverter controller and transmits temperature measurement data via a communication interface.

Bin Huo, Head of the Passive Safety and Sensorics (PSS) segment in Continental’s Automotive division, states that less resource consumption and lower costs make eRTS sensor technology advantageous over current solutions.
Christoph Busch, Head of the E-Mobility Sensors Product Centre, says the center aims to increase efficiency and sustainability in electric vehicles.
Reducing rare earth use contributes to a more sustainable supply chain as EV numbers are expected to greatly increase in coming years and decades.
In combination with other sensor technologies like the e-Motor Rotor Position Sensor, the eRTS can act as a system solution to create synergies that save car manufacturers money and effort.
Continental plans to successively expand its EV sensor portfolio.
source_title: Continental presents temperature sensor for electric motor rotors - electrive.com

facts: Dr. Jochen Etzel will retire as CEO of Continental Tire the Americas, LLC after a more than 30-year career with Continental.
Tansu Işık will assume the CEO role on May 1, 2025.
The press release was issued from Hanover, Germany, and Fort Mill, S.C., USA on March 18, 2025.
Tansu Işık will be the new Head of Continental’s Business Area Replacement Tires the Americas.
Tansu Işık will report directly to Christian Koetz, Executive Board Member of Continental and Head of Continental Tires.
Jochen Etzel has served as CEO since 2011 and played an important role in uniting various Continental tire activities in the region.
Jochen Etzel started his career as a lawyer in Continental’s corporate law department.
Jochen Etzel then took over a financial manager position responsible for developing the company’s tire business in regions outside of Europe.
Jochen Etzel later led Continental’s corporate Mergers and Acquisitions team.
Christian Koetz stated that Jochen’s impact on Continental’s tire business has been immeasurable and thanked him for his leadership, dedication, and outstanding contributions throughout his tenure.
Christian Koetz expressed excitement to welcome Tansu Işık into his new role as Head of the Business Area Replacement Tires the Americas.
Tansu Işık brings more than 20 years of international experience in marketing, supply chain, strategy, and key account management within Continental’s tires business.
Tansu Işık began his career as a Business Development Specialist in Istanbul, Türkiye.
Tansu Işık moved to Continental Tires’ Headquarters in Hanover, Germany, to become Head of Pricing for the Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA) region.
Tansu Işık then moved to Shanghai, China, as Director and Vice President for Key Account Management (OE) China and the Asia-Pacific region (APAC).
From 2017, Işık worked as Head of Marketing for Continental’s replacement tire business in the EMEA region.
Işık led the Strategic Business Development and Global Marketing organization of Continental Tires.

Işık currently serves as the Head of Region South & Channels Fleet and OE Trailer for Continental replacement tire business in the EMEA region, overseeing operations in multiple countries, leading the truck tire business in EMEA, and responsible for retread and digital solutions.
Işık holds an Executive MBA from Koç University and has completed trainings at INSEAD, Duke University, and Ashridge University.
Allison Zora is the Head of PR & Brand Communication for Business Area Replacement Tires the Americas at Continental Tires.
