facts: On Feb. 1, HMGMA supplier Seoyon E-HWA announced it would locate in the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center (SCMC), bringing 740 new jobs and $76 million in capital investment.
Seoyon E-HWA manufactures door trims, headlining seats, bumpers, and interior and exterior components.
On March 6, PHA announced it would create 402 new jobs and invest $67 million in capital for a new location in SCMC.
PHA manufactures door, tailgate, and hood latches.
On Oct. 23, Daechang Seat Savannah Corp. announced it would locate in SCMC to make automotive seat frames for HMGMA, creating 500 new jobs and $72.5 million in capital investment.
On April 6, Gulfstream Aerospace Corp. selected Chatham County for expansions that will bring 1,600 new jobs and $150 million in capital investment.
The Gulfstream projects include construction of a new Customer Support service center and expansion of its manufacturing facility for the G400, G500, and G600 aircraft.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that Gulfstream joined the Savannah community in 1967 and partners with technical college systems and local schools to create opportunity while participating in programs like Georgia Made.
Gulfstream flew its first purpose-built business aircraft in 1958 and relocated to Savannah in 1967, with over 3,000 aircraft currently in service worldwide.
Gulfstream currently employs more than 11,000 people in Chatham County.
SEDA President and CEO Trip Tollison noted that the Gulfstream announcement was a competitive project and thanked the company for choosing Savannah.

Savannah Airport Commission Executive Director Greg Kelly stated the partnership resulted in one of the largest aeronautical complexes in the world at SAV, with new facilities underway and future growth planned.

On Aug. 31, Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution (LGES) announced an additional $2 billion investment in their battery cell manufacturing joint venture at HMGMA in Bryan County, raising the JV's total investment to over $4.3 billion.
The Hyundai/LGES expansion will create another 400 new jobs.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia broke ground on the largest project in state history, landed multiple suppliers for Hyundai's Metaplant, and welcomed LGES to Bryan County.
Hyundai President and Global COO José Muñoz stated the incremental investment reflects a continued commitment to creating a sustainable future powered by American workers.
In May 2023, Hyundai Motor Group and LGES signed an MOU establishing LGES as the partner for HMGMA's onsite battery cell manufacturing JV.
The EV manufacturing facility and battery JV represent an estimated $7.59 billion in investment and will create 8,500 new jobs over the next eight years.

LG Energy Solution President and Head of the Advanced Automotive Battery Division Dong-Myung Kim stated the investment underscores dedication to driving America's EV transition and bolstering the local economy through quality jobs.
The 30 GWh facility will support production of 300,000 EV units annually at full operations.
Hyundai Mobis will assemble battery packs using cells from the plant and supply them to Hyundai Motor Group's U.S. manufacturing facilities for Hyundai, Kia, and Genesis EV models.
Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority Chairman Carter Infinger stated the announcement will bring 400 additional well-paying jobs to the Savannah region.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated Governor Kemp set a goal of Georgia becoming the top location for EV manufacturing in the U.S., highlighting partners like the Savannah-area JDA, utility providers, Georgia Ports Authority, and Georgia Quick Start.

On Nov. 14, VendTech International (Naturals2Go) announced it would relocate its headquarters, training center, assembly manufacturing, and global distribution/technology development facility to SCMC, creating 125 new jobs and contributing over $20 million in capital investment.
Naturals2Go focuses on unattended retail space with healthy product choices and cashless payment systems technology.

SEDA COO Jesse Dillon stated the Savannah Chatham Manufacturing Center was created to attract companies like Naturals2Go and bring their assembly manufacturing, headquarters, and training center to the region.
On Nov. 30, Governor Brian Kemp announced Plastic Express would build a second shipping facility in Chatham County, creating an estimated 200 new jobs and $80 million in investment.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that more than 70 percent of the previous year's economic development projects were expansions of businesses already operating in Georgia.
Plastic Express is headquartered in Pasadena, Texas, founded in 1970, and offers bulk trucking, terminal, packaging, and warehousing services for the plastics industry.
The company operates two existing facilities in Georgia that support more than 200 jobs.
Plastic Express President and CEO Ray Hufnagel stated Savannah presents a solid opportunity for investment in export logistic centers to complement aggressive capability expansions at the port.
Plastic Express Chief Marketing Officer Jennifer Pittman Fussell stated the new location is strategically located adjacent to the CSX operating yard and within five miles of the port.
Plastic Express will construct its second facility at Rockingham Farms Industrial Park, a 980,000-square-foot site served by CSX with 104 dock doors and 7,200 container spots.
The new Plastic Express packing lines will be capable of packaging 4 million pounds of resin per day, with operations expected to begin mid-2024.
SEDA President and CEO Trip Tollison noted Plastic Express will be the first employer to locate at Rockingham Farms.
The Savannah Logistics Innovation Center (SLIC) graduated its second cohort of startup companies in 2023, bringing the total alumni count to 25.
Companies that have gone through the SLIC accelerator program have raised a total of $80 million.

Einride, a Swedish freight mobility company and part of the first SLIC cohort, signed a partnership with Keen Transport to develop and provide battery-electric vehicle trucks for a shuttle between the Port of Savannah and a Wallenius Wilhelmsen processing facility in Pooler.
Einride is supporting the deployment of the trucks by providing driver training, charging infrastructure, and data insights.
source_title: Business Development – SEDA : Annual Report

facts: Kia Motors said it would suspend production at its U.S. plant on Monday and Tuesday.
The suspension followed media reports that a fire broke out at a parts supplier.
News of the suspension sent shares in Kia and parent Hyundai Motor down due to concerns about possible output disruptions.
Kia's U.S. plant is located in Georgia.
The Georgia plant has an annual production capacity of 300,000 vehicles.
The plant produces the Optima sedan, the Sorento SUV, and Hyundai Motor's Santa Fe SUV.
A South Korean media report said a fire struck a Georgia factory operated by Daehan Solution.
Daehan Solution is an unlisted South Korean parts supplier for Kia.
An official at Daehan Solution declined to comment on the fire.
Kia did not identify the specific reason for the plant suspension.
A Kia spokesman said the suspension would have a limited impact on sales because the facility had an inventory of 1.9 months.
Production is scheduled to resume at 6:30 a.m. EDT on Wednesday, March 21.
Ahn Se-hwan, an analyst at IBK Securities, said the closure could lead to short-term production disruptions for the Santa Fe and Sorento.
Shares in Kia Motors were down 1.9 percent.
Shares in Hyundai Motor were down 0.7 percent.
The wider market was up 0.3 percent as of 0312 GMT.
Kia, an affiliate of Hyundai Motor, saw its monthly sales jump 37 percent to a new record high in February compared to a year earlier.
source_title: Kia Motors says to suspend U.S. plant; shares drop | IBTimes

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Daesol Ausys Georgia will establish a new manufacturing facility in West Point.
The facility will create more than 140 new jobs and $72 million in investment in Harris County.
West Point has become an automotive capital in its own right, according to Governor Brian Kemp.

Georgia's automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for the jobs of the future, said Governor Kemp.
Daesol Ausys was established in Korea in 2017.
The company specializes in designing and manufacturing motor and electric vehicle interior components and accessories.
Daesol Ausys is a key supplier for Kia Georgia, Hyundai Motor Group, and General Motors.
Min Ho Kwon, CEO of Daesol Ausys, stated the company is committed to delivering world-class services that exceed clients' requirements and expectations.
The project will be located in the Northwest Harris Business Park, according to CEO Min Ho Kwon.
Daesol Ausys’ new facility will produce luggage boards and luggage covers.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in December 2024.
The company will be hiring for positions in production, purchasing, quality control, maintenance, human resources, and management.
Robert Grant, Chairman of the Harris County Commission, noted that Daesol Ausys’ decision to locate in the Northwest Harris Business Park represents a commitment to Harris County.
West Point and Harris County have a wonderful relationship with Daesol Ausys’ sister companies Daehan Solutions and Daesol Materials, according to Robert Grant.
Craig Greenhaw, Chairman of the Harris County Development Authority, extended a welcome to Daesol Ausys and expressed excitement for a long and mutually beneficial partnership.
West Point and Harris County continue to work well together, bringing jobs and capital investment into the region, said Craig Greenhaw.
Alyce Thornhill, Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy, represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Harris County Commission, Harris County Development Authority, City of West Point, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that economic development is a team sport driven by the local level and congratulated community partners for helping bring Daesol Ausys to West Georgia.
The automotive industry is providing generational change in the region and across the state, according to Commissioner Pat Wilson.

For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices, encouraged collaboration and innovation, and positioned the state as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
As the market transitions to electric vehicles, Georgia has pursued the entire supply chain, creating more than $25 billion in investments and 30,000 jobs since 2018.
In fiscal year 2023, job creation in the automotive industry increased by 324 percent when compared to FY21.
Suppliers attracted by Georgia’s mobility original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) resulted in over $2 billion in investment across the state in FY23.
Daesol Ausys Georgia is a leading manufacturer of automotive components and a key supplier of luggage boards and luggage covers.
The company has a long history as one of the top suppliers to Kia and Hyundai worldwide.
source_title: Gov. Kemp: Automotive Supplier Daesol Ausys Georgia to Invest $72 Million in Harris County - EIN Presswire

facts: Plans to build a $72 million factory in Georgia to make parts for electric vehicles.
Will hire more than 140 workers.
Announced plans on Tuesday (August 29, 2023) to build the factory in West Point, Georgia.
The new facility will produce luggage boards and covers for compartments in electric vehicles.
Supplies parts to Hyundai, Kia, and General Motors.
This will be the third factory in the same West Point industrial park for members of the company's corporate family.
Daehan Solution Co. opened a $1 million factory in the same industrial park in 2008 to make headliners and other sound insulation parts for the nearby Kia plant.
Daesol Material opened a $35 million factory in the same industrial park in 2017 to make automotive fiber padding.
Daehan Solution currently employs 350 full-time workers.
Daesol Material currently employs 50 workers.
Meghan Richardson serves as an economic developer for the city of West Point.
Daehan Solution also operates a plant near Montgomery, Alabama, supplying parts to the Hyundai assembly plant there.

The announcement follows a wave of EV supplier plant announcements in Georgia after Hyundai announced in 2022 plans to build a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia, near Savannah.
The Hyundai Ellabell site could grow to 8,100 employees and is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
EV suppliers have pledged to invest nearly $2.2 billion and hire 5,000 people for the Georgia region.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated in a statement: 'Our state's automotive industry employs over 50,000 hardworking Georgians and will continue to grow as e-mobility suppliers choose all corners of the state for the jobs of the future.'
Daesol Ausys is scheduled to begin production at the new West Point factory in December 2024.
The company will pay workers $15 to $20 an hour.
The state of Georgia will cover the cost of training workers for the new facility.
The state provided $500,000 last year to help Harris County clear and grade the site and build a road, which the county matched using sales tax collections.
The city of West Point and Harris County are granting eight years of property tax breaks worth a projected $5.7 million.
Daesol Ausys could qualify for $875,000 in state income tax credits, calculated at $1,250 per job over five years, contingent on workers earning at least $31,300 annually.

facts: DAS Corp plans to build a USD 35 million factory in eastern Georgia.
The new factory will supply a new Hyundai Motor Group plant near Savannah.
DAS Corp makes metal frames and other parts for automotive seats.
DAS Corp will hire 300 people to work at the new plant in Metter.
The DAS factory will take up about 200,000 square feet (18,600 square meters) of space.
Starting pay for workers at the new plant will be USD 20 an hour.
The state of Georgia will pay to train workers for the new plant.
The state provided USD 500,000 last year to help local officials complete a road and a sewer connection in the industrial park where DAS is located.
The city of Metter and Candler County are granting 10 years of property tax breaks to DAS Corp.
DAS Corp could qualify for USD 4.5 million in state income tax credits, calculated at USD 3,000 per job over five years.
To qualify for the USD 4.5 million in state income tax credits, workers must make at least USD 31,300 a year.

Suppliers to the Hyundai plant have pledged to hire nearly 5,300 people.
DAS Corp is already a supplier to Hyundai and its sister automaker Kia.
DAS Corp opened a plant in Montgomery, Alabama, in 2012.
The Montgomery, Alabama plant has grown to nearly 500 employees.
DAS Corp Chief Operating Officer Sean Kim stated the company wanted its plant located close to the new Hyundai plant.
DAS Corp aims to begin production in Metter in late 2024.
Besides South Korea, DAS Corp has other plants in China, Turkey, the Czech Republic, India, and Brazil.
DAS Corp is the second company in a week to announce an investment in Metter, which has a population of 4,000 people.

The greenhouses will use local agricultural waste as fertilizer.

4 Fungi's Regenerative will pay workers USD 20 an hour.

The new jobs will raise incomes and allow residents to stop driving elsewhere for work.

Doowon Climate Control America Inc., a Korean auto parts manufacturer and supplier, will invest more than $30 million into a new manufacturing facility in Metter, a town in Candler County.
The Doowon investment will create 200 new jobs.
Doowon Group specializes in designing and manufacturing automotive climate control systems.
Doowon will be a key supplier for Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia (Kia Georgia) and Hyundai Motor Group.
In September 2023, automotive parts company DAS Corp. invested $35 million in a manufacturing facility in Metter.

Governor Brian Kemp stated that suppliers for the Hyundai Metaplant resulted in over $2 billion in investment last fiscal year alone, helping Georgia achieve a third straight year of record-breaking economic growth.
Kemp noted that Georgia's logistics assets, including a reliable network of rail lines and highways, connect companies like DAS to key business partners across the state and to markets worldwide.

facts: Denkai America Inc. is located in South Carolina, United States.
Denkai America Inc. has 11-50 employees.
Denkai America manufactures high-quality electrodeposited copper foils for printed circuit board (PCB), industrial, and energy storage applications.
Denkai America has over 40 years of experience manufacturing copper foil with the highest level of quality and service.
Copper foils are electroplated on revolving titanium drums, which is unique for the ability to deposit copper foils with a high-quality surface.
Rolls of the plated foils are subjected to a variety of surface treating operations specially designed for a wide range of end-use requirements.
During treating, copper dendrites are deposited onto the foil surface to enhance bonding to various resins systems during lamination.
A barrier-layer is deposited for promoting reliability in thermal applications.
Specific proprietary organic layers are applied to promote chemical adhesion.
Foils are stain-proofed to prevent oxidation in shipping, storage, lamination, and subsequent customer processing.
source_title: Denkai America Inc. Company Overview, Contact Details & Competitors | LeadIQ

facts: Augusta is hosting a second copper-focused project bringing hundreds of jobs and hundreds of millions in capital investment from a foreign entity to support Georgia’s electric-vehicle sector.

Denkai America makes copper foil used in printed circuit boards for electronics in the aerospace and defense industry and is setting up a new facility producing similar materials for lithium-ion batteries powering future cars.
The initial investment on the 115-acre site in Richmond County will be $150 million.
The Japanese subsidiary plans to invest another $280 million and hire 250 people as it expands over two additional phases.
The Augusta site will serve as the headquarters for Denkai Americas.

Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s president and CEO, stated in a news release: “We are thrilled to expand our manufacturing presence into Augusta, which has the skilled and talented work force our company needs for growth.”

Denkai follows Aurubis AG, which broke ground on a Richmond County recycling plant producing blister copper, bringing more than 100 jobs and nearly $350 million in capital investment, constituting the largest single German investment in Georgia to date.

Both projects reflect Georgia’s ongoing effort to recruit all components of the electric-vehicle value chain, from raw materials to cars produced by $5 billion-plus plants announced by Rivian and Hyundai, to ensure EV supply-chain resiliency.
Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson called the state's workforce opportunity a “generational opportunity.”
Denkai America is a subsidiary of Nippon Denkai, based in Chikusei City in Ikabara prefecture, about two hours drive north of Tokyo.
The company completed an acquisition of Oak Mitsui Inc. in 2020, including its plant in Camden, S.C., which it expanded with $14 million and 10 additional jobs last year.
The South Carolina facility now employs more than 80 people and will continue operations.
Denkai America initially planned the expansion in South Carolina, but site limitations forced a reassessment as growth projections became more ambitious.
Denkai is already the market leader in copper foils for EV batteries in Japan, holding a 60 percent market share there and 40 percent in North America according to Nikkei.
Denkai sees the market for anode materials growing globally by an average of 21.5 percent per year through 2035, with the U.S. and Japan taking a significant share.
Company projections indicate that not only will the electric vehicle market grow, but battery sizes in cars will also increase, necessitating more copper foil for anodes.
The company forecasts electrics in the U.S. and Japan combined will make up about a quarter of the 62 million EVs it believes will be sold globally by 2035, at a rate of more than 14 percent a year.
When the plant begins shipping samples in the summer of 2024, it will be the only copper foil manufacturer on the East Coast.
The facility will help battery makers like Georgia’s SK Battery reduce dependence on imports and lower their geopolitical risk, according to an outline on the company’s website.

In switching from South Carolina to Georgia, Denkai increased its initial phase production capacity from 9,000 metric tons to 9,500 metric tons and raised its planned capital investment by $10 million.
The plant is expected to triple in size over subsequent phases as dictated by market demand.
A key differentiator for Georgia was Georgia Power’s willingness to lock in a competitive rate for energy prices well into the future, according to a presentation on the company’s website.
The company will reportedly receive land valued at $2.25 million, according to the Associated Press, alongside other incentives likely to materialize, including training from Georgia Quick Start.
Training for new hires in Georgia will be completed at the South Carolina plant, located just two hours away.
Denkai will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.

facts: Denkai America announced it will locate its new manufacturing facility and North American Headquarters in Richmond County.
The company’s initial investment will be $150 million to establish an electrodeposited (ED) copper foil production facility.
The facility will focus on electric vehicle (EV) battery foil.
The project will take place on a 115-acre site.
Denkai America aims to invest a total of $430 million and create 250 new jobs over the next five years.
Maxis Advisors handled the multistate site selection and incentives advisory on behalf of Denkai America.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced the project, stating Georgia is proud to be selected as the home of Denkai America.
Governor Kemp noted the selection strengthens Georgia’s status as a leader in the emerging electric mobility and existing aerospace, defense, and technology industries.
Governor Kemp emphasized the state's focus on bringing quality jobs to highly-trained Georgians and fostering a top-ranking pro-business environment.
Denkai America’s materials have traditionally been used in printed circuit board (PCB) applications for the aerospace and defense industries.
The company's materials are now also used in lithium-ion batteries powering electric vehicles.
Denkai has operated for more than 60 years with team members worldwide and operations in Japan and North America.
Heday Nakajima, Ph.D., Denkai Group President and CEO, stated the investment highlights a commitment to leading ED battery foil manufacturing in North America for many years to come.
Nobu Masuda, Denkai America’s President and CEO, said the company is thrilled to expand its manufacturing presence into Augusta due to its skilled and talented workforce.
Construction is expected to begin in July 2022 as the first part of a three-phase investment.
The facility will be fully operational by December 2024.
The company will be hiring for skilled industrial technicians, production managers, and engineers.

Steven Kendrick, Chairman of the Augusta Economic Development Authority, welcomed Denkai America to the Augusta Corporate Park and thanked the Augusta Commission and Augusta Utilities Department for their support.
Barton Lowrey, Global Commerce Director of Corporate Solutions and Cyber Security at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), represented the department on this competitive project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Augusta Economic Development Authority, Georgia Power, and Georgia Quick Start.
Georgia Quick Start is the Technical College System of Georgia’s nationally ranked workforce training program that has provided specialized training tailored to business needs for more than 40 years.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated the state is committed to harnessing the generational opportunity of electric mobility adoption to ensure Georgians in the automotive industry have access to future jobs at home.
Commissioner Wilson noted Denkai America plays a vital role in the EV ecosystem and welcomed the innovative company to Augusta and Richmond County.
The investment serves as evidence of Georgia’s commitment to building lasting international relationships that drive economic partnerships and recruit the wider electric mobility value chain.
Georgia’s prime location, extensive infrastructure, skilled workforce, and business-friendly climate have made it attractive for diverse industries focused on creating a sustainable future.
Georgia is cultivating a vertically integrated supply chain to help companies increase efficiencies by reducing reliance on imported materials.
EV-related projects have contributed more than $12.6 billion in investments and more than 17,800 new jobs to Georgia since 2020.
Augusta Corporate Park is a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) Certified park.
To earn GRAD certification, a property must undergo due diligence and complete specific requirements prior to a visit by a prospective business.
Completing the GRAD certification program is a proactive way for community planners and landowners to help catalyze economic growth and industrial development in their regions.
source_title: Denkai America announces new North American Headquarters, ED Copper Foil Production Facility in Augusta, Georgia | Maxis Advisors LLC

facts: GDEcD’s Global Commerce division supported 218 locations or expansions during the first half of Fiscal Year 2023 (July 1 through December 31, 2022).
These 218 projects are expected to create approximately 17,500 new jobs and more than $13 billion in investment for the state.
Both the number of supported locations and total investment amount increased over the same period last year.
Areas outside the 10-county metro Atlanta region accounted for 85 percent of jobs created and 92 percent of investments from these economic development projects.
Four of Georgia’s largest industries – advanced manufacturing, automotive, aerospace, and food processing – accounted for nearly 75 percent of new jobs and 84 percent of investments.
Planned investments resulting from these projects increased by more than 38 percent compared to the same time last year.
The automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment, after advanced manufacturing.
In October, state and local officials joined company representatives for the groundbreaking of the new Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America (HMGMA) EV facility.
Korean Ambassador to the U.S. Tae-yong Cho attended the HMGMA groundbreaking event.
The HMGMA EV facility will support more than 8,100 direct jobs in Bryan, Bulloch, Chatham and Effingham Counties.
Three off-site HMGMA suppliers announced locations following the HMGMA groundbreaking.
These three supplier additions swiftly surpassed the initial $1 billion in investment predicted to come from them.
International companies accounted for 23 percent of state projects since July 1, 2022.
International companies are valued partners in creating a closed-loop electric mobility supply chain in Georgia.
The partnership between SK On and Hyundai Motor Group in Bartow County is among the top five largest projects by investment in the state’s history.
Next-generation developer FREYR Battery announced a Coweta County facility, which is also among the top five largest projects by investment in the state’s history.
Battery recyclers Sung-Il Hitech and Denkai America joined Georgia’s growing electric mobility ecosystem.
GDEcD Deputy Commissioner for Global Commerce Kristi Brigman highlighted the Georgia Quick Start program as a key resource for companies to build talent and workforce from the ground up.
The state utilizes GRAD certified sites to support accelerated project timelines.
Georgia’s economic development strategy combines resources and talent of local, state, and international partners to drive innovation and create meaningful economic impact.

facts: Governor Glenn Youngkin announced Eaton, an intelligent power management company, will invest over $50 million to expand its existing manufacturing footprint in Henrico County, Virginia.
Upon completion, the project is expected to create 200 new jobs in the Commonwealth.
The company selected Henrico after assessing multiple locations across the country.
Governor Glenn Youngkin stated: “Eaton’s latest investment in Henrico is proof that Virginia companies and workers are stepping up to meet our growing power needs.”

Governor Glenn Youngkin added: “The only thing better than bringing a new business to Virginia is watching an existing Virginia business expand. By producing even more critical power equipment in the Commonwealth, Eaton is helping drive Virginia’s future and strengthening our competitive edge.”
Secretary of Commerce and Trade Juan Pablo Segura stated: “Eaton’s expansion in Henrico is just another reason to celebrate this holiday season.”
Secretary of Commerce and Trade Juan Pablo Segura noted that 200 more employees will soon work to meet the nation’s rising need for critical power equipment.

Secretary of Commerce and Trade Juan Pablo Segura highlighted that Virginia’s manufacturing sector is surging from Southwest Virginia to Hampton Roads and the Greater Richmond region thanks to forward-looking companies like Eaton.

Aidan Graham, senior vice president and general manager of Critical Power Solutions at Eaton, stated: “Eaton is continuing to invest in U.S. manufacturing and is thankful for the strong collaboration and support in Virginia.”
Aidan Graham noted that the expansion builds on the company's history in the region and reflects the abilities of longtime local employees.

Aidan Graham stated: “We’re uniquely positioned to help our data center customers meet the rapidly increasing power requirements for AI factories through our expansive manufacturing footprint and our focus on innovation and engineering excellence.”
Eaton will open a new campus in Henrico, more than doubling its regional footprint.
The company will occupy a to-be-built 350,000-square-foot facility.
The expansion is expected to add 200 new employees, bringing Eaton’s local team to nearly 500 employees.
This expansion will support production of static transfer switches, power distribution units, and remote power panels.
These products are essential technologies that provide reliable, uninterrupted power.

Eaton’s investment highlights its portfolio of intelligent power management solutions for the data center, utility, industrial, commercial, machine building, residential, aerospace and mobility markets.
Henrico EDA Board of Directors Chair Edward S. Whitlock, III stated: “Eaton’s decision to expand in Henrico reinforces our reputation as a place where manufacturers truly succeed.”
Henrico EDA Board of Directors Chair Edward S. Whitlock, III noted this is Eaton's second major investment in the past five years.
Senator Lashrecse Aird stated: “Henrico continues to prove that when companies invest here, our people and our economy grow with them.”

Senator Lashrecse Aird noted Eaton’s decision to expand again in Eastern Henrico is a clear vote of confidence in the workforce and the Commonwealth’s ability to support advanced manufacturing at a national scale.

Senator Lashrecse Aird stated the project will create well-paying jobs and open the door to new opportunities for local families, strengthening both the community and the region’s economic competitiveness.
Governor Youngkin approved a performance-based grant of $1,000,000 from the Virginia Investment Performance Grant to support Eaton’s investment.
The Virginia Investment Performance Grant is an incentive that encourages continued capital investment by existing Virginia companies.

facts: Founded in 1952, Fouts Brothers Fire Equipment is a family-owned company based in Milledgeville, Georgia.
The company operates a second location in La Grange, Texas.
Fouts Brothers produces a wide range of standard line model trucks designed to meet the needs of all fire stations regardless of department size.
The facility produces 7,500 trucks annually for fire stations across the country.
In 2019, the company's headquarters and main production facility were moved to Milledgeville, located about 90 miles south of Atlanta.
The new Milledgeville facility is a 340,000-square-foot building that required significant renovations to support effective product manufacturing.
Fouts Brothers utilizes a three-compressor system with Ultra Series Rail Mounted dryers from Tsunami Compressed Air Solutions to ensure uninterrupted production lines.
The interconnection of the compressor system has prevented extended shutdowns due to component issues over three years of operation.
GSB Industries, a Tsunami distributor, provided assistance during the renovation of the Milledgeville facility.
Heath Lawrence serves as the Director of Facilities at Fouts Brothers Fire Equipment.
The company has used the Ultra Series system for three years and completed required maintenance to keep manufacturing lines running while avoiding many repairs.
The compressed air system has helped reduce tool replacement costs at the facility.
Pneumatic rivet guns are used extensively in the van line, making clean, dry air critical to prevent water damage and extend tool life.
Regular maintenance of tools shows no signs of rust and corrosion, which was previously common due to summer condensation.
Alternatives considered for the new facility included refrigerant and desiccant dryers.
Lawrence noted that refrigerant dryers fail to remove substantial moisture during high-humidity southern summers and have long-term reliability issues.
Desiccant systems were also considered but have limited volume capacity and enormous ongoing costs for changing the desiccant.
The choice to use regenerative desiccant technology was made after consultation with experts.
Fouts Brothers partnered with GSB Industries, which specializes in installation and service for spray booths, compressors, and related equipment.
Tsunami’s Ultra Series tank-mounted dryer system has prolonged the life of many air tools used at the facility.
The system greatly improved the paint process and quality at the facility.
A Tsunami secondary filter is now used in addition to the primary filter at paint locations.
The facility handles work on around 500 trucks each month, requiring extensive use of air tools.
Water through pneumatic tools leads to extensive wear quickly, making dry air essential for achieving high-gloss paint finishes comparable to higher-end sports cars.
In southern climate zones, the quality of compressed air produced by Tsunami systems is far superior to that of refrigerant units.
The compressed air system reduces repair budgets and the long-term cost of media replacement.
source_title: Case Study: Compressed Air Dryer Use by Fouts Brothers

facts: Fouts Defense announced that it has been awarded contracts through the Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) to supply a total of 14 FB-94 fire apparatus to support U.S. military operations.
The contracts include eight units designated for the U.S. Army and six units for the U.S. Navy, with deliveries scheduled to various locations across the United States.
The FB-94 is a flagship model within Fouts Fire’s line of dependable, standardized apparatus designed for rapid deployment, ease of operation, and long-term reliability.
These units are built to support critical response needs while maintaining the efficiency and value Fouts Fire is known for.
Scott Edens, president and CEO of Fouts Bros Inc., stated that the company is honored to be entrusted with supporting the U.S. Army and U.S. Navy.
Edens noted that the FB-94 was designed with mission readiness in mind, delivering reliability, standardized operation, and long-term maintainability.
The awards reflect confidence placed in Fouts Bros Inc.'s team, manufacturing discipline, and ability to deliver dependable fire apparatus on time and without compromise.
Fouts Bros Inc. is a family-owned company since 1952 that continues to focus on providing high-quality, affordable fire apparatus with industry-leading delivery times.
The company’s standardized manufacturing approach allows for consistent quality while meeting the evolving needs of departments and organizations nationwide.

Porsche’s former North American leader departed to join electric-vehicle startup Rivian.
Porsche named 48-year-old Timo Resch as its new CEO for the North American region.
Timo Resch is returning to where he started his career after stints with other major German automakers.

facts: Fox Factory is a manufacturer of performance shock absorbers and racing suspension products.
Fox Factory selected Carroll Daniel to build its relocated assembly facilities in Georgia inside the Gainesville Industrial Park.
The creation of a campus environment provides an opportunity to foster increased innovation and collaboration between Fox's teams and customers.

The building will house functions necessary to utilize material from a raw material standpoint all the way through manufacturing, anodizing, pre-assembly, full-assembly, and shipping within the same building.
Consolidating these steps reduces the need to ship product to other facilities to complete necessary steps prior to packaging.
This building will be the first for the Fox family that will house anodizing and clean room facilities within a building owned by the company itself.
Owning the facility eliminates the need to outsource activities in their overall shock production process.
Site challenges required creative solutions, including using a Sikorsky helicopter to successfully set 33 HVAC units in place on the roof due to limited crane access.
source_title: Fox Factory

facts: FOX Factory relocated their assembly facilities from Southern California to Gainesville, Georgia.
The new facility represents a $47 million investment.
The plant is vertically integrated and energy efficient for manufacturing shocks and suspension products for off-road powered vehicles.
BRPH designed a 300,000 state-of-the-art facility.
The facility provides more than 176,000-square-feet of assembly area.
The building includes clean rooms, an anodizing line, CNC machining areas and shipping/receiving rooms.
The facility supports 200 employees on site.
Interior design features include branding, themed murals and product displays in and around the large cafeteria, locker rooms, and all-hands meeting areas to elevate company culture.
This facility serves as the first phase of their relocation strategy.
The second level temporarily houses corporate offices and support areas with views down into the assembly areas.
An integrated tour experience is included in the office spaces aimed at interacting with vendors, customers, athletes, local partners and industry sponsors.
This is FOX’s first vertically integrated factory, allowing products to move from materials arrival on one side of the building to finished products and shipping on the other.
Locating manufacturing, anodizing, pre-assembly, full-assembly, and shipping within the same building reduces the need to outsource activities in their overall shock production process.
Tooling includes an anodizing line, CNC machining and lathes.
The facility also includes two clean rooms and nine loading docks.
BRPH received the DBIA National Award of Merit in 2021 and the Distinguished Engineering Project Achievement Award from The Engineers’ Council in 2021 for this project.
source_title: Fox Factory - Fabrication and Assembly Facility - BRPH

facts: SK Innovation Co. is seeking White House intervention to overturn a U.S. International Trade Commission (ITC) ruling that would force it to abandon its $2.6 billion battery factory complex under construction in Georgia, which it had hoped to open later this year.
The Georgia battery complex, located northeast of Atlanta in Commerce, Ga., is expected to create 2,600 jobs and supply electric-car batteries to Ford Motor Co. and Volkswagen AG.
SKI disclosed previously unreported plans to expand the factory complex by investing an additional $2.4 billion and creating 3,400 more jobs by 2025.
Rival battery maker LG Chem met with administration trade officials Friday to pitch for preserving the ITC ruling against SKI.
Nearly two years ago, LG Chem filed a complaint with the ITC accusing SKI of stealing trade secrets related to lithium-ion battery production, allegations SKI has denied.

In a Feb. 10 ruling, the ITC upheld an earlier judge's determination that SKI destroyed evidence in the case and prohibited SKI from importing batteries and components needed for its U.S. factory production for a decade.
The ITC allowed SKI to fulfill existing contracts with Ford and Volkswagen for four and two years, respectively, to give those automakers time to find alternative battery suppliers.
The ruling initiated a 60-day review period during which the White House could overrule the decision on policy grounds.

LG Chem stated it can increase its U.S. battery-making capacity to accommodate Ford and Volkswagen during SKI's import ban and is open to accepting financial restitution from SKI to settle the dispute.
A spokesman for the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative declined to comment on discussions with the two battery makers.

The legal dispute underscores the high stakes for automakers in securing batteries to meet electric vehicle growth targets amid tightening tailpipe emission restrictions and government commitments to phase out gas-powered vehicles.

The case aligns with President Biden's policy priorities of transitioning transportation to electric vehicles, creating jobs, and building domestic battery production capacity to reduce reliance on foreign manufacturers.

On Wednesday, the Biden administration ordered a 100-day review of electric vehicle battery supply chains, as well as those for semiconductor chips, pharmaceuticals, and rare-earth minerals, to spur U.S. industry growth.
South Korean Prime Minister Chung Sye-kyun called the dispute embarrassing on Jan. 28 and stated he had spoken with the heads of both companies to urge resolution.
Ford Chief Executive Jim Farley tweeted that a settlement between SKI and LG Chem would be in the best interests of U.S. manufacturers and workers.
Ford and Volkswagen told the ITC last year that sanctions against the SKI plant could significantly hinder their efforts to build electric vehicles in the U.S.
SKI is set to supply batteries for an electric version of Ford's F-150 pickup truck, which is expected to launch next year.
Volkswagen plans to begin building its ID.4 crossover in Tennessee in 2022 using batteries supplied from SKI.
SKI's presentation to administration officials clarified that the ITC judgment was based on evidence destruction rather than a finding of trade-secret theft.

The presentation highlighted other SK Group investments in U.S.-related businesses, such as last year's $9 billion acquisition of Intel Corp.'s flash-memory manufacturing business by subsidiary SK Hynix Inc.

Presidential intervention in ITC rulings has historical precedent, including the Obama administration's 2013 overruling of a ban on Apple Inc. importing iPhones and iPads following patent infringement allegations from Samsung Electronics Co.
source_title: Pair of South Korean EV battery makers seek to overturn ITC ruling against Georgia factory | Fox Business

Acts as a direct supplier of shocks, suspension, and components to leading powered vehicle and bicycle original equipment manufacturers.
Offers premium baseball and softball gear and equipment through its portfolio of brands including FOX, Marucci, and Method Race Wheels.

facts: Fox Factory executives are located in Duluth, Ga.
All orders for Fox Factory's OE bike products are pre-booked for 2022.
Executives expect no letup in demand until next year or beyond.
Fox Factory announced that its revenues last year hit $1.3 billion, a company record and a 45.9% increase over its 2020 sales.
CEO Mike Dennison said bicycle factories will continue to demand Fox, Race Face, Marzocchi and Easton components well into the future.

Dennison noted that while bikes are starting to show up in dealer showrooms more frequently with better selection, the distribution system from centers to hubs regionally and internationally remains fundamentally empty.
Dennison estimated there are multiple months of demand just to deliver pre-orders, followed by 12 or 18 months of rebuilding inventory.
Fox's customers are confident that there will be "new, fresh demand" for bikes well after the inventory is rebuilt.
Dennison stated Fox has not been a choke point in the supply chain but must carefully balance its production with factories' ability to find other parts needed to complete bikes.
Fox manufactures in Taiwan, the U.S., and Canada, but relies on parts from China.
Fox's bicycle product category sales increased 57.8% last year to $579 million, up from $367 million in 2020.
Fox's Powered Vehicles division saw a sales increase of 37.5% last year to $720 million.
Dennison described the raw material and component supply chain as "a daily battle" for Fox.
Dennison expressed concern about brownouts in China manufacturing cutting energy back, which impacts magnesium and magnesium supply monitoring.
Fox has set a goal of reaching $2 billion in annual sales by 2025.
Fox is forecasting full-year 2022 sales of $1,435 million to $1,465 million.
Fox forecasts non-GAAP adjusted earnings per diluted share for 2022 in the range of $4.90 to $5.20.
Fox's shares are traded on NASDAQ under the FOXF symbol.
source_title: Fox Factory’s annual revenues up 46%; execs see no letup | Bicycle Retailer and Industry News

facts: Arizona-based company Solarcycle announced plans to build a $344 million factory in Cedartown, Georgia.
The new facility will be the first time the company expands into making new glass for solar panels.
Solarcycle plans to hire more than 600 workers at the Cedartown factory.
The factory is located in northwest Georgia, about 50 miles (80 kilometers) northwest of Atlanta.
The factory is scheduled to open in 2026.
Solarcycle was founded in 2022.
The company opened its first recycling facility in Odessa, Texas.
Earlier this month, the company opened a headquarters, research lab, and second recycling facility in Mesa, Arizona.
The Mesa expansion hired more than 100 people.
Solarcycle's automated recycling process can extract materials worth 95% of a solar panel’s value, including silver, silicon, copper, and aluminum.
The Cedartown facility will be able to recycle 1 million solar panels.
The company plans to make enough glass to produce solar panels that could generate 5 gigawatts of electricity per year, using a combination of recycled glass and raw material.
Solarcycle intends to sell the manufactured glass to companies that make solar panels in the United States.
South Korean-owned Qcells, which makes solar panels in nearby Dalton, contracted with Solarcycle last week to recycle decommissioned Qcells panels in the United States.
Solarcycle has similar recycling contracts with more than 40 other solar energy companies.
Spokesperson Brooke Havlik stated the company chose Cedartown to be close to domestic solar panel makers and noted the location offers rail and shipping infrastructure and workers.
Solarcycle has raised tens of millions of dollars from private investors for expansion.
The Cedartown factory will largely be funded through private investment, according to Havlik.
The company received $1.5 million from the U.S. Department of Energy to fund research and development.
Havlik stated that companies buying Solarcycle’s glass are expanding, “largely driven by incentives and tailwinds” created by Biden administration actions.

Democratic U.S. Sen. Raphael Warnock credited President Joe Biden’s clean energy and healthcare law, the Inflation Reduction Act, with spurring Solarcycle’s investment, saying Georgians continue “to reap its benefits.”
Gov. Brian Kemp argued that Georgia’s business environment deserves credit for attracting companies like Solarcycle and Qcells.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson said the company approached state economic recruiters at a trade show.
Wilson stated in a quote: “Solarcycle provides a critical piece to the integrated solar supply chain we are building in Georgia.”
Solarcycle did not disclose specific worker wages, only describing pay and benefits as “competitive.”
The company could qualify for $9 million in state income tax credits, calculated at $3,000 per job over five years.
To qualify for the tax credits, workers must make at least $31,300 a year.
Chris Thomas, president and CEO of the Development Authority of Polk County, confirmed Solarcycle will receive property tax breaks from Cedartown and Polk County but did not provide an estimate.
Georgia will pay to train workers for the facility, according to Solarcycle.
source_title: Arizona company plans $344 million Georgia factory

facts: Elon Musk defended Tesla by stating the company 'receives the least subsidies of any automaker.'
Musk tweeted that federal tax credits apply to other EVs but no longer to Tesla.
Texans raised concerns at a public hearing regarding a school district's proposal to offer Tesla tens of millions in property tax breaks over the next decade.
The proposed incentives are intended to attract Tesla to build a potential $1 billion 'gigafactory' in Travis County.
Austin and Tulsa, Oklahoma are competing for Tesla's new U.S. assembly plant location.
The new assembly plant is planned to manufacture the Cybertruck and Tesla Model Y.
Tesla has stated a preference for locating the factory in the center of the country and closer to East Coast markets.
State and local governments covet auto factories because they create many well-paying jobs that generate income and property taxes.
The Del Valle Independent School District's proposal was disclosed in a Tesla tax application filed earlier this month with the Texas comptroller’s office.
Travis County commissioners are considering a separate tax incentive package for Tesla.

The overall proposal would provide Tesla $68 million in property tax breaks over the next decade to build a new plant off Texas 130 just north of the Colorado River on the southeastern outskirts of Austin.
Comptroller documents indicate the real value of the proposed property would average about $600 million per year.
Without the Del Valle district incentives, Tesla would be required to pay almost $8 million in property taxes per year.
Some residents criticized asking a school district to 'hand over millions' during the coronavirus pandemic as 'simply ridiculous.'
Other residents argued that the local tax breaks would come 'at the expense of children's education.'
Supporters countered that the added jobs would stimulate economic development in the area, which is considered underserved and has a large minority population.
The proposed plant would contain 4 million to 5 million square feet of space, making it Tesla's largest factory to date.
The company previously stated the factory would create 5,000 'middle-skill' jobs and spur an additional 4,000 jobs in the surrounding area.
If Austin is selected, the plant would span thousands of acres on land owned and operated by Martin Marietta.
The existing Martin Marietta plant on the site would be relocated.
Tesla has not announced a timeline for making its final decision on the location.
source_title: Musk defends Tesla as Texas county considers millions in tax breaks to land 'gigafactory' | Fox Business

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies’ commitment to community has been honored in the 2020 Georgia Emerging Automotive Recognition (GEAR) Awards.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development presented the Citizenship Award to the Cleveland plant during a videoconference event on Oct. 14.
Officials pointed to multiple initiatives Freudenberg-NOK’s culture team has put in place as part of the recognition.
Freudenberg-NOK engineers assisted students at White County Middle School’s Rocket Day event.
The company donated funds for a school engineering program in White County.
Freudenberg-NOK teamed up with White County 4-H to plant trees.
The company supported the Turkey in a Box Thanksgiving meal program.
Freudenberg-NOK provided gear to aid first responders during the early stage of the COVID-19 pandemic.

Scott McMurray, deputy commissioner for global commerce at the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stated: “It really is evident through the work of your culture teams how much you value your employees, but also how much emphasis you placed on serving the local communities.”
Several Freudenberg-NOK representatives were on hand for the virtual presentation and credited company-wide support for giving back.
Plant Manager Wyman Hare said: “We have a great group of employees here that put a focus on other people outside of our actual company, but also out in our community.”
Building local partnerships for the betterment of others follows guiding principles of the Freudenberg Group, with financial support from the Freudenberg family, according to Rhonda McLean.

Rhonda McLean, human resources manager for Transmission Driveline Lead Center Oil Seals, Americas, said: “It really is a team effort. It comes from our leadership. It comes from our employees. The plant’s been here 31 years, and they’ve always been so philanthropic.”
McLean thanked the White County Chamber of Commerce for nominating the company for the award.

Beth Truelove, president of the White County Chamber of Commerce, told the White County News: “Freudenberg is a fantastic corporate citizen in White County. It was such a pleasure to see them recognized for all of their contributions to our community.”

Truelove added: “From their dedication to the White County School System as they develop a skilled workforce, to the quality of life that they enhance through support and beautification of our towns, they are the shining example of quality manufacturer that has found a home in White County.”
Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies was one of five companies receiving GEAR Awards.
Nifco KTW America in Toccoa received Supplier of the Year for employing 200-plus people.
Sangsin Technology America in McDonough received Supplier of the Year for employing 199 or fewer people.
Kubota Manufacturing of America Corporation in Gainesville was named Transportation Equipment Manufacturer of the Year.
Panasonic Automotive Systems in Peachtree City received the Automotive Innovation Award.
Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: “It’s a pleasure to celebrate each of our 2020 Georgia GEAR Award winners.”

Wilson noted: “The state’s automotive and mobility industries are major drivers of jobs and innovation, and I congratulate each of our winners on their resilience and thank them for their continued commitment to creating jobs and opportunities for Georgians.”
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK’s community partnerships earn state economic development accolades | White County News, Cleveland, GA

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies (FNST) announced plans to expand its Morristown, Indiana manufacturing facility due to an uptick in automotive industry activity.
The company will pursue a 20,000-square-foot expansion of its existing 65,000-square-foot facility.
FNST plans to hire an estimated 75 new production, supervisory, and administrative employees over the next five years.
The plant currently employs 266 workers.
Freudenberg-NOK’s Morristown plant produces products for both general industry and automotive customers.
The facility’s core products include off-set seals, transmission seals, fuel system seals, driveline seals, and diaphragms.
The plant produces more than 100 million components annually.
The company expects to invest $20 million in the five-year expansion project.
The project investment will include new building construction, interior modifications, driveway modifications, and additional employee parking.
The project investment also includes a significant investment in capital equipment.
FNST executives worked with the Indiana Economic Development Corp., Shelby County Development Corp., and the city of Morristown to secure the expansion project within Indiana and Morristown.
source_title: Freudenberg-NOK To Expand Morristown, Indiana, Facility - AftermarketNews

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies is the Americas joint venture between Freudenberg and Co. in Germany and NOK Corp. in Japan.
The company was founded in 1989 under the legal name Freudenberg-NOK General Partnership.
Freudenberg-NOK is headquartered in Plymouth, Mich., and operates more than 20 facilities across the Americas.
The company's seals and gaskets play a critical role in helping OEM customers achieve optimal engine and transmission performance and are found in virtually every vehicle on the road today.

Freudenberg-NOK completed a series of studies on how ethanol, methanol, lubricants and biodiesel fuel-oil blends impact the molecular structure and performance of various elastomeric sealing materials.
The company collaborated with a major customer on analyzing how exhaust fuel-oil condensate affects vehicle seals.
Freudenberg-NOK provides high temperature alloy (HTA) metal materials, originally developed for the aerospace industry, for use in its automotive Stamped Gaskets product division.
HTA is a family of alloys made from a high nickel content metal that possesses extreme temperature and fatigue cracking resistance suited for smaller powertrains.

Freudenberg-NOK developed Low Emission Sealing Solutions (LESS) products for automotive applications that reduce friction, improve service life, cut emissions, and are smaller and lighter than conventional components.
The Levitex® seal reduces friction up to 90 percent over conventional crankshaft seals and may have applications in construction, agricultural and recreational vehicles.
Material innovations associated with the LESS program include unique elastomeric formulas, nanotechnology treatments and use of nonwovens and fiber in rubber formulas.
Freudenberg-NOK offers custom validation testing services that include the development of performance rigs and product protocols.
The company developed a comprehensive Innovation Management System (IMS) to align its product strategies with those of its customers.
Freudenberg-NOK's industrial portfolio spans 32 market segments from automotive and aerospace to heavy industry, food and beverage and fluid technology.
Torsten Maschke serves as president, Automotive Sales and Marketing, Freudenberg Sealing Technologies.
Freudenberg-NOK received the 2014 TOP Innovation Award from the Frankfurter Allgemeine Zeitung (F.A.Z.) Institute in Berlin.
The company earned finalist honors in the 2014 Society of Plastics Engineers (SPE®) Automotive Division Innovation Awards.
Freudenberg-NOK earned finalist honors in the 2014 IndustryWeek Best Plants competition.
The company's technologies help automotive customers navigate a changing landscape focused on lower fuel consumption, CO2 emissions, alternate fuels and propulsion concepts.
source_title: Advanced BioFuels USA – Innovative Research, Applications and Approaches Keep Freudenberg-NOK Ahead of the Competition

facts: Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced that it has acquired Alto Products Corp.
Atmore, Alabama-based Alto is a manufacturer of clutch plates and automatic transmission parts with a 270,000-square-foot manufacturing facility.
Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced an update to the TransTec 68RFE overhaul kit.
Effective immediately, all kits for Chrysler 68RFE transmissions (2019 and newer) will now include valve body gaskets.
TransTec says including valve body gaskets is “a critical component for a complete and efficient rebuild.”
Transmission Digest met with managers and executives at the Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies packaging facility located in Milan, Ohio.
The Milan, Ohio facility is the home of the well-known TransTec brand of kits and related products.
The Corteco brand is woven into the product offerings from the company.
Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies officially announced that it has acquired Toledo Driveline, LLC as part of its recent agreement to purchase Seal Aftermarket Products (SAP).
Toledo Driveline provides miscellaneous transmission hard parts and electronics, such as drums, planetaries, and solenoids.
Toledo Driveline will be integrated into Freudenberg Sealing Technologies’ Corteco Division.
Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies announced that it has acquired Seal Aftermarket Products (SAP), a Florida-based aftermarket company that sells automotive transmission kits and components.
SAP will be integrated into Freudenberg Sealing Technologies’ Corteco Division.
SAP sells a comprehensive line of automatic transmission rebuild kits, hard parts, components, electronic products, and specialty tools.
Dr. Christian Dickopf is the senior vice president of the Corteco Division at Freudenberg-NOK Sealing Technologies.
Dr. Christian Dickopf has more than 18 years of experience at Freudenberg Group, Corteco’s parent company.
source_title: Freudenberg NOK Archives - Transmission Digest

facts: The facility is located in Cleveland, GA.
The page compiles 5 consecutive years of EPA Toxics Release Inventory Form R filings for Freudenberg-Nok General Ptnr, spanning 2019–2023.
Each annual total aggregates on-site air emissions, surface-water discharges, land disposal, and off-site transfers reported by the facility under EPCRA Section 313.
EPCRA Section 313 requires disclosure whenever a listed chemical is manufactured, processed, or otherwise used above threshold quantities.
Reported releases opened at 11.0K lbs in 2019 and closed at 13.8K lbs in 2023, representing an increase of 25.9% over the window.
The top contributor to cumulative mass by chemical is Methanol at 60.5K lbs.
In 2019, total releases were 11.0K lbs involving 1 chemical.
In 2020, total releases dropped to 8.8K lbs involving 1 chemical, a year-over-year change of -20.0%.
In 2021, total releases rose to 13.1K lbs involving 1 chemical, a year-over-year change of +48.4%.
In 2022, total releases were 13.7K lbs involving 1 chemical, a year-over-year change of +5.1%.
In 2023, total releases were 13.8K lbs involving 1 chemical, a year-over-year change of +0.8%.
Methanol release amounts by year were: 11.0K lbs in 2019, 8.8K lbs in 2020, 13.1K lbs in 2021, 13.7K lbs in 2022, and 13.8K lbs in 2023.

TRI figures are estimated rather than metered, so year-over-year deltas can reflect methodology revisions, ownership changes, or shifts in what a facility classifies as reportable, not only physical emission changes.
The page describes the site as a plastics and rubber facility.
A decrease in reported releases does not necessarily indicate improved environmental performance, as it may reflect production cutbacks, facility closures, or changes in reporting requirements.
Increases in reported releases may reflect expanded production rather than degraded environmental practices.
source_title: Freudenberg-Nok General Ptnr - 5-Year Emissions Trend (2019–2023)

GEDIA Georgia operates as part of the global GEDIA Automotive Group.
The company specializes in advanced lightweight body-in-white structures and precision components.
Job locations are at GEDIA Georgia in Dalton, GA (On-site).
One production role involves performing manual weld repairs prior to full production and supporting several key production functions.
The company offers competitive pay and benefits including health care, 401k, holidays, paid time off, short term disability, and paid paternity leave.
Openings include temporary work with flexible hours and shift work options such as afternoon shifts and day shifts.
Specific vacancies listed are Shipping Logistics Clerk (1st Shift) and Manager Machine and Facility Maintenance.

facts: GEDIA Automotive Group is a family-owned international company that specializes in making state-of-the-art automotive parts.
GEDIA currently supplies automotive parts to nearby companies such as Volkswagen, BMW, and Mercedes-Benz.
Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that GEDIA will build an advanced manufacturing facility in Georgia.
The new facility will begin production with a focus on parts for electric vehicles.
GEDIA will invest $85 million in the project.
The project will deliver 200 jobs to the greater Dalton area.
The facility will be located at a Georgia Ready for Accelerated Development (GRAD) certified property in Whitfield County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia is a national leader in manufacturing, logistics, and workforce training.
Governor Kemp expressed confidence that GEDIA will be pleased to join the growing electric vehicle ecosystem in Georgia.
Governor Kemp noted that the state's consistent investment in its Germany-based European office is paying off.
Governor Kemp stated he looks forward to seeing the opportunities GEDIA creates for hardworking Georgians in Whitfield County.
The announcement was sourced from the State of Georgia on July 24, 2020.
Auto manufacturing in Right to Work states is bringing more construction jobs to the region.

facts: The State of Georgia has become a center of international business, with Atlanta and Savannah serving as primary global gateways for people and products through air, sea, and rail.

State leadership under then-Governor Jimmy Carter began establishing Georgia’s physical presence abroad fifty years ago (in 1973), kickstarting the state’s trajectory to becoming a global hub for business and innovation.

Coryn Marsik serves as Director of International Investment at the Georgia Department of Economic Development, stating that over 50 years of international representation has given Georgia's economy remarkable resiliency by recruiting diverse investments that create jobs and help companies reach new global markets.
The State of Georgia’s first international office was established in Tokyo in 1973 to provide direct access to rapidly developing East Asian markets.

Japanese companies continue to be the top international employer in Georgia to this day, supported by an active community of organizations such as the Japan-America Society of Georgia and the Japan External Trade Organization.
Georgia serves as a charter member in the Southeast U.S.-Japan Alliance.
Joseph Huntemann serves as Managing Director of the State of Georgia’s Japan Office, providing insights on how the office supports jobs and opportunity for Georgians.
The State of Georgia’s Europe Office was established shortly after the Tokyo office, with professionals serving the European market from Munich, Germany.
Germany is one of Georgia’s top investor nations and has a robust mobility industry that anchors Georgia’s mission to attract innovative technologies from Europe.
In 2020, Governor Brian Kemp and a State of Georgia delegation met with automotive companies headquartered in Germany, where electrification and alternative fuels were repeatedly discussed.
Following the 2020 trip, the state set a new goal to identify opportunities at every step of the mobility and battery supply chain.
Just three years after the 2020 delegation trip, the mobility and battery supply chain announced more than $25.5 billion in investments and an estimated 30,700 new jobs over the next few years.
European-based companies enhancing Georgia’s mobility ecosystem include GEDIA Automotive, Aurubis AG, and Heliox.

Governor Brian Kemp served on a panel at the 2023 World Economic Forum in Davos-Klosters, Switzerland, sharing that “one thing you can count on is stability and a great economy, a great business environment in the State of Georgia.”

Ellen Kraft serves as Managing Director of the Europe Office, noting that the European market offers opportunities for attracting innovative companies, helping Georgia businesses connect with new customers, and establishing new ties like the North Rhine-Westphalia (NRW) office in Atlanta.
Pat Wilson serves as Commissioner of the Georgia Department of Economic Development, explaining how decades of visionary leadership has allowed Georgia to reap long-term economic benefits.
International businesses operating in Georgia tap into university-backed research, innovative ecosystems, and solutions-oriented partnerships across local, state, and industry levels.

links.context: Commissioner Pat Wilson's explanation of Georgia's industrial success

facts: Dana Incorporated announced a definitive agreement to combine with Eaton Corporation plc's Mobility business in a transaction valued at approximately $5.1 billion.

The combined company is expected to have approximately $11 billion in sales and approximately $1.7 billion adjusted EBITDA (approximately 15% adjusted EBITDA margin) on a fully synergized, pro forma 2026 estimated basis.

The transaction values Eaton Mobility at approximately $5.1 billion on a cash-free, debt-free basis, representing approximately 8.3x estimated 2026 pro forma adjusted EBITDA before synergies, or approximately 5.9x including run-rate synergies.
The combined company is expected to achieve $250 million of annual run-rate synergies within 24 months following closing.
Synergies will be driven by reduced structural costs, purchasing scale, manufacturing optimization, and engineering efficiencies.

The transaction is structured as a Reverse Morris Trust transaction resulting in Eaton shareholders owning at least 50.1% and Dana shareholders owning approximately 49.9% of the combined company at close.
Under the agreement terms, Eaton will receive a cash distribution of approximately $1.1 billion (subject to adjustments for cash and indebtedness), funded through new debt raised prior to closing.
R. Bruce McDonald, Dana's Chairman, will serve as Executive Chairman of the combined company with responsibility for integration and synergy realization.
Byron Foster will serve as Chief Executive Officer of the combined company, assuming his role on July 1, 2026.
Timothy Kraus will continue as Chief Financial Officer of the combined company.
Erin Rowse, Eaton's Senior Vice President Human Resources for Industrial, will serve as Chief Human Resources Officer at closing.
The combined company's Board of Directors will be comprised of all members of Dana's Board of Directors and three Eaton designees.

The combination integrates Dana's global powertrain, thermal, and sealing technologies with Eaton Mobility's commercial vehicle transmissions, engine and emissions products, and advanced electrification capabilities.
Byron Foster stated the transaction marks an important milestone in Dana's transformation and positions it as a leading, scaled provider of powertrain solutions.

Byron Foster noted that expanding presence in core markets with new products and complementary technologies enhances the ability to deliver greater value to customers while strengthening margins through a more balanced portfolio and meaningful synergies.

Paulo Ruiz, Eaton Chief Executive Officer, stated the combination creates a leading global engineering solutions partner well positioned to serve commercial vehicle and light vehicle markets worldwide.

Timothy Kraus stated the addition of Eaton Mobility increases Dana's 2030 strategy targets to $14–$15 billion in sales, approximately 18% adjusted EBITDA margins, and an 8%-9% adjusted free cash flow margin by 2030.
Prior to this announcement, Dana's 2030 targets included approximately $10 billion in sales, 14% to 15% adjusted EBITDA margins, and a 6% adjusted free cash flow margin.
After funding the approximately $1.1 billion cash distribution to Eaton, Dana expects to maintain a strong balance sheet with approximately 1.2x net leverage on a pro forma 2026 estimated basis.
The combined company will continue to operate under the Dana Incorporated name and retain its listing on the New York Stock Exchange.
The transaction is expected to be tax-free to Dana and Eaton shareholders for U.S. federal income tax purposes.
The transaction is expected to close in the first quarter of 2027, subject to approval by Dana shareholders, receipt of regulatory approvals, and other customary closing conditions.
Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC is serving as exclusive financial advisor to the combined company.
Kirkland & Ellis LLP is serving as legal counsel to Dana.
Ernst and Young LLP is serving as transaction advisor.
Goldman Sachs Bank USA is serving as sole underwriter of the committed financing for the transaction.
Dana Incorporated is headquartered in Maumee, Ohio, USA.
Dana reported sales of $7.5 billion in 2025.
Dana employs 27,000 people in 24 countries across six continents.
Dana supplies leading vehicle manufacturers and related aftermarkets with drive systems, electrodynamic technologies, and thermal and sealing solutions.

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. plans to create 100 jobs and invest at least $100 million, with potential up to $330 million, in an expansion at its existing facility in Monroe, Georgia.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. is a Tier 1 supplier of world-class products to the global automotive market.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Hitachi has been a great partner to the Walton County community by investing in employees and creating jobs for more than twenty years.
Governor Brian P. Kemp expressed confidence that the investment will positively impact the local community and encourage continued success in Georgia’s strong automotive industry.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. is a group company of Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd., which is headquartered in Tokyo.

Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd. manufactures and markets engine powertrain systems, electric powertrain systems, and integrated vehicle control systems for major automotive original equipment manufacturers (OEM) worldwide.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. President and CEO Paul Carroll stated the company has been operating in Georgia for over twenty-two years.
The Georgia factory supplies advanced technology products for major automotive OEMs including Ford Motor Company, General Motors, Nissan, Subaru, and Honda.
Paul Carroll highlighted the partnership with stakeholders including the State of Georgia, Walton County, and the City of Monroe.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas, Inc. opened its first facility in Georgia in 1997.
The company currently employs 900 Georgians.
The expansion aims to increase production capacity in response to the growth of the automotive industry.
Walton County Commission Chairman Kevin Little stated that Hitachi has become the county's largest employer.
Kevin Little expressed gratitude for the quality jobs and investment Hitachi provides in Walton County.
City of Monroe Mayor John Howard described Hitachi as one of Monroe's finest corporations investing in high technology and top-quality jobs.
Mayor John Howard praised the company's community support and involvement as second to none.

GDEcD Sr. Project Manager Tammy Caudell represented the Global Commerce Division in partnership with the Development Authority of Walton County, Walton EMC, and the Georgia Department of Community Affairs.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that establishing relationships with job-creating companies like Hitachi elevates their work.
Pat Wilson noted that companies like Hitachi have driven success and increased interest from Japanese companies in Georgia.
Hitachi Automotive Systems, Ltd. is a wholly owned subsidiary of Hitachi, Ltd., headquartered in Tokyo, Japan.

The company develops, manufactures, sells, and services automotive components, transportation related components, industrial machines and systems, and offers engine powertrain systems, electric powertrain systems, and integrated vehicle control systems.

The company aims to uphold 5 core values of the Hyundai Motor group: Challenge, Collaboration, Customer, Globality, and People.
Glovis opens a port in Tampa, FL.

Quality is fundamental to Glovis America’s business, with integrated efforts striving to deliver service excellence to customers and communities by assuring performance, efficiency, consistency and value.
The company is committed to delivering outstanding service to its customers by assuring its performance, efficiency, consistency and value.

facts: Benore Logistic Systems has added 14 hydrogen-powered Hyundai Xcient trucks to its fleet.
The vehicles are now in daily service around Savannah, Georgia.
The trucks are being used on dedicated routes to support Glovis EV America’s logistics operation for Hyundai’s new Metaplant.
The rollout began quietly in January with four trucks, and by March, all 14 were up and running.
Benore is based in Spartanburg County, South Carolina.
The company says the trucks offer zero tailpipe emissions, efficient refuelling, and performance expected in heavy-duty logistics.
The hydrogen trucks join eight battery-electric trucks already in the company’s fleet.
The hydrogen trucks are running full-time, hauling freight on fixed routes, Just-in-Time.
Benore’s involvement started with Glovis, Hyundai’s logistics arm, through a long-standing partnership in Georgia.
That relationship expanded into work with Glovis EV America and led to the company’s role supporting Hyundai’s EV production at the Savannah Metaplant.
Hyundai Motor Company manufactures the Xcient trucks.
Deployment is handled by HTWO Logistics, a joint venture between Hyundai and Glovis America.
Benore manages the fleet and day-to-day operations for these hydrogen trucks.
Drivers have completed specific training for the hydrogen trucks ahead of launch.
Dennis Kunz is Vice President of Revenue Strategy and Operation Development at Benore.
Dennis Kunz stated that the hydrogen fuel cell trucks represent a significant step forward for Benore and its ability to deliver innovative, sustainable logistics solutions.
Dennis Kunz noted that partnerships with both Hyundai and Glovis are key to the company’s mission and values around sustainability and exploring advanced technology.
Benore’s trucks follow Hyundai’s own earlier deployment of 21 Xcient fuel cell trucks at the same site.
The Hyundai fleet is operated by Glovis America and was the first major deployment of Hyundai’s hydrogen trucks in the U.S.
Together, the two fleets are now a regular presence around the HMGMA site in Savannah.
Hyundai sees the Xcient as a ready-for-work fuel cell solution, capable of quick refuelling and uptime logistics operators rely on.
Benore describes the vehicles as part of its push toward cleaner transport, aligned with a broader strategy to adopt advanced vehicle technology.
source_title: Benore adds 14 hydrogen Hyundai trucks to fleet, now ferrying EVs in Georgia - Driving Hydrogen
