entity_name: Department of Energy’s Energy Storage Grand Challenge (ESGC)
facts: The Department of Energy’s (DOE) Energy Storage Grand Challenge (ESGC) is a comprehensive program to accelerate the development, commercialization, and utilization of next-generation energy storage technologies and sustain American global leadership in energy storage.
The ESGC program is organized around five crosscutting pillars: Technology Development, Manufacturing and Supply Chain, Technology Transitions, Policy and Valuation, and Workforce Development.
These pillars are critical to achieving the ESGC’s 2030 goals.

Foundational to these efforts is the need to fully understand the current cost structure of energy storage technologies and identify research and development opportunities that can impact further cost reductions.

The second edition of the Cost and Performance Assessment continues ESGC’s efforts of providing a standardized approach to analyzing the cost elements of storage technologies, engaging industry to identify these various cost elements, and projecting 2030 costs based on each technology’s current state of development.

This data-driven assessment of the current status of energy storage technologies is essential to track progress toward the goals described in the ESGC and inform the decision-making of a broad range of stakeholders.
Not all energy storage technologies are being addressed in the report due to the breadth of technologies available and their various states of development.
Future efforts will continue to expand the list of energy storage technologies covered while providing any significant updates to cost and performance data for previous technologies.

The 2022 Cost and Performance Assessment includes five additional features comprising of additional technologies & durations, changes to methodology such as battery replacement & inclusion of decommissioning costs, and updating key performance metrics such as cycle & calendar life.

The 2020 Cost and Performance Assessment provided installed costs for six energy storage technologies: lithium-ion (Li-ion) batteries, lead-acid batteries, vanadium redox flow batteries, pumped storage hydro, compressed-air energy storage, and hydrogen energy storage.
The assessment adds zinc batteries, thermal energy storage, and gravitational energy storage to the 2022 edition.
The 2020 Cost and Performance Assessment provided the levelized cost of energy (LCOE).
The 2022 Cost and Performance Assessment provides the levelized cost of storage (LCOS) instead.
Both metrics determine the average price that a unit of energy output would need to be sold at to cover all project costs inclusive of taxes, financing, operations and maintenance, and others.

Shifting toward LCOS as a separate metric allows for the inclusion of storage-specific components and terminology that can be more accurately defined when compared to the levelized cost of energy calculation.
This includes the cost to charge the storage system as well as augmentation and replacement of the storage block and power equipment.
The LCOS offers a way to comprehensively compare the true cost of owning and operating various storage assets and creates better alignment with the new Energy Storage Earthshot.
This report incorporates an increase in Li-ion iron phosphate and nickel manganese cobalt Li-ion cycle life and calendar life based on input from industry partners.
Recycling and decommissioning are included as additional costs for Li-ion, redox flow, and lead-acid technologies.
The 2020 Cost and Performance Assessment analyzed energy storage systems from 2 to 10 hours.
The 2022 Cost and Performance Assessment analyzes storage system at additional 24- and 100-hour durations.
In September 2021, DOE launched the Long-Duration Storage Shot which aims to reduce costs by 90% in storage systems that deliver over 10 hours of duration within one decade.
The analysis of longer duration storage systems supports this effort.
source_title: 2022 Grid Energy Storage Technology Cost and Performance Assessment | Department of Energy

facts: U.S. Department of Transportation’s NEVI Formula Program required the Alabama Department of Transportation to submit an annually updated EV Infrastructure Deployment Plan for fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
Plans must describe how the state intends to distribute NEVI funds and their physical and cybersecurity strategies, including a Community Engagement Outcomes Report.
Submitted plans must address updated NEVI Guidance.

The Alabama Department of Economic and Community Affairs offers grants for light-duty EV chargers and the replacement of qualified medium- and heavy-duty diesel vehicles with new diesel or alternative fuel vehicles.

Grants are available for EV chargers; medium- and heavy-duty trucks; school, shuttle, and transit buses; freight switchers; airport ground support equipment; and forklifts and port cargo handling equipment.
Vehicles must meet model year requirements to qualify for grants.
Funding amounts are based on vehicle type, fuel type, and applicant type.
Grants are funded by Alabama’s portion of the Volkswagen Environmental Mitigation Trust.

The Alabama Department of Economic and Community Affairs administers the Alabama Research and Development Enhancement Fund Program to encourage new and continuing research and development efforts within the state to increase employment opportunities and products/services for citizens.
The production of biofuel is eligible for ARDEF funding.
Companies that invest in the development of a biofuel production facility may be eligible for a tax credit of 3% of the previous year’s annual employee wages.

Companies may claim the 3% wage tax credit against utility gross receipts and utility service use taxes for up to 10 years, with options for refundability during the incentive period or carryforward for earned but unused amounts.
Companies may also be eligible for a tax credit of 1.5% of qualified capital investment annually for up to 10 years.

The 1.5% capital investment tax credit may be claimed against income tax, estimated income taxes, financial institution excise tax, or insurance premium tax, with carryforward options for unused amounts.
For the purposes of the credit, biofuel is defined as a motor vehicle fuel produced from grain, starch, oilseeds, vegetable, algae, animal materials, or other biomass.
To be eligible for the biofuel tax credits, companies must execute a project agreement with the Governor.
Any motor vehicle equipped with an auxiliary power unit or other idle reduction technology may exceed gross, axle, tandem, or bridge formula weight limits by up to 400 pounds.
Eligibility for the idle reduction weight exemption requires written proof or certification of the APU weight and demonstration that the technology is fully functional at all times.

A vehicle primarily powered by natural gas may exceed the state’s gross vehicle weight limits by a weight equal to the difference between the NGV tank/system weight and a comparable diesel tank/system weight.
The NGV maximum gross weight may not exceed 82,000 pounds.
The Alabama Department of Economic and Community Affairs administers the EV Charging Infrastructure Program.
Government and non-government entities are eligible to receive funding for installing publicly accessible DC fast chargers and Level 2 EV chargers along non-interstate corridors.
Alabama Power is an investor-owned utility operating in Alabama that provides incentives related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
Central Alabama Electric Cooperative is a cooperative operating in Alabama that provides incentives related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
City of Bessemer Utilities is a municipal utility operating in Alabama that provides incentives related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
City of Tarrant is a municipal utility operating in Alabama that provides incentives related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
Coosa Valley Electric Cooperative is a cooperative operating in Alabama that provides incentives related to alternative fuels and vehicles.
Tennessee Valley Authority is a municipal utility operating in multiple states that provides incentives related to alternative fuels and vehicles.

Alabama Power lists EV Time-of-Use rate, DC fast charging station purchase rebate, and DC fast charging station installation rebate as incentive types, though notes no incentives are currently offered for these categories.
Central Alabama Electric Cooperative, City of Bessemer Utilities, City of Tarrant, and Coosa Valley Electric Cooperative note no residential or non-residential EV incentives are currently offered.

Tennessee Valley Authority lists EV TOU rate, DC fast charging station purchase rebate, DC fast charging station installation rebate, and EV charger deployment pilot programs as incentive types, though notes none are currently offered.
The Alabama Department of Transportation is authorized to administer the Electric Transportation Infrastructure Grant Program to distribute grants for EV charging infrastructure expansion.

An autonomous vehicle may be operated without a driver physically present if capable of complying with federal and Alabama traffic laws, certified per federal motor vehicle safety standards, can achieve a minimal risk condition if unable to perform a task or exits its operational design domain, and has proof of insurance.
An entity that owns, operates, leases, or controls electric vehicle charging stations is not defined as a public utility under Alabama law.
EV owners must pay an annual fee in addition to standard registration fees.
All-electric vehicle owners must pay an annual fee of $203 and plug-in hybrid electric vehicle owners must pay an annual fee of $103.
Beginning in 2023, EV fees increase by $3 every four years.
A portion of EV fees contributes to the Electric Transportation Infrastructure Grant Program for EV charging stations.
The state road tax for propane vehicles is paid through an annual flat fee sticker based on the vehicle’s gross vehicle weight rating.
Each person owning or operating a propane vehicle must obtain a decal annually from the Alabama LPG Board as proof of payment.
Vehicle owners must apply for a propane decal within 10 days of converting a vehicle to operate on propane, or a 20% penalty will be applied.
Out-of-state alternative fuel vehicle operators purchasing propane in Alabama must pay the current Alabama motor fuel tax or elect the annual flat fee decal.
Propane dealers or suppliers must remit collected funds to the LPG Board before the 20th of the month following the date of sale.
The Alabama Department of Revenue administers an excise tax on compressed natural gas and liquefied natural gas used as vehicle fuel.
CNG taxes are set at $0.13 per GGE from October 1, 2023, until September 30, 2028, and $0.18 per GGE from October 1, 2028 onward.
LNG taxes are set at $0.13 per DGE from October 1, 2023, until September 30, 2028, and $0.18 per DGE from October 1, 2028 onward.
A GGE equals 5.66 pounds or 126.67 cubic feet of natural gas for CNG, and a DGE equals 6.06 pounds for LNG.
The Alabama Legislature established a Green Fleets Review Committee and Green Fleets Policy outlining procurement procedures for state vehicles based on fuel economy and life cycle costing.
State fleet managers must classify their vehicle inventory for compliance and submit annual plans for procuring fuel-efficient vehicles.
Plans must reflect a 4% annual increase in average fleet fuel economy for light-duty vehicles, a 3% increase for medium-duty vehicles, and a 2% increase for heavy-duty vehicles per fiscal year.
Government entities must manage and operate fleets to be energy efficient, minimize emissions, and reduce petroleum dependency using proven technology identified by the Committee.

facts: Novelis Inc. released its sustainability report for the 2025 fiscal year (April 1, 2024, to March 31, 2025) on September 24, 2025.
The report outlines progress toward the company's 'Novelis 3x30' vision to advance aluminum as the material of choice with circular solutions.
Novelis achieved a 10% reduction in greenhouse gas intensity in the last fiscal year across Scope 1, location-based Scope 2, and Scope 3 (categories 1 and 4).
The company implemented new technologies in remelt furnaces to improve efficiency, reduce fuel and energy use, and increase the use of low-carbon primary aluminum inputs.
Novelis recycled more than 84 billion used beverage cans during the reporting period.
The company achieved a 63% average recycled content across all its products.
Novelis reduced waste to landfill intensity by 12%.
The company reduced water intensity by 8%.
Novelis donated $8.8 million to the communities in which its employees work and live.
The company aims to accelerate circularity and achieve 75% average recycled content in its products.
Novelis targets becoming the lowest emissions flat rolled products (FRP) aluminum provider with less than 3 tonnes of CO2e per tonne of FRP shipped.

Francisco Pires serves as SVP and Chief Strategy and Sustainability Officer, stating that the company aligns its business strategy with circular economy principles to deliver long-term value for customers, communities, and the planet.
Novelis is a global leader in the production of innovative aluminum products and solutions and the world's largest recycler of aluminum.

The company aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy by partnering with suppliers and customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can, and specialties industries across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.
Novelis reported net sales of $17.1 billion in fiscal year 2025.

Novelis Inc. is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
source_title: Novelis Releases 2025 Sustainability Report

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a new facility in Locust Grove.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The project will bring $72 million in investment to Henry County.
Governor Brian P. Kemp stated that Georgia has a reliable infrastructure network connecting partners globally and a strong workforce that enables job creator success.
Governor Brian P. Kemp highlighted Georgia's incredible growth in the e-mobility and battery industries, calling it a testament to supporting large suppliers like NVH Korea.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in Ulsan, South Korea, established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts currently produced in Korea, Europe, and India.
NVH Korea already operates a manufacturing facility in Georgia that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays.
The new Locust Grove facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States.
Key customers for the new facility include Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
Ja Kyum Koo, Chairman and CEO of NVH Korea, stated that Georgia is the company's first EV battery component manufacturing site globally.
Ja Kyum Koo noted that cooperation between state agencies and company staff guided the project selection process.
The new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and sense the performance of battery cells.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Mayor Robert Price stated that NVH’s investment will increase Locust Grove's manufacturing jobs base and integrate it into Georgia’s electric vehicle ecosystem.
Carlotta Harrell, Chair of the Henry County Board of Commissioners, stated the project adds quality manufacturing jobs for citizens and diversifies the county's economy.
Project Manager Jacob Lee represented the Georgia Department of Economic Development’s (GDEcD) Global Commerce team on this competitive project.
The project was supported in partnership with the Henry County Development Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson noted that manufacturers are creating future jobs aligned with cutting-edge technologies and industry trends.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson welcomed NVH Korea as an innovator establishing its first U.S. or e-mobility manufacturing presence in Georgia.
For over a century, Georgia has fostered healthy industry practices and positioned itself as a leader in automotive and mobility technology development.
From July 1 through December 31, 2022, the automotive industry was the second-largest source of new jobs and investment in Georgia.
Since 2020, Georgia's pursuit of EV supply chain job creation has resulted in more than $22.7 billion in investments and over 28,400 jobs created.
The announcement date is June 22, 2023.
source_title: EV parts supplier NVH Korea to create over 160 jobs, invest $72M in Henry County – Savannah Harbor-Interstate 16 Corridor Joint Development Authority

entity_name: Industrial Training and Assessment Centers (ITAC) Implementation Grants
canonical_name: Industrial Training and Assessment Centers (ITAC) Implementation Grants
facts: 219 small- and medium-sized manufacturers (SMMs) across 38 states selected to receive a combined total of more than $40 million to implement energy saving improvements.
The ITAC Implementation Grants are matched by more than $83 million in industry investment.
The program is funded by the President’s Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
The ITAC Implementation Grants program provides up to $300,000 per manufacturer to implement recommendations made by DOE and other qualified energy assessments.
A total of 34 additional energy assessment providers have been qualified as "ITAC-equivalent."
Projects funded include installing Light-emitting Diode (LED) lighting to optimize energy usage.
Projects funded include installing auxiliary equipment (e.g., monitors, sensors, level and pressure gauges) to improve day-to-day operation equipment & improve energy efficiency.
Projects funded involve upgrading existing heating or cooling equipment to optimize energy usage.
Projects funded include installing onsite power generation equipment (e.g., solar panels, micro-turbines) to generate electricity at the facility/plant.
Projects funded include installing variable-frequency drives on existing equipment (e.g., such as motors, pumps, compressors) to optimize energy usage.
Projects funded include installing a capacitor bank to ensure accurate electricity demand and minimize site energy waste.
Projects funded involve upgrading other industrial grade ancillary equipment (e.g., pipes, fans, blowers, & compressors) to further improve site energy usage.

On December 12, 2024, DOE announced 57 new small- and medium-sized manufacturers will receive a combined total of $14.8 million selected to implement improvements at facilities that will save energy and reduce climate pollution (Round 5).

On September 12, 2024, DOE announced 47 new small- and medium-sized manufacturers will receive a combined total of $9 million selected to implement improvements at facilities that will save energy and reduce climate pollution (Round 4).

On June 27, 2024, DOE announced 37 new small- and medium-sized manufacturers will receive a combined total of $7.3 million selected to implement improvements at facilities that will save energy and reduce climate pollution (Round 3).

On March 14, 2024, DOE announced 37 new small- and medium-sized manufacturers will receive a combined total of $4.8 million selected to implement improvements at facilities that will save energy and reduce carbon emissions (Round 2).

On October 6, 2023, DOE announced 41 small- and medium-sized manufacturers (SMMs) across the country will receive a combined total of $5.2 million as the first round of Industrial Training and Assessment Centers (ITAC) Implementation Grants.
A total of 34 additional energy assessment providers have been selected for qualification as "ITAC-equivalent."

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC (PASA) announced the appointment of Kevin Ostertag as President, effective April 1, 2026.
Kevin Ostertag succeeds Scott Kirchner, who has been serving in a dual role as both President of PASA and Global Chief Operating Officer (COO) of Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd (PAS).

Since 2021, Kevin Ostertag has served as Chief Financial Officer of PASA, where he played a key role strengthening performance, driving efficiencies and strategically positioning the organization for diversified growth.
Scott Kirchner served as President of PASA since 2017.

Under Scott Kirchner's leadership, PASA underwent a significant transformation to position it for accelerated growth while remaining rooted in its cultural values and commitment to delivering high-quality, safe technologies to the world's leading OEMs.
Scott Kirchner will continue to serve as Global COO of PAS after stepping down as President of PASA.
In April 2027, Panasonic Automotive will change its company name and brand to Mobitera to further position the organization for accelerated growth and optimize its overall business portfolio.
PASA is a premier supplier of automotive and mobility technologies for the world's leading OEMs.
PASA's North American operations are headquartered in Peachtree City, GA with locations across the region strategically positioned to serve customers.

links.context: Corporate office list and about page for PASA

source_title: Kevin Ostertag Named President of Panasonic Automotive Systems America, LLC

facts: In January 2025, the U.S. Department of Energy’s Loan Programs Office announced the closing of a $15 billion loan guarantee to Pacific Gas & Electric Company (PG&E).
The loan guarantee for PG&E’s Project Polaris will support a portfolio of projects to expand hydropower generation and battery storage.
The portfolio includes upgrading transmission capacity through reconductoring and grid enhancing technologies.
The portfolio will enable virtual power plants throughout PG&E’s service area in Northern and Central California.
LPO’s financing comes at a lower interest rate than traditional capital market financing, helping reduce upward pressure on electricity costs for PG&E’s 16 million customers.
At full deployment, PG&E’s investments are expected to support thousands of on-going construction and operations jobs.
PG&E plans to expand outreach programs to deliver community benefits in partnership with local governments, Native American Tribes, community-based organizations, and low-and-middle income customers.
PG&E has committed to locate many projects in disadvantaged communities, as identified by the Climate and Economic Justice Screening Tool.
Project Polaris is owned by PG&E.
The loan program is 1706.
The guaranteed loan amount is $15 billion.
The issuance date was January 2025.
The project supports more than 1,000 permanent U.S. jobs.
source_title: Polaris | Department of Energy

facts: Pirelli is reinforcing its long-term commitment to the United States with a key step in its product and industrial strategy.

The Georgia plant, already dedicated to the most technologically advanced products for the U.S. market—both in the High-Value segment and in Motorsport—will be further enhanced by the production of connected tires featuring Cyber™ Tyre technology.
Investment and output expansion details will be communicated in the coming months following the finalization of the development plans.
The announcement was made as Pirelli participated in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, a flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce.
Cyber™ Tyre is being showcased at the summit as a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.
Cyber™ Tyre is the world's first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires.
The system processes collected data through Pirelli's proprietary software and algorithms.
It communicates in real time with the vehicle's electronics to enable new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems.
These functionalities enhance the driving experience, increase safety levels, and support connected infrastructure.
In a highly advanced market such as the United States, where digital and smart mobility solutions are rapidly expanding, Cyber™ Tyre represents a distinctive competitive advantage for Pirelli.
At SelectUSA, Cyber™ Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth.
Pirelli has been established in Georgia for more than 20 years.
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber™ Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the country.

The milestone reflects a commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber™ Tyre closer to the market and further strengthening industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process at the Rome plant.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group.
The MIRS process is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
The new process enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
It is a highly digitalized system that enables a direct link between product design and its industrial application.
This development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli's production capacity in Rome.
The Rome plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility hosts a dedicated R&D center, further strengthening its role in technological development and product innovation.
The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC®-certified natural rubber.
source_title: PIRELLI WILL START CYBER™ TYRE PRODUCTION IN GEORGIA UNDERLINING THE STRATEGIC IMPORTANCE OF THE U.S. MARKET

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia.
PCNA has been headquartered in Atlanta since 1998.
Ayesha Coker was named Vice President, Marketing of PCNA, effective immediately.
Margareta Mahlstedt joined PCNA as Vice President, Customer Experience in October 2021.
The new appointments bring the PCNA executive leadership team to nine members.
PCNA closed 2021 with new records for new vehicle and Certified Pre-Owned car deliveries.
Coker previously served as Director of Experiential Marketing for PCNA and succeeds Pedro Mota.
Coker has led the brand’s experiential brand marketing, strategic partnerships, sports marketing, motorsport, and the Porsche Club of America in the U.S.
Coker oversaw operations of the Porsche Track Experience, Porsche Travel Experience, and the Porsche Experience Centers in Atlanta and Los Angeles.
Mahlstedt is responsible for CRM practices, customer satisfaction, market research, Vehicle Initial Quality, and overseeing Porsche Contact Center operations.
Kjell Gruner serves as President and CEO of PCNA.
Coker previously held marketing roles at Merkley and Partners and GMR Marketing.

Coker holds a Bachelor of Science in Fashion Merchandising from the University of Maryland Eastern Shore and an Associate Applied Science degree in Advertising and Integrated Marketing Communications from the Fashion Institute of Technology.
Mahlstedt previously held leadership roles across marketing and public relations for Porsche Cars Canada, Volvo Cars of Canada, and BMW Group Canada.
Mahlstedt received a B.A. from Western University and an M.B.A. from Copenhagen Business School.
PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera, and Taycan.
PCNA operates the first Porsche Experience Center in North America in Atlanta, featuring a module-based 1.6-mile driver development track, business center, and Restaurant 356.
PCNA operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles on a 53-acre complex with an eight-module, 4.1-mile driver development track, business center, and Restaurant 917.
PCNA supports 193 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships in the U.S.
PCNA supplies parts, service, marketing, and training to its dealership network.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Porsche boasts over 30,000 motorsport wins to date.
source_title: Porsche Cars North America starts new year with two new

entity_name: Electric Vehicles with Final Assembly in North America
canonical_name: Clean Vehicle Credit (IRC 30D) / Inflation Reduction Act of 2022
facts: The Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (Public Law 117-169) amended the Qualified Plug-in Electric Drive Motor Vehicle Credit (IRC 30D), now known as the Clean Vehicle Credit.
A new requirement for final assembly in North America took effect on August 17, 2022.
The final assembly requirement applies to new electric, fuel cell electric, and plug-in hybrid electric vehicles acquired, delivered, and placed in service after August 16, 2022.
For vehicles placed in service on or after January 1, 2023, Clean Vehicle Credit provisions are subject to updated guidance from the IRS and the U.S. Department of the Treasury.
Beginning January 1, 2024, buyers can reduce the vehicle's upfront purchase price by the amount of their Clean Vehicle Credit by transferring the credit to the dealer.
Before 2024, eligible vehicle buyers could only receive the credit amount after filing their tax return.
Starting January 1, 2024, dealers must submit information to the IRS through IRS Energy Credits Online to determine vehicle eligibility and credit amount at the point of sale.
Without dealer submission to IRS Energy Credits Online, buyers cannot claim a tax credit on their return nor transfer it to a dealer.
A dealer must provide the buyer a copy of the IRS's approval of the dealer's submission.
The list of EVs with final assembly in North America applies only to vehicles purchased and delivered between August 17 and December 31, 2022.

Model Year 2022 and Model Year 2023 vehicle eligibility is based on Fuel Economy labeling information and Part 583 data submitted by manufacturers to the EPA and NHTSA respectively as of December 31, 2022.
Verification of final assembly for vehicles purchased beginning January 1, 2023, should use the VIN decoder.
Manufacturer sales caps apply to vehicles placed in service before January 1, 2023.
The Chevrolet Bolt EUV met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The Chevrolet Bolt EV met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The GMC Hummer EV Pickup met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The GMC Hummer EV SUV met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The Tesla Model 3 met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The Tesla Model S met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The Tesla Model X met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The Tesla Model Y met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2022.
The Cadillac Lyriq met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2023.
The Chevrolet Bolt EV met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2023.
The Tesla Model 3 met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2023.
The Tesla Model S met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2023.
The Tesla Model X met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2023.
The Tesla Model Y met its manufacturer sales cap for Model Year 2023.
The list of eligible vehicles will be updated as manufacturers submit Fuel Economy labeling and Part 583 data to relevant government agencies.
Manufacturers can use the website contact form to request changes to this vehicle list.
Final assembly in North America is not required for tax credit eligibility for vehicles purchased before August 17, 2022.
The build location of a particular vehicle should be confirmed by the VIN or an information label affixed to the vehicle.
The U.S. Department of Transportation's NHTSA provides a VIN decoder to identify a vehicle's build plant and country of manufacture.

Verification of North American final assembly is an initial step toward identifying vehicles eligible for the Clean Vehicle Credit, but further eligibility conditions may apply depending on purchase date.
For the example 2019 Volkswagen e-Golf, the build plant country is Germany.
For some manufacturers, the build location may vary based on specific vehicle, trim, or production date because some models are produced in multiple locations.
To be eligible for the Clean Vehicle Credit, a vehicle must have undergone final assembly in North America.
In general, North America includes the United States and Puerto Rico, Canada, and Mexico for determining final assembly location.
The information on this page should not be viewed as an official or legally binding document.
source_title: Alternative Fuels Data Center: Electric Vehicles with Final Assembly in North America

entity_name: SK Innovation electric battery plant in Commerce, GA
facts: U.S. Senator Reverend Raphael Warnock (D-GA), a member of the Senate Banking and Commerce committees, issued a statement after media reports surfaced that a settlement has been reached in a legal dispute threatening the viability of the SK Innovation electric battery plant in Commerce, GA.
Senator Warnock stated his sharp focus has been on protecting jobs with good wages that were committed to Georgians.

Warnock spoke repeatedly to senior leaders at the companies involved and numerous officials in the Biden Administration about the value of the Commerce factory to the local economy and the critical effort in Georgia and across the country to reduce pollution and strengthen environmental sustainability.

The reached settlement ensures that jobs will stay in Georgia, prompting Warnock to express gladness that all parties listened and that the community of Commerce received the decision they hoped for to help keep the local economy moving forward.

Senator Warnock has been a vocal champion for the Commerce factory and advocated strongly for the companies involved to reach a settlement to prevent additional litigation and continued uncertainty for the plant.

Since being sworn in, Senator Warnock and his office have maintained frequent contact with the Biden Administration, senior leaders at SK Innovation and LG Chem, and local residents and business community leaders in Commerce and around Georgia.
These contacts aimed to encourage amicable negotiation to ensure the jobs committed to Commerce would come to fruition.
Senator Warnock pressed Biden Administration officials on the importance of keeping the Commerce plant online to help advance the nation’s long-term environmental sustainability goals.
Prior reporting noted that Democratic Sen. Raphael Warnock said an ITC decision threatened seriously the future of the project in Commerce and President Biden’s own goals.
Warnock’s office was actively working on the SK Battery issue, with his office stating a settlement would be the best outcome.

links.context: Report on ITC decision threatening project future
Article on Warnock's office handling SK Battery issue

source_title: Senator Reverend Warnock Statement Regarding Georgia Battery Factory » Reverend Raphael Warnock

facts: Solvay Specialty Polymers is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
Solvay purchased the Gloucester County facility from prior owner Arkema, Inc. in 1990.
Solvay began producing industrial plastics, coatings, and other chemicals at the site using PFAS compounds.
Solvay continues to utilize and release replacement PFAS known as chloroperfluoropolyether carboxylates (CIPFPECAs) into the environment.
The State's complaint alleges Solvay discharged tens of thousands of pounds of PFAS compounds into the environment.
High concentrations of PFAS compounds, such as perfluorononanoic acid (PFNA) and perfluorooctanoic acid (PFOA), were found in the air, surface water, and groundwater on and near the Solvay property.
A 2007-2009 survey found PFNA in the Delaware River water near the Solvay site at the highest reported concentration in surface water in the world at that time.
Levels of PFNA found in the nearby Borough of Paulsboro’s water supply in 2014 were higher than had ever been reported in drinking water elsewhere in the United States or the world.
Solvay has refused to take full responsibility for necessary investigation and remediation and failed to disclose impacts to DEP and surrounding communities.
Solvay repeatedly sought to blame other actors instead of investigating all contamination pathways and paying for treatment of contaminated drinking water.
Excessive concentrations of VOCs, such as carbon tetrachloride and vinyl chloride, exist at the Solvay site.

Groundwater is contaminated with toxic metals including aluminum, antimony, arsenic, beryllium, chromium, cobalt, iron, lead, manganese, nickel, and sodium at levels exceeding state groundwater quality standards.

Nine counts of a 10-count complaint name both Solvay and former owner Arkema as defendants, alleging violations of NJ Spill Act, Water Pollution Control Act, Air Pollution Control Act, Solid Waste Management Act, Site Remediation Reform Act, public nuisance, trespass, negligence, and abnormally dangerous activity.
A single count filed against Solvay only alleges violation of New Jersey’s Brownfield and Contaminated Site Remediation Act.

links.context: Photographs of the Solvay and Honeywell sites

source_title: State of New Jersey

facts: Storion Energy LLC intends to become a leading U.S.-based manufacturer of vanadium electrolyte.
Storion plans to develop additional vanadium flow battery solutions to satisfy projected demand from the long duration energy storage (LDES) sector.
The joint venture was established through definitive agreements between Largo Clean Energy Corp., a subsidiary of Largo Inc., and Stryten Critical E-Storage, LLC, an affiliate of Stryten Energy LLC.
Storion aims to remove barriers to entry in the flow battery manufacturing industry and become a U.S. domestic leader for vanadium electrolyte production and supply.
The partnership will share product and industry knowledge with Stryten Energy LLC and leverage complementary assets by integrating top-tier talent from both companies.
Upon closing, Largo Clean Energy Corp. and Stryten will each contribute certain vanadium flow battery-related assets and liabilities to Storion.
Upon closing, Stryten will pay US$1 million in cash directly to Largo and contribute over time a total of US$6 million in cash, for the purpose of funding Storion’s operations.
Upon closing, Largo Clean Energy Corp. and Stryten will each hold a 50% equity interest in Storion, with customary pre-emption rights and certain other anti-dilution protections.
Board representation of Storion will be generally proportional to ownership, with Stryten holding one additional seat so long as LCE and Stryten hold similar ownership interests.

At closing, Largo will amend its Safekeeping and Supply Agreements with Largo Physical Vanadium Corp., assign the Safekeeping Agreement to Storion, and enter a separate supply agreement providing Storion a right of first offer to purchase vanadium products.
Gallatin Capital LLC acted as financial advisor to Largo in connection with the transaction, and Linklaters LLP acted as legal counsel.
Storion will have locations in Wilmington, Massachusetts and Alpharetta, Georgia.

Storion intends to support scalable domestic electrolyte production to establish a fully integrated vertical supply chain for utility-scale vanadium redox flow battery (VRFB) long-duration energy storage solutions.
VRFB technology is a safe and reliable option to provide long-duration energy storage greater than four hours to help ensure grid stability and facilitate increased utilization of renewables.
Stryten Energy LLC is headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, and provides energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
Stryten Energy’s stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software.
Largo Inc. is one of the world’s largest primary vanadium producers, sourcing high-quality vanadium and ilmenite from its Maracás Menchen Mine in Brazil.
Largo holds a 50% ownership stake in Storion Energy as part of its strategic investment in the clean energy storage sector.

Largo Physical Vanadium Corp. aims to provide direct exposure to physical vanadium, a metal essential for steel, aerospace and energy storage industries, and plans to actively supply vanadium to end users of vanadium flow batteries.

The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, with management noting risks including failure to satisfy conditions, inability to realize anticipated benefits, and challenges in raising additional funds.
source_title: Largo and Stryten Energy to Form Storion Energy for the Manufacture of Vanadium Electrolyte and Battery Components for Long Duration Energy Storage

facts: Stryten Energy LLC is a U.S.-based energy storage solutions provider headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia.
The company will share the importance of domestically sourced and manufactured battery energy storage systems to support continued increasing power demand at the NAATBatt 2026 Annual Conference.
The NAATBatt 2026 Annual Conference will take place February 9-12 in Tucson, AZ.

Access to reliable, high-quality energy is a significant challenge facing our nation, and advanced battery storage solutions that store and deploy energy generated from hydrocarbons and intermittent sources are a key component of solving that challenge.
The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) projects that power demand will increase to 4,239 billion kWh in 2026.
This is an increase from the previous record of 4,082 billion kWh set in 2024.

The EIA also reports that much of the recent and forecasted growth in electricity consumption is coming from the commercial sector, which includes data centers, and the industrial sector, which includes manufacturing.

Stryten Energy’s Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) addresses the challenge of firming our energy infrastructure by storing energy generated from all sources and deploying that energy in cycling or resiliency applications.
These scalable battery systems enable demand management, providing cost savings and efficient energy utilization through valley filling and peak shaving and frequency matching.
The BESS technology can be sized for the needs of a wide range of applications – from commercial and industrial to utility scale.
Real-time monitoring and control capabilities through Stryten Energy inCOMMAND™ allow seamless control of an energy ecosystem autonomously from anywhere in the world.

Scott Childers, Vice President, Essential Power of Stryten Energy, stated that integrating batteries into our grid infrastructure is a foundational step in the journey to U.S. energy resilience and independence.
Stryten Energy will deliver a company update during the Member Update Presentations: Session 1B at 4:20 p.m. on Tuesday, February 10.

Stryten Energy is also leading a NAATBatt breakfast discussion titled “FEOC Impact on Domestic Battery Manufacturing” on Thursday, February 12, at 7:30 a.m., which will be moderated by Scott Childers.
The company offers advanced lead, lithium, vanadium redox flow or a hybrid of battery chemistries to provide optimal solutions for customer applications and energy storage requirements.

Stryten Energy helps solve the world’s most pressing energy challenges with a broad range of energy storage solutions across the Essential Power, Motive Power, Transportation, Military and Government sectors.
The company powers everything from submarines to subcompacts, microgrids, warehouses, distribution centers, cars, trains and trucks.

Stryten's stored energy technologies include advanced lead, lithium and vanadium redox flow batteries, intelligent chargers and energy performance management software that keep people on the move and supply chains running.
An industry leader backed by more than a century of expertise, Stryten has The Energy to Challenge the status quo and deliver top-performing energy solutions for today and tomorrow.

source_title: Stryten Energy Focuses on Battery Energy Storage Solutions Built in the U.S. at NAATBatt 2026

entity_name: FY23 AMMTO Battery Manufacturing Lab Call
canonical_name: FY23 AMMTO Battery Manufacturing Lab Call
facts: The Office of Advanced Materials & Manufacturing Technologies (AMMTO) announced the selection of five projects for the FY23 AMMTO Battery Manufacturing Lab Call.
Total funding awarded across all selected projects is $16 million.
Topic 1 covers solid-state battery projects, comprising 3 awards totaling $11 million.
Proposals in Topic 1 address barriers for domestic, large-scale solid-state battery production.
The DOE is focusing on translating fundamental solid-state electrolyte R&D into large format/high-volume manufacturing RD&D.
The DOE is also focusing on enhancing precision processing and fabrication of solid-state batteries in large format cells.
The DOE is conducting verification and validation of solid-state battery scalability.
Project 1 focuses on manufacturing large format, high-energy density, and long durability solid-state batteries with oxide electrolyte.
Award amount for the oxide electrolyte project is $4 million.
Oak Ridge National Laboratory leads the oxide electrolyte project.
The project location is Oak Ridge, Tennessee.
Industry partners for the oxide electrolyte project are Intecells and High-T Tech.
ORNL and its partners will develop a scalable process to manufacture solid-state batteries with LiNixMnyCo1-x-yO2 (x≥0.6, NMC) cathode and oxide-based electrolytes.
The oxide-based electrolyte used in the project is intrinsically nonflammable.
The project will transform the current batch process for fabricating and sintering SSB oxide-based electrolytes into a continuous process.
The project will integrate the cathode, electrolyte, and anode into full cells.
The project aims to scale up cells from ≤10 mAh button cells to ≥ 1 Ah cells.
Target performance for the scaled-up cells includes ≥350 Wh/kg energy density and 500 cycles.
Project 2 focuses on solventless processing for precise, reliable, and scalable solid-state battery manufacturing.
Award amount for the solventless processing project is $4 million.
The National Renewable Energy Laboratory leads the solventless processing project.
The project location is Golden, Colorado.
Partners for this project include ORNL, SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory, Solid Power, SkyNano, Epic Advanced Materials, and Mott Corporation.
The multi-lab and industrial team will advance next-generation SSB technology through novel dry process electrode manufacturing.
The effort will combine state-of-the-art modelling, advanced characterization, new material formulation, novel high-throughput processing, and a unique three-dimensional cell architecture.
The project aims to demonstrate high energy (>400 Wh/kg), large-area (> 100cm2) all solid-state batteries with commercially relevant, low failure rate manufacturing processes.
Project 3 focuses on advancing solid-state battery manufacturing through scalable synthesis, processing, and analysis.
Award amount for the scalable synthesis project is $3 million.
Argonne National Laboratory leads the scalable synthesis project.
The project location is Lemont, Illinois.
Partners for this project include ORNL, Ampcera, Arkema, Forge Nano, Ion Storage Systems, Koura Global, NEI Corp., and Soelect.
ANL and ORNL will partner with industry to explore SSB manufacturing through a comprehensive examination of slurry-based and dry processing methods for roll-to-roll (R2R) manufacturing.
The primary aim is to optimize solid-state electrolytes for use in safer and higher-capacity batteries.
The project seeks to enhance ionic conductivity, area-specific resistance, mechanical properties, and stability of SSEs.
The project will investigate the integration of innovative R2R techniques into commercial-scale SSB production.

Expected outcomes include refined manufacturing methodologies, a comprehensive understanding of slurry-based and dry processes, and practical guidelines for implementing R2R processes in SSB production.
Topic 2 covers developing innovative flow battery manufacturing capabilities, comprising 2 awards totaling $5 million.
Proposals in Topic 2 address technical and manufacturing challenges in U.S. flow battery production.
The DOE is targeting optimization of flow batteries across commercial, industrial, and utility applications.
The DOE emphasizes creating new or enhanced cell/reactor architectures and configurations for flow batteries.
The DOE also emphasizes developing manufacturing and process standards for flow batteries.
Project 4 focuses on standard electrolyte preparation and cell stack design for redox flow batteries.
Award amount for the redox flow battery project is $2.9 million.
Pacific Northwest National Laboratory leads the redox flow battery project.
The project location is Richland, Washington.
Partners for this project include ESS, Inc., Otoro Energy Inc., and the Chemours Company.
PNNL and its partners will develop standardized redox flow battery electrolytes and stacks.
The goal is to reduce the cost of manufacturing redox flow battery components and systems by implementing standardized designs and processes.
Project 5 focuses on ultra-high volumetric power density bundled microtubular membrane-enabled flow batteries.
Award amount for the microtubular flow battery project is $2.1 million.
Oak Ridge National Laboratory leads the microtubular flow battery project.
The project location is Oak Ridge, Tennessee.
Partners for this project include Kraton Corp., Stryten Energy, Perma Pure, and the Georgia Institute of Technology.
ORNL will collaborate with partners to develop a novel RFB architecture specifically the co-axial bundled microtubular RFB.
The 3-year goals include developing a prototype based on the new RFB architecture that can deliver >10 kWh power.
The 3-year goals also include standardizing material processing and cell fabrication protocol for this innovative RFB architecture.
source_title: Funding Selections: FY23 AMMTO Battery Manufacturing Lab Call | Department of Energy

facts: Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. (TSV) has its principal location in Augusta, Georgia.
TSV produces off-highway motor vehicle model years 2019-2022.

In August 2023, the California Air Resources Board (CARB) reached a settlement with TSV related to the company’s production of vehicles that were not compliant with exhaust emissions certification requirements.
The violation was in violation of Health and Safety Code sections 43151-43153, and the California Code of Regulations, title 13, sections 2400(a)(2) and 2410(a)(2).
TSV fully cooperated with CARB to resolve this matter.
Terms of the settlement include a requirement for TSV to comply with all other CARB regulations going forward.
The total settlement amount is $72,500.

Of the total settlement amount, $36,250 in civil penalties will be deposited into CARB’s Air Pollution Control Fund, which provides funding for projects and research to improve California’s air quality.
The remaining amount of $36,250 will fund a Supplemental Environmental Project (SEP) entitled Fresno TREES Phase 4 – Malaga Recreation Park.
This SEP provides tree planting, air quality tracking, and tree care and maintenance for Malaga Recreation Park.
source_title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. Settlement | California Air Resources Board

facts: Bonnell Aluminum, a subsidiary of Tredegar Corporation (NYSE: TG), is expanding its TSLOTS line of aluminum structural framing systems into the Southeastern region of the United States.
The expansion project will add the Newnan, Georgia facility as Bonnell Aluminum's newest manufacturing and distribution site.

TSLOTS aluminum framing systems enable manufacturers to create light, durable, attractive, strong, versatile, and functional structures using custom T-slotted aluminum extrusions and connecting hardware.

Randy Johnson, Senior Brand Manager for TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum, stated: 'We have been successful in growing our TSLOTS product line by positioning our manufacturing and distribution sites closer to where our customers operate.'

Johnson added: 'This is our second major investment in TSLOTS in two years, following a very successful expansion into the Midwest in 2021. This capacity addition will increase our footprint across the U.S. and provide our customers with reduced shipping costs and shorter lead times.'
TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum operates as a full-service product line providing project design assistance, fabrication, custom packaging, and full assembly.

Bonnell Aluminum serves the building and construction, automotive and transportation, consumer durables, machinery and equipment, electrical, and distribution markets with custom-fabricated and finished aluminum extrusions.
The company's branded product lines include TSLOTS by Bonnell Aluminum and Futura Transitions by Bonnell Aluminum.
Bonnell Aluminum currently operates manufacturing facilities in Newnan, Georgia; Carthage, Tennessee; Niles, Michigan; Elkhart, Indiana; and Clearfield, Utah.
source_title: TSLOTS Aluminum Framing System to Expand Operations into Southeastern U.S.

facts: A gigantic battery cell production facility is coming to metro Atlanta and will create hundreds of new jobs, state officials announced Friday morning.
Freyr Battery, a Norway-based clean-tech company named after a Norse god, will invest $2.57 billion in Georgia by building a plant on a 368-acre site in Coweta County, according to a news release.
The company said it will produce Lithium-Ion battery cell, which can be used for stationary energy storage, electric mobility and additional applications.
Gov. Brian Kemp touted Freyr as the latest investment in Georgia’s fast-growing electric battery industry, which is bolstered by forthcoming electric vehicle factories and parts suppliers.

Tom Einar Jenson, Freyr’s co-founder and CEO, said his company’s Coweta factory is another large step in the U.S. enhancing its domestic supply chain for Lithium-Ion batteries, technology that foreign countries such as China currently dominate.
Jensen stated: “At Freyr, we are deeply committed to the ambition we share with our U.S. partners to decarbonize the transportation and energy sectors.”

Jensen added that as they advance their U.S. expansion plan in cooperation with key stakeholders, they expect to make meaningful investments to spur job creation and the eventual development of localized, decarbonized supply chains in the U.S. to enhance energy security and economic activity.
The factory will be called Giga America.
The Giga America factory is expected to create 723 jobs over the next seven years.
Freyr said Georgia and Coweta County “are collectively providing strong financial incentives for the Giga America project,” but did not provide specifics.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development declined to provide further detail to The Atlanta Journal-Constitution, saying the “project is still active at this time.”
It will be located at the Bridgeport Industrial Park site, an industrial megasite off I-85 near Newnan.
Local officials welcomes Freyr and the jobs it will create.

Coweta County Development Authority Chair John Daviston said: “We are excited about the opportunities this forward-looking venture brings to our area of the state, and the addition of quality and highly-skilled jobs that will provide a significant stimulus to our local economy.”
source_title: Georgia Lands New Gigantic $2.6B Electric Battery Plant

facts: Kumho Tire U.S.A. is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, and serves as the US sales, marketing, and distribution arm of Kumho Tire Co. Inc.
Kumho Tire U.S.A. announced a new lineup of ultra-high performance tires: the Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71, Ecsta Sport PS72, and Ecsta Sport S PS72.
The new tire lineup leverages advancements in AI and long-standing relationships with OE manufacturers for rapid iterative design testing.
The Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 features next-generation compound technology designed to enhance grip and traction rain or shine without sacrificing performance characteristics.
The Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 includes a 50,000-mile treadwear warranty.
The Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 utilizes wide and stiff outside tread blocks to add stability when cornering on high-horsepower vehicles.
The Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 incorporates lateral grooves in the tread design to aid water drainage for maintained performance in wet weather.
Snow performance for the Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 is enhanced with zig-zag grooves on the inside tread blocks.
The Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 minimizes road noise using rounded edges of tread blocks and a proprietary K-NOISE DEFENDER dimple design to interrupt air flow through tire grooves.
The Ecsta Sport PS72 and Ecsta Sport S PS72 are ultra-high performance summer tires available in two tread configurations.
The summer tire lines provide maximum traction with an enhanced compound for heightened handling, stability, cornering, and steering response.
The summer tires include a 30,000-mile treadwear warranty.
The Ecsta Sport All-Season PA71 is available in 56 sizes ranging from 16-inch to 21-inch wheel diameters.
The Ecsta Sport PS72 is available in 56 sizes from 17-inch to 21-inch wheel diameters.
The Ecsta Sport S PS72 is available in 52 different sizes from 18-inch to 22-inch wheel diameters.
The new tires will debut at SEMA 2024, running Nov. 5-8 in Las Vegas, and will be showcased at Booth #81398 outside the convention center in the Silver Lot.
The new tire lineup will be available to customers in the spring of 2025.
Shawn Denlein serves as the president of sales and marketing for Kumho Tire U.S.A.
Kumho Tire has more than 60 years of history pioneering innovative approaches to developing tires.
source_title: KUMHO TIRE U.S.A. SETTING A NEW STANDARD IN THE ULTRA-HIGH PERFORMANCE CATEGORY WITH LAUNCH OF THREE NEW TIRES

facts: Rivian determined that certain MY 2022 Rivian EDV motor vehicles do not fully comply with paragraph S4.5.1(b)(5)(i) of FMVSS No. 208, Occupant Crash Protection (49 CFR 571.208).
Rivian filed a noncompliance report dated October 24, 2022, and amended the report on November 14, 2022, pursuant to 49 CFR part 573.

Rivian petitioned NHTSA on November 15, 2022, for an exemption from notification and remedy requirements of 49 U.S.C. Chapter 301 based on inconsequential noncompliance regarding motor vehicle safety.
Notice of receipt of Rivian's petition was published with a 30-day public comment period on May 19, 2023, in the Federal Register (88 FR 32275), and no comments were received.
Approximately 1,278 MY 2022 Rivian EDV motor vehicles, manufactured between December 10, 2021, and September 27, 2022, were reported by the manufacturer.
A label displaying the subject vehicle's clearance height is affixed to the same side of the sun visor containing the air bag warning label, causing noncompliance with FMVSS No. 208.

Paragraph S4.5.1(b)(5)(i) of FMVSS No. 208 requires that except for specific labels, no other information shall appear on the same side of the sun visor to which the sun visor air bag warning label is affixed.
Rivian states the air bag warning label meets FMVSS No. 208 content requirements and is displayed as intended by the standard.
Rivian contends the noncompliance is inconsequential because the subject vehicles are exclusively used in a single fleet of delivery vehicles driven by professional drivers.
The subject vehicles are walk-in vans with only the driver's seat designated, and drivers are required to wear seat belts when operating them.
Rivian believes the clearance height label does not affect air bag warning label efficacy due to the vehicle's intended usage.
Rivian contends the labeling requirement aims to mitigate risks for children placed in seating positions equipped with air bags.
Rivian states there is no expectation of children being present in or around the subject vehicles.
If a child occupied the driver's seat, the brake transmission shift interlock required by FMVSS No. 114 would prevent operation, so air bags could not deploy.
Drivers are required to use seat belts at all times, and the air bag warning label is visible, easy to read, and not positioned near the clearance height label.
Rivian believes NHTSA's rule aims to maximize label effectiveness through consistent and uniform messaging to consumers.
Rivian quotes NHTSA's decision on a Maserati petition regarding the purpose of FMVSS No. 208 air bag warning labels.

Rivian contends NHTSA has previously determined that vehicle usage bears upon inconsequentiality, granting exemptions for vocational vehicles where children are unlikely to be in front passenger areas.
Rivian adds NHTSA granted prior petitions for deviations in advanced air bag warning label language, and the rationale applies here.
The subject noncompliance has been corrected for vehicles in production.
Rivian is not aware of any crash, death, injury, field report, or claims related to the subject noncompliance.
NHTSA focuses on safety risk to individuals experiencing events against which a recall would otherwise protect.
NHTSA generally does not consider absence of complaints or injuries when determining inconsequentiality.
Each petition is evaluated on its own facts, and prior determinations are not binding precedent.
The purpose of the labeling requirement is to reduce adverse effects of air bags by attracting occupants' attention to the warning label.
Rivian provided evidence that the FMVSS No. 208 required Figure 1 label is present, unobstructed, and within clear view despite the clearance height information.
The vehicle's clearance height could be frequently used information for delivery van drivers.
NHTSA determined the noncompliance is inconsequential to motor vehicle safety given the nature and intended use of the vocational vehicles.
NHTSA agrees it would be unlikely for children to be placed in the front passenger seating area of these vehicles.
NHTSA finds Rivian met its burden of persuasion that the FMVSS No. 208 noncompliance is inconsequential to motor vehicle safety.
Rivian's petition is granted, exempting it from notification and free remedy obligations under 49 U.S.C. 30118 and 30120.
The decision only applies to subject vehicles Rivian no longer controlled at the time of determining noncompliance.
The petition granting does not relieve distributors and dealers of prohibitions on sale or delivery of noncompliant vehicles under their control after Rivian notified them.
Otto G. Matheke III is the Director of the Office of Vehicle Safety Compliance who signed the decision.

links.context: Federal Docket Management System (FDMS) website to locate docket number NHTSA-2022-0112

source_title:
Federal Register
       :: 
      Rivian Automotive, LLC, Grant of Petition for Decision of Inconsequential Noncompliance

facts: Georgia Power is committed to supplying households with reliable energy and supporting businesses.
Georgia Power was recognized for a third consecutive year as a top utility in Business Facilities Magazine.
Business Facilities Magazine is a leading publication highlighting economic development news and site selection factors and rankings from around the world.
Georgia Power has been featured in Business Facilities Magazine's annual list of top utilities since 2021.
Energy availability continues to be a leading factor in site selection, and Georgia Power Economic Development provides companies with information for choosing sites to locate or expand.
Vogtle 4 entered commercial operation on April 29, 2024.
Plant Vogtle units 3 and 4 will provide reliable, emission-free energy to customers for at least 60 to 80 years.
With all four units now in operation, Plant Vogtle is the largest generator of clean energy in the nation.
Plant Vogtle is expected to produce more than 30 million megawatt hours of electricity each year.
The development supports Southern Company's goal of achieving net-zero carbon emissions by 2050.
Southern Company has an interim target of a 50% greenhouse gas emissions reduction by 2030.
Georgia Power has achieved a 60% reduction in carbon emissions.
Georgia Power's first large-scale battery energy storage system, the Mossy Branch Battery Facility, announced commercial operation in October 2024.
The Mossy Branch Battery Facility supports grid reliability and optimizes renewable energy sources like solar.
The Mossy Branch facility can be deployed back to the grid over a four-hour period to add resiliency to the state's power grid.
In 2023, Georgia Power supported more than 16,000 new jobs and over $9 billion in new commercial investment across Georgia's top industries.

Walt Farrell, Vice President of Economic Development for Georgia Power, stated that the company worked in partnership with the state and local communities to support the 2023 job and investment figures.
Many projects supported by Georgia Power align with national trends in automotive supply chain and solar manufacturing.
GF Casting Solutions AG is investing more than $184 million in a new manufacturing facility in Augusta to produce lightweight components for the mobility and energy industries.
The GF Casting Solutions project in Augusta will create 350 new jobs for Richmond County.
SOLARCYCLE is investing $344 million in a solar glass manufacturing facility in Cedartown.
The SOLARCYCLE facility in Cedartown will create more than 600 new full-time jobs in Polk County.
The SOLARCYCLE facility will be the first-of-its-kind in the country to use recycled materials from retired solar panels to make new solar glass.

These supported projects benefited from Georgia Power's site selection searches, engineering and research services, power delivery timeline needs, and collaboration with state and local economic development agencies.
source_title: Georgia Power Top Utilities Economic Development | Select Georgia

facts: Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp signed a sales use tax exemption for projects of regional significance at the future site of the Hyundai Metaplant in Bryan County.
The signing ceremony took place on Friday at the megasite construction location.
Hyundai's electric vehicle and battery manufacturing facility fits the definition of a project of regional significance.
The factory is scheduled to open in late 2024.
The facility expects to employ 8,100 workers.

Georgia Speaker of the House Jon Burns (an Effingham County native who owns a farm near the site) and Georgia Sen. Ben Watson (a Savannahian whose district includes Bryan County) joined Kemp for the signing ceremony.
In addition to signing the tax exemption extension, Kemp made law the state's Fiscal Year 2024 budget during the event.
House Bill 408 extends a sales use tax break for regional projects meant to spur investment and entice future organizations to do business in Georgia.
Gov. Kemp called Hyundai Metaplant America and another battery plant coming to Bartow County "transformational projects" and cited them as one of the reasons why House Bill 408 is imperative.
Most of the communities that had benefited from this tax tool are located outside the metro Atlanta area.
Suppliers related to the Hyundai project have exceeded financial projections.
Over 4,500 new jobs have been announced for site suppliers in roughly $1.96 billion dollars of investment.
The supplier investment number is nearly double the original estimate for all site suppliers.
source_title: Georgia Gov. Kemp signs tax exemption, state budget into law at Hyundai EV plant site

facts: Inalfa Roof Systems is located in Acworth, GA.
The facility specializes in the design and manufacturing of automotive roof systems.
Product offerings include sunroofs, panoramic roofs, and modular roof systems that enhance vehicle aesthetics and functionality.
The facility serves as a key part of Inalfa's global operations, contributing to the automotive industry with innovative solutions.
Visitors may find it noteworthy for its advanced manufacturing capabilities and commitment to quality in automotive roof technology.

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Inalfa Roof Systems will expand its recently completed 165,000-square-foot regional headquarters in southwest Cherokee County by an additional 45,000 square feet.
The company will invest an additional $6 million for the expansion.
The expansion will create 100 new jobs in addition to the 300 jobs already announced in June 2013.
Governor Nathan Deal stated that Cherokee County is an ideal location for Inalfa Roof Systems and praised the company as a true team player for regional economic development.
The southwest Cherokee facility is strategically located off I-75 at Cherokee 75 Corporate Park, near automotive manufacturers throughout the Southeast.
A new business unit will be added to vertically integrate a bi-processed injection molding operation to place black lining around Inalfa sunroofs.
Inalfa Roof Systems recently received the State of Georgia Deal of the Year award for its partnership with the Cherokee County community.

Mary Ellen McClanahan, metro Atlanta project manager at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), assisted with the expansion alongside the Cherokee Office of Economic Development (COED).
Cherokee County Commission Chairman Buzz Ahrens stated that the community provides a supportive business environment for Inalfa's success.
COED Chairman Marshall Day noted Inalfa is a great example of a company making the most of Cherokee County's offerings and emphasized advocacy for existing industry.
GDEcD Commissioner Chris Carr said the expansion demonstrates Georgia's high-quality workforce and pro-business resources, noting companies who choose Georgia will grow within the state.
The project is expected to be complete by September 2014.
Inalfa Roof Systems is one of the world’s biggest providers of vehicle roof systems, designing, developing, and manufacturing sunroofs and open-roof systems for the automotive industry.
Inalfa's automotive customers include BMW Group, Daimler, Chrysler Group, Ford, General Motors, Volkswagen, Audi, Volvo, Citroen, Chery, Geely, Hyundai Kia, Honda, Land Rover, Renault, and Nissan.
Inalfa Roof Systems has its global headquarters in Venray, the Netherlands, with facilities in Poland, Slovakia, USA, Brazil, Mexico, South Korea, China, and Japan.
source_title: Gov. Nathan Deal: Inalfa Roof Systems Creates 100 More Jobs in Cherokee County | GaPundit

facts: Inalfa Roof Systems Group is a global provider of vehicle roof systems.
The company designs, develops, and manufactures sunroofs and open roof systems for the automotive industry.
Inalfa Roof Systems Group has a global footprint with manufacturing facilities in Europe, Asia, and America.
The company's products are used by major car manufacturers such as BMW, Daimler, Ford, General Motors, and Volvo.
Inalfa Roof Systems Group was founded in 1946.
Inalfa Roof Systems Group is headquartered in Venray, the Netherlands.

facts: Inalfa Roof Systems Group appointed Georges Andary as its new President and Chief Executive Officer (CEO), effective October 1, 2023.
Georges Andary brings over 28 years of executive leadership experience in the automotive industry to his new role.
Inalfa Roof Systems develops and produces integrated roof systems for nearly every major automotive brand.

Shareholder BHAP and the Supervisory Board are confident that Mr. Andary and his leadership team will enhance Inalfa's position as the first-choice partner for the automotive industry in roof systems and integrated technologies.

Georges Andary stated: "It is Inalfa's purpose to open up your world with every drive. I am honored to lead the company to deliver on this promise together with the talented teams and to pursue new opportunities and ensure that we continue to meet and exceed our customers' expectations around the globe."
Prior to joining Inalfa, Mr. Andary held several executive management positions at Bosch.
Most recently, Mr. Andary served as the General Manager of Bosch Automotive Products (Suzhou) and as President of the Automotive Electronics Division in China.
Inalfa Roof Systems is a global automotive roof systems manufacturer based in Venray, The Netherlands, with factories and development centers in Europe, Northern America, and Asia.
Inalfa holds over 800 life patents and maintains a global market share of approximately 25%.
Inalfa delivers roof systems to almost every major car and truck manufacturer in the world.
Since 2011, Inalfa Roof Systems has been part of Beijing Hainachuan Automotive Parts Co., Ltd. (BHAP), one of the biggest suppliers of automotive parts in China.
The strategic acquisition of Inalfa by BHAP further diversifies BHAP's product portfolio, strengthens its R&D and marketing capabilities, and enhances its competitiveness.
source_title: Inalfa Roof Systems Group Appoints Georges Andary as New CEO

facts: The Company develops and produces vehicle roof solutions for the automotive and commercial vehicle industry.
The Company has a global market share in automotive roof systems of approximately 25%.
The Company manufactures approximately six million automotive roof systems per year.
The Company is a wholly owned subsidiary of Beijing Hainachuan Automotive Parts Co., Ltd.
The Company produces inslider roof systems where a front panel slides under the rear glass panel or roof skin.
The Company produces single-panel insliders.
The Company produces two-panel insliders.
The Company produces topslider two-panel roof systems where a front panel slides over the rear panel.
The Company produces bottom-loaded topsliders.
The Company produces top-loaded topsliders.
The Company produces exterior slider roof systems where a front panel slides over rear panel or roof skin without the use of a rear support lever.
The Company produces fixed panel roof systems with stationary panels.
The Company produces sunblinds including rollo fabric that can be rolled up, solid panel sunshades that can slide in front of roof systems, and stacking sunshades for easier storage.
The Company produces truck hatches with only ventilation functionality, hatches with ventilation and slide functionality, and hatches with ventilation and escape functionality.
The Company has in-house glass encapsulation capabilities for polymer trim finish to the edge of a panel.
The Company offers roof functions including glass technologies for reduced weight.
The Company offers improved acoustics and NVH capabilities.
The Company integrates V2X communication systems.
The Company integrates in-textile lighting.
The Company integrates solar cells.
The Company offers switchable glass.
The Company offers hybrid glass solutions.
The Company offers ambient lighting systems.
The Company offers integrated gesture control systems.
The Company offers intuitive machine interfaces.
The Company offers integrated data management systems.
The Company offers integrated augmented or virtual reality systems.
The Company offers integrated car-sharing services.
The Company offers panoramic displays.
The Company produces tailgate actuators.
The Company produces active rear spoilers.
The Company produces electrically controlled steps.
The Company was founded in 1946 by J.A.M Burgman and started in the Netherlands.
The Company started production of sunroofs in 1974.
The Company made a decision to form separate business units for Roof systems and Metal in 1995.
The Company established roof systems and metal business as two separate companies in 1997.
The Company opened its factory in Yantai, China in 2006.
The Company established a plant in Slovakia in 2010 as well as in Mexico in 2011.
The Company was sold to the Chinese company Beijing Hainachuan Automotive Parts Co., Ltd. (BHAP).
The Company established a plant in Chongqing and Lanfang in China in April 2013.
The Company opened its new plant in Wrzesnia, Poland in July 2014.
The Company opened its new assembly facility in Cherokee County, Georgia, USA in November 2014.
The Company opened its new plant in Jiading, Shanghai, China in July 2015.
The Company officially opened its new headquarters in Venray, the Netherlands in April 2016.

facts: Georgia Power requested certification for approximately $16.7 billion in new utility-owned projects through 2030.
The requested $16.7 billion portfolio includes generation, battery storage, and transmission projects.
Georgia Power has signed 8 GW of data center contracts since 2023.
Weather-adjusted commercial kilowatt-hour sales at Georgia Power increased 3.7% year-to-date due to surging demand from new data centers.
Southern Company's retail electric revenues grew 9% year-to-date to $15.1 billion.
Southern Company's net income attributable to the holding company grew minimally by 1.5% year-to-date to $3.93 billion.
Southern Company's total debt load increased by $8.7 billion to $72.2 billion.
Southern Company's year-to-date interest expense surged 14% to $2.34 billion, including a $129 million loss on the extinguishment of debt.
Southern Power's net income declined 47% year-to-date to $141 million.
The decline in Southern Power's earnings was primarily due to an $181 million non-cash charge for accelerated depreciation related to wind repowering projects.
Southern Power's wind repowering strategy aims to extend asset life and secure 96% power purchase agreement coverage through 2029.
Alabama Power and Georgia Power claimed a combined $373 million in zero-emission nuclear Production Tax Credits (PTCs).
The $373 million nuclear PTC claim is deferred as a regulatory liability pending IRS acceptance of the prevailing wage multiplier and state PSC direction.
Southern Company's consolidated cash and cash equivalents increased by $2.3 billion to $3.3 billion year-to-date.
Southern Company faces a maximum potential collateral requirement of $3.8 billion if the credit ratings of any power pool participant fall to non-investment grade.
Georgia Power's construction work in progress (CWIP) increased by $2.4 billion over nine months.
Southern Company Gas property, plant, and equipment increased by $821 million year-to-date.
The Georgia Public Service Commission approved the extension of the 2022 ARP through 2028.
Southern Company generated $10.3 billion in new debt proceeds year-to-date to fund capital-intensive construction programs.
Net cash provided by operating activities decreased by $410 million year-to-date to $7.2 billion, primarily due to adverse timing of vendor payments and decreased retail fuel cost recovery.
