facts: JTEKT North America, a subsidiary of global automotive and industrial supplier JTEKT Corp., has completed a $5.8 million expansion of its headquarters on the campus of the Clemson University International Center for Automotive Research (CU-ICAR) in Greenville, S.C.
The $5.8 million headquarters expansion is expected to create 100 jobs by 2020.
JTEKT North America manufactures steering systems, driveline components, bearings, and precision machine tools for the automotive industry.
The company employs more than 6,000 associates at 24 sites across North America.
JTEKT North America operates four manufacturing facilities located in South Carolina.
The firm previously consolidated a portion of its headquarters to CU-ICAR in 2015.
source_title: JTEKT North America expands in South Carolina

facts: JTEKT North America is expanding its manufacturing operation in Greenville County, South Carolina.
The expansion involves a $48.4 million investment.
The investment will create 87 new jobs.
JTEKT North America is part of JTEKT Corporation.
The company manufactures products under the brands JTEKT Automotive Systems, Koyo Bearings, and Toyoda Machine Tools.
JTEKT North America is a global leader in engineering and manufacturing automotive systems, bearing solutions, and high-performance machine tools.
In addition to its regional headquarters in Greenville, South Carolina, the company operates 12 manufacturing sites, three technical centers, and five distribution centers throughout North America.
The expansion adds seven new machining lines to the company's existing facility.
The expansion also includes two new assembly lines at the existing facility.
Additional processes will be added to machine components internally at the facility.
The existing facility is located at 1866 Old Grove Road in Piedmont, South Carolina.
The expanded facility will gain the capability to produce front drive shafts.
Operations for the expansion are scheduled to come online in the third quarter of 2025.

JTEKT Americas President and CEO Gary Bourque stated that the investment reflects absolute confidence in current and future associates, as well as South Carolina's leadership, while delivering next-generation solutions to customers and trusted brands.

South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster praised the state's exceptional workforce for allowing companies like JTEKT North America to thrive and expressed anticipation for the expansion's impact on Greenville County.

South Carolina Secretary of Commerce Harry M. Lightsey III noted that JTEKT North America contributes to the state's reputation as an automotive powerhouse and that the investment will advance manufacturing potential by providing automakers with additional products built in-state.

Greenville County Council Chairman Dan Tripp expressed pride in supporting the expansion, citing the automotive sector as a target industry, and highlighted Greenville's growing talent pool, strong technical college system, and robust business climate.
source_title: JTEKT North America expands manufacturing operation in Greenville County

entity_name: Leadership Lumpkin County Economic Development Day
canonical_name: Leadership Lumpkin County Economic Development Day
facts: On January 5, 2023 the Development Authority of Lumpkin County (DALC) hosted the Dahlonega-Lumpkin County Chamber of Commerce Leadership Lumpkin County (LLC) Economic Development Day at the JTEKT North America plant in Lumpkin County.
The event included a group of 29 participants who started with fundamentals of economic development and a community profile of Lumpkin County.
LLC Day Chair Dathan Harbert stated that Economic Development Day is an integral part of the LLC program and opens eyes to the diversity of businesses in Dahlonega-Lumpkin County.
A Workforce Development Panel featured Cory Addison, Director of Business & Industry at Lanier Technical College.
The panel also included Bobbi Larson, Director of Economic Development & Community Engagement at the University of North Georgia.
The panel also included Jason Lemley, Director of Community Engagement at the Lumpkin County School System.
These workforce partners shared how they collaborate with DALC, local businesses, and community partners to prepare students for careers in Lumpkin County and the region.
The class included a MythBusters Panel where participants asked questions about traffic issues, affordable housing, attracting jobs to the area, and more.
MythBusters Panel member Ariel Alexander serves as Main Street Manager with City of Dahlonega.
MythBusters Panel member Sean Phipps serves as Executive Director with Lumpkin County Water and Sewerage Authority.
MythBusters Panel member Rhett Stringer serves as District 3 Commissioner with Lumpkin County Board of Commissioners.
MythBusters Panel member Rebecca Mincey serves as Executive Director with the Development Authority of Lumpkin County.
The group toured two local manufacturers: JTEKT North America and North Georgia Still Company.
Both JTEKT North America and North Georgia Still Company contribute to Lumpkin County through capital investment, corporate responsibility, and workforce development.
source_title: DALC hosts Leadership Lumpkin County Economic Development Day – Development Authority of Lumpkin County

facts: Kautex officials announced the company will add another 95,000 square feet of warehouse space to its existing plant on Highway 17 South.
The expansion amounts to a $3.3 million investment.
Kautex is a subsidiary of Textron, Inc., which is based in Bonn, Germany.
Kautex is considered one of the top 100 automotive suppliers worldwide.
The company is a leading global supplier of plastic fuel tank systems, selective catalytic reduction systems, and clear vision systems.
Kautex operates 15 plants worldwide.
In Lavonia, the facility manufactures fuel systems for major automakers including GM, BMW, Daimler-Chrysler, Nissan, Honda, and Mercedes-Benz.
Kautex Director of Operations Tyler Miles stated the expansion will accommodate a new product line called SCR Systems for diesel trucks and vehicles.
According to Miles, the SCR System tank holds a urea fluid that ejects into the catalytic converter, mixes with nitrous oxide, and converts emissions from carbon into water.
Miles stated this process dramatically reduces emissions for diesel-fueled cars and trucks.
The SCR System will be installed in both diesel trucks and diesel-powered vehicles.
Kautex is beginning production of SCR Systems for Volkswagen small products, including the Golf, Jetta, and Passat.
The company notes significant growth in the smaller vehicle segment.
The Lavonia plant is the only facility in North America that provides this technology to customers.
Georgia Commissioner of Economic Development Chris Carr attended the expansion announcement.
Chris Carr noted that Site Selection Magazine named Georgia the number one state in the nation to do business last year.
Chris Carr said, “Today we’re here to congratulate Kautex for their exciting announcement and for the contribution of Lavonia and Franklin County to the State of Georgia.”
Kautex first opened its Lavonia facility in 1993 with 20 employees.
The original product manufactured at the facility was a window fluid system for the automotive industry.
In 2003, Kautex expanded into the fuel system segment of the industry.
The 2003 expansion added a 20,000 square foot facility to the existing building.
The company plans to open the new addition this spring.
source_title: Lavonia Industry Announces Expansion - WNEG

facts: PowerFleet announced it will further deploy its PowerFleet Enterprise industrial telematics solution to Kautex throughout 2022.
Kautex has more than doubled the number of sites employing PowerFleet’s telematics solutions on its forklifts in the past 12 months.
The deployed sites span six countries and three continents.
An additional nine sites are planned for implementation in 2022.
PowerFleet’s Enterprise solution combines the VAC4S telematics platform with Enterprise software to improve workplace safety and security.
The solution restricts vehicle access to trained, authorized operators, enforces electronic safety inspection checklists, and senses vehicle impacts.
The technology empowers Kautex to manage, track, monitor, and control its powered industrial vehicles and their operators.
The technology improves material handling productivity, reduces costs, and increases visibility by establishing accountability for forklifts and other industrial trucks.
It ensures equipment is in the proper place at the right time and streamlines workflow.
Since partnering with PowerFleet, Kautex has been able to right-size its fleet in several locations across multiple time zones.
In the first facility, Kautex reduced its fleet by over 10% after analyzing utilization data from the PowerFleet Enterprise system.
ROI was achieved through cost reductions of more than $10,000 per year for each truck eliminated, including fork truck and maintenance costs.
Martin Weiss, Global Director Material Excellence at Kautex, stated that deploying the solution reduced impacts, accidents, and damage to equipment and goods.
Kautex expanded adoption from a single site to more than eight locations globally with plans for continued deployments.
PowerFleet’s global presence and ability to work across multiple geographies played a major role in Kautex's decision to grow the partnership.
PowerFleet’s easy-to-use enterprise software also played a major role in the decision.
The Enterprise solution allows Kautex to aggregate data from multiple facilities within one application for faster, more accurate decision-making.
This has helped Kautex deliver greater safety, modernization, and assess/implement best practices across facilities.
Joel Norris, Vice President of Sales at PowerFleet, noted that customers quickly see ROI and that PowerFleet’s customer service and global operations support were key to the partnership.
Kautex will continue using the PowerFleet Enterprise solution and analytics platform, PowerFleet IQ, for safety and equipment utilization decisions.
PowerFleet is a global leader of subscription-based wireless solutions that secure, control, track, and manage high-value enterprise assets.
PowerFleet’s patented technologies monitor and analyze assets to improve safety, increase efficiency, reduce costs, and drive profitability.
PowerFleet’s offerings are sold under the global brands PowerFleet, Pointer, and Cellocator.
PowerFleet’s global headquarters are in Woodcliff Lake, New Jersey, with additional offices around the globe.
Kautex has more than 30 plants in 14 countries.

facts: Lund International Holding Company, a portfolio company of Highlander Partners, L.P., announced it has acquired all of the operating assets of AMP Research on October 4, 2013.

Lund will continue to use the trademarked POWERSTEP™, BEDSTEP®, BEDSTEP2™ and BEDXTENDER HD™ product names included in AMP’s Innovation in Motion product lines.
The current Tustin location will continue to be the primary manufacturing facility for the acquired assets.
AMP Research’s current workforce will join the Lund International team.
Mitch Fogle serves as President of Lund.
Jeff L. Hull serves as Chairman of Lund and Managing Partner of Highlander Partners.
The acquisition reinforces Lund’s emerging position as the leader in the running board and nerf bar category.
Lund will sell the patent protected and unique POWERSTEP™ automatic power articulating running board under the Lund brand in its new Innovation in Motion series.
The BEDSTEP®, BEDSTEP2™, BEDXTENDER™ HD, and fuel door products are excellent complements to Lund’s existing business.
This acquisition marks Lund’s third acquisition in 2013.
Regions Business Capital, with Regions Bank as Administrative Agent, along with a syndicate of participating lenders, provided senior financing in support of the transaction.
Lund International Holding Company is a leading designer, manufacturer, and marketer of branded automotive aftermarket accessories for passenger cars, light trucks, and heavy trucks.
Lund’s product portfolio includes Ventvisors®, hood shields, floor mats, fender flares, tonneau covers, storage boxes, and running boards.

Highlander Partners, L.P. is a Dallas-based private investment firm with partners that have committed over $500 million of principal funds for direct private equity investments in North America and Central Europe.
Highlander Partners focuses on making investments in middle market businesses in targeted industries including basic manufacturing, food, building materials and others.
Highlander Partners uses a “buy and build” investment approach to create value by helping companies grow organically and through acquisitions.

facts: Mack Trucks is investing $14.5 million to expand its operations.
The company is adding 51 new jobs at its Roanoke County location.
The expansion adds 72,000 square feet to the Dixie Caverns-area factory.
Gov. Glenn Youngkin announced the investment and job additions during a plant visit on Friday afternoon.
Gov. Youngkin and his wife Suzanne Youngkin broke ground on the expansion alongside company and government officials.
Mack Trucks is part of Volvo Group, which opened the Roanoke County location in 2020.
Volvo operates a heavy-duty truck plant in Pulaski County.
Gov. Glenn Youngkin stated that Mack committed to hiring 250 people four years prior and currently has 420 employees working at the site, with 51 more to be added.
The expansion is expected to begin in April and conclude by late 2025.
The plant's total size will increase to 352,000 square feet following the expansion.
The project will add capacity for two existing medium-duty truck lines: MD-6 and MD-7.
The project will also add capacity for Mack Trucks' emerging medium-duty electric truck line.
Rich Million, Mack Trucks' senior vice president of strategy and business development, cited strong dealer and customer reception as the reason for the expansion.
Million noted that customers want to buy more trucks and praised the local community partnership and talented workforce.
Gov. Youngkin approved a $255,000 grant for the project from the Commonwealth’s Opportunity Fund.
Roanoke County offered an incentive package totaling $842,420.
The Virginia Jobs Investment Program will support employee training for the project.
Mack is eligible to receive state benefits from the Major Business Facility Job Tax Credit for new full-time jobs created by the project.
A Roanoke Regional Partnership analysis estimates the operational increase will add an annual economic impact of $72 million in Roanoke County and the surrounding region.
Phil North, Roanoke County Board of Supervisors Chairman, credited county Administrator Richard Caywood and economic development director Megan Baker for securing the project.
The Virginia Economic Development Partnership and the Roanoke Regional Partnership worked together to secure the project.
Phil North stated the expansion reaffirms the county's strategy to foster high-value advanced manufacturing and showcases the region as an attractive destination for growing businesses.
source_title: Mack Trucks to invest $14.5 million, add 51 jobs in Roanoke County - Cardinal News

facts: President Donald Trump visited Mack Trucks’ Lehigh Valley Operations plant.
The visit focused on domestic manufacturing, jobs, tariffs, trucking, and industrial production.
Mack’s Lehigh Valley Operations facility is a 1.7 million-square-foot facility.
Trump praised Mack’s long history as an American truck brand and highlighted medium- and heavy-duty truck manufacturing.
The site already has a role in Mack’s zero-emission product strategy.
Trump criticized blanket electric vehicles or zero-emission mandates before charging infrastructure is mature.
Trump stated buyers should still be free to purchase EVs if they choose.
Mack’s Lehigh Valley Operations facility assembles Class 8 trucks for North American and export markets.
The facility is tied to the Mack LR Electric, a fully electric refuse truck that entered serial production at the Macungie plant in 2021.
Mack offers the MD Electric, a Class 6 and 7 battery-electric truck designed for medium-duty applications such as urban delivery.
Electric trucks are part of active product portfolios, manufacturing plans, dealer training programs, charging discussions, and fleet deployment decisions.
The pace and scale of EV adoption vary by duty cycle, region, and customer use case.
Trump made domestic manufacturing a central part of his economic platform.
Trump moved to roll back several clean vehicle policies, including California’s ability to enforce certain zero-emission vehicle and heavy-duty truck rules.
This policy rollback created a different environment for fleets and manufacturers compared to previous years.
Electrification in the commercial vehicle industry is moving forward at a slower pace.
Applications showing practical alignment with battery-electric trucks include refuse, urban delivery, drayage, yard operations, and return-to-base routes.
Long-haul and heavy regional applications remain difficult due to range, charging infrastructure, payload, vehicle cost, and operational flexibility.
The industry is focusing on where electric trucks can provide a workable business case today while manufacturers continue building diesel and other powertrain options.
Trump’s appearance at Mack Trucks underscored the importance of truck manufacturing to the U.S. economy.
Even without zero-emission mandates, electrification remains part of the commercial vehicle conversation for manufacturers building electric models and fleets evaluating deployments.
source_title: Trump’s Mack Trucks Visit Puts AVs Back in the Conversation

facts: Quality Control Manager jobs in Georgia are in strong demand, concentrated in manufacturing, food and beverage processing, logistics, and aerospace.
Openings for quality control managers exist at every level from quality technician lead through senior QC director.
The busiest hiring markets for these roles are Atlanta, Savannah, and Gainesville.
Employers maintaining significant operations and ongoing quality leadership needs include Gulfstream Aerospace, Pilgrim's Pride, and NCR Voyix.

FOX Factory designs, engineers, manufactures, and markets performance-defining products and systems primarily for bicycles, side-by-side vehicles, on-road vehicles with and without off-road capabilities, off-road vehicles and trucks, all-terrain vehicles, snowmobiles, specialty vehicles and applications, motorcycles, and commercial trucks.
Some FOX Factory products are specifically designed for and marketed to leading cycling and powered vehicle original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
Fox Factory Holding Corp. is the holding company of FOX Factory, Inc. and is publicly traded (FOXF) on Nasdaq.
Quality control managers in Georgia earn a median of about $127,820 a year based on May 2025 Bureau of Labor Statistics wage data.
The wage range for quality control managers in Georgia ranges from around $75,720 for the lowest 10% to over $194,480 for the top 10%.
Top industries hiring quality control managers in Georgia include manufacturing, construction & real estate, automotive, technology & software, and transportation & logistics.
Employers in Georgia most often look for a bachelor's degree in engineering, manufacturing, food science, or a closely related field.
Demonstrated experience managing quality systems such as ISO 9001, AS9100, or FSSC 22000 is highly sought after.
Certified Quality Manager or Certified Quality Engineer credentials from ASQ are preferred by Georgia employers.
Hands-on experience conducting root cause analysis and leading corrective action processes is required.
Proven ability to supervise quality technicians and coordinate cross-functional inspection teams is expected.
Familiarity with Georgia-relevant regulatory standards including USDA or FDA facility compliance is valued.

Most quality control manager roles in Georgia require a bachelor's degree in a technical field such as industrial engineering, food science, or manufacturing technology, combined with progressive experience in quality assurance.
Georgia does not issue a state license specific to quality control managers.
Employers strongly favor candidates holding American Society for Quality credentials, particularly the Certified Quality Manager or Certified Quality Engineer designation.
Current employers hiring quality control managers in Georgia include FOX Factory, HYUNDAI MOBIS, and Medline Industries based on listings as of July 2026.
Georgia's concentration of aerospace suppliers around Savannah drives consistent quality management demand.
Gainesville's poultry and food processing industry sustains steady hiring in food safety and compliance roles.
Atlanta anchors the job market through its dense base of manufacturing headquarters, distribution centers, and aerospace suppliers.
About 7% of quality control manager openings tied to Georgia are remote or hybrid as of July 2026.
The portions of the role most suited to remote work include documentation review, supplier communication, and quality management system administration.
The most realistic entry path for hiring with little or no experience is to start as a quality technician or quality inspector at a Georgia manufacturing or food processing facility.
Large operations like poultry processors in the Gainesville area and aerospace suppliers near Savannah regularly hire quality technicians without prior management experience.

Earning an entry-level ASQ certification such as the Certified Quality Inspector, or completing a quality assurance associate program at a Georgia Technical College System campus, gives candidates a measurable edge.
source_title: Quality Control Manager Jobs in Georgia | Migrate Mate

facts: Attorney General Tim Griffin announced a settlement of nearly $150 million with Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC, and Mercedes-Benz Group AG for violating state law prohibiting unfair or deceptive trade practices.

Griffin joined a coalition of 50 attorneys general to announce the settlement regarding vehicles equipped with illegal and undisclosed emissions defeat devices designed to circumvent emissions standards.

From 2008 to 2016, the states allege Mercedes manufactured, marketed, advertised, and distributed nationwide more than 211,000 diesel passenger cars and vans equipped with software defeat devices that optimized emission controls during emissions tests.
The states allege that the defeat devices enable vehicles to exceed many legal limits of nitrogen oxide (NOx) emissions.
Mercedes allegedly engaged in the practice to reach new design and performance goals, such as increased fuel efficiency and reduced maintenance.
Mercedes allegedly concealed the existence of the defeat devices from state and federal regulators and the public, falsely marketing the vehicles to consumers as 'environmentally friendly.'
The settlement requires Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC and Mercedes-Benz Group AG to pay $120 million to the states immediately upon the date of the settlement.
Additionally, $29,673,750 will be suspended and potentially waived pending the completion of a comprehensive consumer relief program.
Arkansas will receive $250,000 from the settlement.
Approximately 704 of the impacted vehicles were sold in Arkansas.

The attorneys general of Alabama, Connecticut, Delaware, Georgia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, South Carolina, and Texas led today’s settlement, joined by Alaska, Arkansas, Colorado, the District of Columbia, Florida, Hawaii, Idaho, Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Maine, Massachusetts, Michigan, Minnesota, Mississippi, Missouri, Montana, Nebraska, Nevada, New Hampshire, New Mexico, North Carolina, North Dakota, Ohio, Oklahoma, Oregon, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Dakota, Tennessee, Utah, Vermont, Virginia, Washington, West Virginia, Wisconsin, Wyoming, and Puerto Rico.

Attorney General Tim Griffin stated that protecting consumers from deceptive trade practices is a top priority for his office and that the settlement ensures Mercedes-Benz takes responsibility by compensating consumers and implementing critical reform.
source_title: AG Griffin announces $150 million settlement with Mercedes-Benz USA over emissions fraud

facts: Mercedes-Benz is set to expand its footprint in Georgia, announcing that Metro Atlanta will soon become the company’s North American headquarters.
Governor Brian Kemp announced the decision on Monday, calling it a big step forward for the state’s increasing image as a center for innovation and the auto sector.
The decision means Mercedes-Benz will move some of its most important business teams to the “1MB” building in Sandy Springs.
About 800 Georgians already work at the “1MB” site, which debuted in 2018.
As the corporation becomes more committed to Georgia, up to 500 more jobs are expected to be opened in the area.
Mercedes-Benz is moving its North American headquarters to Metro Atlanta and building a new, high-tech Research & Development center near the Sandy Springs site.
This investment will provide new job opportunities for people in the area and show that the brand is committed to pushing the limits of automotive technology.
Officials think that the whole change will be done by August 2026.

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “Georgia continues to lead the way in the future of mobility and technical innovation, attracting world-class companies like Mercedes-Benz that are driving the automotive industry forward.”

Governor Brian Kemp added: “We’re excited that a job creator that already has close ties to Georgia is doubling down on that choice and growing their presence here in the best state for business and opportunity.”
Jason Hoff, CEO of Mercedes-Benz North America, expressed gratitude to the state for the help the company received.
Hoff stressed that the expansion will bring team members closer together, making the organization more flexible and more capable to provide outstanding client service.
Hoff noted that the Atlanta area’s world-class environment will assist Mercedes-Benz keep growing in the U.S. market.
The “1MB” site continues to be home to the sales and financial services departments at Mercedes-Benz.
The new R&D hub will focus on making the next generation of cars and mobility solutions.

Officials from Georgia, including the Department of Economic Development and partners like the City of Sandy Springs, Select Fulton, the Metro Atlanta Chamber, and Georgia Power, all worked together to get this important project.
Mercedes-Benz AG has over 175,000 employees around the world.
The company is known for its high-end cars, high quality, and strong concentration on electric mobility and vehicle software.
The company’s most recent investment in Atlanta shows that it wants to stay at the top of the quickly changing auto industry.
source_title: Georgia Gov. Kemp celebrates major win as Mercedes-Benz deepens roots in Georgia with 500 new jobs

facts: Michelin announced the full rollout of TreadEye technology across its U.S. retread network after a period of phased implementation.
TreadEye is a proprietary tread depth assessment tool designed to enhance the retread process and deliver measurable value to fleets.
The system accurately and consistently collects 1,200 measurement points to provide precise insights into tread wear and casing condition.
Key benefits include identification of pull points by fleet location and maximization of rubber on the road by avoiding early pulls.
The technology helps prevent casing damage and reduces DOT violations and downtime from late pulls.
TreadEye improves efficiency in the tire removal process, directly impacting a fleet’s bottom line.
Nate Kirian stated that “TreadEye is a game-changer for our retread operations and for the fleets we serve” and highlighted it as an example of Michelin’s innovation benefiting customers.
Michelin is a world-leading manufacturer of engineered materials and composites that has pioneered its craft for more than 130 years.

The company provides data- and AI-based connected solutions for professional fleets alongside high-quality tires and components for mobility, construction, aeronautics, low-carbon energies, and healthcare.
Michelin North America, Inc. is headquartered in Greenville, S.C.
Michelin North America, Inc. has approximately 23,500 employees.
Michelin North America, Inc. operates 36 production facilities in the United States and Canada.

facts: Michelin Retread Technologies (MRT) is expanding its Covington, Ga., manufacturing facility.
The expansion aims to meet the continued increase in customer interest in Michelin retreads.
John Yarbro serves as the facility manager.

The expansion is necessary to meet demand for additional tread volume and keep pace with new tire technology features such as co-extruded rubber and extra wide treads for the new X One wide single tires.
This marks the first major expansion of the facility since it began operation in November 1999.
The company is doubling the production volume, emphasizing high technology volume.
The facility is located southeast of Atlanta.
The facility produces a variety of Pre-Mold treads for MRT franchises and retread plants throughout North America.
MRT offers Pre-Mold treads for long haul, regional, on/off road, urban, and sanitation specific applications.
Tread rubber products include full-width tread for the new generation X One wide single truck tires, which replace two dual tires on truck drive and trailer axles.
Products also include co-extruded extra deep drive treads designed for extra miles and cooler running operating temperatures.
Michelin Retreads apply new-tire technology to existing retreaded tires, offering proven tread designs and higher quality standards tailored to customer needs.
MRT treads are manufactured using Michelin proprietary tread rubber compounds and patterns to mirror new Michelin tire aspects and performance.
Michelin North America Inc., the parent company, is headquartered in Greenville, S.C.
Michelin North America Inc. employs 23,920 people.
Michelin North America Inc. operates 21 plants across 17 locations.

facts: Michelin North America, Inc. presented TreadVision by Michelin Retread Technologies, a new retreading system that integrates AI, robotics and advanced data analytics to improve retread quality, consistency and operational efficiency for fleets.
Michelin introduced TreadVision at the Technology & Maintenance Council Annual meeting.
Janet Foster-Whitley, Senior Director, Enterprise Dealer & North America Retreading, stated that Michelin has a long history of innovation in the mobility space.

With TreadVision, Michelin is driving the industry forward once again by evolving retreading to deliver greater consistency, improved quality and faster turnaround times while helping fleets extend asset life and control operating costs.
A key component of the system is TreadEye, a tread-depth assessment technology which is now available across the Michelin Retread Technologies dealer network.
The tool captures 1,200 measurement points per tyre, providing detailed insights into tread wear and casing condition, which helps fleets determine optimal pull points and protect casing integrity.
It also incorporates AI-powered automated inspection and predictive modelling to identify imperfections that may not be visible through traditional inspection methods.
Automated classification of Casing Integrity Analysis (shearography) results through Vision AI further strengthens real-time quality control by ensuring only suitable casings proceed to retreading.
The system integrates automated tyre handling and plant flow to streamline retread operations and improve turnaround times.
Automated specification management standardises build parameters, helping reduce process variability and human error while improving product consistency.
TreadVision integrates with Michelin’s Fleet Business Insights platform, transforming operational data into actionable insights.
The platform provides fleets with visibility into tyre performance trends, asset tracking, pull-point strategies, and cost-management opportunities across the entire tyre lifecycle.
source_title: Michelin North America Launches TreadVision AI Retread Platform | Retreading Business

facts: Milliken & Company is headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina.
Milliken & Company has been exploring, discovering, and creating ways to enhance people's lives since 1865.
The company possesses expertise across specialty chemical, floor covering, and performance materials.
Milliken employs over 100 PhDs and holds over 2,200 U.S. patents and more than 5,000 patents worldwide.
Milliken is widely acknowledged as an international leader in research technology, innovation, and customer service.

The company operates over 35 manufacturing facilities in the U.S., U.K., Belgium, France, China, India, Mexico, and Australia, along with sales and service operations throughout the Americas, Europe, and Asia.
Milliken has more than 7,000 associates globally.
Valway is located in LaGrange, Georgia.
A Process Improvement Engineer position is open at Valway/Milliken & Company in LaGrange, Georgia.
The role reports to the Process Improvement Manager.
Duties include improving product value through continuous improvement efforts in raw and indirect materials, processing specifications, equipment capabilities, and associate competencies.

Responsibilities include supervising production associates and systems for safe and efficient realization of production needs through control of labor utilization, training, morale, downtime, daily management processes, 5S, and OOF product disposition.
The role ensures products have accurate specifications and are costed correctly.
Quality losses are tracked and focused improvement activities are maintained to reduce those losses.
Efficiency losses involving processing methods and MED are identified and addressed through focused improvement activities.
Weekly quality auditing is performed on each range to verify the quality management system's effectiveness.
The role owns quality maintenance and ensures auxiliary equipment has accurate specifications and technical support.
Customer complaints are investigated through root cause analysis and communicated to the business and customer.
The Safety Process is supported through sub-committee participation and strategic initiative risk reduction.
SAP and Barco support all products that run at Valway.
The role involves timely disposition of Out Of Flow items.
Active involvement in Cross Functional Teams is required.
The position requires a leadership role in safety, exhibiting high standards of safety compliance, and investigating all safety incidents in the area of ownership.
The role tracks and documents all Associate Performance for direct reports including safety, quality, productivity, and attendance.
Payroll responsibilities are included in the role.
Labor costs are controlled through management of non-straight time labor, including training pay, G/M premium, and overtime.
The role drives continuous skills development by executing process-specific and plant-wide training and certifications.
Collaboration with the EH&S Manager for specialized EH&S training and QFs for process-specific training is required.
Process specific training records of all direct reports are maintained.
Production is expedited to realize business delivery requirements, managing prioritization of delivery/Late positions (ZVLOS) and working with Supply Chain to optimize scheduling.

Process Reliability Continuous Improvement involves systematically measuring, analyzing, and improving equipment scheduled stops (start-ups, shutdowns, and changeovers), controlling and reporting against standardized times, and collaborating with PI and QF for improvement.
Daily Team Maintenance is driven, ensuring all equipment meets cleaning standards and operators perform daily and weekly requirements.
5S is driven in areas of responsibility, including the organization and availability of 580300 supplies necessary for production.
Standard work auditing is performed.
Collaboration with Quality Facilitators to track, report, and expedite OOF (WIP OOF, Re-Inspect(1999), Reworks) is required.
Associates are engaged on a daily basis in a positive manner.
A four-year degree in Engineering is required for the position.
Previous work experience in Process Improvement is preferred.

Milliken & Company is an equal opportunity employer that considers all qualified applicants without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, status as a protected veteran, or status as a qualified individual with a disability.
The salary range for the Process Improvement Engineer position is $17.00 - $30.00 per hour.

facts: Milliken & Company plans to close a textile plant in Screven County, Georgia.
The Screven County plant currently employs 260 workers.
Plant operations will be phased out and transferred to Milliken's facility in Cherokee County, South Carolina.

Kevin Brown, senior vice president of global operations for Milliken’s textile business, stated the consolidation benefits customers with increased capacity and helps realize key sustainability and operational metrics.
The company will make every effort to find new jobs for Screven County workers who wish to transfer to another Milliken location.
The facility manufactures finished goods textiles, including engineered flame-resistant textiles, for workwear use.
Milliken operates more than 70 facilities worldwide and will maintain 10 facilities in Georgia following the closure.
The plant is located on the Ogeechee River.
King America Finishing previously owned the plant before selling it to Milliken in 2014.
In 2011, more than 30,000 fish died near the facility due to illegally dumped treated wastewater by the previous owner, King America Finishing.
The Ogeechee Riverkeeper conservation group noted that Milliken made extensive upgrades and improvements to the plant while continuing to discharge treated wastewater under a government permit.

Damon Mullis, executive director of the riverkeeper group, stated the closure removes the primary source of industrial effluent into the river while acknowledging the social and economic impacts on the community.
source_title: Georgia plant announces plans to close, impacting 260 jobs - WJCL

facts: Hyundai Motor Group and LG Energy Solution announced they will build a $4.3 billion electric battery plant as part of Hyundai's new electric vehicle assembly plant in southeast Georgia.
The companies will split the investment for the project.
Production is slated to start as soon as late 2025.
Hyundai Motor Co. CEO Jaehoon Chang said the battery plant would create a strong foundation to lead the global EV transition.
Chang explained the company wants to speed up efforts to produce electrified Hyundai and Kia vehicles in North America.
Chang stated Hyundai Motor Group is focusing on its electrification efforts to secure a leadership position in the global auto industry.
In 2022, Hyundai said it would invest $5.5 billion to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, just west of Savannah.
The Ellabell site is supposed to have 8,100 employees and is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Garrison Douglas, spokesperson for Republican Gov. Brian Kemp, said the 3,000-job battery plant would be part of the 8,100 overall jobs.
Douglas stated the $4.3 billion investment would be part of the previously announced $5.5 billion total.
The Hyundai/LG plant is supposed to be able to supply batteries for 300,000 electric vehicles per year.
This capacity matches the initial projected production of the adjoining vehicle assembly plant.
Hyundai has said the Georgia plant could later expand to build 500,000 vehicles annually.
Gov. Brian Kemp said this is exactly what they envisioned when Georgia landed the Hyundai Metaplant in May of last year.
Kemp stated the project is the latest milestone in Georgia's path to becoming the EV capital of the nation.
Auto parts suppliers have pledged to invest more than $2 billion and hire 4,800 people in the region around the Hyundai site.
The announcements are part of an electric vehicle and battery land rush across the United States.
Under the U.S. Inflation Reduction Act, EVs must be assembled in North America to qualify for a full $7,500 EV tax credit.
A certain percentage of battery parts and minerals must come from North America or a U.S. free trade partner to qualify for the full tax credit.
Currently, no Hyundai or Kia vehicles are eligible for the tax credit unless they are leased.
Hyundai opposed having foreign-made vehicles excluded, in part because it is building American factories.
Gov. Kemp has supported that position against excluding foreign-made vehicles from the tax credit.
Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff said Hyundai should wait until it is producing vehicles domestically using American-made batteries before claiming the credits.
Ossoff told reporters that as soon as those vehicles are produced in Georgia, they can be eligible for the credits through the incentives in the IRA.
Ossoff stated these manufacturing incentives are attracting and accelerating billions of dollars of investments in jobs and advanced energy and electric vehicle production capacity in Georgia.
LG said this would be its seventh battery plant in operation or under construction in the U.S.
LG is concentrating efforts to expand production in the country, citing federal incentives as a lure for manufacturers.
This marks the second huge electric battery plant Hyundai is partnering to build in Georgia.
Hyundai and SK On, a unit of South Korea's SK Group, announced in December they would jointly invest $4 billion to $5 billion to build a new plant northwest of Atlanta.
The Cartersville plant will supply electric batteries for Hyundai and Kia electric vehicles assembled in the U.S.
The Cartersville plant is planned to begin production in 2025 and employ a projected 3,500 people.
Hyundai will need batteries for more than just vehicles made in Ellabell.
The company is already assembling electric vehicles at its plant in Montgomery, Alabama.
In April, Hyundai announced it would start assembling its electric Kia EV9 large SUV at the Kia plant in West Point, Georgia.
Partnering with LG and SK diversifies Hyundai’s supplier base, giving the automaker more than one battery manufacturer to buy from.
Douglas said no additional incentives would be offered because the battery plant is part of the overall Hyundai complex.
The state of Georgia and local governments have already pledged $1.8 billion in tax breaks and other incentives.
Greg LeRoy, executive director of Good Jobs First, stated this is the largest subsidy package a U.S. state has ever promised an automotive plant.
Good Jobs First is described as a group skeptical of subsidies to private companies.
source_title: Hyundai and LG announce $4.3 billion plant in Georgia to build batteries for electric vehicles | Science News | thecanadianpressnews.ca

facts: A number of electric membership cooperatives (EMCs) in Georgia were awarded millions in funding for broadband expansion through the America Rescue Plan Act (ARPA), part of federal coronavirus relief funding allocated to Georgia.

Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp announced almost $408 million in preliminary awards to 49 recipients, including seven EMCs, to provide fast and reliable internet service to communities, households, and businesses in 70 counties in rural parts of the state.
Collectively, seven EMCs will receive approximately $130 million to provide broadband service to approximately 65,000 customers across rural Georgia.
Altamaha EMC received a $5.9 million grant with a total investment of $10 million to serve 2,171 customers in portions of Toombs and Montgomery Counties.

Central Georgia EMC received a $25 million grant with a total investment of $150 million to serve 19,996 unserved customers across its 14-county service territory in partnership with Conexon Connect, with an estimated project completion in summer 2024.

Diverse Power received a $25 million grant with a total investment of $39 million to serve 4,769 customers in portions of Harris, Troup, Meriwether, Quitman, and Coweta Counties as a subsidiary of Kudzu Networks, with an estimated project completion in 36 to 48 months.
Flint Energies received a $25 million grant to serve 10,659 customers across portions of nine counties in its 17-county service area, with an estimated project completion in three to four years.

Ocmulgee EMC received a $19 million grant to serve 9,000 customers in portions of Bleckley, Dodge, Laurens, Pulaski, and Telfair Counties as an LLC, with an estimated project completion in two to four years.

Southern Rivers Energy received a $15.6 million grant with a total investment of $56 million to serve 10,674 customers in portions of Bibb, Crawford, Lamar, Monroe, Pike, Spalding, and Upson counties in partnership with Conexon Connect, with an estimated project completion in the first quarter of 2023.

Washington EMC received a $13.2 million grant with a total investment of $52 million to serve 6,500 customers in portions of Glascock, Hancock, Warren, and Washington counties in partnership with Conexon Connect, with an estimated project completion in mid-2024.

Georgia EMC President/CEO Dennis Chastain stated that the funding is a great day for EMC members and highlighted that rural communities have long waited for essential broadband service to compete in the digital world, with the pandemic shining a light on access disparities.
Five additional EMCs supported 12 applications that led to an additional $172 million in funding and 62,000 customers gaining access to high-speed internet in rural Georgia.
Amicalola EMC, along with Ellijay Telephone Company, supported the Gilmer/Pickens Development Authority application, securing a $21.4 million grant for 4,259 customers in Gilmer and Pickens Counties.

Colquitt EMC supported applications from Berrien, Brooks, Colquitt, Cook, and Lowndes Counties in partnership with Windstream, securing grants of $15.2 million (4,737 customers), $13.8 million (3,749 customers), $22.5 million (7,448 customers), $10.9 million (3,723 customers), and $22.5 million (18,218 customers) respectively.
Grady EMC supported Thomas County's application in partnership with Windstream, securing a $6.8 million grant for 2,098 customers.
Oconee EMC supported Twiggs County's application in partnership with Windstream, securing a $4.6 million grant for 2,034 customers.

Sumter EMC supported applications from Lee, Stewart, and Terrell Counties in partnership with Windstream, securing grants of $12.5 million (4,234 customers), $6.6 million (2,729 unserved customers), and $9.6 million (2,270 customers) respectively.
Sumter EMC also supported the One Sumter Economic Development Foundation application, securing a $25.3 million grant for 5,726 customers with an estimated project completion in one to three years.
Applications were reviewed by the Georgia Jobs and Infrastructure Committee created by Governor Kemp.
Most recipients included local governments, non-profit organizations, and service providers ranging from large companies, EMCs, and local Georgia-based telecommunications companies.

Since 2019, 19 EMCs have made broadband announcements with plans to provide approximately 250,000 EMC members with high-speed internet service in 86 Georgia counties through six affiliates and 14 partnership agreements.
In just two years, EMCs combined have invested more than $680 million to expand broadband in their service areas.
Georgia EMC is the statewide trade association representing the state's 41 EMCs, Oglethorpe Power Corp., Georgia Transmission Corp., and Georgia System Operations Corp.

Collectively, Georgia's customer-owned EMCs provide electricity and related services to approximately 4.4 million people, nearly half of Georgia's population, across 73 percent of the state's land area.
source_title: EMCs Receive $130 Million for Broadband Expansion - Georgia Electric Membership Corporation

facts: Murata Electronics North America Inc. is located in Smyrna, GA.
The company launched a new line of 0201-packaged negative-temperature-coefficient (NTC) thermistors.
The company maintains that the new components are among the smallest chip thermistors available.
Kozo Kagawa serves as Murata's group product manager.
The NTC thermistor features an unusual inner construction intended to make the lead-free device suitable for high-density mounting in general circuits.
Termination of the components is accomplished with a nickel barrier.
The 0201-size chip provides good solderability, supports reflow soldering, and offers high long-time aging stability.
The new components measure 0.6mm x 0.3mm.
The components provide temperature compensation for transistors, ICs, and crystal oscillators in mobile communications equipment.
Temperature compensation and sensing are also provided for LCDs, car audio equipment, and rechargeable batteries.
Currently available resistance values are 10,000 ohms, with additional values to be expanded in the future.
The parts are already in mass production.
Murata will increase the manufacturing capability of all SMT-package NTC thermistors by as much as 50 percent by March.
Component availability lead time is eight to ten weeks.
Pricing ranges from $0.19 to $0.21 cents each in 100,000-piece quantities.
source_title: Murata Electronics Launches NTC Thermistors For Cellular Phones - New Products

Japan-based Elna Co. Ltd will establish a new factory in the Far East in 1999 with an unidentified site.
Elna America Inc. in Cypress, Calif. added national distributors including Bell Microproducts, Future Electronics, and Newark Electronics.
Loren Pleet is national sales and marketing manager at Elna America Inc.
Keith Thomas is vice president of Kyocera Developed Products at AVX Corp. in Myrtle Beach, S.C.
AVX will launch a pilot Internet program for Marshall Industries to track inventory and orders from the Web.
Willie King is director of product marketing at AVX.
Kemet Corp. opened distribution hubs in Guadalajara, Mexico, and Sao Paulo, Brazil in 1998.
Sandy Beck is vice president of marketing at Kemet in Greenville, S.C.
Mark Richey is product marketing manager at Rohm Electronics in San Diego.
Vishay Intertechnology Inc. saw average selling prices of capacitors drop 10% to 15%.
Glyndwr Smith is assistant to the chief executive and senior vice president of marketing intelligence at Malvern, Pa.-based Vishay.
Dennis Zogbi is an analyst at the Paumanok Group in Apex, N.C.
Prices of all fixed capacitors dropped almost 9% last year.
Unit shipments will continue to grow with gradual improvement in demand expected as financial conditions in Asia improve.
Worldwide revenue of fixed capacitors dropped 4.2% last year while unit shipments rose 5.9%.

The worldwide market for fixed capacitors reached $9.5 billion in 1998, with aluminum at $2.9 billion, multilayer at $2.8 billion, tantalum at $1.7 billion, DC film at $1.4 billion, and single-layer at $700 million.
Tantalum capacitors are starting to eat into aluminum's market share while ceramic capacitors offer similar values as some tantalums.
Philips Components in San Jose is developing ceramic surface-mount capacitors with capacitance values up to 33 microfarads available in the first half of 1999.
AVX predicts releasing double-layer supercapacitors in mid-1999 using patented Rechargeable Hydrogen-Ion Solid-State technology for wireless and rechargeable secondary power applications.
Palladium accounts for most raw-material cost in multilayer ceramic capacitors.
Palladium prices soared to $248 per troy ounce in mid-1997 and hovered near $300 in 1998.
Rohm plans to have base-metal electrode caps in 0805 and 0603 packages in the higher range of capacitance values in summer 1999.
Kemet is offering limited base-metal-electrode products in X7R dielectric and will work on Y5E products in early 1999.
Murata plans to shift completely to nickel or copper electrodes, with half its products currently using base-metal electrodes.
Philips has moved to base-metal electrodes in Y5V and Z5U products and will offer X7R devices using them early this year.
AVX expects to reach 100 microfarads for multilayer ceramic capacitors by end of 1999, directing all development toward 100% nickel.
Murata is shipping 47-microfarads product and offering prototype samples of 100-microfarads devices for production quantities this year.
Vishay plans to produce low-ESR polymer-tantalum capacitors in the coming year for high-frequency applications.
Philips will introduce high-frequency microwave-range surface-mount ceramic capacitors in 0603 and smaller cases in second half of 1999.
AVX announced its Z Chip, a discrete impedance-matching series resistor/capacitor chip in October, available in 0603 package.
Integrated passive devices (IPDs) can contain multiple passive and active components in one package to replace several discrete components on a printed-circuit board.
Passives vendors estimate chip-array prices declined 30% to 35% in past 18 months, while IPDs using thin-film on silicon fell 20% in past two quarters.
Marty Peck is vice president of Bradford, Pa.-based resistor maker KOA Spear Electronics Inc.
Manufacturers predict 0402 and 0201 sizes of ceramic capacitors will be available to market in 1999.
Philips plans to introduce 0201-size surface-mount capacitors in third quarter of 1999.
Murata's Denslinger noted only a couple of North American accounts currently use 0201 devices, with placement ability dictating full-scale acceptance.
Initial 0201 offerings will be in tape-and-reel packaging, with bulk packaging developments expected in early 2000.
source_title: Pointing The Spotlight On Customer Needs - EE Times

facts: pSemi unveiled breakthrough power solutions for fast charging mobile devices and next generation humanoid robotics at APEC 2026.
The company showcases the world’s first 4-level buck converter for battery charging applications alongside new charge pump and two-stage step-down DC-DC converters.
New power products are designed to solve power challenges in low-profile, high-energy density applications.
PE26100 architecture supports 4.5V to 18.5V input and enables 4-level buck mode for higher USB-PD voltage inputs.
The PE26100 engages 3-level buck mode for mid-to-low input voltages.
In USB PPS applications, the PE26100 operates as a fixed-ratio, capacitor-divider charge pump with divider ratios of 2:1 and 3:1 depending on programmed input voltage.
pSemi announced the release of the PE25304, an advanced integrated charge pump switching-capacitor power module.
The PE25304 is engineered to deliver unprecedented efficiency in space-constrained, high-power applications.
Designed to divide input voltage by four, the PE25304 is purpose-built for 48V input architectures with a wide operating range from 20V to 60V.
The PE25304 is suitable for emerging dexterous hand robotics and mechatronic systems.
The PE25304 comes in an ultra-low-profile package (2mm) and can deliver up to 72W of output power.
The PE25304 achieves 97% conversion efficiency, significantly reducing power loss and thermal buildup.
Beyond robotics, the PE25304 is suitable for drones, medical devices, embedded AI modules, and industrial automation systems.
pSemi will showcase two-stage buck regulators powering optical transceiver and sub 10nm low output voltage core applications at APEC 2026.
The showcased two-stage buck regulators are ideal for low profile (~1.2mm) applications for under-side PCB placement to reduce board area and power losses.
PE24111 is a latest product in this category, providing 3.3Vin capable of 20A output delivery to voltages in the range of 0.35-0.85V.
pSemi will showcase ultra-high efficiency switching capacitor charge pump divider products for intermediate bus power conversion at APEC 2026.
PE25213 supports divide by two and three ratios at 10A output current with efficiencies up to 99%.
These products can significantly improve overall system efficiency and extend battery life.
pSemi will showcase two-stage boost LED drivers, combining charge pump followed by a boost regulator and LED drivers in a single package.
PE23108 is an 8-channel driver for high efficiency, low-profile panel displays included in this category.
pSemi (formerly Peregrine Semiconductor) is a leader in semiconductor integration design and development.
The company provides high-performance RF, analog, and mixed signal solutions with a 30-year legacy of technology advancements.
pSemi's solution portfolio spans power management, connected sensors, antenna tuning, and RF frontends.
These solutions enable advanced modules for smartphones, base stations, personal computers, electric vehicles, data centers, IoT devices, healthcare, and more.
pSemi is a wholly owned subsidiary of Murata Electronics North America, Inc.
pSemi is headquartered in San Diego with additional offices spanning North America, Europe, and Asia.
source_title: pSemi, a Murata Company, Unveils Breakthrough Power Solutions for Fast Charging Mobile Devices and Next Generation Humanoid Robotics at APEC 2026

facts: Novelis Inc is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Ltd and a producer of aluminum products and solutions.
The company focuses on recycling operations to produce low-carbon aluminum solutions.
Product portfolio includes sheet ingot, foils, recycled aluminum, can stock, and electronic products.
Offers various sheets including automotive sheets, industrial sheets, construction sheets, and painted sheets.

Supplies products to diverse industries including building and construction, signage, foil and packaging, commercial transportation, architectural, industrial and consumer durables, and other markets.
Operates across North America, Europe, Asia, and South America regions.
Headquarters is located in Atlanta, Georgia, the US.
In August 2025, the company received sustainability awards by the Business Intelligence Group for its company vision, Novelis 3x30.
In August 2025, the company partnered with DRT Holdings, LLC to accelerate the adoption of high-recycled-content alloys in aluminum beverage can ends.
In June 2025, the company partnered with The Velux Group for the supply of high recycled content aluminium.
Has 13,250 employees.
Kumar Mangalam Birla serves as Chairman on the Executive Board since 2007.
Steven Fisher serves as President, Director, and Chief Executive Officer on the Executive Board since 2023.
Devinder Ahuja serves as Chief Financial Officer and Executive Vice President in Senior Management since 2016.
Emilio Braghi serves as Chief Operating Officer and President - Novelis Europe in Senior Management starting in 2026.
Stephanie Rauls serves as Senior Vice President, Deputy Chief Financial Officer, and Chief Accounting Officer in Senior Management since 2016.

facts: Fire broke out at Novelis’ facility in Greensboro, Georgia, following an explosion the afternoon of Sunday, March 1.

Greene County Fire Public Safety, Emergency Management/Homeland Security Director Joe Bashore told The Union-Recorder that he heard the significant explosion from 5 miles away and it rattled his house.
Novelis confirmed that an explosion occurred in a piece of equipment at its Greensboro recycling plant in the late afternoon of Sunday, March 1, leading to a fire in the equipment.
There were no injuries to employees or first responders resulting from the incident.
The Greensboro plant is currently shut down while the company cleans the impacted areas.

Novelis stated that it has recycling and casting capacity at its Berea, Guthrie, and Logan facilities in Kentucky, its Oswego plant in New York, and access to third-party casting centers, so it does not expect a significant impact on customers.
The Greensboro facility was the first stand-alone used beverage can (UBC) recycling facility for Novelis and now pioneers many of the company's recycling technologies.
In late 2025, Novelis experienced two major fires at its Oswego plant’s hot mill that affected recovered aluminum demand.
The Oswego plant was previously the biggest producer of auto body sheet and a large producer of can sheet.
Some of Novelis' rolling mills have shifted their manufacturing lines away from other end markets, including beverage cans, to support Ford and other automotive companies.
source_title: Novelis' Greensboro, Georgia, plant site of fire - Recycling Today

facts: Mike Grundmann serves as director of automotive, aerospace and advanced manufacturing at the Georgia Department of Economic Development.
Foreign companies have invested $2.8 billion in Georgia’s automotive sector.
Georgia is strategically located as a center for assembly and manufacturing, possesses a quality workforce, and has developed a reputation for innovation in the industry.
The department along with affiliated organizations announced winners of its inaugural Georgia Automotive Awards in five categories to honor contributions to the state's robust automotive ecosystem.
Novelis Inc. received the automotive innovation award.
Toyo Tire U.S.A. Corp. received the automotive supplier of the year award.
Hitachi Automotive Systems Americas Inc. received the community impact award.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Georgia Inc. received the Georgia automotive company of the year award.
J. Randy Jackson received the Georgia automotive leader of the year award.
All award recipient companies had expanded their operations in the state since initially settling there.
Toyo Tire arrived in Georgia in 2004 and has since expanded its facility several times to 3 million square feet under one roof, representing a local investment of more than $1 billion.
Toyo Tire grew during the recession.
The department looks for automotive suppliers that show strong growth and have an impact on the health of the state.

Novelis developed sophisticated aluminum for Jaguar, Land Rover and Ford vehicles to help them meet evolving fuel efficiency and greenhouse gas emissions standards by reducing vehicle weight without compromising safety or strength.
Monroe-based Hitachi employs 1,000 people and manufactures valve timing systems, propeller shafts and power steering systems.
Hitachi was honored for community involvement through the F.I.S.H. (Faith in Serving Humanity) program, which provides food to combat child hunger in Walton County.
Hitachi is active in the Communities in Schools program.

The Communities in Schools program works to ensure at-risk children remain in school by providing one-on-one relationships with caring adults, a safe place to grow and learn, proper nutrition and exposure to marketable skills upon graduation.
Kia Motors Manufacturing Inc. won automotive company of the year, exemplifying relevant factors and demonstrating overall industry leadership within the state.
Kia celebrated the manufacture of its 2-millionth car earlier this year (relative to December 2016).
Kia donated $2.1 million toward STEM education in local elementary schools.
Kia made a $3.5 million contribution to establish THINC, a new college and career academy at West Georgia Technical College’s LaGrange campus.
J. Randy Jackson was Kia’s chief administrative officer and a key figure behind the company's success in Georgia.
The award for J. Randy Jackson was conferred posthumously to honor his memory for support of STEM education, workforce development and the auto industry within the state.
Grundmann anticipates more foreign investment in the state’s auto sector.
Grundmann cited the success of Georgia’s Quick Start program in preparing the state’s workforce and credited it with attracting Chinese company Sentury Tire.
Grundmann anticipates more Chinese investment, stating that the department is always recruiting from China and many Chinese companies are interested in establishing operations.
Grundmann mentioned continued interest from Korean and Japanese companies.

Volvo Cars of North America LLC announced a $500 million investment for a plant 30 miles northwest of Charleston, S.C., which serves as an incentive for suppliers to move to Georgia with easy access to the plant.
LINDE + WIEMANN GmbH KG, a German manufacturer of structural steel systems for automotive OEMs in Europe, Asia and Africa, announced a $35 million investment in January.
LINDE + WIEMANN will benefit from its location in Hart County, which borders South Carolina and is in fairly easy reach of Charleston.

Georgia’s future role on the cutting edge of automotive innovation is secured by the Panasonic Automotive Innovation Center near the Georgia Institute of Technology’s campus, which will serve as an incubator for next-generation automotive infotainment technologies.

Georgia will benefit from the AT&T Drive Studio in Midtown, where AT&T will develop and test connected car technologies such as internet connectivity, remote diagnostics and over-the-air software updates.
Grundmann stated that Georgia is rich in innovation and at the epicenter of talent and research.
Grundmann added that the state has an autonomous eco center that may now be its most valued asset.
source_title: Georgia Honors Its Foreign Auto Investors and Has High Hopes to Welcome More - Global Atlanta

facts: Novelis describes itself as “the leading provider of innovative sustainable aluminum solutions and the world’s largest recycler of aluminum,” according to the prospectus.
Novelis Inc.’s IPO consists of 45.0 million shares – all offered by the selling shareholder – at a price range of $18.00 to $21.00 – to raise $877.5 million, if priced at the mid-point of its range.
The company will not receive any proceeds from the sale of the selling shareholder’s stock; in other words, Novelis will not receive any of the IPO proceeds.
Novelis, incorporated in Canada, has a U.S. headquarters in Atlanta.

The profitable company makes aluminum for beverage packaging as well as for the automotive, aerospace and specialties markets at 32 plants in North America, Europe, Asia and South America, according to the prospectus.
Novelis intends to pay a quarterly dividend, the prospectus says.
Morgan Stanley, BofA Securities, Citigroup, Wells Fargo Securities, Deutsche Bank Capital Markets and BMO Capital Markets are the joint book-runners.

facts: Novelis Inc. is a leading sustainable aluminum solutions provider and the world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling.
Fourth quarter fiscal year 2025 net income attributable to common shareholders was $294 million, up 77% year-over-year.
Fourth quarter fiscal year 2025 net income excluding special items was $262 million, up 46% year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025 was $473 million, down 8% year-over-year but up 29% quarter-over-quarter.
Rolled product shipments in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025 totaled 957 kilotonnes, up 1% year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA per tonne shipped for the fourth quarter was $494, down 9% year-over-year.
Full fiscal year 2025 net income attributable to common shareholders was $683 million, up 14% year-over-year.
Full fiscal year 2025 net income excluding special items was $764 million, up 11% year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA for full fiscal year 2025 was $1.8 billion, down 4% year-over-year.
Rolled product shipments for full fiscal year 2025 totaled 3,757 kilotonnes, up 2% year-over-year.
Adjusted EBITDA per tonne shipped for full fiscal year 2025 was $480, down 6% year-over-year.
Novelis maintained the recycled content of its products at 63% in fiscal 2025.

Net sales for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025 increased 13% versus the prior year period to $4.6 billion, driven by higher average aluminum prices and a 1% increase in total rolled product shipments.
Higher demand for beverage packaging, aerospace, and specialty products in the fourth quarter was partially offset by lower automotive shipments.
Fourth quarter net income growth was primarily driven by favorable metal price lag, gains in unrealized derivatives, and a lower income tax provision.
Fourth quarter Adjusted EBITDA decline was primarily driven by higher aluminum scrap prices and operating costs, partially offset by higher product pricing.
Net sales for full fiscal year 2025 increased 6% versus the prior year to $17.1 billion, driven by higher average aluminum prices and a 2% increase in total flat rolled product shipments.
The full year shipment increase was mainly due to record high beverage packaging shipments and higher aerospace product shipments, partially offset by lower specialties and automotive products.

Full year net income growth was primarily driven by a favorable change in metal price lag, unrealized gains on derivatives, and a lower income tax provision, partially offset by impacts from the Sierre flooding and lower Adjusted EBITDA.

Full year Adjusted EBITDA decline was mainly driven by higher aluminum scrap prices compared to the prior year, unfavorable product mix, and higher operating costs, partially offset by higher total shipments and higher product pricing.
Net cash flow provided by operating activities was $1.0 billion in fiscal year 2025 compared to $1.3 billion in the prior fiscal year.

Adjusted Free Cash Flow was an outflow of $737 million in fiscal year 2025 compared to a prior year period outflow of $75 million, due primarily to a 24% year-over-year increase in capital expenditures and lower cash flow from operating activities.

Fiscal year 2025 capital expenditures totaled $1.7 billion, reflecting planned increases in strategic, sustainability-focused capital investment projects supporting increased long-term customer demand.
In January 2025, Novelis issued $750 million in senior unsecured notes due January 2030, with proceeds primarily used to repay outstanding borrowings under its ABL revolver.
In March 2025, the company borrowed $1.25 billion of term loans that mature in March 2032, with proceeds primarily used to repay previously issued term loans due in 2026 and 2028.
The company had a net leverage ratio of 2.9x at the end of the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2025.
Total liquidity stood at $2.8 billion as of March 31, 2025, consisting of $1.0 billion in cash and cash equivalents and $1.7 billion in availability under committed credit facilities.
Steve Fisher, president and CEO of Novelis Inc., stated that strong shipments led by robust demand for beverage packaging continue to underpin the fundamental strength of the business.
Steve Fisher noted the company is implementing actions and technologies to expand scrap input types to mitigate scrap cost challenges from elevated aluminum scrap prices.
Steve Fisher emphasized a focus on optimizing cost structure and increasing operational efficiency to drive margin improvements and maintain strong return on invested capital.
Dev Ahuja, executive vice president and CFO of Novelis Inc., stated that disciplined cash management enables strategic investment for growth.
Dev Ahuja highlighted the company's focus on cost efficiency while completing investments to increase rolling and recycling capacity.
Novelis will discuss its fourth quarter and full fiscal year 2025 results via a live webcast and conference call at 7:00 a.m. EDT/4:30 p.m. IST on Monday, May 12, 2025.

Novelis aims to be the leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminum solutions and achieve a fully circular economy by partnering with suppliers and customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage packaging, and specialties industries throughout North America, Europe, Asia, and South America.

Novelis is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai.
