facts: Novelis, a subsidiary of India’s Hindalco Industries, will restart its Oswego hot mill in December, having previously anticipated starting it in the March quarter.
Teams have been working around-the-clock to restore operations at Oswego quickly and safely while leveraging alternative resources to minimize customer disruption.
Novelis recognized charges of $21 million related to the Oswego incident during the quarter.
The plant is insured for property damage and business interruption losses related to such events, with about 70-80% recoverable through insurance in future periods.
Novelis reported a 27% year-on-year growth in net income for the September quarter at $163 million.
Excluding special items, the bottomline was 37% lower compared to the previous year at $113 million.
The US-based aluminum-maker’s net sales were 10% higher compared to the previous year at $4.7 billion, mainly driven by higher average aluminum prices.
Total rolled product shipments for the quarter were in line with the previous year at 941 kilotonnes.
Slightly higher automotive and aerospace shipments were offset by lower beverage packaging and specialty shipments.
Adjusted earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortization (EBITDA) fell 9% on year to $422 million.
The adjusted EBITDA made on each tonne of aluminum stood at $448, down 8% on year.
The fall in EBITDA was primarily on account of a net negative tariff impact and higher aluminum scrap prices, which was partially offset by higher product pricing and cost efficiency actions.
CEO Steve Fisher stated that the second quarter financial performance was in line with expectations for sequential improvement, reflecting solid execution in a continued dynamic environment.

CEO Steve Fisher stated that the company’s diverse global footprint will be further strengthened with a significant investment in the U.S. to construct a state-of-the-art plant in Bay Minette to bring needed capacity to an undersupplied domestic market.
Novelis spent $913 million on capital expenditure in the six months of the current fiscal so far.

This capital expenditure was primarily for strategic investments in new rolling and recycling capacity under construction, including the company's new greenfield rolling and recycling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama.
The company announced its earnings after market hours in India, and shares of parent company Hindalco Industries ended down 1.8% at 830.70 rupees on the BSE.
source_title: Novelis Q2 net income rises 27% YoY to $163 million on higher aluminium prices - The Economic Times

facts: Novelis will invest $365 million to build a highly advanced recycling centre for automotive in North America.
The facility will have an annual casting capacity of 240 kt of sheet ingot.
The company expects the facility to reduce its carbon emissions by more than one million tons each year.
The new recycling facility will be built adjacent to Novelis' existing automotive finishing plant in Guthrie, Kentucky.
The centre will enable Novelis to grow its closed-loop-recycling programs with more automotive customers in North America.
Through closed-loop recycling, the company takes back aluminum remaining after automotive parts are stamped from sheets and remakes it into the same product for new vehicle production.
The centre will also have the capability to process aluminum from vehicles at the end of their lifecycle.
Using recycled aluminum as input material requires only 5 percent of the energy used to make primary aluminum.
This process avoids 95 percent of the carbon emissions associated with production.
The new recycling centre will be equipped with advanced shredding and sorting technology.
The facility will include energy-efficient innovations to support Novelis' sustainability goal to reduce energy intensity by 10 percent by 2026.
Groundbreaking for the recycling centre is scheduled for early 2022.
Commissioning of the facility is expected in 2024.
When fully operational, the expansion will add approximately 140 new jobs in Guthrie.
Novelis held the grand opening of its automotive finishing plant in Guthrie in October.
The existing automotive finishing plant currently employs 150 people.
Employment at the existing automotive finishing plant is expected to grow to 190 over the next two years.

Tom Boney, Executive Vice President and President of Novelis North America, stated that the investment will help Novelis achieve its sustainability goals and support the carbon-reduction targets of automotive customers.
Tom Boney noted that the Commonwealth of Kentucky has been a great business partner with a strong, highly skilled workforce to meet the growing need for high-strength, low-carbon aluminum.
source_title: Novelis to build $365 million automotive recycling center

facts: Novelis announced it will supply Nissan with sustainable, lightweight aluminium body sheet for the all-new Qashqai SUV and create a closed-loop recycling system in Europe.
The Nissan Qashqai, a best-selling SUV in the C-segment, will incorporate Novelis aluminium in the hood and doors to reduce weight and increase fuel efficiency.
Engineers have proven to automakers that lightweighting with aluminium doors and hoods are a cost-effective way to reduce weight.
By using aluminium, the Qashqai has achieved 21kg of the total 60kg of body-in-white weight savings compared to the previous model.
This demonstrates the growing trend by many major automakers to increase aluminium adoption in smaller vehicle segments and high volume production models.

Michael Hahne, VP Automotive at Novelis Europe, stated that Novelis is uniquely positioned to meet the increasing demand for automotive aluminium given its unmatched global footprint and ability to develop innovative, sustainable solutions.
Novelis and Nissan are committed to achieving a more sustainable manufacturing process by establishing a closed-loop recycling system.
Working together, Novelis will deliver aluminum material from its plant in Nachterstedt, Germany to Nissan's car plant in Sunderland UK.
The closed-loop system will collect manufacturing scrap from Nissan and return it to Novelis recycling centers in Europe to be cast and rolled again for production by Nissan.
This process creates a circular supply chain that helps both companies achieve their sustainability goals.

Novelis' ambition is to be the world's leading provider of low-carbon, sustainable aluminium solutions that advance its business, the industry and the society toward the benefits of a circular economy.

Global automotive customers turn to Novelis to help them develop circular economy solutions, like lightweighting and closed-loop recycling that help them sustainably achieve their design and manufacturing goals.

Novelis Inc. is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited, an industry leader in aluminum and copper, and the metals flagship company of the Aditya Birla Group, a multinational conglomerate based in Mumbai, India.

Novelis is the world's largest recycler of aluminium and partners with customers in the aerospace, automotive, beverage can and specialties industries to deliver solutions that maximize the benefits of lightweight aluminium throughout North America, Europe, Asia and South America.
source_title: Novelis to Provide Sustainable Aluminium Body Sheet to Nissan and Create Closed-Loop Recycling System :: Novelis Inc.

facts: Novelis announced it will invest $2.5 billion to build a new low-carbon recycling and rolling plant in Bay Minette, Alabama, USA.
The highly advanced facility will have an initial capacity of 600 kilotonnes of finished aluminium goods per year.
Kumar Mangalam Birla, Chairman of the Aditya Birla Group and the Novelis Board of Directors, stated the investment marks the start of another transformational growth phase for Novelis.
This project is the largest global greenfield expansion project of the Aditya Birla Group.
The investment will take the Aditya Birla Group's total investment in the US across businesses to over $14 billion.

More than half of the capacity of the new facility will be used to serve the growing demand for aluminium beverage can sheets in North America, driven by consumer preference for sustainable packaging.

Steve Fisher, President and CEO of Novelis Inc., stated the investment demonstrates a commitment to grow alongside customers and meet their needs for low-carbon, highly sustainable aluminum solutions.
The facility's construction is backed by strong North American demand for flat-rolled, low-carbon aluminium from can makers and beverage companies.
Aluminium beverage cans, bottles, and cups are described as models of sustainable packaging and the circular economy.
An average "can-to-can" lifecycle lasts just a couple of months, with a recycled can able to return to store shelves in as little as 60 days.
The facility will be the first fully integrated aluminium mill built in the U.S. in 40 years.
The plant is expected to create up to 1,000 high-paying, advanced careers in modern manufacturing.
The facility aims to be net carbon neutral for Scope 1 and 2 emissions.
It will be powered primarily by renewable energy, use recycled water, and operate as a zero-waste facility.
It will rely on railroad transportation, which can reduce logistics-related carbon emissions by up to 70 percent compared to road transport.
The plant will make significant use of advanced automation and digital technologies, including artificial intelligence, augmented reality, and robotics.
With the addition of a new recycling centre for beverage cans, Novelis will soon be able to recycle 90 billion cans globally, up from its current 74 billion used beverage cans.

Novelis has been working to develop circular economies for aluminium through state and federal public policies, as well as partnerships with customers and stakeholders to encourage U.S. consumers to recycle more often.
"Aluminum cans are an important form of packaging that, when recycled, play a vital role in our overall efforts to reduce waste," said John Murphy, Chief Financial Officer of The
In addition to the beverage can market, the facility will serve the automotive market, where aluminium is the fastest-growing material as automakers pursue sustainability goals.
source_title: Novelis to invest $2.5 billion to build low-carbon aluminium recycling and rolling plant | Times Now

facts: Novelis Inc. reported an $84 million loss in its fourth quarter due to two fires at its aluminum plant in Oswego County last year.
The company reported a loss for the three months ended March 31, compared with a profit of $294 million in the same period a year earlier.
Novelis attributed the drop in profits primarily to $630 million in pre-tax losses related to the fires.
For fiscal 2026, Novelis saw its profit fall 98% to $15 million from the previous year, primarily because of the two fires, restructuring charges, and unrealized derivative losses.
CEO Steve Fisher stated the company is focused on restarting the plant in Scriba within the next few weeks, well ahead of the previous estimate of the end of June.
A large portion of the plant has been closed since the fires.
A blaze on Sept. 16 struck the roof of the plant’s hot mill and caused extensive damage.
The second fire occurred on Nov. 20 in a location where repair work from the September fire was taking place, further damaging the mill.
Workers safely evacuated the plant during both fires with no injuries reported.
Novelis stated that commissioning activities are ramping up and expects the hot mill to be back in service in the next few weeks to support pent-up demand and normalize shipments over time.

The company believes several factors contributed to the September fire, including the composition and characteristics of the aluminum coil in the mill, as well as lubricants in the mill and surrounding structure.
The plant is a major producer of aluminum sheet for the auto industry, and its hot mill shutdown has impacted automakers.
Novelis's biggest customer, Ford Motor Co., reported taking a $2 billion hit to its earnings last year because of the disruption the fires caused to its aluminum supply.
With more than 1,120 workers, Novelis is the largest manufacturing employer in Oswego County.
source_title: Novelis reports $84M loss in Q4 after Oswego fires but signals how soon the plant could reopen | Oswego County | nny360.com

facts: Novelis Inc filed for an initial public offering (IPO) with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The proposed IPO shares are expected to be offered by Novelis' sole shareholder, AV Minerals (Netherlands), a 100% subsidiary of Hindalco Industries.
Novelis would not receive any proceeds from the sale of shares by its sole shareholder.
Novelis expects to complete the public offering after the SEC completes its review process, subject to market and other conditions.
The proposed Novelis IPO is unlikely to bring a re-rating for Hindalco Industries stock due to delays and an 65% upward revision in Novelis Bay Minette project capex and also a one-year delay.
Domestic brokerage firm Nuvama Institutional Equities maintains a 'Hold' rating on Hindalco with a target price of ₹508.
According to Nuvama, the money received may reside in Hindalco's 100% overseas subsidiary, AV Minerals (Netherlands) NV.
It is still unclear how many shares the promoter shall sell along with the timeline too, but Nuvama believes it would take at least six months if everything goes smoothly.
The usage of money received post share sale is unclear; it can either be used to grow inorganically or pay a one-time dividend and capital repatriation to Hindalco depending on tax implications.
Hindalco already has net cash of ₹3,400 crore at the end of December quarter, and future capex can be met via internal accruals.
At the end of the third quarter of this fiscal, Novelis had a net debt of $ 4.6 billion.
With its ongoing capex, net debt is likely to increase to $5 billion by FY26-end.
Hindalco’s consolidated net debt stood at ₹34,840 crore at the end of December.
Nuvama assigns 6.5 times EV/EBITDA to value Novelis on FY26 earnings while its derived market cap is $9.6 billion (₹361 per share of Hindalco).
Novelis’s closest competitor, Constellium, accounts for 40% of Novelis’s EBITDA and is listed on the New York exchange, trading at 5.3 times 2025 earnings per Bloomberg consensus.

The brokerage states that as it has already provided a higher valuation multiple to Novelis, this event may not provide any case for a re-rating, and the listing of shares may attract a Holdco discount in the future.
Nuvama added that the event can be sentimentally positive only.
source_title: Hindalco shares rally 5% as US subsidiary Novelis files for IPO, but not everyone is excited - CNBC TV18

facts: Novelis drives automotive innovation with lightweight, low carbon aluminum solutions for parts, components and processes that enhance performance, efficiency and sustainability.
Novelis manufactures components for aluminum industrial applications, tooling, batteries, consumer goods, transportation and more.
Novelis partners with customers across all industries to deliver innovative, high‑performance aluminum solutions that enable lighter, stronger, and more sustainable products.
Novelis operations are built around a circular business model, maximizing the use of recycled aluminum while continuously reducing carbon emissions across its value chain.
Novelis utilizes global scale, advanced rolling and recycling capabilities, and deep technical expertise to turn aluminum into a material that meets industry demands.
source_title: The world leader in aluminum rolling and recycling. - Novelis

facts: A South Korean company will open a factory in suburban Atlanta to make parts for electric batteries.
NVH Korea plans to invest $72 million in a plant in Locust Grove, south of Atlanta.
The plant will hire 140 workers.
The plant will manufacture sensors, connections, and protectors for electric vehicle batteries.
Operations are scheduled to begin in 2024.
Chairman and CEO Ja Kyum Koo stated that this is NVH Korea’s first venture into making components for electric batteries.
NVH Korea is based in Ulsan, South Korea.
NVH Korea will be the 10th supplier to locate in the region.
Hyundai announced in 2022 that it would build a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia, near Savannah.
The Hyundai site could grow to 8,100 employees.
The Hyundai site is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Suppliers have pledged to invest nearly $2.1 billion.
Suppliers have pledged to hire nearly 4,900 people.
This will be NVH Korea’s second plant in Georgia.
Subsidiary AFS America is located in Columbus.
Subsidiary AFS America makes floor mats and cargo trays for the Hyundai plant in Montgomery, Alabama.
Subsidiary AFS America makes floor mats and cargo trays for the sister Kia plant in West Point, Georgia.
Subsidiary AFS America makes floor mats and cargo trays for General Motors.
NVH Korea operates 19 plants worldwide across South Korea, North America, China, India, Europe, and Russia.
NVH Korea manufactures sound-dampening parts and materials, floor mats, headliners, insulators, and parts to help manage engine and cooling system temperatures.
The state of Georgia will pay to train NVH Korea’s workers.
The company could qualify for $1.2 million in state income tax credits, at $1,750 per job over five years.
To qualify for the tax credits, workers must earn at least $31,300 a year.
The company could qualify for additional incentives, including property tax breaks from Locust Grove and Henry County.
source_title: South Korean firm plans $72M Georgia plant to make battery parts, hiring 140 | AP News

facts: Henry County comprises Hampton, Locust Grove, Stockbridge, and McDonough (county seat).
As of 2023, Henry County population was 254,613, representing a 20% increase from 2010's 203,922.
Since 2019, the county has tracked over $2 billion in investments and over 8,000 new jobs, with recent wins heavily concentrated in manufacturing.

In January 2023, Andersen Corporation announced Renewal by Andersen would create 900 new jobs and invest more than $420 million in a new manufacturing and logistics facility in Locust Grove, marking its first Southeast facility.

NewCold completed a $333 million temperature-controlled warehousing and distribution warehouse in McDonough employing 170 workers, described as the largest foreign direct investment in the community's history.
In 2020, The Home Depot invested $108 million to build a 657,600-square-foot warehouse in Locust Grove and hired 600 associates.
Ken’s Foods spent $103 million and created 70 new jobs to expand its manufacturing and distribution operations in McDonough.

Mattress manufacturer Purple established a manufacturing facility in Henry County in 2020 creating 300 jobs, followed by a 2021 call center in McDonough adding 500 employees for a total investment of $46 million.
In 2020, Zinus USA announced its first North American advanced manufacturing facility in McDonough, committing to invest $108 million and create 804 jobs.
In summer 2023, Governor Brian Kemp announced EV parts supplier NVH Korea would establish a new facility in Locust Grove with a $72 million investment creating 160 new jobs.
Henry County is beginning to develop the data center industry, with announcements expected before the end of 2024.
Local manufacturing produces well-known brands including Sweet Baby Ray’s BBQ Sauce, Behr Paint, Curry & Echols Hot & Salty Pickles, Shane’s Rib Shack BBQ Sauce, and De Wafelbakkers frozen pancakes.

The Henry County Chamber of Commerce established a business resource center in 2023 to support small businesses, serving 435 businesses in its first year with SBA counseling, certification classes, and leadership programs.
The chamber reports 10% to 20% consistent business growth over the past five years, with logistics, transportation, advanced manufacturing, and healthcare as the biggest growth areas.

In fall 2023, Piedmont Henry Hospital announced a $215 million project to add a new tower expected to complete in fall 2026, adding 95 inpatient rooms to bring total census to 355 beds and three additional operating room suites for a total of 14.
Piedmont Henry Hospital is on track for 93,000 emergency department visits this year and operates as a Level III trauma center driving population growth.

Piedmont plans to open physical locations with expanded medical services in every Henry County city (Stockbridge, McDonough, Locust Grove, Hampton) within the next two years, including physicians' offices in Hampton.

Locust Grove is implementing a new Bethlehem Road interchange for I-75 access, connecting Peeksville to Francis Ford to relieve Bill Gardner Parkway congestion, and adding a lane to Highway 42, with T-SPLOST funding supporting these efforts.

There are currently 20 transportation projects underway throughout Henry County, primarily road widenings, addressing congestion from the county's quarter-million population using I-75 as a main thoroughfare.

Atlanta Motor Speedway in Hampton hosts over 500 event days annually generating approximately $250 million in regional economic impact, employs 50 full-time staff (up to 3,500 for NASCAR events), and continues hosting Henry County graduations with social distancing protocols.
source_title: McDonough | Henry County: More Than Meets the Eye - Georgia Trend Magazine

facts: Republican Georgia Governor Brian Kemp announced that NVH Korea would be opening a new facility in Locust Grove.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The company will invest $72 million in Henry County.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in the City of Ulsan.
The company was established in 1984.
NVH Korea specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts.
The company currently produces these parts in Korea, Europe, and India.
NVH Korea already operates a manufacturing facility in Georgia that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays.
The new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) in the United States.
EV customers for the new facility include Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
The new facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and sense the performance of battery cells.
The facility is expected to begin operations in the second quarter of 2024.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that Georgia's reliable infrastructure network connects global partners and its great workforce allows job creators to find success in every community.
Governor Brian Kemp noted the incredible growth of the e-mobility and battery industries in Georgia supports large suppliers like NVH Korea.
Governor Brian Kemp expressed pride in welcoming NVH Korea as a partner in making Georgia the epicenter of the industrial revolution of their time.
Ja Kyum Koo, Chairman and CEO of NVH Korea, stated that Georgia was chosen as the company’s first EV battery component manufacturing site.
Ja Kyum Koo noted that cooperation between state agencies and company staff helped guide the project.
Ja Kyum Koo described the new facility as the first step in NVH Korea’s effort to contribute to the electrification of the mobility industry.
source_title: NVH Korea To Open New Facility In Georgia, Creating 160 Jobs And Investing $72 Million | Doc and the Block

facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that automotive parts manufacturer NVH Korea will establish a new facility in Locust Grove.
The project will create over 160 new jobs.
The investment totals $72 million for Henry County.
This is the second economic development project Governor Kemp has announced during his trip to the Paris Air Show.
NVH Korea is an automotive parts manufacturer based in Ulsan, South Korea, established in 1984.
The company specializes in automotive noise, vibration, and heat-control parts.
NVH Korea currently produces its parts in Korea, Europe, and India.
The company already operates a manufacturing facility in Georgia that produces floor mats, cargo mats, and cargo trays.
The new facility will supply battery parts for electric vehicles (EVs) to manufacturers including Hyundai Mobis, Kia Georgia, and Hyundai Motor Group.
Ja Kyum Koo serves as Chairman and CEO of NVH Korea.
NVH Korea selected Georgia as its first EV battery component manufacturing site.
The new facility will be located at Gardner Logistics Park on Colvin Drive in Locust Grove.
The facility will produce EV battery system components that protect, connect, and sense the performance of battery cells.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in the second quarter of 2024.
Mayor Robert Price of Locust Grove stated the investment will increase the local manufacturing jobs base and integrate the city into Georgia's electric vehicle ecosystem.
Chair Carlotta Harrell of the Henry County Board of Commissioners noted the project will add quality manufacturing jobs for citizens and diversify the local economy.
Jacob Lee, Project Manager for the Georgia Department of Economic Development's (GDEcD) Global Commerce team, represented the agency on this competitive project.
The project received assistance from the Henry County Development Authority, Georgia EMC, and Georgia Quick Start.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson commented that the investment enriches the local industry mix and provides well-paying jobs for Georgians.
Since 2020, over $22 billion has been invested into electric vehicle production in Georgia.
This investment has resulted in over 28 thousand jobs created across the state.
GDEcD data from July 1 through December 31, 2022 shows the automotive industry was the second largest source for new jobs and investments during that period.
Georgia is positioning itself as a leader in developing and harnessing emerging technologies for the evolving automotive and mobility industry.
source_title: Georgia Chron - Georgia: Gov. Kemp: EV Parts Supplier NVH Korea to Create Over 160 Jobs, Invest M in Henry County - 10231436

facts: NVH Korea, a Korean autoparts manufacturer, purchased a distribution center in Phenix City, Alabama.
The facility is located at 510 Brickyard Road, just over the border from Columbus, Georgia.
The property sits on approximately 22 acres and contains a 150,420-square-foot warehouse and distribution center.
NVH Korea paid nearly $6.7 million for the building.
The building was originally constructed in 1991 and underwent renovation in 2023.
The property was put up for sale during a period of rising interest rates and buyer caution.
It took 18 months, including a four-month negotiation period, for the facility to find a new owner after being listed.
Jack Hayes, CEO for brokerage NAI G2 Commercial, described the project as significant in its size and economic impact to the east Alabama and west Georgia region.

Missy Kendrick, chief executive of the business booster group Choose Columbus, stated that the acquisition was 'a perfect example of an impactful acquisition' that 'allowed a neighboring county's industry to expand without leaving the market,' filled a long vacant facility, and provided jobs and capital investment in a growing area that benefits the entire region.
The transaction was judged by real estate professionals in the area and garnered a CoStar Impact Award.
Brokers Jack Hayes (CEO) and David Johnson (chief of brokerage operations) handled the listing.
source_title: News | A transaction worth the wait: Korean autoparts maker buys distribution center

facts: Governor Brian Kemp and First Lady Marty Kemp are leading a delegation of state officials on an international mission to the Republic of Korea to reinforce strategic economic development partnerships.
Georgia’s connections to Korea go back decades, with this trip aiming to strengthen relationships that have a generational impact on the No. 1 state for business.
The state delegation includes President Pro Tem John Kennedy, Representative Soo Hong, and Representative Lehman Franklin.
The delegation also features representatives from the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD), Georgia Environmental Protection Division, and Georgia Ports Authority.

Governor Kemp and the delegation will conduct meetings with existing industries operating or under construction in Georgia, specifically Hyundai Motor Group, Kia, CJ Foodville, LG Group, SK Group, and Hanwha Qcells.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that meeting with industry leadership at their headquarters provides a unique opportunity to learn about industry perspectives and position Georgia at the forefront of new opportunities.
The State of Georgia has maintained continuous representation in Korea since 1985.
Korea’s Consular Office, which holds jurisdiction across the Southeast, Puerto Rico, and U.S. Virgin Islands, has been located in Georgia since 1976.
Korea has been one of the top sources of Foreign Direct Investment and international job creation in Georgia for the last three years.
In fiscal year 2023 alone, Korean companies announced more than $10 billion in investments and the creation of 12,605 jobs across the state.
Total trade between the State of Georgia and Korea totaled over $15.8 billion in calendar year 2023, accounting for nearly 10 percent of the state’s total trade.
source_title: Governor Kemp reinforces economic partnerships in Korea

facts: Provides metal stamping capabilities ranging from prototyping initial designs to full mass production.
Produces extremely precise and high-quality products for customers.
Uses advanced engineering and design experience to develop manufacturing techniques in automation by way of robotic transfer.
Employs a team of highly skilled tool and die shop makers that pair traditional methodologies with advanced manufacturing capabilities.
source_title: American-Made Precision Metal Stamping Manufacturer | Oneda

facts: Oneda Corporation (USA) is a family-owned, Georgia-based contract manufacturer.
The company specializes in precision metal stamping, assembly, and tool and die designs.
It utilizes innovative robotic transfer and progressive metal stamping capabilities.
Its press sizes range from 25 to 500 tons.
source_title: Oneda Corporation / Metal Stampings | The Org

facts: Oneda Corporation is a contract manufacturer located in Columbus, Georgia (southeast).
The company utilizes the latest technologies in precision metal stamping, assembly, and tool and die designs.
It specializes in innovative robotic transfer and progressive tooling capabilities with press sizes ranging from 25 to 500 tons.

Oneda Corporation is a subsidiary of Oneda Electric Corporation in Japan serving the automotive, medical and consumer electronics industries with competitive performance and cost effective metal stampings.
The facility address is 4000 Oneda Dr., Columbus, GA 31907, United States.

entity_name: Car Companies Building Batteries in America
canonical_name: Car Companies Building Batteries in America
facts: President Biden signed the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), which includes new credits for purchases of clean energy vehicles and additional credits to automakers for producing batteries in America.
BMW broke ground on a new battery assembly plant in Woodruff, South Carolina named 'Plant Woodruff' in June of this year.
Plant Woodruff will produce next-generation batteries for use in the German brand's electric vehicles built nearby in Spartanburg, South Carolina.
More than 300 jobs will be added with this new plant.

Construction of the facility is part of a $1.7 billion investment BMW announced last year, with $700 million going towards Plant Woodruff and the additional $1 billion earmarked to prepare the company's Spartanburg facility for the production of fully-electric BMWs.
Ford is investing $3.5 billion to build a lithium-iron-phosphate (LFP) battery plant in Marshall, Michigan called BlueOval Battery Park Michigan.
The new facility will employ 2,500 people when battery production starts in 2026.
Ford has also partnered with SK On to build battery production facilities in Tennessee and Kentucky under the name BlueOval SK.
Two plants are under construction in Glendale, Kentucky, which will ultimately produce more than 80 gigawatt hours of lithium-ion batteries, with production expected to start in 2025.
GM has built or is planning at least four battery production facilities in America.
Three battery-production facilities are the result of a joint venture between GM and LG Energy called Ultium Cells.
The first is a 2.8-million-square-foot factory in Warren, OH, that is already up and running, producing batteries for the Hummer EV and Cadillac Lyriq.
A similar plant will be built in Spring Hill, Tennessee, where the Lyriq is made.
A third plant will be located in Lansing, Michigan.

GM has also formed a new partnership with Samsung SDI to build a fourth facility – a $3 billion plant will be built in St. Joseph County, Indiana, creating 1,700 new jobs when it begins production in 2026.
Honda has also formed a joint venture with LG Energy Solutions to produce EV batteries at a new plant near Jeffersonville, Ohio.
Groundbreaking at the 2 million square foot facility took place earlier this year after the two companies committed to investing up to $4.4 billion towards this venture.
The new battery factory is slated to be complete by the end of 2024 and is expected to create 2,200 jobs.
Honda anticipates the new facility will be able to deliver up to 40 GWh of batteries annually with the start of mass production by the end of 2025.
The lithium-ion batteries built in this facility will be used exclusively by Honda in new electric vehicles built in North America.
The Hyundai Motor Group, including the Hyundai, Genesis and Kia brands, has formed a joint venture to build EV batteries with South Korean battery maker SK On.
With a total investment of $5 billion, the two companies are building a new production facility in Bartow County, Georgia.
This new plant is expected to go online by the second-half of 2025.
The plant will eventually produce 35 GWh of batteries annually, enough to power 300,000 EVs.
The new plant will be conveniently located near the Hyundai Group U.S. production facilities in Alabama and Georgia and will support the South Korean brand's plans for EV production in America.
California-based Lucid is presently sourcing its batteries from Panasonic with the lithium-ion cells produced in Japan.
Panasonic has announced plans to build a new facility in De Soto, Kansas expected to be one of the largest in the world, supplying batteries to Lucid and other car brands.
Mercedes-Benz builds the all-new EQE SUV and EQS SUV at its production facility in Tuscaloosa, Alabama.
To support the battery requirements of these new electric models, Mercedes-Benz has built a new battery production facility in nearby Bibb County, Alabama.

Ola Källenius, Chairman of the Board of Management of Mercedes-Benz Group AG, stated that opening their new battery plant in Alabama is a major milestone on their way to going all-electric and emphasized the importance of local cell sourcing and recycling strategy in the US market.
Nissan built a battery production facility next to its current vehicle assembly plant in Smyrna, Tennessee shortly after launching the Leaf in 2010.
With plans to greatly expand its EV offering with a goal of 19 new EVs by 2030, Nissan is looking to build a second battery plant, likely near its Canton, Mississippi facility.
This facility is expected to build new Nissan and Infiniti EVs starting in 2025.
Stellantis has formed a joint venture with Samsung SDI to build an electric vehicle battery manufacturing facility in Kokomo, Indiana.
With a $2.5 billion investment, the new plant will bring 1,400 new jobs to the Kokomo area when it comes online in 2025.
Initial production is expected to deliver 23 GWh of batteries annually, with a plan to increase the output to 33 GWh as demand for Stellantis electric vehicles increases.
Tesla partnered with Panasonic around 20 years ago to supply Tesla automobiles with lithium-ion batteries.
In 2014 Tesla and Panasonic began construction of the Gigafactory in Nevada, and now that factory provides the high-voltage battery packs for use in Tesla automobiles.
Panasonic recently announced plans to invest around $10 billion to establish two new battery production facilities in the U.S., one of which will be a $4 billion facility in Kansas.
Earlier this year, Toyota announced an additional $2.1 billion investment in its new battery production facility located in Liberty, North Carolina, bringing the total investment to $5.9 billion.
This new battery facility is still under construction, with production expected to begin in 2025.
The plant will have six battery production lines – four for hybrids and two for battery-electric vehicles.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America will release the first episode of The Panasonic Sound Experience, a new four-episode podcast that reveals the emerging innovations of immersive sound design in vehicles, and what it takes to build the perfect audio system.

Andrew Poliak, CTO of Panasonic Automotive Systems, stated that Panasonic has a long history of developing cutting-edge technology to create a premium in-car audio experience that makes driving both safer and more enjoyable for consumers.
Panasonic provides fully-integrated solutions revolutionizing the driving experience from navigation and head-up display, to infotainment systems and e-cockpit design.
Panasonic partners with ELS, Fender and Klipsch to deliver next-gen audio that delivers the best in-vehicle experience possible.
The podcast is produced by Muddhouse Media, a start-up original content podcast network that brings creative minds, intellectual experts and gifted storytellers together.

The first episode of The Panasonic Sound Experience focuses on capturing authenticity, artistry and emotion in automotive sound design and features interviews with Panasonic Automotive Executive Vice President Allen Kudla, Grammy Award winning producer and sound engineer Elliot Scheiner and recording producer Matthew Scheiner.

Allen Kudla stated the goal is to share behind-the-scenes innovation in a podcast series to highlight the technology enabling Panasonic to deliver an in-vehicle sound that's been compared to what you'd hear at concerts by today's greatest artists.
Future episodes of the podcast will cover how engineers create an intuitive audio environment through noise cancellation, voice enhancement, voice scrubbing, and voice recognition.

Future episodes will discuss how vehicles have become an essential second space for consumers during the pandemic and why premium audio can elevate the dimensional sound experience with Mark Casavant, Vice President of Sales and Business Development Klipsch Group, and Brett Crockett, Vice President R&D with Dolby Atmos.
A future holiday master class will chart the pursuit for perfect sound across multiple genres of music from rock to classical with a special iconic audio guest.

Panasonic Automotive Systems Company of America is a division company of Panasonic Corporation of North America and is a leading global supplier of automotive infotainment and connectivity system solutions.
Panasonic Automotive is headquartered in Peachtree City, Georgia, with sales, marketing and engineering operations in Farmington Hills, Michigan.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems of America is based in Peachtree City, GA.
At CES 2021, Panasonic Automotive introduced its new Augmented Reality (AR) HUD.
The AR HUD utilizes advances in optics, volume optimization, and imaging technology combined with AI from the SPYDR cockpit domain controller.
The system renders near-field and far-field content for vehicle information like speed, object and pedestrian detection, and mapping/route guidance.
Scott Kirchner serves as president of Panasonic Automotive and executive director of Panasonic Smart Mobility.
Panasonic's AR HUD solutions cover more of the roadway than traditional HUDs by displaying traditional cluster content in the near field and 3D overlays in the far field.
The AR HUD could provide an added level of comfort and assurance for AV passengers in a future with more self-driving vehicles.
The system projects 3D, AI-driven key information into the driver's line of sight to help reduce driver distraction and potentially increase safety.

Panasonic's AR HUD development utilizes a PRIZM process addressing precise placement, reflection via AI smart optical graphic road overlays, intuitive focus discrimination, zonal UX optimized field-of-view organization, and mission control dynamic imaging.
Eye tracking technology projects information at the driver's level of sight based on eye position to eliminate mismatch when the driver moves their head.
Advanced optics provide an expanded field-of-view beyond 10 by 4 degrees for a virtual image distance of 10m or greater.
The advanced optics detect pedestrians and objects through enhanced low light and nighttime view, with tilted virtual image planes adjusting visibility.
An embedded camera system allows discrete monitoring for the driver's eye location.

AI-driven AR navigation technology detects and provides multi-color 3D navigation graphics that adjust with the moving vehicle's surroundings, displaying lane markers and GPS arrows where turns will occur.
A proprietary camera image stability algorithm enables AR icons to lock onto the driving environment regardless of road bumpiness.
Driving environment updates for ADAS, AI, and AR environment information occur in real-time in less than 300 milliseconds.
The system includes a 3D imaging radar providing sensor-captured full 180-degree forward vision up to 90 meters and across approximately three traffic lanes.
The AR HUD features efficient compact packaging designed to fit any vehicle configuration.
The display offers 4K resolution using advanced laser and holography technology for static near-field cluster information and far-field image plane for AR graphic overlay.
Panasonic collaborates with Envisics for striking, dual plane, high-resolution laser holography via a patent-protected dynamic holographic platform.
Panasonic collaborates with Phiar for 3D localization technology and AI navigation and situation awareness analytics via a patent-protected spatial-AI, AR navigation platform.
Panasonic Automotive announced two variants of in-vehicle wireless charging technology: moving coil and static coil.
The wireless charging technology aims to provide efficient in-vehicle charging that meets or exceeds most current in-vehicle charging systems.
The moving coil variant uses exclusive detection technology with patent-pending tracking to target a device and move the coil into an optimum position for alignment.
The moving coil variant has better coupling capability compared to other wireless charging systems.
The moving coil variant delivers 15W of charging power, providing one of the fastest charging times in the industry that rivals wired/tethered systems.
Both static and moving coil variants support NFC BT/Wi-Fi connectivity, allowing calls and app use to continue running while the device is charging.
Both wireless charging variants are set to follow the Qi 1.3 charging standard.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems (headquarters: Yokohama, Kanagawa, Japan; President: Masamasa Nagatoyo) and VicOne (headquarters: Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan; CEO: Cheng Yi-li) announced expanded support for cybersecurity innovation in container environments for next-generation in-vehicle cockpit systems and completed technical validation.
In October 2024, Panasonic Automotive Systems released VERZEUSE® for Virtualization Extensions Type-3 attack detection and protection technology.
The innovation integrates VicOne's automotive cybersecurity software xCarbon to further expand its functionality.

The expanded VERZEUSE® for Virtualization Extensions Type-3 can effectively monitor communications between containers on hardware development boards running Linux-based in-vehicle system software AGL (Automotive Grade Linux).
The technology demonstrates application potential for future in-vehicle cockpit systems.
Panasonic Automotive Systems will exhibit this technology innovation at the 31st ITS World Congress in Atlanta, USA, from August 24 to 28, 2025.

VERZEUSE® primarily monitors communications between containers: (1) software areas like infotainment systems connected to external networks that are vulnerable to attacks in the virtual vehicle control unit; and (2) software areas like dashboard containers in the virtual vehicle control unit that execute basic functions like vehicle control and software updates.

The monitoring function is installed in an isolated container, allowing it to monitor communications between the above containers from a secure area, detect and block suspicious communications, protect core vehicle functions from cyberattacks, and improve overall vehicle security and cybersecurity levels.
The system meets the cybersecurity requirements of next-generation cockpit systems in virtualized environments.
VicOne's xCarbon is vehicle security software for intrusion detection and defense against cyberattacks.

By linking xCarbon to the VERZEUSE® expansion interface, it monitors inter-container communications to protect next-gen cockpit systems from attackers exploiting vulnerabilities or communication errors.
The system can also identify high-risk communications and route them to xCarbon analysis for more efficient security monitoring.
With rapid development of Software Defined Vehicle (SDV) technology and increasing connected vehicles, cybersecurity risks like cyberattacks on vehicles have risen.
The UN issued automotive cybersecurity regulation UN-R155 in January 2021.
In Japan, the UN-R155 regulation was implemented on some vehicles starting July 2022 and will be expanded to all vehicles starting May 2026.
Under the regulation, vehicles lacking sufficient cybersecurity measures will not meet safety standards and will be banned from sale.
Security has become an increasingly important focus for automakers and related companies.
Panasonic Automotive Systems and VicOne will continue to collaborate to advance cybersecurity for next-generation in-vehicle cockpit systems.
VicOne is a subsidiary of Trend Micro (headquarters: Shinjuku, Tokyo, Japan; CEO: Chen Yihua).
VERZEUSE is a trademark registered by Panasonic Automotive Systems or its affiliates in the US.
AGL is an in-vehicle operating system for next-generation cockpit systems.

The technology references 'ST-CSP-18: Definition Document of In-Vehicle Security Function Requirements Using Software Isolation Technology Ver.1.01' by JASPAR (Japan Automotive Software Platform and Architecture Alliance), 2023.
SDV stands for Software Defined Vehicle.
Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. (PAS) was established on April 1, 2022, as an operating company responsible for in-vehicle system business under the Panasonic Group's operating company system.
As of December 2, 2024, PAS is managed by a fund owned 80% by Apollo Global Management, Inc. subsidiary and 20% by Panasonic Holdings Co., Ltd.
PAS headquarters are in Japan with subsidiaries in eight countries globally.
As a Tier 1 automotive component supplier, PAS provides advanced proprietary technology like infotainment systems to Japanese and overseas automakers.
PAS aims to realize the corporate vision 'Joy in Motion' by meeting global customer expectations with technology close to people's needs.

facts: Panasonic Automotive Systems Co., Ltd. (PAS) has been appointed to the Governing Body of SOAFEE (Scalable Open Architecture for Embedded Edge).
The appointment marks a major recognition of PAS's leadership in driving the evolution of Software-Defined Vehicle (SDV) technologies.
SOAFEE is a global initiative uniting automotive and software industry leaders to accelerate the realization of AI-powered, cloud-native SDVs.
PAS joined the SOAFEE Technical Steering Committee in 2022 before becoming the ninth company on the Governing Body.
PAS has been instrumental in developing VirtIO-based, hardware-agnostic solutions such as Unified HMI and Virtual SkipGen.
These innovations have been adopted as official SOAFEE blueprints.
The solutions support the decoupled development of automotive software from hardware to enable faster and more flexible advancements in next-gen cockpit systems.
PAS assumed leadership of the newly created SOAFEE Asia-Pacific (APAC) Regional Hub in November 2024, with full operations beginning May 2025.
The APAC hub is designed to energize SDV collaboration in a region comprising a significant share of SOAFEE’s 150+ global member companies.

Masashige Mizuyama, Executive Vice President and CTO of Panasonic Automotive Systems, stated that PAS fully supports SOAFEE’s vision of enabling seamless, cloud-native environments for fast and continuous delivery of automotive software.
Mizuyama added that device virtualization will be a key enabler of this transition.

As a Governing Body member, PAS will contribute to global standardization efforts, collaborate with key industry organizations, and lead initiatives to overcome challenges in cloud-native and SDV development.
This role reinforces PAS's strategic position in shaping the next generation of connected, intelligent vehicles.
source_title: Panasonic Automotive Joins SOAFEE Governing Body To Lead SDV Advancements - Mobility Outlook

facts: Griffin had a population of 20,472 with a metropolitan population of approximately 27,000 in 1957.
The directory lists a total population of 29,666 for the area.
Two-thirds of non-agricultural workers were employed in textile mills, making it primarily a textile city.
Food processing was the second largest employment category, including Pomona Products Company, described as the largest pimiento pepper canning operation in the world.
The first textile mill opened in Griffin in 1883.
In 1900, there were 1,000 industrial wage earners in Griffin with annual wages less than a quarter of a million dollars.
By 1957, approximately 8,000 workers were on industrial payrolls with more than $17,000,000 as an annual payroll.
The Georgia Experiment Station on the outskirts of town developed new plants including Empire Cotton, Truehart Perfection Pimientos, Hunt muscadines, and Georgia 101 hybrid corn.
The station improved food processing which created new industries for Georgia.
City limits cover 6.7 square miles while Spalding County comprises 128,640 acres (201 square miles).
Approximately 50 manufacturing plants are located in the outlying areas of Griffin.
Dundee Mills, manufacturer of Dundee Towels, is the second largest towel producer in the world.
The consolidated schools of Griffin and Spalding County offer vocational training alongside required and elective courses.
A school expansion program exceeding $1,000,000 was completed with a second $1,250,000 building program underway.
The school system includes 23 grammar schools, one junior high school, and two senior high schools serving approximately 7,543 students under 250 teachers.
Georgia Tech, Emory University, Agnes Scott, Wesleyan College, and Mercer University are within a 50-mile radius of Griffin.
Electric power is supplied to the area by the Georgia Power Company and distributed in the Metropolitan area by a city-owned power system.
The power distribution system includes three transmission lines with 110,000 volts and two with 44,000 volts.
The document states that power supply is no problem for any industrial expansion plant.
The Griffin water supply comes from the Flint River, which originates near East Point, Georgia, forty miles north of Griffin.
The Flint River is capable of supplying 15 million gallons of water per day, with the present filtered system providing 7,000,000 gallons per day.
Griffin is located approximately 35 miles south of the Atlanta Municipal Air Field and is joined to it by a four-lane super highway.
Atlanta serves as a hub for twelve major air routes across the nation with 214 mail, passenger, and cargo planes scheduled daily.

Five major motor express lines handle interstate and intrastate freight shipments from Griffin, including Akers Motor Lines, Georgia Highway Express, Great Southern Trucking Company, Perkins Freight Lines, and Johnson Transfer Company.
The city has had a city management form of municipal government since 1918, composed of a city manager appointed by three commissioners.
Both city and county governments work closely together and are active in projects to secure new industry.
Housing in the vicinity is considered adequate for current needs with approximately 200 low-rent public housing units available.
source_title: Griffin (Spalding County, GA) city directory 1957 including Cherokee Heights, East Griffin, Highland Mills and Experiment, GA - Digital Library of Georgia

facts: A proposed natural gas pipeline will cut through hundreds of private properties in Georgia and Mississippi.
The project is driven by Georgia Power's need for more natural gas, largely to fuel new data centers.
Perry Wells oversees 200 acres of forested land in Bolingbroke, Monroe County, Ga., including softwood pines and 100-year-old hardwood trees.
Two previously buried gas pipelines on the property have caused decades of erosion, trenches, and gullies.
The proposed pipeline may cut a mile through 10 acres of Wells' land and uproot additional soil-stabilizing trees.
The project is South System Expansion 4 (SSE4), a $3 billion joint venture between Southern Natural Gas (SNG) and Elba Express Company (EEC).
Both SNG and EEC are operated by Kinder Morgan, which transports nearly half of the nation's natural gas.
Southern Company owns one half of SNG, while Kinder Morgan subsidiaries own the other half.

More than 90% of the 280-mile pipeline route from Rose Hill, Miss. to Augusta, Ga. will run parallel and within feet of SNG’s existing 6,900-mile South Main Line ending at Savannah's Elba Island LNG terminal.
The pipeline addresses SNG's demand for an additional 1.3 billion cubic feet of natural gas per day.
Much of the new gas supply will go to Georgia Power, which was approved last Friday to build nearly 10 more gigawatts of energy capacity.
About a third of the 1,800 land tracts have already been acquired by Kinder Morgan.
Kinder Morgan Vice President of Public Affairs Allen Fore stated the company will restore most land after construction but acknowledged forested areas will face an altered future.
The pipeline pipe will mostly sit 3 feet underground, requiring the removal of long-rooted trees whose roots extend deeper than the burial depth.
Potential remediation for cleared timber may include compensation based on timber value.
Wells estimates roughly two to three thousand trees will need to be cleared on his property.
Landowners received notices starting last December containing maps and a 32-page FAQ titled "What do I need to know?".
Public information meetings were held from January 27 to March 5 in Macon, Sandersville, Hamilton, Griffin, and other locations.
A second notice arrived in July informing landowners the project was proceeding.
The project will disturb approximately 4,646 acres for the pipeline and above-ground facilities.

About a fourth of the disturbed acreage will be permanently maintained by gas companies for operation, including roughly a dozen newly added or modified compression and meter stations, such as one in Rincon.
The pipeline, ranging 20 to 42 inches wide, will sit within the existing South Main Line's right-of-way, which includes 25 feet on either side of the pipe.
Kinder Morgan officials guarantee that every landowner will receive a negotiation and discussion, framing it as an individual real estate transaction.
Some easements remain unsecured, with follow-up outreach delayed due to route planning coordination with multiple stakeholders.
All natural gas pipeline projects must be submitted to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC).
FERC has approved 99% of pipeline applications since 1999, according to the National Resources Defense Council.
The project defers to FERC's land-taking rules, allowing companies to acquire private property via eminent domain at fair market value under the Natural Gas Act of 1938.
Landowners are not required to enter into easement agreements, and acquisition does not guarantee mutual agreement.

Notification criteria include being directly affected, abutting a right-of-way or facility, having a residence within 50 feet of construction, or being within one-half mile of above-ground facilities.
Kinder Morgan plans to begin construction in 2027, pending submission of an Environmental Impact Assessment in January for FERC approval.
Allen Fore stated there are no significant impediments to construction on the current route, citing past surveys that found the right-of-way suitable and void of environmental concerns.

The Southern Environmental Law Center (SELC) intervened on behalf of Alabama Rivers Alliance, Black Belt Women Rising, Energy Alabama, Georgia Interfaith Power and Light, Southern Alliance for Clean Energy, and a local landowner.
Reverend Ellis Godbee of Historic Spirit Creek Baptist Church in Augusta received FERC notices but is unsure where the pipeline will go near his congregation.
Project details can be found via FERC Docket No. CP25-517-000.
source_title: The pipeline transporting Georgia Power's energy expansion springs concerns

facts: PIOLAX, INC. reported FY2008 sales of 40,677 million JPY, representing a 16.7% decrease from FY2007.
PIOLAX, INC. reported FY2008 operating income of 838 million JPY, a 79.4% decrease from FY2007.
PIOLAX, INC. reported FY2008 ordinary income of 1,210 million JPY, a 75.2% decrease from FY2007.
PIOLAX, INC. reported FY2008 current net income of 500 million JPY, an 84.8% decrease from FY2007.
The company acquired a new order from Ford Motor Company for pickup truck components.
PIOLAX is sending engineers to Ford R&D departments to win more orders by directly engaging in development activities.
The company will begin supplying fasteners to clip both exterior and interior parts to bodies of new pickup trucks planned by Ford for release in 2010.
Fastener production for Ford will begin in the latter half of 2009 at the company's Thai plant.
PIOLAX spent 515 million JPY on overall R&D in FY2008, with 396 million JPY allocated to automotive products.
R&D activities are conducted by the design department, development groups in each SBU, and the development division of subsidiary Pilolax Medical Devices, Inc.
The company conducts joint development with universities and external research institutes.
PIOLAX developed compressed coil spring materials used in engines and gears, commercializing products that are more compact and lighter in weight.
The company strengthened development activities on complex springs to enable easier installation.
PIOLAX worked on developing surface coatings for spring materials that do not contain hexavalent chromium.
The company completed development of hexavalent chromium free surface treatment, complying with EU directives on end-of-life vehicles and restriction of hazardous substances in electrical equipment.
PIOLAX is working to make products meet Japan Automobile Manufacturers Association restrictions on environmentally hazardous substances including hexavalent chromium, vinyl chloride, and VOC.
The company started development activities on products that comply with the new REACH Regulation in Europe.

PIOLAX is developing versatile fasteners for thin and thick metal plates, fasteners requiring less installation force, fasteners with powerful fastening capabilities, and fasteners that are easier to disassemble during vehicle scrapping.
The company is considering migrating to materials that emit less odor in vehicle interiors while developing environmentally friendly products that enhance interior comfort.
PIOLAX is currently developing high-strength, user-friendly net hooks used in car interior parts.
The company developed side locks for glove boxes released in January 2003, which are now being equipped on an increasing number of vehicle models to improve performance and workability.
PIOLAX worked on developing new dampers.
The company is currently developing hinge parts to open/close the console box lid and floor board in the rear luggage compartment.
In fuel systems, PIOLAX enhanced the performance and reduced manufacturing costs of rollover valves and inlet check valves for plastic tanks.

PIOLAX licensed-in patent use for automotive fuel delivery components and plastic fuel tank components from Norma Germany GmbH under contracts with terms spanning from 1995 through 2006, renewing annually.
The company licensed-out manufacturing technology for metallic and plastic fasteners to Piolax of Taiwan Inc. from October 1987 through October 1992, renewable every 10 years.

PIOLAX licensed-out manufacturing technology for pressure springs, torsion springs, and valve springs to Taiwan Houmu Industrial Co., Ltd. from November 1978 through October 1983, renewable every 5 years.

The company licensed-out technology, engineering, and marketing services for precision plastic slips, precision springs, and related assembly products to Piolax Corp.(USA) for an indefinite period starting April 1, 1993.
PIOLAX licensed-out similar technology, engineering, and marketing services to Piolax Ltd. (UK) for an indefinite period starting August 8, 1995.
The company licensed-out related services to Piolax Co., Ltd. (Korea) from June 20, 1995 through June 19, 2006, renewable each year.
PIOLAX licensed-out similar services to Piolax (Thailand) Ltd. for an indefinite period starting August 10, 2000.
The company licensed-out related services to Dongguan Piolax Co., Ltd. from July 1, 2004 through June 30, 2014, renewable each year.
PIOLAX spent 2,876 million JPY on capital investment for automotive products in FY2008.
The company spent a total of 1,805 million yen in Japan and 1,129 million yen outside Japan on capital investments.
Piolax Corp. established a Head Office/Plant in Georgia, USA, focused on production facilities and dies.
The expected investment for the Georgia facility is 295 thousand yen.
Construction of the Georgia facility started in January 2009 and is expected to complete in December 2010.

Other new automobile-related facilities under development include locations in Yokohama City, Tochigi Prefecture, Fuji City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Inchon, Rayong, and Dongguan, all starting construction in April or January 2009 with completion expected in March or December 2010.
