facts: Pirelli Tire North America, Inc. is the North American operation of Pirelli & C. S.p.A., an Italian multinational company and one of the world's leading tire manufacturers.
The company focuses on the high-value tire market, producing innovative tires for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles.
Pirelli Tire North America manages sales, marketing, distribution, and manufacturing operations tailored to the US and Canadian markets.
The Rome, GA headquarters serves as the primary administrative, R&D, and manufacturing hub for Pirelli in North America.
The Rome, GA campus includes a state-of-the-art tire manufacturing plant utilizing Pirelli’s MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) technology.
The Rome, GA campus also houses an advanced R&D center focused on tire innovation for the local market and modern office facilities.
The Rome, GA headquarters is located at 100 Pirelli Drive, Rome, Georgia, USA.
The Rome, GA facility enables localized production, responsive R&D, and strengthened relationships with North American automotive manufacturers and consumers.
Pirelli maintains strong Original Equipment (OE) partnerships with prestigious automotive manufacturers.

The North American operations support Pirelli's worldwide strategy by focusing on the high-value tire segment, contributing to global R&D efforts with insights from the local market, managing OE partnerships with major North American automakers, and adapting global brand strategies for regional consumers.
As of April 2025, Pirelli Tire North America's leadership includes a CEO who transitioned in early 2023 and a new Chief Technical Officer who joined in early 2024.
In February 2024, Pirelli was certified as a 'Top Employer' for 2024, with its North American operations in the USA receiving this recognition for excellent HR policies and people practices.
In October 2023, Pirelli introduced its new Scorpion MS all-season tire, designed specifically for the North American market to meet the demands of modern SUVs.
In February 2024, Pirelli Tire North America announced a price increase of up to 7% on its passenger and light truck replacement tires sold in the U.S., effective April 1, 2024.
The company utilizes standardized employee email formats, typically firstname.lastname@pirelli.com.
source_title: Where is Pirelli Tire North America Located? HQ, Global Offices & Company Insights

facts: Pirelli Tire North America LLC operates a manufacturing facility located at 100 Pirelli Dr, Rome, GA 30161.
The company employs approximately 20+ people.
Pirelli Tire North America LLC is open Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM, and closed on Saturdays and Sundays.
The facility holds a 3.9-star customer rating based on 31 reviews.

A customer review indicates that Pirelli OEM tires were originally installed on a Tesla Model 3 Performance (M3P), with one reviewer reporting two bubbles in the tires after 4,735 miles and stating they will switch to Michelin or Continental tires.
Employee feedback highlights positive workplace culture, with staff described as friendly, respectful, and awesome.
source_title: Pirelli Tire North America LLC in Rome, GA 30161  - 706-368...

facts: Pirelli Tire North America Inc. is preparing to suspend operations in Rome, Georgia, to protect worker health amid the spread of the coronavirus.
The company plans to scale back production starting March 22.
Manufacturing will be suspended for one week starting March 29.
The factory will be cleaned and sanitized while closed.
Slowing the rate of production will reduce the number of employees present to ensure safety while not interrupting supply to clients.
Pirelli noted that the reduced output corresponds to lower demand.
The tire maker is constantly monitoring the situation and is ready to adopt additional measures if necessary.
Pirelli employs 300 workers at the Rome car tire plant.
The Rome plant is 18 years old and has a listed capacity of 400,000 units per year.
The company did not comment on plans for its pair of plants in operation in Silao, Mexico.
source_title: Pirelli to suspend operations at tire plant in Georgia - Rubber News

facts: Pirelli is strengthening its position in the North American market by committing to additional production capabilities with a new tire plant scheduled to be on line by the first half of 2012.
The new greenfield plant is located in Silao, Mexico, about 350 miles south of the Texas border in the Mexican state of Guanajuato.
The Silao facility has a price tag of roughly $210 million.
The Silao plant will have an initial daily production capacity of 10,500 passenger and light truck tires.
By 2015, Pirelli estimates the Silao plant will be able to produce 14,000 tires per day, or a maximum of 5 million tires annually.
Additional land has been purchased at the Silao site to add capacity down the line.
Mauro Pessi serves as President & CEO of Pirelli Tire North America.

Pessi defined Silao as a location with good infrastructure, a qualified and capable workforce offering competitive labor rates compared to other Latin American countries, and solid transportation corridors with tariff-free status under NAFTA.
About 80% of the tires manufactured at the new Silao facility will go to the U.S. and Canada.
Tom Gravalos is vice president of marketing, motorsports and original equipment for Pirelli Tire North America.
The Silao plant will employ conventional tire production technology rather than the Modular Integrated Robotic System (MIRS) featured in Pirelli’s Rome, Ga., facility.
MIRS is designed for shorter runs of high performance tires.
The 120,000-square-meter Mexican facility is part of Pirelli SpA’s five-year industrial plan designed to support projected growth for high performance tires in North America.
The five-year industrial plan calls for added investments in Latin America and the Asia-Pacific region to achieve new sales targets producing an annual average of 8% growth through 2015.
The Mexico facility will be Pirelli’s eighth tire manufacturing facility in Latin America.
Pessi stated that along with the Rome, Ga., facility, the Silao plant sends a strong message to customers that Pirelli is committed and focused.
Pirelli will invest $12 million in the Rome, Ga. plant to add a new compound mixing facility.
The Rome, Ga. investment will assist in supporting market gains and a 30% sales increase made over the past three years in the U.S. and Canada.
Pirelli is budgeting a total of $2.65 billion globally for modernization of its manufacturing facilities over the next five years.
This budgeted modernization cost is 27% higher than what Pirelli spent between 2006 and 2010.
Pirelli agreed to take over the assets of Sibur Russian Tyres after signing a memorandum of understanding with Russian Technologies and oil major Sibur Holding in Moscow on Nov. 26.
The Russia deal is expected to close by June 2011.
Pirelli launched the 38th edition of its high-profile calendar in Moscow, titled “Mythology,” featuring creative direction by Karl Lagerfeld.
source_title: Pirelli: On 5th Avenue and on the Move in North America - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Pirelli Tire was founded in the 1800s in Milan by Italian Giovanni Battista Pirelli to produce elastic rubber items.
The company launched its first tires for velocipedes in 1894.
Pirelli operates 18 production facilities across 12 countries globally.
The company provides premium, high-performance tires for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles.
Pirelli boasts 5.3 billion euros in annual revenue.
Maureen Kline serves as Pirelli’s Vice President of Public Affairs and Sustainability North America.
Decarbonization in the automobile industry is driving demand for sustainable tire products within the automotive supply chain.
Pirelli aims to power all electricity used at its operations with renewable sources by 2025.
The company's factory in Rome, Georgia was the first tire factory anywhere in the world to be Forest Stewardship Council® - certified for sustainable natural rubber and rayon.
The Rome, Georgia facility is a highly automated small factory that produces very high-end tires.
FSC certification requires a complex chain of custody where rubber, mostly sourced from Southeast Asia, is certified at the farm and tracked through the supply chain to production facilities.
Pirelli is exploring innovative materials to increase product sustainability, including rice husk silica as a substitute for traditional silica.
The company is investigating lignin sourced from pulp and paper mills to use as a biopolymer antioxidant instead of fossil-derived products.
In 2021, Pirelli produced a concept tire containing 94% renewable and recycled materials, including silica from rice husks, recycled carbon black, and bio-resins.
Pirelli targets having 70% of its tires in European efficiency categories A and B by 2025 to indicate low rolling resistance.
The company is focusing heavily on developing specialized tires for the electric vehicle market to improve vehicle range through low rolling resistance.
More than half of Pirelli’s global electricity currently comes from renewable sources.
At its Rome, Georgia manufacturing site, Pirelli has partnered with a solar energy company to produce renewable energy on the property.
Industrial decarbonization is expensive, and Pirelli states that achieving it requires partnerships with government, regulations, and incentives.
Pirelli’s R&D and procurement departments are highly specialized in evaluating technological innovations for sustainable materials without compromising tire performance or safety.
source_title: How Pirelli Tire North America is Driving Lower Emissions

facts: Pirelli Tire North America includes a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and research and development center at its Rome, Georgia headquarters.
The Rome, Georgia headquarters is described as a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant.
Pirelli focuses its business on the high-end, premium product segment where it is a world leader.
Pirelli has a commercial presence in over 160 countries and 16 manufacturing sites in 11 countries.
Pirelli Tire North America includes sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City.
The company manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for export and domestic car/motorcycle applications.
Pirelli is the exclusive supplier of the Formula 1 championship, the Superbike world championship, and many other championships around the world.
source_title: MADD and Pirelli Partner to Drive Change and Keep the Nation’s Roads Safe - MADD

facts: Pirelli North America was named a 2026 Ford Supplier of the Year in the “Collaboration: Seek to Understand” category at the Ford Supplier Awards & Summit.
The award recognizes Pirelli's outstanding performance, dedication, and partnership with Ford.
The Ford Supplier Awards & Summit took place in Dearborn on June 23rd, 2026.
Pirelli North America announced the launch of the Scorpion™ AS 4, the next evolution in its successful Scorpion all-season family.
The new tire delivers next-level performance with exceptional wet braking, precise handling, reduced noise, and outstanding mileage engineered specifically for North American roads.
The Scorpion™ AS 4 is designed for crossovers, SUVs, and pick-up trucks.
The launch continues Pirelli’s commitment to innovation and market-focused development, raising the bar in the touring all-season segment with a balanced combination of durability and comfort.
The new tire is equipped with a 70,000-mile treadwear warranty to provide long-lasting performance in all conditions.
The Scorpion™ AS 4 launch announcement was made from Rome, Georgia on June 4th, 2026.
Pirelli North America kicked off its first closed-loop circular recycling initiative, turning scrap tires into new tires.
The recycling initiative is part of a broader strategy to increase the use of recycled and bio-based materials in Pirelli's products.
The closed-loop recycling project was recognized with the Tire Recycling Foundation’s Value Chain Collaboration Award.
The recycling initiative launch was announced from Rome, Georgia on May 14th, 2026.
Pirelli and Swedish company Univrses signed a partnership to enhance Cyber Tyre™ technology by integrating AI-based computer vision systems.
Under the agreement, Pirelli acquired a 30% stake in Univrses, with an option to reach a majority stake.
Univrses’ 3DAI™ technologies will be integrated into Pirelli’s Cyber Tyre system solutions.
The combined technology aims to deliver vehicles that are safer and more performant.
The technology has potential applications in ADAS and autonomous driving systems.
The partnership will provide timely, relevant, and actionable data for a range of road management use cases.
Insights from the partnership will enable road authorities to make better decisions and deploy resources more effectively.
The initiative has the potential to reduce road-related accidents and save lives.
The Pirelli-Univrses partnership was announced in Milan/Stockholm on April 29th, 2026.
source_title: Press releases | Pirelli

facts: The Motor Enclave announced a sponsorship agreement with Pirelli Tire North America to become the official tire partner of the 200-acre experiential motorsports venue.
The venue is located at the corner of I-4 and I-75, less than 15 minutes from downtown Tampa, Florida.
The pit lane will be branded "The Pirelli Pit Lane" with trademark yellow and red branding throughout key development locations.
All events on the 1.6-mile Hermann Tilke-designed performance circuit will begin and end in the newly named Pirelli Pit Lane.
A "Pirelli Podium" architectural element featuring authentic Pirelli Formula 1 tires will be installed for guest photography.

Brad Oleshansky, Founder and CEO of The Motor Enclave, stated that Pirelli's racing heritage and Formula 1 partnership align with the venue's mission to deliver unique experiences to automotive enthusiasts.
Andre Oosthuizen, Chief Marketing Officer of Pirelli Tire North America, noted that Pirelli has tracked the development of The Motor Enclave Tampa since its initial announcement in 2019.

Oosthuizen cited the success of Oleshansky's previous Michigan project, M1 Concourse, as a key factor in selecting this venue to demonstrate Pirelli's full product line across various driving surfaces.
The Motor Enclave development features a 1.6-mile performance circuit, a 2-acre vehicle dynamics pad, and a 100-acre off-road experience with purpose-built trails.
The site includes a 37,000-square-foot corporate event center and over 300 privately owned luxury garages.
Pirelli Tire North America operates its Rome, Georgia headquarters, which houses a Modular Integrated Robotized System (MIRS) facility and a research and development center.
PTNA also operates a state-of-the-art manufacturing plant in Silao, Mexico, along with sales and marketing offices in New York City, Los Angeles, Detroit, Montreal, and Mexico City.
Pirelli manufactures, distributes, and markets original equipment and replacement tires for domestic and export car and motorcycle applications.
The company's product range includes innovative tires for cars, motorcycles, and bicycles developed through partnerships with prestigious manufacturers to meet specific consumer needs.
Founded in Milan in 1872, Pirelli is a global brand recognized for cutting-edge technology, high-end production excellence, and Italian-rooted innovation.
source_title: The Motor Enclave Tampa Announces Partnership With Pirelli Tire North ... 2024-01-08

facts: Pirelli’s only U.S. manufacturing plant is located in Rome, Georgia.
The Rome, Georgia facility produces high-performance P Zero and Scorpion tires for the premium segment.
The Rome, Georgia plant employs approximately 1,000 workers.
The Rome, Georgia plant initially employed over 250 workers when it opened on August 17, 2002.
The choice of Rome, Georgia was driven by the availability of skilled labor and flight connections to Atlanta rather than Italian heritage.
Pirelli has acknowledged the coincidence of the city’s name matching its own.
The Rome plant is designated one of Pirelli’s “Cyber” factories, utilizing Industry 4.0 automation and data analytics throughout production.
The Rome, Georgia plant produces approximately 400,000 tires per year, representing about one percent of Pirelli’s total annual output.
Pirelli’s U.S. production is concentrated in the SUV, truck, and ultra-high-performance segments.
Pirelli ranks as the fifth-largest tire manufacturer in the world by revenue.
Pirelli has a presence in over 160 countries and operates over 21 plants globally.
Pirelli is Italian in origin but majority-owned by ChemChina, a Chinese state enterprise that acquired a controlling stake starting in 2015 (initially 26.2%, later over 50.1%).
ChemChina reduced its position to approximately 45 to 46.7 percent to signal a market-friendly posture in Europe.
Pirelli’s premium tire division remains listed on the Milan stock exchange, with ChemChina holding approximately 37 percent of shares.
Chinese ownership had little operational impact on day-to-day management, and the Rome, Georgia plant is not Chinese-operated.
Pirelli serves as the exclusive tire supplier for Formula One racing since 2011 and has sponsored races since 1907.
Pirelli tires are OEM fitments for Aston Martin, Porsche, Bentley, Ferrari, Lamborghini, Maserati, Pagani, and McLaren, with 100 percent supply agreements on several marques.
Every tire sold in the United States carries a DOT code on the sidewall where the first two characters after “DOT” identify the manufacturing plant.
Pirelli tires made at the Rome, Georgia plant carry the same quality standards as those made in Europe.
Competitors with U.S. manufacturing include Goodyear (seven U.S. facilities), Cooper Tire (Findlay, Ohio and Texarkana, Arkansas), and Michelin (South Carolina, North Carolina, and Alabama).
Pirelli produces roughly 500,000 tires per month in Mexico alone, far exceeding its U.S. output.
Pirelli has had a production facility in China since 2005, with a second factory opening in November 2007, producing roughly 700,000 tires per year there.
Before 2022, Pirelli operated two manufacturing plants in Russia (Voronezh and Kirov) employing approximately 3,200 workers combined, with $224 million invested between 2012 and 2015.
source_title: Where Are Pirelli Tires Made? - All American Made

facts: Pirelli Tire North America will manufacture the Pirelli Cyber Tyre at its plant in Rome, Ga.
The manufacturing move reinforces the firm's long-term commitment to the United States.
Pirelli officials state the development highlights the strategic importance of the U.S. for Pirelli’s global growth roadmap.
The development strengthens the company’s integrated industrial and technological presence in the country.
The announcement was made at the SelectUSA Investment Summit.
The SelectUSA Investment Summit is promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce.
At the summit, Cyber Tyre is being showcased as a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.
The Cyber Tire collects data and information from sensors embedded in tires.
It processes this data through Pirelli’s proprietary software and algorithms.
It communicates in real time with the vehicle’s electronics.
This enables new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems to enhance the driving experience and increase safety levels.
The technology supports connected infrastructure.
In the U.S., where digital and smart mobility solutions are rapidly expanding, Cyber Tyre represents a distinctive competitive advantage for Pirelli.
Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process to enhance Rome's role as a high-tech production site.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group.
This process is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
The MIRS process enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
The start of Cyber Tyre production at the Rome, Ga., plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in the U.S., according to Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli Tire North America.
The production start reflects a commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber Tyre closer to the market.
It further strengthens Pirelli’s industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
The development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli’s production capacity in Rome.
This growth is an integral part of the Pirelli group’s industrial development plans.
It will further strengthen Pirelli’s presence in the United States, one of its most important and strategic markets.
Details regarding production will be communicated in the coming months following the finalization of development plans.
Once development plans are fully finalized, Pirelli will communicate more concrete information, including timing and scale.
The Cyber Tyre is already being introduced through original equipment (OE) partnerships with select automakers.
It is available on certain vehicles in markets including the U.S.
At this stage, the Cyber Tyre technology is primarily tied to OEM collaborations.
The Cyber Tyre is already being introduced on cars like Aston Martin and Pagani.
Pirelli is working on additional OE projects with other car brands.
source_title: Pirelli to Build Cyber Tire in U.S. | Modern Tire Dealer

facts: Pirelli Tire North America is located at 100 Pirelli Dr., Rome GA 30161.
Matteo Battatini serves as a contact for the company.
source_title: Pirelli Tire North America - Tire Review Magazine

facts: Guy Mannino, President of Pirelli Tire North America (PTNA), is leaving the company after 25 years of service to pursue other professional interests.
Mannino began his career in 1982 and embarked on a series of international management assignments with the Pirelli Power Transmission Division.
In 1993, Mannino joined the Tire Division and held management positions in Italy, Venezuela, and the United States.
Hugh Pace, Chairman & CEO of PTNA, will assume leadership for all business and commercial operations in addition to his current executive role.
Under Mannino’s leadership, PTNA relocated its corporate headquarters to Rome, Georgia.
Under Mannino’s leadership, PTNA built a state-of-the-art MIRS manufacturing facility.
Under Mannino’s leadership, PTNA expanded its national distribution network.
Pirelli Tire North America extended its gratitude to Mannino for his many contributions to the company and wished him and his family personal and professional success.
source_title: Mannino Leaves PTNA - Tyrepress

facts: Pirelli is reinforcing its long-term commitment to the United States with a key step in its product and industrial strategy.

The Georgia plant, already dedicated to technologically advanced products for the U.S. market in the High-Value segment and Motorsport, will be enhanced by production of connected tires featuring Cyber™ Tyre technology.
This development highlights the strategic importance of the U.S. for Pirelli's global growth roadmap and strengthens its integrated industrial and technological presence in the country.
The announcement was made on Thursday, May 7th, 2026, as Pirelli participated in the SelectUSA Investment Summit, a flagship event promoted by the U.S. Department of Commerce.
Cyber™ Tyre is being showcased at the summit as a core innovation shaping the future of smart mobility.

Cyber™ Tyre is the world's first hardware-and-software system capable of collecting data and information from sensors embedded in tires, processing them through Pirelli's proprietary software and algorithms, and communicating in real time with vehicle electronics to enable new functionalities integrated with driving and control systems.
The technology enhances the driving experience and increases safety levels while supporting connected infrastructure.
In the highly advanced U.S. market where digital and smart mobility solutions are rapidly expanding, Cyber™ Tyre represents a distinctive competitive advantage for Pirelli.
At SelectUSA, Cyber™ Tyre was showcased at the Georgia State booth, highlighting Pirelli's industrial footprint in the state where it has been established for more than 20 years.
Claudio Zanardo, CEO of Pirelli North America, stated that the start of Cyber™ Tyre production in the Rome, Georgia plant is a significant milestone for Pirelli in this country.

The milestone reflects Pirelli's commitment to bringing advanced technologies like Cyber™ Tyre closer to the market and further strengthening its industrial footprint and innovation capabilities in the United States.
To enhance Rome's role as a high-tech production site, Pirelli is finalizing the introduction of the latest version of the MIRS (Modular Integrated Robotized System) production process.
The MIRS process will be the most advanced manufacturing process for high-end, premium production within the entire Pirelli Group and is exclusive to the Georgia factory.
The process further enhances robotized production capabilities, increasing productivity, flexibility, and quality.
It is a highly digitalized system that enables a direct link between product design and its industrial application.

This development lays the groundwork for growth in Pirelli's production capacity in Rome, an integral part of the Group's industrial development plans, and will further strengthen Pirelli's presence in the United States as one of its most important and strategic markets.
The Rome, Georgia plant specializes in high-value tire production for the North American market.
The facility also hosts a dedicated R&D center, further strengthening its role in technological development and product innovation.
The facility is recognized for responsible sourcing practices, including the use of FSC®-certified natural rubber, underscoring the company's broader environmental commitment across its supply chain.
source_title: Pirelli Will Start Cyber Tyre Production in Georgia - Metro Atlanta CEO

facts: Pirelli has secured US approval to manufacture its connected Cyber Tyre technology at its Georgia plant.
Chinese firm Sinochem owns 34.1 percent of Pirelli.
The US almost blocked the project over China concerns.
Italy intervened using ‘golden powers’ restrictions on Sinochem’s influence to enable the manufacturing approval.
The Italian intervention offsets US tariff impacts on the project.
source_title: Pirelli’s Connected Tires Will Be Made In Georgia, The US Almost Blocked Them Over China - TheAutoExec

facts: Pirelli & C. SpA is a multinational company and the world’s fifth-largest tire manufacturer.
The company operates 19 manufacturing sites around the world.
Pirelli maintains a network of around 10,000 distributors and retailers globally.
Pierluigi Dinelli joined Pirelli Tire North America in January 2015 as head of the company’s businesses in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.
The North American business includes three sales units for car tires, truck tires, and motorcycle tires.
Pirelli operates two manufacturing facilities in North America: one located in Rome, Georgia, USA, and another in Silao, Guanajuato, Mexico.
Dinelli previously served as Chief Marketing Officer of Gewiss SpA for nearly four years before joining Pirelli.
Prior to that role, he was Chief Marketing Officer of high-end brakes manufacturer Brembo SpA and its subsidiaries.
He also held the position of Vice President of Sales, Europe, for the housewares business unit of Bormioli Rocco.
Dinelli began his career at Pirelli in 1999 in the sales and marketing department in Italy.
He quickly advanced to key account manager for Europe, responsible for fleets, leasing companies, and rental companies.
From 2004 to 2006, he served as Marketing Director for Pirelli Tire North America before moving to Romania as Vice President of Sales & Marketing for Southeast Europe.
Pirelli is leveraging its manufacturing facility close to Atlanta to grow consistently in the U.S. market.
The company aims to be closer to consumers by providing region-specific products rather than selling identical products globally.
Many U.S. consumers are unaware that Pirelli tires can fit their vehicles, so local manufacturing helps increase brand appreciation.
Pirelli recently opened a PZero store in Los Angeles as a showroom and shop concept targeting the prestige segment for luxury and sports cars.
In the market where the PZero store operates, Pirelli holds close to 50% market share.
The store provides customized tire fitting services with exact tires developed specifically for vehicles such as Lamborghini, Bentley, and Ferrari.
Pirelli has been the exclusive tire supplier for the Formula 1 Championship for nine consecutive years.
Pirelli is the unique tire supplier for Lamborghini for many years.
The company’s future U.S. strategy involves penetrating both the original equipment business and the replacement business through separate distribution channels.
All-Season tires account for 91% of the U.S. tire market because they can be used year-round, unlike the summer and winter tire split in other regions.
Pirelli currently has 80% of its product range covered for the U.S. market and is developing additional products to complete the range.
The company considers its customers its most important asset and maintains very important partnerships with them.
source_title: HOMBRE1 | HOMBRE Exclusive: PIRELLI Conquers the U.S. Market Led by CEO Pierluigi Dinelli

Providing infrastructure and confidence for Georgia's drivers and business owners to go electric.
Maintaining a balanced mix of hydro, solar, and nuclear power with flexible rate plans.
Lewis Chemical Company is pursuing new product development in automotive care and infrastructure support.
Sledge Tech focuses on air filtration solutions that provide clean indoor air for manufacturing industry workers.

facts: Porsche Cars North America, Inc. is completing it's new $100 million project that began in November 2012.
The 28-acre headquarters will be located at the northeast corner of Hartsfield-Jackson Airport.
It will feature a technical training center.
It will feature a classic car restoration and display area.
It will feature restaurants.
It will feature a Porsche Experience Center with a 1.6 mile test track.
The new Atlanta headquarters will employ 400.

facts: A new 911 GT3 has joined the fleet available for customers at Porsche Experience Centers in Atlanta and Los Angeles for driver development courses.
The road car is designed to fulfill every driving enthusiast’s wish, with a high-revving, naturally aspirated engine that is virtually identical to the track-only 911 GT3 Cup race car.
Kjell Gruner serves as President and CEO of PCNA.
Experience Center customers drive on a variety of track modules, some designed to simulate everyday driving conditions and others to provide more challenging environments.
In Atlanta, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 90-minute 911 GT3 driving experience.
In Atlanta, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 90-minute 911 GT3 vs. 911 Turbo driving experience.
In Atlanta, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 90-minute 911 GT3 Generations experience comparing the 991 and 992 models, available during the 11:00 a.m. and 2:00 p.m. sessions until March 31.
In Los Angeles, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 90-minute 911 GT3 driving experience.
In Los Angeles, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 90-minute 911 GT3 vs. 911 Turbo driving experience.
In Los Angeles, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 4-hour 911 GT3 Accelerated driving experience.
In Los Angeles, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 90-minute 911 GT3 Generations experience comparing the 991 and 992 models, available until March 31.
In Los Angeles, the Porsche Experience Center offers a 4-hour 911 GT3 Generations Accelerated driving experience, available until March 31.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, at One Porsche Drive, Atlanta, GA 30354 USA.
PCNA was established in 1984 and has been headquartered in Atlanta since 1998.
PCNA is the exclusive U.S. importer of the Porsche 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, Panamera, and Taycan.
The Atlanta Porsche Experience Center features a module-based 1.6-mile driver development track, a business center, and a fine dining restaurant named 356.
PCNA operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles on a 53-acre complex.
The Los Angeles Porsche Experience Center features a driver development track with eight educational modules totaling 4.1 miles, a business center, and Restaurant 917.
PCNA supports 193 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships in the U.S. by supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Porsche boasts a racing heritage with over 30,000 motorsport wins to date.

facts: Hertz Corporation became the first U.S. car rental company to purchase vehicles from Porsche and the first major U.S. car rental company to rent Porsche sports cars.

Through its relationship with Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA) and Porsche Motorsport North America, Inc. (PMNA), Hertz enabled consumers in select markets to rent a limited number of Panamera sport sedans as part of the Hertz Prestige Collection.
Mark P. Frissora served as Hertz Chairman and CEO at the time of the announcement.
Hertz was voted Top-Rated for Vehicles in the Zagat 2012/13 U.S. Car Rental Survey.
The Porsche Panamera sports sedan provides sports car technology for four passengers, combining comfort and everyday practicality without compromise.
Hertz and Porsche Motorsport North America agreed that Hertz would be named an official sponsor of the 2012 Porsche IMSA GT3 Cup Challenge by Yokohama.
Hertz was also named a sponsor of the 2012 JDX Racing Team as part of the agreement.
As part of the sponsorship, Hertz will be integrated into the GT3 Cup Challenge and displayed prominently on all vehicles.
The 2012 JDX Racing Porsche GTC car competed throughout 2012 in the American Le Mans Series.
The Porsche Panamera has a manufacturer’s suggested retail price starting at $75,850.
Hertz customers in select California, Florida, and Nevada markets could rent the vehicle with rates starting at $250 per weekend day.
Hertz operates one of the youngest and most diverse fleets in the industry.
Hertz offers a mobile SMS text and email service called Carfirmations that confirms Gold members’ reservations and advises them of their car and location prior to arrival.
Hertz offers Choose Control Gold Choice, which allows Gold members to keep their reserved car or choose a different car from the Gold Choice area.
Hertz offers Zap Technology e-Return, which emails receipts to customers to ensure the fastest car rental drop-off.
Hertz operates Acceler-Rental ExpressRent Interactive Kiosks that allow customers to rent cars with or without reservations through live, face-to-face video kiosks.
Hertz pioneered vehicle specific reservation capabilities via its Collections and maintains the largest fleet of make and model reservable cars in the industry.
Porsche Cars North America, Inc. (PCNA), based in Atlanta, Ga., is the exclusive U.S. importer of Porsche sports cars, the Cayenne SUV, and the Panamera sports sedan.
PCNA was established in 1984 as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
PCNA employs approximately 220 people who provide parts, service, marketing, and training for 192 dealers.
Hertz operates from approximately 8,650 corporate and licensee locations in approximately 150 countries.
Hertz is the number one airport car rental brand in the U.S. and at 119 major airports in Europe.
Hertz was voted the Best Overall Car Rental Company in Zagat’s 2012/13 U.S. Car Rental Survey, earning top honors in 14 additional categories.
Hertz swept global awards for Best Rewards Program and Best Overall Benefits from FlyerTalk.com.
Hertz owns Donlen Corporation, a vehicle leasing and fleet management leader.
Hertz operates the Hertz On Demand car sharing business.
Hertz owns Hertz Equipment Rental Corporation, which includes Hertz Entertainment Services.

facts: Porsche broke ground on the first phase of its North American Headquarters expansion project in September 2020.
The expansion is located between Interstate 75 and downtown Hapeville, positioned at the northeast corner of the Atlanta airport.
The multifaceted addition will be 33 acres and adjacent to the existing 27-acre headquarters that opened in 2015.
Other attractions for the expansion include a museum and two dining concepts: Restaurant 356 and Carrera Café.
Additions to the initial investment include a new standalone service center and a Porsche Classic Restoration facility.
The project includes a technical new driver development course that can be linked with the existing one for almost 3 miles of track.
Corners of the new course were specified by Porsche to be inspired by the Nürburgring carousel turn, the Laguna Seca corkscrew, and the Tail of the Dragon road.
Other features of the new course include a dynamic zone for slaloms, a low friction circle, and a new ice hill.
An additional parking structure is also in the works.
Porsche Cars of North America stated the new track will open in mid-2023.
The restoration building is scheduled to complete the expansion later in 2023.
Porsche has not specified the exact investment amount for the expansion, only calling it a multi-million dollar development.
Porsche representatives noted that the original facilities tallied more than a quarter-million visitors in their first five years alone.
Hapeville City Council member at-large Brett Reichert commended the ongoing project in an email to Urbanize Atlanta.
Reichert stated that Porsche continues to drive major investment and residential development in and around Hapeville.
source_title: How Porsche's Atlanta headquarters expansion is zooming along | Urbanize Atlanta

facts: 2025 Porsche Taycan 4S Cross Turismo was tested on a 1,000-mile road trip from New York City to Raleigh, North Carolina.
The Taycan has an EPA-estimated range of around 280 miles, up from the prior generation's 220-230 mile range.
Porsche has a deal with Electrify America that allows plug-and-charge compatibility without needing multiple apps.
ChargePoint chargers in Bethesda, Maryland were found but did not offer fast enough speeds for the trip.
An EVgo fast charger was used in a parking structure in Henderson, North Carolina, requiring credit card payment due to app connectivity issues.
Tesla Superchargers are plentiful in the Raleigh/Chapel Hill area, and Porsche is part of the Tesla network, allowing access using a Porsche-supplied adapter.
A Tesla charging session cost $43.79 for nearly 80 kWh of charge ($0.55/kWh).
An Electrify America fast charger in Emporia, Virginia featured two plugs but only allowed one plug to be used at a time.
The Taycan was originally released in 2019 and received an update in 2025 with new motors and an improved battery.
The updated Taycan produces nearly 600 horsepower and 523 lb-ft of torque.
The Cross Turismo body style features fender cladding and a ride height roughly 20 millimeters higher than the Sport Turismo, with additional lift capability via air suspension.
Optional Porsche Active Ride system uses adaptive dampers to keep the vehicle level during braking, steering, and acceleration, and can raise the car when doors open for easier entry.
The Taycan has a starting price of around $130,000.
source_title: 1,000 miles in the Porsche Taycan 4S Cross Turismo EV: The good and bad - AOL

facts: PCNA is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, since 1998.
PCNA serves as the exclusive U.S. importer for the Porsche Taycan electric vehicle, alongside the 911, 718 Boxster, 718 Cayman, Macan, Cayenne, and Panamera.
PCNA supports 192 independently owned and operated Porsche dealerships across the United States by supplying parts, service, marketing, and training.

PCNA operates the first Porsche Experience Center in North America located in Atlanta, which features a module-based 1.6-mile driver development track, a business center, and fine dining restaurant 356.

PCNA also operates a second Porsche Experience Center near Los Angeles situated on a 53-acre complex containing an eight-module, 4.1-mile driver development track, a business center, and Restaurant 917.
PCNA is an indirect wholly-owned subsidiary of Porsche AG, which is headquartered in Stuttgart, Germany.
Kjell Gruner holds the title of President & CEO of PCNA, where he oversees operations for both the United States and Canada.
Kjell Gruner also heads the executive leadership team of Porsche Digital, Inc., the sports car manufacturer's digital subsidiary in the U.S.
PCNA introduced the Porsche Parking Plus app on Dec. 7, 2020, making it the second market to launch the application after Porsche AG began offering it earlier that year.
The Porsche Parking Plus app includes search functionality that allows users to filter parking facility features for electric vehicle charging.
The technology platform powering the Porsche Parking Plus app is managed by the Chicago-based company Arrive.

Porsche Cars North America Inc. is building its new headquarters adjacent to Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport as the first company to locate within the Aerotroplis Atlanta business district.

Walmart plans to invest $108 million and employ 400 people over the next three years in a new fulfillment center near Hartsfield-Jackson.

The Atlanta Aerotropolis Alliance, convened by the Atlanta Regional Commission, is comprised of public and private sector leaders fostering development around Hartsfield-Jackson.
Fulton County partnered with Majestic Realty to create a $120-million privately financed real estate development initiative at Charlie Brown Airfield.

Clayton County can offer new businesses that want to expand as much as $4,000 per qualified job for five years.
Kroger announced plans to build a 1-million-square-foot distribution center at the former Fort Gillem property in Forest Park.
Kroger will hire 120 workers and invest more than $175 million over five years for its Forest Park distribution center.
State and local leaders see the Kroger deal as the first step to turning the abandoned army post into a major distribution hub for the metro area and the Southeast.

Southern Crescent Technical College’s new satellite campus opened last summer on a 25-acre site adjacent to Henry County High in McDonough, featuring laboratories for CISCO networking, computers, life science and an MRI/CT simulator.
The Lakes at Green Valley is the first and only “eco-friendly” industrial park in Georgia, located in Spalding County.

facts: Gov. Brian Kemp visited Porsche’s new electric vehicle plant in Stuttgart, Germany.
Detlev von Platen, who led Porsche Cars North America’s Atlanta operation for seven years, served as the tour guide at the Stuttgart plant.
Von Platen presided over a site selection process that considered 73 locations before choosing a 53-acre complex with a test track near the Atlanta airport for Porsche.
Klaus Zellmer succeeded von Platen as president and CEO of Porsche Cars North America in 2015.
Von Platen is now a member of the Porsche AG executive board for sales and marketing.
The Porsche Taycan launched in December with a six-figure starting price.
Gov. Kemp had just announced a new foreign investment to supply a $1.7 billion vehicle battery factory in Jackson County, Georgia.
In a news release, Gov. Kemp noted a desire to see a hub for future-facing vehicles continue growing in Georgia, already an automotive magnet.
Georgia is home to 800 German-owned facilities when including retail outlets.
Hundreds of German manufacturers have made Georgia home, contributing to the 36,000 jobs German firms support here.
Gov. Kemp visited Munich and met with Florian Herrmann, Bavaria’s state minister for domestic, European affairs and media, reciprocating a June 2019 visit by Herrmann to Georgia.

Georgia and Bavaria are partners in the Regional Leaders Alliance, a group of international states and provinces including Shandong, China; Sao Paulo, Brazil and others that meet annually to share best practices.
Gov. Kemp opened Georgia’s new trade and investment office at Max Weber Platz in Munich.
The Georgia office in Munich employs five full-time staff for trade and investment promotion, plus two contractors for tourism promotion.
Frank Sportolari, president of UPS Germany and AmCham Germany, attended the ribbon-cutting for Georgia’s Munich office.

facts: Rivian amended its Department of Energy (DOE) construction loan on April 30, 2026.
The amendment reduces total borrowing to $4.5 billion and reconfigures the Stanton Springs North plant plan to raise initial annual output.
Rivian stated that the capacity increase will lower per-unit manufacturing costs.
Production of the R2 midsize SUV remains on schedule for late 2028.
The original DOE loan finalized in early 2025 was $6.6 billion.
Rivian cut its DOE construction loan by $2.0 billion from the original amount.
The amended $4.5 billion package includes $4.006 billion of principal and $494 million of capitalized interest.
The loan will be structured in tranches tied to the updated facility design and capacity.
The first draw from the loan is expected in early 2027.
Despite the reduced loan size, the overall construction timeline remains unchanged.
The Stanton Springs North site spans about 2,000 acres in southern Morgan and Walton counties, roughly an hour east of Atlanta.
The plant will open with an initial annual capacity of 300,000 vehicles, a 50% increase from the prior 200,000 plan.
The higher opening capacity aims to improve utilization of capital-intensive parts of the plant early on.
This change aims to lower per-vehicle manufacturing costs and accelerate the production ramp.
The plant will primarily produce the R2 midsize SUV.
The facility is designed to support up to 50,000 fully autonomous Uber robotaxis annually under a commercial partnership starting in late 2028.
Rivian has committed $5 billion of company capital to the facility.
Rivian expects to employ about 7,500 workers at the site, including roughly 500 dedicated to robotaxi production.
Preparatory work is underway on the stamping-press area.
The stamping-press area is described as one of the most capital-intensive and technically demanding parts of the project.
Construction activity at the main buildings will accelerate this summer (2026) as vertical construction begins.
Vertical construction is slated to start in summer 2026.
Phase-one production and robotaxi manufacturing are targeted for late 2028.
Rivian stated in a press release on April 30, 2026: “The amended schedule and site design allows Rivian to ramp up its Georgia production efforts more quickly.”
source_title: Rivian DOE Loan Amendment Boosts Georgia Plant Capacity | InsiderFinance

facts: RealTruck, Inc. acquired Mountain Top, the leading designer and manufacturer of truck bed accessories outside North America.
The acquisition was announced on Oct. 17, 2023, in Ann Arbor, Mich.
Mountain Top is based in Denmark and supplies major original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) while selling truck accessories throughout Europe, Asia, Australia, and the U.S.
The acquisition accelerates RealTruck’s international expansion and complements its recent acquisition of Mexico-based brand Go Rhino.
RealTruck CEO Carl-Martin Lindahl stated they are bringing Mountain Top into the RealTruck family to power international expansion and growth.
Mountain Top has state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Denmark, Thailand, and the U.S.
Mountain Top is the only European truck bed cover supplier of scale certified by the International Automotive Task Force (IATF).
Mountain Top’s advanced manufacturing capabilities provide RealTruck with more capacity to support global operations and accelerate sales into markets like Australia, Thailand, and across Europe.
Mountain Top’s flagship product line is the premium retractable truck bed cover available in both electronic and manual versions.
Mountain Top CEO Henrik Støwer Petersen stated the team is excited to join RealTruck to provide more value to business partners with access to a broader range of product offerings.
In August 2023, RealTruck acquired IDDEA (Industria Diseñadora De Autopartes, S.A. De C.V.), which markets under the Go Rhino brand.
Go Rhino holds 40 issued and pending patents for innovative premium products developed and manufactured for truck, Jeep, and SUV owners.
The acquisition strengthens RealTruck’s existing portfolio of brands spanning truck bed covers, truck bed liners, steps, floor liners, fender flares, vent visors, and cargo management.
RealTruck is headquartered in Ann Arbor, Mich., with over 5,000 associates across 47 locations throughout North America.
RealTruck holds more than 630 patents and continuously designs, develops, manufactures, and sells industry-defining products.
RealTruck’s products are sold through its website, more than 12,000 dealer locations, and automotive (OEM) partnerships.

facts: In 2022, Bosch exceeded its business targets in a challenging year, increasing total sales to $93 billion from $83 billion the previous year (a 12.0 percent increase, or 9.4 percent exchange-rate-adjusted).
EBIT from operations reached $4 billion in 2022, up from $3.4 billion previously, with the EBIT margin rising from 4.0 to 4.3 percent.
Dr. Stefan Hartung, chairman of the board of management of Robert Bosch GmbH, stated that both sales and margin were higher than expected despite a demanding economic and social environment.
The company aims for annual sales growth of 6 to 8 percent on average in coming years, with margins reaching at least 7 percent.
Hartung stated the strategic aim is to grow in every global region and be among the leading three suppliers in relevant markets.
Bosch identifies electrification, automation, and digitalization as major trends driving technological transformation and opening growth opportunities in existing and new business areas.
The company's 'Invented for life' ethos emphasizes leveraging software and artificial intelligence to seize these opportunities.
Bosch has maintained a regional presence in North America since 1906.
In 2021, the company generated consolidated sales of $13.5 billion across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
As of December 31, 2021, Bosch employed nearly 35,500 associates in North America across more than 100 locations.
The company's operations are divided into four business sectors: Mobility Solutions, Industrial Technology, Consumer Goods, and Energy and Building Technology.
Bosch is pursuing a vision of sustainable, safe, and exciting mobility by utilizing expertise in sensor technology, software, services, and its IoT cloud to provide connected, cross-domain solutions.
The strategic objective is to facilitate connected living with products and solutions that either contain artificial intelligence or were developed/manufactured with its help.
The company has been carbon neutral since the first quarter of 2020 across its more than 400 locations worldwide.
Bosch employs approximately 85,500 associates in research and development across 136 global locations, including nearly 44,000 software engineers.

facts: Sewon America is a subsidiary of Korean-based Sewon Precision Industry Co.
Sewon America will create 740 new jobs in Rincon.
Sewon America will invest more than $300 million in a new Effingham County manufacturing facility.
The project is the largest known private investment in the City of Rincon to date.
The announcement fulfills a commitment to create jobs and investment in all four Savannah Harbor Interstate-16 Joint Development Authority (JDA) counties.
This commitment stems from Hyundai’s Metaplant location in Bryan County, which is the largest economic development project in Georgia state history.
Suppliers for Hyundai’s Metaplant have committed over $1.8 billion in investment.
This supplier investment surpasses initial estimates by at least $800 million.
Governor Brian Kemp stated that the announcement celebrates delivering on a promise to create jobs in all four JDA counties and the growth of a valued business partner.
Governor Kemp congratulated Chairman Kim on the company's recent expansion during his first international trip as governor to Korea.
Governor Kemp discussed continuing to work together with the company during that trip, which led to the current announcement.
Speaker of the House Jon Burns, who represents part of Effingham County, played a role in securing the project and engaging Sewon America executives.
Speaker Jon Burns celebrated the creation of 740 new jobs in Effingham County and coastal Georgia.

Lt. Governor Jones noted that investments by companies like Sewon build on Georgia's commitment to remain the No. 1 state for doing business and attracting a robust workforce.
Lt. Governor Jones stated that the more than $300 million investment in Effingham County will positively impact the local community and the state.

Sewon America opened a manufacturing facility in LaGrange in 2008.
The LaGrange facility has since doubled in size.
Following the completion of the Rincon facility, Sewon America is expected to support 1,600 jobs in Georgia.
Vice Chairman Kim of Sewon America stated that Sewon America was founded 15 years ago in west Georgia.
Vice Chairman Kim expressed happiness at building another Sewon facility in east Georgia and excitement about the company's future as one family operating in two Georgia locations.
Vice Chairman Kim credited the company's team members, Governor Kemp, the Georgia Department of Economic Development, and Effingham County for supporting the growth.
Vice Chairman Kim stated Sewon America will continue as an excellent corporate and community partner and looks forward to continued success in Effingham County.
Sewon's new facility will be located at the Grande View industrial park in Rincon.
The new manufacturing location will produce electric vehicle (EV) body parts for original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The facility will supply parts to Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Operations at the new facility are expected to begin in 2025.
Rincon Mayor Ken Lee welcomed Sewon America to the community.
Mayor Lee commended the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority for bringing the largest employment and private investment project in the city's history.
Mayor Lee expressed looking forward to working with Sewon to ensure its long-term success in the community.
Chairman of the Effingham County IDA Troy Smith stated Sewon America selected Grande View industrial park as the best location for its future growth.

Lynch noted that the Hyundai announcement continues to bring new economic opportunities to Georgia.
Director of Project Implementation and Supplier Strategy Alyce Thornhill represented the GDEcD's Global Commerce team on the project.
The project was developed in partnership with the Effingham County Industrial Development Authority, Georgia Ports Authority, Georgia Quick Start, and Georgia EMC.

GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that Sewon has integrated into and lent its industry experience to Georgia's automotive community through work with local colleges, Georgia Quick Start, and statewide initiatives like Georgia Made.
Commissioner Wilson expressed excitement that Sewon is reinvesting in Georgia while supporting Hyundai Motor Group's operations in Effingham County.
Commissioner Wilson noted that Effingham County has a skilled workforce, infrastructure, and collaborative spirit to support the company's growth.
Commissioner Wilson congratulated Sewon and Effingham County and looked forward to the opportunities created for the company and community.
On October 25, 2022, Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America officially broke ground in neighboring Bryan County on its first fully dedicated EV and battery manufacturing facility.
Georgia is positioned as a hub for the electric mobility industry.

facts: SHIROKI North America, Inc. is a mid-sized manufacturer of motor vehicle parts based in Smithville, Tennessee.

It operates multiple facilities located in Smithville, Tennessee; Dalton, Georgia; and Gordonsville, Tennessee.

facts: Shiroki Corporation is an automotive parts manufacturer supplying door sashes, window regulators, seat components, and mechatronics components.
Shiroki North America, Inc., located in the state of Tennessee, reported FY2006 sales of 27,020 million yen, a 5.9% year-on-year increase.
Sales growth at the Tennessee facility was mainly due to increased production volume of door sashes for the Nissan Altima, which began mass production in North America in the fall of 2006.
The Tennessee facility's operating profit reached 3 million yen in FY2006, compared to a 141 million yen operating loss the previous year.
In December 2006, Shiroki-GA L.L.C. in Georgia expanded its plant.
Shiroki-GT L.L.C. in Tennessee started operations in March 2007 as Shiroki's third North American plant.
The company prepared for door sash production for the Nissan Altima, with production starting in September 2006.

Shiroki announced plans to increase U.S. production capacity by enlarging its Georgia plant for doorframe production and purchasing a closed local plant in Tennessee for functional seat components production.
The total investment for the U.S. expansion plans, excluding equipment and molds, will be limited to approximately 1.1 billion yen.
Shiroki established SWM-GT, L.L.C. as a fully-owned subsidiary of SW Manufacturing Inc. to oversee its third U.S. plant.
Construction of the new U.S. plant began in August 2006, with operations starting in January 2007.
The total investment for the new plant was 440 million yen.
The new plant will have a workforce of 80 people, comprised mainly of local nationals.
The facility aims to generate sales of over 1.7 billion yen in its first fiscal year.
Starting from January 2007, the new plant will produce mechanical parts for automotive seats, such as seat recliners and adjusters, and parts for doors, such as window regulators and door locks.
For new equipment installation from January 2007 to December 2007, Shiroki North America, Inc. in Tennessee is allocated an estimated investment of 313 thousand USD.
Shiroki-GA, LLC. in Georgia is allocated an estimated investment of 776 thousand USD for new manufacturing equipment from January 2007 to December 2007.
The investment table lists Shiroki-GT, L.L.C. as located in Georgia, USA, with an estimated equipment investment of 160 thousand USD from January 2007 to December 2007.
The company already operates overseas plants in Tennessee, Georgia, China, and Thailand, bringing its total overseas plant count to five after adding the new U.S. facility.
source_title:
Shiroki Corporation - Business Report FY2006
 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: SHIROKI Corporation reported FY ended March 31, 2013 sales of 109,850 million JPY, a 0.6% decrease year-on-year.
Operating income for FY ended March 31, 2013 was 2,506 million JPY, down 53.0% from the prior year.
Ordinary income for FY ended March 31, 2013 was 3,063 million JPY, down 42.8% year-on-year.
Net income for FY ended March 31, 2013 was 1,586 million JPY, down 34.1% year-on-year.
The company's smaller recliner, spring-lock seat rail, and new-type wire regulators are equipped on the Suzuki "Wagon R" and "Spacia".
Luggage closures developed by the company are equipped on the Toyota Lexus series and "Crown".
In March 2013, SHIROKI Corporation was awarded the Quality Management Award from Hino Motors.
Subsidiary PT. Shiroki Indonesia completed its plant building in July 2012 and launched commercial production in February 2013.
The company announced in July 2012 that it will establish a subsidiary in Thailand named Shiroki Asia Corporation Limited to support growth in Asia.
Shiroki Asia Corporation Limited will be capitalized at 10 million baht and 25 million yen.
Sales for Shiroki Asia Corporation Limited are expected to reach about 80 million baht (approximately 200 million yen) in fiscal 2015.
Shiroki North America, Inc. supplied door sashes to Nissan North America, marking the fourth vehicle in a row the company helped launch for them.
The company worked on preparing for the August 2013 production launch of aluminum sashes for GM, which will be the first time supplying GM in North America.
Shiroki North America, Inc. was awarded the American Region Excellence in Quality Award for the second consecutive year from Nissan North America.
SHIROKI Corporation is introducing a "satellite supply system" capable of cutting logistics costs by up to 50 percent compared with the current system.

The satellite supply system carries products such as seat adjusters and door frames as modules or semi-finished products to satellite locations near customer assembly plants for final assembly before delivery.
The new satellite supply system will be deployed primarily in North America and China where supply areas are widely spread.
The company envisions establishing up to four satellite locations through its mid-term business plan ending March 2016.
SHIROKI Corporation announced a new mid-term management plan covering the period ending with the 2016 fiscal year (March 2017).
The mid-term "Rolling Plan" aims to increase consolidated operating income by 21.7% to 140 billion yen and raise its profit margin from 3.4% to 5.0%, using the 2012 fiscal year as a benchmark.
Alongside the Rolling Plan, the company announced Shiroki Vision 2021, which plans a business roadmap for the 2021 fiscal year ending in March 2022.
Performance objectives for Shiroki Vision 2021 include 200 billion yen operating revenue, a 5% profit margin, and a 50% ratio of sales outside Japan.
The company plans to speed up global operations by building new plants at a pace of one every two years and increase business outside Japan.
R&D expenditure for the automotive components business was 2,729 million JPY for FY ended March 31, 2013.
Recent R&D achievements include next-generation power seats, next-generation manual seats, new wire-type regulators, new arm-type regulators, and new door frames.

Overall capital expenditure for FY ended March 31, 2013 was 5,900 million JPY, with most devoted to the automotive component business to improve productivity, reduce costs, and prepare facilities for new vehicle models.
Shiroki North America, Inc. in Tennessee, USA plans to install manufacturing equipment with an estimated investment of 1,209 million JPY from January to December 2013.
Shiroki-GA, LLC in Georgia, USA plans to install manufacturing equipment with an estimated investment of 413 million JPY from January to December 2013.
Shiroki-GT, LLC in Tennessee, USA plans to install manufacturing equipment with an estimated investment of 43 million JPY from January to December 2013.
Subsidiary Shiroki (Thailand), Co., Ltd. in Chonburi, Thailand plans to install manufacturing equipment with an estimated investment of 1,078 million JPY from January to December 2013.
Guangzhou Shiroki Corp. in Guangdong, China plans to install manufacturing equipment with an estimated investment of 684 million JPY from January to December 2013.
The company holds technology licensing agreements for seat recliners with Keiper GmbH & Co. (Germany) until March 31, 2014 and NHK Spring (Thailand) until December 9, 2013.
The company licenses door frames to Summit Laemchabang Auto Body Co. (Thailand) until December 31, 2014 and Asan Co., Ltd. (Korea) until project completion.
The company licenses window regulators to RG Brose (South Africa) and Brose do Brasil (Brazil) until March 31, 2015.
The company licenses seat recliners, seat adjusters, and window regulators to Technico Industries Ltd. (India) until mass production ceases.
source_title: SHIROKI Corporation  Business Report FY ended 2013 - MarkLines Automotive Industry Portal

facts: 3P Safety places BCSP-credentialed Site Safety Managers, Construction Health and Safety Technicians, and Site Safety Coordinators on Georgia EV projects within 48 hours of contract.
Hyundai Motor Group is building a $7.6 billion Metaplant America EV assembly facility in Ellabell (Bryan County, just outside Savannah).
An adjacent SK On battery joint venture is located next to the Hyundai Metaplant.
Rivian announced a $5 billion EV plant in Morgan and Walton counties.
Qcells is expanding its Dalton solar manufacturing complex, which is one of the largest solar manufacturing operations in the Western Hemisphere.
Aspen Aerogels operates a manufacturing facility in Statesboro producing insulation for EV batteries.
Suniva solar and the broader solar supply chain are active in Georgia.
The Port of Savannah is experiencing explosive container growth and continuous capital expansion at Garden City Terminal and the new Mason Mega Rail Terminal.
The Port of Brunswick (Colonel’s Island) is the second-largest U.S. roll-on/roll-off auto import terminal.
CSX intermodal, Norfolk Southern Inman Yard Atlanta, Amazon BFI (Fairburn / Stone Mountain), and the broader Atlanta distribution corridor drive logistics demand.
Lithium-ion battery construction in Georgia demands thermal runaway awareness, electrolyte handling, dry room contractor safety, and high-voltage commissioning.
3P Safety supplies BCSP-credentialed safety professionals for the Hyundai Metaplant America and adjacent Hyundai-SK On battery joint venture.
Staffing for EV battery construction includes expertise in lithium-ion thermal runaway awareness, electrolyte handling, dry room contractor safety, and high-voltage commissioning.

3P Safety covers data center construction in Douglas, Henry, Newton, Walton, and Cherokee counties for companies including Microsoft, Google, Meta, Amazon Web Services, Switch, Digital Realty, QTS, and CyrusOne.
Metro Atlanta data center expansion drives sustained safety staffing demand.
Data center construction demands NFPA 70E arc flash discipline, switchgear commissioning safety, and tight contractor controls.
Stellantis Detroit Mack South Carolina battery JV draws Georgia-based contractors.
3P Safety provides emergency and 24/7 coverage for EV battery commissioning surges on weekends and holidays.
Founder Patrick Salazar has worked safety on industrial and construction projects since 2005.
Standard deployment time for Georgia safety professionals is 48 hours, adjusted for military installation access.
Rates for Coastal Georgia reflect Hyundai Metaplant surge demand and Port of Savannah ongoing capital.
Rates for Augusta and CSRA reflect Savannah River Site nuclear contractor premiums plus Plant Vogtle support work.

Included services cover general liability and workers’ compensation insurance, professional and credential maintenance, weekly reporting, OSHA-compliant recordkeeping, toolbox talks, and incident investigation.

Additional costs apply for travel and per diem for out-of-region work, specialty PPE beyond standard issue, drug testing if site-required, base access processing fees, and after-hours emergency call-out outside contracted shifts.

Staff hold BCSP credentials including Certified Safety Professional (CSP), Associate Safety Professional (ASP), Construction Health and Safety Technician (CHST), Occupational Hygiene and Safety Technician (OHST), and Safety Trained Supervisor Construction (STSC).
Staff hold OSHA outreach and trainer credentials including OSHA 30-hour Construction, OSHA 30-hour General Industry, and OSHA 500 and 510 trainer credentials.
Equipment-specific certifications include NCCCO Mobile Crane, Tower Crane, Crane Inspector, Lift Director, Signalperson, Rigger, Telehandler / Overhead Crane, and Aerial and Scissor Lift (ANSI A92).

Specialty certifications include NFPA 70E Qualified Electrical Worker, Confined Space Entry Supervisor / Attendant, Excavation Competent Person, Scaffold Competent Person, Fall Protection Competent Person and Trainer, Silica Competent Person, RCRA / DOT HazMat shipping, Asbestos Building Inspector, Lead-Safe RRP, and ICRA / ILSM Healthcare Construction.

Staff are familiar with PSM Auditor familiarity, PSSR / MOC participation for refining-adjacent and chemical work, DOE 10 CFR 851 Worker Safety and Health Program for Savannah River Site contractors, and NRC nuclear orientation for Plant Vogtle support.
Staff hold HAZWOPER 40-hour and 8-hour refresher, ICS-100 / 200 / 700 / 800, AHA BLS, First Aid / CPR / AED credentials.

Documented heat illness prevention plan management with NIOSH WBGT criteria is provided for Georgia summer construction sites exceeding federal OSHA Heat NEP trigger thresholds from May through September.

Pre-storm jobsite securement plans, crane stand-down timing coordination, flood-prone site mitigation, and post-storm reentry safety assessments are required for coastal Georgia industrial corridors due to hurricane threats.
