facts: Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that TEKLAS, a Turkish advanced research and development, manufacturing company and supplier of electric vehicle (EV) parts, will invest $6.5 million in opening its first North American facility and headquarters in Gordon County.
The new facility will deliver 120 jobs to Calhoun.

Governor Kemp cited Georgia's highly skilled workforce, top-notch logistics network, and pro-business environment as key factors for TEKLAS's decision to join the state's growing electric vehicle parts industry.
TEKLAS was founded in Turkey in 1971.

The company designs, develops, and manufactures fluid circulation systems for cooling and heating applications, including hoses and pipes made from rubber, plastic, and metal for the global automotive industry.
TEKLAS utilizes highly innovative and automated manufacturing processes using advanced water molding technology.
TEKLAS USA will include assembly lines to serve prominent original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
TEKLAS USA operations are scheduled to start in the spring of 2021.
TEKLAS is headquartered in Turkey with locations in Bulgaria, Serbia, Mexico, and China, along with sales offices in Germany and France.
Regional Director Arcan Ergur stated that the new Calhoun, Georgia plant will help the company be closer to its main customers.
The new facility and headquarters will occupy 200,000 square feet.
The facility will be located at 320 South Industrial Boulevard in Calhoun, Georgia.
TEKLAS will hire for positions across a variety of skill levels, including advanced manufacturing, management, and supervision.
Larry Roye, chairman of the Development Authority of Gordon County, applauded the efforts of state and local partners who helped encourage the company to choose Calhoun.
Gordon County Chamber of Commerce President and CEO Kathy Johnson commended TEKLAS for its research and advancements in electric vehicle manufacturing.
The Calhoun and Gordon County community recognizes TEKLAS's contribution to the expansion of the automotive sector of the local manufacturing base.
GDEcD Project Manager Fernanda Kirchner represented the Global Commerce division on this competitive project.
The project was completed in partnership with the Economic Development Authority of Gordon County and Georgia Power.
The Center of Innovation for Logistics, a division of GDEcD, provided freight analysis to support the project and company planning.
GDEcD Commissioner Pat Wilson stated that the State of Georgia is focused on attracting and growing the jobs of the future.
TEKLAS will establish its North American headquarters and build its very first U.S. advanced manufacturing facility in Calhoun.

Commissioner Wilson noted that as the automotive industry transforms toward electric vehicles and e-mobility, the state will foster an ecosystem of manufacturers and suppliers on the leading edge of this transition.
TEKLAS is a global automotive fluid circulation system supplier for major car original equipment manufacturers (OEMs).
The company has more than 45 years of experience serving customers with design, development, and production capabilities.
TEKLAS provides innovative solutions for thermoplastics, rubber, and metal products to its customers.
source_title: Georgia Chron - Electric Vehicle Manufacturer TEKLAS to Open North American Headquarters in Georgia, Create 120 Jobs in Calhoun - 10089519

facts: Textron Inc. operates in the aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses worldwide across six segments: Textron Aviation, Bell, Textron Systems, Industrial, Textron eAviation, and Finance.
Kautex Textron has secured a new business award for its Pentatonic battery enclosure solution, further strengthening its position in the growing electrified passenger vehicle segment.
Textron E-Z-GO LLC operates out of Augusta, Georgia.
Textron announced its intent to separate its Industrial segment from its core aerospace and defense businesses to enhance strategic focus and drive long-term value.
source_title: Textron (TXT) Stock Price & Overview

facts: Augusta, Georgia-based Textron Specialized Vehicles has recalled about 34,000 E-Z-GO RXV Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTV) or golf carts due to injury and crash hazards.
According to the Consumer Product Safety Commission, the PTV's steering knuckles and A-arms can break during or after an impact and cause loss of steering control, posing crash and injury hazards.
The company received 335 reports of incidents of the steering knuckles and A-arms on the PTVs breaking, including one incident that resulted in two reports of abrasion injuries.
The recall includes model year 2022 and 2023 Freedom and Valor E-Z-GO RXV personal transportation vehicles.
Recalled units have serial numbers within the range of 5656874 through 5656886 and 5659215 through 5700741.
The company has asked its customers to immediately stop using the recalled PTVs and contact Textron Specialized Vehicles for a free repair.
The recalled golf carts were sold at dealerships nationwide and online as well from January 2022 through September 2022 for between $10,000 and $17,000.
source_title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Recalls 34K Golf Carts

facts: Textron Inc. is cutting 725 jobs as part of a global restructuring plan, the company announced late Tuesday.

The company’s board of directors approved a restructuring plan that will reduce operating expenses while downsizing 2% of its total global workforce across its Industrial, Bell and Textron Systems segments.
Textron Inc. spokesperson Michael Maynard said the layoffs do not include corporate employees.

Layoff plans at both the Bell and Textron systems segments include targeted headcount reductions to improve the segments’ cost structures plus a realignment of workforces as these segments transition from legacy production contracts to more development, engineering-focused contracts.
The company’s Bell segment is located in Fort Worth, Texas.

Layoffs in the Industrial segment will occur at the company’s Georgia-based Textron Specialized Vehicles due to a lower demand for some of the company’s power sports products, according to the SEC filing.
Jobs cuts will also commence at its German motor vehicle manufacturer Kautex due to reduced demand for fuel systems from European automotive manufacturers.
Textron expects to save $75 million annually after this restructuring, which is expected to be completed in the first half of 2024.
On Oct 26, Textron reported a third quarter profit of $269 million, an increase from $225 million one year prior.

Revenue totaled $3.34 billion in the quarter, a rise from $3 billion one year prior but still missing Wall Street forecasts; four analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research expected $3.45 billion.
Textron Aviation reported $1.3 billion in revenue in the quarter, an increase from $1.1 billion the year prior, reflecting higher volume and mix and higher pricing.
Textron Aviation delivered 39 jets in the quarter, flat with last year, and 38 commercial turboprops, up from 33 in last year’s third quarter.

Bell segment revenue totaled $754 million, flat with the third quarter of 2022, attributed to lower commercial helicopter volume, largely reflecting supply chain constraints, partially offset by higher military volume.

Textron systems revenue was $309 million, up $17 million from last year’s third quarter, largely reflecting higher volume; segment profit of $41 million was up $10 million, compared with the third quarter of 2022, primarily due to a favorable impact from performance of $8 million.

Industrial segment revenue was $922 million, up $73 million from last year’s third quarter, largely due to higher volume and mix of $45 million at both product lines and an $18 million favorable impact from pricing, principally in the specialized vehicles product line.
source_title: Textron cutting 725 jobs as part of global restructuring plan

facts: Textron Specialized Vehicles Inc. is a Textron Inc. (NYSE: TXT) company based in Augusta, Ga.

The company announced plans to its dealer network regarding the incorporation of Arctic Cat into the company, including future powersports brand portfolio and upcoming products across snowmobile, side-by-side, and ATV lines.
Company President and CEO Kevin Holleran welcomed a select gathering of employees and dealers in Texas and outlined synergies between Arctic Cat and Textron.

Arctic Cat joins a portfolio that includes E-Z-GO golf cars, Textron Off Road side-by-sides, Cessna and Beechcraft fixed-wing aircraft, Bell helicopters, Greenlee professional tools, and Kautex automotive fuel systems.
TSV Vice President of Engineering Chris Spencer spent more than two decades at Bell Helicopter as a lead engineer on military programs such as the Bell-Boeing V-22 Osprey.
All side-by-sides and ATVs will transition to become part of the Textron Off Road brand.
The Arctic Cat brand will remain in use for high-performance snowmobile product lines.
Arctic Cat's legacy in side-by-sides and ATVs will live on through Alterra™, Wildcat™, and Prowler™ products, which will join Textron Off Road's existing Stampede® side-by-side.

The decision to rebrand side-by-sides and ATVs under the Textron Off Road name resulted from consultation and research with consumers and dealers showing it offers the best growth opportunity, particularly outside the snow belt.

A new unnamed crossover side-by-side under Textron Off Road will be released in fall 2017, featuring 100-plus horsepower, King Shocks suspension, bucket seats, extended cab, and industry-leading usable storage.

The next-generation Wildcat XX sport side-by-side will be released in spring 2018, powered by a 998cc triple engine generating 125hp, featuring King shocks suspension with 18-inches of travel, 30-inch tires on 15-inch KMC wheels, and full doors.
E-Z-GO models offer dealers a new customer base seeking personal golf cars and transportation vehicles.
TSV president and general manager for snow and international Erik Nelson stated Textron's engineering expertise will accelerate innovation in Arctic Cat snowmobiles.
New snowmobile models include the M 8000 and ZR 8000 with a new 800cc C-TEC2 two-stroke twin engine, and the ZR200 youth model.
The dealer event took place in an airplane hangar at Fort Worth’s Alliance Airport in Texas.
Textron Specialized Vehicles markets products under brands including E-Z-GO®, Cushman®, Arctic Cat®, Textron Off Road™, Jacobsen®, Dixie Chopper®, Ransomes®, TUG™, Douglas™, Premier™ and Safeaero™.
Textron Inc. is a multi-industry company leveraging aircraft, defense, industrial, and finance businesses globally.
source_title: RELEASE: Textron Officials Outline Plans For Arctic Cat At Dealer Event | Snow Goer

facts: Textron plans to separate its Industrial segment as it moves to sharpen its focus on aerospace and defense businesses led by Textron Aviation, Bell and Textron Systems.
The company intends to shed its Industrial unit, which includes Kautex and Textron Specialized Vehicles, either through a sale or a tax-free separation into a standalone publicly traded company.

The planned separation would leave the remaining company as a standalone aerospace and defense business built around Cessna and Beechcraft, Bell military and commercial rotorcraft, and Textron Systems’ defense and aerospace products.
The company referred to the post-separation aerospace and defense business as “New Textron,” though it did not confirm the final company name.

CEO Lisa Atherton stated that the planned separation creates greater clarity and focus for both businesses, positioning New Textron as a pure-play aerospace and defense company for higher growth while giving Industrial independence to pursue distinct strategies.
The separation is intended to give each business more flexibility to pursue its own capital allocation strategy, product development priorities, and growth opportunities.
The aerospace and defense business is expected to generate more than $12 billion in 2026 revenue and hold a $19 billion backlog after the separation.

Textron expects the separation to improve its revenue growth profile and operating margins while continuing to maintain a strong balance sheet and invest in research and development and capital expenditures.
The Industrial segment is expected to generate more than $3 billion in 2026 revenue.
Kautex supplies plastic fuel systems, battery enclosures, and clear-vision systems for the automotive industry.
Textron Specialized Vehicles brands include E-Z-GO, PACE Technologies, Jacobsen, and TUG Technologies.
Executive Chairman Scott Donnelly stated that separating Industrial would sharpen Textron’s strategic focus and support long-term shareholder value creation.
Textron is targeting completion of the separation within 12 to 18 months.
The timing and structure of the separation remain subject to customary conditions, regulatory approvals, and final board approval.
source_title: Textron to shed industrial unit

ADVICS has development, manufacturing, sales, and distribution bases spread across the globe.
ADVICS aims to be the brake manufacturing group that satisfies all customers worldwide.
ADVICS provides products that support electrification and autonomous driving, as well as carbon neutrality.
ADVICS contributes to solving social issues to create a sustainable society.

facts: Czech-owned provider of gas cylinders held a grand opening of a new distribution facility in Flowery Branch on Tuesday.

Atlanta Cylinders Inc. is the subsidiary of Cylinders Holding a.s., whose facility in Ostrava, Czech Republic, is among the largest in the world for production of steel cylinders used to hold and transport technical gases, hydrogen, and compressed natural gas.
The company arrived in Georgia through diplomatic urging and pull factors rather than standard local booster sales pitches.

Atlanta Cylinders is importing its Czech-made cylinder bundles into a Hall County warehouse located under the same roof as Albaform Inc., a Czech-owned provider of seat frames to automotive suppliers around the South.
The facility is located at 5405 Rafe Banks Drive in Flowery Branch, Georgia.
Albaform CEO and owner Monika Vintrlikova, who also serves as the honorary consul of the Czech Republic in Georgia, welcomed attendees to the reception.
Monika Vintrlikova and her husband Jan Vintrlik started Albaform’s U.S. journey at that brick building exactly 10 years ago.
Czech trailblazers in Georgia like Albaform and Silon LLC in Peachtree City are helping sell the state back in their home country, prompting more companies to take a serious look.
Monika Vintrlikova stated a goal of bringing more Czech direct investment to Hall County.

Miloslav Stašek, Czech ambassador to the United States, made his fourth visit to Georgia in recent months and has become a proponent of the state’s business environment after meeting with Gov. Brian Kemp and others.
The ambassador noted extremely friendly conditions make the metro area a prime hub for firms breaking into the U.S. and that they are searching for synergies among Czech companies.

Albaform uses industrial gases in its robotic welding processes and supplies products to larger assembly plants, overlapping with Cylinder Holding as they explore a business partnership beyond warehouse renting.
Cylinders Holding CEO and Chairman Jan Světlík said entering the U.S. market has been a long-held objective since the company started selling here in 1996.
After a 2008 mishap in China where thieves stole the company’s designs, Cylinders Holding swore off that country and sought to break into the U.S. to boost market share and be closer to customers.
Ambassador Stašek had known the company executives for years and began discussing opening doors for the Czech firm in Georgia before taking his posting.
Consolidation in the gas cylinder industry has left remaining domestic players with little incentive to innovate, putting the U.S. behind on technology, according to Mr. Světlík.
Atlanta Cylinders hopes to partner with domestic manufacturers to provide cylinders that are lighter and boast greater capacity, helping them save time and money on fuel and refilling.
Mr. Světlík stated American manufacturers are not the competition; Chinese manufacturers with government-subsidized pricing are, emphasizing support for remaining U.S. steel cylinder manufacturers.

The company sells DOT cylinders widely used in the U.S., as well as UN/ISO standard cylinders commonly used in Europe that can be imported into the U.S., which are usually larger and withstand higher internal pressures.
Cylinder Holding sells these individually or in large modular arrays, adhering to the highest safety standards.
Mr. Světlík emphasized security as his No. 1 priority to avoid unpleasant surprises.

Vladislav Smrz, chief operations officer for Atlanta Cylinders, said the company wants to help the industry develop in the U.S. and bring the best technology to move it from the 20th century to the 21st century.
In addition to cylinders, Atlanta Cylinder offers technology to track and remotely monitor containers with bundles of cylinders.

While heavy steel extrusion will not happen in the U.S. in the short term, the company is looking to bring lighter manufacturing stateside, including racks that corral cylinders into bundles for transport.
The company has begun talking with Georgia Tech about educational collaborations and is working closely with Hall County to build a local workforce.

Tim Evans, vice president at the Greater Hall Chamber of Commerce, noted that the community benefits when companies like Albaform become de facto ambassadors in their home countries about growth experienced in Georgia.
Hall County has about 60 international subsidiaries, ranging from small headquarters to Kubota Corp., which has 2,500 employees and plans to add 500 more.
Atlanta Cylinders hopes to deepen its reach into Canada and Mexico from its local operation, serving not only the U.S. but also these neighboring countries.
source_title: Czech Investment Grows in Georgia as Cylinder Firm Opens in Hall County - Global Atlanta

facts: Jan Vintrlik is based in Flowery Branch, GA, USA.
He currently serves as President at ALBAform Inc.
He holds a Master of Business Administration (MBA) from Nottingham Trent University.
He holds a Master of Science (MSc) from Nottingham Trent University.
His professional background includes previous positions at SOPO US, Czech North American Chamber of Commerce & Culture, ALBA-METAL, spol. s r.o., and ALBA - METAL.
His competencies include business strategy, automotive, manufacturing, strategic planning, and lean manufacturing.

facts: The company offers competitive wages and growth opportunities.
Employees receive paid time off including vacation, holiday, and sickness leave.
A 401K retirement plan is available with a discretionary year-end company match.
Early access to wages and loan programs is provided to employees.
Insurance benefits include company-subsidized health and prescription coverage, dental, vision, and more.
The company pays for life insurance and long-term disability.
Flexible spending accounts (FSA) for medical and dependent care are available.
An Employee Assistance Program with confidential 24/7 availability is offered.
Additional policies are available for accident, disability, and life coverage.
Employees have easy access to company information via an online portal and mobile app.
A recognition program offers bonuses for excellence in service.
A recognition program offers bonuses for years of service.
A recognition program offers bonuses for perfect attendance.
The company provides company lunches and other perks.
Discounts on many products and services are available to employees.
Job applicants must type the position and shift they are applying for in the message box along with detailed work history or attach a resume.
Applicants will receive a confirmation email after submitting their application.
Work authorization is required for I-9 and E-Verify compliance.
ALBAform is seeking a highly skilled CNC Setter role.
The CNC Setter position requires hands-on experience setting up and adjusting CNC machinery, precision focus, and the ability to write or edit programs based on blueprints and engineering data.
The CNC Setter status is Regular Full Time and eligible for benefits.
Starting pay for the CNC Setter is $18.50 per hour plus a shift differential.
The shift differential pay is $2.00 per hour for the 3rd shift.
The first year pathway range is $18.50 to $22.50 per hour plus shift differential.
The 8-hour shift includes one 30-minute paid break.
Candidates must be able to work an 11PM to 7AM schedule Monday through Friday.
Candidates must be able to work weekend overtime if required.
The position requires prolonged standing, walking, bending, stooping, reaching, lifting, pushing, grasping, pinching, visual checks, and working in a non-temperature-controlled warehouse environment.
Punctuality and excellent attendance are mandatory.
Candidates must be engaged to learning and applying themselves.
The work environment measures productivity, quality, attendance, and safety.
Employees must meet and maintain company standard requirements.
Proper PPE must be worn during all working hours.
Candidates must be capable of performing each essential duty satisfactorily, as the requirements represent the necessary knowledge, skills, and abilities.
source_title: Careers | ALBAform
