facts: Denso Corporation is a global automotive components manufacturer headquartered in Kariya, Aichi Prefecture, Japan.
The company was founded on December 16, 1949, after becoming independent from Toyota Motor Corporation as Nippon Denso Co. Ltd.
About 25% of the company is owned by Toyota Motor Corporation, which holds a 21.25% stake, while Toyota Industries holds a 5.59% stake.
The company's parent organization is the Toyota Group.
As of the year ending March 2016, sales to the Toyota Group accounted for less than 50% of total revenue, with 44% originating from other car manufacturers in Japan, Germany, the U.S., and China.
In 2023, Denso was ranked as the second largest auto parts supplier in the world.
In 2022, Denso was listed at #278 on the Fortune Global 500 list with total revenue of $49.0 billion and 167,950 employees.
As of 2021, the company operates subsidiaries including 64 in Japan, 23 in North America, 32 in Europe, 74 in Asia, and seven in Oceania and other regions.

The company develops and manufactures various auto parts including gasoline and diesel engine components, hybrid vehicle components, climate control systems, instrument clusters, airbag systems, pre-crash radar systems, and spark plugs.
Denso also develops non-automotive components such as household heating equipment and industrial robots.
In June 2020, Denso announced the opening of its Electrification Innovation Center at its plant in Anjō to support development of products and technologies for electric and hybrid vehicles.

As of 2014, global sales breakdown included thermal systems (30.4%), powertrain control systems (35.0%), electronic systems (15.3%), electric systems (9.4%), electric motors (7.0%), other automotive products (1.4%), industrial and consumer products (1.1%), and other non-automotive products (0.4%).
Denso Wave is a subsidiary that produces automatic identification products, industrial robots, and programmable logic controllers.
Engineer Masahiro Hara invented the two-dimensional QR code in 1994 while working at Denso Wave to streamline automotive component tracking.
Denso International America is the American subsidiary of Denso, which employs over 17,000 people at 38 locations across North, Central, and South America.
DENSO Sales California, Inc., was founded in Hawthorne, California, in March 1971 to promote air conditioner systems for Japanese-made vehicles.
In May 1975, Denso Corporation opened a sales division in Southfield, Michigan.
In September 1975, Denso International America opened a service center in Cedar Falls, Iowa, due to an agricultural parts contract with John Deere that included starter motors and meters.
At year end on March 31, 2008, combined sales for all American locations totaled $8.3 billion.

Denso manufactures customized electronics and auto-parts specifically for Toyota-Lexus motorsports development divisions, including spark plugs, starter motors, fuel pumps, alternators, Engine Control Module computer systems, and engine and transmission sensors.
The Toyota TS030 Hybrid used a Denso kinetic energy recovery system to finish second in the 2013 24 Hours of Le Mans.

On January 30, 2012, the US Justice Department announced that Denso agreed to pay a $78 million fine for its role in a massive price-fixing scheme with Yazaki that fixed prices and allocated components to car manufacturers like Toyota and Honda.

In August 2020, a class-action lawsuit was filed in Quebec over alleged defective fuel pumps in Acura, Honda, Lexus, Subaru, and Toyota vehicle models, with a separate lawsuit filed for the rest of Canada.
source_title: Denso - Wikipedia

facts: DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee will expand its automotive components and systems manufacturing plant in Maryville, making it one of the largest operating campuses in DENSO Corporation’s worldwide operations.
The company currently has a workforce of more than 3,100 employees and is Blount County’s largest employer.
DENSO will create 500 new jobs as part of this expansion.
The company will invest $400 million to expand manufacturing capabilities and construct a new warehouse.
Due to expanded production needs at two manufacturing plants, existing warehouses will be converted to manufacturing space.
A new consolidated warehouse will be built on the company’s current campus.
Tennessee Governor Bill Haslam congratulated DENSO on the expansion and the creation of 500 new jobs in Maryville and Blount County.
Economic and Community Development Commissioner Randy Boyd noted this is the second DENSO expansion at a Tennessee facility in less than a year.

DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee President Jack Helmboldt, who also serves as a DENSO Corporation global executive director, thanked skilled associates, state and local leaders, the Blount Chamber Partnership, and TVA for their support.
DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee produces starters, alternators, instrument clusters, and a wide range of electronic components and systems for the automotive industry.
The Maryville campus consists of four manufacturing plants on 194 acres and has undergone eight major expansions.
U.S. Sen. Lamar Alexander stated that the auto industry has spread into 80 of Tennessee's 95 counties, becoming a main contributor to raising family incomes.
Blount County Mayor Ed Mitchell highlighted collaborative efforts with the state, the cities of Alcoa and Maryville, and local partners in supporting the expansion.
Maryville City Mayor Tom Taylor praised DENSO as a community partner for creating jobs and giving back to the region.
To celebrate its 25 years in Maryville, DENSO Manufacturing Tennessee donated $50,000 to Blount Memorial Hospital and $50,000 to East Tennessee Children’s Hospital.
Alcoa City Mayor Don Mull welcomed the opportunity to further the relationship with DENSO for the next 25 years.
TVA Senior Vice President of Economic Development John Bradley congratulated DENSO on adding 500 new jobs and noted partnerships with TNECD and the Blount Partnership.
Blount County Economic Development Board Chairman Fred Lawson stated the investment nearly doubles the capital investment Blount County has enjoyed since 2010.
Maryville and Blount County are represented in the Tennessee General Assembly by Sen. Doug Overbey, Rep. Art Swann, and Rep. Bob Ramsey.

DENSO Corp., headquartered in Kariya, Aichi prefecture, Japan, is a leading global automotive supplier of advanced technology, systems, and components in thermal, powertrain control, electronics, information, and safety.
DENSO's customers include all the world's major carmakers.
Worldwide, DENSO has more than 200 subsidiaries and affiliates in 38 countries and regions and employs more than 140,000 people.
Consolidated global sales for DENSO for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2015, totaled US$35.9 billion.
Last fiscal year, DENSO spent 9.2 percent of its global consolidated sales on research and development.
DENSO common stock is traded on the Tokyo and Nagoya stock exchanges.
In North America, DENSO employs more than 22,000 people at 30 consolidated companies and affiliates.
Of DENSO's North American facilities, 25 are manufacturing plants located in the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

In the United States alone, DENSO employs more than 14,000 people across California, Michigan, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Kentucky, Georgia, Iowa, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Texas, and Arkansas.
DENSO’s North American consolidated sales totaled US$8.0 billion for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2015.
Tennessee was named 2014 and 2013 “State of the Year” for economic development by Business Facilities magazine.

The Tennessee Department of Economic and Community Development seeks to attract new corporate investment and facilitate expansion to make Tennessee the No. 1 location in the Southeast for high quality jobs.
source_title: Governor Haslam, Commissioner Boyd Announce Denso Manufacturing Tennessee, Inc. To Expand Maryville Operations - TNECD

DENSO invests in around 170 facilities worldwide to produce cutting-edge electrification, powertrain, thermal, and mobility electronics products.

facts: Georgia’s largest manufacturing presence is in the food-processing sector, employing over 60,000 people across producers like Coca-Cola, Anheuser-Busch, Frito-Lay, and General Mills.
Transportation is the state’s second largest manufacturing industry, with companies like Kia, Porsche, and Auto Zone operating across Georgia.
Aerospace manufacturers in Georgia include Gulfstream and Lockheed Martin, while heavy machinery includes Caterpillar, John Deere, and Kubota.
Over 90 percent of Georgia’s exports come from its manufacturing sector, mainly going to Canada, China, Mexico, Japan, and the United Kingdom.
Tom Croteau states that billions of dollars worth of goods are exported globally as a result of dedicated, skilled manufacturing in Georgia.
Governor Nathan Deal launched the Georgia Competitiveness Initiative in 2011 to bring state government and business communities together for long-term economic development strategy.
The initiative sought to improve competitiveness by asking the private sector what the state could do to help businesses thrive.
Feedback was received from over 4,000 individuals regarding the Georgia Competitiveness Initiative.
An overriding issue identified in the feedback was the state’s sales tax on electricity and energy, which the state decided to eliminate.

Three years later, GDEcD and private industry teamed up through Governor Deal’s High Demand Career Initiative to address a skilled workforce shortage due to Baby Boomer retirements over the next five to ten years.

The High Demand Career Initiative began with three large statewide meetings personally chaired by Governor Deal, involving private industry leaders and university/technical college/Board of Regents leaders.
Companies were asked about their workforce needs for today, five years from now, and ten years from now.
The resounding response from companies was a need for a skilled workforce capable of keeping up with emerging technology and automation globally.
The initiative targets the educational system to generate awareness and interest in manufacturing careers and ensure graduates have degrees/diplomas matching modern manufacturing needs.
The High Demand Career Initiative uses a multifaceted plan targeting students at college, high school, and middle school levels.
A key issue identified is the younger generation's relative uninformed status regarding modern manufacturing careers.
Generating interest and awareness at the middle school level into high school is seen as crucial for shaping career decisions.
Steps taken include implementing apprenticeships at the high school level and integrating manufacturing-centric skills into core curriculum.
Governor Deal recommended amending state policy to allow computer programming courses to satisfy core requirements (math, science, or foreign language) for a high school diploma.
A press release stated that more than half of the projected job growth in STEM fields will be in computing occupations.
Training in computing must begin prior to post-secondary education to fill rapidly increasing high-wage, high-skill positions.
The Board of Regents of the University System of Georgia announced a new ‘Manufacturing Engineering’ degree to be available at Georgia Southern University.
Manufacturers consistently reported a need for more engineers on plant floors in manufacturing and maintenance areas.
Assistant engineers are needed where a 4-year degree isn't required, but a technical college degree could help solve minor technical issues.
GDEcD worked closely with Walmart via their Made in America campaign to assist international suppliers in moving operations back to the U.S. or helping existing U.S. businesses grow.
Governor Deal stated that they are working to keep Georgia the number one place in the nation for business and need a strong education system responding to company needs.
Tom Croteau serves as Deputy Commissioner, Global Commerce for GDEcD, leading the team to increase jobs/investment through locating/expanding businesses and growing small businesses.
The Global Commerce team includes statewide/existing industry project teams, small business, and Georgia’s international offices in ten strategic regions globally.
Croteau joined GDEcD in 1996 and previously served as Division Director of Projects, Global Commerce.

Recent company locations/expansions assisted by Croteau include Pilgrim’s Pride (Douglas-Coffee County), Medline Industries (Lithia Springs-Douglas County), Dollar Tree (Savannah), DeWaffelbakkers (McDonough-Henry County), and Starbucks (Augusta).
Prior to GDEcD, Croteau worked in economic development divisions of MEAG Power (currently Electric Cities of Georgia) and the Metro Atlanta Chamber of Commerce.
Croteau also worked for Ernst & Young and the Florida Chamber of Commerce.
Croteau currently serves on the board of the Georgia Economic Developers Association and the Georgia Quick Start Industry Services Advisory Council.
Croteau completed Georgia Tech’s Economic Development Course and attended the Economic Development Institute.
Croteau holds a degree in Economics from Florida State University.
source_title: In High Demand - Industry Today - Leader in Manufacturing & Industry News

DMNC produces condensers, radiators, heater cores, evaporators, and HVAC units for the automotive industry.

DENSO Manufacturing North Carolina, Inc. was founded in 1988.

facts: Georgia exported $182,249,506 worth of goods to Israel in 2015.
Total exports from Georgia to Israel between 1996 and the present totaled $2,801,689,491.
Israel ranked as Georgia's 41st trade partner in 2015.
Military contracts with Israel in 2015 were valued at $46,232,283.

The American-Israel Chamber of Commerce Southeast Division (AICC-SD) was established in Atlanta in 1992 to help Israeli businesses explore markets and develop relationships with companies in Georgia, Alabama, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, and Tennessee.
AICC-SD boasts over 450 members and has facilitated completed transactions valued at over $700 million.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) established an Israel Office in Jerusalem in 1994 to promote trade, investment, and tourism.
Governor Zell Miller created the Georgia-Israel Exchange in 1992 to explore emerging technology in industry and agriculture and enhance trade.
In March 2011, the GDEcD planned a mission to Israel focusing on multi-industry trade opportunities in environmental, telecom, advanced manufacturing, and alternative energy industries.
Governor Sonny Perdue led a delegation of 36 Georgians on a technology and trade mission to Israel in November 2005.
Georgia is one of 33 states that have cooperative agreements with Israel.
In 2012, Georgia exported over $158,802,552 worth of manufacturing goods to Israel.
Since 1996, Georgia exports to Israel totaled more than $2,392,988,141, making Israel Georgia's 15th leading trade partner.
In 2012, Georgia received over $97,403,798 in foreign military financing (FMF) for U.S. military aid to Israel.
Companies that received FMF funding include GE Intelligence Platforms Technology in Atlanta, Hewlett-Packard Company in Atlanta, and Horthrop Grumman Support Services in Warner Robins.
More than 100 Georgia companies do business in Israel, including BellSouth, CocaCola, Home Depot, and Holiday Inn.
Apica Cardiovascular, a combined Georgia Tech and Emory University medical-device startup, received a $5.1 million investment in June 2010 from investors including TriVentures of Herziliyah, Israel.
In January 2011, Canton-based J&S Chemicals formed a distribution agreement for specialty chemical products in the Israeli market through Ra'anana-based Orian International Marketing, Ltd.

Ami Bental, President of Systematic Controls in Kennesaw, worked out a partnership agreement to manufacture instrumentation and control systems for Israeli industry with a kibbutz on the Golan Heights.
BellSouth was part of a consortium that won the tender to operate the second cellular phone network in Israel, providing service to approximately 900,000 subscribers.
Advance Building Supply opened the first Ace hardware store in Israel.
At least three industry-specific exchanges between Israel and Georgia have been organized by the AICC to promote health care, telecommunications, and software incubators.
26 Israeli companies now have U.S. or regional headquarters in Georgia.
The Binational Industrial Research and Development Foundation (BIRD) funds joint U.S.-Israeli teams in developing innovative technological products.
Since its inception, BIRD has funded more than 800 joint high-tech R&D projects through conditional grants totaling more than $210 million.
Products from BIRD ventures have generated more than $8 billion in revenues and created an estimated 20,000 American jobs.
Georgia companies have benefited from close to one million dollars in BIRD grants over the last three decades.
Four Georgia companies have received BIRD grants.
ARRIS (Suwanee) and Resolute Networks Ltd. developed an integrated end-to-end solution for Multi-Service Operators based on a BIRD Foundation grant.
The Binational Science Foundation (BSF), established in 1972, supports collaborative research projects between U.S. and Israeli scientists.
Since its inception, BSF has awarded some $480 million through more than 4,000 grants.
Institutions in Georgia have shared with their Israeli counterparts nearly $3 million in BSF grants awarded since 1996 alone.
The University of Georgia, Georgia Tech, and Emory are among the BSF grant recipients.
Albert Padwa of Emory has collaborated with a collaborator from Bar-Ilan University on alkaloid synthesis for cancer inhibitors and antibiotic properties.
The Binational Agricultural Research and Development Fund (BARD) was created in 1978 to fund programs between U.S. and Israeli scientists for agricultural research.
Since its inception, BARD has funded more than 1,000 projects with a total investment of more than $250 million.
Georgia institutions have shared grants worth more than $2.6 million since 1979, with the University of Georgia receiving the largest share.
Edward Law, a biological and agricultural engineer at the University of Georgia's Athens campus, received two BARD grants for an electrostatic sprayer system and research into pollen transfer.

source_title: Dorsett Industries, Inc. | The Org

facts: Enplas (USA) Inc. is located in Marietta, GA.
The company is a leading provider of injection molded plastics products and services.
Enplas specializes in the production of injection molded plastics products known for their precision, durability, and functionality.
The company possesses extensive expertise in manufacturing custom injection molded plastic components tailored to specific client requirements.
Products range from small intricate parts to large complex assemblies.
Enplas provides injection molded plastic parts for the automotive industry.
Automotive product applications include interior and exterior components, lighting systems, electrical connectors, and more.
Products are designed to meet durability, heat resistance, and compliance with industry standards.

The company possesses extensive knowledge of a wide range of engineering-grade plastics and assists clients in selecting suitable materials based on strength, durability, chemical resistance, and regulatory compliance.
Enplas works closely with trusted material suppliers to source high-quality materials.
source_title: Enplas (USA) Inc. | Injection Molded Plastic

facts: EVCO Plastics is a global plastic injection molder with world-class facilities in the United States, Mexico and China.
The company applies consistent and stringent ISO-certified standards in every location.
Each facility is staffed locally, offering expertise in plastic injection molding, tooling, production, quality and logistics.
Each facility is fully supported and connected with a centralized design and engineering team.
All facilities provide the same high level of quality and service regardless of where products are produced.

The company uses a global project management system that allows OEMs to develop projects locally in the United States and then source and manufacture them in any one or more globally located facilities.
OEMs save shipping costs by molding large parts close to where they are needed.
Smaller parts requiring more assembly or a greater amount of labor may be more cost-effective produced in Mexico or China.
In some instances, there are advantages to running production in more than one location.
Each facility is equipped for contract manufacturing, enabling local sourcing and cost savings.
All molds are designed and built such that any molds built in the United States or China can run at any of the company's facilities.
The company provides a globally focused plastic injection molding system equipped to provide best cost savings and competitive advantage.
source_title: Global Plastics Manufacturing | US, Mexico & China | EVCO Plastics

facts: EVCO Plastics is a plastics processing company with nine manufacturing facilities located throughout the world.
The company established a holistic education program called EVCO Educates to combat skilled labor shortages in the plastics industry.
The cornerstone of the program is the EVCO Educates internship targeting college sophomores, juniors, or recent graduates of two-year technical schools.
The summer internship focuses on automation, project engineering, quality engineering, design and manufacturing engineering, emphasizing practical applications on real customer projects.
Dale Evans, President of EVCO Plastics, stated that part of the company's mission is to embrace and foster professional growth for both new industry entrants and current employees.
The EVCO Educates program started about eight to 10 months prior to the January 2, 2018 article date.

Rich Duval, Director of Quality and Human Resources at EVCO Plastics, explained the program is based on recruiting experiences from staff and aims to change their approach to attract needed demographics.
EVCO developed a YouTube video for its website to help potential applicants understand the company before applying.
The company implemented an employee referral program where employees receive rewards ranging from $500 to $2,000 depending on the job if they refer someone who is hired.
EVCO's first recruiting effort under this program was scheduled for February 2018.
The program will be implemented at all of EVCO's U.S. sites, with challenges varying by facility location.
In Deforest, Wisconsin, local unemployment is about 2.2%, while areas up toward Green Bay see 2.6% unemployment.
In North Georgia, unemployment is higher at 4.8%, but the company faces the same recruiting challenges as in Wisconsin.
In Georgia, EVCO utilizes a Georgia Work Ready program to ensure employees have access to training and possess core job skills.
EVCO Educates offers continuous skill enhancement opportunities, including grant-funded training sessions through the technical college system.
The company contracts for custom classes through Technical College Training.
A Supply Base Training program was implemented where EVCO invites suppliers to conduct on-site training regarding materials and colorants.
The company offers tuition reimbursement and pay-for-performance programs, tying higher pay to increased responsibility as employees grow in their roles.
EVCO benchmarked other local manufacturers to gauge benefits for retirement, vacation, and "new baby" leave.
The company provides four weeks of paid leave for new moms and two weeks for new dads, which is in addition to a general Paid Time Off policy.
EVCO is actively seeking automation and maintenance people and moldmakers, noting these are high-paying jobs.
The company tries to grow positions running molding machines and process/technical work internally.
EVCO works with local polytechnic schools and offers plant tours to ensure local students know the company's offerings.
source_title: EVCO Plastics takes aim at skilled labor shortage with EVCO Educates

entity_name: Fictiv and EVCO Plastics Strategic Partnership
facts: Fictiv created a strategic partnership with EVCO Plastics to revolutionize injection molding services for U.S. OEMs.
The alliance combines Fictiv's digitally connected global supply chain with EVCO's extensive manufacturing capabilities to efficiently bring innovations from concept to scale production.
EVCO provides over 1 million square feet of manufacturing space and operates hundreds of injection molding machines, including press sizes as big as 4,400 tons.
The partnership leverages deep expertise in design for manufacturability (DFM), AI, human-powered materials data and insights, program management, and global logistics to mitigate tariff concerns.
Fictiv addresses complexities in Mexico's emerging manufacturing ecosystem, which is currently fragmented for tooling and specialized secondary processes.
Fictiv GlobalFlex integrates tooling inserts produced in China with next-generation manufacturing facilities in the U.S. and Mexico to enhance gross margins and accelerate production timelines.
The collaboration supports the entire product lifecycle, encompassing 3D printing, injection molding, assembly, testing, and packaging.
Advanced capabilities include overmolding, gas-assist molding, in-mold decorating, and liquid silicone rubber (LSR) production.
The partnership holds certifications including ISO 9001:2015, ISO 13485, IATF 16949, and FDA compliance to ensure quality and reliability throughout the supply chain.

Dave Evans, Co-Founder and CEO of Fictiv, stated the partnership simplifies global manufacturing supply chains by reducing the overall number of suppliers involved in managing multiple supplier challenges.

Carlos Gonzales, head of EVCO Plastics de Mexico, noted that Fictiv's capabilities allow them to concentrate on manufacturing excellence while handling complex workflow and supply chain challenges that impact OEM margins.
The collaboration aims to provide scalable, tailored solutions with a shared commitment to innovation, quality, and sustainability in the manufacturing sector.

Fictiv operates four global manufacturing centers in India, Mexico, China, and the U.S., having delivered more than 35 million commercial and prototype parts to date for early-stage companies and large enterprises.

EVCO Plastics has 13 manufacturing facilities strategically located throughout the world, was founded in 1964, employs over 2,100 people, and operates 270 injection molding machines ranging from 28 to 4,400 tons.
source_title: Fictiv And EVCO Plastics Announce Strategic Partnership To Deliver End-To-End Injection Molding Supply Chain Solutions

facts: Fanello Industries is located at 50 E Main Street, Lavonia, GA 30553, United States.
The company supplies Carbon Steel Plate Sheet & Coil materials.
The company supplies Stainless Steel Plate Sheet & Coil materials.
Fanello Industries provides Metal Processing services including Cutting, Forming, and Joining.
The company offers Blanking and Sheeting services.
Fanello Industries provides Laser Cutting services.
The company offers Machining services.
Fanello Industries provides Stamping services.
The company offers Custom Welding services.
source_title: Fanello Industries | Metals and Metalworking Search

facts: Industrial metal processor and distributor Ryerson Holding Corporation (Chicago, IL) has acquired Fanello Industries (Lavonia, GA).
Fanello Industries supplies laser cutting, blanking, stamping, bending, and machining metal solutions to several industries in the Southeastern U.S.
Fanello Industries employs approximately 150 people.
Fanello Industries has annual revenue of approximately $20 million.

Ryerson has worked closely with Vince Fanello and his management team for many years both as a customer and a supplier of value-added fabrication services, according to Kevin Richardson, Ryerson's President, South-East Region.
Fanello will continue to serve the market under its own brand name following the acquisition.
Ryerson has also acquired metal fabricator Laserflex.
source_title: Ryerson acquires laser cutting services provider Fanello Industries | Laser Focus World

facts: Kerry Group has officially opened its newly upgraded facility in Rome, GA.
A total of $137.4 million has been invested to create what the Ireland-based company calls one of the most state-of-the-art food manufacturing facilities in the United States.
The investment represents Kerry’s largest-ever capital expenditure investment and one of the biggest in Georgia’s history.
Officials from Georgia and Ireland attended the March 14 ribbon-cutting ceremony.

Attendees at the event included Irish Minister for Justice Helen McEntee, Kerry CEO Edmond Scanlon, chairman of the board Philip Toomey, Georgia Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson, and state and regional elected officials.
The facility now employs 250 people.
The facility provides integrated taste and nutrition solutions to help customers meet growing consumer demand in the poultry, seafood, and alternative protein markets across the US and Canada.
The facility has a footprint of 316,000-square-feet and offers the latest food technologies and systems with high standards of food safety.
As part of Kerry’s sustainability strategy, Beyond the Horizon, the company prioritized sustainability initiatives across the entire facility.

Sustainability initiatives include using 100% renewable electricity, zero waste to landfill, bulk receiving of materials, local sourcing where possible, and extensive use of energy efficient equipment.

Oliver Kelly, president and CEO of Kerry in North America, stated that the new facility more than doubles their capacity in Rome and is now one of the most advanced and modern food manufacturing facilities in the country.
Kelly noted that increased and changing consumer demand for tasty, nutritious, and sustainable food and beverages is driving accelerated change and reshaping the entire food industry.
Investing in world-class facilities like this one in Georgia allows Kerry to continually work and co-create with customers to respond to changing demand.
The Rome, Georgia facility is an example of Kerry’s dedication to its goal of reaching over two billion people with sustainable nutrition solutions by 2030.
Kerry expressed thanks for the support received from the Governor of Georgia’s office and looks forward to continued partnership.
Kerry is the world’s leading taste and nutrition partner for the food, beverage, and pharmaceutical markets, with ingredient solutions reaching over 1 billion consumers around the world.
Kerry's consumer insights, global RD&A team of 1,100+ food scientists, and extensive global footprint enable the company to solve customers’ complex challenges with differentiated solutions.
source_title: Kerry Group Opens $137M Plant in Georgia with 250 Employees | Food Manufacturing

facts: Due to surges in demand for plastic parts, Flambeau Inc. has invested significantly in its operations recently.
Flambeau added new presses at three of its nine North American plants: Columbus, Indiana; Middlefield, Ohio; and Madison, Georgia.
The press additions represent a company-wide expansion of capabilities for Flambeau’s manufacturing capacity.
A worldwide economic slowdown last year due to the Covid-19 pandemic led to a rapidly increasing company-wide backlog of orders for Flambeau.
Just months into the slowdown, Flambeau’s supply chain began to reach its limits, and demand for vital parts began to overwhelm supply.
Flambeau addressed the backlog by adding presses to its arsenal, among other efforts towards overall improvement.
In Columbus, Indiana, a new 750-ton press is primarily dedicated to specific proprietary products, including Flambeau Brute tool boxes, gun cases, and tackle boxes.
In Madison, Georgia, new 200-ton and 900-ton presses are utilized to increase production of a variety of stock and custom products for Flambeau’s fluids and packaging divisions.

In Middlefield, Ohio, three new presses ranging from 330- to 730-ton are primarily dedicated to manufacturing stock boxes and cases for Flambeau Outdoors, ArtBin and other packaging lines of products.

Flambeau’s additional efforts include the implementation of several creative solutions to reduce backlog, such as 24-hour operations and new production scheduling methodology to improve forecasting practices and address various constraints on production.

Along with the further expansion of injection and blow molding at its several plants, Flambeau is implementing a modern, company-wide ERP system that will continually improve customer support and the company’s standing as a supply chain system.
source_title: Flambeau Inc. Invests in Operations | Medical Product Outsourcing

facts: FREYR Battery announced a target start of production for its U.S. project in 2025.
The acceleration of the U.S. project is driven by the Inflation Reduction Act, which aims to reduce energy inflation and capitalize regional energy security for the United States.
IRA incentives can create as much as 50% direct incentives relative to a battery producer's cost structure.
For every gigawatt hour of batteries produced in the U.S., FREYR sees as much as $37 million in incentives per gigawatt hour of capacity installed yearly until 2030, tapering off until 2032.
The site selection process for the U.S. project culminated in an attractive site in Coweta County, Georgia.
Land acquisition for the Georgia site occurred in November 2022 as a key milestone for the development of the U.S. project.
The U.S. project was initially expected to lag the Giga Arctic project by 10 to 12 months but is being accelerated due to market opportunity.
FREYR is evaluating multiple options to move startup production from an estimated 2026 into 2025.
Engineering and production line equipment for the U.S. project will be replicable from the Giga Arctic project in Norway.
Multiple customers have expressed a preference for FREYR to move downstream into module and DC block production.
Coweta County approved a $20 million land grant for the project, which was closing in the coming days at the time of the call.
The State of Georgia has an application pending for a $7 million land grant that should be approved and closed in the next two to three months.
A $230 million tax abatement incentive program with Coweta County has closed.
FREYR is in the process with the State of Georgia to close a $140 million tax abatement package.
The total incentives provide approximately $410 million over multiple phases of the project.
Meeting minimum capital investment and job creation requirements across two growth phases will be necessary to secure the incentives.
FREYR is building up a team in the U.S. to initiate the first re-engineering required for an accelerated delivery of the plant.
The company approached close to 20 suppliers, including some U.S.-based ones, to secure raw material volumes for the Giga America acceleration.
FREYR's SemiSolid technology platform is already in commercial production in Japan at triple-digit megawatt-hour scale.
The gigawatt-hour scale SemiSolid platform is positioned to be up to 50% more cost effective than fourth quartile producers.
The cell design features a much larger electrode that will structurally fit better with ESS and EV configurations than current battery solutions.
The process dramatically simplifies manufacturing, significantly reducing space, CapEx, energy, and labor consumption.
FREYR has already set its mark on the E-Mobility sector with an off-take agreement for E-Mobility Solutions earlier in the year.
The company has ongoing RFQs and partnership discussions with EV-focused OEMs targeting double to triple digit gigawatt hours scale annually.
FREYR's LFP-based focus is unlocking finance and value accretive opportunities upstream for large-scale plant facilities over time.
Jeremy Bezdek serves as Executive Vice President of Corporate Development and President of FREYR Battery U.S., leading the U.S. developments.
The company ended 2022 with $563 million in cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash with no debt, supporting its balance sheet strength.
source_title: FREYR Battery (NYSE:FREY) Q4 2022 Earnings Call Transcript - Insider Monkey

facts: FREYR Battery has abandoned its plans to build a gigafactory in Coweta County in the US State of Georgia.
Originally, the Norwegian company had intended to invest up to 2.6 billion US dollars.
FREYR cited rising interest rates, falling market prices for batteries, changes in management, and changing strategic goals as reasons for the withdrawal.
Instead, the company is now focusing on a solar panel factory in Texas.
In November 2022, FREYR had announced the construction of Giga America in Coweta County.
A multi-phased development was planned, starting with a battery cell production module with a capacity of approximately 34 gigawatt-hours and an estimated investment of 1.7 billion dollars.
By 2029, the investment was expected to increase to more than 2.6 billion dollars, expanding annual capacity and creating more than 720 jobs.
The production of lithium-ion battery cells was to be used for both vehicles and energy storage systems.
However, construction never began.
source_title: FREYR Cancels 2.6-Billion-Dollar Gigafactory in Georgia - Battery-News

facts: Norway-based battery manufacturer Freyr Battery has scrapped plans to build a factory in metro Atlanta.
The Coweta County project would have been Freyr Battery’s first American factory.
The project promised hundreds of jobs and a $2.57 billion investment in Georgia.
A spokesperson for the Georgia Department of Economic Development confirmed the state was made aware of Freyr’s decision to discontinue its plans in Coweta County.
Georgia’s incentives process protects the Georgia taxpayer, and when a company’s plans change, that process ensures discretionary incentives are repaid.
The company and state are working together to ensure that the state grant used for project site acquisition is repaid expeditiously.
A Freyr spokesperson stated the company is grateful for the support and partnership from Coweta County.
Freyr is focusing at the moment on a solar module manufacturing facility in Texas.
The plant was announced in 2022.
The planned site was a 368-acre location in Bridgeport Industrial Park off I-85.
The plan called for creating more than 720 jobs over a seven-year period.
In 2022, Gov. Brian Kemp stated the plant would have been a massive investment into “Georgia’s sustainable technology ecosystem.”
The plant was designed to produce lithium-ion battery cells, which are often used in cars and for energy storage.
Coweta County officials expressed disappointment in Freyr’s business decision.

The county expressed sincere appreciation to the County staff, Coweta County Development Authority, and the Georgia Department of Economic Development for their assistance, hard work and due diligence throughout the whole process.

County officials noted that despite the outcome, incentives were structured with safeguards to protect taxpayers and the property will remain valuable for creating jobs and investment to benefit the community.
source_title: Freyr Battery scraps plans for $2.5 billion factory in Coweta County, Georgia official says

facts: FREYR Battery has announced a strategic rebranding and will now operate as T1 Energy Inc. (“T1”).
This rebranding initiative includes a new company name, corporate logo, refreshed visual identity, and an upcoming change in its ticker symbol on the New York Stock Exchange to “TE.”
The transition reflects the company’s commitment to establishing itself as a vertically integrated leader in solar and battery storage solutions in the United States.
T1 has also relocated its corporate headquarters to Austin, Texas, as part of this strategic shift.
T1 remains focused on executing its long-term strategy in alignment with the timelines set forth in its Q4 2024 transaction announcement.
The company’s solar module production facility in Wilmer, Texas, has been renamed “G1 Dallas” and currently employs more than 1,000 people.
The production ramp-up of solar modules at this facility is progressing as planned.
Additionally, the site selection process for T1’s future U.S. solar cell manufacturing facility, designated as “G2,” is advancing, with an expected location announcement in the first quarter of 2025.
Construction for this facility is still scheduled to commence by mid-2025.
Daniel Barcelo, T1’s Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer, mentioned “The rebirth of our company as T1 Energy is about American energy, jobs, and advanced manufacturing.
Barcelo stated that the U.S. needs domestic supply chains and manufacturing capacity to harness its abundant solar resources and that T1 is committed to building that capacity.
He noted that more than 80% of new U.S. electricity capacity in 2024 was solar and batteries.

T1 plans to lead in the solar and battery markets by enabling the growth in energy supply required to support AI, data centers, reshoring, and electrification – to restore American industrial capacity and leadership in future advanced industries.”
The official name change to T1 Energy Inc. took effect on February 19, 2025.

In connection with this rebranding, the company anticipates that, as of the opening of trading on March 3, 2025, its common stock and warrants on the New York Stock Exchange will transition from the current ticker symbols “FREY” and “FREY WS” to the new symbols “TE” and “TE WS.”
source_title: FREYR Battery Rebrands To T1 Energy, Signaling A Fresh Vision For The Future - SolarQuarter

facts: FREYR has selected and purchased a site in Coweta County, Georgia for a multi-phase Giga America clean battery manufacturing project.
The facility will be based on the next-generation SemiSolid™ Lithium-Ion Battery Technology platform developed by 24M Technologies Inc (“24M”) in Boston, MA.
The initial battery cell production module is expected to have approximately 34 GWh capacity.
The initial phase has a preliminarily estimated capital investment of $1.7 billion.
FREYR plans to undertake detailed plant engineering in the coming months to finalize plans for the initial Gigafactory module.
The company and key stakeholders are evaluating additional value accretive upstream and downstream modules as well as a second cell production phase.
These additional phases are expected to bring total capital investments to more than $2.6 billion by 2029.
Over the life of the project, FREYR and its partners expect to create more than 720 U.S. jobs for highly skilled workers in Coweta County.
The purchased land is located at the Bridgeport Industrial Park site in Coweta County, GA.
The industrial zoned tract covers 368 acres with options secured for further expansion.
The site was selected through a rigorous evaluation process based on key operational, logistical, and financial criteria.
The Giga America site offers substantial room for additional upstream and downstream development projects to strengthen cost position and improve the carbon footprint of the batteries.
The project positions FREYR to meet rapidly growing customer demand for Energy Storage Systems (“ESS”) applications with U.S.-based conditional offtake partners.
FREYR is seeing very strong interest for increased volume commitments through ongoing customer dialogues.

FREYR is accelerating its previously planned U.S. expansion based on strong tailwinds in U.S. renewable energy development, grid stability initiatives, and recently announced tax incentives from the Inflation Reduction Act.
Projected U.S. demand is augmented by increased interest from existing and new customer dialogues in commercial mobility and electric vehicle segments.
FREYR is intensifying and broadening financing efforts to support capital commitments for Giga America in parallel with ongoing construction of Giga Arctic in Mo i Rana, Norway.
The company is evaluating highly value accretive potential upstream and downstream modules in the Nordic region and the U.S.
The State of Georgia and Coweta County are collectively providing strong financial incentives for the Giga America project.
FREYR intends to apply with the U.S. Department of Energy for packages that could potentially include a grant and/or direct loan to assist with development and possible acceleration of the project.
FREYR is evaluating clean power supply solutions for Giga America with utility providers in the region, including the potential development of a dedicated solar plus storage facility.

Giga America will leverage 24M’s SemiSolid™ platform, which simplifies lithium-ion battery production significantly, enabling highly capital, labor, and energy efficient manufacturing at scale based on U.S. technology originating from MIT.
FREYR’s U.S. project team intends to replicate major elements of the Giga Arctic development by maintaining a continuous technical dialogue with key personnel in Norway.
Upon completion of all contemplated construction phases, the Giga America complex is expected to be one of the largest battery cell manufacturing developments globally.

Tom Einar Jensen, FREYR’s Co-Founder and Chief Executive Officer, stated: “Today’s landmark announcement underscores FREYR’s ambition to develop a very strong and near-term operational footprint in the United States. Expanding into the U.S. has been a foundational aspect of FREYR’s long-term strategy from our inception, and with the recent passage of the Inflation Reduction Act, we expect U.S. demand for ESS, passenger EV and other electric mobility applications to grow rapidly over the next decade.”

Governor Brian Kemp stated: “Job creators and innovators from all over the world bring their operations to Georgia because they know they will have success here. We’re proud to welcome FREYR to the Peach State as the latest company to bring transformational investments and opportunity to our communities. They will be a valued addition to our No. 1 state for business, and I look forward to their growing impact on Coweta and the surrounding region.”

Coweta County Board of Commissioners Chairman Bob Blackburn stated: “We are thrilled to welcome FREYR Battery to the Coweta County community. With the addition of FREYR, Coweta County is quickly becoming a premier destination for top-tier manufacturing industries that support the U.S. economy and beyond. We look forward to the innovation, opportunities, and success that FREYR will generate.”

The total package of financial incentives for Giga America includes robust assistance collectively provided by The State of Georgia and Coweta County in addition to expected production tax credits associated with the recently passed Inflation Reduction Act.
The U.S. energy storage market is already the largest in the world and is expected to experience exponential growth over the next several years.
According to Rystad Energy, to satisfy a 1.6-degree scenario, cumulative collective storage demand of 4.5 TWh from the U.S. and Europe will be required through 2030.

Under the same scenario, Rystad expects annual collective storage demand in the U.S. and Europe to reach 1.4 TWh by 2030, representing a 34-fold increase from projected corresponding annual 2022 demand.

Jensen added: “At FREYR, we are deeply committed to decarbonizing the transportation and energy sectors. As we advance our U.S. expansion plan in cooperation with our key stakeholders, we expect to make meaningful investments to spur job creation and the eventual development of localized, decarbonized supply chains in the U.S. to enhance energy security and economic activity and meet the growing demand for energy storage solutions in the U.S. market.”
The Giga America project will be based on the SemiSolid™ technology of FREYR’s U.S.-based licensing partner, 24M.
The SemiSolid™ platform enables capital and energy efficient production of lithium-ion batteries at scale.
FREYR is currently evaluating clean power supply solutions for the Giga America project with U.S. Independent Power Producers (“IPPs”).
The company’s long-term ambition is to replicate the strategy employed at Giga Arctic to produce next-generation batteries that are among the cleanest anywhere in the world.
At 10:00 am EST (16:00 CET) FREYR Battery will host a launch event live from Coweta County, Georgia.
The webcasted presentation will be streamed online in English.
A recording of the event will be made available after the presentation on FREYR’s website and YouTube channel.
FREYR aims to provide industrial scale clean battery solutions to reduce global emissions.
FREYR is listed on the New York Stock Exchange under ticker FREY.
FREYR has commenced building the first of its planned factories in Mo i Rana, Norway.
FREYR announced potential development of industrial scale battery cell production in Vaasa, Finland, and the United States.
FREYR intends to install 50 GWh of battery cell capacity by 2025 and 100 GWh annual capacity by 2028 and 200 GWh of annual capacity by 2030.

links.context: Video of Giga America initial concept

source_title: FREYR Battery Announces Plans for U.S. Gigafactory in Georgia | Technologies.org

facts: A Norway-based battery manufacturer announced plans to build its first American factory in Georgia.
More than 720 new jobs are coming to Coweta County.
The company is slated to build a plant on a more than 368-acre site in the Bridgeport Industrial Park off I-85 near Newnan, Ga.
The facility will create more than 720 jobs over the next seven years.
The plant will produce lithium-ion battery cells often used in cars and for energy storage.
Tom Jensen, CEO of Freyr Battery, stated that batteries can be put in large containers to store sunlight and wind when the sun isn’t shining and the wind isn’t blowing.
Freyr picked Georgia over more than 130 other sites in 25 states.
Coweta County was chosen because it is perfectly positioned to easily move products in and out and has a workforce hungry for jobs.
Freyr cited the availability of talent and access to competent people in the region as a core factor.
Georgia is already proving to be a leader in the EV battery and car industry.
The state is offering Freyr financial incentives to build its first US plant, but details were not disclosed by the Georgia Economic Development (GED).
A GED representative stated that as an active economic development project, the state isn’t able to comment beyond the press release until agreements are finalized.
Coweta County is offering incentives including a 20-year abatement on property taxes and grant money to help with a payroll of over $60,000 per year.
Michael Fouts of Coweta County Administrator noted the proposed average salary is a little over $60,000 a year.
This average salary represents 120 percent of the current average wage in the county.
Freyr believes battery storage is the future and says it is very likely they will expand and hire even more people.
The company plans to be up and running in about two years and fully operational by 2029.
source_title: Norway-based battery manufacturer Freyr promises 720 new jobs in Coweta County

facts: FREYR Battery has announced the appointment of Jeremy Bezdek to the position of Executive Vice President of Global Corporate Development and President for FREYR Battery U.S.
The Giga America phased development is accelerating, catalyzed by the Inflation Reduction Act.
The project is based on the Giga Arctic blueprint for a world leading Gigafactory in Coweta County, Georgia.
FREYR is significantly expanding its strategic ecosystem of technologies, customers, and industrial scaling partners in parallel with the U.S. development.

Tom Einar Jensen, Co-Founder and CEO of FREYR, stated that seasoned leaders with deep expertise in innovation, business development, and strategy are needed for this important phase of development in the U.S. and globally.
Jeremy Bezdek comes from the position of Managing Director, Strategic Platforms at Koch Industries, where he spent over 26 years in various executive and leadership roles.

Prior to FREYR, Bezdek led Flint Hills Resources Biofuels and Ingredients business and Innovation and M&A team, leading active acquisitions, joint ventures, and investments in early-stage development companies related to refining, biofuels, and chemicals industries.
In his most recent role at Koch Strategic Platforms (KSP), Bezdek led investments as part of KSP’s energy transformation vertical and served on the board of several energy transition companies.
Bezdek graduated from the University of Kansas and currently resides in Kansas.

As a former Board Director now joining the executive team, Bezdek stated he will play a role in driving FREYR's development in an operational capacity during a monumental shift in the battery industry.
FREYR recently announced the selection and purchase of a site in Coweta County, Georgia for the multi-phase Giga America clean battery manufacturing project.
Giga America positions FREYR to meet the growing demand for energy storage systems (ESS) with U.S.-based conditional offtake partners.
FREYR is evaluating clean power supply solutions for Giga America with utility providers in the region, including the potential development of a dedicated solar plus storage facility.
Upon completion of all contemplated construction phases, the Giga America complex is expected to be one of the largest battery cell manufacturing developments globally.

FREYR previously announced the opening of its technology center in Boston, located in close proximity to technology partner 24M, to enhance technology development, collaboration, personnel development, and strategic coordination.

FREYR Battery aims to provide industrial scale clean battery solutions to reduce global emissions and produce green battery cells to accelerate the decarbonization of energy and transportation systems globally.

FREYR has commenced building the first of its planned factories in Mo i Rana, Norway and announced potential development of industrial scale battery cell production in Vaasa, Finland, and the United States.
FREYR intends to install 50 GWh of battery cell capacity by 2025, 100 GWh annual capacity by 2028, and 200 GWh of annual capacity by 2030.
source_title: Energy, Automobile, EV, Renewable News | FREYR Battery Appoints Executive to Lead Global Corporate Development and U.S. Execution

facts: Goodyear Tire & Rubber (NASDAQ: GT) has shed 71% of its value since January 2022.
An investor more than doubled their position in Goodyear between $6.28 and $6.50 per share in mid-March.
Skyrocketing crude oil prices due to Iran war uncertainties are likely to drive up the cost of petroleum-derived rubber, challenging Goodyear over the next couple of quarters.
Goodyear's 2025 full-year operating results showed declining tire units sold across the Americas, EMEA, and Asia Pacific regions, alongside a shrinking operating margin.
Goodyear attributed sales declines to a sharp contraction in demand from its Commercial segment in the Americas.
President Donald Trump's tariffs have negatively impacted Goodyear's business performance.
High rubber prices, Iran war uncertainty, and tariffs are expected to weigh on Goodyear's results throughout 2026.
Goodyear initiated an operational turnaround plan called "Goodyear Forward" in November 2023, focusing on divestitures of non-core assets, cost-savings, and higher-margin products.
Goodyear sold its chemical business to an affiliate of Gemspring Capital Management for $650 million.
Goodyear sold the Dunlop brand to Sumitomo Rubber Industries for $735 million.
Goodyear sold its off-the-road tire operations to Yokohama Rubber Company for $905 million.

facts: Great Dane Trailers is one of the most recognizable trailer manufacturers in the world.

The building spans 453,000 sf.
The structure is a hybrid design composed of both conventional steel and pre-engineered structures along with concrete tilt-wall panels.
Manufacturing areas included 124 specialty concrete equipment foundations that were isolated to reduce the transmission of harmonic vibrations to the adjacent structure and floor slab.
The facility is climate controlled using an evaporative cooling system utilizing push/pull exhaust fans to maintain four air changes per hour.
The building houses administrative offices, a product show room, foam storage silos and a hazardous materials area.
The facility incorporates compressed air, weld gas, paint booths and process conveying systems.

facts: Great Dane Trailers is one of the leading manufacturers of trailers in North America.
The company has a long and rich history, dating back over 120 years.
Great Dane Trailers was founded in 1900 in Savannah, Georgia, by J.P. Wheless and T.H. McMillan.
The company started as a small repair shop for horse-drawn wagons before growing into a manufacturer of high-quality wagons and carriages.
In the 1920s, Great Dane began to transition to the production of trailers for the transportation industry.
The company quickly gained a reputation for building durable, reliable trailers that could withstand the rigors of long-haul transportation.
During the 1930s and 1940s, Great Dane introduced refrigerated trailers for transporting perishable goods and livestock trailers for transporting animals.
During World War II, Great Dane produced trailers for the military to transport troops, supplies, and equipment across the United States and around the world.
During the 1950s and 1960s, Great Dane introduced flatbed trailers for transporting heavy equipment and bulk commodity trailers for transporting grain and other agricultural products.
In the mid-1960s, Great Dane merged with Pines Trailer Corporation, another leading trailer manufacturer, to expand its manufacturing capabilities and offer a wider range of products.
During the 1970s and 1980s, Great Dane introduced double-deck trailers for transporting automobiles and intermodal trailers for transporting shipping containers.
In the late 1980s, Great Dane was acquired by the Chicago-based investment firm CC Industries to continue investing in new technology and expand its product line.
During the 1990s and 2000s, Great Dane introduced lightweight trailers for increased fuel efficiency and trailers with advanced safety features.
In 2007, Great Dane was acquired by the private equity firm Oak Hill Capital Partners to continue investing in new technology and expand its manufacturing capabilities.
Today, Great Dane is one of the largest manufacturers of trailers in North America, focusing on quality and reliability for the transportation industry.
source_title: Great Dane Trailers - A Leading Manufacturer! - Apple Truck & Trailer

facts: Hanon Systems (KS:018880) is a leading global automotive thermal management supplier for electrified mobility.
The company announced a new plant in Woodbridge, Ontario, Canada, to support its growing business with global vehicle manufacturers.
The facility includes approximately 284,200 square feet (26,400 square meters) of manufacturing space.
The facility has room for expansion based on business needs.
It will be the company's first North American location to produce electric scroll compressors.
Electric scroll compressors are essential components for thermal management in hybrid and battery electric vehicles.
Depending on customer demand, the facility can produce up to 900,000 electric compressors annually.
Production is expected to start in the first half of 2025.
At full production, the plant expects to employ nearly 300 hourly and salaried employees.

Nurdal Kücükkaya stated that expanding manufacturing presence increases proximity to vehicle manufacturers, better meets customer demand, demonstrates commitment to growth, and proves customer confidence in delivering electric compressors to the market.

Subu Nagasubramony stated that the company investigated several locations for the new plant, their partnership with the government of Ontario and investment development agency was a significant element in the decision, and they are proud to foster local community development and economic growth.

Vic Fedeli stated that Ontario has all elements necessary to build electric vehicles of the future, including leading global parts manufacturers like Hanon Systems continuing to choose Ontario to expand operations, and that the investment will create 300 new local jobs for world-class auto workers and strengthen North American leadership in automotive manufacturing.
The new facility joins four existing electric compressor manufacturing operations located in Pyeongtaek (Korea), Dalian and Changchun (China), and Portugal (Europe).
The expansion reinforces the company's competitiveness by expanding production of key components for hybrid and battery electric vehicles.
Company executives and local dignitaries gathered at a media event to announce the new plant.
Hanon Systems has a 35-year history in Canada.
The Belleville plant was established in 1989 and produces fluid transport products, HVAC modules, and electronic coolant valves for the North American market.
The new Woodbridge plant is located 200 kilometers from Belleville.
The Woodbridge plant will create synergies with the company's Americas Innovation Center in Novi, Mich.
Proximity to major automakers will allow the company to respond quickly to customer demands.
Hanon Systems is a full-line supplier of automotive thermal and energy management solutions for electrified and conventional vehicles.
Its product offerings include heating ventilation and air conditioning; powertrain cooling; compressor; fluid transport; and electronics and fluid pressure.
The company currently operates 50 manufacturing sites and three regional innovation centers.
The company employs more than 20,000 people across 21 countries.

facts: HELLA was founded in 1899 in Lippstadt, Germany.
The company has grown to a global presence with over 125 locations in 35 different countries.
HELLA develops and manufactures lighting and electronic components as well as systems for automobiles.
The company reported sales of $7.3 billion in the fiscal year 2020/2021.
HELLA employs 36,000 people globally.
HELLA is recognized as one of the leading automotive suppliers.
HELLA Automotive developed an LED headlamp replacement specifically for police and emergency vehicles.
Existing market replacements failed to allow warning lights (turn signals, brake lights, etc.) to function when flashing lights were enabled.
Installation procedures for alternative headlamps often caused costly mistakes like drilling errors and poor silicone seals that could damage vehicles.
Long curing times for epoxy used to lock bezels in place slowed the overall installation process.
HELLA needed a product easily installed into existing mounts across various vehicle makes, models, and years.
The solution involved modifying the cylindrical body to fit a collar with two semi-circle pieces that snap together to match each vehicle's headlamp shape.
Traditional manufacturing methods like injection molding were deemed unviable for producing large varieties of adaptors in small batches.
The product required a material with higher temperature resistance due to operating conditions.
HELLA partnered with 3D Printing Tech to evaluate if 3D printing could solve these challenges.
3D Printing Tech provided reliable final production run cost estimates early on, allowing HELLA to validate ROI during prototyping.
Initial material testing used FDM/FFF printing with filaments including ABS, PETG, PolyMide from PolyMaker, and Alfa+ from FiloAlfa.
Alfa+ filament was selected for providing the best finish and performance in high-temperature environments.
Filament-based parts did not meet installer expectations regarding appearance compared to traditionally manufactured parts.
Producing FDM/FFF parts in exotic materials posed quality control challenges for 3D Printing Tech.
Production shifted to SLS using Nylon12 powder sintered with the MfgPro230 xS from XYZPrinting.
Parts were printed in natural white Nylon12 powder and dyed black to match HELLA's color specifications.
The final parts matched the look of traditionally manufactured items, making them well-received by installation teams.
The semi-circle pieces are easily installed to match headlamp shapes for each vehicle.
First responders can now use their warning lights when flashing lights are enabled.
SLS production allows over 1000 parts per build, ensuring HELLA's volume needs are met quickly.
The product became a profitable, stand-alone addition to HELLA's portfolio that increased market share.
The improved installation method reduced costs and increased revenue for installers.
The design created safer driving conditions by ensuring warning lights function properly, leading to reduced accidents and costs.
source_title: Emergency Vehicle Safety Enhanced with 3D Printing Tech & HELLA

facts: Hitachi Astemo is developing 360-degree camera, semi-active suspension and steer-by-wire technologies to improve electric- and autonomous-vehicle performance.

Senior engineering and product-development executives reviewed Hitachi Astemo’s mobility-technology vision during a press briefing at the company’s headquarters for the Americas in Farmington Hills, Mich.
John Nunneley serves as senior vice president of Design, Engineering and IT at Hitachi Astemo.
Software-defined EVs and autonomous vehicles within the next five to 10 years will depend on the technologies currently under development at Hitachi Astemo, according to John Nunneley.

Hitachi Astemo’s 360-degree Stereo Vision Camera System provides LiDAR-like results to support autonomous driving functions at one-tenth the cost, according to Harsha Badarinarayan, vice president of Advanced Engineering.

While LiDAR has gained prominence in recent years, camera technology has steadily and quietly evolved to offset LiDAR’s advantages, including nighttime operation and the ability to sense the surrounding environment with greater fidelity, noted Harsha Badarinarayan.
Carmakers like Tesla are committed to camera technology, according to Hitachi Astemo.
The company’s vision system processes data from seven to 10 cameras to create a 3D view of the area surrounding a vehicle.
Camera placement can vary according to a vehicle’s design using existing mounting points such as mirror, windshield and grille locations.
In addition to autonomous driving applications, the camera system could have other uses including self-parking or the recording of a vehicle in operation.
Hitachi Astemo’s next generation G5 Semi-Active Suspension improves primary ride balance while maintaining a focus on secondary ride comfort.
The G5 suspension system consists of Astemo’s external-valve semi-active dampers at four corners, five accelerometers and Astemo’s internally developed Vehicle Dynamics Module (VDM) ECU.
The latest-generation G5 system increases damping force capacity to ensure vehicles today and in the future have a high level of handling stability.
The G5 system controls production-spec dampers based on sprung and unsprung inputs calculated using five G-sensors.

Bi-Linear Quadratic control logic with piston speed dependent damping coefficient limitation can coordinate roll and pitch by independently controlling damping force to induce or resist pitch motion in turns.
Astemo’s drivability control can coordinate roll and pitch by independently controlling damping forces to induce or resist pitch motion during turns, according to Harsha Badarinarayan.
Hitachi Astemo’s Smart Steer-by-Wire System replaces the steering wheel with a hand-held device set in a front seat armrest.
The steer-by-wire device makes turns with a natural turn of the wrist and is able to adjust wheel slip during adverse weather conditions and compensate when the vehicle goes off-center.
Steer-by-wire technology allows Hitachi Astemo to literally reinvent the steering function by eliminating the steering wheel altogether, according to Harsha Badarinarayan.
The steer-by-wire system is especially responsive to driver input with little or no lag from input to steering response, according to Harsha Badarinarayan.
source_title: Hitachi Astemo is Developing 360-degree Camera, Semi-Active Suspension and Steer-by-Wire Technologies for EVs and Autonomous Vehicles | MOTOR

facts: Hitachi Astemo, a leader in the direct injection gasoline market, has launched its latest high-pressure pump (HPP Generation 5) on the aftermarket market.
The HPP Generation 5 pump is one of Hitachi Astemo's latest developments.

This mechanically driven model mounts at the cylinder head and can achieve pressures of up to 500 bars.
These performance improvements enhance combustion, reduce fuel consumption, and limit CO2 emissions while complying with current environmental standards.

facts: Hitachi Astemo, Ltd. has developed an autonomous driving technology that helps safely and smoothly pass by oncoming vehicles on narrow roads by utilizing 3D sensing results of the surrounding vehicle environment.
The company aims to strengthen cost competitiveness through the use of 3D sensing data obtained from new stereo cameras with improved cost benefits instead of LiDAR.
Hitachi Astemo is working towards collaborating with a high-precision, versatile new stereo camera launched in March 2023.
The new stereo camera can detect pedestrians and bicycles with a high degree of accuracy and measure distances by combining long-range detection with a wide viewing angle.
The stereo camera stores identification patterns in advance using machine learning techniques to contribute to collision prevention during right and left turns at intersections.
Hitachi Astemo has created software processing that enables advanced image recognition and vehicle control on cost-competitive electronic control units by utilizing its AI and recognition technology.
Collaborating with the new stereo camera will enable further enhancement of the cost competitiveness of autonomous driving technology that enables cooperative behavior in narrow roads.

Hitachi Astemo’s autonomous driving technology using cooperative behavior in narrow roads and the new stereo camera will be introduced at the “Automotive Engineering Exposition 2023 Yokohama” (booth number: 148) held at Pacifico Yokohama from May 23.
Hitachi Astemo is committed to strengthening its business through a strategic portfolio including Powertrain & Safety Systems, Chassis, Motorcycle, Software and Aftermarket businesses.

Aiming for growth based on the pillars of “green,” “digital,” and “innovation,” Hitachi Astemo will contribute to a better global environment by developing xEV systems and highly efficient internal combustion engine systems that reduce emissions.
The company delivers enhanced safety and comfort through autonomous driving systems, advanced driver assistance systems and advanced chassis systems.

To open the location, Four Star Freightliner invested $200,000 in the community and added 10 new jobs.
In Crenshaw County, ITAC Alabama, formerly known as SMART, makes automotive frames for Hyundai vehicles.
In late 2025, ITAC Alabama announced a 37,000-square-foot expansion at its current facility.
When complete, the expansion is expected to create 100 jobs.
Dongwon Autopart Technology Alabama makes automotive door parts, inner assemblies and more in Luverne.
In Lowndes County, Daehan Solutions Alabama makes interior sound solutions components and employs almost 300 workers.
Many of the products from Daehan Solutions Alabama are headed for the Hyundai plant in Montgomery.
Sejong Alabama LLC, located in Fort Deposit, makes mufflers and exhaust systems.
Many of the products from Sejong Alabama LLC are also headed for the Hyundai plant in Montgomery.
In August 2025, the Busy Ant Travel Center broke ground on an $8 million facility in Georgiana, just off Interstate 65.
The facility will include a 12,000-square-foot truck stop featuring several stores including Subway, an electric vehicle charging station and fueling stations.
The project is estimated to bring 30 new jobs to the community.
Jacob Morgan serves as president and CEO of the Covington County Economic Development Commission.
source_title: Spotlight on Butler, Covington, Crenshaw & Lowndes Counties: Economic Engines

From anywhere in the county, it is a less than 2-hour drive to either location.
A 331-acre featured property site is available at HWY 257 GRAD Select Site.

Key industrial sectors in the region are Distribution, Automotive, and Manufacturing.
