facts: NIFCO KTW, a German-based automotive supplier of high-quality injection molded components, will locate its U.S. operations in Stephens County under the name "Project Hummingbird".
The company will establish a 130,000 square foot facility on 32 acres in the Hayestone-Brady Business Park, located on Highway 17 between Toccoa and Martin, as its first North American headquarters.
Over the next five years, NIFCO KTW plans to invest $27 million and create 200 jobs, with officials stating that more than half of those jobs will be created in the first year of operations.

NIFCO KTW Managing Director Roland Furtmayr stated the company is proud to find Toccoa as the home for its new U.S. headquarters and production facility, citing local connectivity and aiming to become a reliable employer contributing to positive economic development in Stephens County.
The Toccoa plant will supply injection molded parts to BMW and Mercedes Benz USA among other automotive manufacturers worldwide.
Stephens County Commission Chair Dennis Bell welcomed the project, noting the community needed an investment of this magnitude.

Toccoa Mayor Terry Carter stated the addition of the company will invigorate the local economy and put Stephens County on the map for future manufacturing prospects, appreciating the state's strong support and incentives during site selection.
The City of Toccoa committed to spending approximately $300,000 to run water, wastewater, and gas utilities to the company's building at no cost to NIFCO KTW.
Stephens County will provide 25 years of full property tax relief, though officials noted the county is not currently collecting tax on the property because it is owned by the Development Authority.
The company is qualifying for state job creation tax credits as part of the project agreement.

Stephens County Development Authority Chairman Brian Akin described the announcement as a huge win for the local job market and praised local leadership for their work through the recruitment process.
Toccoa-Stephens County Chamber Board Chair Greg Roach pledged to help the company acclimate to the local area and thanked the community for their support in bringing the project to fruition.

Governor Nathan Deal stated that automotive suppliers choose Georgia due to trust in its pro-business resources and ready-to-work community, highlighting over 40 years of business ties with Germany in the region and looking forward to the high-quality jobs the company will bring.
The project agreement requires construction to begin later in 2015, with building completion expected in 2016.
Stephens County Development Authority Executive Director Tim Martin indicated the facility could come online in the second half of 2016.
NIFCO KTW has plans to expand its Toccoa site in the future, with additional land available at Hayestone-Brady for that expansion.
source_title: German Company NIFCO KTW to Locate Plant in Stephens County - 92.1 WLHR

facts: Nifco provides products to the automotive industry with a fastening philosophy of connecting, building, and tying.
Nifco's products are designed to give comfort to people, reduce vehicles' weight, and be kind to the earth.
Nifco is expanding its activities into new areas beyond automobiles.
Based on its philosophy and technologies, Nifco provides wide-ranging products for household equipment, consumer electronics, office automation, fashion, and sports.
source_title: Products | Nifco

facts: Nifco Inc. pioneered the use of plastic fasteners, transforming industries, especially automotive.
Founded in 1967 as Nippon Industrial Fastener Corporation, originally focusing on plastic fasteners for automotive and home electronics.
Established on February 13, 1967, as a venture co-founded by Nichiei Bussan Co., Ltd. (holding 60%) and ITW (holding 40%).
Initial capital investment was 48 million yen.
Core vision was to provide efficient and cost-effective fastening solutions by replacing metal fasteners with plastic alternatives.
First product was the 'Plasti Rivet', designed to contribute to labor savings and cost reductions in manufacturing processes.
In December 1970, Nippon Industrial Fastener Corporation was renamed Nifco Inc.
In 1972, entered technology transfer agreements with America's 'Big Three' automotive manufacturers (GM, Chrysler, and Ford).
In October 1977, merged with Nichiei Bussan Co., Ltd., increasing capital to 1 billion yen.
Listed on the Second Section of the Tokyo Stock Exchange in July 1979, with capital increasing to 1.18 billion yen.
First overseas venture was co-founding Nifco Taiwan Corporation in Taiwan in May 1983.
Listed on the First Section of the Tokyo Stock Exchange in March 1984.
Co-founded Korea Industrial Fastener Corporation (now Nifco Korea Inc.) in South Korea in 1985 and listed on the First Section of the Osaka Securities Exchange.
In 1990, acquired Elta Plastics Ltd. (now Nifco U.K. Ltd.) in England and co-founded Nifco (Malaysia) Sdn. Bhd. in Malaysia.
By 1999, sales reached approximately ¥45.7 billion.
Pioneered the world's first mini-damper, popularizing the 'push-open' mechanism globally.
Engineered parts for fuel tanks to meet environmental regulations and developed sophisticated engine transmission components.
Received the Intellectual Property Achievement Award in 2006.
Established the Nifco Technology Development Center (NTEC) as its central hub for advanced technical development in 2013.
Achieved total sales revenue of ¥186 billion for the fiscal year ending March 2024, a 12% year-over-year increase.
Reported operating income of ¥23 billion for FY ending March 2024, a 15% increase from the previous year.
Recorded net income of ¥16 billion for FY ending March 2024, a 10% rise compared to the prior year.
Experienced a 5% decrease in net sales to ¥353,038 million for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025, while operating profit increased by 12% to ¥49,200 million.
Consolidated net sales were ¥353,038 million for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2025.
Market capitalization was approximately $2.39 billion as of July 25, 2025.
Established Nifco America Corporation in the United States in 1987.
Went public with an IPO priced at ¥1,300 per share in 2014.
Moved head office to Yokosuka in 2015.
Achieved ISO 9001 certification in 1998.
Established a subsidiary in Germany in 2006.
Targets a 20% increase in international sales by 2025, with projected revenue contribution rising to approximately ¥31.2 billion.
Aims to reduce its carbon footprint by 25% by 2025, having already achieved a 10% reduction by 2023.
Makes investments in renewable energy, including solar panels and self-delivering solar power plants, to support carbon reduction goals.
Concentrating on developing products for electric vehicles (EVs) and advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS).
Strategically responding to the automotive industry's push for lightweighting to drive growth in the plastic fasteners market.
Developing advanced plastic components that contribute to fuel efficiency and reduced emissions.
The global automotive plastic fasteners market is projected to rise from USD 5.03 billion in 2025 to USD 8.56 billion by 2034.
The automotive plastic fasteners market is projected to reach USD 7.89 billion by 2033.
source_title: What is Brief History of Nifco Company? – MatrixBCG.com

facts: Helena Industries celebrated the ribbon-cutting and opening of its new 180,000 square foot warehouse facility in Cordele, Georgia on October 25, 2016.
The new Helena Industries facility is adjacent to its existing manufacturing and administrative buildings.
Helena Industries will employ 10-12 new workers to operate the new facility, which will house inventory related to its manufacturing operations.
These new Helena Industries workers are in addition to the company's existing Cordele workforce of approximately 95 permanent full-time workers and 50-150 temporary seasonal workers.

On September 8, 2016, Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Sentury Tire, the fifth-largest and fastest-growing tire manufacturer in China, will create more than 1,000 jobs and invest $530 million in a car and light truck tire manufacturing operation in Troup County.
Sentury Tire's manufacturing operation will be located in LaGrange, Troup County.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Sentury Tire’s commitment to locate in LaGrange speaks to Georgia’s business-friendly climate and the strong relationship maintained with economic partners in China.
Gov. Nathan Deal emphasized that Georgia’s automotive manufacturing and technology capabilities will be assets to Sentury Tire as it serves a growing customer base.

On August 29, 2016, Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Variety Wholesalers, Inc., a retail store chain, will establish its second major distribution center in Coweta County, creating 320 jobs and investing approximately $10.5 million in the Newnan area.
Gov. Nathan Deal noted that Georgia’s business climate and transportation infrastructure continue to attract major businesses like Variety Wholesalers to the state.

Gov. Nathan Deal highlighted that Variety Wholesalers’ expansion demonstrates significant investment opportunities offered by Georgia's ports and will further establish the state as an industrial magnet.
On June 22, 2016, Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Amazon will create more than 500 full-time jobs in the Braselton area of Jackson County.
The 600,000 square-foot Amazon fulfillment center in Jackson County will be used for packing and shipping large items such as household decor, sporting equipment, and gardening tools.
Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Amazon’s decision to develop in Jackson County speaks to the strength and vitality of Georgia’s business-friendly environment.

On May 9, 2016, Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Dollar General will build a 1-million-square-foot distribution center in Butts and Spalding Counties, creating more than 500 jobs and representing a capital investment of $85 million.

Gov. Nathan Deal stated that Dollar General’s decision to build a fulfillment center in Georgia reflects the high quality of the workforce and the state's strategic location for accessing the Southeastern market.

facts: Gov. Nathan Deal announced that Nisshinbo Automotive Manufacturing, Inc. (NAMI) will create 100 jobs and invest $72 million in an expansion of its Covington facility.

With this facility expansion in Newton County, NAMI will continue to benefit from close proximity to top vehicle manufacturers while also reaching customers across the nation and around the world with Georgia’s logistics infrastructure, said Deal.

Gov. Nathan Deal noted that NAMI’s commitment directly reflects the strength of Georgia’s manufacturing environment for global industry leaders and appreciated NAMI’s investment over the last 20 years.
NAMI has maintained a presence in Newton County for more than two decades.

Along with NAMI’s business development center in Sterling Heights, Michigan, NAMI’s facility in Newton County produces high-quality friction materials for automotive manufacturers and develops environmentally friendly, copper-free brake pads.

facts: The automotive systems technology program equips students with technical knowledge and skills for employment in the transportation industry.

facts: Rivian has received conditional approval for a loan of up to $6.6 billion from the US Department of Energy to build its production facility in Georgia.
The announcement comes ahead of the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump, who is expected to undo many of the Biden administration's EV-friendly policies and incentives.
Operation of the Georgia plant will begin in 2028.
Rivian plans to build future vehicles at the Georgia facility, including its smaller, less expensive R2 SUVs and R3 crossovers.
Rivian shares are down about 50 percent this year as the company has struggled to produce its electric SUVs and pickup trucks while grappling with part shortages and pushing to slash costs.
To conserve cash and hasten the production of the R2, which is seen critical to Rivian's success amid a slowdown in EV growth, Rivian paused construction of the Georgia plant earlier this year.
Instead of building the R2 in Georgia, Rivian decided to start production there in 2026 at its Normal, Illinois plant where it makes its flagship R1S SUVs and R1T pickup trucks.

CEO RJ Scaringe stated that the loan would enable Rivian to more aggressively scale its US manufacturing footprint for competitively priced R2 and R3 vehicles that emphasize both capability and affordability.
Rivian must satisfy certain technical, legal, environmental, and financial conditions before the energy department grants the loan.
As part of the loan conditions, Rivian will not actively oppose union organizing efforts at the Georgia plant, though the approval will not guarantee unionization at the facility.
Rivian will work with the Energy Department to close the loan before the Trump administration takes over.
The loan comes from the government's Advanced Technology Vehicles Manufacturing loan program, which has previously provided low-cost loans to automakers including Tesla, Ford, and General Motors.
Rivian had earlier forecast the cost of the Georgia plant at $5 billion.
Rivian expects the Georgia plant to employ about 7,500 operations staff through 2030.
The Department of Energy stated in an October assessment that financially supporting the project will help Rivian bring 400,000 electric vehicles to market and into greater use.
The loan includes $6 billion of principal and $600 million of capitalized interest.
The loan announcement comes less than two weeks after Rivian closed its $5.8 billion investment from German automaker Volkswagen as part of their technology joint venture.

Canaccord Genuity analysts noted that the joint venture helps alleviate a significant chunk of capital concerns and likely establishes the Rivian and Volkswagen venture as a platform of choice in the Western world apart from Tesla.
Rivian still faces challenges including a lack of scale, increasing competition, high capital costs, and Trump's plans to end tax credits for EV buyers.
In 2022, Rivian secured $1.5 billion in state and local incentives for the Georgia facility.
In May, Rivian received $827 million in an incentive package from the State of Illinois to expand operations at its Normal facility.
Earlier this month, Rivian posted its first drop in quarterly revenue since going public three years ago, citing a significant shortage of a part used in the drive unit of its vehicles.

The company stuck to its forecast of turning its first gross profit in the current quarter, reflecting cost cuts from renegotiated supplier contracts, revamped manufacturing processes, and a sharp increase in green car credits.
source_title: Rivain's Loan: Rivian Gets $6.6 Billion Conditional Loan Approval To Build Georgia EV Plant | Republic World

facts: The 2026 Rivian R1S Quad delivers an insane 1,025-hp performance from its four electric motors.

The 2026 Rivian R1S Dual Standard features a 92.5 kWh battery, dual-motor AWD, 0 to 60 mph in 4.5 seconds, and a range of 270 miles.
The 2026 Rivian R1S Quad features a 140 kWh battery, quad-motor AWD, 0 to 60 mph in 2.6 seconds, and a range of 374 miles.
During testing, the 2026 Rivian R1S Quad achieved an average efficiency of 1.85 mi/kWh over 165.4 miles driven.
The new NACS port on the 2026 Rivian R1S managed an average Level 1 charging speed of 2.49 mi/hr at home.
Rivian's Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS), officially known as the Rivian Autonomy Platform, includes adaptive cruise control that maintains speed and reacts promptly to traffic conditions.
The 2026 Rivian R1S Quad features stadium headlights with Adaptive Drive Beam technology that dynamically adjusts lighting to avoid blinding other drivers.
The 2026 model introduces RAD Tuner, allowing granular control over ride height and acceleration, and serves as a gateway to the Kick Turn feature for tighter pivots on loose surfaces.
The 2026 Rivian R1S offers three-row seating accommodating up to seven passengers, with fold-flat third-row seats to expand trunk space.
The 2026 Rivian R1S includes usable front trunk (frunk) space capable of holding several grocery bags.
The tested price of the 2026 Rivian R1S Quad ($124,890) is compared to the $163,000+ Mercedes-Benz G 580 and the $99,915 Cadillac Escalade IQ.

Operating expenses decreased from $963 million in Q3 2023 to $777 million in Q3 2024.
Adjusted EBITDA for Q3 2024 was -$757 million, compared to -$902 million in the same period last year.
Rivian produced 13,157 vehicles and delivered 10,018 units between July and September 2024.
Rivian lowered its 2024 annual production target from 57,000 units to between 47,000 and 49,000 vehicles after missing parts paralyzed production for months.
Rivian's annual delivery target for 2024 remains unchanged despite the production cut.
A component shortage affected Rivian's Enduro motor, which serves as the single-motor-per-axle system in the Dual-Motor variant of the R1.
The Enduro motor is also used in the Tri-Motor variant, where it functions as only one of the three motors.
To mitigate the supply constraint, Rivian expects to produce more Tri-Motor vehicles and single-motor commercial vans.
Amazon is increasing its delivery order of commercial vans for the fourth quarter of 2024.
The component shortage limited R1 supply in Q3 2024, while a challenging consumer backdrop also negatively impacted sales.
Rivian expects to return to profitability in the current quarter.
Rivian anticipates increased revenue per unit driven by non-vehicle revenues like service and higher prices for premium variants such as the Tri-Motor R1.
Rivian and Volkswagen Group agreed to jointly develop car software at the end of June, with plans to close the joint venture in Q4 2024.
The approximately $5 billion joint venture deal with Volkswagen, combined with current cash and short-term investments, will fund operations through the ramp-up of the R2 in Normal.

facts: Rivian Automotive has announced a conditional commitment for up to $6.6 billion in funding from the U.S. Department of Energy’s (DOE) Advanced Technology Vehicle Manufacturing (ATVM) Loan Program.
The loan package includes $6 billion in principal and approximately $600 million in capitalized interest.
The funding is contingent upon Rivian meeting specific technical, legal, and environmental benchmarks set by the DOE.
If finalized, the funds will support the construction of a new manufacturing facility at Stanton Springs North, near Social Circle, Georgia.
The upcoming plant will serve as the cornerstone for producing the R2 and R3 vehicle lines, which are a midsize SUV and crossover, respectively.
The facility is designed to achieve a total production capacity of 400,000 units annually, split across two phases with each phase contributing 200,000 units.
Phase 1 production at the new Georgia facility is expected to commence in 2028.
The project is forecast to create around 7,500 permanent jobs by 2030.
The project will also create approximately 2,000 temporary construction roles.

Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe stated that the DOE funding would allow the company to scale production of its affordable and high-performing midsize vehicles while reinforcing U.S. leadership in EV innovation.
The Stanton Springs site is located less than an hour from Atlanta and will be designed with advanced environmental management practices.
Rivian plans to invest in the surrounding communities to ensure the project’s long-term sustainability and positive local impact.
Past beneficiaries of the DOE’s ATVM program include General Motors and Tesla.
Before the loan is finalized, Rivian and the DOE must resolve all outstanding technical, legal, and financial conditions.
Once approved, the loan will be secured against the project’s assets, as well as the fixed assets and guarantees of Rivian Automotive and its subsidiaries.
Rivian’s existing manufacturing plant is located in Normal, Illinois.
source_title: Rivian Automotive receives $6.6bn federal backing for new Georgia EV plant

facts: Rivian boosted initial production capacity at its Stanton Springs North plant in Georgia to 300,000 vehicles annually, up from the originally planned 200,000.
The company's Department of Energy (DOE) loan for the facility was reduced by $2.1 billion, shrinking from $6.6 billion to $4.5 billion.

The original DOE loan was structured around two production phases targeting 400,000 vehicles per year, but the revised deal collapses this into a single phase to allow earlier fund access and higher initial volume construction.
Construction of vertical structures at the Georgia plant is scheduled to begin in the spring, with the stamping press area already in preparation.
Production at the Stanton Springs North plant is targeted for late 2028.
In its recent first-quarter earnings report, Rivian reported an 11 percent revenue increase to $1.38 billion.
The company produced 10,236 vehicles and delivered 10,365 in the reported quarter.

Rivian reported a Q1 net loss of $416 million, improved from a $541 million loss in Q1 2025, though this includes a $506 million accounting gain tied to the Series A capital raise and deconsolidation of Mind Robotics.
Rivian's 2026 guidance projects vehicle deliveries between 62,000 and 67,000 units and an adjusted EBITDA loss between $1.8 billion and $2.1 billion.
The Georgia expansion strategy is centered on the R2 mid-sized SUV, which recently began production at Rivian's existing Normal, Illinois plant.
The R2 is priced to reach a far broader market than the R1T truck and R1S SUV, with early demand signals prompting Rivian to redesign the Georgia factory around higher initial volumes.

Rivian delivered approximately 50,000 vehicles last year, highlighting the scale challenge of tripling or quadrupling output alongside building a dealer-less retail infrastructure, service network, and brand awareness.
The reduced DOE loan may reflect a federal lending environment that has become less generous toward EV ventures since the original terms were negotiated.
Rivian plans to begin drawing on the DOE funds in 2027, requiring it to finance the next 18 months of construction using existing reserves and revenue from the R2 ramp.
The company is spending more than $4 billion on the new Georgia plant while reporting quarterly losses, relying on its cash position and product sales to sustain operations.
source_title: Rivian Goes Bigger in Georgia With $2.1B Less to Spend | Wheel Front

facts: U.S. electric vehicle maker Rivian Automotive plans to start the structure of its production plant in Georgia in 2022.
Site maps discovered through Rivian Forums revealed the California-based automaker was looking to build a 20 million-square-foot facility on about 2,000 acres.
The facility will be five times the length of Rivian’s first plant in Normal, Illinois.
Electrek reported that the massive plant will be 30 minutes east of Atlanta.

Concept plans received via Rivian Forums show the complex stretching on both sides of the line between Walton and Morgan counties, just north of Interstate 20, between Social Circle and Madison, Georgia.
Electrek first reported in December that Rivian had targeted northern Georgia for a second, larger facility.
Rivian announced that it plans to begin electric car production at the Peach State’s $5 billion plant in 2024.
The plant will employ more than 7,500 workers.
The plant represents a key next step as Rivian aggressively moves toward higher capacity production for its next generation of products.
Rivian Forums members reported that the Georgia facility will have thirteen separate buildings plus an R space.

Planning notes indicate the facility is legal under Level 1 zoning and may include garage space, accessory buildings, offices, a medical clinic, recreational facilities, a restaurant, education rooms, a nursery, a hotel, and a gym.
Solar, wind, and environmental energy production can also be built on the site.
Rivian's Illinois facility was originally a Mitsubishi factory before Rivian acquired it in 2017.
Four years after acquiring the Illinois plant, Rivian delivered its first electric passenger vehicle.
The Illinois facility has received two city-approved additions totaling 672,000 square feet, bringing its total domain to nearly 3.9 million square feet.
Rivian is about to deliver its moment of two iconic electric cars in the Illinois plant’s R1S SUV.
Electrek noted that Rivian has neither shown nor denied the plans, but they fit many recent company descriptions.
source_title: Rivian Automotive plans to unveil massive electric vehicle plant and track east of Atlanta — Motor World

facts: Rivian delivered 12,194 vehicles in the second quarter, above its own guidance of 9,000 to 11,000.
Full-year delivery guidance rose to 65,000 to 70,000 vehicles, up from 62,000 to 67,000.
Hitting the new target requires roughly doubling the first-half delivery pace in the second half.
Rivian delivered 10,365 vehicles in the first quarter and 12,194 in the second, for 22,559 in total.
Reaching even the low end of the new full-year range requires about 42,000 deliveries in the second half -- nearly double the first-half pace.
This spike in second-half deliveries would be Rivian's steepest ramp in history, executed in the same six months the company is scaling an entirely new model.
In the first quarter, Rivian's revenue rose 11% year over year to $1.38 billion.
The company generated $119 million in gross profit, a 9% gross margin.
The software and services segment produced $181 million in gross profit.
The automotive segment ran a $62 million gross loss, hurt primarily by a $100 million year-over-year decline in sales of automotive regulatory credits and lower production volumes.
Rivian's first-quarter operating loss widened to $881 million from $655 million a year earlier, on lower gross profit and higher operating expenses as the company builds toward the R2 era.

In its first-quarter update, Rivian said it raised the initial production capacity planned for its Georgia plant by 50%, to 300,000 vehicles annually, backed by an up to $4.5 billion Department of Energy loan.
A completed testing milestone in March unlocked a $1 billion investment from Volkswagen Group.
The second-quarter report, due July 30, is the first checkpoint for automotive gross profit improving as R2 volumes build.
source_title: Rivian Just Raised Its 2026 Outlook While Tesla Stock Stumbled. Is the Electric Vehicle Underdog Finally a Buy? - AOL

facts: Rivian broke ground on what it calls a generational investment in American manufacturing at the site of its future production plant outside Social Circle, Georgia.
The electric vehicle maker is known for adventure-oriented pickup trucks and SUVs.
Groundbreaking ceremony was attended by state leaders including Gov. Brian Kemp, First Lady Marty Kemp, and House Speaker Jon Burns.
Officials described the project as one of the most significant economic development projects in Georgia’s history.
The new facility represents a multibillion-dollar investment in the company’s future and U.S.-based vehicle manufacturing.
The plant is expected to generate 7,500 direct jobs by 2030.
Construction alone will create an additional 2,000 positions.
Analysts project nearly 8,000 indirect jobs across suppliers, vendors, and small businesses in Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton counties.
Once operating at full capacity, the plant is projected to produce an estimated $1 billion in annual labor income for the region.
Rivian plans to build the Georgia plant in two phases.
First phase construction is slated to begin in 2026.
Customer vehicle production is expected by 2028.
The site will initially focus on producing Rivian’s midsize R2 SUV and its companion R3 crossover.
Upon completion of the second phase, the plant will be capable of producing up to 400,000 vehicles a year.

Rivian founder and CEO RJ Scaringe stated the facility will cement the company's future in Georgia and help ensure America maintains its technology leadership and excellence in automobile manufacturing.
Scaringe added that the Georgia facility will support global expansion and provide scale to get millions of future drivers into all-electric vehicles.
State leaders positioned the project as a local economic boost and a signal of Georgia’s growing role in the clean-energy economy.

Gov. Brian Kemp stated the groundbreaking delivers on Rivian's commitment to Georgians and will provide generational benefits for families of thousands of hardworking Americans who will work at the facility.
House Speaker Jon Burns called the plant a milestone accomplishment that lays the foundation for billions in long-term economic benefits and strengthens families and communities for decades.
The nearly 2,000-acre campus will use modern construction techniques and advanced environmental management systems.
Plans for the site include recreational trails and a Rivian-branded experience path.
Rivian has already started building partnerships with Georgia universities, technical colleges, and workforce institutions to prepare skilled workers for the EV industry.

Officials from the Joint Development Authority of Jasper, Morgan, Newton, and Walton counties described the project as the realization of a decades-old vision for high-tech, good-paying jobs in the region.
Jerry Silvio, chairman of the authority, stated that seeing a Rivian vehicle on Georgia roads represents community pride.
Rivian hosted a community event on September 14 to welcome nearly 1,000 residents and local businesses to the Stanton Springs site.
The company has faced production and financial hurdles as it seeks to scale up operations.
Company officials argue the Georgia facility will provide the capacity and stability needed to compete with established automakers while advancing sustainable transportation.
source_title: Rivian American Manufacturing Digging in Social Circle Georgia with 7.5K Jobs | auto connected car news

facts: Toward the end of 2024, Rivian and Volkswagen partnered to develop a state-of-the-art, software-defined-vehicle (SDV) architecture that could be used across the duo's vehicle portfolios.
Volkswagen's initial investment into Rivian was for up to $5 billion, which was quickly bumped to $5.8 billion and would be delivered upon completion of certain objectives and milestones.
Upon the late 2024 launch, a $1.3 billion lump-sum investment was sent Rivian's way.
Early 2025 saw a $1 billion tranche, a mix of equity and debt, to complete operational milestones.
After passing winter testing in the spring of 2026, it unlocked another $1 billion investment from Volkswagen.
The spring 2026 milestone established Volkswagen as Rivian's largest shareholder, displacing Amazon.
First-quarter 2026 consolidated revenue checked in at $1.28 billion, largely driven by automotive and software and services segments.
The automotive segment generated $908 million, a 2% decrease compared to the prior year.
The software and services segment generated $473 million, a 49% increase.
The automotive segment gross profit was $62 million during Q1.
The software and services segment gross profit totaled $181 million.
The profitability boost from the software business powered Rivian to a positive gross profit result during Q1.
Rivian's software business growth opens the door for other traditional automakers to explore potentially lucrative software opportunities with Rivian.
source_title: 2 Stocks That Could Soar, Driven by Billions From Software Innovations. Hint: They Aren't Even Tech Stocks. | The Motley Fool

facts: General Motors expects massive growth from OnStar and Super Cruise subscriptions.
Last year, GM logged $2.7 billion in realized revenue and $5.4 billion in deferred revenue from OnStar and Super Cruise subscriptions.
This represents healthy growth from $1.7 billion realized and only $200 million deferred as recently as 2020.
Management expects those software services to generate $3.1 billion in realized revenue and $7.5 billion in deferred revenue this year.
GM's connected business could approach 70% gross margin.
CFO Paul Jacobson stated that these software-like margins can drive, and potentially over time, dwarf even the wholesale business.
Beginning with the 2025 model year, every new GM vehicle that rolls off the production line includes an eight-year basic OnStar subscription.
Vehicles with Super Cruise will have a three-year subscription built into the price.
At least 30% of the 35,000 GM drivers with an expiring three-year Super Cruise subscription renewed in 2025.
source_title: 2 Stocks That Could Soar, Driven by Billions From Software Innovations. Hint: They Aren't Even Tech Stocks. | The Motley Fool

entity_name: Electric Vehicle Market & Georgia Supply Chain
canonical_name: Electric Vehicle Market & Georgia Supply Chain
facts: Electric-vehicle sales in the U.S. were 325,000 in 2020, accounting for 2.4% of all passenger vehicle sales.
EV sales are projected to rise in 2024 to almost 2.5 million vehicles, or 17% of all sales, according to BloombergNEF’s Electric Vehicle Outlook report.
The projected 2024 EV sales volume would exceed the 1.8 million vehicles Toyota Motor Corp. sold in the U.S. in 2022.
Between August 2022 and May 2023, $70 billion of investments were announced in the EV supply chain.
Battleground states Georgia, Michigan, Arizona, Nevada, and North Carolina each received billions of dollars in projects during that period.
Rivian Automotive Inc. is building an EV production facility east of Atlanta in Georgia.
Hyundai Motor Co. is building an EV production facility outside of Savannah in Georgia.
Both the Rivian and Hyundai facilities in Georgia will be capable of producing hundreds of thousands of vehicles annually.

President Biden is expected to conduct hard-hat tours and ribbon cuttings at these facilities over the next 16 months, touting them as outcomes made possible by the Inflation Reduction Act signed in August 2022.
The facility developments are expected to help fulfill campaign promises to bring manufacturing jobs back to America.
Donald Trump stated he would scrap support for electric vehicles on his 'first day in office' should he win the election.
Florida Governor Ron DeSantis awarded money for electric transit buses and discussed efforts to bolster growing electric vehicle usage.
Florida has not attracted high-profile EV supply chain investments compared to other states despite DeSantis's efforts.
Automakers are committing tens of billions of dollars to shift production to EVs regardless of the presidential election outcome.
China surpassed Japan to become the world’s largest exporter of automobiles in the first quarter.
China’s BYD Co. is competing with Tesla Inc. to become the most important EV company.
China already dominates the EV battery supply chain.
source_title: Booming EV market complicates the GOP’s message - The Dominion Post

facts: The US electric vehicle maker Rivian announced it opened three fast charging sites in Colorado and California this week, marking the first deployments of its nationwide Rivian Adventure Network.
In March 2021, Rivian stated its ambition for the Adventure Network was to have over 3,500 fast chargers at more than 600 sites by the end of 2023.
With first sites launching in mid-June, Rivian would need to build 597 sites with more than 3,400 fast chargers in 18 months to meet its initial goal.
Rivian deleted the end of 2023 goal from its official website, giving no exact timeframe for the Adventure Network plan.
The Level 3 DC fast-charging sites will initially provide over 200 kilowatts of power.
The chargers allow Rivian drivers to add as much as 140 miles of range in just 20 minutes.
The first Rivian Adventure Network site in Salida, Colorado opened to the public on Monday, June 27 with four chargers.
The Salida site includes a pull-through charger to accommodate vehicles towing trailers.
The Salida site also hosts four Level 2 Rivian Waypoints chargers available for use by any EV through the Rivian smartphone app.
The second and third Adventure Network sites in Inyokern and Bishop, California will open on June 28 and 29.
The California sites support areas like Yosemite National Park, Sequoia National Forest, Mammoth Lakes, and Death Valley National Park.
Trent Warnke, Rivian’s Senior Director of Energy and Charging Solutions, stated that the charging was designed to support electrified adventure and enable drivers to reach natural spaces responsibly.
Warnke noted the fast charging rollout is designed to ensure travelers have places to charge along major transportation corridors coast to coast.
In mid-June, Rivian unveiled plans for a large-scale wind turbine at its Normal manufacturing campus to provide clean energy for new R1 vehicles.
Earlier this year, Rivian installed a 783-kilowatt solar canopy scheduled to begin generating electricity later that summer.
The wind project aligns with Rivian’s strategy of adding renewable resources to parts of the country that need them most.
Illinois’ grid is powered by 11% renewables and aims to reach net-zero carbon emissions by 2040, 10 years ahead of the Paris Agreement, according to the company.
Rivian Automotive Field Specialist Patrick Sarsgard announced the opening of a new service center in Boston.
Sarsgard stated that the U.S. electric vehicle maker is actively delivering on the East Coast.
During Q1 2022 Earnings, Rivian reiterated its forecast to produce 25,000 vehicles in 2022.
source_title: Rivian Automotive delays its goal of having over 600 sites by the end of 2023 | EV

facts: SAFT America Inc. is a globally recognized leader in battery technologies, catering to some of the world's most demanding sectors.
The company's journey began with visionary founder Victor Hérold as La Société Industrielle des Accumulateurs Alcalins (S.I.A.A.).
SAFT operates 14 manufacturing sites across 18 countries.

The company produces an extensive range of dependable battery systems and provides comprehensive services tailored for diverse environments, from land-based applications to space missions supported by agencies like NASA and the European Space Agency.
SAFT employs more than 1,500 employees worldwide, including many Ph.D.-qualified experts.
The company's workforce represents 49 countries with a notable 32% representation of women in engineering and management roles.
SAFT America Inc. is based in Florida and operates as part of the Total Group.
The company emphasizes efficiency and customer satisfaction through continual process improvements while remaining mindful of prevailing macro-economic trends that drive growth.
Key Account Management is pivotal to the company's strategy for enhancing customer relationships and maintaining a robust portfolio.
SAFT's strategic acquisitions include Tadiran for lithium technology.
Another strategic acquisition includes Friemann und Wolf Batterietechnik GmbH (Friwo) for nickel-based and thermal batteries.
These acquisitions have solidified SAFT's North American presence and bolstered its international market position via collaborations.
The company has exemplified ventures in battery technologies with Johnson Controls Inc.
CEO Annie Sennet and President Jeffrey S Simmons lead the company towards sustainable energy solutions focused on cost-efficiency and improved supply chain processes.
SAFT America Inc. operates under Florida business registration number F04000002693.
The company is an active foreign for-profit entity incorporated on May 17, 2004.
The company's home state is Delaware (DE).
SAFT America Inc.'s principal address is located at 13575 Waterworks St, Jacksonville, FL, 32221, US.
The company's mailing address is 313 CRESCENT STREET, VALDESE, NC, 28690.
SAFT America Inc.'s FEI Number is 582209171.
source_title: SAFT AMERICA INC.. Jacksonville, FL

facts: Saft is a pioneering French company specialized in the design and manufacture of high-performance industrial batteries.
The company's capabilities span cells, modules, pack assembly, battery management systems (BMS), and recycling.
Saft has 100 years of expertise in developing tailor-made battery solutions.
Solutions are designed for customers in multiple segments including aviation, aerospace, maritime, rail, and energy storage.
Saft is a wholly owned subsidiary of TotalEnergies.
The company offers nickel-based battery ranges for professional and industrial applications with elements ranging from 0.1 to 1,500 Ah.
Nickel batteries are noted for robustness, reliability, and longevity, serving as the reference for difficult or demanding applications.
Nickel batteries provide excellent performance at very low temperatures and are suitable for prolonged storage with limited or non-existent maintenance.
Approximately 80% of all large commercial aircraft use Saft's Ni-Cd batteries.
Saft's nickel batteries are also used in industrial standby applications, on board trains, on offshore platforms, and to power telecom masts.
The primary-lithium battery range consists of three main electrochemistries: Li-SOCl2, Li-SO2, and Li-MnO2.
Primary-lithium batteries provide high-energy and high-power solutions and can last more than 20 years.
Lithium-ion battery solutions are available with different electrochemistries including NMC, NCA, NMC/NCA, and SLFP.
Saft's lithium-ion batteries can discharge in less than 10 minutes.
The company provides emergency power for the Joint Strike Fighter Aircraft F-35 at temperatures as low as -40°C.
Recent Battery Storage Systems (BESS) projects include a Microsoft data center in Sweden that replaced a diesel backup system.
A BESS project at the Myrtle solar farm in Houston, Texas has a total storage capacity of 225 megawatt hours (MWh) using 114 containers.
The Grandpuits solar farm in Ile-de-France features a BESS with a storage capacity of 43 megawatt hours (MWh).
Saft has a global presence across 19 countries.
The company operates 16 factories located abroad and in France.
Saft employs 4,300 people worldwide.
The company maintains two research centers located in Bordeaux and Cockeysville (Maryland, US).
Corporate headquarters is located at 26, quai Charles Pasqua 92300 Levallois-Perret France.
Manufacturing plants are located at Georges Leclanché Street 86000 Poitiers, 111-113 Blv Alfred Daney 33074 Bordeaux, and 10 rue Ampère Zone industrielle 16440 Nersac.
source_title: SAFT

facts: Seohan Auto Georgia held a Grand Opening Ceremony for its new manufacturing facility in the Tradeport East Business Center on September 5, 2024.
The new facility is located in Midway, Liberty County, Georgia.
The facility will produce H/shafts, axles, and brake systems for Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs).
One of the facility's customers is Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Seohan Auto Georgia's operations will create at least 180 new jobs.
The project represents an investment of more than $72 million in Liberty County.
Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation is a new subsidiary of Korea Movenex Co., Ltd.
Parent company Korea Movenex Co., Ltd. was established in 1974 and employs more than 2,800 employees globally.
The process to locate the manufacturing facility in Liberty County began in November 2022.
Other locations were considered before Liberty County won the project for Seohan Auto Georgia.

Georgia Department of Economic Development Commissioner Pat Wilson stated: “Economic development does not happen by accident, and the community’s investment in the Tradeport East Business Center was not only an investment in your own future, but also allowed Seohan Auto Georgia to meet a challenging timeline to get up and running.”

State Representative Al Williams serves as the Liberty County Development Authority Board Chairman and said: “As the Liberty County Development Authority Board Chairman, I am proud to welcome Seohan to Liberty County. I was closely involved in the effort to locate this impressive manufacturing facility here, a process that began in November of 2022. There were other locations being considered, so we had to win this one for our community.”

The Liberty County Development Authority (LCDA) recruits industries, develops industrial parks, constructs shell buildings, supports educational and workforce development programs, and forms partnerships with public and private entities.
The Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD) serves as the state’s sales and marketing arm and lead agency for attracting new business investment.
source_title: Seohan Auto Georgia is Open for Business in Liberty County – LCDA

facts: Seohan Auto Georgia has announced that it will build a new production plant in Liberty County, Georgia.
The company manufactures automotive parts for Hyundai Motor Group America.
Seohan Auto Georgia will invest $72 million into the new facility.
The project will generate 180 new jobs in the process.
Production at the new plant will begin in 2024.
The new plant will produce H/shafts, axles, and brake systems for OEMs.
Jung Kee Koo serves as the CEO of Seohan Auto Georgia Corporation.
CEO Jung Kee Koo stated that sustained growth of the EV market over the past few years suggests accelerated changes to the automotive market are inevitable.
CEO Jung Kee Koo stated that he believes Georgia will be the center of the EV industry and a new frontier for Seohan's future with limitless opportunities and potential.
CEO Jung Kee Koo stated that the company is very pleased to collaborate in partnership with Georgia and move towards a promising new future.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that the Hyundai Metaplant is and will continue to be an economic driver for the entire region, creating more jobs for hardworking Georgians in communities like Liberty County.

Georgia Governor Brian Kemp stated that he is proud to welcome Seohan as the latest supplier in a growing list to invest in Georgia and looks forward to the continued growth of this fast emerging sector.
source_title: Seohan Auto Georgia to invest $72 million into new manufacturing facility | Plant Services

facts: Seohan Auto USA Corporation is a Korean-based company with American roots in Auburn planning to expand.
The company is in its planning stages of building a second facility creating initially 50 jobs and investing millions of dollars into the local economy.

Jobs will be in a manufacturing environment, including roles for machinery operators, machine setters, managers, and engineers, according to City of Auburn Director of Economic Development Phillip Dunlap.
Seohan has been located in Auburn since 2008 and currently employs about 240 people.
The company supplies front axles and rear carrier assemblies to several car manufacturers in the US including Hyundai and Kia.
Seohan's second plant will focus on machining metal components for the auto industry and will represent an additional capital investment of nearly $25 million in Auburn's economy.
Phillip Dunlap states that the investment is value-added manufacturing, more sophisticated manufacturing, and those jobs typically pay better.
The new facility is to be built in Auburn's technology Park West and is planned to be completed by the end of 2014.
Seohan Auto US President Chang-Dug Moon stated that the expansion will allow them to serve their new customer Mitsubishi Motors.
Chang-Dug Moon attributed the success of their business in part to Auburn's strong workforce and the support of the City of Auburn and the State of Alabama.
Phillip Dunlap states that creating those jobs creates a positive effect throughout the economy with more disposable income.
source_title: Seohan Auto to expand in Auburn, creating new jobs

The company's CEO is Lee Hyeon Duk.
SEOHAN INDUSTRIES CO.,LTD. operates as a middle-sized export/import company engaged in the manufacturing of new automotive parts, specifically fittings and flanges.

facts: A South Korean company will build a $72 million factory to make half shafts, axles and brake systems for a Hyundai Motor Group auto assembly plant in coastal Georgia.
Seohan Group announced plans to build the plant near Midway, south of Savannah, with plans to hire at least 180 new employees.

Seohan Auto Georgia is the seventh major supplier to locate in the region after Hyundai said in May it would build a $5.5 billion plant to assemble electric vehicles and batteries in Ellabell, Georgia.
The Hyundai EV/battery plant site could grow to 8,100 employees and is slated to begin producing vehicles in 2025.
Seohan and six other suppliers have pledged to invest nearly $2 billion and hire more than 4,600 people.
Seoul-based Seohan already supplies a Hyundai assembly plant in Montgomery, Alabama and a Kia plant in West Point, Georgia.
Seohan makes axles and driveshafts at two plants in Auburn, Alabama, that opened in 2008 and 2014.
The company said last year it would invest $13.5 million in the Auburn, Alabama plants, which jointly employ more than 200 people, to make parts for electric vehicles.
The company stated it needs additional capacity to make parts as automakers convert to electric propulsion.
Seohan Auto Georgia Corp. CEO Jung Kee Koo stated, “Sustained growth of the EV market over the past few years suggests that accelerated changes to the automotive market is inevitable.”
Jung Kee Koo added, “We believe Georgia will be the center of the EV industry, and will be a new frontier for Seohan’s future with limitless opportunities and potential.”
The new plant will be located in a business park off Interstate 95 near Midway, in Liberty County.
The company plans to begin production at the new Georgia plant in late 2024.
Seohan could qualify for $2.7 million in state income tax credits, at $3,000 per job over five years, provided workers make at least $31,300 a year.
The state of Georgia will also pay to train the plant's workers.
source_title: Hyundai supplier Seohan sets $72M, 180-job Georgia plant | AP News

facts: Seoyon E-HWA plans to invest $76,000,000.00 to build out new space in Savannah, Georgia.
The company plans to occupy the new space in Savannah on or about October 1, 2024.
Seoyon E-HWA was established in 1972 and split its holding company in July 2014.

The company started with the production of Pony interior products before expanding to produce Door Trim, Bumper, Seat, Head Lining, Covering Self, Package Tray, Screen Assy, and Pillar Trim domestically and overseas.
Seoyon E-HWA is recognized as Korea’s leading automobile interior parts company.
The company focuses on securing R&D, quality, and technological competitiveness by designing and proposing new materials, new product development, and new product models.
Affiliates are specialized according to the quality and characteristics of products to increase responsiveness to global automakers.
Audi and Nissan are listed as major global automaker customers for Seoyon E-HWA.
Yongsuk Kang serves as the Chief Executive Officer of the company.
source_title: Seoyon E-HWA to spend $76 Million to expand into new space in Savannah Georgia. - intelligence360

SEOYON E-HWA INTERIOR SYSTEMS GEORGIA INC. conducts environmental activities and supporting processes associated with the manufacture of door trim, luggage side trim and tailgate trim.

facts: SKF is a manufacturer of bearings, seals, and lubrication systems.
The global bearing market is valued at nearly SEK 500 billion (well over $52 billion).
SKF holds about 20% market share in the bearing industry.
SKF competes with Schaeffler, The Timken Company, NSK, NTN, and JTEKT.
Growth in the bearing sector is driven by demand from automotive, aerospace, industrial sectors, renewable energy, electric vehicles (EVs), and smart infrastructure.
More than 90% of SKF’s innovation portfolio is directed towards high-growth segments where sustainability and electrification megatrends drive demand.
SKF announced the separation of its automotive business from its industrial business to focus on distinct competencies and resource allocation in each market.
SKF President and CEO Rickard Gustafson confirmed that the separation provides strategic flexibility for independent business decisions and investments.
SKF engineers use algorithms to predict machinery performance over a bearing's lifespan by modeling physics and converting operational details into predictive maintenance models.
SKF prioritizes digitalization, AI, quantum-inspired methods, and sustainability while integrating sophisticated predictive technologies.
Integrating sensors and condition-monitoring technology into digital products differentiates SKF from competitors and creates opportunities for new markets.
SKF has developed customer tools over the past 15 years including configurators for bearing selection, calculation software for engineering, and digital twins for gearbox or powertrain models.
SKF introduced AI tools that answer technical questions instantly via a generative AI interface based on published technical information.

SKF uses AI internally for smart lead generation and customer data handling in sales, design automation and virtual testing in product development, and optimizing manufacturing processes and logistics in operations and supply chain planning.
SKF's digital product assistant guides users through product specifications, detailed engineering data, and comparisons based on years of engineering application knowledge.
Virtual validation supported by computational power is expected to reduce testing capacity by 50% over the next five years.
Bearings can now last 5 million kilometers between maintenance stops instead of 1 million kilometers.

SKF collaborated with high-precision machine tools manufacturer DMG Mori to develop the Insight super-precision bearing system for monitoring load, temperature, and vibration in a high-speed spindle during machining.

Through the collaboration with DMG Mori, lubrication control was improved, exact load measurements were delivered, closed-loop control maximized performance, and failures were predicted well in advance.
SKF's expertise spans materials selection from steel to ceramics for electric vehicle capabilities.
SKF ensures its bearings can run electric vehicles that require electric insulation without breaking down.
source_title: Investing in Motion: How a Bearings Leader Is Transforming with Digital Technology  | Machine Design

facts: SKF USA has announced the appointment of George Garner as Director of OES Truck Sales for the SKF Vehicle Service Market (VSM), North America Business Unit.
Ray Bihn will assume Garner’s current position as Vice President of Automotive Sales for the same unit.
In his new role, Garner will be responsible for all heavy duty OES sales, including Navistar, Daimler, Paccar and Volvo.
Garner will work closely with the SKF OEM sales team to bring the latest product solutions to the heavy duty OES customers.

As Vice President of Automotive Sales, Bihn will be responsible for developing and implementing sales strategies for SKF VSM in the United States and Canada, with a focus on the automotive aftermarket.

SKF is a leading global supplier of bearings, seals, mechatronics, lubrication systems, and services which include technical support, maintenance and reliability services, engineering consulting and training.
SKF is represented in more than 130 countries and has around 17,000 distributor locations worldwide.
Annual sales in 2015 were SEK 75 997 million ($9.3 billion) and the number of employees was 46,635.
source_title: Top Exec Appointments in Sales by SKF USA  : Fleet News Daily

facts: Guardian Glass Company was founded in Detroit, Michigan in 1932.
At founding, the company had 20 employees producing roughly 100 laminated windshields a day at a plant on Detroit’s Lower East Side.
Initial equipment consisted of only a press used for laminating glass to the interlayer, with all other work done manually.
Sales during the company’s first year totaled $130,000.
Guardian adopted a logo incorporating a big G surrounding a knight on a horse to represent security and protection against wind, weather, and debris for vehicle drivers and passengers.
During World War II, Guardian produced millions of square feet of laminated glass for windshields in trucks, jeeps, and other military vehicles.
In the early 1950s, Guardian began production of curved, laminated windshields.
In 1970, Guardian became the first company to enter the U.S. primary glass industry in nearly 50 years when production started at a new plant in Carleton, Michigan.
The company now operates plants on five continents with products used in vehicles, homes, and construction projects worldwide.
Guardian’s first European float glass plant was located in Bascharage, Luxembourg, establishing its worldwide expansion.
A small number of U.S.-trained managers and technicians moved to Luxembourg to assume leadership roles within the new plant.
The Financial Times later dubbed Guardian the "raging bull" in Europe for shaking up the glass establishment.
In 2016, Guardian celebrated 35 years in Luxembourg with the official opening of its new offices in Bertrange.
Low-Emissivity (low-E) glass features microscopically thin, transparent metal coatings that reflect long-wave radiation to improve energy efficiency in buildings.
Low-E glass was first manufactured in Europe and introduced to North America in the early 1980s.
In 1983, Guardian became one of the first companies in the United States to manufacture low-E glass by adding sputter coating technology at its Corsicana, Texas float glass plant.
Modern low-E coatings are multilayered designs engineered for high visible light transmission, low visible light reflection, and reduced heat transfer.
Guardian Glass manufactures advanced low-E coatings for residential and commercial applications globally.
In 1985, Guardian introduced HealthGuard, a global health program aimed at elevating employee well-being and controlling healthcare costs.
The HealthGuard program features educational initiatives, physical activity, health screenings, and community outreach to help employees and family members improve their lives.
Company health programs became more common later, with Guardian recognized as a leader in the arena, and its approach featured in a CNN News special.
In 2000, Guardian opened its Science & Technology Center (STC), one of the most sophisticated and comprehensive glass laboratories in the world.
Since opening, the STC has launched scores of new products and received hundreds of new patents.
President and CEO William Davidson directed the creation of the STC to shift focus from capital expenditure on float plants to making existing plants more profitable through innovation.
Davidson challenged employees to remain low-cost producers and pursue downstream operations and new products to exploit competitive advantages and develop new capabilities.
The STC location was placed across the street from a float glass plant to keep it connected to day-to-day operations.
The STC's objectives were to provide innovative products to customers and develop efficient manufacturing processes that increase quality while reducing customer costs.
In 2015, Guardian completed an expansion of its Science and Technology Center in Carleton, Michigan.
The expansion added 27,000 square feet to accelerate the rate of product design and development.

Expansion highlights include a full-size vacuum coater, a glass product showcase wall, an electrically wired full-scale demonstration wall for advanced glazing technologies, and upgraded laboratory space.
A cutting-edge curtain wall was added to achieve net zero energy results using Guardian’s SunGuard SNX 51/23, SunGuard Spandrel HT, and building-integrated photovoltaic panels.
Vice President of Research and Development Sheldon Davis stated the high-performance building demonstrates Guardian’s commitment to advancing glass technology.

In 2009, SRG Global Inc. was formed by combining the automotive trim operations of Guardian Automotive and Siegel-Robert, becoming one of the world's largest manufacturers of chrome-plated plastics for the automotive, commercial truck, and consumer goods industries.
The evolution began in 2008 when Guardian Automotive acquired Siegel-Robert Automotive.
SRG Global is headquartered near Detroit, Michigan, with manufacturing presence in North America, Europe, and Asia.
Through Advanced Development Centers, SRG Global enhances manufacturing processes and accelerates delivery of next-generation materials, coating technologies, and sub-systems globally.
SRG Global engineers solutions for greater surface durability, structural integrity, enhanced functionality, vehicle efficiency, and optimal design flexibility.
Koch Industries announced the completion of its acquisition of Guardian Industries Corp. after receiving all regulatory approvals.
Koch initially invested in Guardian in 2012 by acquiring a 44.5 percent minority interest in the company.
Guardian brings expertise in float, value-added, and fabricated glass products, high-value coatings on plastics for the automotive industry, and distribution of specialty building products to Koch.
Ron Vaupel, president and CEO of Guardian, noted the company began as a small windshield company in Detroit in 1932 and now serves a global customer base across multiple industries.

Charles Koch, chairman and CEO of Koch Industries, stated their philosophy is to invest where there is mutual benefit, with Koch's capabilities helping Guardian serve customers while Guardian's expertise bolsters Koch's operations.
Following the acquisition, Koch companies now employ more than 120,000 people worldwide, with about 70,000 of those in the United States.
Guardian companies have nearly 150 locations in more than 25 countries.
Terms of the acquisition deal were not disclosed.
Guardian Glass started full production on a technologically advanced new glass coater on July 6 at its second facility in Czestochowa, Poland.
The state-of-the-art coater uses advanced technology to turn standard float glass into high-performance, value-added glass.
The new coater will enable expanded production of low-emissivity (low-E) and solar control glass products for residential (Guardian ClimaGuard®) and commercial (Guardian SunGuard®) applications.
These high-performance products improve building energy efficiency, aesthetics, and occupant comfort.

links.context: Koch Industries press release regarding acquisition of Guardian Industries Corp.

source_title: Timeline

facts: Suhner Industrial Products LLC operates a facility located at 25 Anderson Rd - Plant 2 in Rome, GA 30161.
The company offers a Battery series of cordless power packs and tools designed for processing metal workpieces to support unlimited mobility.
The ROTOmax 4.2 Twin is a flexible shaft machine suitable for processing and finishing the surface of metal workpieces in multi-shift operation.
The BRUSHmax 3.0 tool cleans, polishes, and passivates stainless steel weld seams in one operation without changing the metal's surface texture.
The liquids used with the BRUSHmax only become reactive at high temperatures and are comparatively harmless to users and the environment under normal conditions.
Suhner ABRASIVE provides free product and application counselling through technical sales engineers to find the best grinding machine and abrasive solutions for customers.
Company vehicles are equipped with most available machines and a wide selection of high-quality abrasives to test different applications on-site.
Technical sales engineers advise customers throughout the entire project life cycle, not just during the decision-making process.
source_title: Suhner Abrasive

facts: Suhner has moved its marketing and sales office from Buffalo Grove to Barrington, Illinois.
The new Barrington office features sample models of tapping and drilling units along with real-world application examples on the walls.
Mike Ricketts serves as the regional manager of the machining division at the Barrington office.
Tom Dunn is the national account representative for the transmission division and specializes in orthopedic surgical tools.
Suhner operates two divisions: the machining division led by Mike Ricketts and the transmission division led by Tom Dunn.
Suhner’s flex shafts have particular advances in the medical industry and its primary automotive market, which includes power windows, seats, side view mirrors, and roof retraction.
The Barrington office is located 30 minutes from O’Hare International Airport to better serve current and future customers.
Suhner has partnered with Velocity Products to maintain the SU-matic line at the highest level of quality and functionality.
Velocity Products is a premier distributor of tooling for Okuma and innovative machine tools throughout North America.
SU-matic tooling is designed and manufactured by SU-matic, which collaborates closely with engineers from Okuma to ensure flawless performance on lathes, multi-tasking machines, and machine tools.
Velocity’s Walter Scheppe has over 30 years of experience maintaining and repairing lathe tooling, angle heads, multi-spindle heads, and specialty tooling.
The Velocity/SU-matic Preventive Maintenance Program offers a 72-hour inspection turnaround, inspection of radial and axial play, and a six-month guarantee on repairs.
Velocity/SU-matic tooling carries a two-year guarantee, with options to send tools to the repair center for inspection and return to original factory specifications.
The repair center is located at Suhner’s new location in Rome, Georgia.
Bob Bauer, Vice President of Velocity Products, stated that the partnership allows them to provide timely and high-quality service to Okuma.
Suhner has opened a new North American service center immediately next to its existing repair center on its campus in Rome, Georgia.
The new North American center features 40% more workspace due to better storage and materials handling operations.
The facility’s repair area has increased by 50%, with new repair function areas and equipment.
The Rome, Georgia campus includes improved training facilities, a test area, substantial room for expansion, vertical storage shelving (18’ racking), industrial cabinets, and dedicated stock areas.
Suhner has hired additional staff to provide onsite training in operations and maintenance of automated tooling equipment.
The service center repairs spindles, multiple spindle heads, slides, live tooling, and will soon service rotary index tables for CNC machines.
Suhner also repairs its own line of abrasive power tools and competitive brands of spindles, multi-heads, and live tooling.
SUHNER introduced the SUNmic 60, an innovative grinding, milling, and polishing system for tool and mold making, aircraft, engine, and motor building.
The SUNmic 60 features an intelligent control unit with a touch panel, glass display, rotation speed regulator, and large digital display.
It is driven by powerful brushless BL/DC micro motors with 280 W power, operating at 1000–60,000 rpm with high torque.
The system operates on a 90–240 V source for global use and connects to two BL/DC motors and an AC motor.
Chuck diameters for the SUNmic 60 range from 2.35, 3.0, and 6.0 mm to 1/8 and 1/4 inch.
SUHNER provides free demo services via a mobile vehicle carrying tools, attachments, and abrasives for onsite field expertise.
SUHNER introduced the ASC 22, a battery-operated straight Die-grinder with a rotation speed of 22,000 rpm.
The ASC 22 is Li-Ion Battery powered with a 4.0 Ah charge capacity and 18 V voltage, featuring intelligent battery management to prevent overheating or overloading.
The tool weighs 1.9 kg including its battery pack and includes two battery packs and a charger with four connector cables in a plastic case.
Suhner introduced the TurboTrim, a complete unit for cutting and trimming beef, pork, and poultry, featuring stainless steel blades, a coated housing, and a patented adjustment-free housing.
The TurboTrim has a stainless steel motor assembly with a slide-out bracket for easy removal and a quick change assembly for flexible shaft replacement.
Suhner introduced the Rotomax flexible shaft machine, which functions as an angle grinder, straight grinder, drum polisher, belt sander, tube polisher, or die grinder via interchangeable hand pieces.
The Rotomax offers power from 2.0-4.7HP with electronically-controlled speeds ranging from 500-15,000 RPM in increments of 100 RPM.
The machine is available as a hanging model, on a basic stand, or on a specially-designed stable trolley (STM) for portability.
Suhner Standard Flexible Shafts offer plug-and-play solutions with standard female couplings, set screws, and bi-directional application options.
K and Ka type plastic casing flex shafts are used throughout the automotive OEM market for engine control, power transmission, and interior controls on power seats and steering wheel adjustments.
Suhner handles speeds up to 50,000 rpm with high accuracy for applications ranging from printing presses to speedometers and power seat actuation.

facts: TDK Corporation was formerly known as TDK Electronics Co., Ltd and started its operations with the production of magnetic tapes that are compact with Cassette tapes.
The company was established in the year 1935 with its head quarters located in Nihonbashi, Chuo, Tokyo, Japan.
It is one of the oldest and leading Japanese based multinational company that offers high quality head phones, home audio and recordable multimedia accessories.
As of 2004, it is the first media manufacturer to join the companies developing BD post DVD technology.
In 2007 Imation acquired TDK for 300 million dollars and started manufacturing products under its name.
The company has operating income of 769.18 million dollar all over the world having around 545.34 million dollar Net income in the year 2011.
Blue-ray, CD, DVD, Power supplies, electrical components are the various types of products offered by company.

TDK Components, USA, Inc is a world leader in the production of Multilayered Ceramic Chip Capacitors and other electronic devices for telecommunications, computer, automotive and consumer electronic industries.

facts: The announcement regarding Newark stocking TDK components for e-bike and pedelec electronically power assisted cycles was made on May 21, 2024.

Newark now stocks a comprehensive range of electronic components for e-bike and pedelec electronically power assisted cycles from TDK, a world leader in technology innovation who is playing a vital role in advancing e-bike technology.

As micromobility takes center stage and the demand for eco-friendly transportation surges, TDK’s contribution is powering the development and production of efficient and reliable e-bikes and pedelecs.

Newark offers a wide range of TDK sensors essential for developing safe and reliable e-bikes, including Barometric Pressure Sensors, Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU), Ultrasonic Sensors, NTC Sensor Assembly and Systems.
Newark offers TDK capacitors, including EMI Suppression Capacitors and Hybrid Polymer Capacitors.
Newark offers TDK power line chokes, including Power Line Common Mode Chokes and Filters.
Newark offers TDK circuit protection products, including Leaded Disk Varistors and Inductors.

Dave Jones, Senior Manager for Passives at Newark, states that these key additions to the portfolio demonstrate a commitment to offering the very best in electric bicycle technology to customers, ensuring every ride is powered by components that set the industry standard for excellence and sustainability.
TDK e-bike components are shaping the future of e-mobility, making electric bicycles a more sustainable and attractive option for transportation.
The products are marketed under the product brands TDK, EPCOS, InvenSense, Micronas, Tronics and TDK-Lambda.
Customers can now purchase TDK’s range of electronic components for e-bike and pedelec electronically power assisted cycles from Newark in North America, Farnell in EMEA and element14 in APAC.
source_title: Newark introduces TDK components for e-bike and pedelec into portfolio to enable next wave of e-mobility revolution – EEJournal

facts: TE Connectivity plc is an American-Irish domiciled technology company that designs and manufactures electrical and electronic components.
The company serves several industries, including automotive, aerospace, defense, medical, and energy.
Terrence Curtin serves as the CEO.
TE Connectivity has a global workforce of 89,000 employees, including more than 8,000 engineers.
TE Connectivity operates a transportation segment that includes an automotive business unit.

TE's products are used by the automotive industry for vehicle body and chassis systems, convenience applications, driver information, infotainment, motor and powertrain applications, and safety and security systems.
Hybrid and electronic mobility offerings include in-vehicle technologies, battery technologies, and charging.
Products are also utilized for on-highway vehicles, off-highway vehicles, and recreational transportation, including construction, agriculture, buses, and other vehicles.
TE offers sensors for industries including automotive, industrial equipment, commercial transportation, medical, aerospace and defense, and consumer applications.

On February 12, 2025, TE Connectivity announced its intent to acquire Richards Manufacturing, a manufacturer of overhead and underground electrical and gas distribution products, for approximately US$2.3 billion.
source_title: TE Connectivity - Wikipedia

facts: Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani downgraded the rating on TE Connectivity to a Hold yesterday, setting a price target of $230.00.

The current valuation is roughly in line with the company’s historical P/E multiple, embedding much of the upside from structural growth drivers such as AI data centers, energy infrastructure, and industrial exposure.
This limits further multiple expansion over the next year.
AI-related revenue is growing but remains a relatively modest share of total sales.
Its quarterly trajectory has recently been more muted.
In autos, TE remains a leader.
Slower EV adoption introduces risk to margins and content growth.
A heavier China mix with lower revenue per vehicle introduces risk to margins and content growth.
Metal cost inflation introduces risk to margins and content growth.
The analyst sees a more balanced risk/reward profile rather than a clearly compelling entry point.
Wells Fargo released another report on June 8 assigning a Hold rating to the stock with a $226.00 price target.
source_title: Amit Daryanani Reiterates Hold on TE Connectivity as Valuation Caps Upside Amid AI and Auto-Sector Uncertainties - TipRanks.com

The Georgia Ports Authority is developing a new rail terminal in Northeast Georgia named the Blue Ridge Connector.
Wesley Barrell stated that the Blue Ridge Connector will move cargo with greater efficiency and lower cost.

facts: Nico Wijnberg holds the title of Director of International Relations at the Georgia Department of Economic Development (GDEcD).
GDEcD's international recruitment initiatives are focused on attracting international businesses to Georgia.
These recruitment efforts are reported to have an impact on communities across the state.
The video interview covering GDEcD's international connectivity and business recruitment was published on Wednesday, September 29th, 2021.
